title: A Baked Apple Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 good sized apples
- moist sugar
- 1 oz. dried currants
- 1 oz. raisins
- 1 oz. almonds chopped
- 2 oz. lemon and orange peel chopped
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel 4 good sized apples, cut them up rather small, put them in a stewpan, with moist sugar, 1 oz. of dried currants, 1 oz. of raisins, 1 oz. of almonds chopped, 2 oz. of lemon and orange peel chopped, a little cinnamon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Baked Apple Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 good sized apples
- moist sugar
- 1 oz. dried currants
- 1 oz. raisins
- 1 oz. almonds chopped
- 2 oz. lemon and orange peel chopped
- cinnamon
- ground cloves
- 1/4 lb suet
- 6 oz. flour
- salt
- cold water
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Peel 4 good sized apples, cut them up rather small, put them in a stewpan with moist sugar, 1 oz. of dried currants, 1 oz. of raisins, 1 oz. of almonds chopped, 2 oz. of lemon and orange peel chopped, a little cinnamon, and a little ground cloves
2. Let it stew till the apples are quite soft
3. Chop 1/4 lb of suet very fine, put it in a dish with 6 oz. of flour, a little salt, mix with a little cold water
4. Make it into a paste, roll it out on your paste-board 3 times
5. Divide it in 4 pieces, grease a pudding basin well, and put in a little brown sugar with a little cinnamon
6. Roll out one of the pieces of dough, put it at the bottom of the basin
7. Put a layer of the stewed fruit, so on alternately, leaving the last layer of paste to form the top
8. Bake it, and turn it out of the basin to send it to table
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Baked Sheep's Head
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 sheep's head, well seasoned and slit in halves
- stale bread
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg, grated
- onion, chopped
- 1 egg
- fat from soup
- potatoes, boiled

## Directions

1. Make a nice stuffing of stale bread with parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and chopped onion
2. Mix well with one egg
3. Lay the stuffing over the inside of the head
4. Put in a dish a good lot of fat from soup or any not required for pastry, with salt and pepper
5. Lay in the head
6. Flour over the dressing
7. Boil some potatoes and put them round the head
8. Bake all together in the oven for about 1 hour and 1/4
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Bola d'Hispaniola
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a half of flour
- three spoonsful of yeast
- two ounces of fresh butter
- one table spoonful of essence of lemon
- eight eggs
- half a tea-cup full of water
- a quarter of a pound of citron
- three quarters of a pound of orange and lemon peel
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- powdered sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Take one pound and a half of flour, with three spoonsful of yeast, two ounces of fresh butter, one table spoonful of essence of lemon, eight eggs, and half a tea-cup full of water
2. Make it into a light dough
3. Set it to rise for about an hour
4. Roll it out and cut it into three pieces
5. Have previously ready, a quarter of a pound of citron, and three quarters of a pound of orange and lemon peel, cut in thin slices, mixed with powdered sugar and cinnamon
6. Form the Bola with the pieces of dough, layers of the fruit being placed between
7. Do not bake in a tin
8. Strew powdered sweet almonds and sugar over it before baking

title: A Brown Fricassee
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of veal
- good beef gravy
- white pepper
- salt
- couple of sliced onions
- oil
- piece of whole ginger
- rich forcemeat
- three cold hard boiled eggs
- lemon juice
- half a glass of white wine or a table-spoonful of walnut liquor
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Cut the breast of veal in pieces and fry them lightly
2. Put them into a stewpan with a good beef gravy, seasoned with white pepper, salt, a couple of sliced onions previously browned in a little oil, and a piece of whole ginger
3. Let it simmer very slowly for two hours taking care to remove the scum or fat
4. Spread rich forcemeat about an inch thick over three cold hard boiled eggs
5. Fry these for a few moments and put them in the saucepan with the veal
6. Before serving, cut these balls in quarters, and render the gravy more savory by the addition of lemon juice and half a glass of white wine, or a table-spoonful of walnut liquor
7. If the gravy is not sufficiently thick by long stewing, stir in a little browned flour

title: A Bullock's Heart Stuffed and Roasted
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bullock's heart
- stuffing (as referenced in previous recipe)
- salt
- pepper
- gravy
- 1/2 pound rice
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Wash well after soaking, removing every particle of blood
2. Dry well on a clean coarse cloth
3. Fill with the same stuffing as the above
4. Put down to roast and baste well
5. Roast for 1 hour and 1/4
6. Boil half pound of rice with salt, pepper, and a little dried saffron
7. Drain rice well and make a bed of it in the dish
8. Lay the heart on the rice
9. Pour good gravy over it when dished up
10. Serve very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Bullock's Heart Stuffed and Roasted
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bullock's heart
- large onion
- small bunch of sage
- pepper
- salt
- soaked bread
- suet
- egg
- gravy
- half pound of rice
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Wash well, after it has been soaked, every particle of blood
2. Dry it well on a clean coarse cloth
3. Fill it with the same stuffing as the poor man's goose
4. Put it down to roast, baste it well
5. It will take 1 hour and 1/4 to roast
6. Pour over it when dished up a good gravy
7. Boil a half pound of rice with salt, pepper, and a little dried saffron
8. Drain it well, make a bed of it in the dish
9. Lay on it the heart
10. Serve it up very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Cabinet Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of savoy biscuits
- 2 oz. of sponge cake
- 2 oz. of ratafias
- 2 oz. of good raisins
- 2 oz. of lemon and orange peel
- almond oil
- sifted sugar
- 1 pint of almond milk
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- orange-flower or vanilla flavour
- 6 eggs
- raspberry, strawberry jam, or orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Grease a shape well with almond oil, with sugar sifted over
2. Make at the bottom a star with the finger biscuits
3. Ornament with a few of the raisins, a few pieces of the candied peel and ratafias
4. Crumble up the whole of the remainder of the cakes, mix with the peel and raisins
5. Mix in a basin a pint of almond milk, 1/4 lb. of sugar, orange-flower or vanilla flavour
6. Beat in the yolks of 6 eggs
7. Beat the whites to a snow and stir in
8. Stir in also a few spoonfuls of raspberry, strawberry jam, or orange marmalade
9. Put the cover on the mould with a piece of writing paper under
10. Stand it in a flat saucepan with boiling water
11. Boil 1.5 hours
12. Serve with a good sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Cake Without Butter
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- six ounces of flour
- beaten almonds
- thin slices of citron

## Directions

1. Beat well five eggs
2. Add six ounces of flour
3. Flavour with beaten almonds
4. Add, if liked, thin slices of citron
5. Bake in a mould in a moderate oven

title: A Cassereet, An East India Dish
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- water or gravy
- pepper
- salt
- cassereet
- rice

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of lamb chops
2. Place them in a stewpan
3. Cover with water or gravy
4. Season only with pepper and salt
5. When the chops are half done, carefully skim off the fat
6. Add two table spoonsful of cassereet
7. Stir it in the gravy which should not be thickened
8. Finish stewing gently till done enough
9. Rice should accompany this dish

title: A Cheap Coffee Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of bread dough
- one well-beaten egg
- one-half cup of sugar
- a generous tablespoon of butter
- finely chopped nuts
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Knead into a pint of bread dough one well-beaten egg, one-half cup of sugar, and a generous tablespoon of butter
2. Roll flat and place in a shallow pan
3. Let rise again until very light
4. Sprinkle with finely chopped nuts, dust over with sugar and cinnamon
5. Bake in a quick oven

title: A Cherry Batter Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine cherries
- butter
- fine batter well sweetened
- sweet sauce

## Directions

1. Stone and pick some fine cherries, put them into a buttered mould, and pour over them a fine batter well sweetened
2. Tie over the mould closely, and boil one hour and a half
3. Serve with sweet sauce
4. Plums or damsons are sometimes used instead of cherries

title: A Common Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- six ounces of butter
- two tea-spoonsful of yeast
- five eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- a quarter of a pint of milk
- currants
- carraways
- candied citron
- lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Rub in with one pound of flour six ounces of butter and two tea-spoonsful of yeast to a paste
2. Set it to rise
3. Mix in five eggs, half a pound of sugar, and a quarter of a pint of milk
4. Add currants or carraways and beat well together
5. If required to be richer, put more butter and eggs, and add candied citron and lemon-peel

title: A Cooling Drink In Fever
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little tea-sage
- a couple of sprigs of balm
- a lemon
- a quart of boiling water

## Directions

1. Put a little tea-sage and a couple of sprigs of balm into a jug with a lemon thinly sliced and the peel cut into strips
2. Pour over a quart of boiling water
3. Sweeten and let it cool

title: A Cosmetic Bath
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of starwort
- two quarts of water
- half a pound of linseed
- six ounces of the roots of the water lily
- one pound of bean meal
- two quarts of milk
- one pint of rose water
- a wine glass of spirits of camphor

## Directions

1. Boil slowly one pound of starwort in two quarts of water, with half a pound of linseed, six ounces of the roots of the water lily, and one pound of bean meal
2. When these have boiled for two hours, strain the liquor
3. Add to it two quarts of milk, one pint of rose water, and a wine glass of spirits of camphor
4. Stir this mixture into a bath of about ninety-eight degrees

title: A Country Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- dried cherries
- citron
- bread
- dried currants
- pint and half of milk
- fresh lemon peel
- 4 ounces of castor sugar
- salt
- 6 eggs
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Butter your mould well
2. Arrange some dried cherries in strips, with some citron cut thin
3. Cut some slices of bread and butter and spread over some dried currants
4. Arrange the slices one over the other in the mould till you have filled it
5. Boil a pint and half of milk with some fresh lemon peel in it, 4 ounces of castor sugar, and a pinch of salt
6. Beat 6 eggs with this
7. When cold then fill up the mould
8. Let it soak in
9. Put the mould in a stewpan to boil for an hour
10. Serve it with wine sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Delicate Receipt for Roast Mutton
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton joint
- cold water
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Put the joint in a saucepan, cover it with cold water
2. let it boil for half an hour
3. have the spit and fire quite ready
4. remove the meat from the saucepan, and place it immediately down to roast
5. baste it well
6. dredge it repeatedly with flour, and sprinkle with salt

title: A Delicious Appetizer
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of delicately browned toast
- several large, firm tomatoes
- one green pepper
- store cheese
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Place a slice of tomato on each slice of toast and season with salt and pepper and a dot of butter
2. Place several long, curly strips of pepper around the tomato, and cover with a thin slice of the cheese
3. Place in the oven until the cheese is melted
4. Serve piping hot

title: A Delicious Cream Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet milk
- cheese-cloth
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Allow sweet milk to stand until it is like a jelly, but does not separate.
2. Pour it into a cheese-cloth bag and hang up to drain until all the water is out of it and only the rich creamy substance remains.
3. When all the water is out, turn the cheese from the bag into a bowl.
4. To every pint of the cheesy substance add a tablespoon of butter and enough salt to season it palatably.
5. Whip it up with a fork until it is a smooth paste.
6. Put enough on a plate to make a little brick, like a Philadelphia cheese.
7. With two knives, one in each hand, lightly press the cheese together in the shape of a brick.
8. Smooth it over the top and put it away to cool.

title: A Dutch Fricandelle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of dressed fish
- two or three anchovies
- crumb of a French roll
- milk
- three eggs
- butter
- raspings
- anchovy sauce
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and bones from the fish, cut in small pieces with two or three anchovies, and season well
2. Soak the crumb of a French roll in milk
3. Beat it up with the fish and three eggs
4. Butter a mould and sprinkle it with raspings
5. Place the fish in the mould and bake it
6. When done, turn out and serve either dry or with anchovy sauce
7. If served dry, finely grated crumbs of bread should be sprinkled thickly over it, and it should be placed for a few minutes before the fire to brown

title: A Family Vegetable Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-lb. nob of bola
- 2-lb. knuckle of mutton
- 3 pints of water
- salt
- pepper
- turnips
- carrots
- celery
- leek
- a small cabbage
- Jerusalem artichokes
- potatoes
- a bunch of dried herbs
- motsa balls
- a little browning
- boiled cauliflower, carrots, or cabbage for garnish
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put bola and mutton on with water, salt, and pepper
2. Cut up all kinds of vegetables
3. Let it all simmer till the meat is done
4. Boil with some motsa balls
5. Colour with a little browning
6. Boil cauliflower, carrots, or cabbage to garnish the boiled meat
7. Throw over a little chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Fine Beefsteak Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef steaks
- salt
- pepper
- mushroom powder
- one pint of strong broth
- a couple of table-spoonsful of Harvey-sauce
- good beef suet paste

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of beef steaks into large collops
2. Fry them quickly over a brisk fire
3. Place them in a dish in two or three layers, strewing between each, salt, pepper, and mushroom powder
4. Pour over a pint of strong broth, and a couple of table-spoonsful of Harvey-sauce
5. Cover with a good beef suet paste
6. Bake for a couple of hours

title: A Fine Fish Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one teacup full of walnut pickle
- one teacup full of mushroom pickle
- three anchovies
- one clove of garlic
- half a tea-spoonful of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Pound three anchovies
2. Pound one clove of garlic
3. Mix all ingredients well together
4. Bottle for use

title: A Fine Receipt for a Savoury Omelette
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- a piece of butter, about an ounce and a half
- finely minced herbs
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Break four eggs, beat them up till thin enough to pass through a hair sieve, then beat them up till perfectly smooth and thin
2. a small omelette frying-pan is necessary for cooking it well
3. Dissolve in it a piece of butter, about an ounce and a half, pour in the egg, and as soon as it rises and is firm, slide it on to a warm plate and fold it over
4. it should only be fried on one side, and finely minced herbs should be sprinkled over the unfried side with pepper and salt
5. A salamander is frequently held over the unfried side of the omelette to take off the rawness it may otherwise have

title: A Fine Sauce for Roast Mutton
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- port wine
- gravy
- vinegar
- oil
- shalot
- mustard
- fried bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mix a little port wine in some gravy
2. Add two table-spoonfuls of vinegar, one of oil, a shalot minced, and a spoonful of mustard
3. Just before the mutton is served, pour the sauce over it
4. Sprinkle it with fried bread crumbs
5. Baste the meat with the sauce

title: A Fine Sauce for Steaks
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fat the size of an egg
- two or three onions sliced
- a little gravy
- flour
- a little vinegar
- a spoonful of mustard
- a little cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Throw into a saucepan a piece of fat the size of an egg, with two or three onions sliced
2. Let them brown
3. Add a little gravy, flour, a little vinegar, a spoonful of mustard, and a little cayenne pepper
4. Boil it and serve with the steaks

title: A Fine Sauce for Steaks
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fat the size of an egg
- two or three onions
- a little gravy
- flour
- a little vinegar
- a spoonful of mustard
- a little cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Throw into a saucepan a piece of fat the size of an egg, with two or three onions sliced, let them brown
2. Add a little gravy, flour, a little vinegar, a spoonful of mustard, and a little cayenne pepper
3. Boil it and serve with the steaks

title: A Fine Soap
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of bitter almonds
- a small piece of camphor
- one ounce and a half of tincture of Benjamin
- one pound of curd soap in shavings

## Directions

1. Blanch and beat to a paste two ounces of bitter almonds, with a small piece of camphor, and one ounce and a half of tincture of Benjamin
2. Add one pound of curd soap in shavings
3. Beat and melt well together
4. Pour into moulds to cool

title: A Fine Soft Drink For A Cough
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pint of new milk
- a beaten new laid egg
- a spoonful of capillaire
- a spoonful of rose water

## Directions

1. Add to a quarter of a pint of new milk warmed a beaten new laid egg with a spoonful of capillaire and the same of rose water

title: A Fine Vegetable or French Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of strong stock made of gravy beef
- carrots
- turnips
- leek
- celery
- brocoli
- peas
- French beans
- white sugar
- pepper
- salt
- force-meat balls

## Directions

1. Take two quarts of strong stock made of gravy beef
2. Add to this, carrots, turnips, leek, celery, brocoli, peas and French beans, all cut as small as possible
3. Add a few lumps of white sugar, pepper, and salt
4. Let it simmer till the vegetables are perfectly soft
5. Throw in a few force-meat balls

title: A Fish Sauce Without Butter
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup of water
- vinegar
- an onion
- a little rasped horse-radish
- pepper
- two or three cloves
- a couple of anchovies
- the beaten yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Put on, in a small saucepan, a cup of water, well flavored with vinegar, an onion chopped fine, a little rasped horse-radish, pepper, and two or three cloves, and a couple of anchovies cut small
2. When it has boiled, stir carefully in the beaten yolks of two eggs, and let it thicken, until of the consistency of melted butter

title: A Floating Island
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 good-sized cooking apples
- 1/2 pint of boiling water
- rind of a lemon
- several cloves
- 3/4 lb. of sifted sugar
- almond or other milk
- vanille
- 4 yolks of eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- pieces of citron or angelica

## Directions

1. Peel thin 6 good-sized cooking apples, core them, cut in pieces, put them in your fruit saucepan with 1/2 pint of boiling water.
2. Let them cook till you can pulp them to a cream, grate in the rind of a lemon, and boil with several cloves.
3. Pass it through a sieve, put it on the fire with 3/4 lb. of sifted sugar.
4. Let it simmer like a preserve, press it down in an earthenware mould till cold.
5. Have ready a custard made with almond or other milk as required, flavoured with vanille, 4 yolks of eggs with a pint of milk, 1/2 lb. of sugar; turn it in a mug to cool.
6. Turn the apples out of the shape into the centre of a glass dish, pour the custard all round; garnish with pieces of citron or angelica.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Fondu
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce and a half of potatoe flour
- one ounce and a half of grated cheese
- one ounce and a half of butter
- a quarter of a pint of milk or cream
- salt
- pepper
- yolks of four fine fresh eggs
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Make into a batter one ounce and a half of potatoe flour, with the same quantity of grated cheese and of butter, and a quarter of a pint of milk or cream
2. Add a little salt, very little pepper, and the well-beaten yolks of four fine fresh eggs
3. When all this is well mixed together, pour in the whites of the eggs, well whisked to a froth
4. Pour the mixture into a deep soup plate or dish, used expressly for the purpose
5. Bake in a moderate oven
6. The dish should be only half filled with the fondu, as it will rise very high
7. Serve the moment it is ready, or it will fall
8. Hold a salamander over it while being brought to table

title: A French Plum Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine dried French plums
- water
- one glass of port wine
- four ounces of white sugar
- rich puff paste

## Directions

1. Stew one pound of fine dried French plums until tender, in water, rather more than enough to cover, with one glass of port wine
2. Add four ounces of white sugar when the plums are quite tender
3. Pour them with the liquor into a pie-dish
4. Cover with a rich puff paste
5. Bake

title: A French Receipt For Making Chocolate
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of chocolate
- a small teacup full of water
- nearly a pint of good milk

## Directions

1. Cut one ounce of chocolate in small pieces
2. Boil it about six or seven minutes with a small teacup full of water
3. Stir it till smooth
4. Add nearly a pint of good milk
5. Give it another boil, stirring or milling it well
6. Serve directly

title: A Good Gravy for Roast Fowls
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little stock
- the juice of a lemon
- a little mushroom powder
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a little stock, squeeze in the juice of a lemon
2. Add a little mushroom powder, cayenne pepper and salt
3. Thicken with flour

title: A Good Plain Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quartern of roll dough
- 1/2 lb. of currants well washed and dried
- 1/2 lb. raisins stoned, or 3/4 lb. sultanas
- 1/4 oz. of mixed spice
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1/2 lb. butter
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Beat it for half an hour
3. Butter well your tin
4. Bake in a good oven one hour and a half
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Good Plum Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1 1/2 lb. of flour
- nutmeg
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1/2 lb. of citron and orange peel

## Directions

1. Beat 1 lb. of butter to a cream with a spoon
2. Add one pound of brown sugar
3. Beat for 10 minutes
4. Add 8 eggs one at the time until it is smooth
5. Stir in 1 1/2 lb. of flour, put in the flour a little at the time till it is well mixed
6. Add a little nutmeg and 1 lb. of currants
7. Add 1/2 lb. of citron and orange peel
8. Bake it 2 hours in a moderate oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Good Seed Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quartern of roll dough
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 oz. of carraway or aniseed well picked
- a pinch of salt
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Beat for half an hour
3. Bake in a good oven one hour and a half
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Good Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lb knuckle of veal
- 2 quarts of water
- a little salt, pepper, a blade of mace
- a carrot
- a turnip
- a leek
- an onion
- a small white cabbage
- 3 sticks of celery
- 1/2 lb. of rice
- a little dried saffron
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put in a good sized soup saucepan 3-lb knuckle of veal, 2 quarts of water, a little salt, pepper, a blade of mace
2. Cut and wash a carrot, turnip, leek, onion, a small white cabbage, 3 sticks of celery
3. Put them all in the saucepan
4. Wash 1/2-lb. of rice and put with
5. Let it simmer for 3 hours
6. Take out the knuckle, strip off the flesh, cut it in nice pieces
7. Put the flesh back in the saucepan
8. Let it all boil for half an hour longer
9. Colour with a little dried saffron
10. Have a little chopped parsley in the tureen
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Harricot
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- best end of a neck of mutton
- flour
- carrots
- turnips
- button onions
- water
- pepper
- salt
- browning
- mushroom powder

## Directions

1. Cut off the best end of a neck of mutton into chops
2. flour and partly fry them
3. lay them in a stewpan with carrots, sliced turnips cut in small round balls, some button onions
4. cover with water
5. skim frequently
6. season with pepper and salt to taste
7. color the gravy with a little browning and a spoonful of mushroom powder

title: A Juditha
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- very little water
- well-beaten yolks of eggs
- white sugar
- a shape of biscuit ice or any other cream ice
- brown-bread ice

## Directions

1. Put some gooseberries into a saucepan with very little water
2. When they are soft, pulp them through a sieve
3. Add several well-beaten yolks of eggs
4. Sweeten with white sugar
5. Have ready a shape of biscuit ice or any other cream ice that may be preferred
6. Take off a thick slice of the ice from the top carefully and without breaking
7. Scoop out a large portion of the ice which may be mixed with the gooseberry cream
8. Fill the hollow with it
9. Cover the shape with the piece that was removed and serve

title: A La Mode Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of ribs, boned and rolled, or a piece of the round
- 1/2 pint of vinegar
- 1/2 pint of water
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- allspice
- 3 or 4 bay leaves
- a cup of raspings from the baker

## Directions

1. Put beef in a stewpan with vinegar and water
2. Season with onion, salt, pepper, allspice, 3 or 4 bay leaves, and a cup of raspings from the baker
3. Let it stew gently for 3 or 4 hours, according to the weight of the beef
4. Strain the gravy before sending up to table, and do not thicken it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A la Mode Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of ribs or round of beef
- 1/2 pint vinegar
- 1/2 pint water
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- allspice
- 3 or 4 bay leaves
- cup of raspings from the baker

## Directions

1. Have a pair of ribs, boned and rolled, or a piece of the round
2. Put it in a stewpan with 1/2 pint of vinegar and 1/2 pint of water
3. Season with onion, salt, pepper, allspice, 3 or 4 bay leaves, a cup of raspings from the baker
4. Let it stew gently for 3 or 4 hours, according to the weight of the beef
5. This dish is excellent when cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Luction, or A Rachael
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin nouilles paste
- water
- beaten eggs
- white sugar
- fresh butter
- grated lemon peel
- preserved cherries
- custard or cream
- brandy

## Directions

1. Make a thin nouilles paste and cut into strips of about two inches wide
2. Leave it to dry
3. Boil the strips in a little water and drain through a colander
4. When the water is strained off, mix it with beaten eggs, white sugar, a little fresh butter, and grated lemon peel
5. Bake or boil in a shape lined with preserved cherries
6. When turned out pour over a fine custard or cream flavored with brandy and sweetened to taste

title: A Nice Breakfast Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of flour
- one ounce of butter
- a very little salt
- two eggs
- a table-spoonful of milk

## Directions

1. Make a paste of half a pound of flour, one ounce of butter, a very little salt, two eggs, and a table-spoonful of milk
2. Set it to rise before the fire
3. Roll it out
4. Cut it into cakes the size of small cheese plates
5. Sprinkle with flour
6. Bake on a tin in a brisk oven, or they may be fried in a clean frying pan
7. Cut in half, butter hot, and serve quickly

title: A Nice Method of Dressing Fowl and Sweetbread
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fowl
- one fine sweetbread
- veal gravy
- white sauce
- forcemeat balls
- truffles
- morels
- beef gravy
- mushroom ketchup or lemon-pickle

## Directions

1. Blanch a fowl and a fine sweetbread
2. Parboil them
3. Cut off in smooth well shaped slices all the white part of the fowl
4. Slice the sweetbread in similar pieces
5. Place them together in a fine well-flavoured veal gravy
6. When done, serve neatly in the dish and pour over a fine white sauce
7. Cut the remainder of the fowl in small joints or pieces, not separated from the bone
8. Fry them to become brown
9. Place them in a stew-pan with forcemeat balls, truffles, and morels
10. Pour over half or three quarters of a pint of beef gravy and simmer till finished
11. Add a little mushroom ketchup or lemon-pickle if desired

title: A Nice Receipt for Shoulder of Lamb
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of lamb
- lemon juice
- grated bread crumbs
- egg
- parsley
- lemon pickle
- mint

## Directions

1. Half boil it
2. Score it and squeeze over lemon juice
3. Cover with grated bread crumbs, egg and parsley
4. Broil it over a clear fire and put it to brown in a Dutch oven, or grill
5. Serve with a sauce seasoned with lemon pickle and chopped mint

title: A Nice Rice Pudding for Children
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of well picked rice
- one quart of fresh milk
- white sugar
- whole cinnamon
- lemon peel
- a bay leaf
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil till tender half a pound of well picked rice in one quart of fresh milk, sweeten with white sugar, and flavour with whole cinnamon, lemon peel, and a bay leaf
2. When the rice is tender, place it in a deep dish, pour over a very little butter warmed in a little milk, and bake until brown
3. A slow oven is requisite unless the rice is extremely soft before it is put in the oven

title: A Nice Sauce to Throw Over Broiled Meats
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little salad-oil
- a table-spoonful of vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- the yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Beat up a little salad-oil with a table-spoonful of vinegar, mustard, pepper and salt
2. Stir in the yolk of an egg
3. This sauce should be highly seasoned

title: A Nice Way of Dressing Red Herrings
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- red herrings
- hot water
- warmed butter
- egg (1 beaten)
- bread crumbs
- flour
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Open them, cut off the tails and heads
2. Soak them in hot water for an hour
3. Wipe them dry
4. Mix with warmed butter one beaten egg
5. Pour this over the herrings
6. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, flour, and white pepper
7. Broil them and serve them very hot

title: A Plain Bola
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound of white sugar
- three quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- two eggs
- one pound and a half of flour
- three spoonsful of yeast
- milk
- two ounces of citron
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut citron thin and mix into a light paste with all ingredients
2. Bake in a tin
3. Strew powdered sugar and cinnamon over it before baking

title: A Plain Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of dough
- a quarter of a pound of sugar
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- a couple of eggs

## Directions

1. Work into two pounds of dough a quarter of a pound of sugar and the same of butter
2. Add a couple of eggs
3. Bake in a tin

title: A Plain Souffle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of rice-flour, arrowroot, or tapioca
- half a pint of milk
- essence of almond
- lemon peel or orange-flour water
- white sugar
- beaten yolks of five eggs
- whisked whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Mix well together six ounces of rice-flour, arrowroot, or tapioca with half a pint of milk flavoured with essence of almond and lemon peel, or orange-flour water
2. Let it thicken over the fire, stirring to keep it smooth
3. Sweeten with white sugar
4. Add the beaten yolks of five eggs
5. Add the whisked whites at the moment of placing the souffle into the oven
6. Bake in a souffle dish or cake-tin lined with fringed paper
7. Twist a napkin around it when brought to table

title: A Poor Man's Goose
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 to 6 lbs. prime bola
- large onion
- small bunch of sage
- pepper
- salt
- soaked bread
- 2 oz. of suet
- egg
- dripping
- melted suet
- apple sauce
- potatoes
- flour
- boiled cabbage

## Directions

1. Take a piece of prime bola from 5 to 6 lbs., according to the family
2. Chop a large onion, a small bunch of sage, pepper, salt, a piece of soaked bread, 2 oz. of suet
3. Mix it all well together with an egg
4. Open the crevices of the meat, fill them up with the stuffing
5. Skewer them tight together, tie it round with string
6. Put it down to roast with some dripping, baste it well with melted suet
7. Serve it hot with some apple sauce
8. Parboil as many potatoes as you require, then put them in a tin dish
9. Take a few spoons of the fat from the dripping pan, put them in the oven to brown
10. Sprinkle over them salt, pepper, and a little flour
11. Serve with a good large boiled cabbage
12. Roast for 3 hours
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Portugal Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. butter
- 3/4 lb. castor sugar
- 9 fresh eggs

## Directions

1. Take half-a-pound of the best flour, put it before the fire to dry for half an hour.
2. Sift it through a sieve, put it aside.
3. Melt 1/2 lb. of butter to oil.
4. Put 3/4 lb. of castor sugar in a skillet or pan that is round at the bottom.
5. Break over the sugar 9 fresh eggs.
6. Stir the eggs and sugar till it froths.
7. Put in the butter by degrees while another person stirs the eggs and sugar.
8. When the butter is well worked in, then put the flour in the same way by spoonfuls.
9. When it is well worked in, grease your tin and sift a little fine sugar in the tin.
10. Put it in a slow oven.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Pound Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of butter
- a pound of sifted loaf sugar
- eight eggs
- three quarters of a pound of flour
- currants

## Directions

1. Beat to cream a pound of butter and a pound of sifted loaf sugar
2. Add eight beaten eggs
3. Stir in lightly three quarters of a pound of flour
4. Beat well together
5. Bake for one hour in a brisk oven
6. Currants may be added if approved

title: A Refreshing Drink
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large apples
- a quart of boiling water
- white sugar
- a little lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cut four large apples in slices and pour over a quart of boiling water
2. Let them stand till cold
3. Strain the liquor and sweeten with white sugar
4. A little lemon peel put with the apples improves the flavour

title: A Relishing Sauce for Fish Served Cold
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of the best oil
- 3 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- a spoonful of mustard pickle

## Directions

1. Mix in a basin 2 tablespoonfuls of the best oil and 3 of vinegar
2. Add a little salt and pepper
3. Rub it well with a spoon till it comes like a cream
4. Chop a spoonful of mustard pickle and put them in your sauceboat
5. Turn the sauce over them
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Relishing Sauce For Steaks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of melted fat, the size of an egg
- 2 onions
- a little beef gravy
- a small spoonful of flour
- a little cayenne
- salt

## Directions

1. Put melted fat into a small saucepan
2. Add 2 onions sliced thin and let them brown
3. Add a little beef gravy with a small spoonful of flour and a little cayenne
4. Add salt and boil it well
5. Throw it over the steak
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Rich Bread and Butter Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread and butter from a French roll
- yolks of four eggs beaten
- four ounces of moist sugar
- a few soaked ratafias
- a table-spoonful of brandy
- a few currants
- milk
- whites of the eggs beaten to a froth

## Directions

1. Lay in a deep dish alternate layers of bread and butter cut from a French roll, and a mixture of the yolks of four eggs beaten, four ounces of moist sugar, a few soaked ratafias, a table-spoonful of brandy and a few currants
2. Fill up the dish with these layers, and pour over a little milk
3. The last layer should be of bread and butter
4. The whites of the eggs beaten to a froth may, if an elegant appearance is wished for, be laid over the top when the pudding is nearly baked

title: A Rich Brown Gravy
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little good beef consomme, or stock
- a small piece of smoked beef, or chorissa
- a lemon sliced
- some chopped shalots
- a couple of onions shred
- a bay leaf
- two or three cloves
- a little oil
- a little minced parsley
- a few chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Take a little good beef consomme, or stock, a small piece of smoked beef, or chorissa, a lemon sliced, some chopped shalots, a couple of onions shred, a bay leaf, two or three cloves, and a little oil
2. Simmer gently
3. Add a little minced parsley, and a few chopped mushrooms
4. Skim and strain

title: A Sauce for Fish When You Can Neither Use Butter or Stock
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of flour
- 2 tablespoons of good oil
- a teaspoonful of made mustard
- salt
- pepper
- a small shalot
- 2 anchovies
- 3 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- a pint of the liquor the fish was boiled in

## Directions

1. Put into a small saucepan a tablespoonful of flour and 2 tablespoons of good oil
2. Rub the flour and oil together to a paste
3. Add a teaspoonful of made mustard, a little salt, pepper, a small shalot chopped fine, 2 anchovies washed and boned, and 3 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
4. Stir all well together
5. Stir in a pint of the liquor the fish was boiled in
6. Let it simmer gently and when it boils it is ready to serve
7. For mackerel add some chopped fennel; for salmon, some capers; for haddock, chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Savoury Pie for Persons of Delicate Digestion
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- sweetbread
- meat
- jelly
- yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- lemon-juice
- white pepper
- salt
- half a pound of rice
- water
- yolk of one or two eggs

## Directions

1. Cut up fowl and sweetbread
2. Lay in the dish in alternate layers with meat, jelly, and the yolks of hard-boiled eggs without the whites
3. Flavor with lemon-juice, white pepper, and salt
4. Boil half a pound of rice in sufficient water to permit it to swell
5. When tender beat it up to a thick paste with the yolk of one or two eggs
6. Season with a little salt
7. Spread it over the dish thickly as cover

title: A Savoury Way of Roasting a Fowl
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- fine seasoning
- clarified veal suet
- bread crumbs
- lemon juice
- forcemeat fritters
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Fill it with a fine seasoning
2. Just before it is ready for serving, baste it well with clarified veal suet
3. Sprinkle it thickly with very dry crumbs of bread
4. Repeat this two or three times
5. Place it in the dish
6. Serve with a fine brown gravy well flavored with lemon juice
7. Delicate forcemeat fritters should be also served in the dish

title: A Savoury Way of Roasting a Fowl
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine seasoning
- clarified veal suet
- very dry crumbs of bread
- lemon juice
- forcemeat fritters

## Directions

1. Fill the fowl with a fine seasoning
2. Just before it is ready for serving, baste it well with clarified veal suet
3. Sprinkle it thickly with very dry crumbs of bread
4. Repeat this two or three times
5. Place it in the dish
6. Serve with a fine brown gravy well flavored with lemon juice
7. Serve delicate forcemeat fritters in the dish

title: A Simple Stewed Steak
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine steak
- a large piece of clarified suet or fat
- a couple of onions
- gravy or water
- a tea-cup full of button onions or a Spanish onion
- a little lemon peel
- pepper
- salt
- a little allspice
- a little browning
- a spoonful of mushroom ketchup
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Put a fine steak in a stewpan with a large piece of clarified suet or fat, and a couple of onions sliced
2. Let the steak fry for a few minutes, turning it several times
3. Cover the steak with gravy, or even water will answer the purpose
4. Add a tea-cup full of button onions or a Spanish onion sliced, a little lemon peel, pepper, salt, and a little allspice
5. Simmer till the steak is done
6. Remove the steak and carefully skim the gravy
7. Add to it a little browning and a spoonful of mushroom ketchup
8. Keep the steak on a hot stove or return it to the stewpan to warm up
9. If the gravy is not thick enough, stir in a little flour

title: A Simple Way of Dressing Mutton
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet from a small leg or shoulder of mutton
- egg
- seasoning
- strong gravy
- balls or fritters

## Directions

1. Take the fillet off a small leg or shoulder of mutton
2. rub it well over with egg and seasoning
3. partly roast it
4. place it in a stewpan with a little strong gravy
5. stew gently till thoroughly done
6. garnish with a few balls or fritters

title: A Simple Way of Making Beef Tea
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of gravy beef
- a pint of water
- a very little salt
- 2 cloves
- 2 allspice
- a small piece of crust

## Directions

1. Cut the gravy beef in pieces the size of dice, put into a pint of water with a very little salt, 2 cloves, 2 allspice
2. Let it boil for 1 hour only
3. Toast a small piece of crust
4. Let it boil with the crust to colour it
5. Strain it through a very clean sieve into a white basin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Simple White Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- a piece of chorissa
- a carrot
- two onions
- three or four turnips
- a blade of mace
- two or three quarts of water or weak broth
- salt
- a sprig of parsley
- whole white pepper
- pounded vermicelli

## Directions

1. Break a knuckle of veal and place it in a stewpan
2. Also place a piece of chorissa, a carrot, two onions, three or four turnips, and a blade of mace
3. Pour over two or three quarts of water or weak broth
4. Season with salt, a sprig of parsley, and whole white pepper
5. When sufficiently boiled, skim and strain it
6. Thicken with pounded vermicelli

title: A Slice of Salmon with Montpelier Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold salmon slice
- 8 anchovies
- 1/4 lb. fresh butter
- 2 shalots
- 1 tooth of garlic
- tarragon
- fennel
- 4 egg yolks boiled hard
- salt
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Put 8 anchovies well washed and boned in a mortar with 1/4 lb. of fresh butter
2. Put into boiling water 2 shalots, 1 tooth of garlic, a little tarragon, a little fennel
3. Take them out and dip them in cold water
4. When quite cold put them in the mortar with the rest
5. Add to them the yolks of 4 eggs boiled hard, with salt and a little cayenne
6. Pound it all well and rub it through a sieve
7. Put it over the slice of cold salmon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Soda Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one drachm of soda
- flour
- eggs
- butter
- fruit

## Directions

1. Mix with the common cake ingredients one drachm of soda, which should be rubbed in with the flour
2. This is reckoned a wholesome cake, and half the quantity of eggs are required
3. It may be rendered a fine rich cake by increasing the quantity of eggs, butter, and fruit

title: A Souffle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of cream
- half a pint of new milk
- four ounces of rice-flour or potatoe-flour
- two to three ounces of fresh butter
- white sifted sugar
- grated lemon peel
- yolks of six to eight eggs
- whites of six to eight eggs

## Directions

1. Warm the cream and milk together in a clean saucepan
2. Make a smooth batter with rice-flour or potatoe-flour
3. Stir the batter into the milk
4. Let it simmer, stirring all the time till it thickens
5. Add fresh butter and white sifted sugar enough to sweeten
6. Add grated lemon peel
7. Take it off the fire and stir quickly the well-beaten yolks of six to eight eggs
8. Butter the pan and pour the mixture into it
9. When on the point of being placed into the oven, add the whites of the eggs thoroughly whisked
10. Fill the pan only half full, as it will rise very high
11. Bake in the oven
12. Serve immediately it is taken from the oven, holding a salamander over it to prevent its falling

title: A Souffle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of cream
- half a pint of new milk
- four ounces of rice-flour or potatoe-flour
- two to three ounces of fresh butter
- white sifted sugar
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take half a pint of cream and the same quantity of new milk, and warm them together in a clean saucepan
2. meanwhile make a smooth batter with four ounces of rice-flour or potatoe-flour
3. stir into the milk
4. let it simmer, stirring all the time till it thickens
5. add two to three ounces of fresh butter, and white sifted sugar enough to sweeten, and a little grated lemon peel
6. take it off the fire

title: A Spring Dish
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of young peas
- a little mint
- a few lumps of sugar
- a little salt and white pepper
- one pint of water
- paste for short crust
- a little brown sugar
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Take one quart of young peas, a little mint, a few lumps of sugar, a little salt and white pepper, simmer them gently in one pint of water
2. when the peas are half done, throw in small dumplings made of paste, as if for short crust, and sweetened with a little brown sugar
3. beat up two eggs, and drop in a spoonful at a time, just before serving
4. it will require a deep dish, as the liquor is not to be strained off

title: A Still More Simple Trifle, and Quickly Made
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- ratafia cakes
- wine
- a little brandy
- a thick custard
- white of eggs
- wine and white sugar

## Directions

1. Soak ratafia cakes in wine with a little brandy
2. Pour over a thick custard
3. Cover with a froth of the white of eggs, flavored with wine and sweetened with white sugar

title: A Superior Forcemeat for Risolles, Fritters, and Savory Meat Balls
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of the fat of smoked beef
- a pound of lean veal, free from skin, vein, or sinew
- chopped mushrooms
- a little minced parsley
- salt
- pepper
- grated lemon peel
- the crumb of two French rolls soaked in good gravy
- three beaten eggs
- mushroom powder
- a spoonful of ketchup
- a bay leaf
- an onion
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scrape half a pound of the fat of smoked beef, and a pound of lean veal, free from skin, vein, or sinew
2. Pound it finely in a mortar with chopped mushrooms, a little minced parsley, salt and pepper, and grated lemon peel
3. Have ready the crumb of two French rolls soaked in good gravy, press out the moisture, and add the crumb to the meat with three beaten eggs
4. If the forcemeat is required to be very highly flavored, the gravy in which the rolls are soaked should be seasoned with mushroom powder
5. Add a spoonful of ketchup, a bay leaf, an onion, pepper, salt, and lemon juice to the panada
6. Add this panada to the pounded meat and eggs
7. Form the mixture into any form required, and either fry or warm in gravy, according to the dish for which it is intended

title: A Superior Forcemeat for Risolles, Fritters, and Savory Meat Balls
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat of smoked beef
- lean veal
- chopped mushrooms
- minced parsley
- salt
- pepper
- grated lemon peel
- crumb of two French rolls
- good gravy

## Directions

1. Scrape half a pound of the fat of smoked beef and a pound of lean veal, free from skin, vein, or sinew
2. Pound it finely in a mortar with chopped mushrooms, a little minced parsley, salt and pepper, and grated lemon peel
3. Have ready the crumb of two French rolls soaked in good gravy
4. Press out the moisture and add it

title: A Superior Ointment for Chaps, Roughness, Etc.
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a gill of fresh cream
- a spoonful of beaten almonds
- spirit of camphor

## Directions

1. Mix with a gill of fresh cream a spoonful of beaten almonds
2. When perfectly smooth put it in toilette pots
3. Use as ointment for chaps
4. Add a little spirit of camphor to it if keeping for a week

title: A Superior Receipt for Passover Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit powder
- beaten eggs
- sugar
- pounded cinnamon
- sifted sugar
- finely chopped almonds
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Make a thin batter of biscuit powder and beaten eggs
2. Drop it into a soufle pan, fry lightly, and strew over pounded cinnamon, sifted sugar, and finely chopped almonds
3. Hold over a salamander to brown the upper side
4. Slide the fritter on to a hot dish, and fold
5. Pour over, when in the dish, clarified sugar

title: A Superior Receipt for Stewed Carp
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- strong rich gravy
- onion
- parsley roots
- allspice
- nutmegs
- beaten cloves
- ginger
- port wine
- vinegar
- coarse brown sugar
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean the fish thoroughly
2. Put it into a saucepan, with a strong rich gravy, season with onion, parsley roots, allspice, nutmegs, beaten cloves, and ginger
3. Let it stew very gently till nearly done
4. Mix port wine and vinegar in equal quantities, coarse brown sugar and lemon juice, a little flour, with some of the gravy from the saucepan, mix well
5. Pour over the fish, let it boil till the gravy thickens

title: A Sweet Omelet
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four eggs
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Beat up three or four eggs
2. Pour them into an omelet pan
3. Sprinkle a little white sugar over them while frying
4. Hold a salamander or hot shovel over the uppermost side of the omelet, as it must only be fried on one side
5. As soon as it is set, slide it onto a hot dish
6. Double it
7. Sprinkle sugar over it and serve quickly

title: A Sweet Tart
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of savoy biscuits
- a pint of milk
- 6 oz. of fresh butter
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- 2 oz. of candied orange and lemon peel
- 4 oz. of castor sugar
- the 4 whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Soak 1/2 lb. of savoy biscuits in a pint of milk
2. Beat them up, add 6 oz. of fresh butter, the yolks of 4 eggs, 2 oz. of candied orange and lemon peel, 4 oz. of castor sugar
3. Mix all well up together
4. Butter well a deep dish
5. Bake in a quick oven
6. Beat the 4 whites to a stiff snow
7. Just before the tart is done spread over the snow
8. Return it to the oven for it to colour a light brown
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: A Valuable Receipt For The Skin
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of new milk
- thick slice of stale bread
- tea-spoonful of gum arabic
- spoonful of oil of almonds
- same quantity of honey
- pinch of common salt

## Directions

1. Boil in half a pint of new milk a thick slice of stale bread, and a tea-spoonful of gum arabic
2. When boiled, set it at a little distance from the fire to simmer almost to a jelly
3. Pass it through a folded muslin, and stir in a spoonful of oil of almonds, and the same quantity of honey, with a pinch of common salt
4. When cold it will be a stiff jelly

title: A Venison Pasty
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison
- broth
- seasoning
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- pounded mace
- allspice
- mutton fat
- strong beef gravy
- port wine
- thick puff paste

## Directions

1. Bake or boil venison two hours in broth, with a little seasoning
2. Cut the meat in pieces
3. Season with cayenne pepper, salt, pounded mace, and a little allspice
4. Place it into a deep dish
5. Lay over thin slices of mutton fat
6. Pour a little strong beef gravy flavored with port wine into the dish
7. Cover with a thick puff paste
8. Bake

title: A Very Fine Emmolient Drink
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of pearl barley
- one ounce of sweet almonds
- a piece of lemon peel
- a little syrup of lemon and capillaire

## Directions

1. Wash and rinse extremely well one ounce of pearl barley
2. Put to it one ounce of sweet almonds beaten fine and a piece of lemon peel
3. Boil together till the liquor is of the thickness of cream and perfectly smooth
4. Put in a little syrup of lemon and capillaire

title: A Very Fine Savoury Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlet, cut in pieces and seasoned
- smoked beef fat
- fine cold jelly made from gravy-beef and veal
- hard boiled eggs
- chicken or sweetbread
- lemon-juice
- essence of mushroom
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- blade of mace
- small cakes of fine forcemeat
- fine puff paste
- egg

## Directions

1. Lay a fine veal cutlet, cut in pieces and seasoned, at the bottom of the dish
2. Lay over it a layer of smoked beef fat
3. Then a layer of fine cold jelly made from gravy-beef and veal
4. Then hard boiled eggs in slices
5. Then chicken or sweetbread, and then again the jelly, and so on till the dish is filled
6. Put no water, and season highly with lemon-juice, essence of mushroom, pepper, salt, and nutmeg; also, if approved, a blade of mace
7. Small cakes of fine forcemeat are an improvement
8. Cover with a fine puff paste, and brush over with egg, and bake

title: A Vol-au-Vent of Fruit
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fruits
- sugar

## Directions

1. Fill vol-au-vents with fruits richly stewed with sugar until the syrup is almost a jelly
2. It forms a very pretty entremet

title: A White Fricandeau of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five pounds of breast of veal, or fillet from the shoulder
- veal stuffing
- water
- calf's-foot
- one onion
- blade of mace
- white pepper
- salt
- sprig of parsley
- beaten yolks of four eggs
- juice of two lemons
- button mushrooms or pieces of celery
- egg and forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Stuff the veal with a finely flavoured veal stuffing
2. Put it into a stewpan with water sufficient to cover it
3. Add a calf's-foot cut in pieces if desired
4. Season with one onion, a blade of mace, white pepper and salt, and a sprig of parsley
5. Stew the whole gently until the meat is quite tender
6. Skim and strain the gravy and stir in the beaten yolks of four eggs, and the juice of two lemons previously mixed smoothly with a portion of the gravy
7. Add button mushrooms, or pieces of celery stewed with the veal if desired
8. Garnish the dish with egg and forcemeat balls

title: A Winter Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half peck tart apples
- one quart cranberries
- cold water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cover with cold water and cook an hour.
2. Strain through a jelly-bag without squeezing.
3. There should be about three pints of juice.
4. Use a bowl of sugar for each bowl of juice.
5. When the juice is boiling add sugar which has been heated in oven and boil twenty minutes.
6. Skim and pour into glasses.

title: Abgeruehrter Kugelhopf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half ounce of yeast or one cake compressed yeast
- lukewarm milk
- a pinch of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- a scant cup of butter
- a scant cup of powdered sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- grated peel of a lemon
- two cups of flour
- one cup of lukewarm milk
- one-half cup of cleaned and seeded dark raisins
- citron
- flour
- whites of the eggs

## Directions

1. Soak one-half ounce of yeast or one cake compressed yeast in a very little lukewarm milk; add a pinch of salt and one tablespoon of sugar, stir it up smooth and set back of the stove to rise
2. In the meantime rub a scant cup of butter and a scant cup of powdered sugar to a cream, add gradually the yolks of four eggs, one at a time and add also the grated peel of a lemon
3. Sift two cups of flour into a bowl, make a depression in the centre, pour in the yeast, one cup of lukewarm milk, and make a light batter of this
4. Add the creamed butter and eggs and stir until it forms blisters and leaves the bowl clean
5. Take one-half cup of cleaned and seeded dark raisins and cut up some citron very fine. Dredge flour over them before adding, and if necessary, add more flour to the dough, which should be of the consistency of cup cake batter
6. Last add the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
7. Place in a well-greased long or round pan with tube in centre; let rise until double in bulk, and bake in moderate oven until browned and thoroughly done

title: Alamode Beef, or Sour Meat
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of the ribs of beef boned and filletted, or a piece of the round
- vinegar diluted with water
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- whole allspice
- three or four bay leaves
- a cup full of raspings

## Directions

1. Cover a piece of the ribs of beef boned and filletted, or a piece of the round with vinegar diluted with water
2. season with onions, pepper, salt, whole allspice, and three or four bay leaves
3. add a cup full of raspings
4. let the whole stew gently for three or four hours, according to the weight of the meat

title: Almond Balls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-eighth pound of almonds
- yolk of one egg
- pinch of salt
- grated rind of lemon
- white of egg
- boiling fat

## Directions

1. One-eighth pound of almonds chopped fine.
2. Yolk of one egg, well beaten.
3. Add almonds to egg, pinch of salt, little grated rind of lemon.
4. Beat white of egg stiff, then mix all together.
5. Drop a little from end of teaspoon into boiling fat.
6. Put in soup just before serving.

title: Almond Blancmange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. of isinglass
- a little water
- 1/2 pint almond milk
- vanille or orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 oz. of isinglass in a little water
2. Add 1/2 pint of almond milk sweetened to taste and flavoured with vanille or orange-flower water, or whatever may be preferred
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Almond Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. of ground almonds
- 1/2 lb. sifted sugar
- 6 oz. flour
- 7 eggs
- raspings of 2 lemons
- oil of sweet almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put the almonds in a pan, with the sugar and yolks of the eggs
2. Mix it all together till it comes like a cream
3. Beat the whites to a strong snow
4. Mix it with the other
5. Put in the flour as light as you can
6. Grease your tin with the oil of sweet almonds
7. Sprinkle a little sugar on the tin
8. Bake it in rather a slow oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Almond Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds
- one pound of sugar
- one or two eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pound almonds
2. Mix with one pound of sugar, one or two eggs and enough cinnamon to give a strong flavor
3. Bake in a shallow pan and cut into small sections

title: Almond Cake or Mandel Torte, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of almonds
- yolks of eight eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one-half lemon
- one tablespoon of brandy
- four lady-fingers
- whites of eight eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch almonds by pouring boiling water over them
2. Pound in a mortar or grate on grater
3. Beat yolks of eight eggs vigorously with one cup of sugar
4. Add grated peel and juice of one-half lemon
5. Add one tablespoon of brandy
6. Add four lady-fingers grated
7. Add the almonds
8. Fold in the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs
9. Bake in moderate oven one hour

title: Almond Cake or Mandel Torte, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-fourth pound of sweet almonds
- one-eighth pound of bitter almonds
- yolks of nine eggs
- eight tablespoons of granulated sugar
- grated peel of one lemon
- one-half teaspoon of mace or vanilla
- juice of one lemon
- whites of nine eggs
- four stale lady fingers

## Directions

1. Blanch almonds the day previous to using
2. Grate or pound them as fine as powder
3. Beat until light the yolks of nine eggs with eight tablespoons of granulated sugar
4. Add the grated peel of one lemon and one-half teaspoon of mace or vanilla
5. Beat long and steadily
6. Add the grated almonds and continue the stirring in one direction
7. Add the juice of the lemon to the stiff-beaten whites
8. Grate four stale lady fingers and add
9. Bake slowly for one hour at least

title: Almond Hills
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound of sweet almonds
- heated sugar
- one-half pound of sifted powdered sugar
- whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Roast one-quarter pound of sweet almonds, cut into strips lengthwise in a spider of heated sugar, not too brown
2. Beat one-half pound of sifted powdered sugar and the whites of five eggs to a very stiff froth
3. Mix all thoroughly and place teaspoonfuls of this mixture on waxed paper, and bake a light brown, in slow oven

title: Almond Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of two eggs
- one-half pound of sweet almonds, blanched and dried
- one-half pound of confectioner's sugar
- a few drops of rosewater
- plain icing
- flour

## Directions

1. Take the whites of two eggs and one-half pound of sweet almonds, which should be blanched, dried and grated or pounded to a paste
2. Beat the whites of the eggs, add half a pound of confectioner's sugar, one tablespoon at a time, until all is used
3. Add the almonds and a few drops of rosewater
4. Spread between or on top of cake
5. Put on thick, and when nearly dry cover with a plain icing
6. If the cakes are well dredged with a little flour after baking, and then carefully wiped before the icing is put on, it will not run and can be spread more smoothly
7. Put the frosting in the centre of the cake, dip a knife in cold water and spread from the centre toward the edge

title: Almond Macaroons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds ground fine
- one and one-half pounds of powdered sugar
- whites of five eggs
- grated rind of two lemons
- olive oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs to a snow
2. Add the sugar and the grated lemon rind and almonds
3. Mix it well together
4. Grease a very thin paper with olive oil
5. Sprinkle some powdered sugar over it
6. Place on a tin
7. Form the cakes and place them a little distance from each other
8. Bake in a very moderate oven
9. When done let them cool before you touch them

title: Almond Macaroons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds
- one and one-half pounds of powdered sugar
- whites of five eggs
- grated rind of two lemons
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Grind almonds fine
2. Beat the whites of eggs to a snow
3. Add the sugar and the grated lemon rind and almonds
4. Mix it well together
5. Grease a very thin paper with olive oil, sprinkle some powdered sugar over it, place on a tin
6. Form the cakes and place them a little distance from each other
7. Bake in a very moderate oven
8. When done let them cool before you touch them

title: Almond Macaroons with Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- one-half pound of sugar
- one-half pound of finely cut figs
- one-half pound of blanched almonds or walnuts
- ironing-wax
- waxed paper

## Directions

1. Beat stiff the whites of three eggs
2. Add one-half pound of sugar
3. Add one-half pound of finely cut figs
4. Add one-half pound of either blanched almonds cut into long slices, or cut up walnuts
5. Heat a large pan
6. Pass ironing-wax over surface
7. Lay in waxed paper
8. Drop spoonfuls of mixture on paper, same distance apart
9. Bake very slowly in very moderate oven
10. Remove and let cool
11. Take paper out with the macaroons
12. Turn over and place hot cloths on wrong side
13. When cakes will drop off

title: Almond Macaroons, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of almonds
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- whites of four eggs
- water
- butter or waxed paper

## Directions

1. Blanch half a pound of almonds
2. Pound in mortar to a smooth paste
3. Add one pound of pulverized sugar and the beaten whites of four eggs
4. Work the paste well together with the back of a spoon
5. Dip your hands in water and roll the mixture into balls the size of a hickory nut
6. Lay on buttered or waxed paper an inch apart
7. When done, dip your hands in water and pass gently over the macaroons, making the surface smooth and shiny
8. Set in a cool oven three-quarters of an hour

title: Almond Macaroons, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of almonds
- whites of five eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of pulverized sugar
- confectioner's sugar
- a tablespoon of rosewater or a teaspoon of essence of bitter almonds
- butter or waxed paper
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the almonds by blanching them in boiling water
2. Strip them of the skins and lay them on a clean towel to dry
3. Grate or pound one-half pound of almonds
4. Beat the whites of five eggs to a stiff, very stiff froth
5. Stir in gradually three-quarters of a pound of pulverized sugar (use confectioner's sugar if you can get it)
6. Add the pounded almonds
7. Add a tablespoon of rosewater or a teaspoon of essence of bitter almonds
8. Line a broad baking-pan with buttered or waxed paper
9. Drop upon this half a teaspoon of the mixture at a time, allowing room enough to prevent their running together
10. Sift powdered sugar over them
11. Bake in a quick oven to a delicate brown
12. Try one on a piece of paper before you venture to bake them all
13. If it runs add a little more sugar

title: Almond Milk
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of good Valentia almonds sweet
- hot water
- cold water
- 1 pint of cold water

## Directions

1. Take 1 lb. of good Valentia almonds sweet
2. Lay them in hot water to get off the skins
3. As you peel them out of the hot water throw them into a basin with cold water
4. Have your marble mortar very clean, pound them by degrees
5. Have a very fine sieve, have a pint of cold water in a mug
6. As you put the pounded almonds in the sieve, throw a little cold water over them till you have used up the pint for the pound
7. Turn the milk with the almonds into a very clean saucepan, boil it up
8. Strain it again into a basin for use
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Almond Paste
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fine almonds
- water
- half a pound of white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch half a pound of fine almonds and pound them to a paste
2. Add a few drops of water from time to time, or they become oily
3. Mix thoroughly with it half a pound of white sifted sugar
4. Put it into a preserving pan and let them simmer very gently until they become dry enough not to stick to a clean spoon when touched
5. Stir constantly

title: Almond Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half cup of grated walnuts or almonds
- one-half cup of grated white bread crumbs
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of four eggs very light with one-half cup of sugar
2. Add one-half cup of grated walnuts or almonds, one-half cup of grated white bread crumbs, then the stiffly-beaten whites of four eggs
3. Put in pudding form and steam from one and one-half to two hours
4. Serve with wine or fruit sauce

title: Almond Pudding, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of seven eggs
- yolks of ten eggs
- one-half pound of pulverized sweet almonds
- one-half ounce of pounded bitter almonds
- one-half pound of powdered sugar
- one tablespoon of orange-flower water
- olive oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well with the orange water
2. Add the sugar and almonds gradually
3. Beat all for one hour or until it bubbles
4. Grease deep pie-dishes with olive oil and pour in the mixture
5. Bake in a rather moderate oven
6. When the mixture is set and browned place over them a paper greased with olive oil to prevent them getting dark
7. Serve cold
8. Sprinkle powdered sugar freely over the pudding before serving

title: Almond Pudding, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound blanched almonds pounded
- eight eggs
- cinnamon
- lemon rind
- one and one-half cups of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs for twenty minutes
2. Add one and one-half cups of sugar gently
3. Add the almonds
4. Mix all together thoroughly
5. Bake in shallow pans and serve cold

title: Almond Rice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of whole rice
- milk
- sugar to taste
- a few pounded sweet almonds
- a few slices of citron
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of whole rice in milk until soft
2. Beat it through a sieve and set it on the fire with sugar according to taste, a few pounded sweet almonds and a few slices of citron
3. When it has simmered a short time, let it cool and place it in a mould
4. When sufficiently firm turn it out, stick it with blanched almonds, and pour over a fine custard

title: Almond Sticks
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of almonds
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one egg yolk
- one cup of butter
- three-fourths cup of sugar
- two whole eggs
- two cups of flour

## Directions

1. Grind two cups of almonds and reserve one-quarter cup each of sugar and nuts, and an egg yolk for decorating
2. Cream one cup of butter, add three-fourths cup of sugar, then two whole eggs, almonds and two cups of flour
3. Roll thin and cut in strips or squares, with fluted cookie cutter
4. Brush with yolk, sprinkle with nuts and sugar, set aside, and bake in medium oven

title: Almond Sticks—Fleischig
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half glass of fat
- two eggs
- four cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one cup of water
- one-half cup of sugar
- Two cups of sugar
- two teaspoons of cinnamon
- one-half pound of grated almonds
- one-half pound of small raisins

## Directions

1. Take one-half glass of fat, two eggs, four cups of flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder, one cup of water, one-half cup of sugar
2. Knead lightly
3. Roll out not too thin
4. Mix Two cups of sugar with two teaspoons of cinnamon
5. Reserve one-half of the sugar and cinnamon, the nuts and raisins
6. Brush the dough with melted fat and sprinkle with almonds and sugar
7. Put a little of the almond and raisin mixture around the edge and roll around twice
8. Cut in small pieces
9. Brush every piece with fat
10. Roll in the sugar and cinnamon

title: Almond Tea-Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half-a-pound of flour
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- half a pound of sugar
- a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds
- a couple of eggs
- ground almonds
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Put aside three ounces of flour for rolling out the cakes
2. Set the other five ounces with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter before the fire for a few minutes
3. Mix with it half a pound of sugar, a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds chopped fine, and a couple of eggs
4. Make these ingredients into thin cakes
5. Strew over them ground almonds and white sugar
6. Bake in a brisk oven

title: Almondegos Soup: A Superior White Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- a calf's foot
- two quarts of water
- a blade of mace
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- a turnip
- white pepper
- salt
- balls of forced meat
- egg balls
- yolks of four eggs
- a desert spoonful of lemon juice
- three ounces of sweet almonds
- a spoonful of powdered white sugar

## Directions

1. Put a knuckle of veal and a calf's foot into two quarts of water, with a blade of mace and a bunch of sweet herbs, a turnip, a little white pepper, and salt
2. When sufficiently done, strain and skim it
3. Add balls of forced meat, and egg balls
4. A quarter of an hour before serving beat up the yolks of four eggs with a desert spoonful of lemon juice, and three ounces of sweet almonds blanched and beaten with a spoonful of powdered white sugar
5. This mixture is to be stirred into the soup till it thickens, taking care to prevent its curdling

title: Amastich
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- stock
- chicken
- lemon
- egg

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of rice in a quart of stock for half an hour, stirring frequently.
2. Then add a chicken stuffed and trussed as for roasting; cover closely and cook thoroughly.
3. After removing the chicken, pass the liquor through a strainer, add the juice of a lemon and the beaten yolk of an egg, and pour over the bird.

title: Amber Marmalade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one grapefruit
- one orange
- one lemon
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Slice thin, skin and all, one grapefruit, one orange, one lemon.
2. Add to this three times its measure of water and allow to stand overnight.
3. Cook for ten minutes the next morning and then allow to stand until the next morning.
4. Finish by adding as much sugar as there is liquid and boiling slowly until done, or until it jellies. The time commonly given is two hours, but a half hour less than this is ample.

title: Ambrosia
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced orange
- cocoanut
- powdered sugar
- maraschino cherry

## Directions

1. Fill glass with alternate layers of sliced orange and cocoanut
2. cover with powdered sugar and place a maraschino cherry on the top of each

title: Ambrosia
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- different kinds of fruit
- nuts
- marshmallows
- sugar
- sherry
- whipped cream
- cherry

## Directions

1. Cut up into small pieces different kinds of fruit.
2. Chop up nuts and marshmallows (not too fine).
3. Mix these and sugar, not allowing it to draw too much juice.
4. Flavor with sherry, if you like.
5. Serve individually, putting whipped cream on the top with a cherry.

title: An Anchovy Relish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- eggs
- parsley
- garlic
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash well a dozen anchovies
2. halve them
3. have boiled 3 eggs hard
4. put them in cold water for 5 minutes
5. peel them
6. chop some parsley
7. take a large china plate
8. chop a tooth of garlic very fine
9. lay it in the plate with 3 spoonfuls of oil, 2 of vinegar
10. pepper it lightly
11. place the halves of the anchovies in strips round the plate (it forms like a star)
12. cut the eggs in round slices very thin with a sharp knife
13. place them in the rows round the plate
14. place the chopped parsley over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: An Apricot Anlauf
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 or 14 ripe apricots
- apricot kernels
- 1/2 pint of milk
- flour
- 2 oz. of butter
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mash 12 or 14 ripe apricots through a sieve
2. Pound the kernels quite fine
3. Make a thick paste with 1/2 pint of milk and as much flour as it will take up
4. When cold put it in a basin
5. Dissolve in the milk 2 oz. of butter
6. Stir the apricots up with this paste
7. Mix with yolks of 6 eggs and 1 lb. of powdered sugar
8. Work this in with the paste and apricots
9. Beat the whites to a snow and stir in lightly
10. Grease the shape and put it in
11. Place in a middling oven
12. When it rises and is a good colour it is done
13. About 1/2 of an hour before you send it up sprinkle sugar over it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: An Easy Pot Roast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of brisket
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- three tablespoons of tomatoes
- an onion
- water

## Directions

1. Take four pounds of brisket, season with salt, pepper and ginger.
2. Add three tablespoons of tomatoes and an onion cut up.
3. Cover with water in an iron pot and a close-fitting cover.
4. Put in oven and bake from three to four hours.

title: An Easy Pudding in Compliment to the Little Cooks who will Study this Book
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- dried cherries or candied peel
- thin slices of bread and butter
- raspberry or strawberry jam
