- 1 pint of milk flavoured with lemon or orange-flower water
- 3 eggs
- ingredients for a good wine sauce

## Directions

1. Grease a mould well with butter
2. Arrange inside some dried cherries or candied peel with taste
3. Cut some thin slices of bread and butter
4. Spread on them some raspberry or strawberry jam
5. Put them in the mould one over the other
6. Make a nice custard with 1 pint of milk flavoured with lemon or orange-flower water
7. Beat up 3 eggs
8. Mix it altogether
9. Put it in the mould
10. Boil it for one hour
11. Turn it out
12. Make a good wine sauce
13. Turn it over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: An Easy Receipt for a Charlotte Russe
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about six ounces of savoy biscuits
- three ounces of ratafias
- six ounces of sugar
- grated rind of half an orange
- grated rind of a lemon
- a small piece of cinnamon
- a wine-glass full of good maraschino
- a wine-glass full of fine noyeau
- one pint of cream
- well beaten yolks of six eggs
- half a pint more cream
- one ounce of dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Trim straitly about six ounces of savoy biscuits, so that they may fit closely to each other
2. Line the bottom and sides of a plain mould with them
3. Put into a stewpan three ounces of ratafias, six of sugar, the grated rind of half an orange, the same quantity of the rind of a lemon, a small piece of cinnamon, a wine-glass full of good maraschino, or fine noyeau, one pint of cream, and the well beaten yolks of six eggs
4. Stir this mixture for a few minutes over a stove fire
5. Strain it
6. Add half a pint more cream, whipped, and one ounce of dissolved isinglass
7. Mix the whole well together
8. Set it in a basin imbedded in rough ice

title: An Easy Trifle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three sponge cakes
- half a pound of macaroons and ratafias
- one wine glass of brandy
- three wine glasses of white wine
- nearly a pint of thick rich custard made of equal portions of milk and cream with seven eggs
- jam and sweetmeats
- cream
- whites of two eggs
- wine and sugar

## Directions

1. Soak three sponge cakes and half a pound of macaroons and ratafias in one wine glass of brandy and three of white wine
2. Lay them at the bottom of the trifle dish
3. Spread jam and sweetmeats over the cakes
4. Pour over nearly a pint of thick rich custard
5. Prepare a fine light froth with cream and the whites of two eggs, flavored with wine and sugar
6. Heap it over the trifle lightly

title: An Easy Way to Cook Tripe
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- flour
- water
- salt
- pepper
- 1 onion, sliced
- fat
- nutmeg, grated
- 1/2 pint water
- parsley, chopped
- capers
- 1 spoonful flour
- dried saffron
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the tripe well
2. Boil it in a saucepan in thin flour and water to keep its colour, with salt and pepper
3. When done, drain it out of the water
4. Slice an onion in a stew pan with a little fat, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
5. Add 1/2 pint of the water in which the tripe was boiled
6. Let it cook for half an hour
7. In a basin, combine chopped parsley, a few capers, a spoonful of flour, a little dried saffron, and 2 spoonfuls of vinegar
8. Mix it all well together
9. Dish it up and serve with mashed potatoes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: An Emollient Paste
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sweet almonds
- two ounces of bitter almonds
- rose water

## Directions

1. Blanch half a pound of sweet almonds, and two ounces of bitter almonds
2. Pound them in a mortar
3. Make them into a paste with rose water

title: An Excellent Carrot Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. chopped suet
- 1/2 lb. grated carrot
- 1/2 lb. of raisins stoned
- 1/2 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- 2 eggs
- yolks of 4 more eggs
- gill of ale
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix in a basin 1/2 lb. flour, 1/2 lb. chopped suet, 1/2 lb. grated carrot, 1/2 lb. of raisins stoned, 1/2 lb. of currants well washed, 1 lb. of sifted sugar
2. Beat up 2 eggs, the yolks of 4 more, with a gill of ale, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Mix it all well together
4. Put it in a well-greased mould
5. Bake or boil it for 1 hour
6. Serve with wine or brandy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: An Excellent Receipt for Lip Salve
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of spermacetti
- oil of almonds
- two or three grains of powdered cochineal

## Directions

1. Melt one ounce of spermacetti
2. Soften sufficiently with oil of almonds
3. Color it with two or three grains of powdered cochineal
4. Pour while warm into small toilet pots

title: An Excellent Receipt for Stewed Fish in the Dutch Fashion
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots (3 or 4)
- onions (several)
- water (1 pint)
- lemon juice
- vinegar
- saffron
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- fish
- flour
- butter
- egg (1 yolk)

## Directions

1. Take three or four parsley roots, cut them into pieces
2. Slice several onions and boil in a pint of water till tender
3. Season with lemon juice, vinegar, saffron, pepper, salt, and mace
4. Add the fish, and let it stew till nearly finished
5. Remove it, and thicken the gravy with a little flour and butter, and the yolk of one egg
6. Return the fish to the stew-pan, with balls made as directed in the preceding receipt, and boil up

title: An Excellent Receipt for Stewing a Rump of Beef
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large onion
- four bay leaves
- a little parsley
- half an ounce of ground ginger
- a tea-spoonful of salt
- a blade of mace
- a little ground allspice
- some lemon sliced
- some lemon peel grated
- three parts of a cup of vinegar
- a calf's-foot cut in small pieces
- a pint of water

## Directions

1. Chop fine a large onion, four bay leaves, and a little parsley
2. add to these half an ounce of ground ginger, a tea-spoonful of salt, a blade of mace, a little ground allspice, some lemon sliced, and some of the peel grated
3. rub all these ingredients well into the meat
4. place it into a stew-pan with three parts of a cup of vinegar, a calf's-foot cut in small pieces and a pint of water
5. stew gently till tender
6. when the fat must be carefully skimmed off the gravy, which must be strained and poured over the meat

title: An Old Roman Receipt For Improving The Skin
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- desert spoonful of the best wheaten flour
- half a pint of fresh asses milk
- table-spoonful of the best honey
- tea-spoonful of rose water

## Directions

1. Boil a desert spoonful of the best wheaten flour with half a pint of fresh asses milk
2. When boiling, stir in a table-spoonful of the best honey, and a tea-spoonful of rose water
3. Mix smoothly, place in small pots, and use a little of it after washing

title: An Old Way of Pickling Cucumbers
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- cayenne pepper
- whole ginger
- whole pepper
- mustard seed
- West India pickles

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers in small pieces, length ways, with the peel left on
2. Lay them in salt for twenty-four hours
3. Dry the pieces with a cloth
4. Lay them in a deep dish
5. Pour over the following mixture: some vinegar boiled with cayenne pepper, whole ginger, a little whole pepper, and mustard seed
6. A few West India pickles may be added by some as an improvement
7. Let this mixture stand till nearly cold before covering the cucumbers
8. Bottle the cucumbers

title: Anchovy Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- anchovy paste
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the bread as for caviar canapes
2. Spread with anchovy paste
3. Chop separately the yolks and whites of hard-boiled eggs
4. Cover the canapes, dividing them into quarters, with anchovies split in two lengthwise
5. Use yolks and whites in alternate quarters

title: Anchovy Canapes With Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slice toast
- anchovy paste
- egg
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. For each person take a thin slice toast covered with anchovy paste
2. Upon this place whole egg which has been boiled four minutes, so that it can be pealed whole and the yolk is still soft
3. Around the toast put tomato sauce

title: Anchovy Paste
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- fresh eggs
- anchovies

## Directions

1. take 2 oz. of fresh butter
2. the yolks of 2 fresh eggs boiled hard rubbed with the butter very smoothly
3. take 6 anchovies well cleaned and boned
4. pound them well with the eggs and butter in a marble mortar
5. when very smooth put it into small pots
6. run over them a little plain butter warmed to oil
7. this will keep for a week
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Anchovy Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- olives
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Pound the anchovies to a paste and mix with an equal quantity of olives stoned and finely chopped

title: Anchovy Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six tablespoons melted butter
- one and one-half teaspoons anchovy paste
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix six tablespoons of melted butter and one and one-half teaspoons anchovy paste, place in double boiler and allow to boil for about six minutes.
2. Flavor with lemon juice.

title: Angel Food
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of pastry flour
- salt
- whites of eight or nine eggs (or just one cup of whites)
- one-half teaspoon of cream of tartar
- one and one-fourth cups of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla or almond extract

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of pastry flour once, then measure and sift three times
2. Add a pinch of salt to the whites of eight or nine eggs or just one cup of whites
3. Beat about one-half, add one-half teaspoon of cream of tartar, then beat the whites until they will stand of their own weight
4. Add one and one-fourth cups of sugar, then flour, not by stirring but folding over and over until thoroughly mixed in
5. Flavor with one-half teaspoon of vanilla or almond extract
6. Bake in an ungreased pan, patent tube pan preferred
7. Place the cake in an oven that will just warm it enough through until the batter has raised to the top of the mold, then increase the heat gradually until the cake is well browned over
8. If by pressing the top of the cake with the finger it will spring back without leaving the imprint of the finger the cake is done through
9. Bake thirty-five to forty minutes
10. When done, invert the pan
11. When cool remove from pan

title: Anise Seed Cookies (Springele)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one and one-half cups of granulated sugar
- two heaping cups of flour
- fourteen drops of anise seed oil

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs thoroughly.
2. Add one and one-half cups of granulated sugar, and beat for thirty minutes.
3. Add two heaping cups of flour and fourteen drops of anise seed oil.
4. Drop from a teaspoon on well-buttered pans.
5. Let them stand from two to three hours in the pans before baking.
6. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Annastich
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of rice
- one quart of strong gravy
- one onion
- cloves
- sweet herbs
- one chicken
- forcemeat
- yolks of four eggs
- juice of a lemon
- saffron

## Directions

1. Stew gently one pint of rice in one quart of strong gravy till it begins to swell
2. Add an onion stuck with cloves and a bunch of sweet herbs
3. Add a chicken stuffed with forcemeat
4. Let it stew with the rice till thoroughly done
5. Take it up and stir in the rice, the yolks of four eggs, and the juice of a lemon
6. Color the rice to a fine yellow with saffron
7. Serve the fowl in the same dish with the rice

title: Another Excellent Receipt
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some mushrooms, young and fresh
- salt
- a little gravy
- the trimmings of the fowl, or of veal
- a blade of mace
- a little grated lemon peel
- the juice of one lemon
- flour
- a table-spoonful of white wine

## Directions

1. Chop some mushrooms, young and fresh, salt them
2. Put them into a saucepan with a little gravy, made of the trimmings of the fowl, or of veal
3. Add a blade of mace, a little grated lemon peel, the juice of one lemon
4. Thicken with flour
5. When ready to serve, stir in a table-spoonful of white wine

title: Another Method of Frying Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces flour
- one ounce olive oil
- egg yolk
- salt
- one gill tepid water
- egg white
- bread crumbs
- boiling oil

## Directions

1. Thoroughly mix six ounces of flour with an ounce of olive oil, the yolk of an egg, and a pinch of salt
2. Stir in one gill of tepid water and allow the whole to stand for half an hour in a cool place
3. Beat the white of an egg stiff and stir into the batter
4. Dip each fish into the mixture, then roll in bread crumbs
5. Cook in boiling oil
6. Do not allow the fish to remain in the spider after it has been nicely browned
7. Be sure that the fish is done

title: Another Sort
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pint of water flavoured with a little salt
- four beaten eggs
- half a pound of matso flour
- a couple of lumps of white sugar
- half a tea-cup of milk

## Directions

1. Warm a quarter of a pint of water flavoured with a little salt
2. In which mix four beaten eggs
3. Then mix half a pound of matso flour, and a couple of lumps of white sugar, and half a tea-cup of milk
4. Mix all well together
5. Bake in a tin

title: Another Sort
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pint of water
- salt
- four eggs
- half a pound of matso flour
- a couple of lumps of white sugar
- half a tea-cup of milk

## Directions

1. Warm a quarter of a pint of water flavoured with a little salt
2. Mix in four beaten eggs
3. Mix half a pound of matso flour and a couple of lumps of white sugar and half a tea-cup of milk
4. Mix all well together
5. Bake in a tin

title: Apple and Honey Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four cups of raw apples cut in small pieces
- two cups of bread crumbs
- one-half cup of hot water
- two teaspoons of butter
- two teaspoons of cinnamon
- one-half cup of honey
- cream

## Directions

1. Put a layer of the apple in a well-buttered pudding dish
2. Then a layer of crumbs
3. Mix the honey and hot water
4. Pour part of this over the crumbs
5. Sprinkle with cinnamon and dot with a few bits of butter
6. Fill the dish with alternate layers of apples, crumbs, honey, etc., having a layer of crumbs on top
7. Cover and bake forty-five minutes
8. Serve with cream

title: Apple and Lady-Finger Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- almonds
- citron
- raisins
- butter
- sugar
- wine
- four yolks of eggs
- one cup of sugar
- beaten whites of eggs
- eight lady fingers
- juice of one whole lemon

## Directions

1. Core and peel apples, take top off, chop the top with almonds, citron and raisins.
2. Butter your pan, fill apples, sugar them and pour over a little wine, bake until tender.
3. When cool add four yolks of eggs beaten with one cup of sugar, then last, add beaten whites and eight lady fingers rolled, and juice of one whole lemon.
4. Pour over apples, bake.
5. Eat cold.

title: Apple And Quince Conserve
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one peck of juicy cooking apples
- two quarts of sugar
- quinces
- water

## Directions

1. Pare the quinces and cut out the cores.
2. Put the parings and cores into a preserving kettle with two quarts of water and boil gently for forty-five minutes.
3. Meanwhile, cut the quinces into eighths, put them into a kettle with three pints of water and simmer until the fruit can be pierced with a straw.
4. Lift the fruit from the water and lay them on a platter to drain.
5. Strain the water in which the parings and cores have cooked into the water in which the quinces have cooked, and after adding the sugar boil for ten minutes.
6. Pare, core and quarter the apples, and place in the syrup with the cooked quinces.
7. Cook slowly for fifteen minutes and seal immediately in sterilized jars.

title: Apple Butter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet cider
- wine apples
- stick cinnamon
- cloves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil down any desired quantity of sweet cider in your preserving kettle to two-thirds the original quantity.
2. Pare, core and slice as many wine apples as you wish to use.
3. Boil slowly, stirring often with a silver or wooden spoon.
4. Spice with stick cinnamon and cloves, and sweeten to taste.
5. Boil from four to five hours.
6. Take from the fire, pour all together into a large crock.
7. Cover and let it stand overnight.
8. Then return it to the preserving kettle and boil down, stirring all the while until it is the consistency of mush, and of a dark brown color.

title: Apple Cake (Kuchen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- dough
- five large apples
- melted butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grease the pan with butter
2. Roll out a piece of dough quite thin, lay it in the pan, press a rim out of the dough all around the pan and let it rise for about ten minutes
3. Pare five large apples, core and quarter them, dipping each piece in melted butter before laying on the cake
4. Sprinkle bountifully with sugar (brown being preferable to white for this purpose) and cinnamon
5. Use tart apples
6. Leave the cake in the pans and cut out the pieces just as you would want to serve them
7. If they stick to the pan, set the pan on top of the hot stove for a minute and the cake will then come out

title: Apple Charlotte
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- oval cake tin
- savoy cakes
- whites of eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the apples as in the last receipt
2. put the apples into an oval cake tin about the size of a small side dish, four or five inches high
3. when cold, turn it out
4. cover the apple-shape with savoy cakes placed closely together perpendicularly
5. all round the top of the charlotte should be covered with whites of eggs and sugar, beaten to a stiff froth, and placed in small balls
6. a salamander should be used to crisp them and to give a slight peach-like colour
7. a tasteful cook will, after crisping the first layer of these balls, add others over them to form a sort of cone high in the centre

title: Apple Compote
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six apples
- water
- white wine
- white sugar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take six apples (Greenings, Baldwins or Bellflowers)
2. Pare, quarter, core and lay them in cold water as soon as pared
3. Take the parings and seeds
4. Put in a dish with a cup of water and a cup of white wine
5. Boil for about fifteen minutes
6. Strain through a fine sieve
7. Put on to boil again
8. Add half a cup of white sugar and the peel of half a lemon
9. Put in the apples
10. Let them stew for fifteen minutes longer
11. When the apples are tender, take up each piece carefully with a silver spoon and lay on a platter to cool
12. Let the syrup boil down to about half the quantity you had after removing the apples
13. Add to it the juice of half a lemon
14. Lay your apples in a fruit dish, pyramid shape
15. Pour the syrup over them
16. Serve

title: Apple Custard Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- apples
- four eggs
- two cups of milk

## Directions

1. Line your pie-plates with a rich crust
2. Slice apples thin, half fill your plates
3. Pour over them a custard made of four eggs and two cups of milk, sweetened and seasoned to taste

title: Apple Delight
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apple sauce
- butter
- chopped peaches and apricots
- blanched almonds
- peach juice

## Directions

1. Put a layer of apple sauce in a buttered pudding dish
2. Dot with butter
3. Add a layer of chopped peaches and apricots
4. Sprinkle with blanched almonds ground rather coarsely
5. Repeat until the pan is full
6. Pour the peach juice over the mixture
7. Bake for one hour

title: Apple Fladen (Hungarian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of sweet butter
- one pound flour (four cups sifted)
- four tablespoons of powdered sugar
- salt
- four egg yolks
- one-half cup of sour cream
- two pounds of sour apples
- one-half cup of light-colored raisins
- sugar and cinnamon
- four tablespoons of bread crumbs
- white of one egg
- two tablespoons of powdered almonds

## Directions

1. Rub together butter and flour on a pastry-board
2. Add powdered sugar, salt, egg yolks and moisten with sour cream
3. Cover and set aside in the ice-box for one-half hour
4. Peel sour apples and cut fine
5. Mix with light-colored raisins, sugar and cinnamon to taste
6. Cut the dough in two pieces
7. Roll out one piece and place on greased baking pan
8. Spread over this bread crumbs and the chopped sugared apples
9. Roll out the other half of dough and place on top
10. Spread with white of one egg
11. Sprinkle with powdered almonds
12. Bake in hot oven

title: Apple Float
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six big apples
- water
- lemon
- nutmeg
- eggs
- whipped or plain cream

## Directions

1. Peel six big apples and slice them
2. Put them in a saucepan with just enough water to cover them
3. Cook until tender
4. Put them through a colander
5. Add the grated rind and juice of half a lemon
6. Sweeten to taste
7. Stir in a trace of nutmeg
8. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites of four eggs
9. Put the dish on ice
10. Serve with whipped or plain cream

title: Apple Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sieved meal
- eggs
- good sized apples
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a batter of some sieved meal and eggs
2. Peel some good sized apples
3. Cut them in nice slices
4. Take out the centre where the core is
5. Dip the slices in the batter both sides
6. Fry them
7. Arrange them prettily in a dish
8. Sprinkle over some cinnamon and sugar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Apple Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four sour apples
- fritter batter
- fat or oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Choose four sour apples
2. Pare, core and cut them into small slices
3. Stir into fritter batter
4. Fry in boiling hot fat or oil
5. Drain on paper
6. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve

title: Apple Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sour, juicy apples
- water
- white wine
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take sour, juicy apples, not too ripe, cut up in pieces, leave the skins on and boil the seeds also.
2. Put on enough water to just cover.
3. Boil on the back of the stove, closely covered, all day.
4. Then put in jelly-bag of double cheese-cloth to drip all night.
5. Next morning measure the juice.
6. Allow a wine-glass of white wine and juice of one lemon to every three pints of juice.
7. Boil a pint at a time, with a pound of sugar to every pint.

title: Apple Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine baking apples
- water
- loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and cut small any kind of fine baking apples—say six pounds in weight.
2. Put them in a preserving pan with one quart of water.
3. Boil gently till the apples are very soft and broken.
4. Pass the juice through a jelly bag.
5. To each pint, add half a pound of loaf sugar.
6. Set it on the fire to boil twenty minutes, skimming it as the scum rises.
7. It must not be over boiled, or the colour will be too dark.

title: Apple Jelly Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- four eggs
- one cup of milk
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- three and one-half cups of flour

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter and two cups of sugar to a cream
2. add four eggs, whites beaten separately
3. add one cup of milk
4. add two teaspoons of baking-powder and three and one-half cups of flour
5. Bake in layer tins

title: Apple Jelly Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of butter
- Two cups of sugar
- Four eggs, whites beaten separately
- One cup of milk
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- Three and one-half cups of flour
- Three large apples (Greenings preferred)
- The juice and peel of a lemon
- One cup of sugar
- One well-beaten egg

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter and two cups of sugar to a cream
2. Add four eggs, whites beaten separately
3. Add one cup of milk
4. Add two teaspoons of baking-powder and three and one-half cups of flour
5. Bake in layer tins
6. For filling: Pare and grate three large apples (Greenings preferred)
7. Add the juice and peel of a lemon, one cup of sugar and one well-beaten egg
8. Put in ingredients together and boil, stirring constantly until thick
9. Cool and fill in cake

title: Apple Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a loaf of bread
- water
- one cup of suet
- tart apples
- sugar
- raisins
- cinnamon
- one-quarter cup of pounded almonds
- yolks of three eggs
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Soak half a loaf of bread in water and squeeze dry
2. Shave one cup of suet very fine and cut up some tart apples in thin slices
3. Add sugar, raisins, cinnamon, about one-quarter cup of pounded almonds and the yolks of three eggs
4. Mix all thoroughly
5. Add whites beaten to a stiff froth last
6. Bake one hour

title: Apple Pie, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four apples
- plain pastry
- bread crumbs
- one-half cup of sugar
- cinnamon, nutmeg or lemon juice or two tablespoons of water

## Directions

1. Pare, core and slice four apples
2. Line a pie-plate with plain pastry
3. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
4. Lay in the apples
5. Sprinkle with sugar and flavor with cinnamon, nutmeg or lemon juice or water if apples are not juicy
6. Cover with upper crust, slash and prick
7. Bake in moderate oven until the crust is brown and the fruit is soft

title: Apple Pie, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of sugar
- one-fourth cup of water
- five large apples or six small ones
- rich milchig pastry
- sugar and cinnamon
- bits of butter

## Directions

1. Put in saucepan sugar and water and let it boil a few minutes
2. Lay in apples which have previously been peeled and quartered
3. Cover with a lid and steam until tender but not broken
4. Line pie-plate with rich milchig pastry
5. Lay on the apples
6. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon and bits of butter
7. Drop a few drops of syrup over all
8. Bake

title: Apple Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three matzoth
- three good-sized apples
- one-quarter pound of currants
- one-quarter pound of raisins
- a little cinnamon
- some rind of lemon
- one-quarter pound of brown sugar
- two ounces of melted fat
- six beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Soak three matzoth and squeeze the water out well
2. Put them in a bowl with three good-sized apples cut in small thick pieces
3. Add one-quarter pound of currants, one-quarter pound of raisins, a little cinnamon, some rind of lemon cut thin, one-quarter pound of brown sugar and two ounces of melted fat
4. Mix all well together with six beaten eggs
5. Pour in a greased dish and bake in a moderate oven
6. Serve with rum sauce

title: Apple Pudding with Sponge Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- two scant cups of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- grated peel of a lemon
- a cup of matzoth flour, sifted very fine
- apples

## Directions

1. Make a sponge cake batter of eight eggs and two scant cups of sugar and a pinch of salt and add the grated peel of a lemon and beat until thick, at least half an hour
2. Fold in a cup of matzoth flour, sifted very fine
3. Pour this batter over the apples and bake in a moderate oven
4. Serve with wine sauce

title: Apple Puddings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 biscuits
- 3 good sized apples
- 2 oz. of currants
- 2 oz. of stoned raisins
- a little cinnamon
- rind of lemon cut thin
- 1/2 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 oz. of melted fat
- 6 eggs
- wine or rum sauce or shrub
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak 3 biscuits and squeeze the water out well
2. Put them in a basin
3. Cut up 3 good sized apples in small thick pieces
4. Add 2 oz. of currants, 2 oz. of stoned raisins, a little cinnamon, some rind of lemon cut thin, 1/2 lb. of moist sugar, and 2 oz. of melted fat
5. Mix all well together with 6 eggs
6. Grease a dish and bake in a good oven
7. This pudding can be boiled if preferred
8. Serve with wine or rum sauce or shrub, remembering always to put lemon juice in the sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Apple Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tart apples
- water
- four tablespoons of sugar
- cinnamon or grated nutmeg
- stick cinnamon or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pare and quarter tart apples
2. Put them in a saucepan with just enough water to keep them from burning
3. Bring to a boil quickly and cook until the pieces are soft
4. Then press through a colander and add four tablespoons of sugar (or less) to each pint of apples
5. If desired, cinnamon or grated nutmeg may be sprinkled over the top after the apple sauce is in the serving dish, or a little stick cinnamon or lemon peel may be cooked with the apples

title: Apple Sauce Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of butter
- one egg
- one cup of sugar
- a saltspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- a little grated nutmeg
- one cup of chopped raisins
- flour
- one cup of unsweetened apple sauce
- one teaspoon of baking-soda
- one tablespoon of boiling water

## Directions

1. In a big mixing bowl, beat to a creamy consistency four tablespoons of butter, one egg and one cup of sugar
2. Add a saltspoon of salt, one teaspoon of allspice, one teaspoon of vanilla and a little grated nutmeg
3. Beat and stir all these ingredients well together
4. Add one cup of chopped raisins, after dusting them with flour
5. Mix these well through the dough
6. Add one cup of unsweetened apple sauce which has been pressed through a fine wire sieve
7. Mix well with the other ingredients
8. Stir in one teaspoon of baking-soda dissolved in one tablespoon of boiling water
9. Last of all, stir in one cup of flour, sifting twice after measuring it
10. Bake forty-five minutes in moderate oven, or one hour if dough is too thin

title: Apple Sauce for Geese and Ducks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four large boiling apples
- a little water
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Pare and core three or four large boiling apples
2. Cut them small into a saucepan with a little water, enough to moisten them
3. When soft beat them up with a fork to a pulp
4. Add some brown sugar
5. Turn them into the sauceboat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Apple Sauce for Goose
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some apples
- a little water

## Directions

1. Slice some apples, put them in a little water to simmer till soft
2. Beat them to a pulp

title: Apple Sauce Victoria
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- water
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Pare, quarter and core the apples
2. Set on to boil in cold water
3. Boil them over a very brisk fire
4. When they are soft mash with a potato masher
5. Pass the mashed apples through a sieve
6. Sweeten to taste
7. Flavor with a teaspoon of vanilla

title: Apple Schalet No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fresh beef heart fat
- one quart of flour
- two tumblers of ice-cold water
- one tablespoon of brown sugar
- saltspoon of salt
- tart apples
- raisins
- grated lemon peel
- citron
- pounded almonds
- melted drippings
- sugar
- brown sugar
- white sugar
- ground cinnamon
- wine
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Take one pound of fresh beef heart fat and shave it as fine as possible with a knife
2. Sift one quart of flour into a deep bowl
3. Add two tumblers of ice-cold water, one tablespoon of brown sugar, a saltspoon of salt, then add the shaved heart fat and work well into the sifted flour
4. Put it on a pie-board and work as you would bread dough with the palm of your hand until it looks smooth enough to roll
5. Do not work over five minutes
6. Take half of this dough, flour your pie-board slightly and roll out as you would pie dough about once as thick
7. Grease a deep pudding-dish (an iron one is best), one that is smaller at the bottom than the top, grease it well
8. Line the pudding-dish bottom and sides clear to the top
9. Fill this one-third full with chopped tart apples, raisins, part of a grated lemon peel, citron cut quite fine, pounded almonds and melted drippings here and there
10. Sprinkle thickly with sugar, half brown and half white, and a little ground cinnamon
11. Moisten each layer with one-half wine-glass of wine
12. Put another layer of dough, rolling out half of the remaining dough and reserving the other half for the top covering
13. Fill again with apples, raisins, etc. until full
14. Put on top layer and press the dough firmly together all round the edge using a beaten egg to make sure of its sticking

title: Apple Schalet, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- schalet dough
- apples
- raisins, seeded
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Line an iron pudding-dish with schalet dough, greasing it well before you do so.
2. Chop up some apples quite fine, put on the crust, also some raisins (seeded), sugar and cinnamon.
3. Put another layer of pie and another layer of chopped apples, and so on until filled, say about three layers, the last being crust.
4. Bake slowly and long until a nice dark brown.

title: Apple Slump
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- water
- sugar
- one pint of flour
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- salt
- two cups of milk
- cream and sugar or soft custard

## Directions

1. Pare, core and quarter apples, add a little water and sugar to taste, stew until tender
2. Sift one pint of flour and one teaspoon of baking powder, add a pinch of salt and two cups of milk, mix and turn out onto a lightly floured board
3. Roll to one-half inch thickness and place over the stewed apples
4. Cover and cook for ten minutes without lifting the lid
5. Serve hot with cream and sugar or soft custard

title: Apple Snow
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large sour apple
- three-fourths cup of powdered sugar
- two eggs
- cold boiled custard

## Directions

1. Peel and grate one large sour apple, sprinkling over it three-fourths cup of powdered sugar as it is grated to keep it from turning dark.
2. Add the unbeaten whites of two eggs; beat constantly for half an hour.
3. Arrange mound fashion on a glass dish with cold boiled custard around it.

title: Apple Soufflet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- milk
- butter
- salt
- pounded sugar
- lemon
- orange-flower water
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Potato Soufflet, only that the apples must not be baked previously to being filled
2. Merely core the apples, and scoop the interior as directed
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Apple Sponge Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight apples
- sugar
- raisins
- cinnamon
- pounded almonds
- white wine
- eight eggs
- two scant cups of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- grated peel of a lemon
- one cup of matzoth flour

## Directions

1. Pare eight apples and cut off the tops carefully, so as to be able to use them as covers to the apples
2. Scrape out the inside with a knife, being careful not to break the apple
3. Mix the scrapings with sugar, raisins, cinnamon, pounded almonds and a little white wine
4. Fill this mixture into the hollow of the apple and clap on a cover for each apple
5. Grease a pudding dish, lay in the apples and stew them for a few minutes, but not long enough to break them
6. Make a sponge cake batter of eight eggs and two scant cups of sugar and a pinch of salt and add the grated peel of a lemon and beat until thick, at least half an hour
7. Fold in a cup of matzoth flour, sifted very fine
8. Pour this batter over the apples and bake in a moderate oven
9. Serve with wine sauce

title: Apple Strudel, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- pinch of salt
- one teaspoon of powdered sugar
- one cup of lukewarm water
- six apples chopped fine
- cinnamon
- sugar
- one-half cup of seedless raisins
- one-half cup of currants
- chicken-fat or oil

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour, add pinch of salt and one teaspoon of powdered sugar
2. Stir in slowly one cup of lukewarm water, and work until dough does not stick to the hands
3. Flour board, and roll as thin as possible
4. Do not tear
5. Place a tablecloth on table, put the rolled out dough on it, and pull gently with the hands, to get the dough as thin as tissue paper
6. Have ready six apples chopped fine, and mixed with cinnamon, sugar, one-half cup of seedless raisins, one-half cup of currants
7. Spread this over the dough with plenty of chicken-fat or oil all over the apples
8. Take the tablecloth in both hands, and roll the strudel, over and over, holding the cloth high, and the strudel will almost roll itself
9. Grease a baking-pan, hold to the edge of the cloth, and roll the strudel in
10. Bake brown, basting often with fat or oil

title: Apple Strudel, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of flour
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one egg
- one-third cup of warm water
- melted butter
- one quart of sour apples, peeled and cut fine
- one-quarter pound of almonds blanched and chopped
- one-half cup of raisins and currants
- one cup of sugar
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Into a large mixing bowl place one and one-half cups of flour and one-quarter teaspoon of salt
2. Beat one egg lightly and add it to one-third cup of warm water and combine the two mixtures
3. Mix the dough quickly with a knife; then knead it, place on board, stretching it up and down to make it elastic, until it leaves the board clean
4. Toss it on a well-floured board, cover with a hot bowl and keep in a warm place
5. Lay the dough in the centre of a well-floured tablecloth on the table; roll out a little, brush well with some melted butter
6. With hands under dough, palms down, pull and stretch the dough gently, until it is as large as the table and thin as paper, and do not tear the dough
7. Spread one quart of sour apples, peeled and cut fine, one-quarter pound of almonds blanched and chopped, one-half cup of raisins and currants, one cup of sugar and one teaspoon of cinnamon, evenly over three-quarters of the dough
8. Drop over them a few tablespoons of melted butter
9. Trim edges
10. Roll the dough over apples on one side, then hold cloth high with both hands and the strudel will roll itself over and over into one big roll
11. Trim edges again
12. Twist the roll to fit the greased pan
13. Bake in a hot oven until brown and crisp and brush with melted butter
14. If juicy small fruits or berries are used, sprinkle bread crumbs over the stretched dough to absorb the juices
15. Serve slightly warm

title: Apple Tapioca Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarter cup of tapioca
- one quart of water
- sugar
- six apples
- nutmeg
- cream

## Directions

1. Soak three-quarter cup of tapioca and boil it in one quart of water until clear, sweetening to taste.
2. Pare and core six apples and place them in a baking dish.
3. Fill the cores with sugar, pour the tapioca around them and grate a little nutmeg over the top.
4. Cover and bake until the apples are soft.
5. Serve with cream.

title: Apples With Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- water or milk
- butter
- salt
- about six apples
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of rice in water or milk
2. Rub the kettle all over with a piece of butter before putting in the rice
3. Season with salt and add a lump of butter
4. When cooked, add about six apples, pared, quartered and cored, sugar and cinnamon
5. This makes a nice side dish, or dessert, served with cream

title: Apricot Ice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen apricots
- kernels
- one pint of water
- juice of a lemon
- one-half pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Pare and grate one dozen apricots
2. Blanch a few of the kernels
3. Pound them and add to the grated fruit
4. Pour a pint of water over them
5. Add the juice of a lemon also
6. Let them stand for an hour and strain
7. Add one-half pound of sugar just before freezing

title: Apricot, Peach, Strawberry, Banana or Pineapple Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one pint of cream
- fruit
- pink coloring

## Directions

1. Make same as Vanilla Ice Cream No. 2, omitting the milk
2. If canned fruit is to be used, drain off the juice and add it to the eggs and cream
3. Mash the fruit through a sieve
4. Add it to rest of mixture
5. Freeze the whole
6. If fresh fruits are used, one pint is required
7. Mash fine, strain and sweeten before adding to the cream
8. For peach and strawberry a few drops of pink coloring may be added
9. Bananas must be mashed smooth, but not sweetened
10. Chop all fruits very fine
11. For pineapple, the sliced is preferred to the grated

title: Arme Ritter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two-thirds cup of milk
- six slices of stale bread
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs slightly
2. add one-half teaspoon of salt and two-thirds cup of milk
3. dip six slices of stale bread in the mixture
4. Have a griddle hot and well buttered
5. brown the bread on each side
6. Serve hot with cinnamon and sugar or a sauce

title: Artichokes (French or Globe)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- French artichokes
- boiling water
- French salad dressing
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Remove all the hard outer leaves.
2. Cut off the stem close to the leaves.
3. Cut off the top of the bud.
4. Drop the artichokes into boiling water and cook until tender, which will take from thirty to fifty minutes.
5. Take up and remove the choke.
6. Serve a dish of French salad dressing with the artichokes, which may be eaten either hot or cold.
7. Melted butter also makes a delicious sauce for the artichokes if they are eaten hot.

title: Asparagus
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- one teaspoon of salt for each quart of water
- boiling water
- well-toasted bread
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the woody part, scrape the lower part of the stalks.
2. Wash well and tie in bunches.
3. Put into a deep stew-pan, with the cut end resting on the bottom of the stew-pan.
4. Pour in boiling water to come up to the tender heads, but not to cover them.
5. Add one teaspoon of salt for each quart of water.
6. Place where the water will boil.
7. Cook until tender, having the cover partially off the stew-pan. This will be from fifteen to thirty minutes, depending upon the freshness and tenderness of the vegetable.
8. Have some slices of well-toasted bread on a platter.
9. Butter them slightly.
10. Arrange the cooked asparagus on the toast, season with butter and a little salt and serve at once.
11. Save the water in which the asparagus was boiled to use in making vegetable soup.

title: Asparagus Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- mayonnaise dressing
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the asparagus in salted water, being very careful not to break the caps; drain, and pour over it when cold a mayonnaise dressing, with some chopped parsley. Serve each person with three or four stems on a plate, with a little mayonnaise dressing. Do not use a fork; take the stems in the fingers and dip in the dressing.

title: Asparagus Sauce to Serve with Lamb Chops
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- gravy made from beef, veal, or mutton
- salt
- white pepper
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Cut some asparagus into half inch lengths
2. Wash them
3. Throw them into half a pint of gravy made from beef, veal, or mutton thickened
4. Season with salt, white pepper, and a lump of white sugar
5. Fry the chops delicately and serve the sauce in the centre of the dish

title: Asparagus Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of gravy beef
- five pints of water
- sweet herbs
- an onion
- pepper
- salt
- a bundle of asparagus
- chopped parsley
- mint
- fried bread
- spinach

## Directions

1. Take eight pounds of gravy beef, with five pints of water, a few sweet herbs, and an onion shred, with a little pepper and salt
2. When the strength of the meat is sufficiently extracted, strain off the soup
3. Add to it a bundle of asparagus, cut small, with a little chopped parsley and mint
4. The asparagus should be thoroughly done
5. A few minutes before serving, throw in some fried bread cut up the size of dice
6. Pound a little spinach to a pulp, and squeeze it through a cloth
7. Stir about a tea-cup full of this essence into the soup
8. Let it boil up after to prevent a raw taste

title: Aspic (Sulz)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two calf's feet
- one pound of beef
- one calf's head
- one quart water
- one cup of white wine
- one celery root
- three small onions
- a bunch of parsley
- one dozen whole peppercorns
- half a dozen cloves
- two bay leaves
- one teaspoon of fine salt
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Set on to boil two calf's feet chopped up, one pound of beef and one calf's head with one quart water and one cup of white wine
2. Add one celery root, three small onions, a bunch of parsley, one dozen whole peppercorns, half a dozen cloves, two bay leaves and a teaspoon of fine salt
3. Boil steadily for eight hours and then pour through a fine hair sieve
4. When cold remove every particle of fat and set on to boil again, skimming until clear
5. Break two eggs, shells and all, into a deep bowl and beat them up with one cup of vinegar
6. Pour some of the soup stock into this and set all back on the stove to boil up once, stirring all the while
7. Remove from the fire and pour through a jelly-bag as you would jelly
8. Pour into jelly-glasses or one large mould
9. Set on ice

title: Auflauf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- one-half cup of sifted flour
- yolks of three eggs
- salt
- beaten whites of three eggs
- butter
- jelly or preserves

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of milk and when boiling stir in quickly one-half cup of sifted flour and work smooth until all lumps are out and it is the consistency of soft mashed potatoes.
2. Stir all the while over fire.
3. When smooth remove from stove and while yet warm break in, one by one, yolks of three eggs, a pinch of salt, then the beaten whites of three eggs.
4. Bake in well-buttered hot square pans, in very hot oven, from fifteen to twenty minutes.
5. Serve as soon as done with jelly or preserves.

title: Baba a la Parisienne
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast
- a scant cup of butter
- four tablespoons of sugar
- grated peel of a lemon
- five eggs
- two cups of sifted flour
- a scant cup of lukewarm milk
- a pinch of salt
- one cup of flour
- one-half cup of almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare the yeast as above
2. cream a scant cup of butter with four tablespoons of sugar, the grated peel of a lemon
3. add five eggs, one at a time, stirring each egg a few minutes before you add the next
4. Have ready two cups of sifted flour and add two spoonfuls between each egg until all is used
5. Make a soft dough of the yeast, a scant cup of lukewarm milk, add two spoonsfuls between each egg until all is used up, a pinch of salt, and one cup of flour
6. Let it rise for fifteen minutes
7. Now mix all well
8. rub the form with butter, and blanch one-half cup of almonds, cut into long strips and strew all over the form
9. Fill in the mixture or cake batter
10. let it rise two hours and bake very slowly

title: Bairische Dampfnudeln, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- a cup of lukewarm milk
- a teaspoon of sugar
- a teaspoon of salt
- a pint of flour
- a small cup of milk
- one egg
- butter
- milk
- vanilla sauce

## Directions

1. Soak one cake of compressed yeast in a cup of lukewarm milk with a teaspoon of sugar, a teaspoon of salt
2. Sift a pint of flour in a bowl, in which you may also stir a small cup of milk and one egg
3. Pour in the yeast and work all thoroughly, adding more flour, but guarding against getting the dough too stiff
4. Cover up the bowl of dough and let it raise until it is as high again, which will take at least four hours
5. Flour a baking-board and mold small biscuits out of your dough, let them raise at least half an hour
6. Butter a large, round, deep pan and set in your dumplings, brushing each with melted butter as you do so
7. Pour in enough milk to reach just half way up to the dumplings
8. Bake until a light brown
9. Eat hot, with vanilla sauce

title: Bairische Dampfnudeln, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough from above recipe
- a tablespoon of butter
- prune sauce

## Directions

1. Make the dough just as you would in the above recipe, adding a tablespoon of butter
2. After they have risen steam instead of baking them
3. Put on a kettle of boiling water, set a colander on top of the kettle and lay in your dumplings, but do not crowd them
4. Cover with a close-fitting lid and put a weight on top of it to keep in the steam
5. When done they will be as large again as when first put in
6. Take up one at first to try whether it is done by tearing open with two forks
7. If you have more than enough for your family, bake a pan of biscuits out of the remaining dough
8. Serve dumplings hot with prune sauce

title: Baked Apple with Oatmeal
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- leftover oatmeal mush
- sugar
- water
- cream

## Directions

1. Pare and core the apples and fill the core space with leftover oatmeal mush
2. Put the apples in a baking dish
3. Sprinkle with sugar
4. Pour a little water into the bottom of the pan and bake in a moderate oven until the apples are tender
5. Serve warm with cream for breakfast or luncheon

title: Baked Apples
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- good baking apples
- 1 lb. sugar
- cloves
- rind of a lemon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pick some good baking apples
2. Prick them with a fork
3. Lay them in a deep dish
4. Add 1 lb. of sugar, some cloves, and the rind of a lemon
5. Add a little of the juice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, juicy apples
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- raisins
- clove
- water
- sugar
- egg
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take large, juicy apples
2. Wash and core them well
3. Fill each place that you have cored with brown sugar, cinnamon and raisins
4. Put a clove in each apple
5. Lay them in a deep dish
6. Pour a teacup of water in the dish
7. Put a little sugar on top of each apple
8. When well done the apples will be broken
9. Remove them carefully to the dish they are to be served in
10. Pour the syrup over them
11. To be eaten cold
12. If you wish them extra nice, glaze them with the beaten white of an egg, half a cup of pulverized sugar and serve with whipped cream

title: Baked Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, juicy apples
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- raisins
- cloves
- water
- sugar
- beaten white of an egg
- pulverized sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Wash and core apples well
2. Fill each cored place with brown sugar, cinnamon and raisins
3. Put a clove in each apple
4. Lay them in a deep dish
5. Pour a teacup of water in the dish
6. Put a little sugar on top of each apple
7. When well done the apples will be broken
8. Remove them carefully to the dish they are to be served in
9. Pour the syrup over them
10. To be eaten cold
11. If you wish them extra nice, glaze them with the beaten white of an egg, half a cup of pulverized sugar and serve with whipped cream

title: Baked Bass a la Wellington
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bass
- one tablespoon of butter
- two cups of stale bread crumbs
- one tablespoon of chopped onion
- one teaspoon of chopped parsley
- two teaspoons of chopped capers
- one-fourth cup of sherry
- paprika
- salt
- sherry wine
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the scales and clean. Do not remove the head, tail, or fins.
2. Put into a double boiler one tablespoon of butter, two cups of stale bread crumbs, one tablespoon of chopped onion, one teaspoon of chopped parsley, two teaspoons of chopped capers, one-fourth cup of sherry.
3. Heat all the above ingredients, season with paprika and salt, and stuff the bass with the mixture.
4. Sew up the fish, put into a hot oven, bake and baste with sherry wine and butter.

title: Baked Beans with Brisket of Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of navy beans
- one and one-half pounds of brisket of beef
- one-half tablespoon of mustard
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of molasses
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one-half cup of boiling water

## Directions

1. Wash, pick over and soak overnight in cold water, two cups of navy beans
2. In the morning, drain and cover with fresh water
3. heat slowly and let cook just below the boiling point until the skins burst
4. When done, drain beans and put in a pot with one and one-half pounds of brisket of beef
5. Mix one-half tablespoon of mustard, one teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of molasses, two tablespoons of sugar, one-half cup of boiling water and pour over beans
6. add enough more boiling water to cover them
7. Cover pot and bake slowly six or eight hours

title: Baked Beets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large beetroot
- drippings
- pepper
- salt
- stock

## Directions

1. Boil large beetroot about two hours, being careful not to pierce it
2. When cold mash very smooth, add a little drippings, pepper, salt and stock
3. Place in a greased pan and bake one hour

title: Baked Black Bass
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- black bass
- salt
- one small cup of water
- butter
- pepper
- parsley
- one glass of sherry
- catsup
- one teaspoon of flour
- potatoes
- two tablespoons of butter
- one small cup of sweet cream
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. After having carefully cleaned, salt well and lay it in the baking-pan with a small cup of water, and strew flakes of butter on top, also salt, pepper and a little chopped parsley.
2. Bake about one hour, basting often until brown.
3. Serve on a heated platter; garnish with parsley and lemon and make a sauce by adding a glass of sherry, a little catsup and thicken with a teaspoon of flour, adding this to fish gravy.
4. Serve potatoes with fish, boiled in the usual way, making a sauce of two tablespoons of butter.
5. Add a bunch of parsley chopped very fine, salt and pepper to taste, a small cup of sweet cream thickened with a tablespoon of flour.
6. Pour over potatoes.

title: Baked Bread Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of stale bread
- 2 oz. of currants
- 2 oz. of raisins
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 oz. of melted fat
- grated nutmeg
- cinnamon
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup of water
- orange or lemon peel
- almonds

## Directions

1. Soak a piece of stale bread, drain it well from the water
2. Beat the bread with a fork
3. Add to it the currants, raisins, sugar, melted fat, nutmeg and cinnamon
4. Beat it up with the eggs and water
5. Grease a dish and place it in
6. Cut a few pieces of orange or lemon peel and a few almonds and put them on the outside with the peel
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Carp
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- chopped onion
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- 2 or 3 potatoes
- 2 eggs
- parsley
- marjoram
- nutmeg
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Split open the carp and lay it in a baking dish with some chopped onion, a little oil, vinegar, pepper and salt
2. Mash 2 or 3 potatoes with 2 eggs, parsley, marjoram, a little nutmeg
3. Lay it over the carp
4. Let it bake a nice colour
5. Send it up with sauce made in the same way with no caper, but flavoured stronger of the anchovy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Chopped Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- water
- sweet milk
- medium-sized onion
- butter
- cream
- two egg yolks
- one-fourth cup of white bread crumbs
- crumbs or cracker dust
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak herring one hour in water and then one and a half in sweet milk.
2. Skin, bone and chop herring.
3. Cut up a medium-sized onion, fry in butter until golden brown.
4. Add a cup of cream, two egg yolks and one-fourth cup of white bread crumbs, then put in a little more cream.
5. Butter pan, sprinkle with crumbs or cracker dust, then put in herring, pepper slightly.
6. Bake in moderate oven three-quarters of an hour.

title: Baked Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of the tail of cod
- mashed potatoes
- butter
- yolk of eggs
- 2 or 3 onions
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- marjoram
- 2 eggs
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Take a nice piece of the tail of cod, cut all the fins off
2. Have some mashed potatoes with butter and yolk of eggs ready
3. Slice in your fish tin 2 or 3 onions, with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg, chopped parsley, and some marjoram
4. Notch the tail in slices just half down to the bone
5. Make layers of mashed potatoes and put where you have cut the slices of the fish to fill up, so that the tail will look twice the size
6. Lay the tail on the fried onions
7. Beat up 2 eggs with the juice of a lemon, and pour it over the fish
8. Put it in the oven to bake a light brown
9. Garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Crab-Apple Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half peck of perfect red Siberian crab-apples
- four pounds of sugar
- one tablespoon of water
- Manila paper

## Directions

1. Wash, wipe and remove the blossom ends of one-half peck of perfect red Siberian crab-apples.
2. Pour one tablespoon of water in bottom of one gallon stone jar.
3. Place in alternate layers of apples and sugar, using four pounds altogether (with sugar on top).
4. Cover with two thicknesses of Manila paper, tied down securely or with close fitting plate.
5. Bake in a very slow oven (that would only turn the paper a light brown), two or three hours.
6. Let stand to cool.
7. Keep in cool, dry place.

title: Baked Cranberries or Cherry Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of large, perfect cranberries, or four pounds of large cherries
- four pounds of sugar
- one tablespoon of hot water

## Directions

1. Pick over, wash and drain four quarts of large, perfect cranberries; or stem and then stone four pounds of large cherries, use a cherry pitter so cherries remain whole.
2. Place a tablespoon of hot water in a jar, then alternately in layers cherries or cranberries and sugar (with sugar on top).
3. Cover closely.
4. Bake in a very slow oven two hours.
5. Let stand.
6. Keep in a cool, dry place.

title: Baked Egg With Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six fresh tomatoes or one-half can of tomatoes
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from six fresh tomatoes or take one-half can of tomatoes, chop them and put them on stove and cook for twenty minutes; season with one tablespoon of chopped parsley, half an onion chopped, salt and pepper.
2. Thicken at the end of that time with one teaspoon of melted butter mixed with one tablespoon of flour.
3. Put aside to cool.
4. Then mix in the yolks of four eggs well beaten, and lastly cut and fold in the four whites.
5. Butter a pudding dish and set this mixture in the oven in a pan of lukewarm water and bake in a moderate oven until a golden brown.

title: Baked Eggplant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- salt
- water
- one tablespoon of butter
- one onion, minced
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- one egg yolk
- cracker crumbs (for topping, optional)

## Directions

1. Parboil eggplant until tender, but not soft, in boiling salted water
2. Cut in half crosswise with a sharp knife
3. Scrape out the inside and do not break the skin
4. Heat one tablespoon of butter, add a minced onion, brown
5. Add scraped eggplant, bread crumbs, salt and pepper to taste and an egg yolk
6. Mix well together
7. Refill shells
8. Place in dripping pan in oven
9. Baste with butter or sprinkle cracker crumbs on top with bits of butter
10. Baste often and brown nicely

title: Baked Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Butter individual baking dishes and break an egg in each, being careful to keep the yolk whole.
2. Put on each egg a bit of butter, a little pepper and salt.
3. Bake in moderate oven from four to six minutes.

title: Baked Eggs With Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- salt
- paprika
- pepper
- cream
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Butter a baking dish of a size necessary for number of eggs desired, break eggs into dish, add salt, paprika, pepper to taste, one tablespoon of cream, and two tablespoons of grated cheese.
2. Place dish in a pan of hot water in moderate oven for five minutes until eggs are set.

title: Baked Fillets of Mackerel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- cotton
- chopped onion
- parsley
- marjoram
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- oil or butter
- vinegar
- fennel

## Directions

1. Wash the mackerel well
2. dry them
3. split them in halves
4. take out the bones
5. roll each half up
6. fasten with a piece of cotton
7. put in a deep dish some chopped onion, parsley and marjoram, pepper, salt, 3 or 4 cloves, with 2 spoonfuls of oil, or a lb. of butter, a cup of vinegar
8. let them bake for 1 hour
9. keep basting them
10. dish them up
11. garnish with fennel
12. put the sauce nicely over the mackerel when cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- bread stuffing
- hot water
- butter
- lemon
- parsley
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and dry the fish, rubbing inside and outside with salt
2. Stuff with a bread stuffing and sew
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and place in a hot oven without water
4. As soon as it begins to brown add hot water and butter
5. Baste every ten minutes
6. Bake until done, allowing an hour or more for a large fish, twenty or thirty minutes for a small one
7. Remove to a hot platter and draw out the strings
8. Garnish with slices of lemon well covered with chopped parsley and serve with Hollandaise sauce

title: Baked Fish - Turkish Style
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- perch
- steamed onion
- one well-beaten egg
- two tomatoes
- bread crumbs
- parsley or celery
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take perch and stuff with steamed onion to which has been added one well-beaten egg, two tomatoes cut up in small pieces, some bread crumbs, chopped parsley or celery, salt and pepper to taste.
2. Bake until the fish is nicely browned.

title: Baked Flounders
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flounders
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- one onion
- celery
- bread crumbs
- one cup of hot water

## Directions

1. Clean, wipe dry, add salt and pepper and lay them in a pan.
2. Put flakes of butter on top, an onion cut up, some minced celery and a few bread crumbs.
3. Put a cup of hot water into the pan to prevent burning.
4. Baste often; bake until brown.

title: Baked Fresh Herrings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh herrings
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- grated nutmeg
- vinegar
- saffron
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the herrings well
2. take off the heads
3. cut in a baking dish some onion, salt, pepper, a little marjoram, grated nutmeg, a teacup of vinegar
4. place in the herrings a little saffron
5. let them bake till done
6. dish them up
7. put a little chopped parsley over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Haddock
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh haddock
- forcemeat
- flour
- warmed butter
- pepper
- salt
- capers

## Directions

1. Carefully clean a fresh haddock, and fill it with a fine forcemeat, and sew it in securely
2. Give the fish a dredging of flour, and pour on warmed butter
3. Sprinkle it with pepper and salt
4. Set it to bake in a Dutch-oven before the fire, basting it, from time to time, with butter warmed, and capers
5. It should be of a rich dark brown
6. Dredge two or three times with flour while at the fire
7. The continual bastings will produce sufficient sauce to serve with it without any other being added

title: Baked Haddock
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddock
- mashed potatoes
- herbs
- spice
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- flour or raspings from the baker
- butter
- tomatoes
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare a stuffing of mashed potatoes, herbs and spice as before described
2. Place it in the inside of the haddock and sew it up
3. Bake in a deep earthenware dish
4. Turn the haddock round with the tail in the mouth
5. Chop some onion very fine
6. Season the fish outside with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
7. Dredge with flour or some raspings from the baker
8. Put in the dish 1/4 lb. of butter to baste it well as it cooks
9. Add 2 or 3 tomatoes sliced with it
10. When dishing it up put on some chopped parsley
11. Prepare some potatoes as for baked plaice
12. When you put it in the dish, pour the gravy all over it with a spoon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Hash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of chopped meat
- one cup of cold mashed potatoes
- one-half an onion
- one well-beaten egg
- one-half cup of soup stock
- salt
- paprika
- celery salt

## Directions

1. Mix together one cup of chopped meat, one cup of cold mashed potatoes, one-half an onion minced, one well-beaten egg and one-half cup of soup stock.
2. Season rather highly with salt, paprika and celery salt.
3. Turn into greased baking dish and bake for twenty minutes in a well-heated oven.

title: Baked Lentils (Linzen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of lentils
- cold water
- one quart of water
- one small onion
- three or four ounces of smoked fat meat
- one pint of boiling water
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-quarter teaspoon of pepper

## Directions

1. Pick and wash one-half pound of lentils and soak them in cold water overnight
2. In the morning put them over the fire in a large saucepan with about a quart of water
3. As soon as the water begins to boil, the lentils will rise to the top
4. Remove them with a skimmer
5. put them in a baking dish with one small onion and three or four ounces of smoked fat meat in the centre
6. pour over them a pint of boiling water, in which one-half teaspoon of salt and one-quarter teaspoon of pepper have been mixed
7. Bake in a moderate oven four or five hours
8. The lentils must be kept moist and it may be necessary to add a little water from time to time

title: Baked Macaroni With Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup broken macaroni
- two quarts boiling salted water
- cream sauce
- one cup grated cheese
- one cup buttered bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook one cup of broken macaroni in two quarts of boiling salted water for twenty or thirty minutes
2. Drain and pour cold water through the colander
3. Put the macaroni in a pudding-dish in layers, covering each layer with cream sauce and grated cheese
4. On the top layers sprinkle one cup of buttered bread crumbs
5. Bake in oven until the crumbs are brown

title: Baked Mackarel with Vinegar
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- mackarel
- bay leaves
- whole pepper
- cloves (1/2 tea-spoonful)
- allspice (1 whole spoonful)
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads and tails
2. Open and clean them
3. Lay them in a deep pan with a few bay leaves, whole pepper, half a tea-spoonful of cloves, and a whole spoonful of allspice
4. Pour over equal quantities of vinegar and water
5. Bake for an hour and a half, in a gentle oven

title: Baked Mackerel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of butter
- two-thirds of a cup of milk

## Directions

1. Split fish, clean, and remove head and tail.
2. Put in buttered pan, sprinkle with salt and pepper and dot over with butter (allowing one tablespoon to a medium-sized fish), pour over two-thirds of a cup of milk.
3. Bake twenty-five minutes in a hot oven.

title: Baked Pears
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- a couple of cloves per pear
- half a pound of brown sugar
- a little water

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and stick a couple of cloves in each pear
2. Place them in a deep dish with half a pound of brown sugar and a little water
3. Let them bake till quite tender

title: Baked Pears
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- cloves
- brown sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Peel them and stick a couple of cloves in each pear
2. Place them in a deep dish with half a pound of brown sugar and a little water
3. Let them bake till quite tender

title: Baked Pike
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potatoes
- butter
- 2 eggs
- chopped parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- lemon rind
- onion
- oil
- lemon
- caper sauce
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing of mashed potatoes with butter and 2 eggs, chopped parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, a little rind of lemon
2. Put this into the fish and sew it up
3. Chop an onion fine at the bottom of a round baking dish or fish tin with salt, pepper, butter or oil
4. Lay the fish on it and squeeze a lemon over it
5. Put a few lumps of butter on top of it
6. Turn the fish round by putting the tail in the mouth, fastened with a skewer
7. Put it in a good oven
8. When baked, dish it up and put over a good caper sauce
9. Send up with nice boiled potatoes, all cut one size
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Pike
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- mashed potatoes
- butter
- eggs
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- lemon rind
- onion
- oil

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing of mashed potatoes with butter and 2 eggs, chopped parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, a little rind of lemon
2. Put this into the fish and sew it up
3. Chop an onion fine at the bottom of a round baking dish (or your fish tin) with salt, pepper, butter or oil
4. Lay the fish on it and squeeze a lemon over it
5. Put a few lumps of butter on top of it
6. Turn the fish round, by putting the tail in the mouth, fastened with a skewer
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Plaice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- onions
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- dried marjoram
- flour
- dried saffron
- water
- small potatoes
- lemon
- parsley
- tomatoes
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Slice in your fish tin two or three onions with two spoonfuls of oil, with salt and pepper, put it in the oven to get hot
2. Wash and dry well a thick plaice notched as above, lay it on the tin, season it well, and sprinkle over a little dried marjoram
3. Dredge it with flour
4. Mix in a cup a little dried saffron with two spoonfuls of water, baste it over the plaice
5. Boil some small potatoes, and when done put them round the tin with the plaice to get brown
6. When you dish it up, squeeze the juice of a lemon over, and a little chopped parsley
7. Dish it up with the potatoes round, and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Potatoes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- cream
- parsley

## Directions

1. Select fine, smooth potatoes and boil them about twenty minutes
2. Drain off the water and remove the skins
3. Pack in a buttered dish
4. Lay a small piece of butter on each potato
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. Sprinkle fine bread crumbs over all with a few tablespoons of cream
7. Bake until a nice light brown
8. Serve in the same dish and garnish with parsley

title: Baked Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- water
- lemon juice and peel

## Directions

1. Wash and soak the prunes
2. Put them in an earthenware bean pot with a very little water
3. Cook slowly in the oven for a long time
4. Lemon juice and peel may be added if desired

title: Baked Quince
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wipe, core, and quarter quinces.
2. Fill sugar in the cavities.
3. Bake same as crab-apples, in a very slow oven three or more hours until clear and glassy.

title: Baked Rhubarb
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into two-inch lengths three bunches of rhubarb
2. Dredge with flour
3. Put in baking dish with one cup of sugar sprinkled over
4. Bake in moderate oven three-quarters of an hour
5. Very nice served hot as a vegetable, or cold as a sauce

title: Baked Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of boiled rice
- grated cheese
- milk
- butter
- salt
- red pepper
- one cup of grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Arrange two cups of boiled rice in a baking dish in layers
2. Cover each with grated cheese, a little milk, butter, salt and red pepper
3. Spread one cup of grated bread crumbs over all
4. Bake in a moderate oven until the crumbs are browned

title: Baked Rice Puddings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of good rice
- salt
- four eggs
- 1/2 pint of water
- 3 ozs. of sugar
- grated nutmeg
- rind of a fresh lemon or chopped lemon and orange peel
- 1 oz. of dried currants
- 2 ozs. of melted suet

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 lb. of good rice in water with a dash of salt
2. When it has absorbed the water, beat up four eggs with 1/2 pint of water, 3 ozs. of sugar, a little grated nutmeg, and part of a rind of a fresh lemon or a little chopped lemon and orange peel, with 1 oz. of dried currants and 2 ozs. of melted suet
3. Mix all well together
4. Grease a dish and bake it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baked Shad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three-pound shad
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Clean and split a three-pound shad.
2. Place in a buttered dripping pan.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, brush with melted butter and bake in a hot oven thirty minutes.

title: Baked Suet Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of water
- six ounces of flour
- three ounces of shred suet
- two or three beaten eggs
- sugar
- raisins or currants

## Directions

1. Mix one pint of water, six ounces of flour, three of shred suet, and two or three beaten eggs
2. sweeten to taste
3. Add raisins or currants if approved
4. bake in a brick oven

title: Baked Tomato and Egg Plant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- eggplant
- salt
- tomatoes
- spaghetti (cooked)
- grated cheese
- butter
- pepper
- green pepper

## Directions

1. Take a deep earthenware dish, pour into it a cup of cream; cut several slices of eggplant very thin, salt well, and line the dish with them.
2. Slice two large tomatoes, place a layer of these on the eggplant, next a layer of spaghetti (cooked); sprinkle with grated cheese, pieces of butter, salt, and pepper.
3. Cover this with layer of tomatoes; salt well and sprinkle with chopped green pepper, and a top layer of eggplant, which also salt and pepper well.
4. Cook gently an hour and a half in slow, hot oven.

title: Baked Trout
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- mashed potatoes
- onions
- lemon juice
- cayenne
- salt
- white pepper
- bread raspings
- butter or oil
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash it very clean and dry it well
2. Make a nice stuffing of mashed potatoes, season the same as for baked pike
3. Sew it up
4. Take your fish tin and slice some onions, put them in the dish
5. Let them fry in the oven a light brown
6. Take out the dish and squeeze the juice of a lemon in with a little cayenne, salt and white pepper
7. Put over the salmon some raspings
8. Put in the baking dish ½ lb. of butter or ½ pint of oil
9. As it cooks keep basting it with a spoon
10. Have some parsley chopped and when you lay the trout in the dish throw over the gravy and the chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Baking Powder Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of flour
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- scant one-half cup of milk or water

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of flour, one-fourth teaspoon of salt, one teaspoon of baking powder, stir in scant one-half cup of milk or water and mix to a smooth batter.
2. Drop one teaspoonful at a time in the boiling soup; cover kettle, let boil five minutes and serve at once.

title: Baking-Powder Biscuits
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- four teaspoons of baking-powder
- four tablespoons of butter
- one-half to two-thirds cup of water or milk

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour with one-half teaspoon of salt and four teaspoons of baking-powder
2. Sift in four tablespoons of butter
