3. Cut butter in with two knives and mix with one-half to two-thirds cup of water or milk
4. Stir this in quickly with a knife
5. When well mixed place on a well-floured board and roll out about one inch thick, work quickly
6. Cut with a biscuit cutter or the cover of a half-pound baking-powder can
7. Place on a greased pan
8. Bake quickly in a well-heated quick oven ten to fifteen minutes

title: Baking-Powder Bunt Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 2.5 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 0.25 cup small raisins

## Directions

1. Beat two whole eggs for ten minutes with two cups of sugar, two and one-half tablespoons of melted butter
2. Add one cup of milk, three cups of flour in which have been sifted two teaspoons of baking-powder
3. Flavor with one teaspoon of vanilla
4. One-fourth cup of small raisins may be added
5. Bake one hour

title: Baking-Powder Cinnamon Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.75 cup sugar
- butter the size of an egg
- 2 eggs
- 0.5 cup milk
- 1.5 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- cinnamon
- flour
- sugar
- water
- chopped nuts

## Directions

1. Cream three-fourths cup of sugar with a piece of butter the size of an egg
2. Beat together
3. Then add two eggs, one-half cup of milk (scant), one and one-half cups of flour, one teaspoon of vanilla and two teaspoons of baking-powder
4. Put cinnamon, flour, sugar and a few drops of water together, and form in little pfarvel with your hand and sprinkle on top of cake
5. Also sprinkle a few chopped nuts on top
6. Do not bake too quickly
7. Bake in flat pan

title: Banana Dainty
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- grated nuts or nutmeg
- white mayonnaise
- maraschino cherries

## Directions

1. Cut the bananas in half crosswise and arrange them on a plate, radiating from the center
2. Sprinkle with grated nuts or nutmeg and heap white mayonnaise in the center
3. Garnish with maraschino cherries

title: Banana Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six bananas
- juice of one lemon
- three or more tablespoons of sugar
- whipped cream or boiled icing

## Directions

1. Mash six bananas, add juice of one lemon and three or more tablespoons of sugar
2. Or add mashed bananas with whipped cream or boiled icing

title: Bananas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- sugar
- wine or orange juice
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Slice according to fancy, either round or lengthwise
2. Set on ice until required
3. Add sugar, wine or orange juice
4. In serving, dish out with a tablespoon of whipped cream

title: Banbury Tarts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of seeded muscatel raisins
- one cup of nuts
- one cup of sugar
- one well-beaten egg
- one tablespoon of water
- juice and grated rind of one lemon
- pie dough

## Directions

1. Cut raisins and nuts in small pieces
2. Add sugar, egg, water, lemon juice and rind
3. Mix well
4. Line patty-pans with pie dough
5. Fill with mixture
6. Bake until crust is brown

title: Barches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- shortening or dripping or rendered fat or vegetable oil
- dozen almonds (blanched)
- two well-beaten eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- salt
- raisins
- grated peel of one lemon

## Directions

1. Use water and a little shortening of dripping or rendered fat or a vegetable oil
2. Grate a dozen almonds (blanched) and add with two well-beaten eggs, one-half cup of sugar, salt, raisins and the grated peel of one lemon
3. Work just as you would butter barches
4. Bake one hour in moderate oven
5. Wrap in a damp, clean towel as soon as baked to prevent the crust from becoming too hard

title: Barley and Vegetable Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup of coarse barley
- two quarts of water
- two onions
- a bunch of carrots
- parsley
- two turnips
- one green pepper
- six tomatoes
- a few green peas
- lima beans
- two ears of corn
- pepper and salt
- a small piece of butter
- quarter teaspoon of sage and thyme

## Directions

1. Take a half cup of coarse barley and two quarts of water.
2. Let boil for one hour and skim.
3. Then add two onions, a bunch of carrots, parsley, two turnips, one green pepper and six tomatoes (all chopped fine).
4. Add a few green peas, lima beans, two ears of corn cut from cob; pepper and salt to taste.
5. Cook for one hour or more until done.
6. Then add a small piece of butter, quarter teaspoon of sage and thyme, if you like, and if soup is too thick add more water.

title: Barley Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pearl barley
- water
- white sugar
- lemon juice
- white wine
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil in an iron saucepan, one tea-cup full of pearl barley, with one quart of cold water
2. Pour off the water when it boils, and add another quart
3. Let it simmer very gently for three hours over or near a slow fire
4. Stir it frequently with a wooden spoon
5. Strain it, and sweeten with white sugar
6. Add the juice of a lemon, a little white wine, and a quarter of an ounce of isinglass dissolved in a little water
7. Pour it into a mould

title: Barley Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tea-cup full of pearl barley
- one quart of cold water
- another quart of water
- white sugar
- juice of a lemon
- a little white wine
- a quarter of an ounce of isinglass
- a little water

## Directions

1. Boil in an iron saucepan one tea-cup full of pearl barley with one quart of cold water
2. Pour off the water when it boils and add another quart
3. Let it simmer very gently for three hours over or near a slow fire
4. Stir it frequently with a wooden spoon
5. Strain it and sweeten with white sugar
6. Add the juice of a lemon, a little white wine, and a quarter of an ounce of isinglass dissolved in a little water
7. Pour it into a mould

title: Barley Milk
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of pearl barley
- one quart of new milk
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Parboil half a pound of pearl barley in water
2. Pour off the water
3. Boil the parboiled barley in one quart of new milk
4. Sweeten with white sugar

title: Barley Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of mutton, or four pounds of the neck
- three quarts of water
- a sprig of parsley
- a couple of sliced turnips
- a carrot
- an onion or more
- white pepper
- salt
- a breakfast cup full of barley

## Directions

1. Put in a stew-pan, a knuckle of mutton, or four pounds of the neck, with three quarts of water, boil it gently and keep it well skimmed
2. Add a sprig of parsley, a couple of sliced turnips, a carrot, an onion or more, if approved, with a little white pepper and salt
3. Scald a breakfast cup full of barley and put it in the stew-pan with the meat
4. If when done, the soup is thin and watery, a little prepared barley, mixed smoothly, should be stirred in

title: Barley Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup barley
- two onions, cut fine
- one-half cup carrots, diced
- one teaspoon salt
- pepper
- two quarts water
- one-half cup celery root

## Directions

1. Take one cup of barley, two onions cut fine, one-half cup of carrots diced, one teaspoon of salt, pepper to taste
2. Add two quarts of water and simmer two or three hours
3. When water has evaporated add soup; if you are making fresh soup, keep adding the "top soup," strained, to the barley and let boil until tender
4. One-half cup of celery root boiled with the barley improves the flavor

title: Barley, Tapioca, Sago, Etc.
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon of salt
- one quart of boiling water
- one-half cup of barley or other hard grain

## Directions

1. Add one teaspoon of salt to one quart of boiling water
2. Pour gradually on one-half cup of barley or other hard grain and boil until tender, from one to two or more hours, according to the grain, and have each kernel stand out distinct when done
3. Add more boiling water as it evaporates
4. Use as a vegetable or in soups

title: Baseler Loekerlein (Honey Cakes)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of strained honey
- half a pound of sifted powdered sugar
- half a pound of almonds (cut in half lengthwise)
- half a pound of finest flour
- one ounce of citron (cut or chopped extremely fine)
- peel of a lemon
- a little grated nutmeg
- a pinch of ground cloves
- a wineglass of brandy

## Directions

1. Set the honey and sugar over the fire together, put in the almonds, stir all up thoroughly.
2. Next put in the spices and work into a dough.
3. Put away in a cold place for a week.
4. Then roll about as thick as a finger.
5. Bake in a quick oven and cut into strips with a sharp knife after they are baked (do this while hot), cut three inches long and two inches wide.

title: Baseler Loekerlein (Honey Cakes)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of strained honey
- half a pound of sifted powdered sugar
- half a pound of almonds
- half a pound of finest flour
- one ounce of citron
- peel of a lemon
- a little grated nutmeg
- a pinch of ground cloves
- a wineglass of brandy

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of strained honey, half a pound of sifted powdered sugar, half a pound of almonds cut in half lengthwise, half a pound of finest flour, one ounce of citron cut or chopped extremely fine, peel of a lemon, a little grated nutmeg, also a pinch of ground cloves and a wineglass of brandy
2. Set the honey and sugar over the fire together
3. Put in the almonds, stir all up thoroughly
4. Put in the spices and work into a dough
5. Put away in a cold place for a week
6. Roll about as thick as a finger
7. Bake in a quick oven

title: Batter Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of flour
- four beaten eggs
- one pint of milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stir in three ounces of flour, four beaten eggs, and one pint of milk
2. sweeten to taste
3. mix to a smooth batter about the thickness of good cream
4. boil in a buttered basin

title: Batter Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teacup of matzoth-meal
- one pint of milk
- two eggs
- three ounces of brown sugar
- two ounces of butter
- rind of a lemon
- one tablespoon of rum

## Directions

1. Mix the meal into a batter with the milk and eggs
2. Add the sugar, melted butter, grated rind of a lemon and a tablespoon of rum, if desired
3. Pour the mixture in a greased basin or mold, and boil for one hour or bake for one-half hour

title: Beans and Barley
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of navy beans
- cold water
- one quart boiling water
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of barley
- fat soup stock

## Directions

1. Soak one-half cup of navy beans in cold water overnight
2. Drain and cook in one quart boiling water with one teaspoon of salt, until tender but not broken
3. add one-half cup of barley and let cook slowly until barley is tender, about one-half hour
4. Add fat soup stock as the water evaporates
5. Season to taste and bake in medium oven about one-half hour or until dry but not browned

title: Bearnaise Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons vinegar
- two tablespoons water
- one slice onion
- yolks of three eggs
- salt
- pepper
- four tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Bring to the boil two tablespoons each of vinegar and water.
2. Simmer in it for ten minutes a slice of onion.
3. Take out the onion and add the yolks of three eggs beaten very light.
4. Take from the fire, add salt and pepper to season, and four tablespoons of butter beaten to a cream, and added slowly.

title: Beef and Beans
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of brisket of beef
- water
- one quartern of French beans cut small
- two onions cut in quarters
- pepper
- salt
- a dessert-spoonful of flour
- one dessert-spoonful of coarse brown sugar
- a large tea-cup full of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a piece of brisket of beef, cover it with water
2. when boiling skim off the fat
3. add one quartern of French beans cut small, two onions cut in quarters
4. season with pepper and salt
5. when nearly done take a dessert-spoonful of flour, one of coarse brown sugar, and a large tea-cup full of vinegar
6. mix them together and stir in with the beans
7. continue stewing for about half an hour longer

title: Beef Collops
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices from any tender part of beef
- flour
- clarified fat
- good stock
- pepper
- salt
- pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices off from any tender part
2. Divide them into pieces of the size of a wine biscuit
3. Flatten and flour them
4. Lightly fry in clarified fat
5. Lay them in a stew-pan with good stock
6. Season to taste
7. Chop pickled gherkins small and add to the gravy a few minutes before serving

title: Beef Dripping Paste
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of clarified dripping
- one pound of flour
- water

## Directions

1. Mix half a pound of clarified dripping into one pound of flour
2. Work it into a paste with water
3. Roll out twice

title: Beef Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of chopped beef
- three egg yolks
- three tablespoons of parsley
- three tablespoons of melted chicken-fat
- four heaping tablespoons of soft bread crumbs
- one-half teaspoon of kitchen bouquet
- two teaspoons of lemon juice
- grated peel of one lemon
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of onion-juice
- one teaspoon of pepper
- one-fourth cup of melted chicken-fat
- one-half cup of boiling water

## Directions

1. To two pounds of chopped beef take three egg yolks, three tablespoons of parsley, three tablespoons of melted chicken-fat, four heaping tablespoons of soft bread crumbs, one-half teaspoon of kitchen bouquet, two teaspoons of lemon juice, grated peel of one lemon, one teaspoon of salt, one-half teaspoon of onion-juice and one teaspoon of pepper.
2. Mix together.
3. Bake twenty-five minutes in a quick oven with one-fourth cup of melted chicken-fat and one-half cup of boiling water.
4. Baste often.

title: Beef Ragout
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small well cut piece of lean beef
- the fat of smoked beef
- good gravy
- allspice
- cloves
- pepper
- salt
- a glass of port wine
- the juice of a lemon
- half a tea-spoonful of cayenne pepper
- a little mushroom ketchup
- forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Take a small well cut piece of lean beef
2. Lard it with the fat of smoked beef
3. Stew it with good gravy, highly seasoned with allspice, cloves, pepper and salt
4. When the meat is well done, remove it from the gravy
5. Skim the gravy carefully and free from every particle of fat
6. Add to it a glass of port wine, the juice of a lemon, half a tea-spoonful of cayenne pepper, and a little mushroom ketchup
7. Glaze the beef when required to have an elegant appearance
8. Poach a few very small forcemeat balls in the gravy
9. Pour the gravy over the meat and arrange the balls round the dish

title: Beef Tea
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- water
- salt
- lemon peel
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut one pound of fleshy beef in dice, or thin slices
2. Simmer for a short time without water, to extract the juices
3. Then add, by degrees, one quart of water, a little salt, a piece of lemon peel, and a sprig of parsley

title: Beef with Celery, White Beans and Peas
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of dried white peas
- half a pint of dried white beans
- a nice piece of brisket of about eight pounds weight
- three heads of celery
- water
- pepper
- salt
- four large lumps of sugar
- nearly a tea-cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the peas and beans for twelve hours, or longer if very dry
2. Put the brisket in a stew-pan with the peas and beans, and three heads of celery cut in small pieces
3. Add water enough to cover
4. Season with pepper and salt only
5. Let it all stew slowly till the meat is extremely tender and the peas and beans quite soft
6. Add four large lumps of sugar and nearly a tea-cup of vinegar

title: Beefsteak Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of chuck steak
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- juice of a lemon
- forcemeat balls
- potatoes
- four or five large floury potatoes
- chopped parsley
- melted fat
- two well-beaten eggs
- yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Cut up two pounds of chuck steak
2. Put it on to stew with salt, pepper and a little nutmeg and the juice of a lemon
3. Cook a few forcemeat balls, made very small, and a few potatoes cut in small pieces
4. Boil four or five large floury potatoes; when done, strain and mash with salt and pepper, a little chopped parsley and a little melted fat
5. Mix it with two well-beaten eggs
6. Put a layer of it around the bottom and sides of a deep pie-dish
7. Lay in the stew, cover with the balance of the potato
8. Brush it over with the yolk of an egg and bake in a quick oven till brown

title: Beer Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of beer
- one cup of water
- salt
- cinnamon
- two egg yolks
- a little sugar
- flour
- one cup of milk
- whites of two eggs
- croutons

## Directions

1. To one pint of beer add one cup of water, let come to a boil
2. season with salt and cinnamon if desired
3. Beat two egg yolks well with a little sugar and flour mixed
4. add one cup of milk, stir until smooth
5. stir all together in the hot beer mixture
6. let come almost to the boiling point
7. fold in the beaten whites of the two eggs and serve at once with croutons

title: Beer Soup (Parve)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beer
- water
- sugar
- grated crust of stale rye bread
- stick cinnamon
- lemon juice
- small pieces of zwieback (rusk)
- a handful of dried currants

## Directions

1. Mix the beer with one-third water, boil with sugar and the grated crust of stale rye bread, add stick cinnamon and a little lemon juice.
2. Pour over small pieces of zwieback (rusk).
3. Some boil a handful of dried currants.
4. When done add both currants and juice.

title: Beer Soup (Parve)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beer
- water
- sugar
- grated crust of stale rye bread
- stick cinnamon
- lemon juice
- zwieback

## Directions

1. Mix the beer with one-third water
2. boil with sugar and the grated crust of stale rye bread
3. add stick cinnamon and a little lemon juice
4. Pour over small pieces of zwieback (rusk)

title: Beet and Cauliflower Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beets
- cold cooked potatoes
- cold cooked cauliflower
- chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one level teaspoon mustard
- one teaspoon anchovy sauce
- one tablespoon milk or cream
- one dessertspoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Take some thin slices of cooked beets, some cold cooked potatoes, some cold cooked cauliflower, and a little chopped parsley. Put one level teaspoon of mustard, one teaspoon anchovy sauce, one tablespoon of milk or cream, and one dessertspoon of vinegar. Mix the mustard with the anchovy, then add the milk, and lastly the vinegar. Pour over the vegetables and add salt and pepper to taste.

title: Beet and Horseradish Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of cold, boiled beets
- one-half cup of grated horseradish
- one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
- one teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons of sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take three cups of cold, boiled beets, grate and add one-half cup of grated horseradish
2. Season with one-quarter teaspoon of pepper, one teaspoon of salt and two tablespoons of sugar
3. Add all the vinegar the horseradish and beets will absorb, and place in covered jar or glass and it is ready for use

title: Beet Greens
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beet greens
- one teaspoon of salt for every two quarts of greens
- boiling water
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Examine the leaves carefully to be sure that there are no insects on them.
2. Wash thoroughly in several waters.
3. Put over the fire in a large kettle of boiling water.
4. Add one teaspoon of salt for every two quarts of greens.
5. Boil rapidly about thirty minutes or until tender.
6. Drain off the water.
7. Chop well and season with butter and salt.

title: Beet Preserves (Russian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- water
- one pound of honey
- one pound of sugar
- white ginger
- one-quarter pound of almonds

## Directions

1. Cut beets in strips like noodles
2. Wash
3. Cook in water one-half hour
4. To three pounds of beets take one pound of honey and one pound of sugar
5. When the beets have cooked on slow fire until white, strain off and add the honey
6. Let cook well and add sugar
7. Cook
8. Add white ginger to taste, stirring continually
9. Add one-quarter pound of almonds, cut in slices
10. One-quarter of an hour before being done, mix
11. When the beets brown put in jars

title: Beet Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon brown sugar
- caraway seeds
- one medium-sized onion
- one-half cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil beets, when tender, skin quickly while hot and slice them into a bowl. Sprinkle salt, pepper, a tablespoon of brown sugar, some caraway seeds, one medium-sized onion in slices, and pour over all one-half cup of vinegar which has been boiled; with a fork mix the hot vinegar through the other ingredients.

title: Beet Soup (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two small beets
- water
- citric acid (sour salt)
- sugar
- salt
- three-quarter cup of sour cream
- sweet cream
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Cut two small beets in strips, cover with water and let cook until tender, add citric acid (sour salt) and a little sugar to make sweet and sour, a little salt, and three-quarter cup of sour cream.
2. Serve cold.
3. Sweet cream may be used and while hot gradually poured over the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, keeping the soup over the stove and stirring all the time until thick and smooth.
4. Remove from stove and serve cold.

title: Beet Soup - Russian Style
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large beet
- one-half pound of onions
- one pound of fat brisket of beef
- water
- three-fourths of a cup of sugar
- citric acid
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut one large beet and one-half pound of onions in thick pieces
2. Put in kettle with one pound of fat brisket of beef
3. Cover with water and let cook slowly two hours
4. Add three-fourths of a cup of sugar and a little citric acid to make it sweet and sour
5. Let cook another hour
6. Season and serve hot

title: Belgian Red Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three sticks of cinnamon
- salt
- pepper
- one-half teaspoon cloves
- one onion
- one bay leaf
- two cups of water
- three tablespoons of drippings
- five or six greening apples
- one medium-sized red cabbage
- two tablespoons sugar
- two tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Put two or three sticks of cinnamon, salt and pepper, one-half teaspoon cloves, one onion sliced thin, one bay leaf, two cups of water, three tablespoons of drippings in saucepan.
2. Add five or six greening apples, peeled and cut in quarters.
3. Put in one medium-sized red cabbage, cut in halves and then sliced very thin.
4. Cook three hours and then add two tablespoons each of sugar and vinegar; cook one minute more.

title: Bell Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup sweet milk
- three cups sifted flour
- three teaspoons baking-powder

## Directions

1. Stir three eggs until very light
2. Stir in one cup of sweet milk
3. Sift in three cups sifted flour
4. Beat for ten minutes
5. Add three teaspoons of baking-powder
6. Fry by spoonfuls in hot oil
7. Serve with any sweet sauce

title: Beolas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- fine meal
- boiling olive oil or fat
- water
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take six eggs
2. Beat them until very light
3. Add a little fine meal, just enough to give it consistency
4. Drop this from the point of a spoon into boiling olive oil or fat
5. When light brown, take out, and drain
6. Serve cold with a syrup made of water, cinnamon and sugar

title: Berliner Pfannkuchen (Purim Krapfen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of flour
- a pinch of salt
- one cup of lukewarm milk
- three-fourths cake of compressed yeast
- warm water
- sugar
- one-half cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs
- five cups of sifted flour
- grated peel of a lemon
- one egg
- one-half teaspoon of marmalade, jam or jelly
- rendered butter or vegetable oil
- pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Take one and one-half cups of flour, a pinch of salt sifted into a deep bowl, one cup of lukewarm milk and three-fourths cake of compressed yeast which has been dissolved in a little warm water and sugar
2. Stir into a dough
3. cover with a towel and set away in a warm place to rise
4. When well risen, take one-half cup of butter, one cup of sugar, a little salt and rub to a cream
5. Add two eggs well beaten, stir all well and add the risen dough, one teaspoon of salt and work in gradually five cups of sifted flour and the grated peel of a lemon
6. Stir the dough till it blisters and leaves the dish perfectly clean at the sides
7. Let the dough rise slowly for about two hours
8. Take a large baking-board, flour well and roll out the dough on it as thin as a double thickness of pasteboard
9. When it is all rolled out, cut with a round cutter the size of a tumbler
10. When all the dough has been cut out, beat up an egg
11. Spread the beaten egg on the edge of each cake (spread only a few at a time for they would get too dry if all were done at once)
12. Then put one-half teaspoon of marmalade, jam or jelly on the cake
13. Put another cake on top of one already spread, having cut it with a cutter a little bit smaller than the one used in the first place
14. This makes them stick better and prevents the preserves coming out while cooking
15. Set all away on a floured board or pan about two inches apart
16. Spread the top of each cake with melted butter and let them rise from one to two hours
17. When ready to fry, heat at least two pounds of rendered butter or any good vegetable oil in a deep iron kettle
18. Try the butter with a small piece of dough
19. If it rises immediately, put in the doughnuts
20. In putting them in, place the side that is up on the board down in the hot butter
21. Do not crowd them in the kettle as they require room to rise and spread
22. Cover them with a lid
23. In a few seconds uncover
24. If they are light brown, turn them over on the other side but do not cover them again
25. When done they will have a white stripe around the centre
26. Take them up with a perforated skimmer, lay on a large platter, sprinkle with pulverized sugar
27. If the butter gets too hot take from the fire a minute

title: Bird's Nest Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five large tart apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- raisins
- a few pounded almonds
- a little white wine
- grated peel of one lemon
- four eggs
- one cup of pulverized sugar
- one cup of flour

## Directions

1. Pare four or five large tart apples and cut off the top of each apple to use as a cover
2. Scrape out all the inside, being careful not to break the apples
3. Mix scrapings with sugar, cinnamon, raisins, a few pounded almonds and add a little white wine and the grated peel of one lemon
4. Fill up the apples with this mixture and put back the top of each apple, so as to cover each well
5. Grease a deep dish, set in the apples and stew a few minutes
6. Make a sponge cake batter of four eggs, one cup of pulverized sugar, one cup of flour and pour over the apples
7. Bake one-half hour
8. Eat warm or cold, with or without sauce

title: Birmoilis (Turkish)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potatoes
- grated cheese
- well-beaten eggs
- boiling oil
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Take some mashed potatoes, grated cheese, well-beaten eggs; make a good paste
2. Take tablespoonfuls of this mixture and drop in boiling oil
3. Fry until brown
4. Serve with a syrup made of sugar and water

title: Biscuit Tortoni, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of granulated sugar
- one-fourth pound of stale macaroons
- one-half pint of heavy cream
- three eggs
- vanilla or sherry wine

## Directions

1. Stir yolks of eggs until thick and add sugar and stir again
2. Add whipped cream and whipped whites of eggs and grated macaroons
3. Flavor to taste
4. Put this all into freezer and pack outside with ice and salt alternately
5. Do not turn
6. Let stand five or six hours, adding ice from time to time
7. When serving put grated macaroons on top

title: Biscuit Tortoni, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- one pint of cream
- eight macaroons
- vanilla
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half cup of milk

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs and the sugar very light
2. Put on milk to a boil
3. When it comes to a boil stir into the beaten eggs and sugar and set away to cool
4. Beat cream and add macaroons, leaving just enough to put in the bottom of your form
5. When your custard is cool, add cream
6. Put all in forms, pack and freeze two hours or longer

title: Bitki (Russian Hamburger Steak)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of clear beef chopped
- two cups of bread crumbs
- water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take two cups of clear beef chopped and two cups of bread crumbs that have been soaked in a little water, leaving them quite moist.
2. Mix thoroughly with the beef.
3. Season with pepper and salt and shape into individual cakes.
4. Fry as directed for Hamburger Steak.

title: Black Bean Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of beans
- cold water
- two tablespoons of chopped onion
- two tablespoons of butter
- two stalks of celery or a piece of celery root
- two quarts of water
- one-eighth teaspoon of pepper
- one-fourth teaspoon of mustard
- a few grains of cayenne
- one tablespoon of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- two-thirds cup
- one lemon
- two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Soak one pint of beans over night, drain, add cold water and rinse thoroughly
2. Fry two tablespoons of chopped onion in two tablespoons of butter
3. put in with the beans, add two stalks of celery or a piece of celery root and two quarts of water
4. Cook slowly until the beans are soft, three or four hours, add more boiling water as it boils away
5. rub through a strainer
6. add one-eighth teaspoon of pepper, one-fourth teaspoon of mustard, a few grains of cayenne
7. Heat one tablespoon of butter in saucepan with two tablespoons of flour, then two-thirds cup and then the rest of the soup gradually
8. cut a lemon (removing seeds) and two hard-boiled eggs in slices and serve in the soup

title: Black Bread Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- pinch of cloves
- pinch of allspice
- one cup of stale rye bread crumbs
- beaten whites
- one cup of claret or white wine
- sherry wine sauce or whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of three eggs with one cup of sugar
2. Add one teaspoon of cinnamon, pinch of cloves, and pinch of allspice
3. Add one cup of stale rye bread crumbs gradually
4. Mix well and add beaten whites
5. Bake slowly
6. Half an hour before serving, add one cup of claret or white wine
7. Serve with sherry wine sauce or whipped cream

title: Black Bread Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- pinch of cloves
- pinch of allspice
- one cup of stale rye bread crumbs
- beaten whites of three eggs
- one cup of claret or white wine

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of three eggs with one cup of sugar
2. Add one teaspoon of cinnamon, pinch of cloves, and pinch of allspice
3. Add one cup of stale rye bread crumbs gradually
4. Mix well and add beaten whites
5. Bake slowly
6. Half an hour before serving, add one cup of claret or white wine
7. Serve with sherry wine sauce or whipped cream

title: Blackberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Treat the same as for raspberries.

title: Blackberry and Currant Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- blackberries
- currants
- sugar
- flour
- pastry

## Directions

1. Mix as much fruit in a bowl as required, sweeten, stirring the sugar through the berries and currants lightly with a spoon
2. Dust in a little flour and stir it through the fruit
3. Cut one of the pieces of pastry in halves, dust the pastry-board with flour and roll the lump of pastry out very thin
4. cover the pie-plate, a big deep one, with the pastry, trim off the edges with a knife, cutting from you
5. Fill the dish with the fruit, dust the surface well with flour
6. Roll out the other piece for the top crust, fold it over the rolling pin, cut a few gashes in it for a steam vent
7. Carefully put on the top crust, trim it well about the edge of the pie-plate
8. Press it closely together with the end of your thumb or with a pastry knife
9. stand the pie in a moderate oven and bake till the surface is a delicate brown
10. Remove the pie and let it stand until it is cool

title: Blackberry Cordial
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- sugar
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- cloves
- allspice

## Directions

1. Simmer the berries until they break
2. strain and to each quart of juice add one pound of sugar
3. Let this dissolve by heating slowly
4. add one tablespoon each of cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and if desired, allspice
5. Simmer altogether twenty minutes
6. Bottle and seal

title: Blackberry Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- blackberries
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for Currant Jelly.

title: Blackberry Wine
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- boiling water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Measure your berries and bruise them
2. to every gallon add one quart of boiling water
3. let the mixture stand twenty-four hours stirring occasionally
4. strain off all the liquor into a cask
5. to every gallon add two pounds of sugar
6. cork tightly and let stand till the following October

title: Blanc Mange
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- four large tablespoons of cornstarch
- one quarter cup of cold milk
- two whole eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- a tiny pinch of salt
- a piece of butter, size of a hickory nut
- vanilla or lemon

## Directions

1. Heat one quart of milk to boiling point.
2. Dissolve four large tablespoons of cornstarch in a quarter cup of cold milk.
3. Beat two whole eggs with one-half cup of sugar until light, and add a tiny pinch of salt.
4. When the milk begins to boil, add a piece of butter, size of a hickory nut, then pour it over the well-beaten eggs and sugar, mix well, and put back on the stove.
5. Stir until it begins to boil, then stir in the dissolved cornstarch until the custard is very thick.
6. Remove from the fire, flavor with vanilla or lemon, pour into a mold, and set on ice till very cold and firm.
7. Serve with cream.

title: Blancmange
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of milk
- half an ounce of fine isinglass
- a handful of beaten almonds
- two or three bitter almonds
- a couple of bay leaves
- a piece of lemon peel
- four ounces of white sugar

## Directions

1. Add half an ounce of fine isinglass to a quart of milk
2. Add a handful of beaten almonds, two or three bitter almonds, a couple of bay leaves, and a piece of lemon peel
3. When the isinglass is dissolved, strain the milk into a basin
4. Sweeten with four ounces of white sugar
5. Pour into a mould

title: Blancmange of Ground Rice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk or almond milk
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- a little cinnamon
- 2 oz. of finely pounded almonds
- 1/2 lb. of ground rice
- a little cold milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
- oil
- raspberry or strawberry jam
- a few cut blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart of milk or almond milk, 1 lb. of sifted sugar, a little cinnamon, 2 oz. of finely pounded almonds
2. Set it at the side, when it boils mix in a basin 1/2 lb. of ground rice
3. Mix it stiff with a little cold milk and 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
4. Stir it into the boiling mixture
5. Let it boil till it is quite thick, keep stirring it all the time to prevent its burning
6. Have a pretty earthenware mould, slightly grease it with the oil, turn the mixture in
7. Set it in a very cold place
8. When ready, turn it out on a glass dish, and put round it some raspberry or strawberry jam
9. Stick it with a few cut blanched almonds
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Blanquette of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal or poultry
- a handful of fresh well cleaned button mushrooms
- a little veal gravy
- a piece of clarified veal fat about the size of the yolk of a hard boiled egg
- a piece of lemon peel
- very little white pepper and salt
- one small lump of white sugar
- a little nutmeg
- veal gravy
- a little lemon juice
- the beaten yolks of two eggs
- mashed potatoes
- a few truffles

## Directions

1. Cut into thin pieces of the size of shillings and half crowns, cold veal or poultry
2. Lay it in a small saucepan with a handful of fresh well cleaned button mushrooms
3. Pour over a little veal gravy, only enough to cover them, with a piece of clarified veal fat about the size of the yolk of a hard boiled egg
4. Flavor with a piece of lemon peel, very little white pepper and salt, one small lump of white sugar, and a little nutmeg
5. Stew all together for fifteen minutes
6. Pour over a sauce prepared in a separate saucepan, made with veal gravy, a little lemon juice, but not much, and the beaten yolks of two eggs
7. Let it simmer for an instant and then serve it up in the centre of a dish prepared with a wall of mashed potatoes, delicately browned
8. A few truffles renders this dish more elegant

title: Blintzes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg
- salt
- water
- oil or fat
- force meat
- one-half pound apples
- one ounce ground sweet almonds
- one ounce powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- juice of one-half lemon
- egg white

## Directions

1. Make dough as directed for cheese blintzes
2. Prepare filling of force meat, highly seasoned, or filling of one-half pound of apples peeled and cored then minced with one ounce of ground sweet almonds, one ounce of powdered sugar, a pinch of cinnamon, juice of one-half lemon
3. Mix well and bind with the beaten white of egg
4. Spread either of these mixtures on the dough
5. Fold over and tuck edges in well
6. Fry in plenty of oil or fat
7. Sprinkle those containing the fruit mixture with sugar and cinnamon
8. May be served either hot or cold

title: Blitz Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of powdered sugar
- one-half cup of butter
- one cup of pastry flour
- one-quarter of a teaspoon of baking-powder
- peel and juice of one lemon
- five or six eggs

## Directions

1. Beat sugar with two whole eggs
2. add butter, beat until foamy
3. add the flour mixed with baking-powder, lemon and four yolks
4. Last the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
5. Mix this well, bake in form in a moderately hot oven

title: Blueberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- blueberries
- powdered sugar
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Wash and pick over carefully, drain off all the water.
2. Sprinkle powdered sugar over them and serve with cream or milk.

title: Blueberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve quarts of berries
- one quart of sugar
- one pint of water

## Directions

1. Put water, berries, and sugar in preserving kettle; heat slowly.
2. Boil sixteen minutes, counting from the time the contents of the kettle begins to bubble.

title: Bohemian Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of double cream
- one pint of grape juice or grape jelly melted
- lady fingers

## Directions

1. Stir together and whip one pint of double cream and one pint of grape juice or grape jelly melted, this must be whipped to a froth.
2. Drain if needed.
3. Put in cups and set on ice for several hours.
4. Serve with lady fingers.

title: Bohemian Potato Puff
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- flour
- drippings or goose-fat
- onions

## Directions

1. Pare, wash and boil potatoes until soft enough to mash well.
2. Drain off nearly all the water, leaving just a little; add one teaspoon of salt and return to the stove.
3. Sift one-half cup of flour into the potatoes after returning to the fire and keep covered closely for about five minutes.
4. Remove from the stove and mash them as hard as you can, so as not to have any lumps.
5. Put about two tablespoons of drippings or goose-fat in a spider, chop up some onions very fine and heat them until they become a light brown.
6. Take a tablespoon and dip it in the hot fat, and then cut a spoonful of the potato dough with the same spoon and put it in the spider, and so on until you have used all.
7. Be careful to dip your spoon in the hot fat every time you cut a puff.
8. Let them brown slightly.

title: Bohemian Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- crisp Romaine or lettuce
- hard-boiled eggs
- boiled beets
- onion
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of the salad bowl with crisp Romaine or lettuce; arrange over the top alternate slices of hard-boiled eggs and boiled beets. Sprinkle with finely chopped onion, cover with French dressing, toss and serve.

title: Boil Chestnuts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- water
- 1/2 an oz. of aniseed

## Directions

1. Put them in a small saucepan covered with water
2. Tie in a piece of muslin 1/2 an oz. of aniseed
3. Boil with the chestnuts for 15 minutes
4. Hang up the aniseed bag for use several times
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Apple Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- salt
- two cups of milk
- one cup of flour
- a second cup of flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- pared apples
- sugar
- pudding cloths
- hard sauce

## Directions

1. Beat well, without separating, two eggs, add a pinch of salt, two cups of milk and one cup of flour
2. To a second cup of flour, add two teaspoons of baking powder; add this to the batter and as much more flour as is necessary to make a soft dough
3. Roll out quickly one-half inch thick
4. Cut into squares, lay two or three quarters of pared apples on each, sprinkle with sugar and pinch the dough around the apples
5. Have a number of pudding cloths ready, wrung out of cold water, and sprinkle well with flour
6. Put a dumpling in each, leave a little room for swelling and tie tightly
7. Drop into a kettle of rapidly boiling water and keep the water at a steady boil for an hour
8. Serve hot with hard sauce

title: Boiled Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small, young string beans
- salt water

## Directions

1. Select small, young string beans, string them carefully
2. Boil in salt water, in a brass kettle, until tender
3. Throw them on a large, clean board to drip
4. Next morning press them into a jar, with alternate layers of salt and beans

title: Boiled Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. of boned brisket or double flank
- 1 quart of water
- salt
- pepper
- a blade of mace
- a tooth of garlic
- 2 carrots

## Directions

1. Put a nice piece of boned brisket, or double flank of 4 lbs., in a quart of water, some salt, pepper, a blade of mace, a tooth of garlic.
2. Boil 2 carrots with to garnish, let it boil fast, skim it, then let it simmer for 2 hours.
3. Dish it up, cut the carrots round the meat, put a spoonful of the liquor over to make it look moist.
4. Serve it up with horseradish or tomato sauce in the sauce tureen.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Beets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- boiling water
- vinegar
- white sauce or melted butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Carefully wash any earth off the beets, but every care is needed to avoid breaking the skin, roots or crown.
2. Lay them in plenty of boiling water, with a little vinegar.
3. Boil them steadily, keeping them well covered with water for about one and one-half to two hours for small beets and two to three and one-half hours for large ones.
4. If they are to be served hot, cut off the roots and crown and rub off the skin directly, but if to be served cold, leave them until they have become cold and then cut into thin slices and sprinkle with salt and pepper and pour some vinegar over them.
5. If to be eaten hot, cut them into thin slices, arrange them on a hot vegetable dish and pour over white sauce or melted butter, or hand these separately.

title: Boiled Carp with Caper Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- oil
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- caper sauce
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the carp well and slit it open
2. Dry it well
3. Lay it in a little oil and the juice of a lemon, with salt and pepper, for an hour before cooking it
4. Then put it on the gridiron before a slow and clear fire
5. Cook well through when broiled on both sides
6. Serve it with caper sauce in which put 2 teaspoonfuls of anchovy sauce
7. Put the sauce over the fish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Celery Root Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- celery roots
- salt
- sugar
- white pepper
- white wine vinegar
- raisins
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Pare and wash the celery roots (they should be the size of large potatoes), put on to boil in a little salted water, and when tender remove from the water and set away until cool. Cut in slices about an eighth of an inch thick; sprinkle each slice with fine salt, sugar and white pepper; pour enough white wine vinegar over the salad to cover. A few large raisins boiled will add to the appearance of this salad. Serve cold in a salad bowl, lined with fresh lettuce leaves.

title: Boiled Chestnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- salt
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the chestnuts a few minutes
2. Drain and remove the shells and skins
3. Boil again until tender, adding sufficient salt to make them palatable
4. Drain again
5. Shake over the fire until dry
6. Cover with cream sauce and serve at once

title: Boiled Chicken, Baked
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- old hen
- chicken-fat
- onion
- garlic
- salt
- paprika
- soft bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make chicken soup with an old hen.
2. Remove chicken from soup just as soon as tender.
3. Place in roasting-pan with three tablespoons of chicken-fat, one onion sliced, one clove of garlic, one-half teaspoon each of salt and paprika.
4. Sprinkle with soft bread crumbs.
5. Baste frequently and when sufficiently browned, cut in pieces for serving.
6. Place on platter with the strained gravy pour over the chicken and serve.

title: Boiled Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put the cod in a fish kettle with cold water and a handful of salt
2. Let it boil fast at first, then draw it to the side
3. Time must be according to the size of the fish
4. When done lift the steamer across the kettle with a cloth to keep it warm
5. Lay it on a fish napkin, garnished with parsley
6. Serve with caper sauce or sauce No. 33
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- cold water
- salt
- parsley
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Put the cod in a fish kettle with cold water and a handful of salt
2. Let it boil fast at first, then draw it to the side
3. The time must be according to the size of the fish
4. When done lift the steamer across the kettle with a cloth to keep it warm
5. Lay it on a fish napkin garnished with parsley
6. Serve with caper sauce or sauce No. 33
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon coffee per cup of boiling water
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- clean egg shells
- 1 tablespoon cold water for settling
- cream or scalded milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix coffee with two tablespoons of cold water
2. Clean egg shells and put in the pot
3. Allow this to come to a boil and add boiling water
4. Bring to a boil and boil for one minute
5. Add a tablespoon of cold water to assist the grounds in settling
6. Stand the pot where it will keep hot, but not boil, for five minutes
7. Serve at once
8. Fill the cup one-fourth full of hot scalded milk
9. Pour on the freshly made coffee, adding sugar

title: Boiled Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoon of coffee per cup
- Boiling water
- Two tablespoons of cold water
- Egg shells

## Directions

1. Allow one tablespoon of coffee to each cup of boiling water
2. Mix coffee with two tablespoons of cold water
3. Clean egg shells and put in the pot
4. Allow this to come to a boil and add boiling water
5. Bring to a boil and boil for one minute
6. Add a tablespoon of cold water to assist the grounds in settling
7. Stand the pot where it will keep hot, but not boil, for five minutes
8. Serve at once, as coffee allowed to stand becomes flat and loses its aroma

title: Boiled Corned Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- corned beef
- cold water
- soup vegetables

## Directions

1. Put corned beef into cold water using enough to cover it well
2. Let it come slowly to the boiling-point then place where it will simmer only
3. Allow thirty minutes or more to each pound
4. It is improved by adding a few soup vegetables the last hour of cooking
5. If the piece can be used a second time, trim it to good shape
6. Place it again in the water in which it was boiled, let it get heated through
7. Set aside to cool in the water under pressure, a plate or deep dish holding a flat-iron being set on top of the meat
8. When cooled under pressure the meat is more firm and cuts better into slices

title: Boiled Custard
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of milk
- lemon-peel
- bay leaf
- yolks of four eggs
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Let a pint of milk simmer in a very clean saucepan
2. Flavor it with lemon-peel and a bay leaf
3. Sweeten to taste
4. Strain the milk
5. While gently boiling, add the beaten yolks of four eggs and the whites of two
6. Continue stirring until the custard thickens
7. Remove from the fire
8. Stir it until it cools

title: Boiled Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- two eggs or the yolks of three eggs
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the milk on to heat in a double boiler.
2. Beat the eggs thoroughly with the sugar.
3. Into them pour the hot milk, stirring to prevent lumps.
4. Return all to the double boiler and cook until the custard coats the spoon, but no longer.
5. If the mixture should curdle, set the boiler in a pan of cold water and beat with a wire egg-beater until smooth.
6. When the steam passes off add the vanilla or other flavoring.

title: Boiled Custards
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of almond milk or plain milk
- 2 oz. of powdered sugar
- 2 laurel leaves or a little piece of vanille or orange-flower water
- 4 eggs
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pint of almond milk or plain milk with 2 oz. of powdered sugar, 2 laurel leaves or a little piece of vanille or orange-flower water
2. beat in a basin 4 eggs
3. stir the boiling milk into the eggs
4. put it in a jug in a saucepan of boiling water
5. keep stirring until it thickens, do not let it boil
6. pour it into custard glasses or cups
7. put a little ground cinnamon on each
8. stand them in a dish for table
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon salt
- one tablespoon mustard
- one tablespoon sugar
- one tablespoon flour
- cayenne
- three eggs
- one-half cup vinegar
- two tablespoons melted butter
- one-half cup cream or milk

## Directions

1. Mix one teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of mustard, one tablespoon of sugar, one tablespoon of flour and a few grains of cayenne. Beat three eggs until lemon-colored and add the dry ingredients with one-half cup of vinegar and two tablespoons of melted butter. Cook over boiling water until thick; strain, add one-half cup of cream or milk. Beat until smooth, and cool.

title: Boiled Dressing with Olive Oil (Parve)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- two tablespoons olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- cayenne pepper
- one-half cup vinegar
- one teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three whole eggs until very light, add two tablespoons of olive oil, stirring constantly, add a good pinch of salt, pepper, mustard and cayenne pepper. Heat one-half cup of vinegar with one teaspoon of sugar in it, stir while hot into the eggs and put it back on the stove in a double boiler or over hot water in another saucepan and stir until thick. Serve cold.

title: Boiled Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling water

## Directions

1. For soft-boiled eggs: drop carefully into boiling water and boil three minutes, or place in a covered vessel of boiling water and allow to stand in a warm place (but not on the stove) for ten minutes
2. For hard-boiled eggs: cook in boiling water for fifteen or twenty minutes and then drop in cold water to prevent the yolk from turning dark

title: Boiled Fish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- 4 yolks of eggs
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- ½ pint of the liquor the fish is cooked in
- juice of ½ a lemon
- chopped parsley
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. For the cooking of fish you must refer to the portion devoted to that cookery
2. Beat up eggs for a good sauce, you will require 4 yolks of eggs
3. Season it with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
4. Boil ½ pint of the liquor the fish is cooked in, and the juice of ½ a lemon
5. Have some chopped parsley in the sauce boat
6. When the sauce has thickened, have ready the white of one egg whipt to a snow
7. This will give it a creamy flavour
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- vinegar
- onion
- celery head
- parsley roots

## Directions

1. Allow ten minutes to the first pound and five minutes for each additional pound
2. Set fish on to boil in hot water from the teakettle, adding salt and a dash of vinegar to keep the meat firm
3. Add an onion, a head of celery and parsley roots for flavor
4. If serving the fish whole, tie it in a napkin and lay it on an old plate at the bottom of the kettle
5. Fish is done when a fin pulled out comes easily and the meat is an opaque white
6. Avoid using too much water

title: Boiled Flour Balls with Almonds
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two yolks of eggs
- a pinch of salt
- pepper
- finely-chopped parsley
- six blanched almonds grated
- sifted flour
- whites of eggs
- one-half teaspoon of baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat two yolks of eggs very light with a pinch of salt, pepper and finely-chopped parsley
2. Add six blanched almonds grated and enough sifted flour to make stiff batter
3. Add the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs and one-half teaspoon of baking powder
4. Drop by teaspoons in soup ten minutes before serving

title: Boiled Flour Balls with Almonds
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two yolks of eggs
- salt
- pepper
- finely-chopped parsley
- six blanched almonds
- sifted flour
- whites of eggs
- one-half teaspoon of baking

## Directions

1. Two yolks of eggs beaten very light, and a pinch of salt, pepper and finely-chopped parsley.
2. Add six blanched almonds grated, enough sifted flour to make stiff batter, then add the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs and one-half teaspoon of baking.

title: Boiled Fowls
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- white sauce
- white cauliflower or vegetable marrow
- white legged poultry

## Directions

1. Boil white legged poultry
2. Serve with a fine white sauce
3. Garnish with pieces of white cauliflower or vegetable marrow

title: Boiled Haddocks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddocks
- salt
- lemon
- parsley
- caper, anchovy, or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Wash haddocks well inside and out
2. Put them on in cold water with salt
3. Let them boil for half an hour
4. Let them drain well on the strainer before dishing them up on a fish napkin
5. Garnish with slices of lemon and parsley
6. Serve with caper, anchovy, or tomato sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one-third cup of boiling water
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. Pour water on sugar until dissolved
2. Heat slowly to boiling point without stirring
3. Boil until syrup will thread when dropped from tip of spoon
4. As soon as it threads, pour slowly over beaten white
5. Beat with heavy wire spoon until of proper consistency to spread
6. Flavor

title: Boiled Lettuce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five heads of lettuce
- salt
- boiling salted water
- butter
- salt and pepper
- white sauce
- grated nutmeg
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Wash four or five heads of lettuce, carefully removing thick, bitter stalks and retaining all sound leaves
2. Cook in plenty of boiling salted water for ten or fifteen minutes
3. Blanch in cold water for a minute or two
4. Drain and chop lightly, and heat in stew-pan with some butter, and salt and pepper to taste
5. If preferred, the chopped lettuce may be heated with a pint of white sauce seasoned with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
6. After simmering for a few minutes in the sauce, draw to a cooler part of the range and stir in the well-beaten yolks of two eggs

title: Boiled Macaroni
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- tomato or cream sauce
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into small pieces
2. Boil for half an hour
3. Drain and blanch in cold water
4. Reheat in tomato or cream sauce and serve
5. Grated cheese may be sprinkled over the dish if desired

title: Boiled Mackerel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- salt
- fennel
- fennel sauce
- apple sauce or gooseberry sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the mackerel well
2. put them on in cold water with salt
3. let them boil very gently
4. dish them on a fish napkin
5. garnish with fennel
6. serve with fennel sauce, and also a little apple or gooseberry sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Mackerel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- salt
- fennel

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the mackerel well
2. Put them on in cold water with salt
3. Let them boil very gently, as it makes the flesh hard
4. Dish them on a fish napkin
5. Garnish with fennel
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- half a shoulder, the knuckle part, or 3 lb. of the best end of the neck
- an onion
- salt
- pepper
- a little nutmeg
- 2 or 3 turnips
- a white cauliflower

## Directions

1. Put it in the saucepan with an onion, salt, pepper, a little nutmeg.
2. Boil with 2 or 3 turnips to garnish, or boil in another saucepan a white cauliflower to garnish.
3. Serve with caper sauce in a sauce tureen made from recipes.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Okra
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- young okra pods
- boiling salted water
- cream
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- tomatoes
- onion
- lemon juice
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Wash and cut off the ends of young pods
2. cover with boiling salted water and cook about twenty minutes, until tender
3. Drain
4. add cream (a scant cup to a quart of okra), a tablespoon of butter, and salt and pepper to taste
5. Another way of stewing is to cook it with tomatoes. To a pint of okra pods, washed and sliced, allow a dozen ripe tomatoes, peeled and sliced, and one medium-sized onion
6. Stew slowly for an hour, adding one tablespoon of butter, a scant teaspoon of salt and pepper to season
7. In the West Indies lemon juice and cayenne are also added to stewed okra

title: Boiled Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- cold water
- salt
- boiling water
- pepper
- butter
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and cut off the roots
2. drop each into cold water as soon as it is peeled
3. When all are ready, drain and put in a saucepan well covered with boiling water, adding a teaspoon of salt for every quart of water
4. Boil rapidly for ten minutes with the cover partly off
5. drain and return to the fire with fresh water
6. Simmer until tender
7. add pepper and butter and serve, or omit the butter and pepper and pour a cream sauce over the onions

title: Boiled Plaice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- caper sauce or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. A very large plaice can be dressed as boiled turbot
2. Send it up just the same, with caper, anchovy, or No. 33 sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Potato Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half cup of blanched and pounded almonds
- grated peel of one lemon
- juice of one lemon
- one-half pound of grated potatoes that have been boiled the day before
- beaten egg whites
- salt

## Directions

1. Stir the yolks of four eggs with one-half cup of sugar.
2. Add one-half cup of blanched and pounded almonds.
3. Grate in the peel, also the juice of one lemon.
4. Add one-half pound of grated potatoes that have been boiled the day before.
5. Lastly add the stiffly beaten whites, some salt and more potatoes, if necessary.
6. Grease your pudding-pan well, pour in the mixture and bake.
7. Set in a pan of water in oven; water in pan must not reach higher than one-half way up the pudding-form.
8. Bake one-half hour.
9. Turn out on platter and serve with a wine, chocolate, or lemon sauce.

title: Boiled Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight potatoes
- boiling water
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Peel six or eight potatoes
2. Put them on in boiling water to which has been added one teaspoon of salt
3. Boil until a fork easily pierces them, about twenty-five to thirty minutes

title: Boiled Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rice
- cold water
- two quarts of water
- one tablespoon of salt
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put one-half cup of rice in a strainer; place the strainer over a bowl nearly full of cold water
2. Rub the rice; lift the strainer from the bowl and change the water
3. Repeat this until the water in the bowl is clear
4. Have two quarts of water boiling briskly, add the rice and one tablespoon of salt gradually so as not to stop the boiling
5. Boil twenty minutes or until soft, do not stir
6. Drain through a colander and place the colander over boiling water for ten minutes to steam
7. Every grain will be distinct
8. Serve as a vegetable or as a cereal with cream and sugar

title: Boiled Rice With Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- pineapple
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil as much rice as desired
2. When done slice up the pineapple and add, with as much sugar as is required to sweeten to taste

title: Boiled Salmon Trout
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon trout
- salt
- fennel
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the salmon trout in boiling water with a good spoonful of salt, no vinegar
2. When it is done, drain it well
3. Serve it up on a fish napkin, garnished with fennel
4. Serve with anchovy sauce or the sauce made expressly for fish No. 33
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Salt Beef with Carrots and Greens
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of salt beef
- two or three carrots
- a cabbage or some greens such as Scotch kale
- a few plain suet dumplings
- ½ lb. of suet
- ¼ lb. flour
- a pinch of salt
- a little cold water

## Directions

1. Put it in a deep saucepan with two or three carrots, let it boil quickly, skim it, then let it simmer at the side.
2. Half an hour before dinner have a cabbage or some greens well washed, such as Scotch kale, let them drain well from the water.
3. Have a few plain suet dumplings boiled with also, they will only take half-an-hour.
4. To 3 lbs. of meat put 3 pints of water.
5. Dish up the meat in a good sized dish, put the greens and cabbage round, with two or three spoonfuls of the liquor.
6. For the dumplings: mix ½ lb. suet, ¼ lb. flour, a pinch of salt, with a little cold water, make them into four dumplings.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Salt Mackerel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- cold water
- hot melted butter
- hot potatoes

## Directions

1. Soak mackerel over night in cold water, with the skin side up, that the salt may be drawn out.
2. Change the water often, and less time is required.
3. Drain.
4. Place mackerel in shallow kettle, pour water over to cover and boil ten to fifteen minutes or until flesh separates from the bone.
5. Remove to platter and pour hot, melted butter over and serve with hot potatoes.

title: Boiled Sauerkraut
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brisket of beef weighing about two or three pounds
- two quarts water
- salt
- soup greens
- one cup soup stock
- sauerkraut
- one tablespoon caraway seed
- two raw potatoes
- one tablespoon brown sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take brisket of beef weighing about two or three pounds. Set it on to boil in two quarts of water, a little salt and the usual soup greens.
2. When the meat is tender take it out, salt it well and put on to boil again in a porcelain-lined kettle, having previously removed all the bones.
3. Add about a cup of the soup stock and as much sauerkraut as you desire.
4. Boil about one hour; tie one tablespoon of caraway seed in a bag and boil in with the kraut.
5. Thicken with two raw potatoes, grated, and add one tablespoon of brown sugar just before serving.
6. If not sour enough add a dash of vinegar.

title: Boiled Sheep's Head
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 sheep's head, well trimmed and slit in halves
- turnip
- carrot
- celery
- onions
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- 1 blade of mace
- 3 pints water
- 1/4 lb. rice
- dried saffron
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- good dripping or soup fat
- 1 egg yolk
- vinegar
- parsley
- boiled turnips

## Directions

1. Put sheep's head in a deep saucepan with turnip, carrot, celery, onions, and a little garlic
2. Season with salt, pepper, marjoram, and a blade of mace
3. Add 3 pints of water
4. Wash 1/4 lb. of rice and put it in with a little dried saffron to colour
5. Let it cook gently for 2 hours
6. Dish up the sheep's head
7. Turn the soup in the tureen
8. In a small saucepan, rub a tablespoonful of flour with a little good dripping or soup fat
9. Add salt, pepper, and the yolk of an egg
10. Rub all till smooth
11. Stir in a little of the clear soup and flavor with a spoonful of vinegar
12. Let it boil until like melted butter
13. Chop some parsley and put in a sauce boat
14. Turn the boiling sauce over the parsley
15. Put round the head some boiled turnips cut in halves
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Slices of Crimped Salmon with Anchovy Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- crimped salmon slices
- anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the slices quickly in boiling water, 15 minutes is enough
2. If you leave it longer in the water it will lose colour
3. Dish it up with anchovy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Smoked Beef with Broad Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of smoked beef
- ½ peck of broad beans

## Directions

1. Soak it first for an hour, put it in a saucepan, only half covering it, let it boil and skim it, then let it cook very gently according to its weight.
2. Have ready picked ½ peck of the beans, put them in with the meat.
3. Dish up the beef, strain the beans into a colander, put them round the beef.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Soles with Montpelier Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- water
- Montpelier butter

## Directions

1. Boil the soles as directed in To Boil Soles
2. Make the Montpelier butter according to recipe
3. Rub it well through a sieve
4. When you use the butter you must not put the fish upon a napkin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Sweet Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Put on in boiling water, without any salt, and boil until a fork will easily pierce the largest.
2. Drain off the water and dry.

title: Boiled Tongue, Sweet and Sour
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh tongue
- cold water
- salt
- one tablespoon of drippings
- goose fat
- onion
- one tablespoon of flour
- pint of tongue liquor
- lemon
- two dozen raisins
- pounded almonds
- stick of cinnamon
- cloves
- four tablespoons of brown sugar
- one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one tablespoon of molasses
- two tablespoons of vinegar

## Directions

1. Lay the fresh tongue in cold water for a couple of hours and then put it on to boil in enough water to barely cover it, adding salt.
2. Boil until tender, closely covered, from three to four hours steadily.
3. To ascertain when tender run a fork through the thickest part.
4. Pare off the thick skin which covers the tongue and cut into even slices.
5. Sprinkle a little fine salt over each piece.
6. Put one tablespoon of drippings in a kettle or spider.
7. Cut up an onion in it, add a tablespoon of flour and stir, adding gradually about a pint of the liquor in which the tongue was boiled.
8. Cut up a lemon in slices, remove the seeds, and add two dozen raisins, a few pounded almonds, a stick of cinnamon and a few cloves.
9. Sweeten with four tablespoons of brown sugar in which you have put one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon, one tablespoon of molasses and two tablespoons of vinegar.
10. Let this boil, lay in the slices of tongue and boil up for a few minutes.

title: Boiled Trout
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- celery root
- onion
- parsley
- fish
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- sweet cream
- fresh butter
- pepper
- flour
- milk
- eggs
- almonds

## Directions

1. Cut up a celery root, one onion, and a sprig of parsley, tie the fish in a napkin and lay it on this bed of roots
2. Pour in enough water to cover and add a dash of vinegar
3. Boil over a quick fire and add more salt to the water in which the fish has been boiled
4. Lay your fish on a hot platter
5. Set a cup of sweet cream in a kettle, heat it, add a tablespoon of fresh butter, salt and pepper
6. Thicken with a tablespoon of flour which has been wet with a little cold milk, stir this paste into the cream and boil about one minute, stirring constantly
7. Pour over the fish
8. Boil two eggs, and while they are boiling, blanch about a dozen or more almonds and stick them into the fish, points up
9. Cover the eggs with cold water, peel them, separate the whites from the yolks, chop each separately
10. Garnish the fish, first with a row of chopped yolks, then whites, until all is used
11. Lay chopped parsley all around the platter

title: Boiled Turbot
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 white and fine skin turbot
- boiling water
- salt
- 1 lemon
- fried smelts
- horseradish
- parsley
- caper sauce or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. An hour before dinner, put the turbot into boiling water and salt
2. Rub the juice of a lemon over it
3. Let it boil fast at first, then draw the fish-kettle to the side
4. Slit the skin at the back a little to prevent it breaking
5. If done before wanted, lift the steamer out of the water and put it across the kettle with a cloth to keep it warm
6. Dish it on a fish napkin garnished with fried smelts, horseradish, and parsley
7. Serve with caper, anchovy sauce, or sauce No. 33
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Turkey
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- boiling water
- salt
- pepper
- blade of mace
- lemon
- veal stuffing
- celery or mushroom sauce
- boiled tongue or smoked beef
- boiled cauliflower
- water cresses

## Directions

1. Put the turkey in a saucepan, cover it with boiling water, as the boiling water sets the colour of the flesh at once
2. Put salt into the water and a little pepper, a blade of mace
3. Rub the turkey over with the juice of half a lemon before you put in the water
4. Stuff it with a veal stuffing
5. Let it simmer for 2 hours gently
6. If a large turkey, send up in the sauce tureen celery or mushroom sauce
7. Boiled tongue or smoked beef must always accompany this dish
8. Garnish the turkey with small pieces of boiled cauliflower and water cresses
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boiled Turnips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- salt
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Have the turnips peeled and sliced
2. Drop the slices into a stew-pan with boiling water enough to cover generously
3. Cook until tender, then drain well
4. Mash with a wooden vegetable masher as metal is apt to impart an unpleasant taste
5. Season with salt, butter, and a little pepper
6. Serve at once

title: Boiled, Smoked, or Pickled Tongue Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- tongue
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from the bread (unless it is very soft)
2. Place the slices of tongue (cut very thin) and lettuce leaves between the slices

title: Bola
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- good, rich bread dough
- two eggs
- one-half pound of butter
- chopped almonds
- citron
- cinnamon
- brown sugar
- lumps of butter

## Directions

1. Make a good, rich bread dough. Let it rise overnight; next morning mix with dough two eggs, one-half pound of butter well kneaded; stand by fire until well risen
2. When risen, roll out into thin sheets and sprinkle with chopped almonds, citron, cinnamon and plenty of brown sugar and lumps of butter all through; roll up like jelly-roll, cut in pieces a finger long
3. Grease pan, stand pieces in centre, others around and let rise before baking
4. Watch it well while baking

title: Bola d'Amor
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of white sugar
- yolks of twenty eggs
- one pound of sweet almonds
- orange flower-water
- citron

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of white sugar
2. To ascertain when it is of a proper consistency, drop a spoonful in cold water, form it into a ball, and try if it sounds when struck against a glass
3. Take the yolks of twenty eggs, mix them up gently and pass them through a sieve
4. Have ready a funnel, the hole of which must be about the size of vermicelli
5. Hold the funnel over the sugar, while it is boiling over a charcoal fire
6. Pour the eggs through, stirring the sugar all the time, and taking care to hold the funnel at such a distance from the sugar, as to admit of the egg dropping into it
7. When the egg has been a few minutes in the sugar, it will be hard enough to take out with a silver fork, and must then be placed on a drainer
8. Continue adding egg to the boiling sugar till enough is obtained
9. Prepare one pound of sweet almonds, finely pounded and boiled in sugar, clarified with orange flower-water only
10. Place in a dish a layer of this paste, over which spread a layer of citron cut in thin slices, and then a thick layer of the egg prepared as above
11. Continue working thus in alternate layers till high enough to look handsome
12. Pile in the form of a cone, and the egg should form the last layer
13. Place in a gentle oven till it becomes a little set, and the last layer slightly crisp
14. Serve in the dish in which it is baked, and ornament with myrtle and gold and silver leaf

title: Bola d'Amour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lb. loaf sugar
- 20 eggs
- 1 lb. ground sweet almonds
- 1/2 lb. clarified sugar
- orange-flower water
- candied citron

## Directions

1. Clarify 2 lb. of loaf sugar to a thick syrup.
2. Take the yolks of 20 eggs, mix them up quickly and pass them through a sieve.
3. Have ready a funnel, the hole must be the size of a vermicelli.
4. Hold the funnel over syrup while it boils over a charcoal fire.
5. Pass the egg through stirring the sugar at the same time, holding the funnel such a distance from the sugar to admit of the eggs dropping into it.
6. When the eggs have been in a few minutes it will become hard enough to take out with a silver fork and must be placed on a drainer.
7. Continue adding the eggs to the boiling sugar until you have used up the whole of them.
8. Prepare 1 lb. of ground sweet almonds, boiled in 1/2 lb. of clarified sugar flavoured with orange-flower water.
9. Place a layer of this paste in a dish, then a layer of candied citron cut thin, then a thick layer of the eggs prepared as above.
10. Continue in alternate layers of almond paste, citron, and the eggs until it is high enough to look handsome.
