11. Pile it in the shape of a cone, with the eggs as the last layer.
12. Put it in a slow oven for it to set a little, and the last layer a little crisp.
13. It must be served in the dish it is baked in and is generally garnished with gold, silver, and myrtle leaves.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Bola d'Amour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lb. of loaf sugar
- yolks of 20 eggs

## Directions

1. Clarify 2 lb. of loaf sugar to a thick syrup
2. Take the yolks of 20 eggs and mix them up quickly
3. Pass them through a sieve
4. Have ready a funnel, the hole must be the size of a vermicelli
5. Hold the funnel over syrup while it boils over a charcoal fire
6. Pass the egg through stirring the sugar at the same time
7. Take care to hold the funnel
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Bola Toledo
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- milk
- one pound of flour
- six beaten eggs
- a quarter of a pound of beaten sweet almonds
- two table-spoonsful of yeast
- egg prepared as for Bola d'Amor
- slices of citron
- egg marmalade
- cinnamon
- rich puff paste
- sugar clarified with orange flower-water

## Directions

1. Warm one pound of butter over the fire with a little milk
2. Put it into a pan with one pound of flour, six beaten eggs, a quarter of a pound of beaten sweet almonds, and two table-spoonsful of yeast
3. Make these ingredients into a light paste
4. Set it before the fire to rise
5. Grease a deep dish
6. Place in a layer of the paste, then some egg prepared as for Bola d'Amor, then slices of citron, and a layer of egg marmalade
7. Sprinkle each layer with cinnamon
8. Fill the dish with alternate layers
9. Line the dish with a rich puff paste
10. Bake in a brisk oven
11. Pour sugar clarified with orange flower-water over till the syrup has thoroughly penetrated the Bola

title: Boned Duck Stewed Ragout Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- forced meat
- sheep's tongue
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- chopped onions
- garlic
- marjoram
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- pickled walnut
- cayenne
- small meat balls
- 2 or 3 slices of fat smoked beef
- flour
- juice of a lemon
- browning
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Take a duck and bone it
2. Make a pound of forced meat well seasoned
3. Have a sheep's tongue that has been salted, boil it, skin and trim it
4. Put it in the centre of the forced meat with 2 hard boiled eggs, peeled whole
5. Make a nice ball of that, put it inside of the duck and give it its original shape
6. Put it in a stewpan and stew it with chopped onions, garlic, marjoram, thyme, salt, pepper, a pickled walnut, a little cayenne
7. When done take out the duck, strain the liquor, put it over the duck
8. Cook with a few small meat balls and 2 or 3 slices of fat smoked beef
9. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour, the juice of a lemon, a little browning if not dark enough
10. Stir it in, mix it well together
11. Dish up duck in the centre, with slices of beef under, balls round with a few quarters of hard boiled eggs
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Boned Smelts, Sautéd
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- smelts
- salt
- butter
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take a dozen raw smelts
2. Split them from the back lengthwise, leaving the head and tail intact
3. Take out the large center bone without opening the stomach and season with salt
4. Put four ounces of butter into a saucepan, and when quite hot place the smelts in it, so that the side which was cut open is underneath
5. When they have attained a nice color, turn them over and finish cooking
6. When ready, arrange them on a very hot dish, pour the butter in which they were cooked over them
7. Squeeze a little lemon on them, then add over all some finely chopped green parsley
8. Serve

title: Borsht
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- red beetroots
- water
- lemon juice
- sugar
- salt
- yolks of eggs
- beef stock (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash beetroots thoroughly and peel
2. Boil in a moderate quantity of water from two to three hours over a slow fire until a strong red liquor is obtained
3. Strain off the liquor
4. Add lemon juice, sugar, and salt to taste
5. When cooled a little, stir in sufficient yolks of eggs to slightly thicken it
6. May be used either cold or hot
7. In the latter case a little home-made beef stock may be added to the beet soup

title: Boston Roast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cooked kidney beans
- one-half pound of grated cheese
- salt and red pepper to taste
- bread crumbs
- butter and water for basting
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Mash one pound of cooked kidney beans and put them through a food chopper
2. Add one-half pound of grated cheese
3. Add salt and red pepper to taste
4. Add sufficient bread crumbs to make the mixture stiff enough to form into a ball
5. Bake in a moderate oven, basting occasionally with butter and water
6. Serve with tomato sauce

title: Bouillon
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one three-pound chicken
- six quarts cold water
- one and one-half or two pounds of beef
- thick part of veal in same quantity as beef

## Directions

1. Put on one three-pound chicken to boil in six quarts cold water
2. Take one and one-half or two pounds of beef and the same quantity thick part of veal, put in a baking-pan, set in the stove and brown quickly with just enough water to keep from burning
3. When brown, cut the meat in pieces, add this with all the juice it has drawn, to the chicken soup
4. Set on the back of the stove, and cook slowly all day
5. Set in a cold place, or on ice over night
6. Next morning after it is congealed, skim off every particle of fat
7. Melt and season to taste when ready to serve
8. When used for the table, cut up carrots and French peas already cooked can be added while heating
9. If cooked on gas stove, cook over the simmering flame the same number of hours

title: Brain (Appetizer)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt
- chopped onions
- juice of one and a half lemons
- olive oil
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cook brains and let cool
2. Add salt
3. Beat up with chopped onions, juice of one and a half lemons and olive oil
4. Serve on lettuce leaves

title: Brain Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- parsley
- onion
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- rind of a fresh lemon
- crumbs of bread
- boiled potato
- 3 eggs
- dripping or oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains as before, then put them in a saucepan to boil
2. When cold chop them with a little parsley, a little onion
3. Put in a little fine marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and a little of the rind of a fresh lemon chopped fine
4. Add a few crumbs of bread, about an ounce, or one boiled potato mashed
5. Mix with 3 eggs
6. Fry them in dripping or oil
7. Serve them very hot, with lemon juice, and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brain Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- boiling hot water
- cold salt water
- one-half cup of vinegar
- sugar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Scald brains with boiling hot water to cleanse thoroughly.
2. Boil until tender, in fresh cold salt water, being careful to remove from water while it is yet firm.
3. Slice lengthwise and lay in dish.
4. Pour over one-half cup of vinegar, which has been sweetened with a pinch of sugar to remove sharp taste, pinch of salt and pepper.
5. Garnish with parsley and serve cold.
6. Can also be served with mayonnaise.

title: Brains (Sweet and Sour)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt
- onion
- celery root
- whole peppers
- crust of rye bread
- vinegar
- water
- lebkuchen
- brown sugar
- tablespoon of molasses
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- seedless raisins
- pounded almonds
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean brains
2. Lay in ice-cold salted water for an hour
3. Cut up an onion, a few slices of celery root, a few whole peppers, a little salt and a crust of rye bread
4. Lay the brains upon this bed of herbs and barely cover with vinegar and water
5. Boil about fifteen minutes
6. lift out the brains with a perforated skimmer, and lay upon a platter to cool
7. Take a lebkuchen, some brown sugar, a tablespoon of molasses, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon, a few seedless raisins and a few pounded almonds
8. Moisten this with vinegar and add the boiling sauce
9. Boil the sauce ten minutes longer and pour scalding over the brains
10. Eat cold and decorate with slices of lemon

title: Brains (Sweet and Sour)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- ice-cold salted water
- onion
- celery root
- whole peppers
- salt
- crust of rye bread
- vinegar
- water
- lebkuchen
- brown sugar
- one tablespoon of molasses

## Directions

1. Clean as described above
2. Lay in ice-cold salted water for an hour
3. Cut up an onion
4. Cut a few slices of celery root
5. Prepare a few whole peppers
6. Lay the brains upon a bed of herbs with salt and crust of rye bread
7. Barely cover with vinegar and water
8. Boil about fifteen minutes
9. Lift out the brains with a perforated skimmer
10. Lay upon a platter to cool
11. Take a lebkuchen, some brown sugar, a tablespoon of molasses

title: Brains Stewed in the Spanish Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- garlic
- 2 spoonfuls of oil
- 3 spoonfuls of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- saffron
- cayenne
- dried winter savory
- new potatoes
- meat balls

## Directions

1. Brains must be well washed and cleansed from every particle of blood, and must be skinned, then throw them into clean water again
2. Cut a good-sized tooth of garlic into a small stewpan, with 2 spoonfuls of the best oil
3. Let it fry, then take out the garlic
4. Put in 3 spoonfuls of vinegar, a little salt, pepper, saffron, and a little cayenne, then the brains
5. Let them cook slowly
6. Add a teaspoonful of dried winter savory
7. Boil a few new potatoes, skin them, and put them in a few minutes before it is finished
8. This dish will only take ¾ an hour to cook
9. The gravy must not be thickened; a few small meat balls can be put also
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brains with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- cup of beef broth
- marjoram
- saffron
- 2 eggs
- lemon
- flour
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains the same as the last
2. Slice in a stewpan some onion, pepper, salt, grated nutmeg, and a cup of beef broth if you have it
3. Put in the brains with a little marjoram, and a little saffron to colour
4. Let it cook gently for half an hour
5. Beat in a basin 2 eggs, the juice of a lemon, a teaspoonful of flour, a little chopped parsley
6. When it is rubbed smooth, stir it into the saucepan, basting it backwards and forwards to mix the sauce well
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brains with Egg Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt water
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- cup of stock
- marjoram
- yolks of two eggs
- juice of a lemon
- teaspoon of flour
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Wash brains well, skin
2. boil fifteen minutes in salt water
3. slice in stew-pan some onions, salt, pepper, ginger and a cup of stock
4. Put in the brains with a little marjoram
5. let it cook gently for one-half hour
6. Mix yolks of two eggs, juice of a lemon, a teaspoon of flour, a little chopped parsley
7. when it is rubbed smooth, stir it into saucepan
8. stir well to prevent curdling

title: Braised Oxtails
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two oxtails
- six onions
- water
- two carrots
- one green pepper
- sprig of parsley
- one-half cup of tomatoes
- six small potatoes
- browned flour
- eight lengths of macaroni

## Directions

1. Joint and wash the oxtails.
2. Slice and brown six onions in pot with oxtails.
3. When nicely browned add water enough to cover and stew slowly one hour.
4. Add two carrots, one green pepper, sprig of parsley, one-half cup of tomatoes and six small potatoes, and cook until tender.
5. Thicken with browned flour.
6. Cook separately eight lengths of macaroni.
7. Place cooked macaroni on dish and pour ragout over it and serve hot.

title: Bran Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four teaspoons of soda
- two teaspoons of salt
- four cups of white flour
- four cups of bran flour
- one cup of molasses
- four cups of sweet milk
- chopped nuts or raisins

## Directions

1. Sift four teaspoons of soda, two teaspoons of salt with four cups of white flour
2. Add four cups of bran flour and mix well
3. Add one cup of molasses and four cups of sweet milk
4. Use chopped nuts or raisins or both as desired
5. Place in greased pans (four and a half by nine inches)
6. Bake one hour in a moderate oven

title: Bran Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of white flour
- one teaspoon of soda
- three tablespoons of molasses
- one tablespoon of butter
- two cups of bran
- one and one-half cups of sweet milk
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of chopped dates or raisins

## Directions

1. Sift one-half cup of white flour with one teaspoon of soda
2. Mix three tablespoons of molasses with one tablespoon of butter
3. Add two cups of bran, one and one-half cups of sweet milk
4. Add the flour and one-half teaspoon of salt
5. Stir all together
6. One-half cup of chopped dates or raisins may be added if so desired
7. Bake in muffin-pans in a moderate oven thirty minutes

title: Brandied Cherries
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe black cherries
- two pounds of loaf sugar
- a quart of brandy
- a few cloves

## Directions

1. Put into a large wide mouthed bottle very ripe black cherries
2. Add to them two pounds of loaf sugar, a quart of brandy, and a few cloves
3. Bruise a few more cherries, and simmer with sugar
4. Strain and add the juice to the cherries in the bottle
5. Cork closely
6. Keep in a warm dry place

title: Brandied Cherries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet cherries
- sugar
- brandy
- water

## Directions

1. Select the largest sweet cherries, leaving the stems on
2. Allow half a pound of sugar to every pound of fruit, and a pint of good brandy for every five pounds of fruit
3. Make a syrup of the sugar, using as little water as possible
4. Pour it over the cherries and let them remain in the syrup all night
5. Next day put them in a preserving kettle and heat slowly
6. Boil about eight minutes
7. Take up the cherries with a perforated skimmer and boil the syrup fifteen minutes
8. Add the brandy to the boiling syrup, remove from the fire and pour over the cherries hot, and seal

title: Brandied Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- clingstone peaches
- sugar
- brandy
- water
- blanched peach kernels

## Directions

1. Allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, and a pint of the best brandy to every four pounds of peaches
2. Make a syrup of the sugar with enough water to just dissolve it, and boil about half a dozen blanched peach kernels with it
3. When the syrup boils put in the fruit and let it boil about five minutes
4. Remove the fruit carefully upon platters, and let the syrup boil fifteen or twenty minutes longer, skimming it well
5. Put the peaches in wide-mouthed glass jars
6. If the syrup has thickened pour in the brandy
7. Remove from the fire at once, pour over the fruit and seal

title: Brandied Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- sugar
- brandy
- water

## Directions

1. Pare the fruit, leaving the stems on
2. Weigh
3. Proceed as with peaches

title: Brandied Quinces
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow pear-shaped quinces
- sugar
- quince water
- stick cinnamon
- cloves
- brandy

## Directions

1. Select large yellow, pear-shaped quinces, and peel and quarter them
2. Take out the cores and throw into cold water, until all are pared
3. Then boil until tender, so they can easily be pierced
4. Take them out with a perforated skimmer and weigh
5. Take three-quarters of a pound of sugar to a pound of quinces, and boil in a little over half the quince water
6. Add stick cinnamon and cloves (removing the soft heads)
7. Boil until quite a thick syrup
8. Pack the quinces in jars, add a pint of good brandy to the syrup and pour boiling hot over the quinces and seal immediately

title: Brandy Blancmange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- isinglass
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 oz. of castor sugar
- thin rind of a lemon
- a piece of cinnamon
- 1 pint of sherry
- 1/2 pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Make just the same as Wine Blancmange, by adding to the wine 1/2 pint of brandy
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brandy Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of water
- a quarter glass of brandy
- one cup of sugar
- juice of half a lemon
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Take one cup of water, a quarter glass of brandy, one cup of sugar, and juice of half a lemon
2. Boil all in double boiler
3. Beat the yolks of two eggs light
4. Add the boiling sauce gradually to them, stirring constantly until thick

title: Braune Mehlsuppe (Brown Flour Soup), No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of flour
- boiling water
- salt
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Heat a spoon of butter in a spider
2. add a spoon of flour, stir briskly, but do not let it get black
3. pour boiling water over it
4. add salt and caraway seeds

title: Bread Balls for Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of stale bread the size of half-penny roll
- 2 oz. of chopped suet
- a little parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- half an egg

## Directions

1. Soak a piece of stale bread the size of half-penny roll
2. Drain the water well from it by wringing it in a small piece of cloth
3. Put it in a basin with 2 oz. of chopped suet, a little parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
4. Mix with half an egg, make into balls
5. Use these balls as you require them to change the style of your cooking
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Bread Dressing for Fowl
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- drippings or fat
- onion
- stale baker's bread
- cold water
- soup stock
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- finely chopped parsley
- fat
- egg

## Directions

1. In a fryer on the stove heat two tablespoons of drippings or fat
2. Drop in one-half onion cut fine and brown lightly
3. Add one-quarter loaf of stale baker's bread which has previously been soaked in cold water and then thoroughly squeezed out
4. Cook until it leaves the sides of the fryer, stirring occasionally
5. If too dry add a little soup stock
6. Remove from the fire, put in a bowl
7. Season with salt, pepper, ginger, and finely chopped parsley
8. Add a small lump of fat, break in one whole egg
9. Mix well and fill the fowl with it

title: Bread Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- two French rolls
- 1/2 pint of cold milk
- 2 or 3 eggs
- best oil
- sugar
- cinnamon
- good thick clarified sugar flavoured with orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Cut two French rolls in rounds (each roll will cut in four rounds)
2. lay them in a dish with 1/2 pint of cold milk
3. let them soak for 1/2 an hour
4. take them out, lay the rounds between a clean cloth
5. beat up in a plate 2 or 3 eggs
6. put the frying-pan on with the best oil
7. before putting in the fritters fry a piece of crumb of bread to take off the flavour of the oil
8. dip carefully each fritter in the eggs on both sides
9. fry them a light brown
10. when they are done put them in a deep dish
11. sprinkle over sugar and cinnamon
12. have some good thick clarified sugar ready flavoured with orange-flower water in a sauceboat
13. pour it over the fritters
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Bread Fruit Tarts
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of bread
- fruit
- brown sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Line a dish with thin slices of bread
2. Lay the fruit with brown sugar in alternate layers with slices of bread
3. When the dish is filled, pour over half a tea-cup full of water
4. Let the top be formed of thin pieces of bread thickly strewed over with brown sugar
5. Bake until thoroughly done

title: Bread Pancakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- sour milk
- one-half teaspoon salt
- two eggs
- two teaspoons baking soda
- finely sifted flour

## Directions

1. Soak stale bread overnight in sour milk
2. Mash the bread fine in the morning
3. Put in one-half teaspoon of salt, two eggs, two teaspoons of baking soda dissolved in hot water
4. Thicken with finely sifted flour

title: Bread Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread or crumb of a French roll
- milk
- two or three eggs
- wine or essence of lemon or beaten almonds
- peel of one whole lemon
- juice of lemon

## Directions

1. Grate stale bread, or soak the crumb of a French roll in milk, which must be warmed
2. beat with it two or three eggs
3. flavor and sweeten to taste, sometimes with a little wine or essence of lemon, or beaten almonds
4. boil about half an hour
5. This pudding is excellent made as above, with the addition of the peel of one whole lemon grated, with its juice, and baked

title: Bread Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of bread crumbs
- a pint of sweet milk
- two eggs
- a half cup of sugar
- grated peel of one lemon
- raisins
- powdered sugar
- jelly sauce or whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soak one and one-half cups of bread crumbs in a pint of sweet milk for half an hour
2. Separate the whites and yolks of two eggs, setting the whites in a cool place until needed
3. Beat the yolks with a half cup of sugar and add the grated peel of one lemon and stir into the bread crumbs
4. Put in some raisins and pour into a greased pudding dish and bake in a moderate oven, about half an hour
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth, adding half a cup of powdered sugar
6. Spread this on top of pudding and return to the oven and brown delicately
7. May be eaten hot or cold, with jelly sauce or whipped cream

title: Bread Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large onion
- pepper
- milk
- grated stale bread
- a piece of butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a large onion and boil it, with a little pepper till quite soft, in milk
2. Take it out, and pour the milk over grated stale bread
3. Boil it up with a piece of butter, and dredge it with flour
4. It should be well beaten up with a silver fork

title: Bread Sauce (Alternative)
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large onion
- a little veal gravy
- grated bread
- pepper
- the yolk and white of an egg

## Directions

1. Slice a large onion thin
2. Put it into a little veal gravy
3. Add grated bread, pepper, &c.
4. Add the yolk and white of an egg well beaten

title: Bread Sauce for Roast Turkey and Fowls
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of clear stock
- the crumb of a stale roll
- a small onion
- salt
- cayenne
- a pinch of ground mace
- a spoonful of the fat from plain broth

## Directions

1. Chop a small onion very fine
2. Soak the roll crumb in water first, then well drain it from the water
3. Beat it well up to a cream
4. Put the 1/2 pint of clear stock, the crumb, onion, salt, cayenne, and mace together
5. Let it all boil together
6. While it is boiling stir in a spoonful of the fat you have taken off plain broth that has no vegetable flavor
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Bread Sticks
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- raised bread dough
- beaten egg yolk
- poppy seed

## Directions

1. Take pieces of raised bread dough, roll three-eighths inch thick and four or five inches long
2. Place in floured pan, far apart
3. brush tops with beaten yolk and poppy seed
4. Let rise
5. bake in a hot oven until brown

title: Breakfast Cocoa
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons prepared cocoa
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- salt
- 1 cup boiling water
- 3 cups scalded milk

## Directions

1. Mix two tablespoons prepared cocoa with two tablespoons of sugar and a few grains of salt
2. Dilute with one-half cup of boiling water to make a smooth paste
3. Add one-half cup of boiling water and boil five minutes
4. Turn into three cups of scalded milk and beat two minutes, using Dover beater
5. Serve

title: Breast Flank (Short Ribs) and Yellow Turnips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small ribs
- water
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- yellow turnip
- potatoes
- sugar

## Directions

1. Get the small ribs and put on with plenty of water, an onion, pepper and salt
2. After boiling about one and one-half hours add a large yellow turnip cut in small pieces
3. One-half hour before serving add six potatoes cut in small pieces
4. Water must be added as necessary
5. A little sugar will improve flavor
6. As it simmers the turnip will soften and give the whole dish the appearance of a stew

title: Breast of Mutton Baked with Rice and Potatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- dried saffron
- 1/2 lb rice
- potatoes
- water
- parsley
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Cut the breast of mutton in nice pieces, cut off the fat
2. Take a deep baking dish and slice an onion at the bottom
3. Add salt, pepper, a little dried saffron
4. Wash the rice and cut as many potatoes as you require
5. Put them in the dish
6. Put in the pieces of mutton
7. Fill the dish with water
8. Chop a little parsley and marjoram, put it in the oven to bake
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Breast of Mutton Stewed with Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- salt
- ground ginger
- cold water
- carrots
- potatoes
- fat
- flour
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- gravy

## Directions

1. Salt the mutton on both sides, adding a little ground ginger.
2. Put on to boil in cold water, cover up tightly and stew slowly.
3. In the meantime pare and cut up the carrots, add these and cover up again.
4. Pare and cut up about half a dozen potatoes into dice shape and add them three-quarters of an hour before dinner.
5. Cover up again, and when done, make a sauce as follows: Skim off about two tablespoons of fat from the mutton stew, put this in a spider and heat.
6. Brown a tablespoon of flour in the fat.
7. Add a heaping tablespoon of brown sugar, some cinnamon and pour the gravy of the stew into the spider.
8. Let it boil up once, and then pour all over the carrots and stew until ready to serve.

title: Breast of Mutton Stewed with Celery
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- nice breast of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- half pint of water
- large head of celery
- garlic
- flour
- juice of a lemon
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Take a nice breast of mutton, not too fat, put it in a stewpan with salt, pepper, nutmeg, half pint of water
2. Cut and wash well a large head of celery in good size pieces
3. Put it in with the mutton and a tooth of garlic chopped fine
4. Let it simmer nicely till tender
5. Skim off all the fat
6. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour with the juice of a lemon and a little dried saffron
7. Put it into the gravy and stir it well
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Breast of Mutton with French Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- stuffing
- sliced onion
- salt
- pepper
- French beans
- 1/2 pint of water
- flour
- moist sugar
- vinegar
- browning

## Directions

1. Prepare the breast just as you did for the peas, with the stuffing
2. Put it in a stewpan with sliced onion, salt, and pepper
3. Wash and cut the French beans
4. Put them on top of the mutton with 1/2 pint of water
5. When done, rub in a basin one spoonful of flour, one of moist sugar, two of vinegar, and browning to colour
6. Mind that you take off all the fat before you put the sauce in
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Breast of Mutton with Green Peas
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- soaked bread
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- grated nutmeg
- grated rind of a lemon
- one egg
- chopped onion
- peas
- mint
- flour
- cold water
- lumps of sugar

## Directions

1. Take a breast of mutton not too fat and lay it on your board
2. Make a nice dressing of soaked bread, parsley, salt, pepper, marjoram, grated nutmeg, the grated rind of a lemon
3. Mix up with one egg
4. Put it between the bone and flesh
5. Skewer it through with a fine iron skewer
6. Put it in a stewpan with some chopped onion, salt and pepper
7. When it has simmered for an hour, have washed some peas and put them in when done
8. Chop some parsley and mint and put it in a basin with a spoonful of flour mixed with a little cold water
9. When you put in the peas, put three or four lumps of sugar
10. Stir in the sauce just before you dish it up
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Breast of Veal—Roasted
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bread stuffing
- caraway seed
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Have the butcher cut a pocket to receive the stuffing
2. Prepare bread stuffing and sew up the pocket
3. Roast as directed for roast veal
4. Sprinkle a little caraway seed on top of the roast
5. A tablespoon of lemon juice adds to the flavor
6. Baste often

title: Bremen Apple Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- seven peeled and cored apples
- six tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of butter
- six eggs
- one pint of sour cream
- one tablespoon of vanilla
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- sugar to taste
- three cups of finely rolled zwieback
- lumps of butter

## Directions

1. Cook apples with six tablespoons of sugar and two tablespoons of butter together until apples are soft
2. Cream six eggs
3. Add to them one pint of sour cream, one tablespoon of vanilla, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon, and sugar to taste
4. Pour into the cooked apples and let all boil together till thick
5. Remove from stove
6. In the bottom of a well-greased pan put a layer of two cups of crumbs
7. Add a layer of the apple mixture
8. Add a layer of the remaining crumbs
9. Top with lumps of butter over all
10. Bake one hour

title: Brisket and Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of boned brisket
- a large Spanish onion
- salt and pepper
- a tooth of garlic
- a small piece of mace
- 2 or 3 cloves
- a pint of water
- a pound of French beans
- a tablespoonful of flour
- one tablespoonful of brown sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Put boned brisket in a stewpan with a large Spanish onion sliced, salt and pepper, a tooth of garlic cut small, a small piece of mace, 2 or 3 cloves, and a pint of water.
2. Let it simmer gently till done, it will take nearly 3 hours.
3. When half cooked have a pound of French beans nicely cut, well washed, and drained, put them in with the meat.
4. When done rub in a basin a tablespoonful of flour, one of brown sugar, 2 of vinegar.
5. When quite smooth turn it into the saucepan and baste the sauce well over the beef and beans.
6. Let it stand for five minutes.
7. Colour the sauce with a little browning, not too deep a colour.
8. It is then ready to dish up.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brisket and Carrots
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of brisket
- a pint of water
- salt and pepper
- mace
- 3 or 4 good size carrots
- a tooth of garlic
- flour
- a little browning
- a spoonful of vinegar
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put brisket on with a pint of water, salt, pepper, mace.
2. Cut up in strips of 2 inches long and one thick, 3 or 4 good size carrots.
3. Add a tooth of garlic chopped small.
4. Let them cook for three hours beef and carrots together.
5. When done thicken with flour and a little browning just to turn the colour.
6. Rub the flour with a spoonful of vinegar and a little chopped parsley.
7. Dish up with the meat in the centre and the carrots all round.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brisket and Peas
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of boned brisket
- a pint of water
- salt and pepper
- a small piece of mace
- an onion
- 1/2 peck of peas
- 3 or 4 sticks of mint
- a little parsley
- 4 or 5 lumps of sugar
- a spoonful of flour
- a little cold water

## Directions

1. Put boned brisket in a stewpan with a pint of water, salt, pepper, a small piece of mace, an onion chopped small.
2. Let it simmer for 3 hours.
3. Half an hour before it is finished have 1/2 peck of peas carefully shelled and wash them through a colander.
4. Put them in with the beef, chop 3 or 4 sticks of mint, a little parsley, 4 or 5 lumps of sugar, put all in together.
5. Let it finish cooking.
6. Stir in a spoonful of flour rubbed smooth with a little cold water to thicken the liquor.
7. Have the beef in the centre and the peas all round.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brisket of Beef with Sauerkraut
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of fat, young beef
- salt
- one quart of sauerkraut
- vinegar
- grated raw potato
- brown sugar
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Take about three pounds of fat, young beef (you may make soup stock of it first), then take out the bones, salt it well and lay it in the bottom of a kettle.
2. Put a quart of sauerkraut on top of it and let it boil slowly until tender.
3. Add vinegar if necessary, thicken with a grated raw potato and add a little brown sugar.
4. Some like a few caraway seeds added.

title: Brisket Stewed
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about six or seven pounds of brisket of beef
- water
- a little spice
- turnips
- carrots
- mushrooms
- cauliflowers
- or other vegetables in season

## Directions

1. Take about six or seven pounds of brisket of beef
2. Place it in a stewpan with only enough water to cover it
3. Season with a little spice tied in a bag
4. When the meat is tender and the spices sufficiently extracted to make the gravy rich and strong, part of it must be removed to another saucepan
5. Have ready a variety of vegetables cut into small shapes, such as turnips, carrots, mushrooms, cauliflowers, or whatever may be in season
6. Stew them gently till tender in the gravy
7. Glaze the meat and pour the gravy in the dish
8. Arrange the vegetables round

title: Brisket Stewed with Onions and Raisins
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about five pounds of brisket of beef
- water
- allspice
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- four large onions
- half a pound of raisins
- a tea-spoonful of brown sugar
- a table spoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Stew about five pounds of brisket of beef in sufficient water to cover
2. Season with allspice, pepper, salt, and nutmeg
3. When nearly done, add four large onions cut in pieces and half a pound of raisins stoned
4. Let them remain simmering till well done
5. Just before serving, stir in a tea-spoonful of brown sugar and a table spoonful of flour

title: Brisket with Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four pounds of boned brisket
- a pint of water
- pepper and salt
- a tooth of garlic
- a small onion
- a dozen artichokes
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a little grated nutmeg
- little chopped parsley
- the juice of a lemon
- a little dried saffron

## Directions

1. Put boned brisket into a stewpan with a pint of water, pepper, salt, a tooth of garlic chopped fine, a small onion.
2. Put it on to simmer, it will take 3 hours.
3. Half an hour before it is done have ready peeled and washed a dozen artichokes as near one size as possible.
4. Let them drain from the water and put them in with the meat.
5. Rub in a basin a tablespoonful of flour, a little grated nutmeg, little chopped parsley, the juice of a lemon.
6. Turn it into a saucepan and mix it well with the liquor.
7. Colour with a little dried saffron.
8. Dish it up nicely, the meat in the centre, the artichokes round.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brisket with the Large White Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of boned brisket
- a pint of water
- salt and pepper
- 1 pint of the large white beans
- a large onion
- a tooth of garlic
- the white heart of a head of celery
- a few drops of browning

## Directions

1. Put in soak the night before 1 pint of the large white beans.
2. Strain them well from the water they were soaked in.
3. Put boned brisket in a stewpan with a pint of water, salt, pepper.
4. Put the beans on with the beef, a large onion sliced, a tooth of garlic, the white heart of a head of celery cut very fine.
5. Let all cook together, this will require no thickening.
6. Colour with a few drops of browning.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broad Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- fine noodles
- two tablespoons of sweet dripping
- soup stock (optional)

## Directions

1. Make noodles as above and when drained sprinkle with fine noodles which have been browned in two tablespoons of sweet dripping; serve as a vegetable.
2. If so desired, a cup of soup stock may be added and noodles browned in stove.
3. Serve hot.

title: Brod Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- seven tablespoons of granulated sugar
- seven tablespoons of bread crumbs
- one-eighth pound of chopped almonds
- one-half teaspoon of allspice
- one tablespoon of jelly
- grated rind and juice of one lemon
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-half teaspoon of cloves
- one-half wine glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs well
2. Add sugar and beat until it blisters
3. Add bread crumbs, almonds, jelly, spice, lemon, and brandy
4. Add beaten whites
5. Bake slowly about forty minutes

title: Broiled Beefsteak
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- steak
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat the gridiron, put in the steak
2. Turn the gridiron over the hot coals at intervals of two minutes and then repeatedly at intervals of one minute
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, and serve on a hot platter

title: Broiled Eggplant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- salt and pepper
- olive oil
- dried bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Slice the eggplant and drain it as for frying
2. Spread the slices on a dish
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Baste with olive oil
5. Sprinkle with dried bread crumbs and broil

title: Broiled Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil or butter
- lemon
- Maitre d'Hotel butter

## Directions

1. For large fish, split down the back and remove head and tail
2. For salmon and halibut, cut into one-inch slices
3. For smelts and other small fish, leave whole
4. Wipe the fish as dry as possible
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. If the fish is dry and white, brush the flesh side well with olive oil or butter
7. Put in a well-greased broiler, placing the thickest parts toward the middle or back
8. Hold over a hot fire until the flesh side is nicely browned
9. Cook the skin side just long enough to make the skin crisp
10. Small fish require from ten to fifteen minutes, large fish from fifteen to twenty-five minutes
11. Loosen one side first, then the other, and lift carefully with a cake turner
12. Place on a platter and spread with butter
13. Stand in the oven for a few minutes
14. Garnish with lemon and serve with Maitre d'Hotel butter

title: Broiled Fish for Breakfast
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- juice of a lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Lay the fish in a marinade after it is washed and dried in a little oil, pepper, salt, vinegar, or juice of a lemon
2. When done, rub some butter over, or more oil and lemon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Fish for Breakfast
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Lay it in a marinade just the same as another time
2. Lay the fish after it is washed and dried in a little oil, pepper, salt, vinegar, or juice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Fowl and Mushrooms
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fine fowl
- good gravy
- one pint of fresh button mushrooms
- mushroom powder
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Truss a fine fowl as if for boiling
2. Split it down the back and broil gently
3. When nearly done, put it in a stewpan with a good gravy
4. Add a pint of fresh button mushrooms
5. Season to taste
6. Add a little mushroom powder and lemon juice to improve the flavour

title: Broiled Fresh Herrings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh herrings
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- lemon juice
- cayenne
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads
2. draw out the entrails
3. wash and dry them well
4. give 2 or 3 cuts across on each side
5. grease the gridiron
6. season the herrings with salt and pepper
7. dredge a little flour on them lightly
8. put them on the fish gridiron
9. broil them on both sides
10. put them on a hot dish with a piece of butter, lemon juice, a little cayenne and chopped parsley
11. serve on very hot dish and plates
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Green Peppers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- green peppers
- cold water
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Broil on all sides
2. place the broiled peppers in a dish of cold water so that the skin can be easily removed
3. When the peppers are all peeled put in a bowl or crock
4. add French dressing and cover closely

title: Broiled Haddocks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddock
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- lemon
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash a haddock and dry it well
2. Lay it in the marinade (oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt)
3. Prepare the gridiron as for other broiled fish
4. Dish it up with one of the sauces or the plain oil and vinegar, or butter and lemon
5. Serve with a dish of mashed potatoes for lunch
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled John Dory
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- John Dory
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Broil John Dory the same as turbot
2. Lay it in marinade first
3. Serve with same sauces
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Mackerel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- 2 ozs. fresh butter
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Wash them well in two or three waters
2. dry them in a cloth well
3. season them with salt, pepper, and rub some juice of a lemon over them
4. grease the gridiron
5. lay them on
6. let them broil both sides well
7. put in a cup to melt 2 ozs. fresh butter
8. dish them on a hot dish
9. squeeze the juice of a lemon over it with a little cayenne
10. send up hot plates with broiled fish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- buttered toast
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash the mushrooms and remove the stems and peel the caps
2. Place them in a broiler and broil for five minutes, with the cap side down during the first half of broiling
3. Serve on circular pieces of buttered toast, sprinkling with salt and pepper and putting a small piece of butter on each cap

title: Broiled Pigeons and Mushrooms
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- pepper
- salt
- fat
- lemon
- rich gravy
- 2 or 3 large mushrooms

## Directions

1. Split the pigeon open down the back, dry it well
2. Season it with pepper and salt
3. Rub them well over with fat
4. Put them on the gridiron to a very clear fire
5. When done squeeze the juice of a lemon over, and a rich gravy
6. Broil 2 or 3 large mushrooms, lay them under
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Plaice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- salt
- sauce or plain butter
- lemon or vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a fine thick plaice, wash, scrape the skin well, and lay it in salt for half an hour
2. Drain it well, dry it in a cloth
3. Notch it in slices like for frying, but do not cut it through
4. Broil it like the soles, season it well
5. Serve it with sauce or plain butter, and lemon or vinegar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Red Mullet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- salt
- pepper
- butter or oil
- paper
- lemon juice
- cayenne
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the mullet well
2. take care of the liver
3. dry them
4. season well with salt, pepper
5. butter or oil a sheet of paper well to wrap up each mullet
6. broil before a clear fire
7. when done, take off the paper carefully
8. squeeze over them some lemon juice with a little cayenne
9. serve very hot, garnished with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Salmon Trout
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon trout
- oil
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- caper or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Split them open like a mackerel and wash it
2. Dry it well and lay on it a spoonful of oil, the juice of a lemon, salt, pepper, a little cayenne
3. Leave it for an hour
4. Broil it before a clear fire
5. You can put over it more oil and lemon, or caper or anchovy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Salt Mackerel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salt fish
- cold water
- butter
- pepper
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Freshen the fish by soaking it over night in cold water, with the skin uppermost.
2. Drain and wipe dry, remove the head and tail.
3. Place it upon a butter broiler, and slowly broil to a light brown.
4. Place upon a hot dish, add pepper, bits of butter, a sprinkling of parsley and a little lemon juice.

title: Broiled Slices of Salmon with Caper Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon slices
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- caper sauce
- white paper

## Directions

1. Marinade your slices of salmon with oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt 3/4 of an hour before you want to broil it
2. Broil them before a very slow fire on both sides
3. Lay them on white paper at the bottom of the dish
4. Send up with caper sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Soles
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter or oil
- caper or anchovy sauce
- plain butter
- lemon
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Take a fine pair of soles, wash and dry them well
2. Season them with salt and pepper
3. Dredge them lightly with flour
4. Rub the gridiron with butter or oil
5. Put them in before a very clear and bright fire, keep turning them
6. You must not have them done a minute before wanted
7. Slip them off the gridiron on to a very hot dish
8. Serve them with caper or anchovy sauce, or plain butter rubbed over them, with the juice of a lemon and a little cayenne
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Sprats
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- large sprats
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pick the largest sprats you can get and take off the heads
2. Put them in layers on the gridiron and let them broil nicely both sides
3. Have a dish at the side of the fire with a good lump of butter
4. Turn them on the dish and season well with salt and pepper and the juice of a lemon
5. Serve with very hot plates; no garnish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Spring Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- young spring chickens
- salt
- pepper
- chicken-fat
- giblet sauce

## Directions

1. Take young spring chickens of one to one and one-half pounds in weight, and split down the back, break the joints and remove the breast bone.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and rub well with chicken-fat.
3. Place in broiler and broil twenty minutes over a clear fire, or under the flame in broiling oven of gas stove, being careful to turn broiler that all parts may be equally browned.
4. The flesh side must be exposed to the fire the greater part of the time as the skin side will brown quickly.
5. Remove to hot platter.
6. Alternatively, chicken may be placed in dripping-pan, skin side down, seasoned with salt and pepper and spread with chicken-fat, and bake fifteen minutes in a hot oven and then broiled to finish.
7. Serve with giblet sauce.

title: Broiled Squabs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- salt
- water
- pepper
- goose fat
- bread
- crust

## Directions

1. Clean the squabs
2. Lay them in salt water for about ten minutes and then rub dry with a clean towel
3. Split them down the back and broil over a clear coal fire
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Lay them on a heated platter, grease them liberally with goose fat and cover with a deep platter
6. Toast a piece of bread for each pigeon; removing the crust
7. Dip the toast in boiling water for an instant
8. In serving lay a squab upon a piece of toasted bread

title: Broiled Steak with Carrots
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot
- steak
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- salad oil

## Directions

1. Boil a carrot and cut it in the shape of dice
2. Put your dish to get hot
3. Take a tooth of garlic and rub it over the hot dish to give it a flavour
4. Lay the cut carrot in the dish
5. Sprinkle over it a little salt and pepper
6. Pour over it a teaspoonful of salad oil
7. Take your steak off the gridiron and place it on top
8. Season it with salt and pepper
9. Serve it up directly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Broiled Turbot
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- small turbot
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Take a small turbot and lay it in a marinade of oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt
2. Let it lay for an hour
3. Broil it a nice colour
4. Remember to slit the skin on the black side
5. Dish it up and serve with caper sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brown Betty
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four medium-sized apples
- one-quarter cup of butter
- juice of half a lemon
- one cup of bread crumbs
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- grated rind of one-half lemon
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one-quarter cup of boiling water
- cream
- soft custard or lemon sauce

## Directions

1. Pare, quarter, core and slice four medium-sized apples
2. Melt one-quarter cup of butter and pour it with the juice of half a lemon over one cup of bread crumbs
3. Mix one-half teaspoon of cinnamon, grated rind of one-half lemon and one-quarter cup of sugar together
4. Butter a baking dish
5. Put in alternate layers of apple and bread crumbs, sprinkling the apples with the sugar mixture, and making the last layer of crumbs
6. Pour one-quarter cup of boiling water on before adding the last layer of crumbs
7. Cover and bake for thirty minutes or until the apples are soft
8. Uncover and brown the crumbs
9. Serve with cream or with soft custard or lemon sauce

title: Brown Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup rye
- one cup graham flour
- one cup corn-meal
- one teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of soda
- one cup of molasses
- two cups of sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix and sift together one cup each of rye, graham flour, corn-meal and one teaspoon of salt
2. Dissolve one teaspoon of soda in one cup of molasses
3. Add alternately to flour with two cups of sour milk
4. Grease one-pound baking-powder cans and put in the dough
5. Boil two and one-half hours, keeping the water always three-fourths up around the tins
6. Turn out on baking-tins and place in the oven fifteen minutes to brown
7. Serve warm or steam again or toast leftovers

title: Brown Bread Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cottage cheese
- salt
- cayenne
- one pimento
- white and brown bread

## Directions

1. Season one cup of cottage cheese with salt, cayenne, and add one pimento cut in shreds.
2. Cut white and brown bread in finger lengths about one inch wide.
3. Spread with cheese mixture and place a brown and white slice together.

title: Brown Cucumber Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cucumbers
- butter or clarified fat
- strong beef gravy
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- chopped onion

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in thick slices one or more fresh cucumbers
2. Fry them until brown in a little butter or clarified fat
3. Add to them a little strong beef gravy, pepper, salt, and a spoonful of vinegar
4. Some cooks add a chopped onion browned with the cucumbers

title: Brown Flour Soup, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of fresh butter
- four tablespoons of flour
- one quart water
- salt
- pepper
- a little nutmeg
- one pint of milk

## Directions

1. Heat two tablespoons of fresh butter in a spider
2. add four tablespoons of flour to it and brown to light golden brown
3. then add one quart water, stirring constantly
4. Season with salt and pepper and a little nutmeg
5. Add one pint of milk
6. let boil up once or twice and serve at once

title: Brown Rappee Snuff Dentifrice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of brown rappee snuff
- one ounce of powder of bark
- one ounce and a half of powder of myrrh

## Directions

1. Mix together two ounces of brown rappee snuff, one ounce of powder of bark, and one ounce and a half of powder of myrrh

title: Brown Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon chopped onion
- one tablespoon fat
- one tablespoon of flour
- one cup of soup stock
- one teaspoon lemon juice
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry one tablespoon chopped onion in one tablespoon fat
2. Add one tablespoon of flour, one cup of soup stock, one teaspoon lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste
3. Strain before serving

title: Browning
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of coarse brown sugar
- a lump of clarified suet
- a wine-glass of port wine
- half an ounce of black pepper
- a little mace
- four spoonsful of ketchup or Harvey's sauce
- a little salt
- the peel of a lemon grated

## Directions

1. Put one pound of coarse brown sugar in a stew-pan with a lump of clarified suet
2. When it begins to froth, pour in a wine-glass of port wine, half an ounce of black pepper, a little mace, four spoonsful of ketchup or Harvey's sauce, a little salt, and the peel of a lemon grated
3. Boil all together
4. Let it grow cold, when it must be skimmed and bottled for use

title: Browning for Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. sugar
- half a pint of boiling water

## Directions

1. Put into a small yellow basin 1/4 lb. sugar
2. Put it into your oven and let it get almost like hardbake, a deep brown colour
3. While it is bubbling up pour over it half a pint of boiling water off the fire
4. Let it stand, then stir it well
5. Strain it through a sieve
6. When quite cold bottle it for use
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Brunswick Stew
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound brisket of beef
- three pounds young chicken
- one pint soup stock or water
- one pint Lima beans
- four ears cut corn
- three potatoes
- two tomatoes
- one small onion
- one teaspoon paprika
- one teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of brisket of beef and three pounds of young chicken with one pint of soup stock or water, one pint of Lima beans, four ears of cut corn (cut from cob), three potatoes diced, two tomatoes quartered; one small onion, one teaspoon of paprika and one teaspoon of salt
2. Let all these simmer until tender
3. Before serving remove the meat and any visible chicken bones

title: Brussels Sprouts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- salt
- water
- butter
- pepper
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Remove any wilted leaves from the outside of the sprouts
2. Let them stand in cold salted water from fifteen to twenty minutes
3. Put the sprouts into salted, rapidly boiling water
4. Cook, uncovered, fifteen or twenty minutes or until tender, but not until they lose their shape
5. Drain them thoroughly in a colander
6. Place them in a saucepan with butter, pepper and salt
7. Toss them until seasoned or mix them lightly with just enough white sauce to coat them

title: Buckwheat Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- two level teaspoons of brown sugar
- two cups of lukewarm water
- one cup of milk
- two cups of buckwheat
- one cup of sifted white flour
- one and one-half teaspoons of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve yeast and brown sugar in lukewarm water and scalded and cooled milk
2. Add buckwheat and sifted white flour gradually
3. Add salt
4. Beat until smooth
5. Cover and set aside in a warm place, free from draft, to rise about one hour
6. When light stir well
7. Bake on a hot griddle

title: Buns
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tea roll dough
- egg
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make same as tea rolls
2. When well risen mold into small, round buns
3. place in well-greased pans, one inch apart
4. Cover, set aside to rise until light — about one hour
5. Brush with egg, diluted with water
6. bake twenty minutes
7. Just before removing from the oven, brush with sugar moistened with a little water

title: Burnt Almond Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of sifted powdered sugar
- one cup of sifted flour
- one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
- three tablespoons of water
- one-fourth pound of almonds
- three tablespoons of white sugar
- whites of three eggs
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- rose-water

## Directions

1. Beat up four eggs with one cup of sifted powdered sugar
2. Beat until it looks like a heavy batter
3. Stir one cup of sifted flour with one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
4. Stir it into batter gradually and lightly, adding three tablespoons of water
5. Bake in jelly tin
6. For filling: Scald one-fourth pound of almonds by pouring boiling water over them and remove skins
7. Put them on a pie plate and set them in the oven to brown slightly
8. Melt three tablespoons of white sugar, without adding water, stirring it all the while
9. Stir up the almonds in this
10. Remove them from the fire and lay on a platter separately to cool
11. Make an icing of the whites of three eggs, beaten very stiff, with one pound of pulverized sugar, and flavor with rose-water
12. Spread this upon layers and cover each layer with almonds
13. When finished frost the whole cake, decorating with almonds

title: Butter Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of sugar
- lemon rind
- cinnamon
- flour
- spice
- candied orange peel
- candied lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of butter and sugar, say half a pound of each
2. Grate the rind of a lemon and add a little cinnamon
3. Add as much flour as will form it into a paste with spice
4. Roll it out and cut it into two small cakes
5. Bake
6. A piece of candied orange or lemon-peel may be put on the top of each cake

title: Butter Caper Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 spoonful of flour
- 1/2 a teacup of milk or water
- salt
- pepper
- good spoonful of capers

## Directions

1. Put in a small saucepan 2 oz. of butter, 1 spoonful of flour, 1/2 a teacup of milk or water, salt, pepper
2. Set it to warm and keep shaking the saucepan all the time till it thickens
3. Stir in a good spoonful of capers
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Butter Cressy Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large carrots
- 1 onion
- 2 oz. butter
- 1 pint and 1/2 water
- 1 tooth of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- fish
- 1 spoonful flour
- 1/2 lemon
- grated nutmeg
- dried saffron
- 1 round of bread
- 2 oz. more butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Boil and rub through a sieve 4 large carrots to a pulp.
2. Fry an onion in a saucepan with 2 oz. of butter, fry it a light brown.
3. Put in pint and 1/2 of water, then add the pulp of the carrot with a tooth of garlic chopped very fine, salt and pepper.
4. Boil some fish and strain the liquor into the soup.
5. If not thick enough stir in a basin a spoonful of flour with the juice of 1/2 a lemon, a little grated nutmeg and a pinch of dried saffron.
6. Toast a round of bread, cut it in dice, put them in the tureen with 2 oz. more of butter and a little chopped parsley.
7. Throw over the soup, and stir it well.
8. Serve the fish with parsley, boil a cauliflower or some turnips or any vegetable you like to put round the fish with anchovy or caper or tomato sauce.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Butter Kleis for Soups German Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- ginger or ground mace
- egg

## Directions

1. Rub in a basin 2 ounces of fresh butter with 3 spoonfuls of flour, a little salt, pepper, a little chopped parsley, and a little ginger or ground mace
2. Beat an egg and mix it well with the other ingredients
3. It ought to be like a thick batter
4. Take up a piece with a spoon and drop it into the boiling soup
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Butterbarches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- one-half cup of lukewarm milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- eight cups of sifted flour
- about two cups milk
- one-half cup of sugar
- four tablespoons of butter
- the grated peel of a lemon
- two or three dozen raisins seeded
- two eggs well beaten

## Directions

1. Dissolve one cake of compressed yeast in one-half cup of lukewarm milk, add a teaspoon of salt, and a tablespoon of sugar and let it rise
2. Make a soft dough of eight cups of sifted flour and as much milk as is required to work it, about two cups
3. Add the yeast, one-half cup of sugar, four tablespoons of butter dissolved in the warm milk, the grated peel of a lemon, two or three dozen raisins seeded, and two eggs well beaten
4. Work this dough perfectly smooth with the palm of your hand, adding more flour if necessary
5. Work the dough as directed, set it aside covered until it is double the bulk of the original piece of dough
6. Then work again and divide the dough into two parts, and divide each of the pieces of dough into three parts
7. Work the six pieces of dough thoroughly and then roll each piece into a long strand; three of which are to be longer than the other three
8. Braid the three long strands into one braid (should be thicker in the centre than at the end), and braid the shorter strands into one braid and lay it on top

title: Buttered Toast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- baker's bread
- butter
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Slice even slices of baker's bread, not too thin
2. put in a biscuit pan on the top rack of a very hot oven
3. brown nicely on one side, then turn and brown on the other
4. spread with butter, and a little powdered sugar, if desired
5. serve at once

title: Cabbage and Rice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine summer white cabbage
- large cup of rice
- water
- large piece of butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Scald till tender a fine summer white cabbage
2. Chop it up small, and put it into a stew-pan, with a large cup of rice, also previously scalded
3. Add a little water, a large piece of butter, salt and pepper
4. Let it stew gently till thoroughly done, stirring from time to time, and adding water and butter to prevent its getting too thick
5. There should be no gravy in the dish when served

title: Cabbage Strudel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of goose-fat
- one medium-sized cabbage
- strudel dough
- one cup of raisins and currants mixed
- two cups of granulated sugar
- one-half cup of chopped almonds
- one teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Heat one-half cup of goose-fat, add one medium-sized cabbage and let it simmer until done, stirring constantly to keep from burning
2. While cooling prepare strudel dough
3. Fill with cabbage and one cup of raisins and currants mixed, two cups of granulated sugar, one-half cup of chopped almonds and one teaspoon cinnamon
4. Roll and put little pieces of grease on top
5. Bake in hot oven and baste frequently
6. The pans in which the strudel is baked must be greased generously
7. Serve this strudel hot
8. This strudel may be made for a milk meal by substituting butter for fat

title: Cabbage, Beet and Horseradish Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of boiled beets
- two quarts of cabbage
- one cup of grated horseradish
- two cups of sugar
- two teaspoons of salt
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Take two quarts of boiled beets chopped, two quarts of cabbage chopped, one cup of grated horseradish
2. Mix with two cups of sugar and two teaspoons of salt
3. Add cold vinegar to cover, and place in gallon jar

title: Cafe a la Glace
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five tablespoons of fresh-roasted and ground coffee
- four cups of boiling water
- one-half pound of sugar
- sweetened whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take five tablespoons of fresh-roasted and ground coffee
2. Pour four cups of boiling water over it
3. Cover quickly and put on the back of the stove
4. Add one-half pound of sugar
5. When cold, press through a sieve
6. Fill in the can to be frozen
7. Let it remain in freezer five minutes longer before you begin to turn the freezer
8. Serve in glasses, and put sweetened whipped cream on the top

title: Calf's Brains (Sour)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- ice-water
- cold salted water
- vinegar
- water
- crust of rye bread
- one onion
- ten whole peppers
- one bay leaf
- one or two cloves
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Lay the brains in ice-water and then skin
2. Skin by taking them up in your hands and patting them
3. Lay in cold salted water for an hour at least
4. Put on to boil in half vinegar and half water
5. Add crust of rye bread
6. Add one onion, cut up fine
7. Add ten whole peppers
8. Add one bay leaf
9. Add one or two cloves and a little salt
10. Boil altogether about fifteen minutes
11. Serve on a platter and decorate with parsley
12. Eat cold

title: Calf's Brains Fried
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rolled cracker flour
- salt
- pepper
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Clean as described in calf's brains cooked sour
2. wipe dry
3. roll in rolled cracker flour
4. season with salt and pepper and fry as you would cullets

title: Calf's Brains Fried
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- rolled cracker flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean as described in calf's brains cooked sour
2. Wipe dry
3. Roll in rolled cracker flour
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Fry as you would cutlets

title: Calf's Feet au Fritur
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- water
- pepper
- salt
- egg
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Simmer them for four hours in water till the meat can be taken easily from the bone
2. Cut them in handsome pieces
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Dip them in egg, and sprinkle thickly with grated bread crumbs
5. Fry of a fine even brown
6. They may be served dry or with any sauce that may be approved
7. Continue to stew the liquor with the bones, and it can be used for jelly

title: Calf's Feet Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two calf's feet
- two quarts or five pints of water
- lump sugar
- lemon juice
- white wine
- lemon peel
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Boil two feet in two quarts or five pints of water, till the water has half wasted
2. Strain, and when cold, take off the fat
3. Put it in the saucepan with lump sugar, lemon juice, and white wine to taste
4. Add a little lemon peel
5. When simmered a few minutes, throw in the whites of two eggs and their shells broken
6. Let it boil about ten minutes after the scum rises
7. Pour it through a flannel bag or thick cloth, dipping the bag or cloth first into hot water
8. Pass the jelly through it until clear
9. Pour it into moulds and put them in a cool place to set

title: Calf's Feet Stewed for Invalids
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine foot
- water
- salt
- white pepper
- sprig of parsley or pinch of saffron
- thin egg sauce
- lemon juice
- sippets of toast or well boiled rice

## Directions

1. Clean and soak a fine foot
2. Put it on in very little water
3. Let it simmer till tender
4. Cut it in pieces, without removing the bone
5. Continue stewing for three hours, till they become perfectly soft
6. If the liquor boils away, add a little more water, but there should not be more liquor than can be served in the dish with the foot
7. Season with a little salt and white pepper, and a sprig of parsley, or a pinch of saffron to improve the appearance
8. A little delicately-made thin egg sauce, with a flavor of lemon juice, may be served in a sauce-tureen if approved
9. Garnish the dish with sippets of toast or well boiled rice

title: Calf's Feet, Prunes and Chestnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two calf's feet
- one-half pound Italian chestnuts
- one-eighth pound of raisins
- one pound of fine prunes
- one small onion
- one small head of celery root
- two olives
- one-eighth teaspoon of paprika
- one cup of soup stock
- a wine glass claret
- a wine glass sherry
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Season calf's feet with pepper and salt a day before using
2. Place in an iron pot one-half pound Italian chestnuts that have been scalded and skinned, then the calf's feet, one-eighth pound of raisins, one pound of fine prunes, one small onion, one small head of celery root, two olives cut in small pieces, one-eighth teaspoon of paprika, one cup of soup stock
3. Stew slowly for five hours
4. One hour before serving, while boiling, add a wine glass claret and a wine glass sherry
5. Do not stir

title: Calf's Feet, Scharf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- salt
- pepper
- clove or two of garlic

## Directions

1. Saw calf's feet into joints
2. Put on to boil within cold water and boil slowly until the gristle loosens from the bones
3. Season with salt, pepper, and a clove or two of garlic
4. Serve hot or cold to taste

title: Calf's Foot Jelly, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's foot
- one quart water
- stiff-beaten whites of two eggs
- sherry
- sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully wash one calf's foot, split and put it on with one quart water
2. Boil from four to five hours
3. Strain and let stand overnight
4. Put on stove next day and when it begins to boil add the stiff-beaten whites of two eggs
5. Boil till clear, then strain through cheesecloth
6. Add sherry and sugar to taste
7. Let it become firm before serving

title: Calf's Head Stewed
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- quart of stock made from shin of beef
- eschalots
- small head of celery
- small bunch of sweet herbs
- onion
- carrot
- mace
- dozen cloves
- piece of lemon peel
- sprig of parsley
- salt and pepper
- fine forcemeat balls
- egg
- brains
- glass and half of white wine
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and soak the head till the cheek-bone can be easily removed
2. Parboil it and cut it into pieces of moderate size
3. Place them in about a quart of stock made from shin of beef
4. Season the gravy highly with eschalots, a small head of celery, a small bunch of sweet herbs, an onion, a carrot, a little mace, a dozen cloves, a piece of lemon peel, and a sprig of parsley, salt and pepper
5. Strain the gravy before the head is added
6. Prepare fine forcemeat balls rolled in egg and fried
7. Make small fritters with the brains
8. When ready for serving, add a glass and half of white wine and the juice of a lemon to the gravy
9. Serve the forcemeat balls and brain fritters in the dish

title: Calf's Hearts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's hearts
- bread dressing
- flour
- hot fat
- hot water

## Directions

1. Remove veins and arteries from the hearts
2. Stuff with a highly seasoned bread dressing and sew
3. Dredge in flour, brown in hot fat
4. Cover with hot water, and place on the back of the stove or in a hot oven
5. Cook slowly for two or three hours
6. Thicken the liquor with flour and serve with the hearts

title: Calf's Liver And Smoked Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of calf's liver
- water
- boiling fat
- salt
- pepper
- slices of smoked beef
- potatoes
- the juice of a lemon
- a little ketchup

## Directions

1. After the liver has been properly prepared, lay it in water in another pan
2. Cut it in slices first and leave it for an hour
3. Take it out and dry it in a cloth
4. Put it on the gridiron before the fire for a few minutes
5. Have a pan on the fire with boiling fat
6. Salt and pepper the liver and fry it a nice brown
7. When nearly done put in the slices of smoked beef
8. Have some potatoes nicely mashed and lay the liver and beef upon them
9. Pour off the fat from the pan
10. Put in the juice of a lemon with a little ketchup and turn it over the liver, beef, and potatoes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Calf's Liver Fried With Sweet And Sour Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- boiling fat
- a large Spanish onion
- slices of sausage
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a spoonful of brown sugar
- 2 of vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- a cup of water
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Prepare the liver as before when it comes from the gridiron
2. Put it on a plate
3. Have on the fire a pan with boiling fat
4. Slice a large Spanish onion very fine and fry this first a light brown
5. Have at the side of the fire a sieve on a plate
6. When the onion is done put it on the sieve to drain
7. Fry the liver and some slices of sausage
8. Take them out and pour off the fat from your pan
9. Rub in a basin a tablespoonful of flour with a spoonful of brown sugar, 2 of vinegar, some pepper and salt, with a cup of water
10. Let this all boil in the pan with a little browning to colour
11. Put back the onions, then the liver and sausage
12. Have a very hot dish and dish up the liver and sausage
13. Put the onions round and the sauce over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Calf's Liver Smothered in Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose fat
- onion
- calf's liver
- fine salt
- flour
- bay leaf
- five cloves
- two peppercorns
- hot water

## Directions

1. Heat some goose fat in a stew-pan with a close-fitting lid.
2. Cut up an onion in it and when the onion is of a light yellow color, place in the liver which you have previously sprinkled with fine salt and dredged with flour.
3. Add a bay leaf, five cloves and two peppercorns.
4. Cover up tight and stew the liver, turning it occasionally and when required adding a little hot water.

title: Calf's Liver Stewed With Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- a good-sized onion
- white pepper
- salt
- a little grated nutmeg
- 1/2 pint of water
- 2 yolks of eggs
- a teaspoonful of flour
- the juice of 2 lemons
- a little chopped parsley
- a few pieces of marjoram
- a few small meat balls

## Directions

1. Prepare the liver as before
2. Put it on the gridiron for a short time
3. Cut it in pieces the size of a walnut
4. Slice a good-sized onion in a stewpan with white pepper, salt, and a little grated nutmeg
5. Add 1/2 pint of water and let it cook till done
6. Stir in a basin 2 yolks of eggs, a teaspoonful of flour, the juice of 2 lemons, a little chopped parsley, and a few pieces of marjoram
7. Turn the sauce over the liver and serve up very hot
8. You may put a few small meat balls in the dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Calf's-Foot Jelly
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- rind of a lemon, cut very thin
- a small piece of whole cinnamon
- 2 cloves
- a pinch of salt
- a little dried saffron
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- whites of 2 eggs with the shells
- 1/2 pint of sherry
- 2 wine-glasses of rum
- juice of a large lemon
- 1 bullock's foot or 2 calf's feet

## Directions

1. Through a fine sieve, let it stand till the next morning, then take off all the fat and wipe the top with a clean cloth
2. The liquor will have reduced to a quart; put it in a clean saucepan with the rind of a lemon, cut very thin, not a particle of the white or it will taste bitter
3. Add a small piece of whole cinnamon, 2 cloves, a pinch of salt, a little dried saffron, 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
4. Beat the whites of 2 eggs with the shells, stir it in well
5. Set it on to boil, keep stirring all the time; when the whites of the eggs set on the top thick move the saucepan from the fire
6. Pour in 1/2 pint of sherry and 2 wine-glasses of rum, with the juice of a large lemon well strained
7. Let it stand at the side of the fire well covered over, so that the strength of the wine and spirit does not evaporate
8. Rinse the jelly bag well in boiling water before you strain the jelly
9. Have it as near as you can to the fire, it soon cools; rinse the mould with cold water, fill it
10. Set it in a very cold place, if for company you must stand it in ice an hour before you are ready to turn it out
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Calves-Feet With Spanish Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two fine feet
- egg
- bread crumbs
- chopped parsley
- chalot
