- ground cloves
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- two fine Spanish onions
- chopped truffles or mushrooms
- suet
- cayenne
- white pepper
- white sugar
- good strong stock
- wine glass of Madeira wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean, boil and split the two fine feet
2. Dip them into egg and bread crumbs mixed with chopped parsley and chalot, a few ground cloves, a little nutmeg, pepper and salt
3. Fry them a fine brown
4. Arrange them in the dish
5. To make the sauce: slice two fine Spanish onions and put them in a saucepan, with some chopped truffles or mushrooms, a little suet, cayenne and white pepper, salt, one or two small lumps of white sugar
6. Let all simmer in some good strong stock till the gravy has nearly boiled away
7. Stir in a wine glass of Madeira wine, and a little lemon juice
8. Return it to the saucepan, to be made thoroughly hot before serving
9. Pour the sauce over the feet

title: Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, two days old
- butter
- prepared sandwich filling
- watercress or lemon
- optional: stuffed olives
- optional for cheese canapes: grated cheese, salt, pepper

## Directions

1. Cut bread oblong, diamond shaped, in rounds, or with a fluted edge cutter.
2. Toast or fry the bread crouton fashion, or butter it lightly and set in a hot oven to brown delicately.
3. While the toast is quite hot, spread with the prepared mixture.
4. Serve on a small plate with sprigs of watercress or points of lemon as a garnish.
5. Alternative: cut the bread into delicate fingers, pile log-cabin fashion, and garnish the centre with a stuffed olive.
6. For cheese canapes: sprinkle the toast thickly with grated cheese, well seasoned with salt and pepper, set in a hot oven until the cheese melts and serve immediately.

title: Candied Cherries, Pineapple and Other Fruits
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half pound of loaf sugar
- One breakfast cup of water
- Cherries or desired fruit

## Directions

1. Boil, but do not stir, one-half pound of loaf sugar in one breakfast cup of water
2. Pit some cherries, or prepare any desired fruit, and string them on a thread, then dip them in the syrup
3. Suspend them by the thread
4. When pineapples are used, slice them crosswise and dry them on a sieve or in the open air
5. Oranges should be separated into sections and dried like pineapple

title: Candied Lemon and Orange Peel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon and orange peel
- boiling water
- sugar
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Take out the greater portion of the white inside
2. Throw the rinds into boiling water and simmer gently for twenty minutes
3. Drain, weigh, and take a pound of sugar to every pound of peel
4. Put a layer of sugar and a layer of fruit into the preserving kettle
5. Stand it over a slow fire until the sugar melts
6. When melted, cook slowly until the rinds are transparent
7. Lift them out
8. Drain them and when nearly dry roll in granulated sugar

title: Candied Sweet Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter or chicken-fat
- sugar
- water
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil sweet potatoes, peel and cut into long slices.
2. Place in an earthen dish; place lumps of butter or chicken-fat if desired on each side, and sprinkle with sugar.
3. A little water or juice of half a lemon may be added.
4. Bake until the sugar and fat have candied and the potatoes are brown.

title: Canned Asparagus
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- canned asparagus
- cold water
- boiling water
- hot platter
- salad dressing or Hollandaise Sauce

## Directions

1. Open one end of the can, as indicated on wrapper, so tips will be at opening.
2. Pour off the liquid and allow cold water to run over gently and to rinse.
3. Drain and pour boiling water over them in the can and set in a hot oven to heat thoroughly.
4. When ready to serve, drain and arrange carefully on hot platter and serve same as fresh asparagus, hot on toast or cold with salad dressing, or with Hollandaise Sauce poured over.

title: Canned Corn
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one can corn
- one tablespoon butter
- one-half cup milk
- one tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. To one can of corn take one tablespoon of butter, one-half cup milk.
2. Sprinkle one tablespoon of flour over these.
3. Stir and cook about five minutes, until thoroughly hot.
4. Season to taste and serve hot.

title: Canned Fruit Frozen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- can of pears
- ice and salt
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Without opening, pack a can of pears in ice and salt, as for ice-cream
2. Let it remain for three or four hours
3. When taken out, cut the can open around the middle
4. If frozen very hard, wrap around with a towel dipped in hot water
5. The contents can then be clipped out in perfect rounds
6. Cut into slices and serve with a spoonful of whipped cream on each slice

title: Canned Gooseberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six quarts of berries
- three pints of sugar
- one pint of water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in the water, using three pints of sugar if the gooseberries are green and only half the quantity if they are ripe.
2. Add the fruit and cook fifteen minutes.

title: Canned Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- three quarts of sugar
- two quarts of water

## Directions

1. Wash peaches, put them in a square of cheese-cloth or wire basket.
2. Dip for two minutes in kettle of boiling water.
3. Plunge immediately into cold water.
4. Skin the peaches; leave whole or cut as preferred.
5. Pack peaches in hot jars.
6. Fill hot jars with hot syrup or boiling water.
7. Put tops in position.
8. Tighten tops but not airtight.
9. Place jars on false bottom in wash-boiler.
10. Let the water boil sixteen minutes.
11. Seal as directed.

title: Canned Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight quarts of peaches
- One quart of sugar
- Three quarts of water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of the sugar and water
2. Bring to a boil and skim it
3. Draw the kettle aside where the syrup will keep hot but not boil
4. Pare the peaches, cutting them in halves or not as desired; if in half leave one or two whole peaches for every jar, as the kernel improves the flavor
5. Put a layer of fruit in the kettle
6. When it begins to boil skim carefully
7. Boil gently for ten minutes
8. Put in jars and seal
9. Then cook more of the fruit in similar fashion
10. If the fruit is not ripe it will require a longer time to cook

title: Canned Plums
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of plums
- one quart of sugar
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Wash, drain and prick the plums.
2. Make a syrup of the sugar and water.
3. Put part of the fruit in the boiling syrup; cook five minutes; fill and seal the jars.
4. Put more fruit in the syrup; remove and continue the process until all the fruit has been cooked.

title: Canned Raspberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six quarts of berries
- one quart of sugar

## Directions

1. Put one quart of the fruit in the preserving kettle; heat slowly, crushing with a wooden potato masher; strain and press through a fine sieve.
2. Return the juice and pulp to the kettle; add the sugar; stir until dissolved; then add the remaining quarts of berries.
3. Boil sixteen minutes, counting from the time they begin to boil.
4. Skim well while boiling, and put into jars as directed.

title: Canned Rhubarb
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- cold water

## Directions

1. Wash the rhubarb thoroughly in pure water; cut it into pieces and pack it in sterilized jars.
2. Cover with cold water; let it stand ten minutes; pour off the water.
3. Fill again to overflowing with fresh cold water; seal with sterilized rubber rings and covers.
4. Set away in a cool, dark place.

title: Canned Rhubarb Ready to Use
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- eight ounces of loaf sugar to every quart of rhubarb

## Directions

1. Strip the skins from the stalks, and cut into small pieces as you would for pies.
2. Set the sugar over the fire with as little water as possible.
3. Throw in the rhubarb and boil ten minutes.
4. Put in jars and seal.

title: Canned Tomatoes, Stewed
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- canned tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- sugar
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Add salt and pepper to the tomatoes.
2. Add a lump of butter the size of an egg and add one tablespoon of sugar.
3. Thicken with one teaspoon of flour wet with one tablespoon of cold water, stir into the tomatoes and boil up once.

title: Caper Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- melted butter
- a few pickled capers

## Directions

1. This is merely melted butter with a few pickled capers simmered in it
2. Or they may be put into a sauce made of broth thickened with egg, and a little flour

title: Caper Sauce for Boiled Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of the liquor the mutton is boiled in
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1 spoonful of the vinegar from the capers
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 3 teaspoonfuls of capers

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 pint of the liquor the mutton is boiled in in a small saucepan and let it boil
2. Stir in a teaspoonful of flour mixed with a spoonful of the vinegar from the capers
3. Season nicely with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
4. Chop 3 teaspoonfuls of capers and put them into the sauceboat
5. Turn the sauce over them and it is ready for table
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Caramel Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of sugar
- four cups of scalded milk
- eggs
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Melt one-half cup of sugar until it is light brown in color.
2. Add four cups of scalded milk.
3. Beat the eggs, add the milk and sugar, one-quarter teaspoon of salt, one teaspoon of vanilla and bake in cups as directed for cup custard.
4. Serve with caramel sauce.

title: Caramel Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half cup of sugar
- One-half cup of boiling water
- One cup butter
- One and one-half cups of sugar
- Yolks of two eggs
- One cup of water
- Two cups of flour
- One teaspoon of vanilla
- One-half cup of flour
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- Whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Place one-half cup of sugar in pan over fire
2. Stir until liquid smokes and burns brown
3. Add one-half cup of boiling water and cook into syrup
4. Take one cup butter, one and one-half cups of sugar, yolks of two eggs, over one cup of water and two cups of flour
5. Beat all thoroughly
6. Add enough of the burnt sugar to flavor
7. Also add one teaspoon of vanilla, another half cup of flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder and whites of two eggs
8. Bake in two layers, using remainder of burnt sugar for icing

title: Caramel Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cut loaf sugar
- yolks of three eggs
- two cups of sweet milk
- one teaspoon of vanilla essence
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Put one cup cut loaf sugar in a saucepan on the stove without adding a drop of water
2. Let it melt slowly and get a nice brown without burning
3. Beat the yolks of three eggs until light
4. Stir in two cups of sweet milk
5. When the sugar is melted, stir all into the saucepan and continue stirring until the sugar is dissolved and the sauce is somewhat thickened
6. Remove from the fire and add one teaspoon of vanilla essence
7. Put in a bowl and put the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs on top
8. Serve with puddings, cakes or fritters

title: Caraway Seed Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of butter
- a pound of sugar
- three eggs
- one saltspoon of salt
- a gill of caraway seeds
- a teaspoon of powdered mace
- a cup of sour milk
- a level teaspoon of baking-soda
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat three-quarters of a pound of butter and a pound of sugar to a cream
2. Add three eggs, one saltspoon of salt, a gill of caraway seeds and a teaspoon of powdered mace, stirring all well together to a cream
3. Pour in a cup of sour milk in which a level teaspoon of baking-soda is stirred
4. Hold the cup over the mixing bowl while stirring in the soda, as it will foam over the cup
5. Stir in enough sifted flour to make a light dough, stiff enough to roll thin
6. Roll on a pastry board well dusted with flour
7. Cut in round shapes and place in baking-tins well rubbed with flour
8. Sprinkle a little sugar over the cookies and bake them in a moderate oven till a light brown
9. When cool, carefully lift the cookies from the pans with a pancake turner

title: Caraway, Or Kimmel Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoon drippings
- a little flour
- a cup of soup stock
- half a teaspoon of caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Heat a tablespoon drippings in a spider
2. Add a little flour
3. Stir smooth with a cup of soup stock, added at once, and half a teaspoon of caraway seeds

title: Cardamom Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one-half pound of sugar
- grated peel of a lemon
- one-half wineglass of brandy
- one-half pound of butter
- flour
- about two cents worth of cardamom seed
- a little rosewater
- beaten egg
- pounded almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil six eggs hard
2. When cold shell and grate the yolks (reserve the whites for salads or to garnish vegetables)
3. Add one-half pound of sugar, the grated peel of a lemon and one-half wineglass of brandy
4. Stir in one-half pound of butter which has been worked to a cream
5. Sift in as much flour as you think will allow you to roll out the dough; take as little as possible, a little over half a pound, and flour the board very thick
6. Put in about two cents worth of cardamom seed and a little rosewater
7. Cut out with a fancy cake-cutter and brush with beaten egg
8. Sprinkle pounded almonds and sugar on top
9. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Cardamom Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs, hard-boiled
- one-half pound of sugar
- the grated peel of a lemon
- one-half wineglass of brandy
- one-half pound of butter
- flour
- about two cents worth of cardamom seed
- a little rosewater

## Directions

1. Boil six eggs hard.
2. When cold shell and grate the yolks.
3. Add one-half pound of sugar, the grated peel of a lemon and one-half wineglass of brandy.
4. Stir in one-half pound of butter which has been worked to a cream.
5. Sift in as much flour as you think will allow you to roll out the dough; take as little as possible, a little over half a pound, and flour the board very thick.
6. Put in about two cents worth of cardamom seed and a little rosewater.
7. Cut out with a fancy cake-cutter.

title: Carnatzlich (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of tenderloin
- an egg
- paprika
- black pepper
- salt
- four cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of tenderloin and add an egg, a little paprika, black pepper, salt and four cloves of garlic (which have been scraped, and let stand in a little salt for ten minutes, and then mashed so it looks like dough).
2. Form this meat mixture into short sausage-like rolls.
3. Boil one-half hour and serve at once.

title: Carp Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of fresh butter
- dessert spoonful of flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- wine glass of cold water
- spoonful of fine capers

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan 1/2 lb. of fresh butter, a dessert spoonful of flour, salt, pepper, nutmeg
2. Add a wine glass of cold water
3. Put it on the fire and stir it till it comes smooth
4. Taste it
5. Add to it a spoonful of fine capers without the vinegar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Carrot Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Six ounces of grated carrot
- 6 oz. of bread crumbs
- 2 oz. of orange and lemon peel
- 6 oz. of chopped suet
- 8 eggs
- 1 lb. ground almonds
- 6 oz. of sifted sugar
- wine or brandy sauce

## Directions

1. Beat all up well together
2. Grease an earthenware mould, and sift sugar over it
3. Put in the mixture and boil for 3 hours
4. Serve with wine or brandy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Carrot Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one-third cup of butter
- one cup of grated carrots
- one cup of grated potatoes
- one cup of raisins
- one cup of currants
- two cups of bread crumbs
- one-half teaspoon of baking-soda
- one teaspoon of cloves
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of allspice
- a little syrup
- four tablespoons of whiskey

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Add a little syrup and four tablespoons of whiskey
3. Steam four hours
4. Serve with hard sauce

title: Carrot Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups of powdered sugar
- yolks of eight eggs
- one and a half cups peeled and grated raw carrots
- one cup of grated almonds
- rind of half a lemon chopped finely
- one tablespoon of wine
- whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Beat one and a half cups of powdered sugar and the yolks of eight eggs
2. Take one and a half cups peeled and grated raw carrots and stir all together
3. Add one cup of grated almonds, the rind of half a lemon chopped finely, one tablespoon of wine, and last the beaten whites of the eggs
4. Bake in a well-buttered and flour-sprinkled form at least one hour in a slow oven

title: Carrot Schalet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of carrots
- one heaping tablespoon of drippings or chicken fat
- four tablespoons of sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- grated peel of one lemon
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- grated nutmeg
- three tablespoons of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- pinch of salt
- beaten egg whites
- fat for pudding-dish

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of carrots, let them get perfectly cold before grating them.
2. In the meanwhile cream a heaping tablespoon of drippings or chicken fat and four tablespoons of sugar.
3. Add gradually the yolks of four eggs, the grated peel of one lemon, one teaspoon of cinnamon, a little grated nutmeg, three tablespoons of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, pinch of salt, and the beaten whites last.
4. Heat a few tablespoons of fat in a pudding-dish.
5. Pour in the mixture and bake in a moderate oven one hour.
6. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon and return to oven for a few moments to brown.
7. Serve hot.

title: Carrot Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen carrots
- two heads of celery
- two onions
- a good stock, about two quarts
- a little lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a dozen carrots scraped clean and rasp them, but do not use the core
2. Take two heads of celery and two onions thinly sliced
3. Season to taste
4. Pour over a good stock, say about two quarts
5. Boil it
6. Pass it through a sieve
7. Return it to the saucepan
8. Boil it up and squeeze in a little lemon juice or add a little vinegar

title: Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt
- boiling water
- butter
- pepper or cream sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots lightly
2. Cut them into large dice or slices and drop them into salted boiling water, allowing one teaspoon of salt to one quart of water
3. Boil until tender
4. Drain and serve with butter and pepper or with cream sauce

title: Carrots and Peas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of carrots
- one pint cooked green peas
- two tablespoons of flour
- salt, pepper and sugar to taste
- two tablespoons of fat or butter
- one-half cup of milk or soup stock

## Directions

1. Wash, scrape and cut one pint of carrots in small cubes
2. Cook until tender
3. Drain and reserve one-half cup of carrot water
4. Mix carrots well with one pint cooked green peas
5. Sprinkle with two tablespoons of flour, salt, pepper and sugar to taste
6. Add two tablespoons of fat or butter, one-half cup of milk or soup stock and carrot water
7. Boil a little longer and serve

title: Carrots au Beurre
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- a piece of butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Boil them enough to be perfectly tender, then cut them in quarters, and again in lengths of three inches
2. drain them from the water, and put to them a piece of butter, salt and pepper
3. simmer them for a few minutes without boiling; a large piece of butter must be used

title: Carrots Boiled With Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- breast of lamb or mutton
- salt
- cabbage
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of brown sugar
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pare the carrots and cut them into finger lengths, in thin strips.
2. Put a breast of lamb or mutton on to boil, having previously salted it well.
3. When boiling, add the carrots and cover closely.
4. Prepare the cabbage as usual and lay in with the mutton and carrots; boil two hours at least.
5. When all has boiled tender, skim off some of the fat and put it into a spider.
6. Add to this one tablespoon of flour, one tablespoon of brown sugar and one-half teaspoon of cinnamon.
7. Keep adding gravy from the mutton until well mixed, and pour all over the mutton and vegetables.
8. Serve together on a platter.

title: Carrots with Brisket of Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds fat brisket of beef
- salt and pepper
- two bunches carrots
- boiling water
- two tablespoons fat
- two tablespoons flour
- one cup carrot and meat liquid

## Directions

1. Salt and pepper two pounds of fat brisket of beef and let stand several hours.
2. Wash and scrape two bunches of carrots and cut in small cubes.
3. Place in kettle with meat, cover with boiling water and cook several hours or until the meat and carrots are tender, and the water is half boiled away.
4. Heat two tablespoons of fat in a spider, let brown slightly, add two tablespoons of flour and gradually one cup of carrot and meat liquid.
5. Place in kettle with meat and carrots and boil until carrots become browned.

title: Casserole Au Riz
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- one egg
- a couple of tablespoons of strong gravy
- fricassee of fowl and sweetbread
- rich white sauce

## Directions

1. Boil some rice till quite tender
2. Make it into a firm paste with one egg and a couple of tablespoons of strong gravy
3. Line the inside of a mould with the paste of sufficient thickness to turn out without breaking
4. After it is turned out the rice must stand till cold, before it is removed from the mould
5. Fill the rice with fricassee of fowl and sweetbread, with a rich white sauce
6. Place it in the oven to become hot and brown

title: Caudle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- grits
- a few spices
- white wine
- a little brandy
- nutmeg
- lemon peel
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a fine smooth gruel of grits with a few spices boiled in it
2. Strain it carefully and warm as required
3. Add white wine and a little brandy, nutmeg, lemon peel, and sugar according to taste

title: Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Trim off the outside leaves and cut the stalk even with the flower
2. Let it stand upside down in cold salted water for twenty minutes
3. Put it into a generous quantity of rapidly boiling salted water and cook it uncovered about twenty minutes or until tender, but not so soft as to fall to pieces
4. Remove any scum from the water before lifting out the cauliflower
5. If not perfectly white, rub a little white sauce over it
6. Serve with it a white, a Bechamel, or a Hollandaise sauce

title: Cauliflower (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- minced onion
- cauliflower
- water
- salt
- white pepper
- sour salt
- red tomatoes
- one-fourth cup of rice
- butter, fat or olive oil

## Directions

1. Brown a minced onion
2. Add cauliflower cut in pieces with a small quantity of water
3. Stew, add salt, white pepper, a little sour salt and red tomatoes
4. When half done add one-fourth cup of rice
5. Cook until rice is done
6. The onion may be browned either in butter, fat or olive oil, as desired

title: Cauliflower Croquettes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cooked cauliflower
- one small onion
- one small bunch of parsley
- one-half cup of bread crumbs
- one well-beaten egg
- cracker dust

## Directions

1. Finely chop cold cooked cauliflower
2. Mix in one small, finely chopped onion
3. Mix in one small bunch of parsley finely chopped
4. Mix in one-half cup of bread crumbs and one well-beaten egg
5. Carefully mix and mold into croquette forms
6. Dip in cracker dust
7. Fry in deep, smoking fat until a light brown

title: Cauliflower or Asparagus (Hungarian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower or asparagus
- salt water
- bread crumbs
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook in salt water until tender
2. Spread with bread crumbs and butter
3. Pour some sour cream over the vegetable and bake until the crumbs are a golden brown

title: Cauliflower Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- lemon juice
- lettuce leaves
- French dressing
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the cauliflower carefully, tie in a cloth and cook in boiling salt water until thoroughly tender
2. When done, remove the cloth, pour two tablespoons of lemon juice over the cauliflower and set it on the ice to cool
3. When ready to serve, separate the flowerets, lay them on lettuce leaves
4. Cover with French dressing and sprinkle one tablespoon of chopped parsley over the top

title: Cauliflower with Brown Crumbs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- hot cauliflower
- two tablespoons fine bread crumbs
- one tablespoon of hot butter or fat

## Directions

1. Drain and place the hot cauliflower in serving dish
2. Pour over it two tablespoons fine bread crumbs browned in one tablespoon of hot butter or fat
3. Serve hot

title: Caviar and Salmon Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rye bread
- mustard
- caviar
- smoked salmon
- pickle

## Directions

1. Take a piece of rye bread and cut round with a biscuit cutter.
2. Spread with mustard.
3. Put some caviar in centre of the bread.
4. Place strips of smoked salmon around the caviar and strips of pickle around the salmon.

title: Caviar Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- ice cold caviar
- one teaspoon of chopped onion
- one teaspoon chopped parsley
- quarters of lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the bread about one-quarter of an inch thick and two inches square or round
2. Toast the bread
3. Spread over each slice a teaspoon of ice cold caviar
4. Mix one teaspoon of chopped onion and one teaspoon chopped parsley
5. Spread the mixture over the caviar
6. Serve with quarters of lemon

title: Celery Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about six pieces of celery root
- salt
- pepper
- onion powder
- one teaspoon of home-made mustard
- plenty of mayonnaise
- caviar or sardellen butter
- slice of beets
- lettuce leaf

## Directions

1. Boil about six pieces of celery root
2. When soft, peel and mash
3. Season with salt, pepper, a little onion powder, a teaspoon of home-made mustard and plenty of mayonnaise
4. Shape into pyramids, put mayonnaise on the top of the pyramid, and on top of that either a little well-seasoned caviar or some sardellen butter shaped in a pastry-bag
5. Serve on a slice of beets and a lettuce leaf

title: Celery Root Baskets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large celery roots
- beets
- French dressing
- sugar
- vinegar
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Buy large celery roots, parboil them and cut in shape of baskets and scallop the edge
2. Boil beets until soft and cut them in small balls (like potato-balls)
3. Set celery root baskets in French dressing for several hours to flavor, and the beet-balls in boiling sugar and vinegar
4. Fill the baskets with pickled beet-balls
5. Roll lettuce and cut it into shreds and put it around the celery root basket

title: Celery Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of chopped celery
- two tablespoons of chopped walnuts
- two tablespoons of chopped olives
- quarter of a cup of Mayonnaise dressing
- thin buttered bread

## Directions

1. Spread between slices of thin buttered bread.

title: Celery Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- heart of a white head of celery
- 1/2 pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- 1 yolk of an egg
- juice of a lemon
- a couple of spoonfuls of the liquor the turkey was boiled in
- a little of the green leaves of the celery

## Directions

1. Boil the heart of celery in 1/2 pint of water with a little salt and pepper
2. When tender rub it through a sieve
3. Beat in a basin one yolk of an egg with the juice of the lemon
4. Rub the celery in with a couple of spoonfuls of the liquor the turkey was boiled in
5. Chop a little of the green leaves of the celery and put them in the sauceboat
6. Send it to table
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Celery Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about four to five heads of celery
- half a pint of veal gravy
- a spoonful of flour
- the yolks of three eggs
- white pepper
- salt
- the juice of one lemon
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cut in small pieces from about four to five heads of celery, which if not very young must be peeled
2. Simmer it till tender in half a pint of veal gravy
3. Add a spoonful of flour, the yolks of three eggs, white pepper, salt, and the juice of one lemon
4. Previously mix these together with a little water till perfectly smooth and thin
5. Stir in with the sauce

title: Chalk and Myrrh Dentifrice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of prepared chalk
- half the quantity of powdered myrrh

## Directions

1. Mix two ounces of prepared chalk with half the quantity of powdered myrrh

title: Charcoal and Magnesia Dentifrice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- charcoal, reduced to an impalpable powder
- equal quantity of magnesia

## Directions

1. Reduce charcoal to an impalpable powder
2. mix with an equal quantity of magnesia

title: Charcoal, Kino, and Sage Dentifrice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of finely powdered charcoal
- one ounce of red kino
- a table spoonful of the leaves of sage, dried and powdered

## Directions

1. Mix one ounce of finely powdered charcoal, one ounce of red kino, and a table spoonful of the leaves of sage, dried and powdered

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce of isinglass
- 1/2 pint of water
- piece of vanilla
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- milk
- savoy finger cakes
- ratafias
- dried cherries
- oil of sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Break isinglass small and boil it in 1/2 pint of water
2. Put in a piece of vanilla to boil with
3. When the isinglass is dissolved strain it through a sieve
4. Beat the yolks of 8 eggs
5. Stir in 1/2 pint of almond milk or if for a supper 1 pint of milk
6. Beat the whites to a strong snow
7. Stir it into the eggs, isinglass, and almond milk
8. Take a round mould and grease it well with the oil of sweet almonds
9. Line it with savoy finger cakes, crossing them at the bottom to form a star in the mould
10. Put several ratafias and a few dried cherries
11. When this is fixed well, pour your mixture in gently
12. Cover the top with the finger cakes like the bottom
13. Put it in ice or a very cold place
14. Pass a hot cloth round
15. Turn it out on your dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cheese and Nut Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- thin rounds from rye bread
- one cream cheese
- salt
- paprika
- one stalk of chopped celery
- one-fourth cup of chopped nut meats
- buttered bread
- stuffed olive

## Directions

1. Cut thin rounds from rye bread.
2. Rub one cream cheese to a cream, season to taste with salt and paprika.
3. Add one stalk of chopped celery, and one-fourth cup of chopped nut meats.
4. Spread on buttered bread and place a slice of stuffed olive on top, in the centre of each piece of bread.

title: Cheese and Sweet Green Peppers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of cheese
- one tablespoon of chopped peppers
- one tablespoon of butter
- paprika
- salt
- mustard
- hot buttered toast

## Directions

1. Melt two ounces of cheese
2. Add a tablespoon of chopped peppers and the same amount of butter
3. Add a little paprika, salt, and if liked, mustard
4. When the ingredients have been well blended pour the mixture on hot buttered toast and serve

title: Cheese Balls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed cream cheese
- butter
- cream
- a little paprika
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Take mashed cream cheese
2. Add butter, cream and a little paprika
3. Chop either green peppers, almonds or olives in this mixture, or add the juice of an onion
4. Roll into small balls
5. Serve on lettuce leaves

title: Cheese Balls, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of cream cheese
- one-quarter of a pound of chopped figs
- one-quarter of a pound of chopped walnuts
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Take one cake of cream cheese, one-quarter of a pound of chopped figs, one-quarter of a pound of chopped walnuts.
2. Roll into balls and serve on lettuce leaves.

title: Cheese Balls, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake Neufchatel cheese
- a piece of butter
- one tablespoon of cream
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- six dashes of Tabasco Sauce
- chopped pecan nuts

## Directions

1. Mix one cake Neufchatel cheese, a piece of butter the size of the cheese, one tablespoon of cream, one-quarter teaspoon of salt and six dashes of Tabasco Sauce.
2. Form one large ball or several small ones and roll in chopped pecan nuts.

title: Cheese Blintzes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one cup water
- one cup sifted flour
- butter or oil
- one-half pound potcheef
- butter size of an egg
- one egg
- pinch of salt
- cinnamon
- sugar
- grated peel of a lemon

## Directions

1. Beat up one egg, one-half teaspoon of salt with a fork
2. Add one cup of water and one cup of sifted flour
3. Beat until smooth
4. Grease a frying-pan very slightly with butter or oil
5. Pour in two tablespoons of the batter, tilting the pan so as to allow the batter to run all over the pan
6. Fry over a low heat on one side only
7. Turn out the semi-cooked cakes on a clean cloth with the uncooked side uppermost and let cool
8. Prepare filling using one-half pound of potcheef, a piece of butter size of an egg, one egg, pinch of salt, a little cinnamon and sugar to taste and grated peel of a lemon
9. Spread this mixture on the cooled dough
10. Fold over and tuck the edges in well
11. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and cinnamon
12. Fry in plenty of oil or butter
13. Serve hot

title: Cheese Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six thick slices of stale bread
- butter
- milk
- thinly sliced cheese
- one-half cup of milk
- one-half teaspoon of dry mustard

## Directions

1. Take six thick slices of stale bread, well buttered
2. Cut them in two
3. Dip into milk
4. Place in a baking dish with alternating layers of thinly sliced cheese, having cheese for top
5. Add half a cup of milk into which a half teaspoon of dry mustard has been put
6. Bake in quick oven fifteen minutes
7. Serve at once

title: Cheese Cake or Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of cheese
- sweet butter the size of an egg
- one-half cup of sugar
- yolks of three eggs
- pinch of salt
- peel of a lemon
- one-half cup of cleaned currants
- citron
- kuchen dough or pie dough
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Take one and one-half cups of cheese, rub smooth with a silver or wooden spoon through a colander or sieve
2. Rub a piece of sweet butter the size of an egg to a cream
3. Add gradually one-half cup of sugar and the yolks of three eggs, a pinch of salt
4. Grate in the peel of a lemon
5. Add one-half cup of cleaned currants and a little citron cut up very fine
6. Line two pie-plates with some kuchen dough or pie dough, roll it out quite thin
7. Butter the pie-plates quite heavily, and let the dough in them rise at least a quarter of an hour before putting in the cheese mixture
8. Whip up the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth and stir through the cheese mixture
9. Put the cheese into the plates and bake immediately

title: Cheese Fondue
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of bread crumbs
- one scant cup of milk
- a speck of bicarbonate of soda
- hot water
- one egg yolk and white beaten separately
- one-half cup of dry cheese grated
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt
- pepper
- dry crumbs moistened with butter

## Directions

1. Soak one-half cup of bread crumbs in one scant cup of milk
2. Dissolve a speck of bicarbonate of soda in a drop of hot water and add to the milk
3. Add one egg, yolk and white beaten separately
4. Add one-half cup of dry cheese grated
5. Add one tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste
6. Beat well, pour into a well buttered baking dish
7. Strew dry crumbs moistened with butter over the top
8. Bake in a hot oven until light brown
9. Serve at once in the dish in which it is baked

title: Cheese Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one cup of cheese cut up
- four egg yolks beaten
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- four egg whites beaten stiff
- a pinch of baking powder

## Directions

1. Cook in double boiler one cup of milk
2. Add one tablespoon of butter and one tablespoon of flour blended together
3. Cook till thick
4. Add one cup of cheese cut up and stir till dissolved
5. Remove from fire and stir in yolks of four eggs beaten
6. Add one-half teaspoon of salt and pepper
7. Fold in whites of four eggs beaten stiff and a pinch of baking powder
8. Bake in a buttered dish one-half hour

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half tablespoons of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one and one-half cups of rich milk
- pepper
- salt
- four egg yolks
- three-quarters of a cup of grated cheese
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Dissolve one and one-half tablespoons of butter, add one tablespoon of flour, stir until it loosens from the pan.
2. Add one and one-half cups of rich milk, pepper and salt.
3. Take from the fire, add gradually four egg yolks and three-quarters of a cup of grated cheese, then the stiffly beaten whites of eggs.
4. Bake in a hot oven in china ramekins about fifteen minutes and serve immediately.

title: Cheese Straws
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of flour
- two tablespoons of butter
- four tablespoons of grated cheese
- yolk of one egg
- dash of cayenne pepper
- ice-water

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, grated cheese, yolk of one egg and cayenne pepper as little as possible
2. Moisten with enough ice-water
3. Roll out about a quarter of an inch thick
4. Cut into long, narrow strips
5. Shake a little more cheese on top
6. Bake in hot oven

title: Cheese Timbals for Twelve People
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- four tablespoons of flour
- one-quarter pound imported Swiss cheese grated
- four egg yolks
- two egg whites
- tomato sauce
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take one pint of milk, four tablespoons of flour, and use enough of the milk to dissolve the flour
2. Put the balance in double boiler
3. When it boils, add the dissolved flour
4. Add one-quarter pound imported Swiss cheese grated
5. Let these two boil for fifteen minutes
6. When cool, add the yolks of four eggs, drop one in at a time and beat
7. Strain through a fine sieve about ten minutes before you put in the pans
8. Beat the whites of two eggs and put in the above and mix
9. Grease timbal forms, fill three-quarters full only
10. Bake in pan of boiling water twenty minutes
11. Let them stand about two minutes, turn out on little plates, and serve with tomato sauce with a sprig of parsley put on top of each one

title: Cheesecakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of butter
- four ounces of loaf-sugar
- rind of three lemons
- juice of one lemon
- three well-beaten eggs
- nutmeg
- spoonful of brandy
- light puff paste

## Directions

1. Warm four ounces of butter, mix it with the same quantity of loaf-sugar sifted
2. Grate in the rind of three lemons, squeeze in the juice of one
3. Add three well-beaten eggs, a little nutmeg, and a spoonful of brandy
4. Put this mixture into small tins lined with a light puff paste, and bake

title: Cheesecakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ozs. of butter
- 4 ozs. of castor sugar
- the rind of 3 lemons
- the juice of 1 lemon
- 3 eggs
- a spoonful of brandy
- a little grated nutmeg
- rich puff-paste

## Directions

1. Warm 4 ozs. of butter, mix with 4 ozs. of castor sugar
2. Grate in the rind of 3 lemons, the juice of one (remember always where the juice of lemon is used to strain it)
3. Beat well 3 eggs, a spoonful of brandy, a little grated nutmeg
4. Mix all well together
5. Line your tartlet pans with rich puff-paste
6. Put a small quantity in each
7. Bake in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Chegados
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- half a pint of water
- a moderately sized cocoa nut
- rose water
- the beaten yolks of six eggs
- thin nouilles pastry
- light brown baking

## Directions

1. Clarify a pound of sugar in half a pint of water
2. Peel and grate a moderately sized cocoa nut and add it to the syrup
3. Let it simmer till perfectly soft, putting rose water occasionally to prevent its becoming too dry
4. Stir it continually to prevent burning
5. Let it cool and mix it with the beaten yolks of six eggs
6. Make a thin nouilles pastry and cut it into rounds of the size of a tea-cup
7. Pinch up the edges deep enough to form a shape and fill them with the sweet meat
8. Bake of a light brown

title: Cherries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- half a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as you would for preserving.
2. After putting the cherries into the syrup do not let them boil more than five minutes; then fill your cans to overflowing.
3. Seal immediately and then screw tighter as they grow cold.
4. Remove the little bag of stones which you have boiled with the syrup.

title: Cherries for Pies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- two tablespoons of sugar to a quart of fruit
- one-half cup of water to each quart of cherries

## Directions

1. Stem the cherries — do not pit them.
2. Pack tight in glass fruit jars.
3. Cover with syrup.
4. Let them boil fifteen minutes from the time they begin to boil.

title: Cherries in Brandy or Rum
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fine Morelia cherries
- 1 oz. of sifted sugar per bottle
- brandy or rum

## Directions

1. Pick some fine Morelia cherries, the larger the better
2. Cut off half the stalk
3. Put them in dry bottles
4. Put 1 oz. of sifted sugar in each bottle
5. Fill up with cherries
6. Fill up with brandy or rum
7. Cork the bottles
8. Put skin over them tied tightly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cherry Bounce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- little wild cherries
- sugar
- whole spice
- cloves
- stick cinnamon
- good whiskey

## Directions

1. Wash carefully the little wild cherries
2. sugar plentifully
3. add whole spice, cloves (with the heads removed) and stick cinnamon
4. Fewer cloves than the other spices
5. Get good whiskey and allow one-half as much cherries as whiskey
6. To a quart bottle allow scant half pint sugared cherries to one and one-half pints of whiskey
7. Bottle and seal
8. Let stand at least two months
9. Open, shake bottle well and taste
10. if necessary add more sugar
11. Seal again, and let stand another month

title: Cherry Brandy
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of brandy
- two quarts of cherries
- one pound of cut loaf sugar

## Directions

1. To one gallon of brandy allow two quarts of cherries
2. Mash and pound them until all the stones are broken
3. put in the brandy and add a pound of cut loaf sugar
4. Set in the sun for two or three weeks
5. shake daily
6. strain and bottle

title: Cherry Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- cake-pan
- kuchen dough
- two pounds of cherries
- sugar
- four eggs
- one cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Line a cake-pan, which has been well-buttered, with a thin layer of kuchen dough
2. Stone two pounds of cherries and lay them on a sieve with a dish underneath to catch the juice
3. Sprinkle sugar over them and bake
4. In the meantime beat up four eggs with a cup of sugar, beat until light and add the cherry juice
5. Draw the kuchen to the oven door, pour this mixture over it and bake

title: Cherry Conserve
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three and one-half pounds of large red cherries
- two and a half pounds of sugar
- one-quarter pound of seeded raisins
- juice and pulp of three oranges

## Directions

1. Take three and one-half pounds of large red cherries, stone them and cook for fifteen minutes.
2. Heat two and a half pounds of sugar in the oven.
3. Add it to the cherries.
4. Also add one-quarter pound of seeded raisins and the juice and pulp of three oranges.
5. Cook until the mixture is as thick as marmalade.

title: Cherry Diplomate
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white cake
- maraschino or fine brandy
- plain white ice-cream
- cherry ice
- candied cherries
- strawberry ice-cream or plain white vanilla
- ice

## Directions

1. Line a mold with white cake, thinly sliced
2. Previously dip cake in maraschino or some other fine brandy
3. Fill in with plain white ice-cream
4. Add a layer of cherry ice
5. Add a layer of candied cherries
6. Add a layer of cherry ice
7. Add a layer of strawberry ice-cream or the plain white vanilla
8. Finish it up with a layer of cake again
9. Be sure to dip the cake in the maraschino
10. Cover all up tight and pack in ice until wanted

title: Cherry Marmalade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of sweet cherries
- one pound of sour cherries
- two pounds of sugar
- cinnamon bark
- cloves
- brandied paper

## Directions

1. To three pounds of sweet and one pound of sour cherries allow two pounds of sugar.
2. Weigh the cherries when stemmed and pitted.
3. Make a syrup of the sugar, add cinnamon bark and cloves.
4. Put in the sweet cherries first, adding the sour ones half an hour later.
5. Boil down thick and cover the jars with brandied paper.

title: Cherry Pie, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich paste
- cornstarch
- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with rich paste
2. sprinkle cornstarch lightly over the bottom crust
3. fill with cherries and regulate the quantity of sugar you scatter over them by their sweetness
4. Bake with an upper crust, secure the edges well by pinching firmly together
5. Eat cold

title: Cherry Pie, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the stems out of your cherries and put them in an earthen crock
2. set them in the oven until they get hot
3. Take them out and seed them
4. Make tarts with or without tops and sugar to your taste

title: Cherry Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of crackers or bread crumbs
- one quart of boiling milk
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- a good pinch of salt
- four eggs
- a cup and a half of sugar
- a little ground cinnamon
- a quart of stoned cherries

## Directions

1. Scald a pint of crackers or bread crumbs in a quart of boiling milk
2. Add a piece of butter the size of an egg, a good pinch of salt, four eggs, a cup and a half of sugar, a little ground cinnamon and a quart of stoned cherries
3. Bake in quick oven

title: Cherry Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of stale rye bread
- a wineglass of red wine
- two tablespoons of almonds
- yolks of four eggs
- half a cup of powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- one-half pound of sweet cherries
- one-half pound of sour cherries
- butter

## Directions

1. Grate one-half pound of stale rye bread and wet with a wineglass of red wine
2. Pound two tablespoons of almonds
3. Stir the yolks of four eggs with half a cup of powdered sugar and flavor with cinnamon
4. Add the grated bread and almonds
5. Stone one-half pound each of sweet and sour cherries
6. Mix all thoroughly with the beaten whites added last
7. Do not take the juice of the cherries
8. Butter the pudding mold well before putting in the mixture
9. To be eaten cold

title: Cherry Roley-Poley
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich baking-powder biscuit dough
- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a rich baking-powder biscuit dough and roll it out until it is about two-thirds of an inch thick
2. Pit and stew enough cherries to make a thick layer of fruit and add sugar to taste
3. Spread them over the dough thickly and roll it up, taking care to keep the cherries from falling out
4. Wrap a cloth around it and sew it up loosely with coarse thread
5. Allow plenty of room for the dough to rise
6. Lay the roley-poley on a plate, set it in a steamer and steam for an hour and a half
7. Serve in slices with cream or sauce

title: Cherry Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of sago
- one cup of boiling water
- one quart of large red or black cherries
- one cup of claret
- one tablespoon of broken cinnamon
- one-fourth cup of sugar
- one-half lemon
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Cook two tablespoons of sago in one cup of boiling water until tender, add more as water boils down.
2. Put one quart of large red or black cherries, one cup of claret, one tablespoon of broken cinnamon, one-fourth cup of sugar, and one-half lemon sliced fine, up to boil and let boil fifteen minutes; add the cooked sago, let boil up and pour very gradually over the well-beaten yolks of two eggs.
3. Serve cold.

title: Cherry Strudel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- pinch of salt
- lukewarm water
- two quarts of sour cherries
- stale bread
- peel of half a lemon
- one cup of sugar
- ground cinnamon
- four ounces of pounded sweet almonds
- melted goose-oil or butter

## Directions

1. Make a dough of two cups of flour, a pinch of salt and a little lukewarm water; do not make it too stiff, but smooth
2. Slap the dough back and forth repeatedly for about fifteen minutes
3. Put the dough in a warm, covered bowl and set it in a warm place for half an hour
4. In the meantime stem and pit two quarts of sour cherries
5. Grate into them some stale bread (about a plateful); also the peel of half a lemon, and mix
6. Add one cup of sugar, some ground cinnamon and about four ounces of pounded sweet almonds, mix all thoroughly
7. Roll out the dough as thin as possible, lay aside the rolling-pin and pull, or rather stretch the dough as thin as tissue paper
8. Walk all around the table, for when well stretched it will cover more than the size of an ordinary table
9. Pull off all of the thick edge, for it must be very thin to be good (save the pieces for another strudel)
10. Pour a little melted goose-oil or butter over this
11. Sprinkle the bread, sugar, almonds, cherries, etc., over it
12. Roll the strudel together into a long roll
13. Have ready a long baking-pan well greased with either butter or goose-fat
14. Fold the strudel into the shape of a pretzel
15. Butter or grease top also and bake a light brown
16. Baste often while baking
17. Eat warm

title: Cherry Syrup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mash and pound the cherries until the stones are all broken
2. press through a cloth
3. Use a pound of sugar to a quart of juice
4. boil, skim and bottle
5. When cold, seal

title: Cherry, Currant, and Raspberry Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. cherries
- 1/2 pint of raspberries
- 1/2 pint of currants
- 1/2 lb. of moist sugar
- puff paste or medium paste

## Directions

1. Pick, wash, and drain your fruit well
2. Put to stew with 1/2 lb. of moist sugar
3. If too much syrup, strain off the syrup and put it in a bottle to drink with cold water
4. Let the fruit get cold
5. Line a dish (if for company with puff paste, if for ordinary occasions with medium paste)
6. Cover over, and follow out directions for baking
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Chesnut Paste for Rendering the Hands White and Soft
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen fine large chesnuts
- milk
- rose water

## Directions

1. Boil a dozen fine large chesnuts, peeled and skinned, in milk
2. When soft beat them till perfectly smooth with rose water
3. Throw a tea-spoonful of this mixture into the water before washing the hands

title: Chestnut Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chestnuts
- a cup of milk
- apple sauce
- currant jelly
- lemon juice
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of chestnuts fifteen minutes
2. Shell and skin them, then put back on stove with a cup of milk and boil till tender
3. Rub through a colander
4. Butter a mold, line it with the pulp, then add a layer of apple sauce that has been colored with currant jelly, then another layer of chestnuts, and again apple sauce
5. Squeeze lemon juice over all, and bake in a moderate oven
6. Turn out on a platter and serve with whipped cream colored with currant jelly

title: Chestnut Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chestnuts
- one cup of milk
- butter
- apple sauce
- currant jelly
- lemon juice
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of chestnuts fifteen minutes.
2. Shell and skin them, then put back on stove with a cup of milk and boil till tender.
3. Rub through a colander.
4. Butter a mold, line it with the pulp, then add a layer of apple sauce that has been colored with currant jelly, then another layer of chestnuts, and again apple sauce.
5. Squeeze lemon juice over all, and bake in a moderate oven.
6. Turn out on a platter and serve with whipped cream colored with currant jelly.

title: Chestnut Puree
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chestnuts
- two cups of milk
- salt and pepper
- one teaspoon of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put one pound of chestnuts, which have been shelled and skinned, on to boil in two cups of milk
2. Cook until tender
3. Mash smooth
4. If necessary add more milk while boiling
5. Strain and season with salt and pepper and one teaspoon of fresh butter
6. Serve hot

title: Chestnut Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled chestnuts
- shredded celery
- bananas
- apples
- mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Combine equal parts of boiled chestnuts and shredded celery with bananas, apples, celery and chestnuts
2. Dress with mayonnaise and serve on lettuce leaves

title: Chestnut Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one slice each of white and brown bread
- butter
- chestnuts
- hard-boiled eggs
- Mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Boil chestnuts tender, peel and rub through a sieve.
2. Mash with hard-boiled eggs to a paste and moisten with Mayonnaise.
3. Cut white and brown bread thin and butter.
4. Spread with the chestnut mixture.

title: Chestnut Stuffing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- salted water
- chicken fat
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- soup stock

## Directions

1. Shell and blanch two cups of chestnuts
2. Cook in boiling salted water until tender
3. Drain and force through a colander or a potato ricer
4. Add one-fourth cup of melted chicken fat, one-fourth teaspoon of pepper, three-fourths of a teaspoon of salt, one cup of grated bread crumbs
5. Add enough soup stock to moisten

title: Chestnut Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chestnuts in the shells
- yolks of six eggs
- six tablespoons of sugar
- three teaspoons of baking-powder
- whites of six eggs
- one-half pint of cream

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of chestnuts in the shells
2. Peel them while warm
3. Put nuts through potato ricer or colander
4. Beat well the yolks of six eggs with six tablespoons of sugar
5. Add all the chestnut puree but two or three tablespoons reserved for top of torte
6. Add three teaspoons of baking-powder and the well-beaten whites of the six eggs
7. Bake in moderate oven fifteen to twenty minutes
8. Whip one-half pint of cream
9. Add to this the chestnut puree which was reserved and a little sugar
10. Garnish torte with this mixture

title: Chestnuts and Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of chestnuts
- one pint of prunes
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel one pint of chestnuts and skin
2. Boil until tender
3. Boil one pint of prunes till tender
4. Mix chestnuts and prunes together, leaving whatever of sauce there is on the prunes
5. Season with sugar, cinnamon, and lemon juice
6. Cook all together

title: Chestnuts and Raisins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of chestnuts
- boiling water
- cold water
- one cup of raisins
- two bay leaves
- stick cinnamon
- salt
- one teaspoon of butter
- two tablespoons of white wine
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of vinegar
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Remove the outer shells from one quart of chestnuts
2. Pour boiling water over them and remove the skins
3. Put in cold water for half an hour
4. Drain and put on in a boiler with cold water and boil until tender
5. Do not add any salt as it toughens them
6. In another boiler put one cup of raisins which have been stemmed and cleaned
7. Cover with cold water
8. Add two bay leaves and some stick cinnamon
9. Boil until tender
10. Pour them into the boiler containing the chestnuts
11. Add a pinch of salt and one teaspoon of butter
12. Continue until chestnuts are done
13. Add two tablespoons of white wine, two tablespoons of sugar, one-half teaspoon of vinegar
14. Thicken with one tablespoon of flour dissolved in water
15. More sugar or vinegar may be added to suit taste
16. Boil a few minutes, then serve

title: Chestnuts with Celery (Turkish)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- table celery
- celery root
- roasted chestnuts
- two tablespoons of olive oil
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and cut table celery and some celery root
2. Take roasted chestnuts, season with two tablespoons of olive oil
3. Put on to boil with the celery and one tablespoon of lemon juice
4. Boil all until celery is tender
5. Season with salt and pepper and serve hot

title: Chicken (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- salt
- chestnuts

## Directions

1. Brown a chicken, cover with water and season, cook until tender.
2. When chicken is tender; slash the skin of chestnuts, put them in oven and roast, then skin them, put in chicken and let come to a boil and serve with the chicken.

title: Chicken a la Italienne
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold chicken or turkey
- one tablespoon of olive oil
- one teaspoon of tarragon vinegar or lemon juice
- a few drops of onion juice
- salt
- pepper
- finely chopped parsley
- fritter batter
- boiling fat
- tomato or brown sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the remains of cold chicken (or turkey) into pieces about an inch long
2. Marinate them in a bowl containing one tablespoon of olive oil, one teaspoon of tarragon vinegar or lemon juice, a few drops of onion juice, salt and pepper
3. At the end of half an hour sprinkle with finely chopped parsley
4. Dip them in fritter batter, and fry in boiling fat
5. Drain on a brown paper
6. Serve with or without tomato or brown sauce

title: Chicken Broth
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- liquid
- mace
- onion
- salt
- lemon peel
- vermicelli

## Directions

1. After the white parts have been removed for the panada, return the rest of the chicken to the saucepan, with the liquid
2. Add one blade of mace, one slice only of onion, a little salt, and a piece of lemon peel
3. Carefully remove every particle of fat
4. Vermicelli is very well adapted for this broth

title: Chicken Broth
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- one quart of water

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into small pieces and place it in a deep earthen dish
2. Add one quart of water; cover it and set over a kettle of boiling water, letting it steam until the meat of the chicken has become very tender
3. Strain off the broth and let it stand over night
4. In the morning remove the fat and return the liquid to the original earthen dish

title: Chicken Casserole
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- potatoes
- carrots
- green peas
- small white onions
- flour
- soup stock or water

## Directions

1. Bake chicken in covered casserole until nearly tender.
2. Add three potatoes cut in dice.
3. Boil small pieces of carrots, green peas, and small white onions — each to be boiled separately.
4. Just before serving, thicken gravy with a teaspoon of flour mixed with a half cup of soup stock or water.
5. Season to taste and place vegetables around the dish.

title: Chicken Croquettes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half tablespoon flour
- one tablespoon chicken-fat
- one-half cup of soup stock
- one-half teaspoon onion juice
- one-half teaspoon lemon juice
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-quarter teaspoon pepper
- one and one-half cups of veal or chicken

## Directions

1. Cook one-half tablespoon of flour in one tablespoon chicken-fat.
2. Add one-half cup of soup stock gradually, and one-half teaspoon each of onion juice, lemon juice, salt, and one-quarter teaspoon of pepper.
3. Add one and one-half cups of veal or chicken.

title: Chicken Croquettes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon chicken-fat
- one-half cup of soup stock
- one-half teaspoon onion juice
- one-half teaspoon lemon juice
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-quarter teaspoon pepper
- one and one-half cups of veal or chicken
- one pair of brains
- one well-beaten egg
- beaten egg
- powdered bread or cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook one-half tablespoon of flour in one tablespoon chicken-fat
2. Add one-half cup of soup stock gradually, and one-half teaspoon each of onion juice, lemon juice, salt, and one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
3. Add one and one-half cups of veal or chicken, chopped very fine
4. Add one pair of brains which have been boiled
5. Mix these well
6. Remove from the fire and add one well-beaten egg
7. Turn this mixture out on a flat dish and place in ice-box to cool
8. Roll into small cones
9. Dip in beaten egg
10. Roll again in powdered bread or cracker crumbs
11. Drop them into boiling fat
12. Fry until a delicate brown

title: Chicken Curry
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- flour
- hot fat
- one onion
- one tablespoon of curry powder
- three-fourths of a tablespoon of salt
- one tablespoon of wine vinegar
- boiling water
- steamed rice

## Directions

1. Cut chickens in pieces for serving
2. Dredge in flour and sauté in hot fat
3. Cut one onion in thin pieces
4. Add one tablespoon of curry powder, three-fourths of a tablespoon of salt and one tablespoon of wine vinegar
5. Add to chicken and cover with boiling water
6. Simmer until chicken is tender
7. Thicken sauce and serve with steamed rice

title: Chicken Fricassee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- onion
- parsley root
- celery
- hot water
- parsley
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a chicken, cut off the wings, legs and neck.
2. Separate the breast from the chicken, leaving it whole.
3. Cut the back into two pieces.
4. Prepare a mixture of salt, ginger and a little pepper in a saucer and dust each piece of chicken with this mixture.
5. Take all the particles of fat you have removed from it and lay in the bottom of the kettle, also a small onion, cut up, some parsley root and celery.
6. Lay the chicken upon this, breast first, then the leg and so on.
7. Cover up tight and let it stew slowly on the back of the stove or over a low gas flame, adding hot water when necessary.
8. Just before serving chop up some parsley, fine, and rub a teaspoon of flour in a little cold water, and add.
9. Let it boil up once.
10. Shake the kettle back and forth to prevent becoming lumpy.
11. The parsley root and celery may be omitted if so desired.

title: Chicken Liver Paste, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- several fowls' livers
- strong soup stock
- sliced mushroom
- minced onion
- pepper
- salt
- yolks of eggs
- rounds of toast
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly several fowls' livers
2. Let them simmer until tender in a little strong soup stock, adding some sliced mushroom, minced onion, and a little pepper and salt
3. When thoroughly done mince the whole finely, or pound it in a mortar
4. Put it back in the saucepan and mix well with the yolks of sufficient eggs to make the whole fairly moist
5. Warm over the fire, stirring frequently until the mixture is quite thick, taking care that it does not burn
6. Serve upon rounds of toast on a hot dish garnished with parsley

title: Chicken Liver Paste, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound chicken livers
- one-quarter cup of fresh mushrooms
- two tablespoons of chicken fat
- salt
- pepper
- paprika
- a little onion
- lemon juice
- rye bread slices
- red radish or sprigs of parsley

## Directions

1. Take one-quarter pound chicken livers that have been boiled soft
2. Drain and rub through grater
3. Add one-quarter cup of fresh mushrooms that have been fried for three minutes in two tablespoons of chicken fat
4. Chop these mushrooms
5. Mix smooth with the liver, moistening with the fat used in frying the mushrooms
6. Season with salt, pepper, paprika and a little onion and lemon juice
7. Spread on rye bread slices
8. Garnish plate with a red radish or sprigs of parsley

title: Chicken Livers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four chicken livers
- flour
- one minced onion
- one tablespoon of fat
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-eighth teaspoon of paprika
- one-half cup of strong soup stock
- one tablespoon claret or sherry
- toast

## Directions

1. Slice three or four livers from chicken or other fowl and dredge well with flour.
2. Fry one minced onion in one tablespoon of fat until light brown.
3. Put in the liver and shake the pan over the fire to sear all sides.
4. Add one-half teaspoon of salt, one-eighth teaspoon of paprika and one-half cup of strong soup stock.
5. Allow it to boil up once.
6. Add one tablespoon claret or sherry and serve immediately on toast.

title: Chicken Panada
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken till rather more than half done in a quart of water
2. Take of the skin, cut off the white parts when cold
3. Pound it to a paste in a mortar, with a small quantity of the liquor it was boiled in
4. Season with salt, a little nutmeg, and the least piece of lemon peel
5. Boil it gently, and make it with the liquor in which the fowl has been boiled of the required consistency
6. It should be rather thicker than cream

title: Chicken Paprika with Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a three and one-half pound fat chicken
- salt
- one-fourth cup of fat
- one medium-sized onion
- paprika
- boiling water
- one cup of rice
- one teaspoon of salt
- three cups of chicken broth

## Directions

1. Cut a three and one-half pound fat chicken in pieces to serve
2. Salt it and let stand several hours
3. Heat one-fourth cup of fat in an iron kettle
4. Add one medium-sized onion, minced
5. Fry golden brown and set aside
6. Fry the chicken in the fat and when nicely browned, add paprika to taste and boiling water to cover
7. Let simmer one hour
8. Soak one cup of rice in cold water and drain
9. Add the fried onion and one teaspoon of salt
10. Gradually add three cups of chicken broth, more if necessary
11. When nearly done add the chicken and finish cooking in a slow oven, one-half hour

title: Chicken Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- good beef-suet paste
- a small chicken
- thin slices of chorissa
- sliced smoked veal
- good beef gravy
- mushroom essence or powder
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- mace

## Directions

1. Line a basin with a good beef-suet paste
2. Cut up a small chicken
3. Lightly fry the pieces
4. Place them in a stew-pan, with thin slices of chorissa, or, if at hand, slices of smoked veal
5. Add enough good beef gravy to cover them
6. Season with mushroom essence or powder, pepper, salt, and a very small quantity of nutmeg, and mace
7. Simmer gently for a quarter of an hour
8. Fill the pudding
9. Pour over part of the gravy and keep the rest to be poured over the pudding when served in the dish
10. Cover closely with the paste, the ends of which should be wetted with a paste brush to make it adhere closely

title: Chicken Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- one onion
- a bay leaf
- six cloves
- lemon juice
- white celery
- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- one teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of the finest olive oil
- one teaspoon of prepared mustard
- white pepper
- two teaspoons of white sugar
- white of one egg
- one-half cup of vinegar
- lettuce leaves
- hard-boiled eggs
- olives
- capers

## Directions

1. Place the chicken in boiling water, add one onion, a bay leaf and six cloves.
2. Bring to a boil and let it boil rapidly for five minutes.
3. Reduce the heat to below the boiling point, and let it cook until tender.
4. Let chicken cool in the broth.
5. When the chicken is cold, cut into half inch cubes, removing all the fat and skin.
6. To each pint allow one tablespoon of lemon juice, sprinkle the latter over the prepared chicken and place on ice.
7. When ready to serve, mix the chicken with two-thirds as much white celery, cut into corresponding pieces.
8. Rub the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs as fine as possible, add one teaspoon of salt.
9. Add, a drop at a time, one teaspoon of the finest olive oil.
10. Stir constantly, add one teaspoon of prepared mustard and white pepper, and two teaspoons of white sugar.
11. Whip the white of one egg to a froth and add to the dressing.
12. Add about one-half cup of vinegar last, a spoonful at a time.
13. Put the salad into the dressing carefully, using two silver forks.
14. Line the salad bowl with lettuce leaves, and garnish the top with the whites of hard-boiled eggs chopped up, or cut into half-moons.
15. Garnish this salad with the chopped yolks and whites of hard-boiled eggs, being careful to have the whites and yolks separate.
16. A few olives and capers will add to the decoration.

title: Chicken Salad For Twenty People
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two large chickens
- salt
- white, crisp celery
- fine salt
- yolks of six hard-boiled eggs
- white pepper
- four tablespoons of chicken-fat
- one tablespoon of best prepared mustard
- two teaspoons mustard seed
- two teaspoons celery seed
- two tablespoons of white sugar
- one cup of white wine vinegar
- lettuce leaves
- whites of six hard-boiled eggs
- one pint of mayonnaise
- olives

## Directions

1. Boil two large chickens in enough water to cover them, add salt while boiling.
2. When very tender remove from the fire and allow the chickens to cool in the liquor in which they were boiled.
3. When cold skim off every particle of fat, and reserve it to use instead of oil.
4. Cut the chickens up into small bits (do not chop), cut white, crisp celery in half inch pieces, and sprinkle with fine salt, allowing half as much celery as you have chicken.
5. Mix the chicken and celery, using two silver forks to do this.
6. Rub the yolks of six hard-boiled eggs as fine as possible, add one-half teaspoon of salt, white pepper.
7. Add four tablespoons of chicken-fat that has been skimmed off the broth, adding one at a time, stirring constantly.
8. Add one tablespoon of best prepared mustard, two teaspoons mustard seed and celery seed, and two tablespoons of white sugar.
9. Add gradually, stirring constantly, one cup of white wine vinegar.
10. Pour this dressing over the chicken and celery and toss lightly with the silver forks.
11. Line a large salad bowl with lettuce leaves, pour in the salad and garnish the top with the chopped whites of six hard-boiled eggs.
12. Pour a pint of mayonnaise over the salad just before serving.
13. Serve the salad in individual salad dishes, lining each dish with a lettuce leaf, garnish the salad with an olive stuck up in the center of each portion.

title: Chicken Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast or boiled chicken
- gravy
- hard-boiled eggs
- bread
- mustard (optional)

## Directions

1. Mince some cold roast or boiled chicken in a chopping bowl
2. Mix the gravy with it
3. Add a few hard-boiled eggs, which have been minced to a powder
4. Mix all into a soft paste
5. Cut thin slices of bread
6. Spread the chicken between the slices (if desired you may add a little mustard)
7. Press the pieces gently together

title: Chicken Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast or boiled chicken
- gravy
- hard-boiled eggs
- thin slices of bread
- mustard

## Directions

1. Mince some cold roast or boiled chicken in a chopping bowl.
2. Mix the gravy with it, adding a few hard-boiled eggs, which have been minced to a powder.
3. Mix all into a soft paste.
4. Cut thin slices of bread and spread the chicken between the slices (if desired you may add a little mustard).
5. Press the pieces gently together.

title: Chicken Sandwiches With Mayonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- mayonnaise
- chicken soup
- finger-rolls

## Directions

1. Grind up chicken in meat chopper
2. To each cup of chicken add one tablespoon of mayonnaise and one tablespoon of chicken soup
3. Mix into soft paste
4. Put in finger-rolls

title: Chicken Sandwiches with Mayonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- one tablespoon of mayonnaise
- one tablespoon of chicken soup
- finger-rolls

## Directions

1. Grind up chicken in meat chopper.
2. To each cup of chicken add one tablespoon of mayonnaise, and one tablespoon of chicken soup.
3. Mix into soft paste, and put in finger-rolls.

title: Chicken Soup, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large chicken
- four quarts of water
- parsley root
- an onion
- some asparagus
- salt
- the yolk of an egg
- one tablespoon of cold water

## Directions

1. Take one large chicken, cook with four quarts of water for two or three hours
2. Skim carefully, when it begins to boil add parsley root, an onion, some asparagus, cut into bits
3. Season with salt, strain
4. Beat up the yolk of an egg with one tablespoon of cold water, add to soup just before serving
5. Rice, barley, noodles or dumplings may be added
6. Make use of the chicken either for salad or stew

title: Chicken Soup, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- the carcass of a cold, cooked chicken
- one-half cup of chopped celery
- one onion chopped fine
- cold water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take the carcass of a cold, cooked chicken and break into small pieces
2. Add one-half cup of chopped celery and one onion chopped fine
3. Cover with cold water; simmer slowly for two hours
4. Strain, add salt and pepper to taste

title: Chicken with Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- ground ginger
- water
- rice

## Directions

1. Joint a chicken; season with salt and ground ginger and boil with water enough to cover.
2. Allow one-half pound of rice to one chicken.
3. Boil this after chicken is tender.
4. Serve together on a large platter.

title: Chicken with Spaghetti en Casserole
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- young chicken
- olive oil
- white wine
- bouillon
- salt
- cayenne
- dried mushrooms
- water
- mushrooms
- olives
- chicken-fat
- flour
- spaghetti

## Directions

1. Prepare and truss a young chicken, as if for roasting.
2. Put it in a casserole, and pour over it two tablespoons of olive oil, a cup of white wine, a cup of bouillon, salt and cayenne to taste, one spoon of dried mushrooms soaked in one cup of water and chopped fine, and one-half can of mushrooms.
3. Cover tightly and simmer in the oven for about an hour, turning the chicken occasionally.
4. Add a dozen olives and a tablespoon of chicken-fat, smoothed with one tablespoon of flour, and bring to a boil.
5. Remove the chicken and add about a pint of boiled spaghetti to the sauce.
6. Place the chicken on a platter, surround with the spaghetti, and serve.

title: Chiffonade Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- dandelion
- chicory
- chopped beet
- chopped celery
- tomato
- French dressing
- onion
- garlic

## Directions

1. Mix lettuce, dandelion, chicory, a little chopped beet, chopped celery, and a bit of tomato and cover with French dressing. The dressing is usually flavored both with onion and garlic.

title: Chili con Carne
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
