## Ingredients

- two broilers
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot fat
- six large red peppers
- two small onions
- three cloves of garlic
- one-fourth cup of capers
- water
- fat and flour

## Directions

1. Cut two broilers in pieces for serving
2. Season with salt and pepper, and dredge in flour
3. Brown in hot fat
4. Parboil six large red peppers until soft
5. Rub through a wire sieve
6. Chop two small onions fine, three cloves of garlic and one-fourth cup of capers
7. Combine and add to chicken
8. Cover with water and cook until chicken is tender
9. Thicken the sauce with fat and flour melted together

title: Chilled Bananas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ice-cold bananas
- lemon
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cut ice-cold bananas down lengthwise
2. Lay these halves on a plate with a quarter of a lemon and a generous teaspoon of powdered sugar
3. Eat with a fork or spoon after sprinkling with lemon juice and dipping in sugar

title: Chocolate Brod Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of ten eggs
- two cups of pulverized sugar
- one and three-fourth cups of sifted dry rye bread crumbs
- one-half pound of sweet almonds
- bitter almonds
- one-fourth pound of citron
- one cake of chocolate
- grated peel of one lemon
- juice of one orange and one lemon
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one-half teaspoon of cloves
- one wine glass of brandy
- whites of ten eggs
- chocolate icing
- white of one egg
- scant cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Separate the yolks and whites of ten eggs
2. Beat the yolks with two cups of pulverized sugar
3. When thick add one and three-fourth cups of sifted dry rye bread crumbs
4. Add one-half pound of sweet almonds and some bitter ones, grated or powdered as fine as possible
5. Add one-fourth pound of citron shredded fine
6. Add one cake of chocolate grated
7. Add the grated peel of one lemon
8. Add the juice of one orange and one lemon
9. Add one tablespoon of cinnamon, one teaspoon of allspice, one-half teaspoon of cloves
10. Add one wine glass of brandy
11. Fold in the beaten whites
12. Bake very slowly in ungreased form
13. For icing: Melt a small piece of chocolate
14. Beat the white of an egg stiff with scant cup of sugar
15. Stir into the melted chocolate and spread with a knife

title: Chocolate Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- one-half cup of sifted sugar
- one-quarter pound of grated sweet almonds
- one-quarter pound of finely-grated vanilla chocolate
- one-quarter pound of raisins
- one-half cup of matzoth meal sifted fine
- juice of an orange
- one-quarter cup of wine
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of four eggs with one-half cup of sifted sugar
2. Add one-quarter pound of grated sweet almonds, one-quarter pound of finely-grated vanilla chocolate, and one-quarter pound of raisins
3. Add one-half cup of matzoth meal sifted fine, juice of an orange, one-quarter cup of wine, and lastly the stiffly-beaten whites
4. Bake one hour in a slow oven, in a form lined with greased paper

title: Chocolate Coffee Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bunt kuchen dough
- boiled chocolate icing

## Directions

1. Pour a bunt kuchen dough into long, well-buttered tins, and when baked remove from the oven and cover thickly with boiled chocolate icing

title: Chocolate Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- three-fourths cup of powdered sugar
- one cup of ground sweet chocolate
- one cup of walnuts
- three tablespoons of flour

## Directions

1. Beat whites of three eggs to a snow
2. Add three-fourths cup of powdered sugar, one cup of ground sweet chocolate, one cup of walnuts chopped, three tablespoons of flour
3. Drop by teaspoonful on greased baking-tin
4. Bake in slow oven

title: Chocolate Cornstarch Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- one and one-half cups of sugar
- seven heaping tablespoons of cocoa
- six level tablespoons of cornstarch
- one tablespoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Place milk and sugar up to boil.
2. When boiling, add cocoa, dissolved to a smooth paste.
3. Add cornstarch dissolved in cold water.
4. Let come to a boil, remove from fire and add vanilla.
5. Place in mold and allow to get cold.
6. Serve with whipped cream.

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- a small piece of whole cinnamon
- rind of a fresh lemon
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of good chocolate
- 1/4 of an ounce of isinglass
- water
- yolks of 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Half pint of almond milk, a small piece of whole cinnamon, the rind of a fresh lemon, 1/2 lb. of sugar. Boil this.
2. Cut 1/2 lb. of good chocolate in a basin, pour over some of the boiled milk.
3. When the chocolate is soft, mix it well with the boiled milk, rub it through a sieve.
4. Dissolve 1/4 of an ounce of isinglass in a little water, strain it and add to it the milk and chocolate.
5. Let this get cold, beat up the yolks of 8 eggs to a froth, pour it through the sieve into the milk and chocolate, mixing it all the time.
6. When well mixed, pour it into the mould; put it in a flat saucepan on the fire like the vanille cream; attend to it just the same.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Chocolate Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- three tablespoons of sugar
- one heaping tablespoon of grated unsweetened chocolate
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one tablespoon of hot water
- one pint of milk heated to boiling
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of three eggs, three tablespoons of sugar till light.
2. Dissolve one heaping tablespoon of grated unsweetened chocolate, one tablespoon of sugar and one of hot water.
3. When dissolved, add slowly one pint of milk heated to boiling.
4. Pour this hot mixture over the beaten eggs and sugar.
5. Cook in double boiler, stirring constantly till it thickens.
6. When cool, flavor with vanilla, and place on ice.
7. When ready to serve, half-fill small punch glasses with the custard, heap over them sweetened whipped cream, flavored; putting on top of each glass, and serve cold.

title: Chocolate Eclairs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Batter prepared as in cream puffs recipe
- One cup of brown sugar
- One-half cup of molasses
- One tablespoon of butter
- Two tablespoons of flour
- One-fourth pound of grated chocolate
- One-fourth cup of sweet milk
- Vanilla to flavor

## Directions

1. Spread the batter in long ovals
2. When done cover with frosting
3. For frosting: Boil one cup of brown sugar with one-half cup of molasses, one tablespoon of butter and two tablespoons of flour
4. Boil for one-half hour
5. Stir in one-fourth pound of grated chocolate wet in one-fourth cup of sweet milk
6. Boil until it hardens on the spoon
7. Flavor with vanilla
8. Spread this upon the eclairs

title: Chocolate Glazing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two sticks of bitter chocolate
- five tablespoons of powdered sugar
- three tablespoons of boiling water

## Directions

1. Grate two sticks of bitter chocolate, add five tablespoons of powdered sugar and three tablespoons of boiling water
2. Put on the stove, over moderate fire, stir while boiling until smooth, glossy and thick
3. Spread at once on cake and set aside to harden

title: Chocolate Ice Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cream
- 4 oz. of the best chocolate
- 4 oz. of sifted sugar
- a cup of boiling water

## Directions

1. Dissolve in a cup of boiling water 4 oz. of sifted sugar
2. Mix it all with 1 pint of cream and 4 oz. of the best chocolate
3. Stir it over a slow fire
4. When cold, put it in the freezing pot and work it according to directions
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Chocolate Ice Cream, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one pint of cream
- one cup of sweet milk
- one-half pound of sweet chocolate
- one cup of sweet milk
- vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Make same as Vanilla Ice Cream, No. 2
2. Dissolve on stove one-half pound of sweet chocolate in one cup of sweet milk
3. Rub smooth and thick
4. Let get cold
5. Add to the eggs, just before putting in cream
6. Flavor with vanilla

title: Chocolate Ice Cream, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of cream
- one pint of new milk
- two eggs
- one teacup of grated chocolate
- two cups of pulverized sugar
- one teaspoon of cornstarch
- one teaspoon of extract of vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and sugar and let them come to a boil
2. Take them quickly from the fire
3. Dissolve the chocolate in a little milk over the fire, stirring all the time
4. When smooth mix with the milk and eggs
5. Add the cream and vanilla
6. Freeze when cold

title: Chocolate Icing, Unboiled
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- one and one-half cups of pulverized sugar
- four tablespoons of grated chocolate
- two teaspoons of vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of three eggs and one and one-half cups of pulverized sugar, added gradually while beating
2. Beat until very thick, then add four tablespoons of grated chocolate and two teaspoons of vanilla

title: Chocolate Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One scant half cup of butter
- One cup of sugar
- One-half cup of sweet milk
- Yolks of two eggs
- Whites of two eggs
- One-half cup of sifted flour
- One-half cup of milk
- One cup of grated chocolate
- One-half cup of granulated sugar
- Yolk of one egg
- One and one-half cups of sifted flour
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- One teaspoon of vanilla flavoring
- Plain white icing

## Directions

1. Stir one scant half cup of butter to a cream with one cup of sugar
2. Add alternately one-half cup of sweet milk and yolks of two eggs which you have previously beaten until quite light
3. Add whites of two
4. Add one-half cup of sifted flour
5. Make a custard of one-half cup of milk with one cup of grated chocolate and one-half cup of granulated sugar
6. Boil until thick
7. Add the yolk of one egg
8. Remove from the fire and stir until cool
9. Add this to the cake batter
10. Add one and one-half cups of sifted flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder and one teaspoon of vanilla flavoring
11. Bake in layers
12. Ice between and on top with plain white icing flavored to taste
13. You may substitute almond or colored icing

title: Chocolate Nectar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons chocolate syrup
- cream or milk
- chopped ice
- soda water, apollinaris, or milk
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Put into a glass two tablespoons of chocolate syrup, a little cream or milk and chopped ice
2. Fill up the glass with soda water, apollinaris, or milk
3. Drop a little whipped cream on top

title: Chocolate Sauce No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound chocolate
- one cup of water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve one-half pound chocolate in one cup of water
2. Add sugar to taste
3. Boil somewhat thick and flavor with vanilla

title: Chocolate Sauce No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- two tablespoons of cornstarch
- one-half cup of cold milk
- three squares of chocolate
- three tablespoons of sugar
- three tablespoons of hot water

## Directions

1. Scald two cups of milk
2. Add two tablespoons of cornstarch diluted with one-half cup of cold milk
3. Cook ten minutes over boiling water
4. Melt three squares of chocolate over hot water
5. Add three tablespoons of sugar and three tablespoons of hot water and stir until smooth
6. Add to cooked mixture

title: Chocolate Sauce, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound chocolate
- one cup of water
- sugar to taste
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve one-half pound chocolate in one cup of water and sugar to taste
2. boil somewhat thick
3. flavor with vanilla

title: Chocolate Sauce, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- two tablespoons of cornstarch
- one-half cup of cold milk
- three squares of chocolate
- three tablespoons of sugar
- three tablespoons of hot water
- whites of three eggs
- three-fourths of a cup of powdered sugar
- yolks of three eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Scald two cups of milk
2. add two tablespoons of cornstarch diluted with one-half cup of cold milk
3. cook ten minutes over boiling water
4. Melt three squares of chocolate over hot water
5. add three tablespoons of sugar and three tablespoons of hot water
6. stir until smooth
7. add to cooked mixture
8. Beat the whites of three eggs until stiff
9. add three-fourths of a cup of powdered sugar
10. add the yolks and stir into cooked mixture
11. cool and add vanilla

title: Chocolate Syrup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1 cup cocoa
- salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve two cups of sugar in one cup of water and boil five minutes
2. Mix one cup of cocoa with one cup of water and add to the boiling syrup
3. Boil slowly for ten minutes
4. Add salt
5. Cool and bottle for further use

title: Chocolate Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nine eggs
- one-half pound of pulverized sugar
- one-half pound of almonds
- one-half pound of finest vanilla chocolate
- one-half pound of raisins
- seven soda crackers
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- juice of three lemons
- one-fourth glass of wine

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth
2. Beat yolks with sugar until very light
3. Add chocolate
4. Add almonds (half cut and grated)
5. Add raisins (cut and seeded)
6. Add seven soda crackers rolled to a powder
7. Add one teaspoon of baking-powder
8. Add juice of three lemons and one-fourth glass of wine
9. Stir in the beaten whites last
10. Bake as with other torten

title: Chocolate Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nine eggs
- one-half pound of pulverized sugar
- one-half pound of almonds, half cut and grated
- one-half pound of finest vanilla chocolate grated
- one-half pound of raisins, cut and seeded
- seven soda crackers, rolled to a powder
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- juice of three lemons
- one-fourth glass of wine

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth and stir in last
2. Beat yolks with sugar until very light
3. Add chocolate
4. Proceed as with other torten

title: Chopped Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- one onion
- one sour apple
- a slice of white bread
- vinegar
- one teaspoon oil
- a little cinnamon
- pepper
- hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Soak herring a few hours
2. Wash and clean, bone and chop
3. To one herring take one onion, one sour apple, a slice of white bread which has been soaked in vinegar
4. Chop all these
5. Add one teaspoon oil, a little cinnamon and pepper
6. Put on platter in shape of a herring with head at top and tail at bottom of dish
7. Sprinkle the chopped white of a hard-boiled egg over fish
8. Then sprinkle the chopped yolk

title: Chopped Meat With Raisins (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of chopped meat
- grated onion
- an egg
- matzoth flour
- white pepper
- one-half cup of water
- fat
- sugar
- a quarter cup of large black raisins
- a few slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Take a pound of chopped meat and add grated onion, an egg, matzoth flour, white pepper, mix and form into small balls.
2. Put in pot with one-half cup of water, fat, sugar, a quarter cup of large black raisins and a few slices of lemon and let stew one-half hour.
3. Thicken gravy with tablespoon of flour browned in a tablespoon of fat and serve.

title: Chopped Onion and Chicken Fat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one yellow onion
- four tablespoons of chicken fat, melted
- salt
- slices of rye bread

## Directions

1. Chop one yellow onion very fine
2. Add four tablespoons of chicken fat, melted
3. Add salt to taste
4. Serve on slices of rye bread

title: Chorisa Omelette
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- half a tea-cup full of finely minced chorisa

## Directions

1. Add to the eggs, after they are well beaten as directed in the last receipt, half a tea-cup full of finely minced chorisa
2. this omelette must be lightly fried on both sides, or the salamander held over long enough to dress the chorisa

title: Chow-Chow
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pickles
- cauliflower
- beans
- little onions
- green and red peppers
- salt
- one gallon or more of best pickling vinegar
- one pound of ground mustard
- cold water
- cloves
- whole peppers
- mustard seed
- curry powder
- cloth

## Directions

1. Take pickles, cauliflower, beans, little onions and a few green and red peppers
2. Cut all up fine, except the onions
3. Salt well overnight
4. Drain off next morning and put in a large jar
5. Mix one gallon or more of best pickling vinegar with a pound of ground mustard
6. Wet the mustard with cold water before using
7. Put in a bag the following spices: Cloves, whole peppers and mustard seed
8. Boil the vinegar and spices and then throw over pickles boiling
9. Add a tablespoon of curry powder
10. When cold tie up, having previously put a cloth with mustard seed over all

title: Chrimsel, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of matzoth meal
- one cup of boiling soup stock or wine
- one tablespoon of chopped almonds
- one teaspoon of sugar
- salt
- yolks of four eggs
- whites of four eggs
- butter or goose grease
- powdered sugar
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of matzoth meal in a bowl
2. Stir into it one cup of boiling soup stock or wine
3. When mixed add one tablespoon of chopped almonds, one teaspoon of sugar, a pinch of salt and the yolks of four eggs well beaten
4. Add the stiffly-beaten whites of the four eggs
5. Fry by tablespoonfuls in boiling hot butter or goose grease
6. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve with wine sauce

title: Chrimsel, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about three matzoth
- handful of raisins
- almonds
- salt
- peel of a lemon
- yolks of four eggs
- one cup of sugar
- cinnamon
- oil
- whites of four eggs
- stewed prunes

## Directions

1. Soak about three matzoth
2. Seed a handful of raisins and pound as many almonds as you have raisins
3. Press every drop of water out of the matzoth, put them in a bowl and stir them to a cream
4. Add a pinch of salt, the peel of a lemon, yolks of four eggs and a cup of sugar, the raisins and almonds, and also a little cinnamon
5. Heat some oil in a spider; the more fat the lighter the chrimsel will be
6. Last add the stiffly-beaten whites to the dough
7. Fry a light brown on both sides; use about a tablespoonful of batter for each chrimsel
8. Serve with stewed prunes
9. Lay the chrimsel on a large platter and pour the prunes over all
10. Eat hot

title: Cinnamon Buns
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of flour
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- two tablespoons of butter
- milk
- soft butter
- granulated sugar
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, sugar, salt, and baking-powder
2. Rub in butter
3. Mix with milk to soft dough
4. Roll out one-half inch thick
5. Spread with soft butter, granulated sugar, and powdered cinnamon
6. Roll up like jelly roll
7. Cut in inch slices
8. Lay close together in greased pan
9. Bake in quick oven

title: Cinnamon Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of flour
- half a pound of sifted sugar
- a tea-spoonful of pounded cinnamon
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Rub half a pound of fresh butter into a pound of flour
2. Work it well together, then add half a pound of sifted sugar and a tea-spoonful of pounded cinnamon
3. Make it into a paste with three eggs
4. Roll it and cut into small cakes with tin cutters

title: Cinnamon Rolls or Schnecken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half the kuchen dough
- melted butter
- scraped maple, brown or granulated sugar
- cinnamon
- brown or maple sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Take half the kuchen dough. Roll one-half inch thick and spread well with melted butter
2. Sprinkle generously with scraped maple, brown or granulated sugar and cinnamon, then roll
3. Cut the roll into equal parts about one inch thick, place close together endwise in a spider, generously buttered, spread with one-fourth inch layer of brown or maple sugar
4. Let rise until light, and bake ten to twenty minutes in a hot oven, a golden brown
5. Invert the spider, remove rolls and serve caramel side up

title: Cinnamon Sticks
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of almonds
- whites of four eggs
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- boiled frosting

## Directions

1. Grate one-half pound of almonds
2. Beat the whites of four eggs to a stiff froth
3. Add gradually one pound of pulverized sugar and a tablespoon of cinnamon
4. Roll out this dough into half finger lengths and about as thick as your little finger
5. Bake
6. When done ice each one with boiled frosting

title: Cinnamon Toast for Tea
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- powdered cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut bread thin and brown, but not dried
2. Butter the toast while very hot, thinly and evenly
3. sprinkle over each piece some powdered cinnamon and sugar

title: Citron Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of butter
- one cup and a half of sugar
- two eggs
- three-quarters of a cup of milk
- one-half cup of citron
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one teaspoon of cloves
- one heaping teaspoon of baking-powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Take one-half cup of butter and one cup and a half of sugar, and rub to a cream
2. Add two eggs, three-quarters of a cup of milk, one-half cup of citron cut up very fine, one teaspoon of allspice and one of cloves
3. Sift one heaping teaspoon of baking-powder into enough flour to thicken
4. Make stiffer than ordinary cup cake dough
5. Flavor to suit taste, and drop on large tins with a teaspoon
6. Grease the pans, and bake in a quick oven
7. Try one on a plate; if the dough runs too much add more flour

title: Citron Preserve
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- citron
- a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit
- four lemons and a quarter of a pound of ginger root for six pounds of fruit
- water

## Directions

1. Pare and core the citron
2. Cut it into strips and notch the edges; or cut it into fancy shapes
3. Allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, and to six pounds of the fruit allow four lemons and a quarter of a pound of ginger root
4. Tie the ginger in a cloth, and boil it in a quart and a half of water until the flavor is extracted
5. Remove it, and add to the water the sugar and the juice of the lemons
6. Stir until the sugar is dissolved and the syrup is clear
7. Take off any scum
8. Add the citron and cook until it is clear, but not soft enough to fall apart
9. Can and seal while hot

title: Citron Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of citron
- two ounces of candied orange and lemon peel
- four ounces of loaf sugar
- four ounces of fresh butter
- fine puff paste
- yolks of four eggs
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Cut in slices two ounces of citron and the same quantity of candied orange and lemon peel
2. Add to them four ounces of loaf sugar and four ounces of fresh butter
3. Line a shallow dish with fine puff paste
4. Beat up to a froth the yolks of four eggs and the whites of two
5. Fill the dish with these ingredients
6. Bake half an hour

title: Claret Cup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strained juice of one and one-half lemons
- two tablespoons of powdered sugar
- one tablespoon of red curagao
- three cups of claret
- one cup of apollinaris water
- a few slices of orange or pineapple, or both
- a few maraschino cherries
- two cucumbers
- a good-sized lump of ice
- a bunch of mint

## Directions

1. Squeeze into a glass pitcher the strained juice of one and one-half lemons
2. add two tablespoons of powdered sugar
3. add one tablespoon of red curagao
4. pour in three cups of claret, and one cup of apollinaris water
5. Mix thoroughly
6. add a few slices of orange or pineapple, or both, and a few maraschino cherries
7. Cut the rinds from two cucumbers without breaking them
8. hang them on the inside of the pitcher from the top
9. drop in a good-sized lump of ice
10. serve at once in thin glasses
11. Place a bunch of mint at the top of the pitcher

title: Claret Cup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strained juice of one and one-half lemons
- two tablespoons of powdered sugar
- one tablespoon of red curagao
- three cups of claret
- one cup of apollinaris water
- a few slices of orange or pineapple, or both
- a few maraschino cherries
- rinds from two cucumbers
- a good-sized lump of ice

## Directions

1. Squeeze into a glass pitcher the strained juice of one and one-half lemons, add two tablespoons of powdered sugar, one tablespoon of red curagao.
2. Pour in three cups of claret, and one cup of apollinaris water.
3. Mix thoroughly, add a few slices of orange or pineapple, or both, and a few maraschino cherries.
4. Cut the rinds from two cucumbers without breaking them, hang them on the inside of the pitcher from the top.
5. Drop in a good-sized lump of ice and serve at once.

title: Cocoa Nut Doce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cocoa nut
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the cocoa nut and sugar as above, without egg
2. Serve in small glasses, or baked

title: Cocoa Nut Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about half a pound of finely grated cocoa nut
- half a pound of fresh butter
- half a pound of white sugar
- six whites of eggs
- rich puff paste

## Directions

1. Take about half a pound of finely grated cocoa nut
2. Beat up to a cream half a pound of fresh butter and add it to the cocoa nut
3. Add half a pound of white sugar and six whites of eggs beaten to a froth
4. Mix the whole well together
5. Bake in a dish lined with a rich puff paste

title: Cocoa Nut Tart
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a cocoa-nut
- 1 lb. very fine grated cocoa-nut
- a 1/2 lb. of castor sugar
- a 1/2 lb. of the clarified veal fat
- a tablespoonful of brandy
- 4 drops essence of vanilla
- the whites of 6 eggs
- good puff paste
- pounded sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Break a cocoa-nut, cut the brown skin off, cut the nut in pieces and put them in cold water
2. When ready to make the tart take it out of the water, dry it well
3. Grate 1 lb. very fine into a plate
4. Put in a 1/2 lb. of castor sugar, 1/2 lb. of the clarified veal fat well beaten
5. Stir the sugar and fat together till it comes like a cream
6. Add a tablespoonful of brandy, 4 drops essence of vanilla
7. Beat up the whites of 6 eggs well
8. Add gradually the fat and sugar, keep stirring it
9. Then add gradually the grated cocoa-nut
10. Line a pie dish with good puff paste
11. Put in the mixture, crossbar the top
12. Put it in a moderate oven for 1/2 an hour
13. Sift over some pounded sugar and cinnamon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cocoanut Kisses
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white of one egg
- one-half cup of sugar
- vanilla
- one cup of shredded cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the white of one egg
2. Add one-half cup of sugar with a flavoring of vanilla
3. Fold in one cup of shredded cocoanut
4. Drop by teaspoonfuls on a well-greased baking-pan, inverted
5. Bake for about ten or twelve minutes in a slow oven
6. Remove from pan when cookies are cold

title: Cocoanut Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half cup of butter
- One and one-half cups of pulverized sugar
- Three eggs
- One-half cup of milk
- Two cups of flour
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- One grated cocoanut and all of its milk
- Beaten whites of two eggs
- One cup of powdered sugar
- Five tablespoons of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Rub to a cream one-half cup of butter and one and one-half cups of pulverized sugar
2. Add gradually three eggs, one-half cup of milk and two cups of flour
3. Add two teaspoons of baking-powder in last sifting
4. Bake in layers
5. For filling: Take one grated cocoanut and all of its milk
6. To half of it add the beaten whites of two eggs and one cup of powdered sugar
7. Lay this between the layers
8. Mix with the other half of the grated cocoanut five tablespoons of powdered sugar
9. Strew thickly on top of cake, which has been previously iced

title: Cocoanut Lemon Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of six eggs
- one cup of sugar
- juice of three lemons
- rind of two lemons
- one-half of a cocoanut grated
- whites of six eggs
- rich pastry
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of six eggs and one cup of sugar until very light
2. squeeze in the juice of three lemons and the rind of two of them, stir well
3. add one-half of a cocoanut grated
4. lastly add the whites of six eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
5. Line a deep pie-plate with rich pastry, sprinkle a little flour over it
6. pour in the lemon mixture and bake

title: Cocoanut Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- butter size of an egg
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one tablespoon of flour
- three-fourths cup of grated cocoanut
- one tablespoon of milk
- vanilla
- pinch of salt
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with puff paste
2. fill with the following custard: Butter size of an egg, creamed with one cup of granulated sugar, one tablespoon of flour, three-fourths cup of grated cocoanut, one tablespoon of milk, vanilla, pinch of salt, and the beaten whites of three eggs

title: Cocoanut Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one grated cocoanut
- six eggs
- grated rind and juice of two lemons
- one cup of granulated sugar
- milk of the cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar and the grated rind of lemon until light and creamy
2. Add gradually the cocoanut and the beaten whites of the eggs, and lastly put in the milk of the cocoanut, to which has been added the juice of the lemons
3. Bake in a moderate oven for half an hour and serve quite cold

title: Cod Fish Balls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- lukewarm water
- boiling water
- mashed potatoes
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt
- pepper
- one egg
- milk
- flour
- hot oil

## Directions

1. Put the fish to soak over night in lukewarm water.
2. Change again in the morning and wash off all the salt.
3. Cut into pieces and boil about fifteen minutes, pour off this water and put on to boil again with boiling water.
4. Boil twenty minutes this time, drain off every bit of water, put on a platter to cool and pick to pieces as fine as possible, removing every bit of skin and bone.
5. When this is done, add an equal quantity of mashed potatoes, a tablespoon of butter, a very little salt and pepper.
6. Beat up one egg and a little milk, if necessary, mix with a fork.
7. Flour your hands well and form into biscuit-shaped balls.
8. Fry in hot oil.

title: Cod Stewed with Turnips
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 white, hard turnips
- onion
- 2 spoonfuls of oil
- 1/2 pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- cod
- 1 spoonful of flour
- 2 spoonfuls of vinegar or the juice of one lemon
- a little dried saffron
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel and cut turnips in nice size pieces, say one turnip in 4 pieces
2. Slice an onion in the stewpan with 2 spoonfuls of oil, let it fry
3. Put in a 1/2 pint of water, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
4. Put the turnips in, let them stew tender
5. Then lay the slices of cod, let them stew till done
6. Take out the cod, put it in the dish intended to send it to table
7. Mix in a basin a spoonful of flour, 2 of vinegar or the juice of one lemon, with a little dried saffron, some chopped parsley
8. Throw it in with the turnips, mix it well
9. Dish the turnips round the cod, and put all the sauce over fish and turnips with a spoon
10. Garnish with sprigs of parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Coffee Cake (Kuchen) Dough
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half ounce of yeast
- one-half cup of lukewarm milk
- one cup of sifted flour
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- one cup of sugar
- three-fourths cup of butter
- three eggs
- five cups of sifted flour
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Soak one-half ounce of yeast in one-half cup of lukewarm milk; when dissolved put in a bowl, or round agate pan, and stir in one cup of sifted flour, one teaspoon of sugar and one-fourth teaspoon of salt, mix thoroughly, and put in a warm place (not hot) to rise, from one to two hours
2. When well risen, cream well together one cup of sugar and three-fourths cup of butter, then add three eggs, five cups of sifted flour, one cup of milk and one teaspoon of salt, mix together until light, then stir in the risen yeast, and with a spoon work well for ten minutes, and set aside to rise again, five or six hours or all night

title: Coffee Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of warmed-over or freshly made coffee
- two tablespoons of powdered sugar
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla
- one cup of milk or cream
- one tablespoon of cornstarch
- cold water

## Directions

1. Put three cups of warmed-over or freshly made coffee in a small casserole, add two tablespoons of powdered sugar, one-half teaspoon of vanilla
2. When at boiling point (do not let it boil), add one cup of milk or cream
3. Add one tablespoon of cornstarch which has been moistened with cold water
4. Stir in while cooking till it is smooth and glossy
5. When the cake is cool, pour mixture over the layers

title: Coffee for Twenty People
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound coffee finely ground
- 1 egg
- cold water
- 7 quarts boiling water
- cream

## Directions

1. Add and mix one pound of coffee finely ground, with one egg and enough cold water to thoroughly moisten it
2. Cover and let stand several hours
3. Place in thin bag and drop in seven quarts of boiling water
4. Boil five minutes
5. Let stand ten minutes
6. Add cream to coffee and serve

title: Coffee Ice Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oz. of fresh roasted Mocha coffee
- 1 pint of cream
- 4 oz. of sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Grind 4 oz. of fresh roasted Mocha coffee
2. Put it into a pint of cream with 4 oz. of sifted sugar
3. Let it simmer over the fire
4. Let it stand for 1/2 an hour
5. When cool put it in the freezer
6. Work it the same
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Coffee Ice Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one pint of cream
- one cup of sweet milk
- one and one-half tablespoons of mocha extract
- one cup of grated walnuts

## Directions

1. Make same as Vanilla Ice Cream No. 2
2. Flavor with one and one-half tablespoons of mocha extract
3. Add one cup of grated walnuts
4. Freeze

title: Cold Cream
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of oil of almonds
- one ounce of white wax
- four ounces of rose water

## Directions

1. Warm gently together four ounces of oil of almonds, and one ounce of white wax
2. Gradually add four ounces of rose water

title: Cold Egg Wine
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- wine
- one egg per glass
- sugar to taste
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. To each glass of wine allow one egg, beat up, and add sugar to taste.
2. Add wine gradually and grated nutmeg.
3. Beat whites separately and mix.

title: Cold Meat Cookery for the Passover
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- onion
- parsley
- a tooth of garlic
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 2 eggs
- potato crust

## Directions

1. If you have any cold meat left, either boiled, roasted or stewed, chop it very fine
2. Mix with onion, parsley, a tooth of garlic, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
3. Mix it with 2 eggs
4. Make the potato crust as for pigeon pie
5. Put a layer of potatoes at the bottom
6. Put in the minced meat
7. Cover over and bake a nice colour
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cold Meat Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- egg
- fat or oil

## Directions

1. Prepare some cold meat as above, if too thick add another egg
2. Have your frying pan ready with boiling fat or oil
3. Put a large spoonful at the time as many as the pan will hold, only mind that they do not touch each other
4. Flatten them as you put them in, they ought to be flattened to the thickness of an inch
5. Fry them a light brown
6. Have a dish nice and hot at the side with a sheet of white paper
7. Lay them on with a slice of lemon upon each, and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cold Slaw
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one firm, white head of cabbage
- one onion
- one sour apple
- salt
- white pepper
- white sugar
- one tablespoon goose oil or butter
- white wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a firm, white head of cabbage; cut it in halves; take out the heart and cut as fine as possible on slaw-cutter.
2. Cut up one onion at the same time and a sour apple.
3. Sprinkle with salt and white pepper and a liberal quantity of white sugar.
4. Mix this lightly with two forks.
5. Heat one tablespoon of goose oil or butter, and mix it thoroughly in with the cabbage.
6. Heat some white wine vinegar in a spider; let it come to a boil and pour over the slaw, boiling.
7. Keep covered for a short time.
8. Serve cold.

title: Cold Slaw or Cabbage Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small, compact cabbage
- hot dressing

## Directions

1. Select a small, compact cabbage and strip off the outside leaves
2. Cut the head in quarters
3. With a sharp knife slice very thin
4. Soak in cold water until crisp
5. Drain and dry between clean towels
6. Mix with hot dressing and serve when cold

title: Cold Sour Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of sour grass (sorrel)
- three potatoes
- a bunch of young onions
- salt
- two tablespoons of sugar
- a bit of sour salt
- two well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Take a pound of sour grass (sorrel), remove leaves, wash well, cut and squeeze well.
2. Peel three potatoes, mince a bunch of young onions, salt and set on to boil, when boiling add the sour grass and let boil well, add two tablespoons of sugar, and a bit of sour salt, let simmer a bit, afterward add two well-beaten eggs.
3. Do not boil this soup after adding the eggs.
4. This soup is to be eaten cold.
5. It can be kept for some time in jars.

title: Cold Tongue Omelet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- eggs
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- oil or fat
- white paper

## Directions

1. Cut the slices very thin
2. Beat up 3 or 4 eggs with some chopped parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
3. Put in the frying pan a little oil or fat to grease it
4. You must not have too much in or your omelet will not turn out well
5. Mix the slices of tongue with the beaten eggs
6. Turn it into the pan, fry it nicely
7. Turn it on a dish with a sheet of white paper under
8. Garnish with parsley
9. Grated cold tongue is very nice in fowl salads
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Collared Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of veal, the breast is the best part
- chopped herbs
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- slices of smoked tongue
- beetroot
- chopped parsley
- hard yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the bones, gristle, &c., from a nice piece of veal, the breast is the best part for the purpose
2. Season the meat well with chopped herbs, mace, pepper, and salt
3. Lay between the veal slices of smoked tongue variegated with beetroot, chopped parsley, and hard yolks of eggs
4. Roll it up tightly in a cloth
5. Simmer for some hours till tender
6. When done, it should have a weight laid on it to press out the liquor

title: Collared Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5-lb. of breast of veal
- a pound of chopped meat, well seasoned
- slices of boiled beetroot
- slice of smoked beef
- 3 or 4 gherkins
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- a small quantity of spinach
- a boiled carrot
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Order 4 or 5-lb. of breast of veal to be boned for collaring
2. Lay it flat upon your paste board
3. Beat it down with the rolling pin
4. Have a pound of chopped meat well seasoned
5. Lay it on the veal
6. Put some slices of boiled beetroot and slice of smoked beef
7. Add 3 or 4 gherkins and 2 hard boiled eggs cut in slices
8. Boil a small quantity of spinach and drain it from the water
9. Put that in and add a boiled carrot cut in halves
10. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper
11. Roll it up and sew it
12. Put it in a cloth and tie the ends tight with string
13. Put in a long stock pot
14. Let it boil for 3 hours
15. Take it out and untie the cloth
16. Lay a coarse clean cloth on it and press it down to the thickness of 4 inches
17. Let it remain till cold
18. Take it out, cut off the rough ends, and glaze the top
19. When served at table cut in very thin slices, garnished with meat jelly and parsley and strips of beetroot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: College Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumbs
- currants
- shred suet
- brown sugar
- eggs
- brandy
- grated nutmeg
- lemon peel
- sweet sauce

## Directions

1. Make in a similar way to Cumberland pudding, with the omission of the apples
2. Make them in balls, and fry or bake in cups
3. A sweet sauce is served with them

title: Colored Mayonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mayonnaise
- parsley leaves
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop parsley leaves very fine; pound them in a small quantity of lemon juice; strain and add the juice to the dressing.

title: Common Veal Stuffing
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of finely shred suet
- grated crumbs of bread
- chopped sweet herbs
- grated lemon peel
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Have equal quantities of finely shred suet and grated crumbs of bread
2. Add chopped sweet herbs, grated lemon peel, pepper, and salt
3. Pound it in a mortar

title: Compote of Carrots (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup sugar
- one cup water
- two cups carrots
- two tablespoons hot fat or butter

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of one cup of sugar and one cup of water by boiling ten minutes.
2. To this syrup add two cups of carrots diced, which have previously been browned in two tablespoons hot fat or butter.
3. Cook all together until carrots are tender.
4. Brown in oven and serve.

title: Compote of Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- sugar
- water
- stick cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Pare the fruit, leaving on the stems
2. Stew in sugar and a very little water
3. Flavor with stick cinnamon and a few cloves (take out the head of each clove)
4. When soft place each pear carefully on a platter until cold
5. Arrange them nicely in a glass bowl or flat glass dish, the stems all on the outer rim
6. Pour over them the sauce, which should be boiled thick like syrup
7. Eat cold

title: Compote of Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- sugar
- juice of one or two tart oranges
- brandy

## Directions

1. Cut off the rind of a pineapple
2. Core and trim out all the eyes
3. Cut into desired slices
4. Set on to boil with half a pound of sugar and the juice of one or two tart oranges
5. When the pineapple is tender and clear, put into a compote dish
6. Boil the syrup until clear
7. Pour over all and cool
8. The addition of a wineglass of brandy improves this compote very much

title: Compote of Raspberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water
- berries

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of half a pound of sugar and half a cup of water
2. Put into it one quart of berries which have been carefully picked and washed
3. Boil up once
4. Serve cold

title: Consomme
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of beef
- three quarts of cold water
- one-half cup carrots
- one-half cup celery
- one-half cup onion
- one-half teaspoon of peppercorns
- one tablespoon of salt
- one egg shell broken fine
- slightly beaten white of one egg

## Directions

1. Cut beef in dice and cover with three quarts of cold water
2. Simmer slowly for four hours
3. The last hour add one-half cup each of carrots, celery, onion, and season with one-half teaspoon of peppercorns and one tablespoon of salt
4. Strain, cool, remove fat and clear by allowing one egg shell broken fine and the slightly beaten white of one egg to each quart of stock
5. Add to the stock, stir constantly until it has reached the boiling point
6. Boil two minutes and serve

title: Cook Sole with Vermicelli
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion
- 1 teacup water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1 pint milk
- 1 large sole
- 1/2-lb. vermicelli
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Chop an onion and put it in a stewpan with a teacup of water, salt, and pepper, a little grated nutmeg.
2. Let it boil till the onion is quite tender, add a pint of milk.
3. Wash a large sole well, let it cook in the milk.
4. Then add a 1/2-lb. of vermicelli.
5. When done put the sole in a dish, and the vermicelli round, throw over a little chopped parsley.
6. This is an exquisite and delicate dish for an invalid.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of matzoth meal
- one-fourth cup of potato flour
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-fourth cup of chopped almonds
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Sift together one-half cup of matzoth meal and one-fourth cup of potato flour
2. Add one-half cup of sugar, one-fourth cup of chopped almonds and two eggs
3. Roll out in potato flour mixed with sugar
4. Cut and bake on greased tins in a hot oven

title: Cordial
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of water
- two and three-quarter pounds of sugar
- one quart of alcohol

## Directions

1. Boil two quarts of water and two and three-quarter pounds of sugar for thirty minutes
2. Take off stove and add one quart of alcohol
3. Color and flavor to taste

title: Corn
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the corn
2. Cut it off the cobs
3. Pack in jars in alternate layers of salt and corn, using plenty of salt in packing
4. Pack the corn in this way: First a layer of salt, half an inch deep; then about two inches of corn; then salt again, and so on
5. The top layer must be salt
6. Spread two inches of melted butter over the top layer and bind with strong perforated paper
7. Keep in a cool cellar

title: Corn Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of corn-meal
- one cup of flour
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- three teaspoons of baking-powder
- one tablespoon of butter
- one egg
- milk

## Directions

1. Mix and sift one cup of corn-meal, one cup of flour, two tablespoons of sugar, one-half teaspoon of salt, three teaspoons of baking-powder
2. Melt one tablespoon of butter and add to one egg
3. Mix milk and egg and beat this into the dry ingredients
4. Pour this mixture into well-greased tins
5. Bake in a hot oven one-half hour
6. Cut in squares and serve hot

title: Corn Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of corn
- one egg
- one cup of sweet milk
- soda
- two teaspoons of melted butter
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Grate two cups of corn from the cob
2. Beat one egg light
3. Stir a bit of soda the size of a pea into one cup of sweet milk
4. Mix the corn with the egg, milk mixture, two teaspoons of melted butter, a pinch of salt and enough flour to make a thin batter
5. Beat well together
6. Fry on a griddle as you would cakes for breakfast

title: Corn Muffins, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two cups of flour
- one cup of white flour
- three-quarters cup of corn-meal
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- pinch of salt
- boiling water
- sugar
- tepid milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites of two eggs separately
2. Add to this two cups of flour, of which one is a full cup of white and three-quarters of the corn-meal
3. Sift flour three times
4. Put into this flour two teaspoons of baking-powder, together with a pinch of salt
5. Mix the prepared flour with a little boiling water, adding the eggs
6. A little sugar may be put in, if desired
7. Add enough tepid milk to make the mixture into a batter
8. Pour into your pans or, if corn-bread is desired, into the plain pan thin
9. Bake in a quick oven
10. Butter your pan well, or the small gem-pans, and heat the pan a little

title: Corn Muffins, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of white flour
- one-half cup of corn-meal
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of soda
- one egg
- one cup of sour milk
- one tablespoon of melted butter

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of white flour, one-half cup of corn-meal, one tablespoon of sugar, one-half teaspoon of salt and one-half teaspoon of soda
2. Add one egg beaten into one cup of sour milk and one tablespoon of melted butter
3. Beat thoroughly
4. Bake in well-greased tins

title: Corn Off the Cob
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. With a sharp knife cut off the grains, not cutting closely enough to remove any of the woody portion of the skins.
2. With a knife press out all the pulp and milk remaining in the cob; add this to the corn.
3. Season well with salt, pepper and butter; add a little more milk if the corn is dry.
4. Cook, preferably in the oven, for about ten minutes, stirring occasionally. If the oven is not hot, cook over the fire.

title: Corn Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of canned corn
- one egg
- pepper
- salt
- two tablespoons of cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Take one-half cup of canned corn and chop it very fine (or the same amount cut from the cob).
2. Add to that the yolk of one egg, well beaten with pepper and salt to taste, and two tablespoons of cream.
3. Beat the white of the egg very stiff and stir in just before cooking.
4. Have the pan very hot and profusely buttered.
5. Pour the mixture on, and when nicely browned, turn one half over the other, as in cooking other omelets.

title: Corn Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six ears of green corn
- two cups of milk
- one-half cup of butter
- three eggs
- a little salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape with a knife six ears of green corn, cutting each row through the middle
2. Add two cups of milk, one-half cup of butter, three eggs with the whites and yolks beaten separately, a little salt and white pepper
3. Stir the yolks into the milk and corn
4. Pour into a baking dish, stir in the whites and bake one and one-half hours

title: Corn Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn ears
- Cabbage
- Salt
- Green peppers
- White mustard-seed
- Vinegar
- Granulated sugar
- Turmeric
- Cornstarch
- Dry mustard

## Directions

1. Boil nine ears of corn and cut from cob.
2. Chop fine large head of cabbage and salt it.
3. Chop six green peppers.
4. Dissolve two tablespoons of cornstarch and one tablespoon of dry mustard in three pints of vinegar.
5. Put on to thicken.
6. Strain salt-water from the cabbage.
7. Mix all the ingredients (two tablespoons of white mustard-seed, one cup of granulated sugar, two tablespoons of turmeric) and stir in pot of vinegar.
8. Let all get very hot and seal in pint jars.

title: Cornflake Cocoanut Kisses
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of two eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half cup of shredded cocoanut
- two cups of corn flakes
- a pinch of salt
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix the whites of two eggs, beaten stiff, with one-half cup of sugar
2. Add one-half cup of shredded cocoanut
3. Fold in two cups of corn flakes, a pinch of salt, one-half teaspoon of vanilla
4. Make and bake same as kisses

title: Cornmeal Mush
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cornmeal
- one teaspoon of salt
- one cup of cold water
- two cups of boiling water
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together one cup of cornmeal and one teaspoon of salt, and add one cup of cold water gradually, stirring until smooth
2. Pour this mixture into two cups of boiling water in a double boiler and cook from three to five hours
3. Serve hot with cream and sugar

title: Cornmeal Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- one-half cup of Indian meal
- three eggs
- one tablespoon of flour
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of ginger
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- pinch of salt
- one pint cold milk
- hot maple sugar or cream

## Directions

1. Bring one pint of milk to the boiling point
2. Pour it gradually on one-half cup of Indian meal, stirring all the while to prevent lumps
3. When cool add three eggs well beaten, and one tablespoon of flour, one-half cup of sugar, one-half teaspoon of ginger, one teaspoon of cinnamon, pinch of salt and one pint cold milk
4. Pour into buttered pudding dish and bake an hour and a half
5. Serve with hot maple sugar or cream

title: Cornstarch Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- four large tablespoons of cornstarch
- one quarter cup of cold milk
- two whole eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- a tiny pinch of salt
- a piece of butter, size of a hickory nut
- vanilla or lemon

## Directions

1. Heat one quart of milk to boiling point.
2. Dissolve four large tablespoons of cornstarch in a quarter cup of cold milk.
3. Beat two whole eggs with one-half cup of sugar until light, and add a tiny pinch of salt.
4. When the milk begins to boil, add a piece of butter, size of a hickory nut, then pour it over the well-beaten eggs and sugar, mix well, and put back on the stove.
5. Stir until it begins to boil, then stir in the dissolved cornstarch until the custard is very thick.
6. Remove from the fire, flavor with vanilla or lemon, pour into a mold, and set on ice till very cold and firm.
7. Serve with cream.

title: Cottage Cheese (Pot Cheese)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sour milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat sour milk slowly until the whey rises to the top.
2. Pour it off, put the curd in a bag and let it dry for six hours without squeezing it.
3. Pour it into a bowl and break it fine with a wooden spoon.
4. Season with salt.
5. Mold into balls and keep in a cool place.

title: Cottage Cheese Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cheese
- one and one-half tablespoons of cream
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- salt
- cold water
- waxed paper
- pimento
- lettuce leaves
- French dressing
- wafers

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly one pound of cheese, one and one-half tablespoons of cream, one tablespoon of chopped parsley and salt to taste
2. First fill a rectangular tin mold with cold water to chill and wet the surface
3. Line the bottom with waxed paper
4. Pack in three layers, putting two or three parallel strips of pimento between layers
5. Cover with waxed paper and set in a cool place until ready to serve
6. Run a knife around the sides and invert the mold
7. Cut in slices and serve on lettuce leaves with French dressing and wafers

title: Covered Cheese Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- butter the size of an egg
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup water
- 2.5 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- 2 eggs
- 0.5 cup sugar
- 0.5 pound pot cheese
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 cup milk
- lemon extract
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of sugar with butter the size of an egg
2. Add two eggs well beaten and one cup of water alternately with two and one-half cups of flour in which has been sifted two teaspoons of baking-powder
3. For filling: Beat two eggs with one-half cup of sugar, add one-half pound of pot cheese, one tablespoon of cornstarch boiled in one cup of milk
4. Cool this and add, flavor with lemon extract
5. Put one-half of the batter in cake pan, then the filling and the other half of batter
6. Bake in slow oven thirty-five minutes
7. Sift sugar on top when done

title: Crab-Apple Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight quarts Siberian crab-apples
- four quarts water
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take eight quarts of Siberian crab-apples, cut up in pieces, leaving in the seeds, and do not pare.
2. Put into a stone jar, and set on the back of the stove to boil slowly, adding four quarts of water.
3. Let them boil, closely covered all day, then put in a jelly-bag and let them drip all night.
4. Boil a pint of juice at a time, with a pound of sugar to every pint of juice.
5. Boil five minutes steadily, each pint exactly five minutes.
6. Weigh another pound of sugar and measure another pint of juice.
7. Keep on in this way and you will be through before you realize it.
8. You may press the pulp that remains in the jelly-bag through a coarse strainer, add the juice of two lemons and as much sugar as you have pulp, and cook to a jam.

title: Crackers and Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Bent's water biscuits
- hot water
- melted butter
- French mustard
- grated cheese
- paprika or cayenne

## Directions

1. Split in two some Bent's water biscuits
2. Moisten them with hot water
3. Pour over each piece a little melted butter and French mustard
4. Spread with a thick layer of grated cheese
5. Sprinkle with paprika or cayenne
6. Place them in a hot oven until the cheese is soft and creamy

title: Cranberry Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cranberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and pick ripe cranberries and set on to boil in a porcelain-lined kettle closely covered
2. When soft strain the pulp through a fine wire sieve
3. Measure the juice and add an equal quantity of sugar
4. Set it on to boil again and let it boil very fast for about ten minutes — but it must boil steadily all the time
5. Wet a mold with cold water, turn the jelly into it and set it away to cool
6. When firm turn it into a glass salver

title: Cranberry Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of cranberries
- one and one-quarter cups of water
- three-fourths of a pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Put the cranberries on with the water and cook until soft
2. Strain through a cloth
3. Weigh and add three-fourths of a pound of sugar to every pint of juice
4. Cook ten minutes
5. Pour into molds and set aside to cool

title: Cream Cheese Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cream cheese
- cream
- preserved gooseberries
- biscuits

## Directions

1. Moisten cream cheese with cream and beat to a froth
2. Arrange in a mound shape on a dish and turn preserved gooseberries over it
3. Serve with biscuits

title: Cream Cheese Salad with Pineapples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one slice of Hawaiian pineapple
- lettuce leaves
- cream cheese
- chopped walnuts
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Serve one slice of Hawaiian pineapple on lettuce leaves
2. On the pineapple slice place a spoon of cream cheese and some chopped walnuts
3. Top off with a dash of mayonnaise dressing

title: Cream Codling Tart
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- 6 eggs
- almond milk
- rind of a lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stew the apples
2. Lay them in a dish
3. Make a custard of 6 eggs and almond milk
4. Flavour with the rind of a lemon
5. Sprinkle over some cinnamon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cream Codling Tart
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 codling apples
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- 2 or 3 cloves
- 1 wine-glass of orange-flower water
- puff paste
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of sifted sugar
- 1/2 pint of almond milk

## Directions

1. Peel and core 6 codling apples, cut them in small thick pieces
2. Put them in a saucepan with 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar, 2 or 3 cloves
3. Let them boil to a pulp, then beat them well with a spoon till they are quite smooth
4. Mix with them a wine-glass of orange-flower water
5. Line a pie dish with puff paste
6. When the apples are cold turn them into the pie dish
7. Put a border of paste round, crossbar the top with pastry
8. Bake it in a quick oven
9. Mix in a basin 3 eggs, with 3 tablespoonfuls of sifted sugar, mix it well together
10. Boil 1/2 pint of almond milk, and stir your eggs in with a little more orange-flower water
11. Let it cool, then turn it over the top
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cream Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- three-fourths of a cup of sugar
- one-third cup of flour
- one-eighth teaspoon of salt
- three slightly-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Scald two cups of milk
2. Mix together three-fourths of a cup of sugar, one-third cup of flour and one-eighth teaspoon of salt
3. Add to three slightly-beaten eggs and pour in scalded milk
4. Cook twenty minutes over boiling water, stirring constantly until thickened
5. Cool and flavor
6. This can be used as a foundation for most fillings, by adding melted chocolate, nuts, fruits, etc.

title: Cream Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of butter
- Two scant cups of sugar
- Yolks of four eggs
- One cup of milk
- Three cups of sifted flour
- Three teaspoons of baking-powder
- Whites of four eggs
- Two tablespoons of cornstarch
- Cold milk
- One-half pint of milk
- One-half cup of sugar
- A pinch of salt
- Yolks of two eggs
- One teaspoon of sweet butter

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter and two scant cups of sugar to a cream
2. Beat in the yolks of four eggs well
3. Add gradually one cup of milk and three cups of sifted flour
4. Add three teaspoons of baking-powder in last sifting
5. Put whites in last
6. Bake in layers as for jelly cake
7. When cold, prepare filling by moistening two tablespoons of cornstarch with enough cold milk to work it into a paste
8. Scald one-half pint of milk with one-half cup of sugar and a pinch of salt
9. Beat the yolks of two eggs light
10. Add the cornstarch to this
11. As soon as the milk is scalded pour in the mixture gradually, stirring constantly until thick
12. Drop in one teaspoon of sweet butter
13. When this is mixed in, set away until cool
14. Spread between layers

title: Cream Mustard Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white sauce
- one tablespoon of mustard
- a teaspoon of cold water

## Directions

1. Make white sauce as directed
2. Mix one tablespoon of mustard with a teaspoon of cold water
3. Stir into the sauce about two minutes before serving
4. The quantity of mustard may be increased or diminished, as one may desire the flavor strong or mild

title: Cream of Almond Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound almonds
- one pint of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one of flour
- one cup of milk
- one cup of cream
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Blanch, and grind or pound one-half pound almonds
2. let simmer slowly in one pint of milk for five minutes
3. Melt one tablespoon of butter, blend with one of flour
4. Do not allow to bubble
5. Add one cup of milk and thicken slightly
6. Then add the almond mixture and simmer again until creamy
7. Remove from fire and add one cup of cream
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste
9. Cream may be whipped or left plain

title: Cream of Celery Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three stalks of celery
- one slice of onion
- three cups of milk
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one-fourth teaspoon of pepper
- one teaspoon of salt
- two-thirds of a cup
- one cup of cream

## Directions

1. Break three stalks of celery in one-inch pieces and pound in a mortar
2. Cook in double boiler with one slice of onion and three cups of milk for twenty minutes
3. Remove onion
4. heat two tablespoons of butter, add two tablespoons of flour, one-fourth teaspoon of pepper, one teaspoon of salt
5. add first two-thirds of a cup, and gradually the rest of the celery broth
6. add one cup of cream
7. cook until smooth and serve at once

title: Cream of Corn Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of corn or six ears of corn
- bay leaf
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Take a can of corn or six ears of corn
2. Run a sharp knife down through the center of each row of kernels, and with the back of a knife press out the pulp, leaving the husk on the cob
3. Break the cobs and put them on to boil in sufficient cold water to cover them
4. Boil thirty minutes and strain the liquor
5. Return the liquor to the fire, and when boiling add the corn pulp and bay leaf
6. Cook fifteen minutes
7. add the cream sauce and serve

title: Cream of Herring Soup (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- two cups of water
- one small onion
- salt and pepper to taste
- two herrings

## Directions

1. Place two cups of milk, two cups of water, one small onion, salt and pepper to taste in a saucepan, and boil for ten minutes
2. add two herrings which have been previously soaked and cut in small pieces
3. cook until herring is tender

title: Cream of Lentil Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of lentils
- one-half teaspoon of baking soda
- two tablespoons of butter or butter substitute
- two teaspoons of salt
- one-half teaspoon of sugar
- three cups of milk
- croutons

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of lentils over night
2. Drain and boil slowly for one hour in water containing one-half teaspoon of baking soda
3. drain and boil again very gently in fresh water
4. when the lentils are tender drain off most of the liquid and return to the fire
5. Add two tablespoons of butter, or butter substitute, two teaspoons of salt, and one-half teaspoon of sugar
6. Bring three cups of milk to a boil in the double-boiler
7. Just before serving mash the lentils through a strainer directly into the milk
8. Serve in cups and pass croutons with the soup

title: Cream of Tomato Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart tomatoes
- one pint water
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one and one-half cups of milk
- cream
- one teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
- chopped parsley
- celery
- one-fourth teaspoon of baking soda

## Directions

1. Cook one quart tomatoes (fresh or canned) with one pint water until done, and strain through a sieve
2. Meanwhile melt two tablespoons of butter, add two tablespoons of flour
3. add gradually one and one-half cups of milk (or half cream and half milk), one teaspoon of salt, one teaspoon of sugar, one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
4. add a little chopped parsley and celery, and let this boil for fifteen minutes
5. Just before ready to serve add one-fourth teaspoon of baking soda to the hot strained tomatoes
6. pour gradually into the cream sauce stirring constantly and serve at once

title: Cream Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- yolks of four eggs
- a little more than a pint of milk
- one tablespoon of cornstarch
- four tablespoons of sugar
- two teaspoons of vanilla
- whites of eggs
- two tablespoons of powdered sugar
- half a teaspoon of cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with puff paste and bake
2. make a cream of the yolks of four eggs, a little more than a pint of milk, one tablespoon of cornstarch and four tablespoons of sugar, and flavor with two teaspoons of vanilla
3. Pour on crust and bake
4. beat up the whites with two tablespoons of powdered sugar and half a teaspoon of cream of tartar
5. Spread on top of pie and set back in the oven until baked a light brown

title: Cream Puffs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of hot water
- One-half cup of butter
- One cup of sifted flour
- Three eggs
- One cup of milk
- One cup of sugar
- One egg
- Three tablespoons of flour
- Vanilla to flavor

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of hot water and one-half cup of butter together
2. While boiling stir in one cup of sifted flour dry
3. Take from the stove and stir to a thin paste
4. After this cools add three eggs unbeaten
5. Stir vigorously for five minutes
6. Drop in tablespoonfuls on a buttered tin
7. Bake in a quick oven twenty-five minutes
8. Open the oven door no oftener than is absolutely necessary
9. Be careful that they do not touch each other in the pan
10. For cream: Stir the flour in a little of the milk
11. Boil the rest
12. Turn this in and stir until the whole thickens
13. When both the puffs and cream are cool open the puff a little way with a sharp knife
14. Fill them with the cream

title: Cream Salmon
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon from the can
- one cup of hot cream sauce
- patty cups or toasted bread or crackers

## Directions

1. Remove salmon from the can, place it in a colander and wash under running water or scald with boiling water.
2. Break into small pieces, stir into one cup of hot cream sauce.
3. Bring all to a boil and serve in patty cups or on toasted bread or crackers.

title: Cream Wine Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of white wine
- one-half cup of cold water
- stick cinnamon
- seven lumps of cut loaf sugar
- one cup of sweet cream
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Put one cup of white wine and one-half cup of cold water on to boil
2. add a few pieces of stick cinnamon and seven lumps of cut loaf sugar
3. while boiling scald a cup of sweet cream in double boiler
4. Have ready the well-beaten yolks of two eggs
5. pour over this the hot cream, stirring all the time
6. then pour in the boiling wine, being careful to stir well or it will curdle
7. Very nice for invalids
8. Can be eaten hot or cold

title: Cream Wine Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of white wine
- one-half cup of cold water
- a few pieces of stick cinnamon
- seven lumps of cut loaf sugar
- one cup of sweet cream
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Put one cup of white wine and one-half cup of cold water on to boil
2. add a few pieces of stick cinnamon and seven lumps of cut loaf sugar
3. while boiling scald a cup of sweet cream in double boiler
4. Have ready the well-beaten yolks of two eggs
5. pour over this the hot cream, stirring all the time
6. then pour in the boiling wine, being careful to stir well or it will curdle
7. Can be eaten hot or cold

title: Creamed Celery
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks
- salt
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the stalks of celery
2. Scrape off all rusted or dark spots
3. Cut into small pieces and drop in cold water
4. Having boiling water ready, put the celery into it, adding one-half teaspoon of salt for every quart of water
5. Boil until tender, leaving the cover partly off
6. Drain and rinse in cold water
7. Make a cream sauce, drop the celery into it, heat thoroughly and serve

title: Creamed Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- black pepper
- cream
- flour
- milk
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Wash them thoroughly in cold water, peel them and remove the stems
2. cut them in halves or quarters, according to their size
3. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan over the fire
4. add the mushrooms and let them simmer slowly in the butter for five minutes
5. season them well with salt and black pepper, freshly ground
6. add a gill of cream
7. sift one tablespoon of flour in a bowl and add one-half pint of milk
8. Stir these briskly till flour is all dissolved
9. pour it gradually in the saucepan with the mushrooms and cream, stirring the whole constantly to keep it from lumping
10. Let it just bubble a moment
11. add another tablespoon of butter and pour the creamed mushrooms over hot buttered toast on a hot platter and serve

title: Creamed New Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of boiled and minced new cabbage
- one-half pint of hot milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one teaspoon of flour
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-half teaspoon pepper
- one teaspoon finely minced parsley
- sweet paprika

## Directions

1. To one pint of boiled and minced new cabbage add one-half pint of hot milk, one tablespoon of butter, one teaspoon of flour, one-half teaspoon each of salt and pepper, one teaspoon finely minced parsley and a generous dash of sweet paprika.
2. The butter and flour should be creamed together before stirring in.
3. Let simmer for about ten minutes, stirring occasionally to keep from burning.
4. Serve hot on toasted bread.

title: Creamed Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cream sauce
- milk
- two cups boiled potatoes
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make a cream sauce, a little thinner than usual by adding extra milk
2. Cut boiled potatoes into small cubes
3. Mix them thoroughly with the cream sauce
4. Cook in a double boiler until the potatoes are thoroughly hot
5. Add a little chopped parsley if desired
6. Serve

title: Creme Brun
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large cup of cream
- essence of almonds
- cinnamon
- the yolk of three eggs
- powdered sugar
- beaten almonds

## Directions

1. Boil a large cup of cream and flavor with essence of almonds and cinnamon
2. Mix with it the yolk of three eggs, carefully beaten and strained, stirring one way to keep it smooth
3. Place it on a dish in small heaps
4. Strew over powdered sugar and beaten almonds and brown with a salamander

title: Creole Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small onion
- green pepper
- butter
- tomatoes
- sugar
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- parsley
- thick cream

## Directions

1. Take one small onion and half a green pepper, chop them fine and cook until tender in a tablespoon of butter.
2. Cut six tomatoes in half, sprinkle with a little sugar, season on both sides with salt, pepper and a little flour, and put them into the pan with skin-side down to cook partially, then turn them once; they must cook over a slow fire.
3. Then sprinkle one tablespoon of chopped parsley over them, pour in one cup of thick cream and when this has become thoroughly hot and has been combined with the other ingredients, the tomatoes are ready to serve.

title: Crescent Rolls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread or kuchen dough
- melted butter
- beaten egg yolk
- cold water
- poppy seed

## Directions

1. Take bread or kuchen dough, and when well risen, toss on floured baking board
2. roll into a square sheet, one-quarter inch thick
3. Spread with melted butter, and cut into six-inch squares
4. cut each square into two equal parts through opposite corners, thus forming two triangles
5. Roll over and over from the longest side to the opposite corner and then shape the rolls into half moons or crescents
6. Place in floured or greased pans, rather far apart
7. brush with beaten yolk to which a little cold water has been added
8. sprinkle tops of crescents with poppy seed
9. Set in warm place to rise and when double its bulk, bake in hot oven until brown and crusty

title: Cressy Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 nice fresh-looking large carrots
- a pint of water
- a little salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Take 4 or 5 nice fresh-looking large carrots.
2. Cut them in small pieces and put them in a saucepan with a pint of water, a little salt and pepper.
3. Let them boil to a pulp and rub them through.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cressy Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 large carrots
- a pint of water
- salt and pepper
- a quart of stock No. 1
- flour
- a little cayenne pepper
- a few very small pieces of toasted bread
- a little fine chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut carrots in small pieces
2. Put them in a saucepan with a pint of water, a little salt and pepper
3. Let them boil to a pulp
4. Rub them well through a sieve
5. Have ready a quart of stock No. 1
6. When hot, put in the liquor the carrots were boiled in and the pulp
7. Let it all boil together if not thick enough
8. Rub a couple of spoonfuls of flour with a little cold water and stir it in
9. The soup should be as thick as good cream
10. Add a little cayenne pepper
11. Have ready in the tureen a few very small pieces of toasted bread cut in the shape of dice and a little fine chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Croquante Cakes (Small Cakes)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three-fourths pound of shelled almonds
- one-half pound of citron
- flour
- three-fourths pound of sugar
- six eggs
- three-fourths pound of flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Blanch and cut in halves three-fourths pound of shelled almonds
2. Slice one-half pound of citron
3. Mix well together and roll in a little flour
4. Add to them three-fourths pound of sugar
5. Add six eggs well beaten
6. Add the rest of the flour (three-fourths pound)
7. Butter shallow pans
8. Put in the mixture about two inches thick
9. Bake in a quick oven
10. Slice cake in strips three-fourths of an inch wide and turn each piece
11. Put back in oven and bake a little longer
12. When cold put away in tin box

title: Croquettes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
