## Ingredients

- cold poultry, meat, or fish
- minced mushrooms
- herbs
- parsley
- grated lemon peel
- minced anchovies
- beaten eggs
- bread crumbs or pounded vermicelli

## Directions

1. Pound any cold poultry, meat, or fish, make it into a delicate forcemeat
2. Vary the flavor according to taste; use minced mushrooms, herbs, parsley, grated lemon peel for poultry and veal; use minced anchovies instead of mushrooms when the croquettes are made of fish
3. Form the mixture into balls or oval shapes the size of small eggs
4. Dip them into beaten eggs
5. Thickly sprinkle with bread crumbs or pounded vermicelli
6. Fry of a handsome brown

title: Croquettes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold poultry or veal
- mushrooms
- two eggs
- fine bread crumbs or pounded vermicelli
- clarified dripping or melted veal fat

## Directions

1. Pound very fine cold poultry or veal and make it into delicate forcemeat
2. Vary the flavour according to taste; with poultry a few mushrooms chopped will improve it
3. Form the forcemeat into balls round or the shape of olives, the size of small eggs
4. Beat up two eggs and dip the balls well into the eggs
5. Dip into fine bread crumbs or pounded vermicelli
6. Fry them in clarified dripping or melted veal fat to a handsome brown colour
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Croquettes of Calf's Brains
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt water
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- beaten egg
- one-half cup of bread crumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Lay the brains in salt water an hour, or until they look perfectly white
2. Take out one at a time
3. Pat with your hands to loosen the outer skin and pull it off
4. Beat or rub them to a smooth paste with a wooden spoon
5. Season with salt and pepper and a very little mace
6. Add a beaten egg and about one-half cup of bread crumbs
7. Heat fat in a spider
8. Fry large spoonfuls of this mixture in it

title: Croquettes of Cold Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cod
- parsley
- marjoram
- a little rind of lemon
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- a few crumbs of bread
- an egg
- fine bread crumbs
- oil
- slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Take all the white pieces of flesh free from bone from any cold cod that may be left
2. Chop it very finely with parsley, marjoram, and a little rind of lemon
3. Season with pepper, salt, grated nutmeg
4. Mix them up with a few crumbs of bread and an egg
5. Roll them into balls the size of a pippin apple
6. Have a plate with fine bread crumbs, and one with eggs
7. Have your pan with oil, and when boiling hot put the croquettes in
8. Fry a light brown
9. Serve this on a fish napkin garnished with parsley and slices of lemon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Croquettes of Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled fish
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one egg
- one teaspoon of milk
- flour
- cracker crumbs or bread

## Directions

1. Take any kind of boiled fish
2. Separate it from the bones carefully
3. Chop with a little parsley, salt and pepper to taste
4. Beat up one egg with one teaspoon of milk and flour
5. Roll the fish into balls
6. Turn them in the beaten egg and cracker crumbs or bread
7. Fry a light brown
8. Serve with any sauce or a mayonnaise

title: Croutons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- butter
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut stale bread into cubes, place in pan and brown in the oven; or butter the bread, cut into cubes and then brown the same way.
2. Fry small cubes of stale bread in deep hot fat until brown or fry them in a little butter or fat in a hot spider until brown.

title: Crullers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- one-half cup of sugar
- two whole eggs
- one-half cup of milk
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- flour
- one-half teaspoon cinnamon
- Crisco

## Directions

1. Cream butter with sugar
2. Beat in one at a time two whole eggs
3. Mix well, then add milk, baking-powder, and sufficient flour to make a soft batter to roll out
4. Add one-half teaspoon cinnamon last
5. Roll one-half inch thick
6. Cut in strips one inch wide and three inches long
7. Fry in hot Crisco

title: Crumb Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken fat
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- chopped parsley
- egg

## Directions

1. Take one tablespoon of chicken fat, mix in two cups of bread crumbs
2. Add pinch of salt and pepper, a few drops of onion juice, one tablespoon of chopped parsley
3. Add one well-beaten egg
4. Mix all on stove in skillet
5. Remove from fire and stuff fowl

title: Cucumber Mango
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- large cucumber
- vinegar
- mustard-seed
- garlic
- spices
- highly-seasoned forcemeat
- rich gravy

## Directions

1. Cut a large cucumber in half, length ways, scoop out the seedy part, and lay it in vinegar that has been boiled with mustard-seed, a little garlic, and spices, for twenty-four hours
2. Fill the cucumber with highly-seasoned forcemeat
3. Stew it in a rich gravy, the cucumber must be tied to keep it together

title: Cucumber Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- onion
- salt
- white pepper
- parsley
- vinegar
- white or red radishes

## Directions

1. Pare thickly, from end to end, and lay in ice-water one hour
2. Wipe them, slice thin, and slice an onion equally thin
3. Strew salt over them, shake up a few times, cover and let remain in this brine for another hour
4. Squeeze or press out every drop of water which has been extracted from the cucumbers
5. Put into a salad bowl, sprinkle with white pepper and scatter bits of parsley over them
6. Add enough vinegar to cover
7. You may slice up an equal quantity of white or red radishes and mix with this salad

title: Cucumber Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two large cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- paprika
- one-half cup cream

## Directions

1. Pare two large cucumbers; remove seeds, if large; chop fine and squeeze dry.
2. Season with salt, vinegar, paprika and add one-half cup of cream.

title: Cucumbers In Oil
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one hundred medium-sized cucumbers
- one pint salt
- two pints of sliced onions
- four tablespoons of black mustard seed
- four tablespoons of white mustard seed
- two tablespoons of celery seed
- one-half pint of best olive oil
- one-half pint of white vinegar
- one teaspoon of powdered alum
- warm water

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers thin lengthwise
2. Add one pint salt and let stand overnight
3. Drain thoroughly in morning
4. Add two pints of sliced onions
5. Add dressing, consisting of four tablespoons of black mustard seed, four of white mustard seed, two of celery seed, one-half pint of best olive oil, one-half pint of white vinegar
6. Put cucumbers and onions into this
7. Add one teaspoon of powdered alum, dissolved in a little warm water
8. Add enough vinegar to cover it well
9. Let stand three weeks before using

title: Cumberland Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumbs
- apples finely chopped
- currants
- shred suet
- brown sugar
- three eggs
- a little brandy
- grated nutmeg
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of bread crumbs, apples finely chopped, currants and shred suet
2. Sweeten with brown sugar, and mix all together with three eggs, a little brandy, grated nutmeg, and lemon peel
3. Boil in a round mould from one to two hours, according to the size of the pudding

title: Cup Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 4 eggs
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- 1 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of butter with two cups of sugar
2. Add gradually the yolks of four eggs, one at a time
3. Sift three cups of flour, measure again after sifting, and add two teaspoons of baking-powder in the last sifting
4. Add alternately the sifted flour and one cup of sweet milk
5. Add last the beaten whites of the eggs
6. Flavor to taste
7. Bake in loaf or jelly-tins

title: Cup Custard for Six
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs, yolks and whites
- four tablespoons of sugar
- four cups of scalded milk
- pinch of salt
- one teaspoon of good vanilla

## Directions

1. Stir until quite light four eggs, yolks and whites, and four tablespoons of sugar.
2. Have ready four cups of scalded milk.
3. Mix, add pinch of salt and one teaspoon of good vanilla.
4. Pour into cups and place cups into pan of boiling water.
5. Put into oven and bake exactly twenty-five minutes.

title: Currant Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half ripe currants
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Pick over half ripe currants, leaving stems on.
2. Wash and place in preserving kettle.
3. Pound vigorously with wooden masher until there is juice enough to boil.
4. Boil slowly until fruit turns white and liquid drops slowly from the spoon.
5. Stir to prevent scorching.
6. Remove from fire and dip mixture into the jelly bags, under which large bowls have been placed to catch the drip.
7. Drip overnight.
8. Next morning measure the juice.
9. For every pint of juice allow a pint of granulated sugar, which is put in a flat pan.
10. Put juice in kettle and allow to come to boiling point.
11. Place sugar in oven and heat.
12. When juice boils add sugar and stir until dissolved.
13. When this boils remove from fire and skim. Do this three times.
14. Test liquid with syrup gauge to see if it registers twenty-five degrees, or let it drip from spoon when half cooled to see if it jells.
15. Strain into sterilized jelly glasses.
16. Place glasses on a board in a sunny exposure until it hardens.
17. Cover with melted paraffin one-fourth inch thick.

title: Currant Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Half ripe currants
- Granulated sugar, allowing one pint for every pint of juice

## Directions

1. Pick over half ripe currants, leaving stems on
2. Wash and place in preserving kettle
3. Pound vigorously with wooden masher until there is juice enough to boil
4. Boil slowly until fruit turns white and liquid drops slowly from the spoon
5. Stir to prevent scorching
6. Remove from fire
7. Take an enamelled cup and dip this mixture into the jelly bags, under which large bowls have been placed to catch the drip
8. Drip overnight
9. Next morning measure the juice
10. For every pint allow a pint of granulated sugar, which is put in a flat pan
11. Put juice in kettle and allow to come to boiling point
12. Place sugar in oven and heat
13. When juice boils add sugar

title: Currants
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve quarts of currants
- four quarts of sugar

## Directions

1. Treat the same as raspberries.

title: Curried Chops
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- chops
- 2 spoonfuls of curry
- 1 spoonful of flour
- juice of a lemon
- large rice
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Chop an onion in a stewpan with salt and pepper and a little nutmeg
2. Place in the chops and cut off some of the fat
3. Let them cook
4. When done stir in a basin 2 spoonfuls of curry and 1 of flour with the juice of a lemon
5. Stir this over the chops
6. Have some large rice boiled quite dry with a little salt
7. Put a bordering of rice round your dish
8. Throw a little chopped parsley over it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Curried Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of butter
- one onion
- one tablespoon of curry powder
- one-fourth of a cup of water
- two tablespoons of flour
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- six hard-boiled eggs
- rice

## Directions

1. Melt four tablespoons of butter in a frying-pan
2. Add one onion chopped fine and cook until straw colored
3. Add one tablespoon of curry powder
4. Make a smooth paste of one-fourth of a cup of water and two tablespoons of flour
5. Add one tablespoon of lemon juice and one-half teaspoon of salt
6. Add to the first mixture
7. Boil five minutes
8. Arrange six hard-boiled eggs in a border of rice
9. Pour the dressing over all

title: Curried Fowl
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- onion
- garlic

## Directions

1. Place a fowl in a stewpan with 1 pint of water, salt and pepper, a little grated nutmeg, one chopped onion, a tooth of garlic
2. Let it simmer gently for three quarters of an hour
3. After you have skimmed it, then take the fowl out, strain the
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Curried Fowl
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 1 pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- one chopped onion
- a tooth of garlic
- 1/2-lb. of the best rice
- the juice of a large lemon
- 2 spoonfuls of curry powder
- slices of lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place a fowl in a stewpan with 1 pint of water, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, one chopped onion, a tooth of garlic.
2. Let it simmer gently for 1/2 of an hour.
3. After you have skimmed it, then take the fowl out, strain the liquor through a clean sieve, place the fowl back in the saucepan, wash and drain well.
4. Mix 1/2-lb. of the best rice with salt and pepper, the juice of a large lemon, 2 spoonfuls of curry powder, put it in the saucepan with the fowl.
5. Let it simmer gently till the rice is quite done and dry.
6. Lay the fowl in the dish and lay the rice round like a little wall.
7. Cut some slices of lemon thin, then cut them in halves, and make a border round the rice.
8. Chop some very green parsley, sprinkle a little on the lemon.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Curried Mutton
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of mutton
- cold water
- boiling water
- one cup of chopped onion
- one teaspoon of peppercorns
- one-half of a red pepper
- flour
- two tablespoons of drippings
- one tablespoon of minced parsley
- one teaspoon of curry powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- molded rice

## Directions

1. Have three pounds of mutton cut in one inch squares.
2. Wipe, put in kettle and cover with cold water.
3. Cook for five minutes, drain and again cover with boiling water.
4. Add one cup of chopped onion, one teaspoon of peppercorns, and one-half of a red pepper, cut in small strips.
5. Place on back of stove and allow it to simmer until tender.
6. Strain liquor and thicken with flour.
7. Add two tablespoons of drippings, one tablespoon of minced parsley, one teaspoon of curry powder, and one-half teaspoon of salt.
8. Serve with molded rice.

title: Curried Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons fat
- one onion
- two cups boiled potatoes
- one-half cup stock
- one tablespoon curry powder
- one-half teaspoon salt
- red pepper
- one teaspoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of fat in a frying-pan
2. Add one onion chopped fine and cook until straw color
3. Add two cups of boiled potatoes cut in dice
4. Add one-half cup of stock and one tablespoon of curry powder
5. Cook until the stock has been absorbed
6. Add one-half teaspoon of salt, a dash of red pepper, and one teaspoon of lemon juice

title: Curried Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a breast of veal
- a chopped onion
- currie powder
- a good gravy of veal and beef
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a breast of veal into pieces
2. Fry lightly with a chopped onion
3. Rub the veal over with currie powder
4. Put it into a good gravy of veal and beef
5. Season simply with pepper, salt, and lemon juice

title: Curry Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon of curry
- ingredients for white sauce

## Directions

1. Use one teaspoon of curry in the flour while making white sauce

title: Custard Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- four eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- one pint of milk
- grated nutmeg or grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Line the pie-plate with a rich crust
2. Beat up four eggs light with one-half cup of sugar, a pinch of salt, one pint of milk and grated nutmeg or grated lemon peel
3. pour in shell and bake in slow oven

title: Custard Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one desert spoonful of flour
- one pint of fresh milk
- yolks of five eggs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- lemon-peel
- two whites of eggs

## Directions

1. To one desert spoonful of flour, add one pint of fresh milk and the yolks of five eggs
2. flavor according to fancy, with sugar, nutmeg, or lemon-peel
3. beat to a froth two whites of eggs and pour to the rest
4. boil rather more than half an hour

title: Cutlets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets
- egg
- flour
- a spoonful of flour
- a small cup of gravy
- salt
- pepper
- a little lemon juice or lemon pickle

## Directions

1. Cut them into proper shape and beat them with a roller until the fibre of the meat is entirely broken
2. Cover them with egg and sprinkle with flour, or a preparation for cutlets may be spread over them
3. Fry them of a fine brown
4. Remove the cutlets to a hot dish
5. Add to the fat in which the cutlets have been fried, a spoonful of flour, a small cup of gravy, salt, pepper, and a little lemon juice or lemon pickle

title: Cutlets in White Fricassee
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets
- veal gravy made with the trimmings
- fresh button mushrooms
- a piece of lemon peel
- the yolks of two beaten eggs
- forcemeat in which mushrooms must be minced
- thin slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Cut them in proper shapes
2. Put them in a veal gravy made with the trimmings enough to cover them
3. Season delicately, and let them simmer till quite tender, but not long enough to lose their shape
4. Remove the meat when done
5. Take all fat from the gravy, and thicken it with the yolks of two beaten eggs
6. Poach small balls of forcemeat in which mushrooms must be minced in the gravy when about to be served
7. Return the meat to the saucepan to be made hot
8. When placed in the dish, garnish with thin slices of lemon

title: Cutlets with Gherkin Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 plump chops
- bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- chopped parsley
- powdered marjoram
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- boiling fat
- 1 onion
- dripping
- juice of a lemon
- 2 green gherkins pickled
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Take 4 plump chops and take out the bones
2. Cut each chop into 3 small chops and flatten them very nicely
3. Have some bread crumbs in one plate and 2 eggs beat up in another plate
4. Add to the eggs some chopped parsley, a little powdered marjoram, pepper, salt, and grated nutmeg
5. Beat it all well
6. Have your frying-pan on the fire with some boiling fat
7. Dip the little cutlets first in the bread crumbs, then into the egg
8. Fry them a light brown
9. For the sauce, chop an onion and put it in a small saucepan with a small piece of dripping
10. Fry it and add some salt, pepper, the juice of a lemon, and two green gherkins pickled cut up small
11. Do not let the gherkins boil
12. Mix a teaspoonful of flour with the juice of the lemon, and 1/2 a teaspoonful of brown sugar
13. Stir all this well together
14. Place cutlets in a graceful pile in the centre of the dish and pour the sauce round
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Cutlets à la Française
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets
- egg
- salt
- white pepper
- mushroom powder
- grated lemon peel
- pieces of bread, smoked meat or tongue
- puree of mushrooms or spinach

## Directions

1. Cut them thinner than the English, and trim them into rounds of the size of a tea-cup
2. Brush them over with egg, and sprinkle with salt, white pepper, mushroom powder, and grated lemon peel
3. Put them into a saute pan and fry of a very light brown
4. Cut pieces of bread, smoked meat or tongue of the same size as the cutlets, and prepare in the same manner
5. Lay them alternately in the dish with the cutlets
6. Serve without sauce and with a puree of mushrooms or spinach in the centre of the dish

title: Damson Jam
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of sugar for each pound of fruit
- plums
- water

## Directions

1. After washing the plums carefully, put them in a preserving kettle with just enough water to keep them from sticking to the bottom
2. Set them over a moderate fire and let them simmer for half an hour
3. Turn them, juice and all, into a colander, filling the colander not more than half full
4. Have the colander set over a large earthen bowl
5. With a potato masher, press juice and pulp through the colander into the bowl, leaving skins and pits as dry as possible
6. Remove these from the colander and repeat the process until all the pulp and juice is pressed out
7. Pour it into the kettle and, while it is heating slowly, heat the sugar in the oven
8. As soon as the juice and pulp begins to simmer stir in the hot sugar
9. When it drops from the spoon like a thick jelly pour it into the glasses

title: Damson Jam
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Weigh three-quarters of a pound of sugar for each pound of fruit
2. After washing the plums carefully, put them in a preserving kettle with just enough water to keep them from sticking to the bottom
3. Set them over a moderate fire and let them simmer for half an hour
4. Then turn them, juice and all, into a colander, filling the colander not more than half full
5. Have the colander set over a large earthen bowl
6. With a potato masher, press juice and pulp through the colander into the bowl, leaving skins and pits as dry as possible
7. Remove these from the colander and repeat the process until all the pulp and juice is pressed out; then pour it into the kettle

title: Damson Marmalade
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- damsons
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Make in the same manner as quince marmalade

title: Dandelions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one peck dandelions
- two quarts boiling salted water
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash one peck of dandelions; remove roots.
2. Cook one hour in two quarts of boiling salted water.
3. Drain, chop fine; season with salt, pepper and butter.
4. Serve with vinegar.

title: Date and Fig Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of dates and figs
- blanched almonds
- orange juice
- thin slices of buttered bread

## Directions

1. Wash equal quantities of dates and figs and stone the dates.
2. Add blanched almonds in quantity about one-fourth of the entire bulk.
3. Run the whole mixture through a food chopper.
4. Moisten with orange juice and press tightly into baking-powder tins.
5. When ready to use, dip the box in hot water and turn out the mixture.
6. Slice and place between thin slices of buttered bread.

title: Date Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- one and one-quarter cups of pulverized sugar
- two tablespoons of ground cinnamon and cloves mixed
- one cup of matzoth-meal
- one-half pound seeded dates
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and sugar together until very light
2. Add the matzoth-meal, spices, dates and lemon
3. Put in the whites of the eggs, beaten to a stiff froth
4. Bake in a moderate oven three-quarters of an hour

title: Date Macaroons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty dates
- one-half pound of almonds
- whites of two eggs
- one cup of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Stone thirty dates and chop them fine
2. Cut one-half pound of almonds lengthwise in slices, but do not blanch them
3. Beat the whites of two eggs until foamy
4. Add one cup of powdered sugar, and beat until stiff
5. Add the dates, then the almonds, and mix very thoroughly
6. Drop mixture with teaspoon in small piles on tins, one-half inch apart
7. Bake thirty minutes in a very slow oven or until dry
8. They are done when they leave the pan readily

title: Date Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons of butter
- one-half cup of molasses
- one-half cup of milk
- one and two-third cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of baking-soda
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one-quarter teaspoon of cloves
- one-quarter teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-quarter teaspoon of nutmeg
- one-half pound of dates

## Directions

1. Melt three tablespoons of butter
2. Add one-half cup of molasses and one-half cup of milk
3. Add one and two-third cups of flour sifted with one-half teaspoon of baking-soda and one-quarter teaspoon of salt
4. Add one-quarter teaspoon each of cloves, cinnamon, and nutmeg
5. Add one-half pound of dates, stoned and cut
6. Turn into a well-buttered mold
7. Butter the cover also and steam two and one-half hours
8. Keep at a steady boil
9. Serve with any kind of sauce

title: Date Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of pulverized sugar
- yolks of six large eggs
- one-half pound of dates, cut very fine
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one-fourth pound of chocolate grated
- juice and peel of one lemon
- three and one-half soda crackers, rolled to a fine powder
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- whites of eggs, stiff-beaten

## Directions

1. Beat pulverized sugar with the yolks of six large eggs
2. Beat long and steadily until a thick batter
3. Add dates, allspice, cinnamon, chocolate, juice and peel of lemon, soda crackers, and baking-powder
4. Add last the stiff-beaten whites
5. Bake slowly
6. Cake can be cut in half and put together with jelly

title: Dates Stuffed with Fondant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Dates
- Fondant
- Pecan or walnut
- Granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Fill dates with fondant, letting it project slightly
2. Insert in it a pecan or half a walnut
3. Roll in granulated sugar

title: Dates Stuffed with Ginger and Nuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Choice dates
- Preserved ginger
- Blanched nuts chopped
- Fondant or confectioner's sugar and ginger syrup

## Directions

1. Remove the stones from choice dates
2. Chop together equal measures of preserved ginger and blanched nuts chopped (hickory, pecan, or almond)
3. Mix with fondant or a paste of confectioner's sugar and ginger syrup
4. Use only enough fondant or paste to hold the ingredients together
5. Fill the open space in the dates, cover securely, and roll in granulated sugar

title: Delicious and Nourishing Summer Drink
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rind of three large lemons pared thinly
- one pound of raisins stoned and finely chopped
- one pound of sugar
- juice of the lemons
- one gallon of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pare thinly the rind of three large lemons, put it into a large jug with one pound of raisins stoned and finely chopped, one pound of sugar, and the juice of the lemons.
2. Add one gallon of boiling water, leave to stand for five days, stirring well every day.
3. Then strain and bottle for use.

title: Delicious Mustard Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about two dozen large, yellow pickles
- silver knife
- salt
- about two quarts of small pickling onions
- horseradish
- whole peppers
- bay leaves
- mustard seed
- allspice
- whole cloves
- best white wine vinegar
- double cheese-cloth

## Directions

1. Pare pickles with a silver knife to prevent them from turning dark
2. Cut lengthwise
3. Remove all the seeds and soft inner pulp with a silver spoon
4. Cut into strips about as long as your finger
5. Sprinkle salt over them and continue until all are cut up
6. Put in a wooden pail or large china bowl overnight
7. Take about two quarts of small pickling onions, scald them with boiling water
8. Remove the skins with a silver knife
9. Salt the same as you did the pickles
10. In the morning take a clean dish towel and dry each piece
11. Lay them in a stone jar in the following manner: First a layer of pickles then a layer of onions
12. Add some horseradish, sliced, between the layers
13. Add a few whole peppers, a very few bay leaves, and sprinkle mustard seed, allspice and whole cloves between each layer
14. Remove the soft little heads of the cloves to prevent the pickles from turning dark
15. Cover all with the best white wine vinegar
16. Put a double cheese-cloth filled with mustard seed on top
17. In two weeks pour off the vinegar carefully and boil, and let it get perfectly cold before pouring over the pickles again
18. You may pack them in small glass jars if you prefer

title: Depilatory for Superfluous Hair
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of finely powdered pumice-stone
- one ounce of powdered quick-lime

## Directions

1. Mix one ounce of finely powdered pumice-stone with one ounce of powdered quick-lime
2. rub the mixture on the part from which the hair is to be removed, twice in twenty-four hours

title: Descaides
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- livers of chickens or any other poultry
- good gravy
- onion
- mushroom essence
- pepper
- salt
- yolks of one or two eggs
- round of toast

## Directions

1. Stew the livers gently in a little good gravy seasoned with a little onion, mushroom essence, pepper, and salt
2. When tender, remove the livers, place them on a paste board, and mince them
3. Return them to the saucepan, and stir in the yolks of one or two eggs, according to the quantity of liver, until the gravy becomes thick
4. Have a round of toast ready on a hot plate, and serve the livers on the toast

title: Dessert with Whipped Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lady fingers
- whipped cream
- macaroons
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Line the edges of a mold or a large glass dish with lady fingers and fill up with whipped cream.
2. Ornament with macaroons and candied fruit.
3. Serve cold.

title: Deviled Brains
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of fat
- two tablespoons of flour
- one-half can of tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- finely-chopped parsley
- cayenne pepper
- brains
- bread crumbs
- chicken-fat
- green peas

## Directions

1. Put one tablespoon of fat in skillet
2. when hot add two tablespoons of flour, rub until smooth, and brown lightly
3. add one-half can of tomatoes
4. season with salt, pepper, finely-chopped parsley, and a dash of cayenne pepper
5. add the brains, which have previously been cleaned, scalded with boiling water, and cut in small pieces
6. Cook a few minutes
7. fill the shells with the mixture
8. Over each shell sprinkle bread crumbs, and a little chicken-fat
9. Put shells in pan and brown nicely
10. Serve with green peas

title: Deviled Eggs With Hot Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard-boiled eggs
- one dessertspoon of melted butter
- Cayenne pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- a pinch of Cayenne pepper
- one pint of milk
- yolk of one egg
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Take six hard-boiled eggs, cut lengthwise
2. Remove yolk and add to same: one dessertspoon of melted butter, Cayenne pepper, salt and chopped parsley
3. Mash this mixture very fine
4. Refill the whites of the eggs
5. Turn over on platter
6. For sauce: put one tablespoon of butter, one tablespoon of flour, a pinch of Cayenne pepper, salt and one pint of milk in a pan
7. Stir this mixture continually until it thickens
8. Beat the yolk of one egg and pour the hot gravy over the same
9. Dress with chopped parsley and eat very hot
10. Sherry wine can be added if desired

title: Deviled Tongue Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- mustard
- soup
- mayonnaise
- white bread

## Directions

1. Grind up tongue (root will do) in meat chopper
2. To a cup of ground tongue add one teaspoon of mustard, one tablespoon of soup, and one teaspoon of mayonnaise
3. Mix into soft paste
4. Spread on white bread cut very thin

title: Devilled Biscuits
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuits
- butter
- pepper
- chopped anchovies or fine cheese
- mustard
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Butter some biscuits on both sides, and pepper them well
2. make a paste of either chopped anchovies, or fine cheese, and spread it on the biscuit, with mustard and cayenne pepper
3. grill them

title: Diet-Bread Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- half a pound of white sugar
- six ounces of flour
- lemon-juice
- lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Beat together five eggs and half a pound of white sugar
2. Add six ounces of flour well dried and sifted, a little lemon-juice and grated lemon-peel
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Dill Pickles for Winter Use
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized cucumbers
- salt water
- grape leaves
- green grapes
- whole peppers
- green peppers
- bay leaves
- whole ginger
- cloves
- horseradish
- dill
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take two or three dozen medium-sized cucumbers and lay them in salt water overnight
2. Wipe each one dry, discarding all that are soft
3. Lay them in a wooden vessel along with grape leaves and green grapes, if you can get them, whole peppers, or one or two green peppers, a few bay leaves, a few pieces of whole ginger, a few cloves and a stick of horseradish sliced upon top of all
4. Use plenty of dill between each layer
5. Boil enough water to cover the pickles
6. Use about one pound of salt to six quarts of water, and one cup of vinegar
7. If you wish to keep them all winter, have your barrel closed by a cooper

title: Dimpes Dampes (Appley Slump)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of sugar
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- two cups of flour
- two cups of milk
- one-half cup of butter
- one quart of sliced apples

## Directions

1. Mix one-half cup of sugar, one-quarter teaspoon of salt, two cups of flour and gradually two cups of milk to make a smooth batter
2. Melt one-half cup or a little less of butter in a large shallow dripping-pan and let it spread all over the pan to grease it well
3. Pour one-half cup of butter and one quart of sliced apples to the batter
4. Mix and pour into pan or pans not more than three-quarters of an inch deep
5. Bake in a moderate oven, thirty to forty-five minutes, until a golden brown

title: Divinity
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of granulated sugar
- One-half cup of corn syrup
- One-half cup of water
- Two eggs
- One cup of nuts

## Directions

1. Boil two cups of granulated sugar, one-half cup of corn syrup and one-half cup of water until it will thread
2. Beat into the stiff whites of two eggs
3. Add one cup of nuts
4. Beat until cool and thick
5. Pour out, cool, and when set, cut into squares

title: Dobos Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of six eggs
- One-half pound of powdered sugar
- Three-fourths cup of flour
- Whites of six eggs
- Butter
- One-half pound of sweet butter
- One-half pound of sweet chocolate
- One cup of strong liquid coffee
- One-half pound of granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Cream yolks of six eggs with one-half pound of powdered sugar
2. Add three-fourths cup of flour sifted three times
3. Add beaten whites of six eggs lightly and carefully into the mixture
4. Butter pie plates on under side
5. Sprinkle with flour lightly over the butter
6. Spread the mixture very thin
7. This amount makes one cake of twelve layers
8. Remove layers at once with a spatula
9. For filling: Cream one-half pound of sweet butter and put on ice immediately
10. Take one-half pound of sweet chocolate and break it into a cup of strong liquid coffee
11. Add one-half pound of granulated sugar
12. Let it boil until you can pull it almost like candy
13. Remove from fire and stir the chocolate until it is quite cold
14. When cold add the chocolate mixture to the creamed butter
15. Spread filling thin between the layers
16. Spread the icing thicker on top and sides of the cake
17. Bake and make filling a day or two before needed

title: Dominoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake batter
- currant or cranberry jelly
- ordinary white icing
- dark chocolate icing
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Make a sponge cake batter, and bake in long tins, not too large
2. The batter should not exceed the depth of one-fourth of an inch
3. Spread it evenly and bake it in a quick oven (line the tins with buttered paper)
4. As each cake is taken from the oven, turn it upside down on a clean board or paper
5. Spread with a thin layer of currant or cranberry jelly, and lay the other cake on top of it
6. With a hot, sharp knife cut into strips like dominoes
7. Push them with the knife about an inch apart
8. Ice them with ordinary white icing, putting a tablespoonful on each piece
9. Set the cakes in a warm place, where the frosting will dry
10. Make a horn of stiff white paper with just a small opening at the lower end
11. Put in one spoon of dark chocolate icing and close the horn at the top
12. By pressing out the icing from the small opening, draw a line of it across the centre of each cake, and then make dots like those on dominoes
13. Keep the horn supplied with the icing

title: Doughnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half tablespoons of melted butter
- one cup of granulated sugar
- two eggs
- one cup of milk
- one-half nutmeg grated
- sifted flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one teaspoon of salt
- Crisco
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix melted butter, sugar, eggs, and milk
2. Add grated nutmeg to sifted flour along with baking-powder and salt
3. Add sifted flour mixture to wet ingredients to make a batter as stiff as biscuit dough
4. Flour your board well and roll dough out about half an inch thick
5. Cut into pieces three inches long and one inch wide
6. Cut a slit about an inch long in the centre of each strip and pull one end through this slit
7. Fry quickly in hot Crisco
8. Sprinkle powdered sugar on top of each doughnut

title: Drawn Butter Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons flour
- one cup boiling water
- one-half teaspoon salt
- pepper
- paprika

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter and stir in two tablespoons of flour.
2. Add carefully one cup of boiling water.
3. Season with one-half teaspoon of salt and a dash of pepper and paprika.

title: Dressing for Cold Slaw
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- sugar
- pepper
- salt
- dry mustard
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of two eggs until light
2. Add one tablespoon of sugar, one teaspoon of pepper, one-half teaspoon of salt and dry mustard
3. Pour one cup of vinegar over
4. Stir well
5. Pour over the slaw
6. This dressing may be cooked over boiling water if so desired, taking care to add the vinegar gradually, and add sliced onions to the salad

title: Dressing for Lettuce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- one teaspoon salad oil or melted butter
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-half teaspoon prepared mustard
- pepper
- two tablespoons white sugar
- one-half teacup vinegar

## Directions

1. Rub the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs to a paste, adding one teaspoon of salad oil or melted butter, being careful to add only a few drops at a time. Add one-half teaspoon salt, one-half teaspoon of prepared mustard, very little pepper, two tablespoons of white sugar. Stir very hard, then pour in gradually one-half teacup of vinegar.

title: Dried Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- cold water
- soda
- salt
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Look the beans over carefully to remove all dirt and pebbles, then wash clean.
2. Soak them overnight in plenty of cold water.
3. In the morning pour off the water and put them in a stew-pan with cold water enough to cover them generously.
4. Let them come to the boiling point in this water, then drain.
5. If the beans are old and hard, for each quart put a piece of soda about the size of a large bean in the water in which they are soaked overnight, also in the first water in which they are boiled.
6. The scalded and drained beans should be put back in the stew-pan and covered generously with boiling water.
7. Add one tablespoon of salt for one quart of beans.
8. Cook slowly, with the cover partially off the stew-pan until they have reached the required degree of tenderness.

title: Dried French Plum Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of French plums
- water
- 1 glass of port wine
- 4 ounces of loaf sugar
- lemon peel
- rich puff crust

## Directions

1. Stew 1 lb. of French plums until tender, in a very little water
2. When done add 1 glass of port wine and 4 ounces of loaf sugar
3. Put them into a pie dish
4. Line it and cover it with a rich puff crust
5. A little grated lemon peel adds to the flavour
6. Bake in a hot oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Dried Lima Beans, Baked
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of dried Lima beans
- fresh water
- salt
- paprika
- two tablespoons of poultry or beef fat
- two tablespoons of flour
- one cup of bean liquid

## Directions

1. Wash one pound of dried Lima beans, let soak overnight
2. Drain, add fresh water, bring quickly to the boiling point
3. then let simmer until tender
4. Add salt and paprika
5. Heat two tablespoons of poultry or beef fat in a spider, add two tablespoons of flour
6. when brown add one cup of bean liquid, and the beans
7. Let simmer and bake in casserole one-half hour
8. Reserve the bean broth and add more if necessary

title: Dried Pea Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup picked and cleaned dried split peas
- cold water
- two quarts cold water
- smoked beef-cheek or other smoked meat
- one-half cup celery, diced
- one small onion, cut fine
- one teaspoon salt
- one-eighth teaspoon pepper
- one tablespoon fat
- one tablespoon flour
- croutons

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of picked and cleaned dried split peas in cold water over night
2. Drain, put on with two quarts cold water, a smoked beef-cheek or any other smoked meat
3. Let boil slowly but steadily four hours or more
4. Add one-half cup of celery, diced, one small onion cut fine, one teaspoon of salt, one-eighth teaspoon of pepper
5. Cook until the meat and peas are tender
6. Remove meat when tender
7. Skim fat off the top of the soup
8. Heat one tablespoon of the fat in a frying pan, add one tablespoon of flour and gradually the rest of the soup
9. Season to taste and serve with the smoked meat, adding croutons

title: Drop Biscuit
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- four teaspoons of baking-powder
- four tablespoons of butter
- two-thirds to three-quarter cup of water or milk

## Directions

1. Add to ingredients for baking-powder biscuit enough more milk or water to make a thick drop batter, about two tablespoons
2. Mix as directed for biscuit
3. Drop by spoonfuls an inch apart on a greased baking-sheet or into greased gem pans, small size
4. Bake in a hot oven ten to twelve minutes

title: Drop Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- one pound of currants
- a couple of eggs
- orange flower-water
- white wine

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with the same quantity of butter, sugar, and currants
2. Make these into a paste with a couple of eggs
3. Add a little orange flower-water and a little white wine
4. If the paste is likely to be too thin when two eggs are used, omit the white of one
5. Drop the mixture when ready on a tin plate
6. Bake

title: Drop Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of three eggs
- milk
- tablespoon of fresh butter
- one cup of sifted flour
- yolks of three eggs
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Break into a cup the whites of three eggs
2. Fill the cup with milk
3. Put it with a tablespoon of fresh butter and one cup of sifted flour in a spider
4. Stir as it boils until it leaves the spider clean
5. Set aside until cool and stir in the yolks of three eggs
6. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg and mix thoroughly
7. Drop by teaspoons in the boiling soup ten minutes before ready to be served

title: Dry Tomato Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple of onions
- oil
- about six tomatos
- cayenne pepper and salt
- thin slices of bread
- yolks of two or three eggs
- two ounces of butter
- crumbs of bread

## Directions

1. Brown a couple of onions in a little oil, about two table-spoonsful or more, according to the number of tomatos
2. when hot, add about six tomatos cut and peeled, season with cayenne pepper and salt, and let the whole simmer for a short time
3. then cut thin slices of bread, and put as much with the tomatos as will bring them to the consistency of a pudding
4. it must be well beaten up, stir in the yolks of two or three eggs, and two ounces of butter warmed
5. turn the whole into a deep dish and bake it very brown
6. crumbs of bread should be strewed over the top, and a little warmed butter poured over

title: Duck
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- liver
- gizzard
- heart
- nutmeg
- celery root
- onion
- stale bread
- fat
- egg
- onions
- celery
- fat

## Directions

1. Singe off all the small feathers
2. Cut off neck and wings, which may be used for soup
3. Wash thoroughly and rub well with salt, ginger and a little pepper, inside and out
4. Take the liver, gizzard and heart and chop to a powder in chopping bowl
5. Grate in a little nutmeg
6. Add a piece of celery root and half an onion
7. Put all this into your chopping bowl
8. Soak some stale bread, squeeze out all the water and fry in a spider of hot fat
9. Toss this soaked bread into the bowl
10. Add one egg, salt, pepper and a speck of ginger and mix all thoroughly
11. Fill the duck with this and sew it up
12. Lay in the roasting-pan with slices of onions, celery and specks of fat
13. Put some on top of fowl
14. Roast two hours, covered up tight and baste often
15. Stick a fork into the skin from time to time so that the fat will try out

title: Duck a la Mode in Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- duckling of about five pounds
- calf's foot
- eight to ten small onions
- young carrots
- bunch of parsley
- water
- salt
- bay leaf
- dry white wine
- brandy
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the foot slowly in one quart of water, one teaspoon of salt and a small bay leaf
2. Put aside when the liquor has been reduced to one-half
3. Fry the duck and when well browned wipe off the grease
4. Put in another pan
5. Add the calf's foot with its broth, one glass of dry white wine, a tablespoon of brandy, the carrots, parsley and the onions—the latter slightly browned in drippings
6. Add pepper and salt to taste and cook slowly under a covered lid for one hour
7. Cool off for about an hour
8. Take off the grease
9. Bone and skin the duckling and cut the meat into small pieces
10. Arrange nicely with the vegetables in individual earthenware dishes
11. Cover with the stock and put on the ice to harden

title: Dumplings for Cream Soups
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk or water
- butter
- salt
- yolk of an egg
- beaten white

## Directions

1. Scald some flour with milk or water
2. Mix in a small piece of butter and salt
3. Boil until thick
4. When cool beat in yolk of an egg
5. If too stiff add the beaten white

title: Dumplings For Stew
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two teaspoons baking powder
- two cups flour
- one egg
- one cup cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix two teaspoons of baking powder with two cups of flour, one egg, one cup of cold water and a little salt
2. Stir all lightly together and drop the batter from the spoon into the stew while the water continues to boil
3. Cover closely and do not uncover for twenty minutes, boiling constantly, but not too hard
4. Serve immediately in the stew

title: Dutch Toast
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of any cold poultry or meat
- seasoning
- cold dressed vegetable
- one or more eggs
- gravy
- square of toast
- lemon-juice
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Mince any cold poultry or meat and season highly
2. Add to it any cold dressed vegetable
3. Mix it up with one or more eggs
4. Let it simmer till hot in a little gravy
5. Have ready a square of toast and serve it on it
6. Squeeze over a little lemon-juice and sprinkle with white pepper

title: Eastern Depilatory
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of quick-lime
- one ounce of orpiment
- barley-water
- sweet oil or cold cream

## Directions

1. Mix with one ounce of quick-lime, one ounce of orpiment
2. put the powder in a bottle with a glass stopper
3. when required for use, mix it into a paste with barley-water
4. apply this over the part, and let it remain some minutes
5. then gently take it off with a silver knife
6. the part should then be fomented to prevent any of the powder being absorbed by the skin
7. a little sweet oil or cold cream should be wiped over the surface with a feather

title: Eau de Cologne
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of essence of bergamot
- one ounce of essence of lemon
- one ounce of lavender
- one ounce of orange flower-water
- two ounces of rosemary and honey-water
- one pint of spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Mix together one ounce of essence of bergamot, the same quantity of essence of lemon, lavender, and orange flower-water, two ounces of rosemary and honey-water, with one pint of spirits of wine
2. Let the mixture stand a fortnight
3. Put it into a glass retort, the body of which immerse in boiling water contained in a vessel placed over a lamp, while the beak of the retort is introduced into a large decanter
4. Keep the water boiling while the mixture distils into the decanter, which should be covered with cold wet cloths

title: Egg and Olive Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- two tablespoons of chopped olives
- salt
- olive oil
- vinegar
- thin slices of buttered bread

## Directions

1. Chop four eggs which have been boiled fifteen minutes.
2. Add two tablespoons of chopped olives, season and moisten with olive oil and vinegar.
3. Spread between thin slices of buttered bread.

title: Egg Appetizer
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- French dressing
- sardellen butter
- small squares of toasted bread
- caviar
- finely chopped onion
- pepper
- lemon juice
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard
2. Cut slice off the end, so that the egg will stand firm
3. Dip egg in French dressing
4. With a pastry bag arrange sardellen butter on the top of egg
5. Have ready small squares of toasted bread, spread with a thin layer of sardellen butter
6. Stand the eggs on the prepared bread
7. Caviar, mixed with some finely chopped onion, pepper and lemon juice, may be used instead of the sardellen butter, but mayonnaise must be used over the caviar

title: Egg Balls
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard yolks of eggs
- the yolk of a raw egg

## Directions

1. Beat the hard yolks of eggs in a mortar
2. Make it into a paste with the yolk of a raw egg
3. Form the paste into very small balls
4. Throw them into boiling water for a minute or so, to harden them

title: Egg Balls for Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- bread crumbs
- half an egg from meat balls

## Directions

1. Boil 2 eggs very hard
2. When done throw them into cold water for 5 minutes
3. Take the yolks only, rub them quite smooth with a spoon
4. Have about the same quantity of bread crumbs
5. Mix with a little salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
6. Mix them with the half of egg left from the meat balls
7. Make them very small, as they swell very much
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Egg Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- two tablespoons of milk
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat slightly the yolks of two eggs, add two tablespoons of milk and a few grains of salt.
2. Pour into small buttered cup, place in pan of hot water and bake until firm; cool, remove from cup and cut in fancy shapes with French vegetable cutters.

title: Egg Dumplings for Soups
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- one-half teaspoon of melted butter
- whites of two eggs
- a raw egg yolk

## Directions

1. Rub the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs to a smooth paste
2. Add a little salt and grated nutmeg and one-half teaspoon of melted butter
3. Add the chopped whites of two eggs and a raw egg yolk to be able to mold the dough into little marbles
4. Put in boiling soup one minute

title: Egg Lemonade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- juice of two lemons
- four tablespoons of sugar
- four cups of water
- ice

## Directions

1. Break two eggs and beat the whites and yolks separately.
2. Mix juice of two lemons, four tablespoons of sugar, four cups of water and ice as for lemonade.
3. Add the eggs.
4. Pour rapidly back and forth from one pitcher to another and serve before the froth disappears.

title: Egg Marmalade
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- half a pint of water
- one ounce of sweet almonds blanched and pounded
- the yolks of twenty eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Clarify one pound of sugar in half a pint of water till it becomes a thick syrup
2. While clarifying, add one ounce of sweet almonds blanched and pounded
3. Let it cool and stir in gently the yolks of twenty eggs which have been previously beaten and passed through a sieve
4. Stir it continually the same way
5. When well mixed, place it over a slow fire till it thickens, stirring all the time to prevent burning

title: Egg Marmalade
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- 12 yolks of eggs
- 36 almonds blanched and pounded

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar with 1/2 pint of water to a syrup
2. Beat up 12 yolks of eggs with 36 almonds, blanched and pounded
3. When well beat up, stir the almonds and eggs into the boiling syrup
4. Keep stirring all the time
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Egg Marmalade
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- 12 yolks of eggs
- 36 almonds, blanched and pounded
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar with 1/2 pint of water to a syrup
2. Beat up 12 yolks of eggs with 36 almonds, blanched and pounded
3. When well beat up, stir the almonds and eggs into the boiling syrup
4. Keep stirring all the time in a jug, put in a saucepan of boiling water
5. When it becomes thick, pour it into custard glasses
6. Put a little cinnamon on the top
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Egg Marmalade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- one-half pint of water
- one ounce of pounded almonds
- yolks of twenty eggs
- vanilla or wine

## Directions

1. Make a thick syrup by dissolving one pound of sugar in one-half pint of water over the fire
2. Add one ounce of pounded almonds while the syrup is clarifying
3. Take the saucepan off the fire and when the contents have become moderately cool stir in carefully the well-beaten yolks of twenty eggs
4. Stir prolongedly to blend the eggs with the syrup
5. Flavor with vanilla or wine
6. Cook over a slow fire, stirring constantly and taking great care that the mixture does not burn

title: Egg Omelet with Herbs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- marjoram
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. Beat 4 or 5 eggs with a little salt and pepper, a little grated nutmeg, some chopped parsley and marjoram, a very little of each
2. Put a little oil or butter in the pan to grease it
3. Fry it like a pancake
4. Turn it on a hot dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Egg Paste, Called In Modern Cookery Nouilles
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Form a paste of flour and beaten eggs, without either butter or water
2. Roll out extremely thin and left to dry
3. Cut into narrow strips or stamp with paste cutters

title: Egg Rarebit
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cream sauce
- one-half pound American and Swiss cheese mixed or American alone
- three hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Make a cream sauce
2. Grate one-half pound American and Swiss cheese mixed, or American alone
3. Add to the sauce
4. Chop three hard-boiled eggs and add to the sauce
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Serve on buttered toast

title: Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- a dessert spoonful of flour
- a little salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- the juice of a large lemon or two small ones
- a ladle of the liquor the fowls were boiled in
- chopped parsley or capers

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of 2 eggs in a basin with a dessert spoonful of flour well rubbed in a little salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
2. Have the juice of a large lemon, or two small ones, squeezed in a teacup, strain all the pips
3. Turn it gently, stirring all the time the egg and lemon till you have it quite smooth
4. If for boiled fowls take a ladle of the liquor the fowls were boiled in, stir it in
5. Let it boil up to thicken
6. Turn it into the sauceboat with some chopped parsley or capers
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Egg Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- the yolks of four eggs
- the juice of a fine lemon
- a tea-spoonful of flour
- a little cold water

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of four eggs with the juice of a fine lemon, a tea-spoonful of flour, and a little cold water
2. Mix well together
3. Set it on the fire to thicken, stirring it to prevent curdling

title: Egg Wine
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fresh egg
- a tumbler of white wine and water
- sugar
- spices

## Directions

1. Beat a fresh egg
2. Add it to a tumbler of white wine and water, sweetened and spiced
3. Set it on the fire and stir it gently one way until it thickens

title: Egg-Nog
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sugar
- brandy or rum
- milk or cream
- whipped cream
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Separate the whites and yolks of the eggs
2. To each yolk add one tablespoon of sugar and beat until very light
3. Beat whites to a stiff froth
4. To each glass of egg-nog add two tablespoons of brandy or rum, then one-half cup of milk or cream
5. Add the whites of the eggs
6. Pour in glass
7. put a spoon of whipped cream over and grated nutmeg on top

title: Eggless Ginger Gems
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of molasses
- one-half cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of butter
- one-half teaspoon of ginger
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-half cup of milk
- two and one-half cups of sifted flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- one-fourth cup of currants or seeded raisins

## Directions

1. Mix one-half cup of molasses, one-half cup of sugar, one tablespoon of butter, and warm slightly
2. Beat up well and stir at least ten minutes
3. Add the following spices: one-half teaspoon each of ginger and cinnamon
4. Gradually add one-half cup of milk and two and one-half cups of sifted flour in which has been sifted two teaspoons of baking powder
5. One-fourth cup of currants or seeded raisins may be added
6. Bake in well-greased gem pans and eat warm for tea or lunch

title: Eggless Gingerbread with Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one teaspoon of soda
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two teaspoons of ginger
- three-fourths cup of grated cheese
- one-half cup of hot water
- one-half cup of molasses

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour, one teaspoon of soda, one-half teaspoon of salt and two teaspoons of ginger
2. Melt three-fourths cup of grated cheese in one-half cup of hot water
3. Add one-half cup of molasses and blend perfectly
4. Add the flour and seasonings very gradually and beat thoroughly
5. Bake in muffin rings for fifteen minutes
6. Serve while warm

title: Eggless, Butterless, Milkless Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One package of seeded raisins
- Two cups of sugar
- Two cups of boiling water
- One teaspoon of cinnamon
- One teaspoon of cloves
- Two tablespoons of Crisco, chicken schmalz or clarified drippings
- One-half teaspoon of salt
- One teaspoon of soda dissolved in water
- Three cups of flour

## Directions

1. Boil all together five minutes
2. Cool, add one teaspoon of soda dissolved in water
3. Add three cups of flour
4. Bake forty-five minutes
5. Make two cakes in layer pans

title: Eggplant (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- eggplant
- water
- salt
- white pepper
- sour salt
- red tomatoes
- one-fourth cup of rice

## Directions

1. Brown onion
2. Peel eggplant raw, cut in quarters, put in when onions are brown with a little water
3. Stew
4. Add salt, white pepper, sour salt, red tomatoes
5. When half done add one-fourth cup of rice
6. Cook until rice is tender

title: Eggplant Croquettes (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- hot water
- two eggs
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons of matzoth or white flour or bread crumbs
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplant
2. Place in hot water and boil until tender
3. Drain
4. Add two eggs, salt, pepper, two tablespoons of matzoth or white flour or bread crumbs
5. Beat together
6. Fry in butter or oil by tablespoonfuls

title: Eggplant Fried in Oil (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant, sliced
- chopped meat
- egg
- chopped parsley
- salt and pepper
- olive oil
- water

## Directions

1. Arrange in oiled pan in layers: one layer of sliced eggplant, one layer of chopped meat seasoned with egg, chopped parsley, salt and pepper
2. Repeat as many layers as desired
3. Add a little olive oil
4. Cover with water
5. Bake one-half hour

title: Eggplant Salad (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- red onion
- garlic
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Broil eggplant
2. When cool, skin and lay on platter
3. Cut with wooden spoon
4. Add a red onion cut fine, or garlic cut very fine, salt and a little vinegar

title: Eggs a la Mexicana
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six dried Spanish peppers
- butter
- half an onion
- one clove of garlic
- one cup of uncooked rice
- one cup of water
- salt
- six eggs

## Directions

1. Boil six dried Spanish peppers twenty minutes
2. Drain and remove the seeds
3. Chop fine
4. Fry in butter half an onion and one clove of garlic
5. Add one cup of uncooked rice
6. Cover with one cup of water and cook till tender
7. Add a lump of butter and salt
8. When done, cover with six eggs
9. Scramble all together
10. Serve on a hot dish

title: Eggs and Anchovy
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- anchovies
- bread
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Boil some eggs very hard
2. Lay them in cold water before you peel them for 5 minutes
3. Cut each egg in 4 or 5 slices
4. Wash and bone some anchovies
5. Dry them well
6. Spread on the bread some mayonnaise
7. Put on 1 piece of egg with 1/2 an anchovy to each sandwich
8. Place the 4 pieces of egg on the bread so that when you divide the 4 sandwiches you do not cut the egg
9. Dish them up on a paper expressly for sandwiches or a napkin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs and Cheese
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 oz. of butter
- 2 oz. of good Cheddar cheese
- 4 eggs
- pepper
- salt
- 2 tomatoes
- toast

## Directions

1. Put in your small butter tin 1/2 oz. of butter
2. Cut up small 2 oz. of good Cheddar cheese
3. As soon as the butter is warm lay that in
4. Beat in a basin 4 eggs with a little pepper, not much salt, as the cheese flavours
5. Let it cook in the oven, on the hob, or before the fire on a stand
6. You can cook this for a light lunch, with 2 tomatoes cut in slices at the bottom of the tin with the butter
7. Have 2 or 3 slices of well-made toast to send up
8. Place them in the toast rack as you make them
9. This must also be sent up in the dish or tin it is made in
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs and Tomato Omelet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- ½-lb. of sausage
- 3 tomatoes
- 4 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put a little fat into the fryingpan, when it is hot skin a ½-lb. of sausage, cut it in very thin slices, lay it in the pan
2. Slice 3 tomatoes lay with it
3. Beat up 4 eggs with a little salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
4. When done, slip it on to the dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs Baked In Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- steamed rice
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Line a buttered dish with steamed rice
2. Break the eggs in the centre
3. Dot with butter
4. Sprinkle with salt, pepper
5. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Eggs En Marinade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- good meat gravy
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- two egg yolks
- half dozen poached eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Mix equal quantities of water and good meat gravy, two tablespoons each, with a teaspoon of vinegar and a seasoning of pepper and salt.
2. Put in a stew-pan and stir in gradually two well-beaten yolks of eggs.
3. When it thickens and before it boils, have ready a half dozen nicely poached eggs and pour the sauce over them.
4. Garnish with parsley.

title: Eggs in Mask
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 lb. of forced meat
- chopped onion
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- dark mushrooms
- quarter pint of water
- small meat balls
- flour
- juice of a lemon
- browning
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil 4 eggs very hard, throw them into cold water for 5 minutes, then peel them
2. Have ready 1 lb. of forced meat well seasoned, divide it in 4 equal portions
3. Roll each egg in one piece fixing them well so that you cannot see where the eggs were put in
4. Press them by putting your fingers in cold water
5. Chop an onion very fine, a tooth of garlic, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg and a few of the dark mushrooms, if you can get them, chopped fine
6. Put all this in a stewpan with quarter pint of water, let it boil up
7. Put in the large balls and a few small meat balls, let them cook gently for 1 hour
8. When done rub in a basin a spoonful of flour with the juice of a lemon, a little browning
9. Stir it into the saucepan, take out the eggs, put them on a plate
10. Cut them each in halves, place them in the dish, put round the small balls
11. Pour the gravy round the dish, be careful not to put it over the eggs
12. Garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs Piquant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- half a cup of vinegar
- fresh eggs
- cold water
- two hard-boiled eggs
- one tablespoon of butter
- sugar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Pour into the kettle water to the depth of about one inch, adding salt and half a cup of vinegar
2. Bring to boil
3. Break in as many fresh eggs one at a time as desired, carefully so as not to break the yolks
4. As soon as the whites of the eggs are boiled, take up carefully with a perforated skimmer
5. Lay in cold water
6. Remove to a large platter
7. Strain the sauce the eggs were boiled in and set away
8. Rub or grate two hard-boiled eggs, yolks only
9. Add one tablespoon of butter rubbed very hard and also some sugar and part of the strained sauce
10. Boil up once and pour over the eggs
11. Garnish with parsley

title: Eggs Poached in Tomato Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one and one-half cups of canned tomatoes
- a pinch of soda
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- eggs
- squares of toast

## Directions

1. Make a sauce of butter, flour, canned tomatoes rubbed through a strainer, soda, salt, pepper and sugar to taste
2. Cook sufficiently
3. Drop in the required number of eggs
4. Cook until the white is firm, basting the eggs often with the sauce
5. When done, lift the eggs carefully to squares of toast
6. Pour the sauce around them

title: Eggs Spanish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- fresh or canned tomatoes
- minced green onions
- garlic
- parsley
- laurel leaf
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne pepper
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard
2. After cooling, remove shells and halve lengthwise
3. Cook for thirty minutes fresh or canned tomatoes with minced green onions, garlic, parsley, a laurel leaf, salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper to taste
4. Strain
5. Melt a slice of butter
6. Add a little flour
7. Add sauce gradually
8. Cook ten minutes
9. Place eggs carefully in sauce and serve

title: Eggs Stirred with Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- an ounce of butter

## Directions

1. Break as many eggs as you require in a small basin
2. Beat them well with salt and pepper to taste
3. Have in a small saucepan an ounce of butter
4. Warm it well
5. Turn in the eggs
6. Keep stirring them with a fork
7. Dish up on a hot dish
8. Have hot plates ready
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs with Butter Whole
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- salt
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Have a small white dish or plate that will stand the fire
2. Put it on the hob quite close to the fire
3. Put a good piece of butter (1 an ounce will be enough for 6 eggs)
4. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper
5. Crack the eggs in a cup
6. Slip each one separately on the dish that is ready to cook them
7. They will set nicely
8. Put over them a little salt and pepper so that they will be seasoned right through
9. Send them up to table on the same dish
10. Another dish under
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Eggs With Cream Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- three tablespoons of flour
- one and one-half cups of milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- six hard-boiled eggs
- slices of toast

## Directions

1. Blend two tablespoons of butter with three tablespoons of flour
2. Place on range and stir until the butter is melted
3. Add one and one-half cups of milk, stirring all the time until the mixture is thick
4. Season with one teaspoon of salt and a few grains of pepper
5. Separate the whites of six hard-boiled eggs from the yolks
6. Chop the whites fine and add to the dressing
7. Arrange slices of toast on a hot platter
8. Pour the dressing over them
9. Force the yolks through a ricer onto the toast and dressing
10. Serve hot

title: Egoless, Butterless, Milkless Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one package of seeded raisins
- two cups of sugar
- two cups of boiling water
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of cloves
- two tablespoons of Crisco, chicken schmalz or clarified drippings
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of soda
- three cups of flour

## Directions

1. Boil one package of seeded raisins, two cups of sugar, two cups of boiling water, one teaspoon of cinnamon, one teaspoon of cloves, two tablespoons of Crisco, chicken schmalz or clarified drippings, and one-half teaspoon of salt together five minutes
2. cool
3. add one teaspoon of soda dissolved in water
4. add three cups of flour
5. Bake forty-five minutes, make two cakes in layer pans

title: Einlauf (Egg Drop)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one-eighth teaspoon of salt
- three tablespoons of flour
- one-fourth cup of water
- one teaspoon of chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Beat one egg
2. Add one-eighth teaspoon of salt, three tablespoons of flour and one-fourth cup of water
3. Stir until smooth
4. Pour slowly from a considerable height from the end of a spoon into the boiling soup
5. Cook two or three minutes and serve hot
6. Add one teaspoon of chopped parsley to the soup

title: Enchiladas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cornmeal
- wheat flour
- water
- one cup of sausage
- one-half grated onion
- one tablespoon of Worcestershire sauce
- two tablespoons of drippings
- two tablespoons of flour
- one cup of soup stock
- one cup of strained tomatoes
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- one tablespoon of Spanish pepper sauce

## Directions

1. Make a dough of cornmeal and wheat flour and water
2. Roll it out in thin, round cakes
3. Cook quickly in a pan that has not been greased
4. Roll in a cloth to keep soft and warm
5. Grind one cup of sausage, add one-half grated onion, one tablespoon of Worcestershire sauce and fill the warm cakes with this mixture
6. Roll them when filled
7. Pour over them a sauce made of two tablespoons of drippings into which two tablespoons of flour have been smoothed
8. Add one cup of soup stock, one cup of strained tomatoes, two tablespoons of vinegar, one tablespoon of Spanish pepper sauce

title: English Bola
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 teacup milk
- 1/2 oz. German yeast
- 6 oz. sugar
- 1 lb. sweetmeat
- salt

## Directions

1. Work 1 lb. flour, 1/2 lb. butter, 5 eggs, 1 teacup of milk, and 1/2 oz. of German yeast into a paste.
2. Let it stand for 1 hour.
3. Work in 6 oz. of sugar and 1 lb. of sweetmeat.
4. Grease your tin.
5. Bake it in a slow oven.
6. Remember in all sweets to put a little salt.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: English Egg Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- melted butter
- white pepper
- salt
- blade of mace
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil two eggs hard
2. Chop them finely
3. Warm them up in finely made melted butter
4. Add a little white pepper, salt, a blade of mace, and a very small quantity of nutmeg

title: English Lemon Stewed Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nape or head and shoulders of halibut, shad, or any good firm fish
- one pint of water
- three or four good sized onions
- oil
- cayenne pepper
- one teaspoon of ground ginger
- two teaspoons of salt
- two eggs
- six good sized lemons
- one tablespoon of potato flour
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Have washed and scraped clean the nape or head and shoulders of halibut, a shad, or any good firm fish; cut it up small and lay it in a stew-pan with one pint of water and three or four good sized onions, fried in oil a light brown
2. Put them on top of the fish with a pinch of cayenne pepper, and a teaspoon of ground ginger, with two teaspoons of salt
3. Let it stew gently until it is done; if there should be too much gravy on it before adding the sauce, take some off
4. Prepare two eggs and six good sized lemons, squeezed and strained; then take some of the gravy from the fish while it is boiling, add it to the lemon, with the two eggs well beaten, and a tablespoon of potato flour
5. Mix smoothly with some chopped parsley; when all is well mixed, add it to the fish
6. Shake it gently for five minutes while it is boiling, taking care not to let it burn
7. When it is sufficiently cooked let it stand for an hour and serve it
8. Garnish with slices of lemon and parsley
9. To be eaten cold

title: English Macaroons, Sweet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of sweet almonds
- 1.5 lb. of sifted sugar
- whites of 6 eggs
- grated rind of 2 lemons
- blanched almonds
- almond oil
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Pound the almonds very fine (they must be pounded, as the ground almonds are seldom fine enough)
2. Beat the whites of eggs to a snow
3. Add the sugar and grated lemon
4. Mix it all well together, it will be like a paste
5. Shape them as you please
6. Have some almonds blanched, cut them in quarters, and put a few on each cake
7. Grease a very thin paper with almond oil, and sift some fine sugar on it
8. Put it on the tin, and place the cakes at a distance from each other
9. Bake them in a moderate oven
10. When done, let them cool before you touch them
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: English Muligatawny
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- six onions
- a table spoonful of curry powder
- a desert spoonful of cayenne pepper
- salt
- a little Harvey's sauce
- lemon pickle

## Directions

1. Stew a knuckle of veal till half done
2. Cut off the greatest part of the meat
3. Continue to stew down the bone in the stock
4. Cut the meat into small pieces and fry with six onions thinly sliced and a table spoonful of curry powder
5. Add a desert spoonful of cayenne pepper and salt
6. Add the stock and let the whole gently simmer for nearly an hour
7. Flavour it with a little Harvey's sauce and lemon pickle

title: Erbsen Lievanzen (Dried Pea Fritters)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of dried peas
- two ounces of butter
- soaked bread
- milk
- salt
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one yolk and one whole egg
- one ounce of blanched and pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of dried peas
2. Pass through a hair sieve
3. Pour into a bowl
4. Add two ounces of butter rubbed to a cream
5. Add soaked bread (soaked in milk)
6. Stir all into a smooth paste
7. Add salt, one teaspoon of sugar, one yolk and one whole egg, and one ounce of blanched and pounded almonds
8. If too thick add more egg, if too thin add more bread
9. Fry a nice brown

title: Escobeche
