author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some cold fried fish
- half a pint of vinegar
- two table spoonsful of water
- one table spoonful of oil
- a little grated ginger
- allspice
- cayenne pepper
- two bay leaves
- a little salt
- a table spoonful of lemon juice
- sliced onions

## Directions

1. Take some cold fried fish, place it in a deep pan
2. Boil half a pint of vinegar with two table spoonsful of water, and one of oil, a little grated ginger, allspice, cayenne pepper, two bay leaves, a little salt, and a table spoonful of lemon juice, with sliced onions
3. When boiling, pour it over the fish
4. Cover the pan, and let it stand twenty-four hours before serving

title: Essence of Beef Tea
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of beef
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Cut up 1 lb. of beef into a mince, put it into a small jug with only a pinch of salt
2. Stand the mug in a saucepan with boiling water, let it boil at the side of the fire for several hours
3. Tie down the top of the mug for no steam to escape
4. From the 1 lb. of meat you will have half a teacup of juice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Essence of Lavender
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- lavender
- spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the recipe for Essence of Roses, the lavender being substituted for the roses

title: Essence of Musk
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dram of musk
- one dram of pounded loaf sugar
- six ounces of spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Mix one dram of musk with the same quantity of pounded loaf sugar
2. add six ounces of spirits of wine
3. shake together and pour off for use

title: Essence of Roses
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- petals of the common rose
- spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Put into a bottle the petals of the common rose
2. pour upon them spirits of wine
3. cork the bottle closely
4. let it stand for three months

title: Eve's Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 oz. of chopped suet
- 8 oz. of bread crumbs
- 8 oz. of currants
- 6 oz. of apple
- 8 eggs
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well, the yolks and whites separately
2. Mix all ingredients well together
3. Beat for 20 minutes
4. Grease a pretty shape well and sift over some sugar
5. Boil for 2 hours
6. Serve with a rich rum sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Family Barley Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lb. of boned brisket
- a quart of water
- a little salt, pepper
- an onion
- a carrot
- a turnip
- a few pieces of celery
- a little of the green leaves
- half pound of barley
- a little chopped parsley
- a little grated nutmeg
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Put 3-lb. of boned brisket on with a quart of water
2. Add a little salt, pepper
3. Cut an onion small, a carrot, turnip, a few pieces of celery, with a little of the green leaves chopped
4. Wash them well
5. Also add half pound of barley
6. Let it boil quick
7. Skim it well, then only let it simmer
8. You can boil in the same soup a couple of carrots whole to garnish the beef for dinner
9. Have in the tureen a little chopped parsley and a little grated nutmeg
10. Colour the soup with dried saffron
11. It will take 3 hours to be well done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Family Julienne Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lb. boned brisket
- 3 pints of water
- salt, pepper
- a tooth of garlic
- a blade of mace
- a few vegetables in season: carrot, turnip, cabbage, peas, beans, celery, potatoes, leeks
- a bunch of marjoram, thyme, parsley
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Take 4-lb. boned brisket
2. Put it in a soup saucepan with 3 pints of water, with salt, pepper, a tooth of garlic, a blade of mace
3. Cut up a few of the vegetables in season: carrot, turnip, cabbage, peas, beans, celery, potatoes, leeks
4. Boil in a bunch of marjoram, thyme, parsley
5. Let it all boil quick
6. Skim it well, then let it boil slowly for 3 hours
7. Do not cut your vegetables too small
8. Make a few suet balls according to recipe and let them boil only 1 hour
9. Just before serving up for dinner rub a tablespoonful of flour with a little browning
10. Stir into the boiling soup
11. Turn it into the tureen
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fancy Creams
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of cream
- four ounces of powdered white sugar
- rind of a lemon
- glass of sherry wine
- half an ounce of dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Put into a basin a pint of cream
2. Add four ounces of powdered white sugar
3. Add the rind of a lemon rubbed on a lump of sugar
4. Add a glass of sherry wine
5. Whisk them well
6. Mix with it half an ounce of dissolved isinglass
7. Beat it all thoroughly together
8. Fill the mould, which should be set in ice till wanted

title: Farina
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of farina
- one teaspoon of salt
- three cups of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour farina gradually into three cups of boiling water and cook the mixture in a double boiler for about one hour

title: Farina Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- three tablespoons of goose, turkey or chicken fat or clear beef drippings
- farina
- whites of eggs
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of four eggs with three tablespoons of goose, turkey or chicken fat, but if these are not convenient, clear beef drippings will do
2. Put in enough farina to make a good batter
3. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth with pinch of salt, and stir in batter
4. Put on in large boiler sufficient water to boil dumplings and add one tablespoon of salt
5. When boiling drop in by tablespoons
6. Boil one hour

title: Farina Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one kitchen spoon of fresh butter
- one cup of milk
- farina
- yolks of two eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- one-half cup of grated almonds

## Directions

1. Put in a double boiler one kitchen spoon of fresh butter, stir in one cup of milk.
2. When it begins to boil stir in enough farina to thicken.
3. Take off the stove and when cold add the yolks of two eggs and the stiffly-beaten whites, and a little salt and nutmeg and one-half cup of grated almonds if desired.
4. Let cool, then make into little balls, and ten minutes before soup is to be served, drop in boiler and let boil up once or twice.

title: Farina Pudding with Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- one-half cup of farina
- salt
- a small piece of butter
- yolks of four eggs
- four tablespoons of white sugar
- vanilla
- whites of the eggs
- pulverized sugar
- stewed or canned peaches
- rum sauce

## Directions

1. Add one-half cup of farina, salt, and a small piece of butter to one quart of milk
2. Boil in a double boiler until thick
3. Beat the yolks of four eggs with four tablespoons of white sugar, and add this just before taking off the fire
4. Stir it thoroughly, but do not let it boil any more
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth with pulverized sugar
7. Butter a pudding dish, put in part of the custard, in which you have mixed the whites (if you have any extra whites of eggs beat and use them also)
8. Add a layer of stewed or canned peaches
9. Cover with the remaining custard and bake
10. Eat with rum sauce

title: Farina Pudding, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half pints of milk
- nine level tablespoons of sugar
- five bitter almonds chopped fine
- five sweet almonds chopped fine
- twelve level tablespoons of farina
- vanilla
- beaten whites of five eggs
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Bring one and one-half pints of milk with nine level tablespoons of sugar, five bitter and five sweet almonds chopped fine to boiling point
2. Drop in slowly twelve level tablespoons of farina and stir constantly
3. Cook for twelve minutes
4. Add vanilla to taste
5. Add slowly the beaten whites of five eggs
6. Put it in a form and when cold serve with a fruit sauce

title: Farina Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- soup stock
- one-quarter cup of farina
- the yolk of one egg
- a teaspoon of cold water

## Directions

1. When the soup stock has been strained and every particle of fat removed, return it to the kettle to boil
2. When it boils hard stir in carefully quarter of a cup of farina, do this slowly to prevent the farina from forming lumps
3. Stir into the soup bowl the yolk of one egg, add a teaspoon of cold water
4. Pour the soup into the bowl gradually and stir constantly until all has been poured into the bowl
5. Serve at once

title: Farsole Dulce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dried Lima beans
- cold water
- one tablespoon of fat
- peel of lemon or orange
- one tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak dried Lima beans in cold water overnight
2. Drain, put on with very little water
3. add one tablespoon of fat, peel of lemon or orange
4. When beans are half done, add a tablespoon of sugar which has been browned in a pan
5. stew slowly until the beans are tender

title: Farspjle
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, very hard Lima beans
- two large onions
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-quarter cup of rice

## Directions

1. Soak the large, very hard Lima beans overnight
2. To a pound of beans take two large onions
3. When the beans are soft add the onions browned in fat, salt, pepper, a tablespoon of sugar, a quarter cup of rice
4. let all simmer until the rice is done

title: Fig Dessert
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups white figs
- two cups of sugar
- sponge cake or lady fingers
- whipped cream
- candied cherries or angelica

## Directions

1. Soak two cups white figs overnight.
2. In the morning boil slowly until tender, add two cups of sugar and boil until a thick syrup is formed.
3. Line a dish with sponge cake or lady fingers.
4. Pour the figs in the centre and cover with whipped cream that has been sweetened and flavored.
5. Decorate with candied cherries or angelica.

title: Fig Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of figs
- one cup of water
- one-half cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of figs fine
2. Cook figs, water, and sugar all together until soft and smooth

title: Fig Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- water
- lemon juice
- thin slices of buttered bread

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and chop the figs fine.
2. Put in a double boiler with a little water and cook until a paste is formed.
3. Add a few drops of lemon juice and set aside.
4. When cool spread on thin slices of buttered bread.

title: Fig Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- loaf sugar
- port or other wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Stew figs slowly for two hours, until soft
2. Sweeten with loaf sugar, about two tablespoons to a pound of fruit
3. Add a glass of port or other wine and a little lemon juice
4. Serve when cold

title: Filled Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large, solid head of cabbage
- one quarter of a loaf of stale bread
- two tablespoons of drippings
- one large-sized onion
- one pound of chopped beef
- two eggs
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- sage

## Directions

1. Take a large, solid head of cabbage; take off the large top leaves, and scoop out the centre of the cabbage so as to leave the outside leaves intact for refilling.
2. Chop your cabbage fine as for slaw.
3. Take a quarter of a loaf of stale bread, soak it in water and squeeze very dry.
4. Heat two tablespoons of drippings in a spider, add a large-sized onion chopped fine, do not let the onion get too brown.
5. Add the bread, one pound of chopped beef well minced and the chopped cabbage and let it get well heated.
6. Take off stove and add two eggs, pepper, salt, nutmeg, a little parsley and a little sage, season very highly.
7. Put this all back in the cabbage, and cover this with the large leaves.
8. Put into small bread-pan and bake for two hours, put just enough water in to keep the pan from burning; don't baste.

title: Filled Lemons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- good-sized lemons
- one box of boneless sardines
- six anchovies
- two green peppers
- lemon-juice
- pimento
- horseradish
- watercress

## Directions

1. Select good-sized lemons
2. cut off tip to stand the lemon upright
3. cut top for cover
4. Scoop out all the lemon pulp, and put in a bowl
5. put shells in a bowl of cold water
6. For six lemons take one box of boneless sardines, six anchovies, and two green peppers, cut very fine
7. Wet with lemon-juice until moist
8. fill in shells after wiping dry
9. insert a pimento on top
10. put on cover of lemon
11. serve on doily with horseradish and watercress

title: Filled Matzoth Kleis
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- matzoth dough
- cooked prunes
- goose fat
- onion

## Directions

1. Prepare a matzoth dough as for the soup kleis.
2. Make round flat cakes of it with your hands, and fill with cooked prunes (having previously removed the kernels).
3. Put one of the flat cakes over one that is filled, press the edges firmly together and roll until perfectly round.
4. Boil them in salt water — the water must boil hard before you put them in.
5. Heat some goose fat, cut up an onion in it and brown; pour this over the kleis and serve hot.

title: Filled Tongue
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled tongue
- corned beef
- one egg
- onion
- soaked bread

## Directions

1. Take a pickled tongue and cut it open.
2. Chop or grind some corned beef.
3. Add one egg.
4. Brown a little onion, and add some soaked bread.
5. Fill tongue with it, and sew it up and boil until done.

title: Fillet de Sole a la Creole
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large flounders
- butter
- brown flour
- onion
- one-half can of tomatoes
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- green peppers
- parsley
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Fillet some large flounders, and have fishman send you all the bones.
2. Put the bones on to boil.
3. Wash, dry, and season the fillets; roll them (putting in some bits of butter), and fasten each one with a wooden toothpick.
4. Strain the water from the bones; thicken with a little brown flour and onion.
5. Add to this one-half can of tomatoes, a little cayenne pepper, salt, and chopped green peppers.
6. Let this sauce simmer for a couple of hours (this need not be strained).
7. Put the fillets in a casserole, and pour some of this sauce over them, and put in the oven for about fifteen minutes.
8. Pour over the rest of the tomato sauce, sprinkle a little chopped parsley and serve.
9. One can add a few mushrooms to the sauce. The mushrooms must be fried in butter before being added to the sauce.

title: Fillet of Sole a la Mouquin
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of spinach
- thin fillets of flounder, halibut, or whitefish
- water
- one slice of lemon
- one slice of onion
- one sprig of parsley
- one bit of bay leaf
- butter
- cream sauce
- one tablespoon of grated cheese

## Directions

1. Thoroughly wash and pick over a pound of spinach, put it over the fire with no more water than clings to the leaves and cook for ten minutes.
2. Drain the spinach and chop it fine.
3. Have ready thin fillets of flounder, halibut, or whitefish.
4. Cover them with acidulated warm water — a slice of lemon in the water is all that is wanted, and add a slice of onion, a sprig of parsley and a bit of bay leaf.
5. Simmer for ten minutes and drain.
6. Put the minced spinach into the bottom of the buttered baking-dish, arrange the fillets on it, cover with a cream sauce to which a tablespoon of grated cheese has been added, and brown in the oven.

title: Fillet of Soles Fried
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice pair of soles
- flour
- eggs
- oil or butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Fillet a nice pair of soles into 8 fillets
2. Roll each fillet up and fasten with a fine piece of cotton
3. Dip them in flour, and then well in the eggs
4. Fry in boiling oil or butter
5. Dish on a fish napkin with little tufts of parsley
6. A few fried potatoes in the dish is an improvement
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fillets of Fish
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon, soles, or other fish fillets
- fish forcemeat
- good stock
- cayenne pepper
- lemon juice
- mushroom essence
- piccalilli

## Directions

1. Fry fillets of salmon, soles, &c., of a delicate brown according to the receipt already given
2. Serve with a fine gravy
3. If required to be very savory, make a fish forcemeat, and lay it thickly on the fish before frying
4. Fillets dressed in this way are usually arranged round the dish
5. Serve with a sauce made of good stock, thickened and seasoned with cayenne pepper, lemon juice, and mushroom essence
6. Piccalilli are sometimes added cut small

title: Fillets of Pike
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- potatoes
- butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Slit the pike open and cut it in pieces as near the shape of cutlets as possible
2. Season them with salt and pepper
3. Well beat in a plate 2 eggs
4. Have on another plate some crumbs of bread with chopped parsley
5. Dip the pieces in the eggs, then in the crumbs, then again in the eggs, and then in the crumbs
6. Fry them nicely
7. Dish them up in a dish that you have put in some potatoes mashed with butter, cutlet fashion
8. Garnish with slices of lemon and parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fillets of Turbot with Cream Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. butter
- tablespoonful of flour
- salt
- cayenne
- water from boiled turbot
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. When the turbot comes from table take off the fillets and skin them at once while hot
2. The next day cut your fillets as even as possible
3. Put them in a deep dish at the side of the fire with some salt and pepper, a little water, to keep them warm
4. Put into a small saucepan 1/4 lb. of butter, a tablespoonful of flour, salt, a little cayenne
5. Do not let this sauce boil, only melt it till it is thick, and season high
6. Have some mashed potatoes in a dish for table
7. Take the fillets out carefully and lay them on the potatoes
8. Pour the sauce over and send it up
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Filling for Chrimsel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- unblanched, pounded almonds
- grated apples
- chopped raisins
- brown sugar
- plenty of cinnamon
- grated rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Mix the ingredients together and fill the hollowed out center of the chrimsel with them.
2. Then place one chrimsel upon another, being careful not to let the filling escape from its hollow and fasten the edges securely together with the fingers, keeping the rounded shape uninjured.
3. Fry them in boiling fat, turning them from one side to the other until a dark brown.
4. Serve hot with sugar syrup.

title: Filling for Chrimsel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- unblanched, pounded almonds
- grated apples
- chopped raisins
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- grated rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Mix unblanched pounded almonds, grated apples, chopped raisins, brown sugar, plenty of cinnamon and the grated rind of a lemon together
2. Fill the hollowed out center of the chrimsel with them
3. Place one chrimsel upon another, being careful not to let the filling escape from its hollow
4. Fasten the edges securely together with the fingers, keeping the rounded shape uninjured
5. Fry them in boiling fat

title: Filtered Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup finely ground coffee
- 6 cups boiling water

## Directions

1. Place one cup of finely ground coffee in the strainer of the percolator
2. Place the strainer in the pot and place over the heat
3. Add gradually six cups of boiling water and allow it to filter
4. Serve at once

title: Finnan Haddie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- finnan haddie
- milk
- water
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Parboil ten minutes and then broil like fresh fish.
2. To bake, place the fish in a pan, add one cup of milk and one cup of water and cover.
3. Cook ten minutes in hot oven.
4. Remove cover, drain, spread with butter and season with pepper.

title: Finnan Haddie and Macaroni
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- finnan haddie
- drawn butter sauce
- cornstarch or milk or cream dressing
- bread or cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Break up and cook until tender about a package of macaroni.
2. Pick up the finnan haddie until you have about three-quarters as much as you have macaroni.
3. Mix in a greased baking-dish and pour over a drawn butter sauce, made with cornstarch or with any good milk or cream dressing.
4. Cover with bread or cracker crumbs or leave plain to brown in oven.
5. Bake from twenty to thirty minutes.

title: Fish Chowder
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half pounds of codfish or haddock
- six large tomatoes
- six large potatoes
- two large onions
- salt
- pepper
- three pints of water
- one-half pint of cream
- one-fourth cup of butter
- paprika

## Directions

1. Skin and bone one and one-half pounds of codfish or haddock
2. Cut six large tomatoes, six large potatoes, two large onions in small pieces
3. add salt, pepper, three pints of water and cook one hour
4. Add one-half pint of cream, one-fourth cup of butter, and paprika
5. Cook five minutes and serve

title: Fish Forcemeat
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- any kind of fish
- a couple of anchovies or a little anchovy essence
- the yolk of a hard boiled egg
- a little butter
- parsley or any other herb
- grated lemon peel
- a little lemon juice
- a little bread previously soaked

## Directions

1. Chop finely any kind of fish
2. Pound it in a mortar with a couple of anchovies, or a little anchovy essence, the yolk of a hard boiled egg, a little butter, parsley or any other herb which may be approved, grated lemon peel, and a little of the juice
3. Add a little bread previously soaked, and mix the whole into a paste
4. Form into balls, or use for stuffing

title: Fish Fried in Oil
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Soles, plaice, or salmon
- beaten eggs
- flour
- the best frying oil

## Directions

1. When prepared by salting or drying, have a dish ready with beaten eggs
2. Turn the fish well over in them, and sprinkle it freely with flour, so that the fish may be covered entirely with it
3. Place it in a pan with a good quantity of the best frying oil at boiling heat
4. Fry the fish in it gently, till of a fine equal brown colour
5. When done, place it on a cloth before the fire for the oil to drain off
6. Fish prepared in this way is usually served cold

title: Fish Fritada
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- chopped parsley
- marjoram
- 3 or 4 eggs
- 1 onion
- 2 or 3 tomatoes
- 2 spoonfuls oil
- 1 spoonful vinegar

## Directions

1. Take any cold fish you may have left, mince the same as for the patties.
2. Put some chopped parsley and marjoram, beat with 3 or 4 eggs.
3. Slice in a pie dish an onion, and, if you have them, 2 or 3 tomatoes with 2 spoonfuls of oil.
4. When it is fried, put in a spoonful of vinegar, stir it together.
5. Then add the fish and let it bake a nice brown.
6. Slip it out of the dish on to a hot dish.
7. Garnish it with parsley.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fish Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- thin paste

## Directions

1. Make a force-meat of any cold fish
2. Form it into thin cakes and fry of a light brown
3. Or enclose them first in thin paste and then fry them
4. The roes of fish or the livers are particularly nice prepared in this way

title: Fish Omelet
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- beaten eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Shred finely any cold fish and season it
2. Mix with beaten eggs
3. Make it into a paste and fry in thin cakes like pancakes
4. Serve hot on a napkin
5. There should be plenty of boiling butter in the pan, as they should be moist and rich
6. There should be more eggs in the preparation for omelets than for fritters

title: Fish Patties
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. cod
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- capers
- 1 egg
- 1/2 lemon
- puff paste
- 1 egg yolk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of cod, or if you have any cold left, mince it with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg.
2. Chop a few capers, mix it all up with an egg and juice of 1/2 a lemon.
3. Make a good puff paste, grease the little patty pans, line them with the pastry.
4. Put in each a little of the minced fish, cover them with pastry and turn the edges neatly.
5. Brush them over with the yolk of an egg.
6. Put them in a quick oven.
7. When done, take them out of the tins, dish them on a fish napkin, garnished with parsley.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fish Piquant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt water
- vinegar
- onions
- celery root
- parsley
- eggs
- Colman's mustard
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- water
- fish gravy
- green onions
- capers
- shallots
- vinegar pickle
- astragon

## Directions

1. Cook any large fish in salt water, add one cup of vinegar, onions, celery root and parsley
2. When the fish is cooked enough, remove it from the fire, kettle and all, letting the fish remain in its sauce
3. Take the yolks of two eggs, one-half teaspoon of Colman's mustard (dry), salt, pepper, a tablespoon of butter, a tablespoon of vinegar, one-half glass water and some fish gravy
4. Boil in double boiler until thick
5. Take some parsley, green onions, capers, shallots and one large vinegar pickle and some astragon, chop all up very fine
6. Chop up the hard-boiled whites separately and then add the sauce
7. Mix all this together thoroughly, then taste to see if seasoned to suit

title: Fish Salad
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold dressed fish (turbot or salmon)
- small salad (1/2 pint)
- lettuce
- onions (2 boiled)
- truffles (thinly sliced)
- hard eggs
- cucumber
- hard boiled eggs (3 yolks)
- mustard (1 spoonful)
- salt
- cream (1 cup)
- vinegar (4 table-spoonsful)

## Directions

1. Cut in small pieces any cold dressed fish, turbot or salmon are the best suited
2. Mix it with half a pint of small salad, and a lettuce cut small
3. Add two onions boiled till tender and mild, and a few truffles thinly sliced
4. Pour over a fine salad mixture, and arrange it into a shape, high in the centre
5. Garnish with hard eggs cut in slices
6. A little cucumber mixed with the salad is an improvement
7. For the salad mixture, take the yolks of three hard boiled eggs, with a spoonful of mustard, and a little salt
8. Mix these with a cup of cream, and four table-spoonsful of vinegar
9. Add the different ingredients carefully and work them together smoothly
10. The whites of the eggs may be trimmed and placed in small heaps round the dish as a garnish

title: Fish Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound cold boiled fish
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- pinch of salt and pepper
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Take one pound cold boiled fish left over from the day previous, or boil fresh fish and let cool, then skin, bone and flake.
2. If fresh fish is used, mix two tablespoons of vinegar, a pinch of salt and pepper with the fish.
3. Make a mayonnaise dressing (French mayonnaise preferred), and mix half with the fish, leaving other half to spread over top of salad, after it is put in bowl.
4. Serve either with or without lettuce leaves.

title: Fish Salad for Twenty People
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of halibut
- six hard-boiled eggs
- one pint bottle of pickles or chow-chow
- celery
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Boil four pounds of halibut, cool and shred fish.
2. Marinate the fish as directed.
3. When ready to serve add six hard-boiled eggs chopped, and one pint bottle of pickles or chow-chow.
4. The pickle may be omitted and celery cut fine be added.
5. When these are well mixed serve on lettuce leaves with mayonnaise dressing, of which one pint will be required.

title: Fish Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- tartar sauce
- lettuce leaf
- hard-boiled egg
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Spread one piece of bread with any kind of cold fish that has been shredded and mixed with tartar sauce.
2. Put a lettuce leaf on that and then a slice of hard-boiled egg that has been dipped in tartar sauce.
3. Cover with a slice of buttered bread.

title: Fish Stewed Brown
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (soles, slices of salmon, halibut, or plaice)
- oil
- onion
- water (1 cup)
- mushroom ketchup or powder
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- browned flour
- port wine (1 glass)
- truffles or mushrooms

## Directions

1. Fry some fish of a light brown, either soles, slices of salmon, halibut, or plaice
2. Let an onion brown in a little oil
3. Add to it a cup of water, a little mushroom ketchup or powder, cayenne pepper, salt, nutmeg, and lemon juice
4. Put the fish into a stew-pan with the above mixture, and simmer gently till done
5. Take out the fish and thicken the gravy with a little browned flour
6. Stir in a glass of port wine
7. A few truffles, or mushrooms, are an improvement

title: Fish Stewed White
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- an onion, finely chopped
- a little oil
- half a pint of water
- fish
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- ground allspice
- nutmeg
- ginger
- the beaten yolks of four eggs
- the juice of two lemons
- a tea spoonful of flour
- a table spoonful of cold water
- a little saffron

## Directions

1. Put an onion, finely chopped, into a stew-pan, with a little oil, till the onion becomes brown
2. Add half a pint of water, and place the fish in the stew-pan
3. Season with pepper, salt, mace, ground allspice, nutmeg, and ginger
4. Let it stew gently till the fish is done
5. Prepare the beaten yolks of four eggs, with the juice of two lemons, and a tea spoonful of flour, a table spoonful of cold water, and a little saffron, mix

title: Fish Stewed White
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion, finely chopped
- oil
- water
- fish
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- ground allspice
- nutmeg
- ginger
- eggs (4 yolks)
- lemon juice (2)
- flour (1 tea spoonful)
- cold water (1 table spoonful)
- saffron
- fish liver and roe
- chopped parsley
- warmed butter
- crumbs of bread

## Directions

1. Put an onion, finely chopped, into a stew-pan, with a little oil, till the onion becomes brown
2. Add half a pint of water, and place the fish in the stew-pan, seasoning with pepper, salt, mace, ground allspice, nutmeg, and ginger
3. Let it stew gently till the fish is done
4. Prepare the beaten yolks of four eggs, with the juice of two lemons, and a tea spoonful of flour, a table spoonful of cold water, and a little saffron, mix well in a cup
5. Pour it into the stew-pan, stirring it carefully one way until it thickens
6. About twenty minutes before serving, throw in balls made from a little of the fish, the liver, and roe if there is any, beat it up finely with chopped parsley, and spread warmed butter, crumbs of bread, and seasoning according to taste
7. Form this into a paste with eggs, and make it into balls of a moderate size
8. Garnish with sliced lemon and parsley

title: Fish Stock
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter or butter substitute
- one tablespoon of chopped onion
- one tablespoon of chopped carrot
- one tablespoon of chopped turnip
- fish-bones, head, and trimmings
- one stalk of celery
- sprigs of parsley
- sprigs of thyme
- one bay-leaf
- one tomato or one slice of lemon
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a tablespoon of butter or butter substitute
2. Add a tablespoon each of chopped onion, carrot and turnip
3. Fry them without browning, then add fish-bones, head, and trimmings, a stalk of celery, sprigs of parsley and of thyme, a bay-leaf, a tomato or a slice of lemon
4. Cover with water and let them simmer for an hour or more
5. Season with salt and pepper and strain

title: Fish With Garlic
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salt fish
- garlic

## Directions

1. Clean, salt fish one half hour, wash and dry with a clean cloth.
2. Cut garlic very thin, rub over fish.
3. Place in oven to bake.
4. Bake until odor of garlic has disappeared, then let fish cool.

title: Fish with Horseradish Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of fresh salmon
- salt
- one teaspoon of salt
- one quart of water
- one-fourth cup of melted butter
- one tablespoon of parsley
- one-half pound of horseradish root
- one pint of cream

## Directions

1. Clean three pounds of fresh salmon, bone, salt and let stand several hours.
2. Place in fish kettle with boiling salt water (one teaspoon of salt to one quart of water), and let boil one-half hour or until well cooked.
3. Lift out carefully, place on hot platter and pour over one-fourth cup of melted butter and sprinkle well with one tablespoon of parsley.
4. Peel one-half pound of horseradish root, grate and mix well with one pint of cream beaten stiff.
5. Serve fish with sauce in a separate bowl, a large spoonful with each portion of fish. The fish must be hot and the sauce cold.

title: Fish with Sauerkraut
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter or vegetable oil
- sauerkraut
- fish
- salt water
- two minced onions
- two egg yolks
- pepper
- one tablespoon of breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Fry an onion in butter (or vegetable oil), add sauerkraut and cook.
2. Boil the fish in salt water, then bone and shred.
3. Fry two minced onions in butter or oil, put them into the kettle with the fish, add two egg yolks, butter or oil, a little pepper and a tablespoon of breadcrumbs.
4. Steam for half hour and serve with the kraut.

title: Flat Gingerbread Nuts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. butter
- 1 1/2 lb. of sweetmeats
- 2 oz. allspice
- 1 oz. ginger
- 1/2 lb. moist sugar
- 2 lbs. of treacle
- 3 lbs. of flour
- orange and lemon peel
- citron
- blanched almond

## Directions

1. Sift the flour on your board and make a wall round, it means to make a hole in the centre of the flour, so that it is higher all round
2. Put in the butter, treacle, spices, the orange and lemon peel, and citron chopped very fine
3. Make this all into a paste, using as little extra flour as you can
4. Roll it out and cut in pieces the size of half-a-crown
5. Put them on your buttered tins
6. Flatten them with your finger and put on each half a blanched almond
7. Bake in a quick oven; they are soon done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fleischig Pie Crust
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of shortening (drippings and goose, duck or chicken fat, or rendered beef fat)
- one and one-half cups of flour (pastry flour preferred)
- a pinch of salt
- six or eight tablespoons of cold water (ice-water in summer)
- soft drippings for spreading

## Directions

1. With two knives cut the fat into the sifted flour until the shortening is in pieces as small as peas
2. Pour in six or eight tablespoons of cold water; in summer use ice-water; work with the knife until well mixed (never use the hand)
3. Flour a board or marble slab, roll the dough out thin
4. Sprinkle with a little flour and put dabs of soft drippings here and there
5. Fold the dough over and roll out thin again and spread with fat and sprinkle with flour
6. Repeat this and then roll out not too thin and line a pie-plate with this dough
7. For fruit fillings, brush over top of the dough with white of egg slightly beaten, or sprinkle with one tablespoon of bread crumbs to prevent the dough from becoming soggy
8. Put in the filling, brush over the edge of pastry with cold water
9. Lay the second round of paste loosely over the filling; press the edges together lightly, and trim, if needed
10. Cut several slits in the top crust or prick it with a fork before putting it in place
11. Bake from thirty-five to forty-five minutes until crust is a nice brown

title: Flemish Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of carrots
- one quart of boiling water
- one teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons fat
- one small onion
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Scrape, slice and cook one quart of carrots in one quart of boiling water to which has been added one teaspoon of salt, until tender
2. Drain
3. Heat two tablespoons fat, add one small onion, brown lightly, add the carrots, season with one teaspoon of sugar, one-quarter teaspoon of salt

title: Flemish Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart carrots
- one quart boiling water
- one teaspoon salt
- two tablespoons fat
- one small onion
- one teaspoon sugar
- one-quarter teaspoon salt
- one-eighth teaspoon white pepper
- one and one-half cups soup stock
- one teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Scrape, slice and cook one quart of carrots in one quart of boiling water to which has been added one teaspoon of salt, until tender; drain.
2. Heat two tablespoons fat, add one small onion, brown lightly, add the carrots, season with one teaspoon of sugar, one-quarter teaspoon of salt, one-eighth teaspoon of white pepper and shake well over the fire for ten minutes.
3. Add one and one-half cups of soup stock, cover and simmer for one-half hour.
4. Add one teaspoon chopped parsley and serve hot.

title: Floating Island
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one pint of milk

## Directions

1. Beat light the yolks of three eggs with one-quarter cup of sugar.
2. Scald a pint of milk, beat up the whites of three eggs

title: Floating Island
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one pint of milk
- vanilla or bitter almond

## Directions

1. Beat light the yolks of three eggs with one-quarter cup of sugar.
2. Scald a pint of milk, beat up the whites of three eggs very stiff and put them into the boiling milk, a spoonful at a time.
3. Take out the boiled whites and lay them on a platter.
4. Pour the hot milk gradually on the beaten yolks, when thoroughly mixed, return to the fire to boil.
5. When it begins to thicken remove.
6. When cool, flavor with vanilla or bitter almond.
7. Pour into a deep glass dish; put the whites on top, and garnish with jelly or candied fruit.
8. Eat cold.

title: Foam Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter cup of butter
- one cup of powdered sugar
- whites of two eggs
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- one-half cup of hot water

## Directions

1. Cream one-quarter cup of butter with one cup of powdered sugar, until very light
2. Add separately the unbeaten whites of two eggs, stirring briskly and beat again
3. Add one teaspoon of vanilla and one-half cup of hot water
4. Pour in sauceboat
5. place boat in a pan of boiling water on stove, until it becomes frothy
6. serve immediately

title: Foam Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg whites
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- one cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of vanilla
- sliced bananas, peaches and cream or strawberries

## Directions

1. Beat four egg whites well
2. Add one tablespoon of vinegar drop by drop
3. Add one cup of sugar and one tablespoon of vanilla
4. Beat for twenty minutes
5. Line spring form with this batter on all sides
6. Reserve a little of the mixture and drop by drops on top of torte
7. Bake forty-five minutes in moderate oven
8. Serve with sliced bananas, peaches and cream or strawberries

title: For Geese or Ducks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sage
- onions
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Add to the sage and onions also a little mashed potatoes
2. Apple sauce can be used for these also during Passover
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Force-Meat for Kreplech
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of beef, soup meat, cold veal, or lamb
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-eighth teaspoon of pepper
- ginger or nutmeg
- one-half teaspoon of onion juice
- one egg

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of beef, soup meat, cold veal, or take lamb chopped very fine, season with one teaspoon of salt, one-eighth teaspoon of pepper, ginger or nutmeg, one-half teaspoon of onion juice, mix with one egg.
2. This force-meat may also be made into balls one-half inch in diameter, roll the balls in flour and cook them in the boiling soup, or fry them in fat.

title: Forcemeat for Dressing Cutlets, Etc.
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- grated stale bread
- an equal quantity of chopped parsley
- clarified suet
- beaten yolks of eggs
- dry and fine bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Add to grated stale bread, an equal quantity of chopped parsley
2. Season it well, and mix it with clarified suet
3. Brush the cutlets with beaten yolks of eggs
4. Lay on the mixture thickly with a knife
5. Sprinkle over with dry and fine bread crumbs

title: Forcemeat for Dressing Fish Fillets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- grated bread
- chopped parsley
- the yolk of a hard boiled egg
- grated lemon peel
- a little lemon juice
- pepper
- salt
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Pound finely anchovies, grated bread, chopped parsley, and the yolk of a hard boiled egg
2. Add grated lemon peel, a little lemon juice, pepper and salt
3. Make into a paste with two eggs

title: Fowl Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- bread
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Roast the fowls and let them get quite cold
2. Cut off the flesh as even as you can and thin
3. Have the bread for the sandwiches made expressly
4. Cut it very thin
5. Leave the crust on
6. Make a mayonnaise according to recipe for same
7. Spread it thinly over the bread
8. Lay on the fowl
9. See that all the edges look neat
10. Cut each 2 rounds into 4 sandwiches
11. Do not make the sandwiches until a couple of hours before you want them
12. Keep them covered with a damp cloth
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fowls Stewed with Rice and Chorisa
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fowl
- sufficient water or gravy
- half a pound of rice
- one pound of chorisa
- white pepper
- salt
- saffron

## Directions

1. Boil a fowl in sufficient water or gravy to cover it
2. When boiling for ten minutes, skim off the fat
3. Add half a pound of rice and one pound of chorisa cut in about four pieces
4. Season with a little white pepper, salt, and a pinch of saffron to color it
5. Stew till the rice is thoroughly tender
6. Serve with the rice perfectly moist and no gravy

title: French Artichokes with Tomato Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- French artichokes
- olive oil
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Pick off from the solid green globes the outer tough petals.
2. Scoop out with a sharp-pointed knife the fuzzy centres, leaving the soft base.
3. Cut each artichoke in halves, wash, drain and fry brown on each side in olive oil.
4. Make tomato sauce and cook thirty minutes in that mixture.
5. Then serve.

title: French Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- very fine ground coffee
- milk

## Directions

1. Have your coffee ground very fine and use a French drip coffee-pot
2. Pour milk through, brought just to the boiling point
3. Take care not to let scum form on the milk

title: French Doughnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pint of water
- one-half pint of milk
- six ounces of butter
- one-half pound of flour
- six eggs
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Heat the butter, milk, and water
2. When it boils remove from the fire and stir in the flour, using a wooden spoon
3. When well mixed, stir in the eggs, whipping each one in separately until you have a hard batter
4. Pour your dough into a pastry bag with a small fluted or fancy tin funnel
5. Pour the paste on buttered paper, making into ring shapes
6. Fry in hot oil or butter substitute
7. Dust with powdered sugar

title: French Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-fourth teaspoon pepper
- one teaspoon sugar
- paprika
- two tablespoons vinegar
- four tablespoons olive oil

## Directions

1. Mix one-half teaspoon of salt, one-fourth teaspoon of pepper, one teaspoon of sugar, a dash of paprika, two tablespoons of vinegar and four tablespoons of olive oil. Stir until well-blended and use at once.

title: French Fried Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Pare the potatoes and throw them into cold water until needed
2. Dry them with a towel
3. Cut into small pieces lengthwise of the potato
4. Drop them into hot fat and remove when lightly browned
5. Fry only a few at a time, letting those done stand in a colander in the oven to keep hot
6. When all are done, sprinkle with salt and serve at once

title: French Fried Sweet Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- uncooked sweet potatoes
- fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash and cut small uncooked sweet potatoes into quarters.
2. Dry them and lower them into boiling hot fat.
3. Brown thoroughly; remove with a skimmer; drain and dry on paper.
4. Sprinkle with salt and serve.

title: French Method Of Making Coffee
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of coffee berries
- half a pint of water
- an equal quantity of boiling milk

## Directions

1. Grind the berries at the instant of using them in the proportion of one ounce to half a pint of water
2. Put the powder into a coffee biggin and press it down closely
3. Pour over a little water sufficient to moisten it
4. Add the remainder by degrees with the water perfectly boiling all the time
5. Let it run quite through before the top of the percolator is taken off
6. Serve with an equal quantity of boiling milk

title: French Pancake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg-yolks
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one-quarter cup flour
- one cup cold milk
- two tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Stir three egg-yolks with one-half teaspoon of salt and one-quarter cup of flour until smooth
2. Add one cup of cold milk gradually
3. Fold in the beaten whites
4. Heat pan and add two tablespoons of butter
5. When hot pour in pancake
6. Let cook slowly and evenly on one side
7. Finish baking in oven

title: French Prunes In Cognac
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- white wine
- sugar
- stick cinnamon
- mace
- cloves
- cognac

## Directions

1. Lay the prunes in white wine for two days
2. Put on a wire sieve to drip, but do not squeeze them
3. When they look dry, which will be in about half an hour, lay in glass jars with alternate layers of sugar and stick cinnamon and a few pieces of mace and a very few cloves
4. When the jars are full, fill up with cognac and seal
5. Set in the sunniest place you can find for three days

title: French Puffs (Windbeutel)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of water
- one-quarter pound of butter
- one-quarter pound of sifted flour
- four eggs
- grated peel of a lemon
- rendered butter
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put one cup of water and one-quarter pound of butter on to boil
2. When it begins to boil stir in one-quarter pound of sifted flour
3. Stir until it leaves the kettle clean
4. Take off the fire and stir until milk-warm
5. Stir in four eggs, one at a time, stirring until all used up
6. Flavor with the grated peel of a lemon
7. Put on some rendered butter in a kettle
8. When the butter is hot, dip a large teaspoon in cold water and cut pieces of dough with it as large as a walnut
9. Drop into the hot butter
10. Try one first to see whether the butter is hot enough
11. Do not crowd as they want plenty of room to raise
12. Dip the hot butter over them with a spoon
13. Fry a deep yellow
14. Sprinkle powdered sugar over them

title: French Roll Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a long round French roll
- boiling milk
- several eggs
- fine salad oil
- clarified sugar
- cinnamon
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Take off the crust of a long round French roll and cut the crumb in thin slices
2. Soak them in boiling milk, taking care they do not break
3. Have a dish ready with several eggs beaten up
4. With a fish slice remove the bread from the milk, letting the milk drain off
5. Dip them into the dish of eggs
6. Half fry them in fine salad oil
7. Then again soak them in the milk and dip in the egg
8. Fry of a handsome light brown
9. While hot, pour over clarified sugar flavored with cinnamon and orange flower water

title: French Rolls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast
- flour
- salt
- one-quarter cup of butter
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one whole egg
- one egg yolk
- mace or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare the yeast as for bread and work just the same
2. add one-quarter cup of butter, one-quarter cup of sugar, one whole egg and one egg yolk beaten very light
3. flavor with mace, or a few gratings of lemon peel
4. work until it leaves the hands perfectly clean
5. form into rolls
6. let raise
7. brush with beaten egg
8. place rolls in pan close together and bake

title: Fresh Cod or Striped Bass
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- water
- onion
- carrot
- turnip
- celery root
- butter
- cinnamon
- pepper
- flour
- eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces ready to serve, after which salt them for an hour
2. Into the fish kettle put a quantity of water, large onion sliced, carrot also sliced, turnip, celery root, and boil fifteen minutes
3. Add the fish and two tablespoons of butter, tiny piece of cinnamon, pepper to taste
4. Boil fifteen minutes longer, then add teaspoon of flour mixed with cold water
5. Boil up well and add salt or pepper if needed
6. Remove fish and arrange on platter
7. Beat yolks of two eggs with a tablespoon of cold water
8. After straining out vegetables, add the hot gravy in which fish was boiled
9. Return to fire and stir till thick enough
10. Garnish with chopped parsley

title: Fresh Mushrooms With Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nine good-sized mushrooms
- two tablespoons of butter
- two finely chopped onions
- salt
- pepper
- paprika
- six whole eggs
- hot buttered toast

## Directions

1. Peel nine good-sized mushrooms without using the stems
2. Chop very fine
3. Fry two tablespoons of butter and two finely chopped onions without browning
4. Add the mushrooms
5. Steam them by covering the pan after seasoning with salt, pepper and paprika
6. Before serving, beat six whole eggs and scramble with the mushrooms
7. Serve on hot buttered toast

title: Fresh Prune Cake (Kuchen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- biscuit-pan
- coffee cake dough
- fresh prunes
- sugar
- cinnamon
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Line a greased biscuit-pan with some of the coffee cake dough
2. Roll the dough thin and let it come up on the sides of the pan, then set aside to rise
3. When risen, cut the prunes in halves (they must be the fresh ones, not dried)
4. Lay in rows thickly and close together all over the bottom of the pan, do not leave any space between the prunes
5. Sprinkle very thickly with sugar, lightly with cinnamon, and lay bits of fresh butter all over the top
6. Bake until done in a moderately hot oven

title: Fricandeau of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece from the shoulder, about three to four pounds
- a couple of onions
- a carrot sliced
- sweet herbs
- two or three bay leaves
- a large piece of chorissa or a slice of the root of a tongue smoked
- a little whole pepper and salt
- gravy made from the trimmings of the veal

## Directions

1. Take a piece from the shoulder, about three to four pounds
2. Trim it and form it into a well shaped even piece, the surface of which should be quite smooth
3. Pique it thickly
4. Put it into a stewpan with a couple of onions, a carrot sliced, sweet herbs, two or three bay leaves, a large piece of chorissa or a slice of the root of a tongue smoked, a little whole pepper and salt
5. Cover it with a gravy made from the trimmings of the veal
6. Stew till extremely tender, which can be proved by probing it with a fine skewer
7. Reduce part of the gravy to a glaze
8. Glaze the meat with it and serve on a puree of vegetables

title: Fricasseed Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard-boiled eggs
- flour
- egg
- one-half teaspoon of oil
- one-half teaspoon of vinegar
- onion juice
- one teaspoon chopped parsley
- nutmeg
- salt
- vermicelli
- beef drippings

## Directions

1. Take six hard-boiled eggs and remove shells
2. Roll them in flour
3. Roll in egg to which has been added one-half teaspoon of oil, one-half teaspoon of vinegar, a few drops of onion juice, one teaspoon chopped parsley, a little nutmeg and salt
4. When quite covered, roll in vermicelli that has been broken into fine bits
5. Fry in deep beef drippings

title: Fricasseed Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard-boiled eggs
- flour
- egg
- one-half teaspoon of oil
- one-half teaspoon of vinegar
- a few drops of onion juice
- one teaspoon chopped parsley
- a little nutmeg
- salt
- vermicelli
- deep beef drippings
- one tablespoon of fat
- one tablespoon of flour
- one-half cup of white wine
- one cup of bouillon
- cayenne
- one teaspoon chopped chives
- one teaspoon chopped parsley
- some chopped olives
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Take six hard-boiled eggs, remove shells.
2. Roll them in flour, then in egg to which has been added one-half teaspoon of oil, one-half teaspoon of vinegar, a few drops of onion juice, one teaspoon chopped parsley, a little nutmeg and salt.
3. When quite covered, roll in vermicelli that has been broken into fine bits and fry in deep beef drippings.
4. For the sauce: brown one tablespoon of fat and one tablespoon of flour together.
5. Add one-half cup of white wine and a cup of bouillon.
6. Season with salt and cayenne and boil five minutes.
7. Add one teaspoon each of chopped chives and parsley, some chopped olives and mushrooms.
8. Bring to a boil again and pour over the eggs.

title: Fricasseed Veal with Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast or shoulder
- fine salt
- ginger
- tablespoon of goose-oil or poultry drippings
- one onion
- one or two tomatoes
- canned tomatoes
- water
- teaspoon of flour
- parsley
- cauliflower
- salt

## Directions

1. Use the breast or shoulder for this purpose, the former being preferable, and cut it up into pieces, not too small
2. Sprinkle each piece slightly with fine salt and ginger
3. Heat a tablespoon of goose-oil or poultry drippings in a stew-pan, and lay the veal in it
4. Cut up an onion and one or two tomatoes (a tablespoon of canned tomatoes will do), and add to this a little water
5. Stew two hours, closely covered
6. When done mix a teaspoon of flour and a little water and add to the veal
7. Chop up a few sprigs of parsley, add it and boil up once and serve
8. Place the cauliflower around the platter in which you serve the veal
9. Boil the cauliflower in salt and water, closely covered

title: Fried Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five apples
- sugar
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Quarter and core five apples without paring
2. Put into a frying-pan one cup of sugar, one tablespoon of butter and three tablespoons of water
3. Let this melt
4. Lay in the apples with the skin up
5. Cover and fry slowly until brown

title: Fried Beefsteak
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- third cut of chuck or tenderloin
- fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take third cut of chuck or the tenderloin
2. Have the spider very hot, use just enough fat to grease the spider
3. Lay in the steak, turning very often to keep in the juice
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Serve on a hot platter

title: Fried Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium head of cabbage
- three tablespoons of fat (from top of soup stock preferred)
- one sour apple
- caraway seed to taste
- salt
- paprika
- one-half onion

## Directions

1. Cut one medium head of cabbage fine, soak ten minutes in salt water.
2. Drain, heat three tablespoons of fat (from top of soup stock preferred), add cabbage, one sour apple peeled and cut up, caraway seed to taste, salt, paprika and one-half onion minced.
3. Cover very closely and cook slowly for one hour.

title: Fried Cucumbers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- water
- beaten egg
- cracker dust
- boiling fat

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the cucumbers lengthwise in about the same thickness observed with egg plant.
2. Lay these slices in salt and water for about an hour.
3. Dip in beaten egg and cracker dust.
4. French fry in boiling fat, taking care to carefully drain in a colander before serving.

title: Fried Eggplant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- salt
- flour
- butter
- egg and cracker (optional, for dipping)
- fat for deep frying (optional)

## Directions

1. Pare eggplant, cut in very thin slices
2. Sprinkle with salt, pile slices on a plate
3. Cover with a weight to draw out juice
4. Let stand one hour
5. Dredge with flour and fry slowly in a little butter until crisp and brown, or dip in egg and cracker and fry in deep fat

title: Fried Eggs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- oil or butter
- bread
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Have a small frying-pan
2. Put in a little oil or butter
3. Let it boil
4. If oil, put a very small piece of bread to fry quite brown before you put in the eggs
5. Break each egg separately in a teacup
6. Be careful not to break the yolk
7. Put them into the pan
8. Keep them as round as possible
9. Have a very small slice to take them out of the pan
10. Put in a dish a sheet of writing paper
11. Let the dish be very hot
12. Put on the eggs
13. Garnish with nice little dry sprigs of parsley
14. Remember the hot plates
15. Sprinkle over the eggs salt and pepper
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Melt in a frying-pan a piece of butter, or fat for a meat meal.
2. When hot, drop in the eggs, one at a time, being careful not to break the yolk.
3. When the white of the egg is set they are done, though some persons like them turned over and cooked on the other side.
4. Remove from the pan with a cake turner.

title: Fried Fowl or Turkey with Sausage
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- best pieces from the carcase
- boiling fat
- egg
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- slices of sausage
- potatoes
- the juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut off the best pieces you can from the carcase left, make them of a good shape, the thigh will cut in two pieces, the drumsticks two pieces if you chop the stick off, and cut pieces off the breast.
2. Have a pan with boiling fat, dip the pieces in egg, mix with parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg, fry them a light brown.
3. Fry some slices of sausage in the same way.
4. Mash some potatoes nicely, lay them in a dish, put the fried fowl round.
5. Squeeze the juice of a lemon, strain over the fowl, garnish with parsley.
6. This is a nice dish for lunch.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Fresh Herrings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh herrings
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Wash them well
2. split them open
3. dip them in flour and eggs
4. fry them
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Green Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large green tomatoes
- salt
- cornmeal
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut into thin slices large green tomatoes, sprinkle with salt and dip into cornmeal.
2. Fry slowly in a little butter till well browned; keep the frying-pan covered while they are cooking, so they will be perfectly tender.

title: Fried Guard Fish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- guard fish
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut them open and wash and dry them well
2. Lay them in salt; each fish can be cut in 4 pieces
3. Fry them like the other fish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Matsos
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some of the thickest matsos
- milk
- boiling fresh butter

## Directions

1. Soak some of the thickest matsos in milk, taking care they do not break
2. Fry in boiling fresh butter

title: Fried Matsos
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- matsos
- milk
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Soak some of the thickest matsos in milk, taking care they do not break
2. Fry in boiling fresh butter

title: Fried Parsley
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- butter or clarified suet

## Directions

1. Prepare the parsley as in crisped parsley
2. Fry it in butter or clarified suet
3. Drain it on a cloth placed before the fire

title: Fried Perch
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- perch
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- flour
- eggs
- oil or butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Split them open from the back and wash them well
2. Dry them
3. Season them well with salt and pepper, with the juice of a lemon
4. Let them lay in that for 1/2 an hour
5. Dip them in flour and eggs
6. Fry them in oil or butter
7. Send them up hot on a fish napkin garnished with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Plaice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- salt
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. Cut the plaice in slices, wash it well, salt it, and let it drain all the water
2. Lay it in your fish cloth for half an hour before you are ready to fry it
3. Prepare it just the same as other fried fish in oil or butter
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Potato Balls
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 large potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- 2 eggs
- flour
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Mash 4 or 5 large potatoes with a little salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg, and a little chopped parsley.
2. Mix it up with 2 eggs into a paste.
3. Roll it out on a pasteboard with flour the same as making paste.
4. Cut it in strips 2 inches wide and 6 long.
5. Roll up each strip, dust it with flour.
6. Fry them in butter or oil.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Potato Chips
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Wash some potatoes and dry them in a cloth.
2. Pare them in thin ribbons.
3. Fry them dry, lay them on a strainer to drain off the oil or butter.
4. Put them on a hot dish, sprinkle over salt and pepper mixed together.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Shad
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- lemon
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Prepare as the other fried fish
2. It should be eaten hot with lemon and cayenne
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Smoked Beef and Eggs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of fat smoked beef
- dripping
- eggs

## Directions

1. Put in your meat fryingpan a little dripping, fry the beef, put it on a plate at the side to keep hot.
2. Then fry the eggs nice and round a light brown, put the beef on the dish and one egg on each slice, turn a little of the hot fat in the dish.
3. Serve very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Soles in the English Way
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- egg
- stale bread crumbs
- boiling butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the soles as directed in the previous receipt
2. Brush them over with egg, dredge them with stale bread crumbs
3. Fry in boiling butter

title: Fried Spring Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- spring chicken
- goose-fat or poultry drippings
- salt
- ground ginger or pepper
- cracker or bread crumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut it up as for fricassee and see that every piece is wiped dry.
2. Have ready heated in a spider some goose-fat or other poultry drippings.
3. Season each piece of chicken with salt and ground ginger, or pepper.
4. Roll each piece of chicken in sifted cracker or bread crumbs which you have previously seasoned with salt.
5. Fry in the spider, turning often, and browning evenly.
6. You may cut up some parsley and add while frying.
7. If the chicken is quite large, it is better to steam it before frying.

title: Fried Steak and Potatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- steak
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- potatoes
- yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Put a little fat in a pan
2. Season the steak with salt and pepper and sprinkle a little flour over the steak
3. Fry it a nice brown colour
4. Have boiled and washed a few potatoes and mashed with the yolk of an egg
5. Turn the potatoes out in the dish you intend sending up the steak
6. Shape it nicely and lay the steak upon it
7. Serve it on a very hot dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fried Steak with Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- steak
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- fat
- white onions
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Season the steak with salt and pepper, and dredge with flour
2. If tough, chop on both sides with a sharp knife
3. Lay in a pan of hot fat; when brown on one side, turn and brown on the other
4. While the steak is frying, heat some fat in another fryer and drop in four or five white onions that have been cut up
5. Fry crisp but not black
6. Remove the steak to a hot platter
7. Stir one tablespoon of flour in the fryer until smooth
8. Add one-half cup of boiling water
9. Lay the crisp onions over the steak, then over all pour the brown gravy

title: Fried Sweet Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- sweet drippings or butter

## Directions

1. Boil, peel and cut lengthwise into slices a quarter of an inch thick.
2. Fry in sweet drippings or butter (cold boiled potatoes may also be fried in this way).

title: Fried Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large sound tomatoes
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Cut large, sound tomatoes in halves and flour the insides thickly.
2. Season with a little salt and pepper.
3. Allow the butter to get very hot before putting in the tomatoes.
4. When brown on one side, turn, and when done serve with hot cream or thicken some milk and pour over the tomatoes hot.

title: Fried Veal Sausages
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of veal sausages
- flour
- boiling fat
- 3 or 4 potatoes

## Directions

1. Take a pound of veal sausages and divide them
2. Roll them in flour
3. Cut 3 or 4 potatoes, wash them, and dry them on a cloth
4. Sift some flour lightly over them
5. Have a pan with boiling fat and fry the potatoes crisp and brown
6. Take them out of the pan with a slice and put them just inside of the oven to keep hot
7. Fry the sausages lightly
8. Have a dish very hot, put the sausages in, and the potatoes round
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fritada
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- four good-sized tomatoes
- chopped parsley
- scallions or leeks
- celery
- salt
- pepper
- four eggs
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Steam the fish and bone
2. Take four good-sized tomatoes and cut them up
3. Add chopped parsley, scallions or leeks cut in small pieces, a little celery, salt and pepper to taste and four eggs well-beaten
4. Mix all these ingredients very well with the boned fish
5. Form in omelet shape
6. Place in oven in pan greased with olive oil and bake until well browned

title: Fritada of Cold Meat
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- salt
- pepper
- six eggs
- onion
- two spoonfuls of oil
- tomato
- parsley
- lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare some cold meat as before
2. To about 1 lb. of the cold chopped meat, well seasoned, add 6 eggs well beaten
3. Take a deep pie dish, slice an onion thinly, with 2 spoonfuls of oil
4. Slice a tomato, or if not liked, do it without
5. Let it fry in the oven
6. When ready put your mixture, shaping it from the sides to be higher in the centre
7. Let it bake a nice brown
8. When done loosen it all round with a knife, and slip it on to a dish
9. Garnish with parsley, and send it to table with a lemon for use
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fritter Batter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-third cups flour
- two teaspoons baking-powder
- one-quarter teaspoon salt
- two-thirds cup milk or water
- one egg

## Directions

1. Mix and sift one and one-third cups of flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder, one-quarter teaspoon of salt
2. Add two-thirds cup of milk or water gradually
3. Add one egg well beaten
4. For fruit batter add a little sugar, for vegetables pepper and salt

title: Fritter Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- three-fourths teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of flour
- two tablespoons of water
- deep hot fat
- hot broth

## Directions

1. Beat one egg until light
2. Add three-fourths teaspoon of salt, one-half cup of flour and two tablespoons of water
3. Put through colander into deep hot fat and fry until brown
4. Drain and pour hot broth over them

title: Frosted Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large apples
- water
- sugar
- egg
- lemon juice
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pare and core six large apples
2. Cover with one pint of water and three tablespoons of sugar
3. Simmer until tender
4. Remove from the syrup and drain
5. Wash the parings and let simmer with a little water for one-half hour
6. Beat the white of one egg to a stiff froth and add one tablespoon of sugar
7. Coat the top of the apples lightly with the meringue
8. Place in a cool oven to dry
9. Strain the juice from the parings
10. Add two tablespoons of sugar
11. Return to the fire and let boil for five minutes
12. Add a few drops of lemon juice and a little nutmeg
13. Cool and pour around the apples

title: Frosted Currants
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Red currants
- White of egg
- Boiled sugar syrup
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pick fine, even, large bunches of red currants (not too ripe)
2. Dip each bunch, one at a time, into a mixture of frothed white of egg, then into a thick, boiled sugar syrup
3. Drain the bunches by laying on a sieve
4. When partly dry dip again into the boiled syrup
5. Repeat the process a third time
6. Sprinkle powdered sugar over them and lay on a sheet of paper in a slightly warm oven to dry

title: Frozen Cream Cheese with Preserved Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three Neufchatel cheeses
- one-half cup of thick cream
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one rounding teaspoon of sugar
- preserved figs
- lettuce leaf

## Directions

1. Take three Neufchatel cheeses
2. Mash the cheese to a smooth paste
3. Add one-half cup of thick cream, one-half teaspoon of salt and one rounding teaspoon of sugar
4. Place in a small square mold
5. Bury in salt and ice and let stand several hours
6. When ready to serve unmold and cut in squares
7. Place each on a lettuce leaf
8. Decorate the centre of the cheese square with a preserved fig and serve at once

title: Frozen Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- yolk of five eggs
- sugar to taste
- vanilla to taste
- beaten whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the milk first
2. Beat the yolks of eggs and stir into the milk
3. When cold add the beaten whites and vanilla
4. Put in freezer and turn

title: Fruit and Nut Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two bananas
- two oranges
- one-half cup of English walnuts
- juice of one-half lemon
- French dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Slice two bananas, two oranges and mix them with one-half cup of English walnuts and the juice of one-half lemon with French dressing
2. Serve on lettuce leaves

title: Fruit Cake (Wedding Cake)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- yolks of twelve eggs
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one-half teaspoon of mace
- one-half teaspoon of cloves
- one-fourth of a pound of almonds pounded
- two pounds of raisins (seeded and chopped)
- three pounds of currants (carefully cleaned)
- one pound of citron (shredded very fine)
- one-quarter of a pound of orange peel (chopped very fine)
- one pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Take one pound of butter and one pound of sugar rubbed to a cream
2. add yolks of twelve eggs, one tablespoon of cinnamon, one teaspoon of allspice, one-half teaspoon of mace, one-half teaspoon of cloves, one-fourth of a pound of almonds pounded, two pounds of raisins (seeded and chopped), three pounds of currants (carefully cleaned), one pound of citron (shredded very fine), and one-quarter of a pound of orange peel (chopped very fine)
3. Soak all this prepared fruit in one pint of brandy overnight
4. Add all to the dough and put in the stiffly-beaten whites last
5. Bake in a very slow oven for several hours, in cake pans lined with buttered paper
6. When cold wrap in cloths dipped in brandy and put in earthen jars
7. If baked in gas oven have light very low and keep oven the same temperature for four or five hours

title: Fruit Charlotte
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- liquid jelly
- pottle of scarlet strawberries
- half a pound of white sugar
- pint of whipped cream
- one ounce and a half of dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Line a jelly mould with fine picked strawberries, which must first be just dipped into some liquid jelly, to make them adhere closely
2. Fill the mould with some strawberry cream prepared as follows: take a pottle of scarlet strawberries, mix them with half a pound of white sugar, rub this through a sieve, and add to it a pint of whipped cream, and one ounce and a half of dissolved isinglass
3. Pour it into the mould, which must be immersed in ice until ready to serve
4. Carefully turn it out on the dish, and garnish according to fancy

title: Fruit Charlotte Russe
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- oil of almonds
- powdered sugar
- fresh strawberries
- isinglass
- basket of strawberries
- 1/2 lb. sifted sugar
- 1 pint of milk
- 1/2 an oz. of isinglass

## Directions

1. Oil a pretty jelly mould with the oil of almonds
2. Sift over some powdered sugar
3. Take some fine fresh strawberries and dip them in a little dissolved isinglass to make them stick to the mould
4. Have a basket of strawberries, pick off the stalks, rub them through a clean sieve with 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar, rub them through quite dry
5. Mix with them a pint of milk with 1/2 an oz. of isinglass dissolved in, stir it all together
6. Turn into the garnished mould
7. Put it in rough ice till you are ready to serve it
8. Turn it out very carefully and garnish the dish to fancy
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fruit Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half cup of raisins
- One-half cup of nuts
- One-half cup figs or dates
- Honey or corn syrup

## Directions

1. Chop coarsely one-half cup of raisins, one-half cup of nuts, one-half cup figs or dates
2. Add enough honey or corn syrup to make a stiff loaf, about two tablespoons
3. Place in ice-box for one hour
4. Slice and serve in place of candy, rolling each slice in cornstarch

title: Fruit Punch for Twenty People
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pineapple, or one can of grated pineapple
- one cup of boiling water
- two cups of freshly made tea (one heaping tablespoon of Ceylon tea, steep for five minutes)
- one dozen lemons
- three oranges sliced and quartered
- one quart bottle apollinaris water
- three cups of sugar
- one and one-half cups of water
- one quart of water
- ice
- Pieces of pineapple, strawberries, mint-leaves or slices of banana

## Directions

1. Grate the pineapple, add the one cup of boiling water, and boil fifteen minutes.
2. Strain through jelly-bag, pressing out all the juice; let cool, and add the lemon and orange juice, the tea and syrup.
3. Add apollinaris water just before serving.
4. Pieces of pineapple, strawberries, mint-leaves or slices of banana are sometimes added as a garnish.

title: Fruit Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pineapple
- three oranges
- three bananas
- French mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves
- one cup of sugar
- one-half cup of water
- lemon juice
- one cup of claret

## Directions

1. Slice one pineapple, three oranges, and three bananas
2. Pour over it a French mayonnaise, put on lettuce leaves and serve at once
3. Alternatively, make a syrup of one cup of sugar and one-half cup of water, boil until thick, add juice of lemon slightly cool, then pour over fruit
4. Let stand on ice one to two hours
5. Another nice dressing is one cup of claret, one-half cup of sugar, and piece of lemon
6. Always use lemon juice in preference to vinegar in fruit salads
7. All fruits that go well together may be mixed
8. This is served just before desert

title: Fruit Sauce for Puddings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients
