- yolks of 2 eggs
- teaspoonful of arrowroot
- jam
- hot water
- spoonful of lemon juice
- teacupful of almond milk

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of 2 eggs with a teaspoonful of arrowroot very smooth
2. If fresh fruit is not in season take some jam
3. Pour over it a little hot water to enable you to run it through a sieve
4. Stir it into the egg with a spoonful of lemon juice
5. Boil a teacupful of almond milk
6. Stir it in and let it boil to thicken
7. Turn it round your pudding
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Fruit Sauces
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar to taste
- wine or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the fruit well
2. put on the stove in a saucepan without adding any more water
3. Cover with a lid
4. let the fruit get thoroughly heated all through until it comes to a boil, but do not boil it
5. Stir occasionally
6. mash the fruit well with a wooden potato masher
7. strain through a fine sieve, being careful to get every drop of substance from the fruit
8. Sweeten the juice with sugar to taste
9. add a few drops of wine or lemon juice
10. put back on the stove
11. cook until it thickens, stirring occasionally
12. Serve with cake, fritters or puddings

title: Fruit Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of plums, cherries, or red currants and raspberries
- a pint of water
- yolk of an egg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of plums, cherries, or red currants and raspberries and carefully pick and wash, and boil to a pulp with a pint of water.
2. Let it slightly cool and then stir in the beaten yolk of an egg and a little sugar.
3. Strain the soup, which should be served cold.

title: Fruit Tartlets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- canned or fresh fruit
- sugar
- water
- puff paste

## Directions

1. If using canned fruit, drain syrup and put in saucepan with equal quantity of sugar
2. Cook until it forms a syrup, then pour in the fruit which has been stoned if necessary
3. Cook until the whole is a syrupy mass
4. If using fresh fruit, put two parts sugar to one of water and cook until syrupy
5. Add fruit which has been peeled, sliced and stoned
6. Cook until the whole is a thick syrupy mass
7. Line patty cases or plain muffin rings with puff paste
8. Put a spoonful or two of fruit in each one
9. Bake a nice brown

title: Fruit Tarts Or Pies
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- half a pound of sugar to a quart of fruit
- whites of eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Acid fruits are best stewed before putting into a pie
2. Use half a pound of sugar to a quart of fruit, not quite so much if the fruit is ripe
3. Lay the fruit high in the middle of the dish, to make the pie a good shape
4. Lay over the crust, when nearly baked, an icing of the whites of eggs whisked with sugar
5. Replace the tart or pie in the oven

title: Fruit Wheels
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- two large tablespoons of butter
- milk
- soft butter
- one teaspoon of flour
- four tablespoons of granulated sugar
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-half cup of seeded and cut raisins
- one-half cup of chopped citron
- one-half cup of cleaned currants

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, baking-powder, salt, and sugar
2. Rub in butter
3. Mix to soft dough with milk
4. Roll out one-half inch thick
5. Spread thickly with soft butter
6. Dust with flour, granulated sugar, and cinnamon
7. Sprinkle over raisins, citron, and currants
8. Roll up
9. Cut in one-inch slices
10. Put one inch apart on greased, flat pans
11. Bake in hot oven

title: Fudge
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of granulated sugar
- One-eighth teaspoon of salt
- One cup of milk or cream
- One-half a cake of Baker's chocolate
- Two tablespoons of butter
- One teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil together two cups of granulated sugar, one-eighth teaspoon of salt and one cup of milk or cream, until when tried in cold water, it will form a soft ball (about eight minutes)
2. Add one-half a cake of Baker's chocolate, two tablespoons of butter and one teaspoon of vanilla
3. Beat until smooth and creamy
4. Pour into greased pans
5. Cool and cut in squares

title: Gaenseklein
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- wings, neck, gizzard, heart and back of goose
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- garlic
- cold water
- fat
- small piece of garlic
- heaping teaspoon of flour

## Directions

1. Rub wings, neck, gizzard, heart and back of goose with salt, ginger, pepper and garlic
2. Set on the fire in a stew-pan with cold water
3. Cover tightly and stew slowly but steadily for four hours
4. When done skim off all the fat
5. Put a spider over the fire and put into it about two or three tablespoons of the fat that you have just skimmed off
6. Add the fat to the meat again
7. Cut up fine a very small piece of garlic and add a heaping teaspoon of flour (brown)
8. Add the hot gravy and pour all over the goose
9. Cover up tightly and set on back of stove till you wish to serve

title: Gansleber in Sulz (Goose-Liver Aspic)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a large goose liver
- goose-fat
- salt
- pepper
- a few whole cloves
- a very little onion
- sulz
- whites of hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Fry a large goose liver in goose-fat
2. Season with salt, pepper, a few whole cloves and a very little onion
3. Cut it up in slices and mix with the sulz and the whites of hard-boiled eggs

title: Gateau De Pomme
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twelve fine baking apples
- milk and water
- half an ounce of dissolved isinglass
- white sifted sugar
- grated lemon peel
- fine custard

## Directions

1. Take ten or twelve fine baking apples, peel and take out the cores
2. let them simmer in milk and water
3. when soft drain them
4. beat them up with a wooden fork, with half an ounce of dissolved isinglass, white sifted sugar, sufficient to sweeten, and grated lemon peel
5. Put the mixture, when perfectly smooth, into a mould
6. set it in ice or a very cool place
7. when it is turned out it should be covered with a fine custard

title: Gateau De Tours
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pound-cake
- jam or preserve
- whites of eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a pound-cake, cut it in slices about half an inch in thickness
2. spread each slice with jam or preserve
3. replace them to the original form
4. cover the cake with whites of eggs and sugar, whisked to a froth
5. set it in a cool oven to dry

title: Gefillte Fisch
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- trout, pickerel, or pike
- one-quarter pound of butter
- two tablespoons chopped parsley
- soaked white bread
- one onion
- salt
- pepper
- pounded almonds
- two eggs
- nutmeg
- salt water
- celery root
- parsley
- one onion
- flour
- bits of butter

## Directions

1. After the fish has been scaled and thoroughly cleaned, remove all the meat that adheres to the skin, being careful not to injure the skin
2. Take out all the meat from head to tail, cut open along the backbone, removing it also, but do not disfigure the head and tail
3. Chop the meat in a chopping bowl
4. Heat about a quarter of a pound of butter in a spider
5. Add two tablespoons chopped parsley, and some soaked white bread
6. Remove from the fire and add an onion grated, salt, pepper, pounded almonds, the yolks of two eggs, also a very little nutmeg grated
7. Mix all thoroughly and fill the skin until it looks natural
8. Boil in salt water, containing a piece of butter, celery root, parsley and an onion
9. The fish should be cooked for one and one-quarter hours, or until done
10. Remove from the fire and lay on a platter
11. Thicken the sauce with yolks of two eggs, adding a few slices of lemon
12. This fish may be baked but must be rolled in flour and dotted with bits of butter

title: Gefillte Fisch With Egg Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one five-pound haddock
- four large onions
- one sprig of parsley
- two eggs
- pepper
- cinnamon
- salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- cracker dust
- one sliced onion
- one sprig of parsley
- one small sliced carrot
- pepper
- salt
- cold water
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Cut a five-pound haddock into four-inch slices
2. Cut a big hole into each slice, preserving the backbone and skin
3. Put this meat, cut from the fish, into a wooden tray
4. Add to it four large onions and a sprig of parsley
5. Chop until very fine, then add two eggs, a dash of pepper and cinnamon, a pinch of salt, and a tablespoon of sugar
6. To this add enough cracker dust to stiffen it
7. Put this filling into the holes cut in the fish
8. Take a saucepan, put in one sliced onion, a sprig of parsley, a small sliced carrot, a dash of pepper, and a pinch of salt
9. Put the fish into the saucepan, cover with cold water, and let it boil slowly for one hour
10. At the end of the hour take out the fish, and put on a platter
11. Preserve the water or gravy in which the fish was boiled for the sauce
12. Beat the yolks of two eggs thoroughly
13. Into the beaten yolks slowly pour the gravy in which the fish was boiled, stirring constantly
14. Stand this on the back of the stove to boil for five minutes, stirring constantly so as to prevent burning

title: German Bola
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 oz. German yeast
- 1 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 lb. butter
- 1 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. sweetmeats
- 1 lb. clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 oz. of German yeast in a cup of milk and 2 eggs all beat together.
2. Work 3/4 lb. of butter into 1 lb. of flour with the yeast, milk and eggs, to a paste.
3. Set it to rise for an hour.
4. Roll it out and work in 1/2 lb. of sweetmeats.
5. Bake in a slow oven.
6. Have ready 1 lb. of clarified sugar and pour over the cake boiling hot, as it comes out of the oven.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: German Coffee Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 3 teaspoons baking-powder
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 0.67 cup milk
- melted butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take three cups of flour sifted, one teaspoon of salt, three tablespoons of sugar, three teaspoons of baking-powder, two eggs, two tablespoons of butter, and two-thirds of a cup of milk
2. Stir well together, adding more milk if necessary
3. Keep batter very stiff
4. Sprinkle with melted butter (generously) sugar and cinnamon, and again with melted butter
5. Put into well-buttered shallow pans and bake about half an hour

title: German Fried Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut up raw potatoes quite thin
2. Salt and pepper them
3. Drop in boiling fat
4. Cover up at first to soften them
5. Turn frequently to prevent burning
6. Remove the cover to brown slightly

title: German Hazelnut Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of eight eggs
- one-half pound of granulated sugar
- whites of eggs
- a small pinch of salt
- one-half pound of grated hazelnuts
- grated rind of one-half lemon

## Directions

1. Beat together for twenty minutes until very light the yolks of eight eggs with one-half pound of granulated sugar
2. Add the very stiffly-beaten whites of eggs
3. Place the bowl in which it has been stirred over a boiler in which water is boiling on the stove
4. Stir continually but slowly until all the batter is well warmed but not too hot
5. Add a small pinch of salt and one-half pound of grated hazelnuts
6. Add the nuts gradually and mix well
7. Pour into a greased spring form
8. Bake very slowly
9. Ice with boiled icing

title: German or Spanish Puffs
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a tea-cup full of cold water
- four table spoonsful of flour
- four well beaten eggs
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Put a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, and a tea-cup full of cold water into a saucepan
2. When the butter is melted, stir in, while on the fire, four table spoonsful of flour
3. When thoroughly mixed, put it in a dish to cool
4. Add four well beaten eggs
5. Butter some cups
6. Half fill them with the batter
7. Bake in a quick oven
8. Serve with clarified sugar

title: German Pancakes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two and one-half tablespoons of flour
- one cup of milk
- two tablespoons of butter
- sugar
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Beat eggs very thoroughly without separating yolks and whites
2. Add salt and sift in flour
3. Add milk gradually and beat the whole very well
4. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a large frying-pan
5. Turn mixture in and cook slowly until brown underneath
6. Grease the bottom of a large pie plate and slip the pancake on the plate
7. Add the other tablespoon of butter to the frying-pan
8. When hot, turn uncooked side of pancake down and brown
9. Serve at once with sugar and lemon slices or with any desired preserve or syrup

title: German Pancakes, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one-half cup of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one cup of milk
- butter
- powdered sugar
- six lemon slices

## Directions

1. Beat eggs until very light
2. Add flour and salt and beat again
3. Add milk slowly and beat thoroughly
4. Heat a generous quantity of butter in a frying-pan
5. Pour all the batter into this at one time
6. Place on a hot stove for one minute
7. Remove to a brisk oven
8. When edges turn up on sides of pan, reduce heat and cook more slowly until light, crisp and brown, about seven minutes
9. Slide carefully on a hot plate
10. Sprinkle plentifully with powdered sugar
11. Send to the table with six lemon slices

title: German Pancakes, No. 3
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one-half cup of milk
- three-quarters cup of sifted flour
- one-eighth teaspoon of baking-powder
- salt
- two tablespoons of rendered butter
- powdered sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs until very light
2. Add milk and stir in flour, baking-powder, and salt
3. Just before frying, add stiffly-beaten whites of eggs and mix well together
4. Put on fire an iron skillet with a close-fitting top
5. Heat in rendered butter
6. When very hot, pour in enough batter to cover the bottom of the skillet
7. Cover at once with the top
8. When the pancake is brown on one side, remove the top and let it brown on the other side
9. Take it up with a perforated skimmer and lay on a plate
10. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and some lemon juice
11. Serve at once

title: German Prune Butter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove pits and wash prunes.
2. Take three-quarters of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit, and enough water to keep from burning.
3. Do not stir but remove from the sides of the kettle occasionally.
4. Let boil for hours.
5. When done, place in glasses.
6. Let cool.
7. Cover with paraffin.

title: German Puffs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of melted fat
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1/2 lb. of meal finely sifted
- 4 eggs
- clarified sugar
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 lb. of melted fat into a 1/2 pint of water
2. Have a 1/2 lb. of meal finely sifted
3. Stir it into the boiling fat and water
4. Beat up 4 eggs and stir it in
5. Grease your cups
6. Put a spoonful in each
7. Bake in a quick oven
8. Cover with clarified sugar
9. When you dish up the puffs, sprinkle over cinnamon and sugar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: German Puffs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of flour
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 lb of sugar
- lemon
- orange-flower water
- vanilla
- jam

## Directions

1. Boil the milk
2. While boiling stir in the flour
3. Beat well 4 eggs and stir them into the paste of milk and flour
4. Have some teacups and grease them with butter
5. Drop in a spoonful of the mixture and put them on a baking sheet
6. Put them in a quick oven, they will rise over the top of the cup
7. Clarify 1/2 lb of sugar flavoured with lemon, orange-flower water, or vanilla
8. Serve the puffs hot or cold, with the sugar in a sauce tureen
9. You can if you like when you take them out of the oven put jam on the top
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: German Puffs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pint of water
- one-quarter pound of melted fat
- one-quarter pound of meal, finely sifted
- four eggs
- cinnamon
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Into one-half pint of water put one-quarter pound of melted fat
2. When boiling add one-quarter pound of meal, finely sifted
3. Beat up four eggs, remove the mixture from the fire and stir in the eggs
4. Grease some cups and put a spoonful in each
5. Bake in a quick oven
6. When done sprinkle with cinnamon and cover with clarified sugar

title: Geroestete Fervelchen Pfarvel (Egg Barley)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- eggs
- salt water or milk

## Directions

1. Make just as you would a noodle dough, only stiffer, by adding and working in as much flour as possible and then grate on a coarse grater.
2. Spread on a large platter to dry.
3. Boil one cup of egg barley in salt water or milk, which must boil before you put in the egg barley until thick.
4. Serve with melted butter poured over them.
5. Alternatively, sift a cup or more of flour on a board, break in two eggs, and work the dough by rubbing it through your hands until it is as fine as barley grains.

title: Gewetsh (Servian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large onion
- one tablespoon of fat
- one teaspoon of paprika
- two pounds of neck or shoulder of lamb
- one pound of rice
- six large tomatoes
- two sliced green peppers
- salt

## Directions

1. Brown one large onion in a tablespoon of fat.
2. Add one teaspoon of paprika and two pounds of neck or shoulder of lamb, cook one hour.
3. Have ready one pound of rice that has been boiled for twenty minutes.
4. Take a twelve inch pudding dish, grease, place a layer of sliced tomatoes on bottom of pan.
5. Place half the rice, half the meat, two sliced green peppers, sprinkle a little salt and pour part of gravy over this.
6. Place another layer of tomatoes, rice, meat, with two sliced peppers and tomatoes on top.
7. Salt, and pour remainder of gravy.
8. Put lumps of fat here and there.
9. Bake in hot oven three-quarters of an hour.
10. Use plenty of gravy and fat for this dish or else it will be too dry.

title: Giblet Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- giblets
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Prepare the giblets as for stewed giblets
2. They should then be laid in a deep dish, covered with a puff paste, and baked

title: Giblet Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of chuck steak
- a set of goose or turkey giblets
- a few small forced meat balls

## Directions

1. Take a pound of chuck steak and cut it in pieces the length of 2 inches long and square
2. Have a set of goose or turkey giblets well washed and cut in small pieces
3. The liver must be broiled first
4. Have ready a few small forced meat balls
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Giblet Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound chuck steak
- goose or turkey giblets
- forced meat balls
- 1 onion
- 1 tooth of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- powdered marjoram
- lemon thyme
- 1/4 rind of a lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- juice of a lemon
- cayenne
- browning
- 2 eggs
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Cut the chuck steak in pieces 2 inches long and square
2. Have the goose or turkey giblets well washed and cut in small pieces, broil the liver first
3. Have ready a few small forced meat balls, well seasoned
4. Chop an onion very small and a tooth of garlic in your stewpan
5. Add salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, some powdered marjoram and lemon thyme, and chop a quarter of the rind of a lemon
6. Lay in the steak, then the giblets, set it on the fire, and when it boils draw it to the side to simmer
7. Put in the balls when it boils
8. When done rub in a basin 1 tablespoonful of flour, the juice of a lemon, a little cayenne, and browning to colour
9. Mix it all well together
10. Have 2 eggs boiled hard and cut in quarters
11. Make a good puff paste and line the dish
12. Put in the steak at the bottom, then the giblets, the balls, and the hard egg quarters
13. Cover the dish with the paste and trim the edges neatly
14. Roll out a piece of paste, cut it in two strips, divide each strip in two to make four leaves for the top
15. Make a hole in the centre and form a piece of the dough the shape of a cork, cut the top across twice, put it in the centre of the 4 leaves
16. Wash the pie over with the yolk of an egg
17. Put it in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Giblet Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine strong well-seasoned beef stock
- two sets of giblets
- water
- white pepper
- salt
- rind of lemon
- white wine
- mushroom ketchup
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Add to a fine strong well-seasoned beef stock, of about three quarts, two sets of giblets
2. The giblets should be previously stewed separately in one quart of water
3. The gizzards require scalding for some time before they are put in with the rest
4. Season them with white pepper, salt, and the rind of lemon
5. When they are tender, add them with their gravy to the stock
6. Boil for about ten minutes together
7. Stir in a glass of white wine, a table spoonful of mushroom ketchup, and the juice of half a lemon
8. It will require to be thickened with a little flour browned
9. The giblets are served in the soup

title: Giblet Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a set of giblets
- a quart of the dark stock
- a pint of water
- a little salt and pepper
- an onion
- a little marjoram
- a pickled walnut
- a few small meat balls
- a spoonful of flour
- a spoonful of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the giblets well.
2. Skin the feet and chop them and the wings in several pieces.
3. Chop the neck in several pieces.
4. Cut the gizzard and heart in several pieces.
5. Put the giblets in a small saucepan with a pint of water, a little salt and pepper, an onion chopped fine, a little marjoram, and a pickled walnut chopped small.
6. Let them stew well and add a few small meat balls.
7. When all this is well cooked, put them into the quart of dark stock.
8. Mix a spoonful of flour with a spoonful of lemon juice in a small basin and mix it into the soup.
9. Stir it well.
10. Serve it up to table very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Giblet Stew
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. edge-bone steak
- giblets
- small forced meat balls
- pickled walnut
- neck skin
- hard boiled eggs
- forced meat
- flour
- juice of half a lemon
- walnut liquor

## Directions

1. Cut the edge-bone steak in nice pieces larger than for a pie
2. Prepare all the seasoning and put it in the stewpan, with steak at bottom and giblets on top, with small balls
3. Cut up a pickled walnut
4. Take the skin of the neck, tie one end, put in a little forced meat, then a hard boiled egg peeled, then some more meat, then another hard egg, then some more meat, fill it well
5. Tie the other end and put it to stew with the other ingredients
6. When the stew is done take out the neck and cut it in even slices
7. Stir in a basin a spoonful of flour, the juice of half a lemon, and a little of the walnut liquor
8. Turn it on the stew and mix it well
9. Put the slices of the neck round as a garnish
10. Mind not to put the gravy over the eggs, it spoils the appearance
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Giblets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- heart
- liver
- gizzard
- neck
- cold water

## Directions

1. Wash them; put them in cold water and cook until tender.
2. This will take several hours.
3. Serve with the chicken; or mash the liver, mince the heart and gizzard, and add them to the brown sauce.
4. Save the stock in which they are cooked for making the sauce.

title: Ginger Wafers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of molasses
- half a cup of cold coffee
- two teaspoons of soda
- one teaspoon of ginger
- flour

## Directions

1. Take one cup of butter, one cup of sugar, one cup of molasses, half a cup of cold coffee, with two teaspoons of soda, one teaspoon of ginger, and flour enough to make a dough stiff enough to roll out thin
2. Shape with cutter and bake in quick oven

title: Gingerbread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of soda
- one tablespoon of butter
- one or two eggs
- one teaspoon of ginger
- one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. To one cup of molasses add one cup of milk, sour or sweet
2. Dissolve one teaspoon of soda in the milk
3. Add one tablespoon of butter, one or two eggs, one teaspoon of ginger and one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
4. Add enough sifted flour to make a light batter
5. Bake in a shallow pan

title: Gingered Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds hard pears (preferably fresh green Bartlett variety)
- 4 pounds sugar
- quarter pound Canton ginger
- 1 pint of water
- 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Pare, core and cut small eight pounds hard pears
2. Combine with half as much sugar and quarter pound Canton ginger
3. Let these stand together overnight
4. In morning add one pint of water and four lemons, cut small
5. Cook slowly for three hours
6. Pour into small jars
7. Seal when cold

title: Glace for Candies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of sugar
- One-half pint of water
- One-half cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of sugar with one-half pint of water until it ropes
2. Add one-half cup of vinegar and boil until it hardens
3. Dip in fruit, orange slices, nuts or green grapes with stems on
4. Put aside on a buttered platter to set

title: Glaze
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint dark stock No. 2
- a good spoonful of browning No. 23

## Directions

1. Put the dark stock at the side of the fire in a small iron saucepan with a good spoonful of browning No. 23 and let it simmer till it comes to 1/2 pint.
2. Then put it in a nice clean pot or small basin for use.
3. Keep a small brush for it.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Globe Artichoke or Turnip Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- one and one-half pounds of sliced turnips or artichokes
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt
- three cups of hot milk
- three cups of hot water
- sugar
- cream or butter

## Directions

1. Heat two tablespoons of butter, add one and one-half pounds of sliced turnips or artichokes and stir them in the butter
2. add one tablespoon of flour, a little salt, three cups of hot milk, three cups of hot water, stirring them in slowly
3. When the vegetables are done, rub them through a sieve
4. put them back in the saucepan, add a little sugar and more seasoning, if required, and heat thoroughly
5. A little cream or butter may be put into the tureen, and the soup stirred into it

title: Gluten Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one cup of milk (scalded and cooled)
- one cup of lukewarm water
- one level tablespoon of butter
- three cups of gluten flour
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve one cake of compressed yeast and one tablespoon of sugar in one cup of milk, scalded and cooled, and one cup of lukewarm water
2. Add one level tablespoon of butter then three cups of gluten flour gradually, and one teaspoon of salt
3. Knead thoroughly until smooth and elastic
4. Place in well-greased bowl
5. Cover and set aside in a warm place, free from draught, to rise until light, which should be in about two hours
6. Mold into loaves
7. Place in greased pans, filling them half full
8. Cover, let rise again, and when double in bulk, which should be in about one hour, bake in moderate oven forty-five minutes

title: Gluten Gems
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one cup of milk
- one and one-half cups of gluten flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of two eggs
2. Add one cup of milk
3. Add one and one-half cups of gluten flour, two teaspoons of baking powder
4. Beat well
5. Stir in the whites of the two eggs
6. Bake in hot buttered gem pans about twenty minutes

title: Gold Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 6 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 1.5 teaspoons baking-powder
- 1 lemon or 1 orange
- 0.75 cup milk

## Directions

1. Take one cup of powdered sugar, one-half cup of butter rubbed to a cream
2. Add yolks of six eggs and stir until very light
3. Sift two cups of flour with one and one-half teaspoons of baking-powder sifted in well (sift the flour two or three times)
4. Grate in the peel of a lemon or an orange, add the juice also
5. Add three-quarters cup of milk alternately with the flour
6. Bake in moderate oven

title: Golden Buck
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cheese
- one-eighth pound of butter
- one-half glass of ale
- one teaspoon of mustard
- one egg well beaten
- salt
- paprika
- hot buttered toast

## Directions

1. Put butter in pan, and when melted add cheese cut up or grated
2. Stir, and as cheese melts, add ale
3. When it begins to bubble, add egg well beaten
4. Stir continually to keep from getting stringy
5. In two or three minutes it will be ready to serve
6. Pour over hot buttered toast

title: Goose Cracklings (Grieben)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- thick fat of a fat goose
- salt
- cold water
- soft or linda fat

## Directions

1. Cut the thick fat of a fat goose in pieces as big as the palm of your hand
2. Roll together and run a toothpick through each one to fasten
3. Put a large preserve kettle on top of hot stove
4. Lay in the cracklings
5. Sprinkle a tiny bit of salt over them
6. Pour in a cup or two of cold water
7. Cover closely and let cook not too fast, until water is cooked out
8. Then add the soft or linda fat
9. Keep top off and let all brown nicely
10. About one to two hours is required to cook them
11. If you do not wish the scraps of Greben brittle, take them out of the fat before they are browned
12. Place strainer over fat crock, to catch the clear fat and let greben drain
13. If greben are too greasy place in baking-pan in oven a few minutes to try out a little more
14. Serve at lunch with rye bread

title: Goose Liver
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- fine salt
- pepper
- goose-fat
- a few cloves
- a little onion
- flour
- lemon

## Directions

1. If very large cut in half
2. Dry well on a clean cloth, after having lain in salted water for an hour
3. Season with fine salt and pepper
4. Dredge the liver with flour before frying
5. Fry in very hot goose-fat and add a few cloves
6. While frying cut up a little onion very fine and add
7. Cover closely and smother until you wish to serve
8. Turn occasionally
9. Serve with a slice of lemon on each piece of liver

title: Goose Liver with Glaced Chestnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- chestnuts
- goose-fat
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare goose liver as directed in the basic recipe
2. For the chestnuts: Scald until perfectly white
3. Heat some goose-fat, add nuts, a little sugar and glaze a light brown
4. Garnish the liver with the glaced chestnuts

title: Goose Liver with Mushroom Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a large white goose liver
- salt water
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- goose-fat
- a piece of onion
- a few cloves
- a few slices of celery
- whole peppers
- one bay leaf
- mushrooms
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Lay goose liver in salt water for an hour
2. Wipe dry, salt, pepper and dredge with flour
3. Fry in hot goose-fat
4. Cut up a piece of onion, add a few cloves, a few slices of celery cut very fine, whole peppers, one bay leaf, and some mushrooms
5. Cover closely and stew a few minutes
6. Add lemon juice to sauce

title: Goose Meat, Preserved in Fat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt

## Directions

1. Render the fat of goose
2. Remove and cut the skin into small pieces
3. The scraps, when brown, shriveled and crisp, are then Greben, and are served hot or cold
4. When fat is nearly done or clear, add the breast and legs of goose, previously salted
5. Boil in the fat until tender and browned
6. Place meat in crock and pour the clear, hot fat over it to cover
7. Cool
8. Cover crock with plate and stone and keep in a cool, dry place
9. Will keep for months
10. When ready to serve, take out meat, heat, and drain off fat

title: Gooseberry Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds gooseberries
- 6 pounds sugar
- 3 cups water

## Directions

1. Cut the brush part from the berry, but leave the stem on
2. Wash thoroughly and let drip in colander overnight
3. Prepare a syrup of six pounds of sugar and three cups of water
4. When syrup has boiled till clear put in the berries and boil for three-quarters of an hour
5. Put in jars or glasses

title: Grafton Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 1.5 cups sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder

## Directions

1. Cream four tablespoons of butter with one and one-half cups of sugar
2. Beat in separately two whole eggs
3. Add one cup of milk alternately with two cups of flour in which has been sifted two teaspoons of baking-powder
4. Beat all thoroughly
5. This recipe will make two layer cakes which may be spread with any of the cake fillings or icings
6. To make small cakes omit one of the egg-whites, fill well-buttered gem pans a little more than half full, and bake in a moderately hot oven until a delicate brown
7. The white reserved may be beaten to a stiff froth and then gradually stir in four tablespoons of powdered sugar and the juice of half a lemon
8. When the cakes are cool, spread with the icing and decorate with raisins, nut meats, one on top of each or sprinkle with candied caraway seeds

title: Grafton Cake, Layers and Small Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of butter
- one and one-half cups of sugar
- two whole eggs
- one cup of milk
- two cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- four tablespoons of powdered sugar
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cream four tablespoons of butter with one and one-half cups of sugar.
2. Beat in separately two whole eggs.
3. Add one cup of milk alternately with two cups of flour in which has been sifted two teaspoons of baking-powder.
4. Beat all thoroughly.
5. To make two layer cakes, spread with any of the cake fillings or icings.
6. To make small cakes omit one of the egg-whites.
7. Fill well-buttered gem pans a little more than half full.
8. Bake in a moderately hot oven until a delicate brown.
9. Beat the reserved white to a stiff froth.
10. Gradually stir in four tablespoons of powdered sugar and the juice of half a lemon.

title: Graham Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- four tablespoons of light brown sugar or molasses
- one cup of lukewarm water
- one cup of milk (scalded and cooled to lukewarm)
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- four cups of Graham flour
- one cup of white flour (sifted)
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve one cake of compressed yeast and four tablespoons of light brown sugar or molasses in one cup of lukewarm water and one cup of milk which has been scalded and cooled to lukewarm
2. Add two tablespoons of melted butter, then four cups of Graham flour and one cup of white flour (sifted), adding flour gradually, and one teaspoon of salt
3. Knead thoroughly, being sure to keep dough soft
4. Cover and set aside in a warm place to rise for about two hours
5. When double in bulk, turn out on kneading board, mold into loaves, and place in well-greased pans
6. Cover and set to rise again—about one hour or until light
7. Bake one hour, in a slower oven than for white bread

title: Graham Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of Graham flour
- one cup of wheat flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- one-half cup of sugar
- one well-beaten egg
- one and one-half cups of milk

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of Graham flour, one cup of wheat flour, one-half teaspoon of salt, two teaspoons of baking-powder
2. Add to this one tablespoon of melted butter creamed with one-half cup of sugar and one well-beaten egg
3. Moisten with one and one-half cups of milk
4. Beat all well
5. Bake in muffin-tins in moderately hot oven one-half hour

title: Grape Conserve
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of grapes
- three juicy oranges
- five pounds of sugar
- one pound of raisins
- one pound of shelled walnuts

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and skins from five pounds of grapes and boil the pulp until tender.
2. Press it through a sieve.
3. Boil the skins of three juicy oranges until tender, then chop fine.
4. Put the grape skins and the pulp into a saucepan.
5. Add the orange juice, the boiled skins, five pounds of sugar, one pound of raisins (the muscat seeded), and one pound of shelled walnuts.
6. Boil until quite thick.

title: Grape Fruit
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grape fruit
- sugar
- white wine or sherry
- powdered sugar
- maraschino cherry

## Directions

1. Cut in half with a sharp knife
2. Remove seeds
3. Loosen pulp
4. Cut out pithy white centre
5. Wipe knife after each cutting, so that the bitter taste may be avoided
6. Pour in white wine or sherry and sprinkle with powdered sugar
7. Let stand several hours in ice-chest to ripen
8. Serve cold in the shell
9. Decorate with maraschino cherry

title: Grape Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Weigh the grapes on the stems and for every pound of grapes thus weighed allow three-quarters of a pound of the best quality of granulated sugar.
2. After weighing the grapes, place them in a big tub or receptacle of some kind nearly filled with cold water.
3. Let them remain ten minutes, then lift them out with both hands and put them in a preserving kettle over a very low fire.
4. Do not add any water.
5. With a masher press the grapes so the juice comes out, and cook the grapes until they are rather soft, pressing them frequently with the masher.
6. When they have cooked until the skins are all broken, pour them, juice and all, in a small-holed colander set in a big bowl, and press pulp and juice through, picking out the stems as they come to the surface.
7. When pulp and juice are pressed out, pour them into a cheese-cloth bag.
8. Hang the bag over the preserving kettle and let the juice drip all night.
9. In the morning put the kettle over the fire and let the grape juice boil gently for a half hour, skimming it frequently.
10. While the juice is cooking put the sugar in pans in a moderate oven and let heat.
11. As soon as the juice is skimmed clear stir in the hot sugar, and as soon as it is dissolved pour the jelly in the glasses, first standing them in warm water.
12. Place glasses after filling them in a cool dry place till jelly is well set, then pour a film of melted paraffin over the top and put on the covers.
13. Label.

title: Grape Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes
- sugar

## Directions

1. Squeeze the pulp into one bowl and put the skins into another.
2. Press the pulp through a sieve.
3. Weigh the grapes before you squeeze them and allow three-quarters of a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit.
4. Put the strained pulp and sugar on to boil, the skins also, and boil slowly until thick.

title: Grape-Fruit Cocktail
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grape-fruit
- powdered sugar
- sherry
- maraschino cherry or bright-colored jelly
- paper doilies or asparagus fern

## Directions

1. Cut the grape-fruit into halves, crosswise, and scoop out the pulp, rejecting the white inner skin as well as the seeds
2. Clean the shells
3. cut the edges with a sharp knife into scallops and throw them into cold water
4. Set the pulp on the ice
5. At serving time put a teaspoon of cracked ice in the bottom of each shell
6. fill with the pulp, mixed thoroughly with powdered sugar and a little sherry, if desired
7. place a maraschino cherry or bit of bright-colored jelly in the centre of each
8. Lay on paper doilies or surround with bits of asparagus fern

title: Grape-Fruit Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grape-fruit
- bananas
- oranges
- white mayonnaise dressing
- maraschino cherries
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the grape-fruit in halves and remove the pulp, being careful to get none of the tough white skin
2. Mix with bananas and oranges and stir in white mayonnaise dressing
3. Remove all skin from the inside of the grape-fruit and fill with the mixture, heaping it high and ornamenting with maraschino cherries
4. Lay each half in a bed of lettuce leaves and serve

title: Grated Apple Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich puff paste
- four or five large tart apples
- yolks of two eggs
- about half a cup of sugar
- a few raisins
- a few currants
- a few pounded almonds
- a pinch of ground cinnamon
- grated peel of a lemon
- whites of the eggs
- about three tablespoons of pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with a rich puff paste
2. Pare and grate four or five large tart apples into a bowl into which you have stirred the yolks of two eggs with about half a cup of sugar
3. Add a few raisins, a few currants, a few pounded almonds, a pinch of ground cinnamon, and the grated peel of a lemon
4. Bake in a quick oven with no top crust
5. In the meantime, make a meringue of the whites of the eggs by beating them to a very stiff froth and add about three tablespoons of pulverized sugar
6. Spread this over the pie when baked and set back in the oven until brown
7. Eat cold

title: Grated Apple Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six good-sized apples
- six yolks of eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half pound of grated almonds or one-half cup of matzoth-meal
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- about three tablespoons of chicken or goose grease

## Directions

1. Pare the apples and leave them whole
2. Grate all the apple from the pulp
3. Add the yolks, almonds or matzoth-meal, salt, cinnamon and grease
4. Add the whites well beaten to a stiff froth
5. Mix very light
6. Bake in well-greased baking dish

title: Grated Apple Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six good-sized apples
- six yolks of eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half pound of grated almonds or one-half cup of matzoth-meal
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take six good-sized apples, six yolks of eggs, one-half cup of sugar (or to taste), one-half pound of grated almonds, or one-half cup of matzoth-meal, one-half teaspoon of salt, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
2. Pare the apples and leave them whole
3. Grate all the apple from the pulp
4. Add the remaining ingredients to the grated apple

title: Grated Irish Potato
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large Irish potato, or two medium-sized ones
- hot water
- white of one egg
- yolk of one egg

## Directions

1. Peel, wash and grate one large Irish potato, or two medium-sized ones.
2. Put it in a sieve and let hot water run over it until it is perfectly white.
3. Have the white of one egg beaten to a very stiff froth, then stir in the potatoes and twenty minutes before serving add it to the boiling soup.
4. Beat the yolk of one egg up in the soup tureen, and pour the hot soup over it, stirring carefully at first.

title: Gravy for a Fowl, When There is No Stock to Make It With
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- the feet of a fowl
- the neck and gizzard
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- parsley
- a breakfast-cup of water
- flour
- a little browning
- a small quantity of any store sauce

## Directions

1. Take the feet, wash them, cut them small, also the neck and gizzard
2. Season them with pepper and salt, onion, and parsley
3. Let them simmer gently for some time, in about a breakfast-cup of water
4. Strain
5. Thicken with flour
6. Add a little browning, and if liked, a small quantity of any store sauce at hand

title: Gravy Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about three quarts of any strong stock
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- a carrot
- turnip
- a head of celery
- Vermicelli, maccaroni, or thin slices of carrot and small sippets of fried bread cut in fancy shapes

## Directions

1. Take about three quarts of any strong stock, seasoned with a bunch of sweet herbs, a carrot, turnip, and a head of celery, which must not be served in the soup
2. Serve with Vermicelli, maccaroni, or thin slices of carrot and small sippets of fried bread cut in fancy shapes

title: Gravy Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of any strong stock
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- a carrot
- a turnip
- a head of celery
- vermicelli, maccaroni, or thin slices of carrot and small sippets of fried bread cut in fancy shapes

## Directions

1. Season the stock with a bunch of sweet herbs, a carrot, turnip, and a head of celery, which must not be served in the soup
2. Serve vermicelli, maccaroni, or thin slices of carrot and small sippets of fried bread cut in fancy shapes in this soup

title: Gravy Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lb. of gravy beef
- 2 or 3 cloves
- an onion
- a tooth of garlic
- salt and pepper
- stale crust of bread
- 1 pint and a half of stock 2
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a little browning
- a few meat balls

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan 2-lb. of gravy beef, cut small, with 2 or 3 cloves, an onion, a tooth of garlic, salt and pepper
2. Let it boil for 2 hours
3. If you have any stale crust of bread put it in also
4. Rub it all well through a sieve
5. Put to it pint and a half of stock 2
6. Rub a tablespoonful of flour with a little browning to colour
7. Turn it into the tureen with a few meat balls very small
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Green Corn, Tomatoes and Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of melted butter
- two cups of grated cheese
- three-quarters of a cup of canned or grated fresh corn
- one ripe green pepper
- one egg yolk
- one-half cup of tomato puree
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of paprika
- five slices of bread

## Directions

1. Into one tablespoon of melted butter stir two cups of grated cheese until it, too, is melted
2. Add three-quarters of a cup of canned or grated fresh corn
3. Add one ripe green pepper, stir them
4. Add one egg yolk mixed with one-half cup of tomato puree
5. Add one teaspoon of salt
6. Add one-half teaspoon of paprika
7. Toast five slices of bread and pour this mixture over it
8. Serve hot

title: Green Dill Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small firm green tomatoes
- dill
- whole peppers
- bay leaves
- whole ginger
- cloves
- horseradish
- salt water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Select small firm green tomatoes
2. Follow recipe for Dill Pickles, using the green tomatoes in place of the pickles

title: Green Kern Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of green kern
- water
- two pounds of soup meat
- a carrot
- an onion
- a stalk of celery
- a sprig of parsley
- one or two tomatoes
- a potato
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak one-half cup of green kern in a bowl of water over night
2. Put on two pounds of soup meat, add a carrot, an onion, a stalk of celery, a sprig of parsley, one or two tomatoes, a potato, in fact any vegetable you may happen to have at hand
3. Cover up closely and let it boil slowly over a low heat three or four hours
4. Put the green kern on to boil in water slightly salted
5. As it boils down keep adding soup stock from the kettle of soup on the stove, always straining through a hair sieve, until all has been used
6. Serve as it is or strain through a colander and put pieces of toasted bread into the soup

title: Green Lima Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled beans
- boiling water
- salt
- pepper
- butter or hot cream or cream sauce

## Directions

1. Cover the shelled beans with boiling water
2. Bring to a boil quickly
3. Then let them simmer slowly till tender
4. Drain and add salt, pepper and butter or hot cream or cream sauce

title: Green or Yellow Plum Tomato Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of small yellow or green tomatoes
- three pounds of sugar
- one-half cup boiling water
- juice and rind of two lemons

## Directions

1. Wash and dry four pounds of small yellow or green tomatoes and prick each one in five or six places
2. Stir three pounds of sugar in one-half cup boiling water until dissolved
3. Add the tomatoes and cook until clear
4. When half done add the juice and the rind of two lemons sliced very thin
5. When the fruit is clear remove it with a skimmer and put in small jars, filling them two-thirds full
6. Boil the syrup fast for a few minutes longer or until thick and syrupy, fill up the jars
7. Cover with a cloth until the next day then cover closely and stand away in a cool place

title: Green Pea Puree
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of green peas
- one quart of milk
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook one quart of green peas until very tender
2. Then mash through colander
3. To this amount heat one quart of milk in double boiler
4. Add butter, salt and pepper to taste, and last the mashed green peas

title: Green Pea Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- soup stock
- one pint washed peapods
- one tablespoon drippings
- peas
- chopped parsley
- one teaspoon salt
- two teaspoons sugar
- drop dumplings

## Directions

1. Make your soup stock as usual, adding a pint of washed peapods to the soup
2. Heat a tablespoon of drippings, put in the peas, with a little chopped parsley
3. Cover closely and let simmer; keep adding soup stock when dry
4. When the peas are tender put into the strained soup
5. Season with one teaspoon of salt and two teaspoons of sugar
6. Add drop dumplings to this soup before serving

title: Green Peas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- water
- salt
- butter
- one teaspoon of sugar (if peas are not the sweet variety)

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and cover them with water
2. Bring to a boil
3. Push aside until the water will just bubble gently
4. Keep the lid partly off
5. When the peas are tender add salt and butter
6. Cook ten minutes longer and serve
7. If the peas are not the sweet variety, add one teaspoon of sugar

title: Green Peas and Egg Barley (Pfarvel)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pfarvel
- one-quarter cup of butter or other fat
- one quart of boiling water
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one can or one-half peck of green peas strained

## Directions

1. Make the pfarvel
2. Heat one-quarter cup of butter or other fat, add the pfarvel and when golden brown
3. Add one quart of boiling water, one-half cup of sugar, one-half teaspoon of salt, and one can or one-half peck of green peas strained
4. Set in moderate oven and bake one-half hour or until every kernel stands out separately
5. Serve hot

title: Green Peas and Kleis
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck of peas
- 1/2 pint of milk
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1 oz. of butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Pick the peas, wash them well, let them drain
2. Put them in a saucepan with 1/2 pint of milk, seasoned with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
3. Let it simmer till the peas are cooked
4. Mix in a basin 1 oz. of butter, 2 eggs, with two spoonfuls of flour into a very loose paste
5. As the peas and milk boil, take up a dessert spoonful at the time of the mixture, drop it into the peas
6. The kleis will adhere to the peas; dish it up in a deep dish very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Green Peas and Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half peck of green peas
- one-fourth cup of butter or other fat
- one cup of rice
- one quart of boiling water
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Shell one-half peck of green peas and wash them well; if canned peas are used pour off liquid and rinse with cold water
2. Heat one-fourth cup of butter or other fat in a spider, add one cup of rice and let simmer, stirring constantly until rice is a golden brown
3. Add one quart of boiling water, then the drained peas and one-half teaspoon of salt, and one-half cup of granulated sugar
4. Place in pudding dish, set in the oven and bake until rice is tender
5. Serve hot

title: Green Peppers for Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- whole, green sweet pepper
- boiling water
- vinegar
- salt
- sugar
- oil

## Directions

1. Put whole, green sweet pepper in boiling water and cook until tender
2. Place on platter and drain
3. Make a dressing of vinegar, salt, sugar and oil
4. Serve

title: Green Peppers Stuffed with Vegetables
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large white onions
- uncooked rice
- salt
- citric acid
- water
- green peppers
- tomatoes
- red cabbage
- onions
- fat

## Directions

1. Brown large white onions
2. add one-half cup of uncooked rice
3. add a little salt and piece of citric acid (size of a bean dissolved in a little water)
4. fill peppers
5. stew with tomatoes
6. Or fill peppers with red cabbage which has been steamed with onions and fat and add moistened rice

title: Green Peppers Stuffed with Vegetables
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large white onions
- one-half cup of uncooked rice
- salt
- citric acid (size of a bean dissolved in a little water)
- tomatoes
- red cabbage

## Directions

1. Brown large white onions, add one-half cup of uncooked rice, a little salt, piece of citric acid (size of a bean dissolved in a little water)
2. Fill peppers, stew with tomatoes like Arday-influs
3. Or fill peppers with red cabbage which has been prepared

title: Green Salads
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- imported or domestic endive, chicory, escarole and Romaine or lettuce
- French dressing
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Wash endive, chicory, escarole and Romaine or lettuce, make crisp in cold water, and dry in a bag on the ice. Serve them with French dressing. Imported endive may be served with mayonnaise, if desired.

title: Green Tomato Pickle (French Pickle)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a peck of green tomatoes
- eight large white onions
- six green-bell peppers
- a pint of salt
- a pint of cold water
- a quart of cider vinegar
- a half pint of tarragon vinegar
- a pound of granulated sugar
- a half pound of yellow mustard seeds
- four bay leaves
- an ounce of stick cinnamon
- six whole cloves

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly a peck of green tomatoes, eight large white onions and six green-bell peppers
2. Remove the seeds from the peppers
3. Slice all the vegetables very thin
4. Put them in a stone jar
5. Sprinkle a pint of salt over them, add a pint of cold water
6. Cover them with a napkin and let stand overnight
7. In the morning put as much of the pickle as it will hold in a colander
8. Let cold water run over
9. Drain the vegetables a moment, then turn them from the colander into a large preserving kettle
10. Repeat the process till all are in the kettle
11. Then add a quart of cider vinegar, a half pint of tarragon vinegar, a pound of granulated sugar, a half pound of yellow mustard seeds, four bay leaves, an ounce of stick cinnamon (broken in short lengths), six whole cloves
12. Stand the kettle over a slow fire and let the whole simmer for an hour with the cover of the kettle drawn back two inches
13. Stir the mixture frequently
14. At the end of the hour put the pickle in a stone crock or in glass jars

title: Green Tree Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of granulated sugar
- one-half cup of butter
- three eggs
- one cup of milk
- two and one-half scant cups of sifted flour
- one teaspoon of vanilla extract
- two teaspoons of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar together as usual
2. break in three eggs and beat until very creamy
3. Add the flour and milk alternately, reserving a little of the flour to add after the vanilla and baking-powder
4. Beat well and bake in layer cake tins

title: Green Tree Layer Cake Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of confectioner's sugar
- butter the size of a large egg
- two tablespoons of cocoa
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- cream or whipped cream or lukewarm coffee

## Directions

1. Cream the sugar and butter together thoroughly
2. add the cocoa and vanilla
3. Lastly add the cream or coffee to make the mixture the consistence of very thick cream
4. Mark the icing to represent the bark of a tree
5. Add a little green candy on it

title: Grimslich
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a loaf of bread, soaked and pressed
- two eggs
- one-half cup sugar
- one-fourth cup raisins
- one tablespoon cinnamon
- one-fourth cup almonds pounded fine

## Directions

1. Beat whites to a froth and add last
2. Drop by tablespoonful and fry
3. Serve with stewed fruit

title: Grimstich
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of biscuit powder
- brown sugar
- grated lemon peel
- six eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of warmed fresh butter
- four apples
- a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds blanched and chopped
- half a pound of stoned raisins
- nutmeg
- half a pound of coarse brown sugar
- a glass of white wine or brandy
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make into a stiff paste one pint of biscuit powder, a little brown sugar, grated lemon peel, six eggs, and three-quarters of a pound of warmed fresh butter
2. Prepare four apples chopped finely, a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds blanched and chopped, half a pound of stoned raisins, a little nutmeg grated, half a pound of coarse brown sugar, and a glass of white wine or a little brandy
3. Mix the above ingredients together and put them on a slow fire to simmer for half an hour
4. Place in a dish to cool
5. Make the paste into the form of small dumplings and fill them with the fruit
6. Bake them
7. When put in the oven, pour over a syrup of brown sugar and water flavored with lemon juice

title: Ground Rice and Milk
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 2 ozs. of ground rice
- 1/2 pint more milk
- 2 ozs. of loaf sugar
- cinnamon
- 2 yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pint of milk with whatever flavouring you like
2. Mix 2 ozs. of ground rice with 1/2 pint more milk and 2 ozs. of loaf sugar
3. Let it boil till it thickens
4. Turn it out and put cinnamon over
5. Stir in 2 yolks of eggs to improve it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Ground Rice Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. ground rice
- a cup of cold milk
- 1 pint of milk
- 1/2 lb. of fresh butter
- 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar
- a tablespoonful of orange-flower water
- the yolks of 3 eggs
- the whites of 2 eggs
- 2 oz. of lemon and orange peel
- good puff paste
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Take a 1/2 lb. ground rice mixed with a cup of cold milk
2. Boil 1 pint of milk, stir in the rice, let it boil till it thickens
3. Put it out to get cold
4. Put in 1/2 lb. of fresh butter, 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar, a tablespoonful of orange-flower water, the yolks of 3 eggs, and the whites of 2
5. Add 2 oz. of lemon and orange peel cut in slices
6. Make a good puff paste, line the dish
7. Put in the rice, put over lemon and orange peel
8. Crossbar it with strips of paste, and between each put half of a blanched almond
9. Bake in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Gum Arabic Bandoline
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a tea-spoonful of gum arabic
- a little Irish moss
- half a pint of water

## Directions

1. Take a tea-spoonful of gum arabic with a little Irish moss
2. boil them in half a pint of water till half is boiled away
3. strain and perfume

title: Gurnet Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- gurnet
- water
- salt
- pepper
- blade of mace
- dried saffron
- vegetables
- rice or macaroni or vermicelli
- fish balls
- German kleis
- cauliflower
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a fine gurnet and put it in a saucepan with a quart of water, salt, pepper, a blade of mace, a little dried saffron
2. Cut up some vegetables and put it on
3. Wash a lb. of rice or 1/2 lb of macaroni or vermicelli
4. Make a few fish balls, small, or a few German kleis
5. The kleis must only be dropped in 10 minutes before going to table
6. Take out the fish, as it will not require boiling so long as the ingredients, or put your ingredients on first, then half an hour before dinner you can put in the fish, and 10 minutes before the kleis
7. Dish up the fish with a boiled cauliflower cut round, and pour over a good sauce made with butter
8. Before turning the soup out, rub in a basin 1/4 lb. of fresh butter with a spoonful of flour
9. Stir it in the soup, then turn it in the tureen or basin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Gurnet Stewed with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- gurnet
- onions
- tomatoes
- oil or butter
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- lemon
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the gurnet the same as the haddock
2. Slice the onions and tomatoes at once with 2 spoonfuls of oil, or 1/4 lb. of butter, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
3. Use no water to any fish that is stewed with tomatoes
4. When done, take out the fish
5. Rub the sauce through a sieve
6. Add the juice of a lemon and cayenne pepper
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Haddock Stewed with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddock
- tomatoes
- parsley

## Directions

1. Stew the haddock and tomatoes exactly like the sole and tomatoes
2. Garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Hair Lotion
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tea-spoonful of honey
- one tea-spoonful of spirits of wine
- one tea-spoonful of rosemary
- half a pint of rose-water or elder flower-water
- half a pint of soft water

## Directions

1. Mix one tea-spoonful of honey, one of spirits of wine, one of rosemary in half a pint of rose-water or elder flower-water, and the same quantity of soft water

title: Hair Pomatum
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of mutton suet and marrow
- sweet oil
- perfume

## Directions

1. Melt down equal quantities of mutton suet and marrow, uncooked
2. add a little sweet oil to make it of a proper consistency
3. add any perfume desired

title: Halibut or Cod Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mayonnaise
- halibut or cod
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare just the same as salmon sandwiches
2. Remember to put a little salt and pepper on all fish sandwiches
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Haman's Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of the best Florence oil
- one pound of flour
- four beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Scald the oil
2. When hot, mix with it the flour
3. Add four beaten eggs and make it into a paste
4. Roll it out thin and cut it into pieces about four inches square
5. Let them dry and fry them in oil
6. The moment the pieces are put in the frying pan, they must be drawn up with two silver skewers into different forms according to fancy
7. Fry for a few minutes until crisp

title: Hamburger Steak
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of raw beef
- salt
- pepper
- onion

## Directions

1. Take one pound of raw beef, cut off fat and stringy pieces and chop extremely fine.
2. Season with salt and pepper and grate in part of an onion or fry with onions.
3. Make into round cakes a little less than one-half inch thick.
4. Heat pan blue hot and grease lightly.
5. Add cakes and count sixty, then turn them and cook on the other side until brown.
6. When well browned they are done if liked rare, or cook ten minutes if liked well done.

title: Hard Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one-half cup of sweet butter
- grated lemon peel or essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Take one cup of sugar, one-half cup of sweet butter and stir to a cream
2. Flavor with grated lemon peel or essence of lemon
3. Make into any shape desired
4. serve

title: Haricot Beans and Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of haricot beans
- two pints of water
- one onion
- fat
- one pound of beef
- one carrot
- turnip
- salt
- pepper
- pea meal or flour
- vinegar
- potatoes
- green vegetables

## Directions

1. Wash two cups of haricot beans and leave them covered with two pints of water overnight
2. Next day brown one coarsely chopped onion in a little fat and put it with the beans and their water into a casserole or stew-jar
3. Cook closely covered and rather slowly in the oven or by the side of the fire one hour
4. then put in a pound of beef in fairly large pieces
5. An hour later add one carrot cut into dice, half as many dice of turnip, and salt and pepper to taste
6. Continue the slow cooking until these vegetables are tender
7. a few minutes before serving thicken the stew with pea meal or flour previously baked to a fawn color
8. Flavor with vinegar
9. serve with an abundance of potatoes and green vegetables

title: Haricot Mutton Chops
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 chops
- carrot
- turnip
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- 2 or 3 lumps of sugar
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Put 5 or 6 chops cut short in a baking tin in the oven
2. When they get a little brown take them out
3. Have in a stewpan cooking one large carrot and 1 turnip cut in strips about 2 inches long and 1 wide, with a chopped onion, pepper, salt, a little grated nutmeg
4. When this is nearly done put in the chops with 2 or 3 lumps of sugar
5. Let it all stew together
6. Skim off the fat
7. Rub a spoonful of flour with a little cold water and add to the pan
8. Dish up very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Hartshorn Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- hartshorn shavings
- water
- sherry wine
- white sugar
- eggs
- lemon juice
- saffron

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of hartshorn shavings in two quarts of water over a gentle fire until it becomes thick enough to hang about a spoon
2. Strain it into a clean saucepan and add half a pint of sherry wine, and a quarter of a pound of white sugar
3. Clear it by stirring in the whites of a couple of eggs, whisked to a froth
4. Boil it for about four or five minutes
5. Add the juice of three lemons, and stir all together
6. When it is well curdled, strain it and pour into the mould
7. If the color is required to be deeper than the wine will make it, a little saffron may be boiled in it

title: Hashed Brown Potatoes, Lyonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six cold boiled potatoes
- one tablespoon butter
- one onion
- one-half teaspoon salt
- two saltspoons white pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Finely hash up six cold boiled potatoes and keep on a plate
2. Heat one tablespoon of butter in a frying-pan
3. Add a finely chopped onion and lightly brown for three minutes
4. Add the potatoes
5. Season with one-half teaspoon of salt and two saltspoons of white pepper, evenly sprinkled over
6. Nicely brown them for ten minutes, occasionally tossing them meanwhile
7. Give them a nice omelet form
8. Brown for eight minutes more
9. Turn on a hot dish
10. Sprinkle a little freshly chopped parsley over and serve

title: Hashed Calf's Lung and Heart
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lung
- heart
- water
- soup meat
- one tablespoon of goose fat
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- soup stock
- flour
- vinegar
- brown sugar
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- one tablespoon of molasses

## Directions

1. Lay the lung and heart in water for half an hour and then put on to boil in a soup kettle with your soup meat intended for dinner.
2. When soft, remove from the soup and chop up quite fine.
3. Heat one tablespoon of goose fat in a spider; chop up an onion very fine and add to the heated fat.
4. When yellow, add the hashed lung and heart, salt, pepper, soup stock and thicken with flour.
5. You may prepare this sweet and sour by adding a little vinegar and brown sugar, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon and one tablespoon of molasses.
6. Boil slowly; keep covered until ready to serve.

title: Hashed Turnips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- water
- flour
- meat stock or milk

## Directions

1. Chop the drained turnips into rather large pieces
2. Return to the stew-pan
3. For one and one-half pints of turnips add one teaspoon of salt, one-fourth teaspoon of pepper, one tablespoon of butter, and four tablespoons of water
4. Cook over a very hot fire until the turnips have absorbed all the seasonings
5. Serve at once
6. Or the salt, pepper, butter, and one tablespoon of flour may be added to the hashed turnips
7. Then the stew-pan may be placed over the hot fire and shaken frequently to toss up the turnips
8. When the turnips have been cooking five minutes in this manner add one-half pint of meat stock or of milk
9. Cook ten minutes
10. When meat or soup stock is used substitute drippings for the butter

title: Hasty Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- any kind of old cake
- wine
- one pint of milk
- four eggs
- one tablespoon of potato flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut any kind of old cake in slices and dip in wine or sprinkle some wine over all
2. Make a custard with one pint of milk and four eggs
3. Put one tablespoon of potato flour with the yolks, sweeten to taste
4. Boil the custard, flavor and pour over cake in pudding dish
5. Beat whites to a stiff froth, add sugar and spread over all
6. Put in oven to brown slightly
7. Eat cold

title: Hecht (Pickerel)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- celery roots
- parsley roots
- onion
- fresh butter
- salt
- white pepper
- peppercorns
- water
- lemon
- almonds
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean your fish thoroughly and salt the day previous
2. Wrap it in a clean towel and lay it on ice until wanted
3. Line a kettle with celery and parsley roots
4. Cut up an onion, add a lump of fresh butter, and pack the fish in the kettle, head first, either whole or cut up
5. Sprinkle a little salt and white pepper over all and add about a dozen peppercorns
6. Put on enough water to just cover, and add a whole lemon cut in slices
7. Do not let the fish boil quickly
8. Add about a dozen pounded almonds
9. Turn the fish when ready
10. Beat up the yolks of two eggs in a bowl to be added to the sauce after the fish is boiled
11. Try the fish with a fork and if the meat loosens readily it is done
12. Take up each piece carefully, if it has been cut up, and arrange on a large platter, head first and so on, make the fish appear whole
13. Garnish with the slices of lemon and sprigs of parsley
14. Mince up some parsley and garnish top of the fish, around the lemon slices
15. Thicken the gravy by adding the beaten yolks, add a tablespoon of cold water to the yolks before adding to the boiling sauce
16. Stir, remove from the fire at once and pour over the fish

title: Herb Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- two tablespoons of cold water
- a quarter of a teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- a teaspoon of chopped parsley
- a quarter of a teaspoon of grated onion
- a teaspoon of fine butter

## Directions

1. Take six eggs and beat well in a bowl.
2. Add two tablespoons of cold water and a quarter of a teaspoon of salt, a pinch of pepper, a teaspoon of chopped parsley, a quarter of a teaspoon of grated onion and a teaspoon of fine butter, shaved in little pieces.
3. Mix well with a wooden spoon.
4. Dissolve in the spider the butter and add at once the beaten eggs, etc., inclining the spider to the handle for an instant and then

title: Herb Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- two tablespoons of cold water
- a quarter of a teaspoon of salt
- a pinch of pepper
- a teaspoon of chopped parsley
- a quarter of a teaspoon of grated onion
- a teaspoon of fine butter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well in a bowl
2. Add cold water, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, grated onion and butter
3. Mix well with a wooden spoon
4. Dissolve butter in the spider
5. Add at once the beaten eggs
6. Incline the spider to the handle for an instant
7. Shake the omelet into the centre
8. Turn up the right edge, then the left
9. Fry briskly five minutes
10. Serve

title: Herring Salad, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four herrings
- two apples
- one-half cup of coarsely chopped almonds
- one onion
- one-half cup of vinegar
- one teaspoon of sugar
- pinch of pepper
- sliced lemons

## Directions

1. Soak four herrings in cold water overnight, and then rinse several times in fresh cold water.
2. Skin, bone, and cut in one-half inch pieces.
3. Peel two apples, and cut in dice.
4. Mix with herring, then add one-half cup of coarsely chopped almonds and one onion chopped fine.
5. Remove the milsner or soft egg from the inside of herring, and mash perfectly smooth.
6. Add one-half cup of vinegar, one teaspoon of sugar, pinch of pepper.
7. Mix well, and then pour over herring, stirring with a fork to prevent mashing.
8. Set in ice-box until ready to serve.
9. Put sliced lemons on top.

title: Herring Salad, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three nice herrings
- one-half cup of English walnuts
- one tablespoon of boiled beets
- two tablespoons of capers
- one large apple
- one dill pickle
- two cups of white vinegar
- one teaspoon of sugar
- three cloves
- allspice
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Soak three nice herrings in cold water three hours.
2. Remove the head and tail and bones.
3. With a scissors cut in pieces as small as dice, add one-half cup of English walnuts cut fine, one tablespoon of boiled beets cut fine, two tablespoons of capers, one large apple cut in small pieces and one dill pickle cut up.
4. Take the soft egg (milchner) and mix with two cups of white vinegar until soft, add one teaspoon of sugar, three cloves and allspice and pour the sauce over the ingredients.
5. Mix all well together, and serve a spoonful on a lettuce leaf for each person.

title: Home Made Yeast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large raw potatoes
- one gallon of water
- one and one-half cups of hops
- a scant cup of sugar
- one-half cup of salt
- a yeast cake or a cup of fresh yeast

## Directions

1. Grate six large raw potatoes
2. Have ready a gallon of water in which you have boiled one and one-half cups of hops
3. Strain through a fine hair sieve, boiling hot, over the potatoes, stirring well, or the mixture will thicken like starch
4. Add a scant cup of sugar and one-half cup of salt
5. When cold, add a yeast cake or a cup of fresh yeast
6. Let it stand until a thick foam rises on the top
7. Bottle in a few days
8. If kept in a cool place, this yeast will last a long time
9. Use one cup of yeast for one large baking

title: Home-Made Chicken Tamales
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large chicken
- stock
- one cup of corn meal or farina
- one tablespoon of chili sauce
- three tablespoons of tomato catsup
- salt
- one teaspoon of Spanish pepper sauce

## Directions

1. Boil till tender one large chicken
2. Have two quarts of stock left when chicken is done
3. Remove chicken and cut into medium-sized pieces
4. Into the stock pour gradually one cup of corn meal or farina, stirring until it thickens
5. If not the proper consistency, add a little more meal
6. Season with one tablespoon of chili sauce, three tablespoons of tomato catsup, salt, one teaspoon of Spanish pepper sauce
7. Simmer gently thirty minutes
8. then add chicken
9. Serve in ramekins

title: Hominy
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- unbroken hominy
- water
- two tablespoons of butter
- one teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons of cream
- sugar (for serving)

## Directions

1. Get the unbroken hominy and after careful washing soak it twenty-four hours in the water
2. Cook one cup of hominy slowly in the same water in a covered vessel for eight hours or until all the water has been absorbed by the hominy
3. Add two tablespoons of butter, one teaspoon of salt and two tablespoons of cream and serve as a vegetable or as a cereal with sugar and cream

title: Honey Cakes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of real honey, not jar
- one cup of granulated sugar
- four eggs
- one tablespoon of allspice
- three tablespoons of salad-oil
- four cups of flour, well sifted
- three teaspoons of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Warm up or heat honey, not hot, just warm.
2. Rub yolks well with sugar, beat whites to a froth.
3. Mix ingredients, add flour and bake in moderate oven for one hour.

title: Honey Cakes, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
