- one cup of sugar
- one cup of honey
- one cup of blanched almonds chopped finely
- one teaspoon each of allspice, cloves, and cinnamon
- one cup of chocolate
- flour enough to make a thick batter
- one teaspoon of baking-soda

## Directions

1. Spread very thin on square, buttered pans.
2. Bake in a hot oven.
3. When done, spread with a white icing, cut into squares, and put a half blanched almond in the centre of each square.

title: Honey Corn Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of pure honey
- one pound of cornmeal
- ground allspice
- cloves
- pepper
- one wineglass of brandy
- white flour
- one pound of chopped nuts
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of pure honey
2. Take one pound of cornmeal mixed with a little ground allspice, cloves, and pepper
3. Add the boiled honey
4. Make a loose batter
5. Add one wineglass of brandy
6. Mix all, and cool
7. Wet the hands with cold water, take pieces of the dough and knead until the dough comes clear from the hand
8. Afterwards knead with white flour so it is not too hard
9. Add one pound of chopped nuts
10. Sprinkle flour on tins, spread dough, not too thin
11. Leave the stove door open till it raises
12. Then close door, and when done take out
13. Spread with brandy and cut in thin slices

title: Honey Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of honey
- six ounces of bread crumbs
- one-half cup of milk
- one-half teaspoon of ginger
- grated rind of half a lemon
- yolks of two eggs
- two tablespoons of butter
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Mix one-half cup of honey with six ounces of bread crumbs
2. Add one-half cup of milk, one-half teaspoon of ginger, and grated rind of half a lemon
3. Add yolks of two eggs
4. Beat the mixture thoroughly
5. Add two tablespoons of butter and the whites of the eggs well beaten
6. Steam for about two hours in a pudding mold which is not more than three-quarters full

title: Horseradish Sauce for Boiled Meat
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- an egg
- salt
- pepper
- a cup of clear broth
- scraped horseradish
- a little vinegar
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat an egg and season it with salt and pepper
2. Stir in a cup of clear broth and make it smooth
3. Put in the scraped horseradish with a little vinegar and grated nutmeg
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Horseradish Sauce, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a good-sized stick of horseradish
- soup stock
- a tablespoon of fat
- salt
- pepper
- a little grated stale bread
- a few pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Grate a good-sized stick of horseradish
2. Take some soup stock and a tablespoon of fat, salt and pepper to taste, a little grated stale bread, a few pounded almonds
3. Let all boil up and then add the meat

title: Horseradish Sauce, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of fat
- one-quarter of an onion
- one tablespoon cracker meal or flour
- two tablespoons of grated horseradish
- soup stock
- one tablespoon of brown sugar
- two cloves
- two bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons of vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat one tablespoon of fat in a frying-pan
2. When hot cut up one-quarter of an onion in it, and fry light brown
3. Brown one tablespoon cracker meal or flour and add two tablespoons of grated horseradish
4. Let this brown a bit, then add some soup stock, one tablespoon of brown sugar, two cloves, two bay leaves, salt, pepper and two tablespoons of vinegar
5. Let cook a few minutes then add one more tablespoon of horseradish and if necessary a little more sugar or vinegar
6. Lay the meat in this sauce and cover on back of stove until ready to serve
7. If gas stove is used, place over the simmering flame

title: Hot Chocolate
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces unsweetened chocolate
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- stick cinnamon
- 1 cup boiling water
- 3 cups hot milk
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Scrape two ounces of unsweetened chocolate very fine
2. Add three tablespoons of sugar, small piece of stick cinnamon and one cup of boiling water
3. Stir over moderate heat until smooth
4. Add three cups of hot milk
5. Return to the fire for a minute, do not let it boil
6. Remove and add one teaspoon of vanilla
7. Beat with an egg-beater and serve

title: Hot Slaw
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- one tablespoon of butter
- one-half to one cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into thin shreds as for cold slaw.
2. Boil it until tender in salted fast-boiling water.
3. Drain it thoroughly, and pour over it a hot sauce made of one tablespoon of butter, one-half teaspoon of salt, dash of pepper and of cayenne, and one-half to one cup of vinegar, according to its strength.
4. Cover the saucepan and let it stand on the side of the range for five minutes, so that the cabbage and sauce will become well incorporated.

title: Hot Wine (Glueh)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart claret
- 1 pint water
- 2 cups sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon whole cloves
- 1 teaspoon whole cinnamon
- lemon rind

## Directions

1. Mix one quart claret, one pint water, two cups of sugar, one-half teaspoon of whole cloves, one teaspoon of whole cinnamon, lemon rind cut thin and in small pieces
2. Boil steadily for fifteen minutes and serve hot

title: Hot Wine (Glueh)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart claret
- one pint water
- two cups of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of whole cloves
- one teaspoon of whole cinnamon
- lemon rind cut thin and in small pieces

## Directions

1. Mix one quart claret, one pint water, two cups of sugar, one-half teaspoon of whole cloves, one teaspoon of whole cinnamon, lemon rind cut thin and in small pieces.
2. Boil steadily for fifteen minutes and serve hot.

title: How to Arrange Jellies with Fruit or Blancmange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's-foot jelly or jelly made from isinglass
- almond oil
- fresh strawberries or bunches of currants

## Directions

1. Rub the mould very slightly with 2 or 3 drops of almond oil, do it with a small piece of clean rag
2. Pour in some of the jelly just to take the pattern at the top of the mould
3. Set it to get cold; when set, take some fresh strawberries or bunches of currants
4. Wipe them well, see that they are quite clean; fix the fruit into the jelly lightly, so that they hang gracefully
5. Pour over another layer of warm jelly enough to cover them, let that set
6. Put in another layer of fruit, not too many
7. When this is quite set, fill your mould with the jelly, leave it in a cool place till you are ready for it
8. Before you turn it out just loosen it gently all round with a clean cloth, only the top
9. Wring a cloth out of boiling water quite dry, pass it all over the mould
10. Turn the mould on a dish, it will easily slip out
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Huckleberry Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of butter
- Two cups of powdered sugar
- Yolks of four eggs
- Three cups of flour
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- One cup of sweet milk
- One teaspoon of cinnamon
- Whites of four eggs
- Two cups of huckleberries

## Directions

1. Stir to a cream one cup of butter and two cups of powdered sugar
2. Add gradually the yolks of four eggs
3. Sift into this three cups of flour, adding two teaspoons of baking-powder in the last sifting
4. Add one cup of sweet milk alternately with the flour to the creamed butter, sugar and yolks
5. Spice with one teaspoon of cinnamon
6. Add the stiff-beaten whites of the eggs
7. Lastly, stir in two cups of huckleberries which have been carefully picked over and well dredged with flour
8. Be careful in stirring in the huckleberries that you do not bruise them
9. Use a wooden spoon for this purpose
10. Bake in a moderately hot oven
11. Try with a straw, if it comes out clean, your cake is baked

title: Huckleberry Compote
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- huckleberries or blueberries
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cornstarch
- water

## Directions

1. Pick over a quart of huckleberries or blueberries
2. Wash them
3. Set to boil
4. Do not add any water to them
5. Sweeten with half a cup of sugar
6. Spice with half a teaspoon of cinnamon
7. Just before removing from the fire, add a teaspoon of cornstarch which has been wet with a little cold water
8. Do this thoroughly in a cup and stir with a teaspoon so as not to have any lumps in it
9. Pour into a glass bowl
10. Eat cold

title: Huckleberry Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a loaf of stale bread
- three eggs
- one quart berries
- brown sugar
- salt
- hard sauce

## Directions

1. Take a loaf of stale bread; cut off the crust and soak in cold water, then squeeze dry
2. Beat three eggs light, yolks and whites together, add one quart berries and mix all together with a little brown sugar and a pinch of salt
3. Boil steadily one hour, serve with hard sauce

title: Huckleberry Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- cake-pans
- rich kuchen dough
- cornstarch
- one cup of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one teaspoon of cornstarch
- cold milk
- sugar
- vanilla
- huckleberries

## Directions

1. Line your cake-pans, which should be long and narrow, with a rich kuchen dough, having previously greased them well
2. Make a paste of cornstarch, one cup of milk, one tablespoon of butter and one teaspoon of cornstarch wet with cold milk
3. Boil until thick, sweeten and flavor with vanilla and spread on top of the cake dough
4. Sprinkle thickly with huckleberries which have been carefully picked, sugared

title: Huckleberry Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- kuchen dough
- butter
- cornstarch
- milk
- sugar
- vanilla
- huckleberries
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Line cake-pans which should be long and narrow with rich kuchen dough, having previously greased them well
2. Make a paste of cornstarch, one cup of milk, one tablespoon of butter and one teaspoon of cornstarch wet with cold milk
3. Boil until thick, sweeten and flavor with vanilla and spread on top of the cake dough
4. Sprinkle thickly with huckleberries which have been carefully picked, sugared and sprinkled with ground cinnamon
5. Bake in a quick oven

title: Huckleberry Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of huckleberries
- one egg
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one tablespoon of milk
- rich pastry or cookie dough

## Directions

1. Clean, pick and wash two cups of huckleberries, then drain them
2. Beat yolk of one egg and two tablespoons of sugar until light
3. Add one tablespoon of milk, then the drained berries
4. Line one pie-plate with rich pastry or cookie dough
5. Pour on it the berry mixture
6. Put in the oven and bake light brown
7. Remove from the oven and spread with a meringue made of the white of the egg beaten stiff, and two tablespoons of sugar added
8. Brown nicely

title: Huckleberry Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich pastry
- One pint of huckleberries
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with rich pastry
2. Pick, clean and wash one pint of huckleberries, drain and lay them thickly on the crust
3. Sprinkle thickly with sugar, lightly with cinnamon, and drop bits of butter over the top
4. Bake a nice even brown

title: Huckleberry Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of flour
- one and one-half pints huckleberries
- one pint crumbed bread
- one quart milk
- three tablespoons of sugar
- pinch of salt
- flakes of butter

## Directions

1. Sprinkle four tablespoons of flour over one and one-half pints huckleberries and set aside for half an hour
2. Soak one pint crumbed bread in one quart milk
3. Add three tablespoons of sugar, pinch of salt, and the huckleberries
4. Put all into a greased pudding dish with flakes of butter on top
5. Bake forty-five minutes
6. Serve with hard sauce

title: Hungarian Almond Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- scant one-quarter of a pound of almonds, blanched and grated
- scant one-half pound of sweet butter
- not quite three-quarters of a pound of flour
- a little sugar
- a pinch of salt
- two yolks

## Directions

1. Mix this well, pound the dough well with the rolling-pin.
2. Roll out not too thin.
3. Bake.

title: Hungarian Fruit Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of banana, orange, pineapple, grape-fruit
- one-half cup of chopped nuts
- French dressing
- apple or orange skins
- watercress or lettuce leaves
- paprika

## Directions

1. Mix together equal parts of banana, orange, pineapple, grape-fruit and one-half cup of chopped nuts
2. Marinate with French dressing
3. Fill apple or orange skins with mixture
4. Arrange on a bed of watercress or lettuce leaves
5. Sprinkle with paprika

title: Hungarian Fruit Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of banana, orange, pineapple, grapefruit
- one-half cup of chopped nuts
- French dressing
- apple or orange skins
- watercress or lettuce leaves
- paprika

## Directions

1. Mix together equal parts of banana, orange, pineapple, grapefruit and one-half cup of chopped nuts.
2. Marinate with French dressing.
3. Fill apple or orange skins with mixture.
4. Arrange on a bed of watercress or lettuce leaves.
5. Sprinkle with paprika.

title: Hungarian Goulash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beef
- flour
- water
- one tablespoon of salt
- one-eighth of a teaspoon of pepper
- one cup of tomatoes
- one stalk of celery
- bay leaf
- two whole cloves
- four tablespoons of flour
- two tablespoons of water
- cooked diced potatoes
- carrots
- green and red peppers

## Directions

1. Have two pounds of beef cut into one inch squares.
2. Dredge in flour and fry until brown.
3. Cover with water and simmer for two hours.
4. The last half-hour add one tablespoon of salt and one-eighth of a teaspoon of pepper.
5. Make a sauce by cooking one cup of tomatoes and one stalk of celery cut in small pieces, a bay leaf and two whole cloves, for twenty-five minutes.
6. Rub sauce through a sieve and add to stock in which meat was cooked.
7. Thicken with four tablespoons of flour moistened with two tablespoons of water.
8. Serve meat with cooked diced potatoes, carrots, and green and red peppers cut in strips.

title: Hungarian Vegetable Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup each of cold cooked peas, beans, carrots, and potatoes
- French dressing
- one cup of smoked salmon or haddock
- chopped whites of four hard-boiled eggs
- two stalks of celery
- yolk of egg
- cucumbers
- beets

## Directions

1. Mix together one cup each of cold cooked peas, beans, carrots, and potatoes.
2. Cover with French dressing and let stand for twenty minutes.
3. Add one cup of smoked salmon or haddock, cut in small pieces, the chopped whites of four hard-boiled eggs and two stalks of celery.
4. Mix thoroughly, garnish top with yolk of egg pressed through a wire sieve, and with cucumbers and beets, cut in fancy shapes.

title: Hungary Water
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of spirits of wine
- one gill of water
- half an ounce of oil of rosemary

## Directions

1. Put into a bottle one pint of spirits of wine, one gill of water, and half an ounce of oil of rosemary
2. shake well together

title: Hurry Ups (Oatmeal)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one teaspoon of salt
- one cup of rolled oats
- one tablespoon of sugar
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- one-half cup of milk
- raisins or dates

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of flour with two teaspoons of baking-powder, one teaspoon of salt
2. Add one cup of rolled oats, one tablespoon of sugar and two tablespoons of melted butter
3. Mix with one-half cup of milk
4. Drop by teaspoons onto a greased pan
5. Press well into each two or three raisins, or a split date
6. Bake for twenty minutes in a hot oven
7. Can be served with butter, honey, or maple sugar

title: Ice-Box Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of butter
- one-half cup of confectioner's sugar
- three whole eggs
- one-half pound of chopped nuts
- one tablespoon mocha essence or one square of bitter chocolate melted, or one teaspoon of vanilla
- two dozen lady fingers
- one dozen macaroons
- one-half pint of cream

## Directions

1. Cream one-half cup of butter with one-half cup of confectioner's sugar.
2. Add three whole eggs, one at a time, beat these all for twenty minutes.
3. Add one-half pound of chopped nuts, one tablespoon mocha essence or one square of bitter chocolate melted, or one teaspoon of vanilla.
4. Grease a spring form, put two dozen lady fingers around the edge, at the bottom put one dozen macaroons, then add the filling and let this all stand for twenty-four hours in ice-box.
5. When ready to serve, pour one-half pint of cream, whipped, over all and serve.

title: Iced Chocolate
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled chocolate
- 1 egg
- finely chopped ice
- 3/4 cup milk

## Directions

1. Follow recipe for boiled chocolate, but do not beat
2. Add one egg, finely chopped ice and three-fourths cup of milk
3. Put in a bowl and beat thoroughly with a Dover beater or pour into jar with cover and shake thoroughly
4. Serve in tall glasses

title: Iced Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled coffee
- sugar
- 1 quart coffee
- 1/2 cup cream
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take boiled coffee and strain
2. Add sugar to taste and chill
3. When ready to serve, add one quart of coffee, one-half cup of cream and pour in pitcher
4. Serve in tall glasses
5. Have ready a small bowl of whipped cream and place a tablespoon on top of each glass if desired

title: Iced Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarters of a pound of Jordan almonds
- one quarter of bitter almonds
- three quarters of a pound of white pounded sugar
- six yolks of beaten eggs
- one quart of boiled cream

## Directions

1. Parboil three quarters of a pound of Jordan almonds, and one quarter of bitter almonds
2. Remove the skins and beat them up to a paste, with three quarters of a pound of white pounded sugar
3. Add to this six yolks of beaten eggs, and one quart of boiled cream
4. Stir the whole for a few minutes over a stove fire
5. Strain it, and pour it into a freezing pot, used for making ices
6. Work it with a scraper, as it becomes set by freezing
7. When frozen sufficiently firm, fill a mould with it, cover it with the lid, and let it remain immersed in rough ice until the time for serving

title: Icing for Bride's Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 4 fresh eggs
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Break the whites of 4 fresh eggs in a deep basin
2. Mix in 1 lb. of sifted sugar
3. Add the juice of a lemon well strained
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Icing for Bride's Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 4 fresh eggs
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- juice of a lemon well strained

## Directions

1. Break the whites of 4 fresh eggs in a deep basin
2. Mix in 1 lb. of sifted sugar and the juice of a lemon well strained
3. Whisk it all well together till it comes very thick, and feels quite hard
4. Lay it all over the cake
5. Stand it inside of a cool oven or in a warm place to harden the sugar
6. When you put on the ornaments do not disturb the sugar more than you can help
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Icing for Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sifted white sugar
- one wine glass of orange flower-water
- the whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Whisk half a pound of sifted white sugar with one wine glass of orange flower-water
2. Add the whites of two eggs, well beaten and strained
3. Whisk until it is quite thick and white
4. When the cake is almost cold, dip a soft camel's hair brush into it
5. Cover the cake well
6. Set it in a cool oven to harden

title: Icing for Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of sifted sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water or the juice of a lemon well strained
- whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat up 1/4 lb. of sifted sugar with 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water or the juice of a lemon well strained
2. beat the whites of 2 eggs to a stiff snow
3. whisk it quickly into the sugar and flavouring
4. beat until it is thick and white
5. When the cake is nearly cold put it on smoothly with a brush all over the cake
6. put it in a cool oven to dry, mind that the oven is scarcely warm
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Imberlach
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of matzoth flour
- one-quarter pound of powdered ginger
- three eggs
- one-half pound of honey
- three-quarters pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of matzoth flour and one-quarter pound of powdered ginger together with three eggs
2. Set this dough aside until it dries
3. Take one-half pound of honey and three-quarters pound of sugar and boil until it gets a reddish color
4. Beat in the ginger and matzoth dough
5. Mix it with honey
6. Set back on stove, stirring constantly
7. When the mixture is thick and a reddish color, place on the board so as to cool
8. Roll and cut in two-inch lengths

title: Imitation New Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- parsley
- fat

## Directions

1. Buy a potato cutter at a first-class hardware store
2. Cut the potatoes to the size of a hickory nut
3. Fry or steam them
4. Parboil and brown in fat, or boil and add parsley as you would with new potatoes
5. The remainder of the raw potatoes may be boiled and mashed or fried into ribbons

title: Imitation Pate De Foi Gras
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- livers and gizzards of fowl
- three tablespoons of chicken or goose fat
- a finely chopped onion
- one tablespoon of pungent sauce
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Take as many livers and gizzards of any kind of fowl as you may have on hand
2. Add to these three tablespoons of chicken or goose fat, a finely chopped onion, one tablespoon of pungent sauce, and salt and white pepper to taste
3. Boil the livers until quite done and drain
4. When cold, rub to a smooth paste
5. Take some of the fat and chopped onion and simmer together slowly for ten minutes
6. Strain through a thin muslin bag, pressing the bag tightly
7. Turn into a bowl and mix with the seasoning
8. Work all together for a long time
9. Grease a bowl or cups and press this mixture into them
10. When soft cut up the gizzards into bits and lay between the mixture
11. Season this highly, or to suit taste

title: Impanada
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- halibut, plaice, or soles
- potatoes
- short-crust paste
- brown sugar
- small pickles
- peppers
- gherkins
- West India pickles
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut in small pieces halibut, plaice, or soles
2. Place them in a deep dish in alternate layers with slices of potatoes and dumplings made of short-crust paste, sweetened with brown sugar
3. Season well with small pickles, peppers, gherkins, or West India pickles
4. Throw over a little water and butter warmed
5. Bake it thoroughly

title: Individual Apple Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- six muffin rings
- sliced apples
- one and one-half cups of pastry flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- three level teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-fourth of a cup of shortening
- three-fourths of a cup of milk or water
- cream
- sugar
- hard sauce
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Butter six muffin rings and set them on a shallow agate pan which has been well buttered
2. Fill the rings with sliced apples
3. Make a dough of one and one-half cups of pastry flour sifted several times with one-half teaspoon of salt and three level teaspoons of baking-powder
4. Chop into the dry ingredients one-fourth of a cup of shortening, gradually add three-fourths of a cup of milk or water
5. Drop the dough on the apples on the rings
6. Let bake about twenty minutes
7. With a spatula remove each dumpling from the ring, place on dish with the crust side down
8. Serve with cream and sugar, hard sauce or with a fruit sauce

title: Individual Loaves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough prepared according to white bread recipe
- beaten egg or sweetened water
- poppy seed

## Directions

1. Make dough according to the white bread recipe
2. Work small pieces of dough into strands a finger long, and take three strands for each loaf
3. Make small as possible
4. Brush with beaten egg or sweetened water and sprinkle with poppy seed
5. Allow them to rise before setting them in the oven

title: Instantaneous Frosting
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white of one unbeaten egg
- one and one-fourth cups of pulverized sugar
- three drops of rose-water
- ten drops of vanilla
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. To the white of an unbeaten egg add one and one-fourth cups of pulverized sugar and stir until smooth
2. Add three drops of rose-water, ten of vanilla, and the juice of half a lemon

title: Irish Moss
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of carrageen or Irish moss
- a pint and a half of water or milk

## Directions

1. Boil half an ounce of carrageen or Irish moss in a pint and a half of water or milk till it is reduced to a pint

title: Irish Stew
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of chops, cut nicely, not too fat
- 1 lb. of good chuck steak, cut in pieces
- 4 lb. of large potatoes all one size, cut in thick slices
- 3 large onions
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 pint of water

## Directions

1. Put the steak at the bottom of your stewpan with a layer of the potatoes, and some thick slices of onions, then a layer of chops, potatoes, and onions, let the last layer only be onions and potatoes.
2. Add pepper and salt to taste and 1 pint of water.
3. Stew gently for 2½ hours, keep the lid close, not to let the steam out, shake the saucepan occasionally.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Irish Stew
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of chops
- 1 lb. of good chuck steak
- 4 lb. of large potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the chops nicely, not too fat
2. Cut the chuck steak in pieces
3. Use large potatoes all one size
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Irish Stew
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half pounds of lamb
- flour
- water
- one quart of potatoes
- one-half cup of carrots
- three onions
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons of flour

## Directions

1. Cut one and one-half pounds of lamb into small pieces
2. Dredge each piece of meat in flour
3. Brown in the frying-pan
4. Put in kettle, cover with water and cook slowly one hour or until tender
5. Add one quart of potatoes cut in small dice, one-half cup of carrots and three onions, after cooking thirty minutes
6. Season with salt, pepper, and thicken with two tablespoons of flour moistened in enough cold water to form a smooth paste
7. Serve with dumplings

title: Irish Stew
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of mutton
- potatoes
- water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into chops without frying
2. lay them in a stewpan with potatoes
3. cover with water
4. skim frequently
5. season with pepper and salt to taste

title: Italian Salad
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- white parts of a cold fowl
- mustard and cress
- lettuce
- equal quantities of vinegar and the finest salad oil
- salt
- mustard
- yolks of hard boiled eggs
- yolk of one raw egg
- tarragon vinegar
- whites of the eggs
- grated smoked beef
- two or three hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut up the white parts of a cold fowl, and mix it with mustard and cress, and a lettuce chopped finely
2. Pour over a fine salad mixture, composed of equal quantities of vinegar and the finest salad oil, salt, mustard, and the yolks of hard boiled eggs, and the yolk of one raw egg, mixed smoothly together
3. Add a little tarragon vinegar, and pour the mixture over the salad
4. Mix the whites of the eggs, and serve to garnish the dish, arranged in small heaps alternately with heaps of grated smoked beef
5. Cut up two or three hard boiled eggs with the chicken in small pieces and mixed with the salad
6. Alternatively, pile the fowl in the centre of the dish, pour over the salad mixture, and make a wall of any dressed salad, laying the whites of the eggs (after the yolks have been removed for the mixture), cut in rings on the top like a chain

title: Italian Salad
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fowl
- cold smoked beef
- 4 eggs
- lettuce
- mustard and cress
- 2 spoonfuls of best salad oil
- 2 spoonfuls of vinegar
- 1 spoonful of tarragon vinegar
- 2 yolks of eggs raw
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- garlic
- beetroot

## Directions

1. Cut up the white part of any cold fowl that may be left
2. Grate some cold smoked beef
3. Boil 4 eggs hard
4. Have a lettuce nicely washed, also some mustard and cress
5. Take a salad bowl and put little heaps of the cold chicken, the grated smoked beef, the yolks of the eggs chopped, and some of the white
6. Arrange it tastefully, mixed with little portions of the lettuce and mustard and cress
7. Put round some slices of hard eggs
8. Make a mixture of 2 spoonfuls of the best salad oil, 2 of vinegar, 1 of tarragon vinegar, 2 yolks of eggs raw, some salt, pepper, a little mustard, and a tooth of garlic chopped very fine
9. Rub the mixture together like a cream
10. Do not put on till dinner or supper time
11. You can vary the style by putting the fowl and meat in the centre, and making walls of the salad round with strips of beetroot and hard eggs
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Jaumange
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of white sugar
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- thinly cut peel of two lemons
- one ounce of dissolved isinglass
- juice of three lemons
- one glass of brandy
- three glasses of sherry
- yolks of five or six eggs

## Directions

1. Simmer half a pound of white sugar in three-quarters of a pint of water, with the thinly cut peel of two lemons
2. when the sugar is melted, add an ounce of dissolved isinglass, and the juice of three lemons, a glass of brandy and three of sherry
3. beat up with this the yolks of five or six eggs
4. Place the basin in which it is mixed into a pan of boiling water to thicken it
5. pour it into a mould and set it to cool
6. if it does not thicken by being put in a pan of boiling water, set the pan on the fire and stir it for a few minutes

title: Jellied Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- pepper
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs
- one-quarter of a cup aspic

## Directions

1. Boil a chicken in as little water as possible until the meat falls from the bones
2. chop rather fine and season with pepper and salt
3. Put into a mold a layer of the chopped meat and then a layer of hard-boiled eggs, cut in slices
4. Fill the mold with alternate layers of meat and eggs until nearly full
5. Boil down the liquor left in the kettle until half the quantity
6. While warm, add one-quarter of a cup aspic, pour into the mold over the meat
7. Set in a cool place overnight to jelly

title: Jelly Roll
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one cup of flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half cup of boiling water
- jelly
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cream three eggs with one cup of granulated sugar
2. Sift flour with two teaspoons of baking-powder
3. Add one-half cup of boiling water
4. Bake in broad pan
5. While hot, remove from pan and lay on cloth wet with cold water
6. Spread with jelly and roll quickly
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar

title: Jelly Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- thin jelly
- one cup boiling water
- brandy or wine
- sugar
- one teaspoon cornstarch
- cold water
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Take thin jelly, add one cup boiling water and brandy or wine (one-half cup)
2. add a little more sugar
3. thicken with one teaspoon cornstarch dissolved in a little cold water
4. The beaten white of egg may be added

title: Jerusalem Artichoke
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichoke
- milk
- one small onion
- one tablespoon of butter
- one level tablespoon of flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-fourth teaspoon of pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the washed and peeled artichoke into cubes.
2. Put in a stew-pan, and cover with milk (a generous pint to a quart of cubes).
3. Add one small onion and cook twenty minutes.
4. Beat together one tablespoon of butter and one level tablespoon of flour, and stir this into the boiling milk.
5. Season with one teaspoon of salt and one-fourth teaspoon of pepper, and continue the cooking one-half hour longer.
6. The cooking should be done in a double boiler.

title: Jerusalem Artichokes Fried
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- batter

## Directions

1. Cut in slices after parboiling them, dip in batter, and fry

title: Jewish Method of Frying Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- flour
- beaten eggs
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. Scale the fish with the utmost thoroughness
2. Remove the entrails and wash very thoroughly
3. Salt both inside and out
4. Cut the fish into convenient slices
5. Place them on a strainer and leave for an hour
6. Place some flour in one plate and some beaten eggs in another
7. Heat a large frying-pan half full of oil or butter
8. Wipe fish slices thoroughly with a clean cloth
9. Dip them first in flour and then in beaten eggs
10. Fry until browned
11. Put fish in a frying basket, then into the hot oil and cook five minutes
12. Drain on brown paper and arrange on platter
13. Do not stick knife or fork into fish while it is frying

title: Johnnie Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- two cups corn-meal
- one heaping teaspoon of soda
- one-half cup sugar
- two eggs
- one and one-half cups of buttermilk
- one half cup of molasses
- one-half cup of shortening

## Directions

1. Mix one cup flour and two cups corn-meal, one heaping teaspoon of soda, one-half cup sugar
2. Add two eggs beaten with one and one-half cups of buttermilk, one half cup of molasses and one-half cup of shortening, melted
3. Beat all ingredients as fast as possible for a minute
4. Pour the dough into a warm, well-buttered pan
5. Bake quickly and steadily for half an hour
6. Serve hot

title: Julienne Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- half a carrot
- half a turnip
- a leek
- 2 sticks of celery
- a Jerusalem artichoke
- a potato
- several French beans
- a few green peas
- a small piece of lettuce
- a little cabbage
- a pint of water
- a little salt
- pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- a few meat balls
- a quart of stock
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Cut in thin long strips the length of 2 inches: half a carrot, half a turnip, a leek, 2 sticks of celery, a Jerusalem artichoke, a potato, several French beans, a few green peas, a small piece of lettuce, and a little cabbage.
2. Wash them well and strain through a colander.
3. Put them into a clean saucepan with a pint of water, a little salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg.
4. Boil a few meat balls with the vegetables.
5. When quite done put it all into a quart of the stock.
6. Put in a little browning to colour it.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Julienne Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- soup stock
- one cup green peas
- three carrots
- some cabbage
- two tablespoons of flour
- hot fat
- livers and gizzards of fowls

## Directions

1. Have soup stock ready
2. Boil in water until tender one cup green peas, three carrots cut up in small pieces, and some cabbage chopped fine
3. Brown two tablespoons of flour in a skillet in hot fat, then stir in the vegetables
4. Fry some livers and gizzards of fowls, if handy, and add
5. Stir in the strained soup stock

title: Kaese Kraepfli (Cheese Kreplich)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- a tablespoon of water
- a pinch of salt
- flour
- pot cheese or schmierkaese
- a piece of butter (the size of an egg)
- one egg
- sugar
- cinnamon
- grated peel of a lemon
- a pinch of salt
- pounded almonds
- boiling milk
- melted butter or cream

## Directions

1. Make a dough of one egg with a tablespoon of water; add a pinch of salt; work this just as you would noodle dough, quite stiff.
2. Sift the flour in a bowl, break in the egg, add the salt and water, mix slowly by stirring with the handle of a knife, stirring in the same direction all the time.
3. When this dough is so stiff that you cannot work it with the knife, flour your noodle board and work it with the hollow of your hands, always toward you, until the dough is perfectly smooth.
4. Roll out as thin as paper and cut into squares three inches in diameter.
5. For filling: Stir up a piece of butter the size of an egg, adding one egg, sugar, cinnamon, grated peel of a lemon and pinch of salt, pounded almonds, which improve it.
6. Fill the kraepfli with a teaspoon, wet the edges with beaten egg, fold into triangles, pressing the edges firmly together.
7. Boil in boiling milk; when done they will swim to the top.
8. Eat with melted butter or cream.

title: Kaffee Kuchen (Cinnamon)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- dough
- melted butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- chopped almonds
- flour

## Directions

1. Butter long and broad cake-pans thoroughly, roll out enough dough to cover them, and let it rise about half an hour before baking, then brush it well with melted butter
2. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top and some chopped almonds
3. Take a small lump of butter, a very little flour, some sugar and cinnamon and rub it between the hands until it is like lumps of almonds, then strew on top of cakes

title: Kal Dolmar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- rice
- chopped meat
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Boil cabbage whole for ten minutes.
2. Let it cool and boil the rice.
3. Mix chopped meat, rice, and salt and pepper.
4. Separate the cabbage leaves; put about three tablespoons of the meat and rice in the leaves, roll up and tie together with string.
5. Fry in fat until brown.
6. Boil for half an hour in a little water.
7. Make brown gravy and pour over.

title: Kartoffel Kloesse (Potato Dumplings)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about eight potatoes
- two eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- one wine-glass farina
- one tablespoon chicken fat
- one scant cup flour
- salted boiling water

## Directions

1. Boil about eight potatoes in their jackets and when peeled lay them on a platter overnight
2. When ready to use them next day, grate, add two eggs, salt, a little nutmeg if desired, one wine-glass of farina, a tablespoon of chicken fat, one scant cup of flour gradually
3. If not dry enough add more flour, but be sure not to make the mixture too stiff as this makes the balls heavy
4. Place balls in salted boiling water, cook until light and thoroughly done
5. Serve just as they are or fried in chicken fat until brown

title: Kartoffel Kloesse With Bread Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about eight potatoes
- two eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- one wine-glass farina
- one tablespoon chicken fat
- one scant cup flour
- bread
- chicken fat
- onions
- salted boiling water

## Directions

1. Make dumplings of the same mixture as regular Kartoffel Kloesse
2. In the centre of each dumpling place stripes of bread one inch long and one-fourth inch thick which have been fried in chicken fat and onions
3. Flour your hands well and make into dumplings
4. Put into boiling salted water, boil about twenty-five minutes
5. Serve at once with chopped onions browned, or browned bread crumbs and chicken fat

title: Kedgeree
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about a pound of any kind of cooked fish except herring
- two eggs
- one cup of rice
- salt
- a good dessertspoon of butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut up in small pieces about a pound of any kind of cooked fish except herring.
2. Boil two eggs hard and chop up.
3. Take one cup of rice and boil in the following manner: After washing it well and putting it on in boiling water, with a little salt, let it boil for ten minutes, drain it almost dry and let it steam with the lid closely shut for ten minutes longer without stirring.
4. Take a clean pot and put in the fish, eggs, rice, a good dessertspoon of butter, and pepper and salt to taste.
5. Stir over the fire until quite hot.
6. Press into a mould and turn it out at once and serve.

title: Kentucky Chrimsel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half cups of meal
- four eggs
- two cups of sugar
- one kitchen-spoon of goose fat
- one kitchen-spoon of beef fat
- four apples
- salt
- pepper
- ground clove
- allspice
- cinnamon
- one glass of wine
- cranberries or any fruit

## Directions

1. Put the meal in a bowl with salt, pepper, ground clove, allspice, and cinnamon mixed into it
2. Peel and grate the apples
3. Melt the fat and mix
4. Put in eggs and then stir in the sugar which has been boiled with water to a thin syrup and cooled off
5. Hollow out two pieces, put cranberries or any fruit between them
6. Form into balls the size of a medium apple, and bake them on a well-greased pie-plate for about one hour

title: Kidney Beans with Brown Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint (two cups) of kidney beans
- one teaspoon butter
- one cup of brown sauce

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash one pint (two cups) of kidney beans
2. Let soak overnight in cold water
3. Drain and cook in fresh salted water till tender
4. Drain; shake in saucepan with one teaspoon butter three minutes
5. Add one cup of brown sauce and simmer five minutes

title: Kidney Beans with Peppers and Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of kidney beans
- one quart of water
- one tablespoon of butter
- four green peppers
- one small red pepper
- one onion
- one pint of tomatoes
- one teaspoon of salt
- three tablespoons of uncooked rice

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash two cups of kidney beans, soak in one quart of water
2. Next morning bring to a boil in fresh water
3. Drain, cover beans with boiling water and cook until tender
4. Half an hour before beans are to be served, put one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan
5. Chop and add four green peppers, one small red pepper, one onion, one pint of tomatoes, one teaspoon of salt
6. Cook fifteen minutes
7. Add to beans with three tablespoons of uncooked rice
8. Simmer until thick

title: Kimmel Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- round of beef
- water
- 2 ounces carraway seeds
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- allspice
- brown paper

## Directions

1. Place a piece of round of beef in a deep baking pan with a pint of water
2. Tie 2 ounces of carraway seeds in a piece of muslin and add to the pan
3. Add salt, pepper, and a few cloves and allspice
4. Cover the pan down tight with the lid and tie brown paper over it
5. Put it in a moderate oven for 3 or 4 hours
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Kimmel Meat
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small piece of the rump of beef, or the under cut of a sirloin
- three pints of vinegar
- two ounces of caraway seeds
- salt
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Place a small piece of the rump of beef, or the under cut of a sirloin in a deep pan with three pints of vinegar
2. add two ounces of caraway seeds tied in a muslin bag, salt, pepper, and spices
3. cover it down tight
4. bake thoroughly in a slow oven

title: Kindel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of soup fat
- three pints of flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- two-thirds cup of granulated sugar
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- two teaspoons of vanilla
- flour
- beer
- One and one-half cups of brown sugar
- two tablespoons of honey
- two pounds of walnuts
- one pound of stewed prunes
- two cups of sponge cake crumbs
- juice of one lemon
- spices
- few raisins and currants
- a little citron
- a little wine
- a little chicken schmalz

## Directions

1. Render two pounds of soup fat a day or two before using
2. Mix three pints of flour, one teaspoon of salt, two-thirds cup of granulated sugar, one teaspoon of baking-powder, two teaspoons of vanilla
3. Knead well
4. Add enough beer to be able to roll
5. Let it stand two hours
6. Roll, cut in long strips three inches wide
7. Make filling: Mix one and one-half cups of brown sugar, two tablespoons of honey, two pounds of walnuts chopped fine, one pound of stewed prunes chopped fine, two cups of sponge cake crumbs, juice of one lemon, spices to taste, few raisins and currants, and a little citron chopped fine
8. Add a little wine, a little chicken schmalz
9. Heat a few minutes
10. You may use up remnants of jellies, jams, marmalades, etc.
11. Put plenty of filling in centre of strips
12. Fold over
13. With a round stick (use a wooden spoon), press the dough firmly three inches apart
14. Then with a knife cut them apart
15. Grease the pan and the top of cakes
16. Bake in moderate oven

title: Kindlech
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine flour
- one yeast cake
- milk
- three well-beaten eggs
- a quarter of a pound of butter
- six ounces of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- two cups of lukewarm milk
- melted butter
- chopped almonds
- sultanas
- currants
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Into a large bowl sift one pound of fine flour
2. Make a depression in the centre and pour into it one yeast cake dissolved in a little milk
3. Let this remain until the milk and yeast have risen a little
4. Stir in the surrounding flour together with three well-beaten eggs, a quarter of a pound of butter, six ounces of sugar, a pinch of salt and two cups of lukewarm milk
5. Knead the whole into a smooth dough
6. Roll this out very lightly on a well-floured board, brush over with a feather dipped in melted butter and strew thickly with chopped almonds, sultanas and currants
7. Fold over about three fingers' width of the dough
8. Brush the upper surface of this fold with melted butter and strew with mixed fruit and almonds
9. Fold over again and repeat the operation until the whole of the dough is folded up in layer somewhat resembling a flattened roley poley pudding
10. Brush the top well with another feather dipped in beaten egg and cut the whole into thick slices or fingers
11. Let them stand for half an hour and then bake for an hour in a rather slow oven

title: Kirsch Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- two cups of water
- two tablespoons of cornstarch
- one-quarter cup of cold water
- one cup of Kirsch wine

## Directions

1. Put one cup of sugar and two cups of water on to boil
2. Mix two tablespoons of cornstarch in one-quarter cup of cold water
3. when the water in the saucepan is boiling, add cornstarch and stir for two minutes
4. Remove from stove
5. add one cup of Kirsch wine and stir again
6. Strain and serve with pudding

title: Kischkes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beef casings
- fat
- flour
- chopped onions
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare as above.
2. Boil for three hours in plenty of water.
3. When done, put in frying-pan with one tablespoon of fat, cover and let brown nicely.
4. Serve hot.

title: Kischkes - Russian Style
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beef casings
- fat
- flour
- chopped onions
- salt
- pepper
- two large onions

## Directions

1. Make a filling of fat, flour (using one-third cup fat to one cup flour) and chopped onions.
2. Season well with salt and pepper.
3. Cut them in short lengths, fasten one end, stuff and then fasten the open end.
4. If they are not already cleaned plunge into boiling water and scrape the surface exposed after filling the casing until cleaned.
5. Slice two large onions in a roasting-pan, and roast the kischkes slowly until well done and well browned.
6. Baste frequently with liquid in the pan.

title: Knoblauch Sauce (Garlic)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoon of drippings
- one or two cloves of garlic
- a tablespoon of flour
- a cup of soup stock or warm water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat a tablespoon of drippings, either of meat or goose in a frying-pan
2. Cut up one or two cloves of garlic very fine and let it brown slightly in the heated fat
3. Add a tablespoon of flour, a cup of soup stock or warm water, salt, pepper to taste

title: Knuckle of Veal Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- 2 quarts of water
- salt
- pepper
- blade of mace
- turnip
- carrot
- celery
- leek
- onion
- tooth of garlic
- 1 lb. of potatoes
- a little dried saffron
- motsa balls

## Directions

1. Boil a knuckle of veal in water with salt, pepper, blade of mace, turnip, carrot, celery, leek, onion, and garlic
2. Let it boil till the meat leaves the bone
3. Take out the knuckle and cut all the flesh in small pieces
4. Put the flesh back in the soup
5. Boil and mash quite fine 1 lb. of potatoes and stir them well in
6. Make a few small motsa balls and boil with
7. Colour with a little dried saffron
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Koch Kaese (Boiled Cheese)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of fine cottage cheese
- salt
- one-fourth as much caraway seed
- yolks of two eggs
- one even tablespoon of flour
- one-half cup of cold milk
- a lump of butter
- a clean cloth
- beer

## Directions

1. Press one quart of fine cottage cheese through a coarse sieve or colander and set it away in a cool place for a week, stirring it once or twice during that time.
2. When it has become quite strong, stir it smooth with a wooden or silver spoon.
3. Add a saltspoon of salt and one-fourth as much of caraway seed.
4. Add yolks of two eggs and an even tablespoon of flour which has been previously dissolved in about one-half cup of cold milk.
5. Stir the flour and milk until it is a smooth paste, adding a lump of butter, about the size of an egg.
6. Add all to the cheese.
7. Put the cheese on to boil until quite thick, stirring occasionally.
8. Boil altogether about one-half hour, stirring constantly the last ten minutes.
9. The cheese must look smooth as velvet.
10. Pour it into a dish which has been previously rinsed in cold water.
11. Set it away in a cool place.
12. To keep it any length of time, cover it with a clean cloth which has been dipped in and wrung out of beer.

title: Koenig Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- one-quarter pound of raisins
- one-quarter pound of currants
- juice and peel of one lemon
- one spoon of rum
- twenty blanched and grated almonds
- two cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
- two stiffly-beaten whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Cream one-quarter cup of butter with one cup of sugar, yolks of six eggs, one-quarter pound of raisins, one-quarter pound of currants, juice and peel of one lemon, one spoon of rum, twenty blanched and grated almonds
2. Mix with two cups of flour mixed with one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
3. Fold in two stiffly-beaten whites of eggs
4. Bake in an ungreased form one to one and one-half hours

title: Kohl-Rabi With Breast of Lamb
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- young kohl-rabi leaves
- salt
- water
- breast of mutton or lamb
- ginger
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Strip off the young leaves and boil in salt water
2. Peel the heads thickly, cut into round, thin slices, and lay in cold water for an hour
3. Put on to boil a breast of mutton or lamb, which has been previously well salted
4. Spice with a little ground ginger
5. When the mutton has boiled one-half hour add the sliced kohl-rabi
6. Boil covered
7. In the meantime, drain all the water from the leaves, which you have boiled separately, and chop them, but not too fine
8. Add them to the mutton
9. When done thicken with flour
10. Season with pepper and more salt if needed
11. You may omit the leaves if you are not fond of them

title: Kraepfli
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- egg
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- salt
- pounded almonds
- pot cheese or schmierkaese
- boiling milk
- melted butter or cream

## Directions

1. Knead dough on board and work it with the hollow of your hands, always toward you, until the dough is perfectly smooth
2. Roll out as thin as paper and cut into squares three inches in diameter
3. Prepare filling: stir up a piece of butter the size of an egg, adding one egg, sugar, cinnamon, grated peel of a lemon and pinch of salt, pounded almonds
4. Fill the kraepfli with a teaspoon, wet the edges with beaten egg, fold into triangles, pressing the edges firmly together
5. Boil in boiling milk
6. When done they will swim to the top
7. Eat with melted butter or cream

title: Kraut Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- fat
- one-fourth of a loaf of bread
- water
- one-half cup of flour
- one-half cup of brown sugar
- one-eighth pound of raisins
- finely chopped citron
- one-fourth pound of almonds
- bitter almonds
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- cinnamon
- allspice
- juice and peel of one lemon
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Chop up cabbage and let stew in fat slowly until quite brown, doing this the day previous to using
2. Next day mix in with the stewed cabbage one-fourth of a loaf of bread soaked in water and squeezed dry
3. Add one-half cup of flour, one-half cup of brown sugar, one-eighth pound of raisins, some finely chopped citron, one-fourth pound of almonds mixed with a few bitter almonds, one-half teaspoon of salt, some cinnamon and allspice about a teaspoon, juice and peel of one lemon and four eggs
4. Mix all thoroughly
5. Pour into well-greased iron pan (kugel pot) and bake slowly

title: Kremslekh
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- matzoth-meal
- egg
- salt
- sugar
- ground almonds
- stoned and chopped raisins
- ground cinnamon
- oil or beef dripping
- water

## Directions

1. To each tablespoon of matzoth-meal take one egg, a pinch of salt, half a teaspoon of sugar, a teaspoon of ground almonds, a few stoned and chopped raisins, a pinch of ground cinnamon, a spoon of oil, or its equivalent of beef dripping, and just enough water to make the whole into a stiff paste
2. Mix the ingredients very thoroughly
3. Take a large enameled saucepan and about half fill it with oil or fat
4. Bring this to boiling point but do not let it burn
5. Shape the paste into small pieces and drop them into boiling fat
6. Turn them continually until well browned
7. Take out and drain carefully on a strainer
8. May be eaten hot or cold

title: Kreplech or Butterflies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle dough
- one tablespoon of force-meat

## Directions

1. Roll noodle dough into pieces two and one-half inches square.
2. Place on each one tablespoon of force-meat, then fold squares into three cornered pockets, pressing edges well together.
3. Drop in boiling soup or salted water and boil fifteen minutes.

title: Krosphada
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two sliced onions
- two ounces of sugar
- spices
- pepper
- salt
- one pint of pure malt vinegar
- six beaten eggs
- crumbled ginger-bread

## Directions

1. Place two sliced onions with two ounces each of sugar and spices, pepper and salt to taste in a pint of pure malt vinegar
2. Boil gently until the onions are nearly done
3. Let it cool a little
4. Stir in six beaten eggs and sufficient crumbled ginger-bread to make the whole quite thick
5. Place again over the fire for a few minutes, stirring frequently and mashing the mixture into a uniform paste
6. Be very careful that it does not boil

title: Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five ounces of white bread
- cold water
- three-fourths cup of soft goose fat in small pieces
- five whole eggs
- one cup of flour
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-fourth cup of cracker meal
- three apples
- two pears
- two dozen raisins with the seeds removed
- salt to taste
- tiny pinch of pepper
- one-quarter teaspoon each of cinnamon and allspice
- goose-fat
- one cup of cold water

## Directions

1. Soak five ounces of white bread in cold water
2. Squeeze out every bit of water and put it in a bowl
3. Add three-fourths cup of soft goose fat in small pieces
4. Add five whole eggs, one cup of flour, one-half cup of sugar, one-fourth cup of cracker meal, three apples and two pears cut in small pieces, two dozen raisins with the seeds removed, salt to taste, a tiny pinch of pepper, one-quarter teaspoon each of cinnamon and allspice
5. Mix all well together
6. Pour into an iron pan that has the bottom well covered with goose-fat
7. Stick a few pieces of cut apple or pear in the top of the pudding
8. Pour a cup of cold water over all
9. Place in the oven to bake
10. Bake slowly for five or six hours
11. If the water cooks out before it is ready to brown, add more
12. Bake brown, top and bottom

title: Kugel (Scharfe)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. If one desires an unsweetened kugel omit the sugar and cinnamon and season with salt and pepper
2. When required for any other meal but Shabbas, a kugel can be baked brown in two hours

title: Kugel and Commean
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of Spanish peas
- one pint of Spanish beans
- three pints of water
- two marrow bones
- a calf's-foot
- three pounds of fine gravy-beef
- one pound of flour
- half a pound of shred suet
- a little grated nutmeg
- ground ginger
- cloves
- allspice
- one pound of coarse brown sugar
- the crumb of a slice of bread

## Directions

1. Soak one pint of Spanish peas and one pint of Spanish beans all night in three pints of water
2. take two marrow bones, a calf's-foot, and three pounds of fine gravy-beef
3. crack the bones and tie them to prevent the marrow escaping
4. put all together into a pan
5. take one pound of flour, half a pound of shred suet, a little grated nutmeg and ground ginger, cloves and allspice, one pound of coarse brown sugar, and the crumb of a slice of bread, first soaked in water and pressed dry
6. mix all these ingredients together into a paste
7. grease a quart basin and put it in, covering the basin with a plate set in the middle of the pan with the beans, meat, &c.
8. Cover the pan lightly down with coarse brown paper
9. let it remain all the night and the next day (until required) in a baker's oven
10. when done, take out the basin containing the pudding, and skim the fat from the gravy which must be served as soup
11. The pudding must be turned out of the basin, and a sweet sauce flavored with lemon and brandy is a fine addition

title: Lady Fingers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one-quarter pound of powdered sugar
- flavoring to taste: vanilla, lemon juice, grated rind of lemon or orange
- whites of three eggs
- one-half saltspoon of salt
- one-half cup of flour
- powdered sugar
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of three eggs until light and creamy
2. Add one-quarter pound of powdered sugar (sifted) and continue beating
3. Add flavoring to taste, vanilla, lemon juice, grated rind of lemon or orange
4. To the whites of the three eggs add one-half saltspoon of salt and beat until very stiff
5. Stir in lightly one-half cup of flour and then fold in the beaten whites very gently
6. Press the mixture through a pastry tube on a baking-tin, covered with paper in portions one-half inch wide by four inches long, or drop on oblong molds
7. Sift a little powdered sugar on top of each cake
8. Bake from ten to fifteen minutes in a moderate oven
9. Do not let brown
10. Remove immediately from pan
11. Brush the flat surface of one cake with white of egg and press the underside of a second cake upon the first

title: Lamb and Macaroni
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of concentrated tomato sauce
- one quart of water
- two medium-sized onions
- olive oil or drippings
- one and one-half pounds of lean neck of lamb
- one clove of garlic
- two bay leaves
- two teaspoons of sugar
- pepper
- salt
- boiled macaroni

## Directions

1. Dilute one can of concentrated tomato sauce with one quart of water.
2. Mince two medium-sized onions very fine and fry slowly in olive oil or drippings until they are a golden brown, and add to tomatoes.
3. Fry one and one-half pounds of lean neck of lamb in a little drippings until the meat is nicely browned all over and add to the tomatoes.
4. Season with one clove of garlic, two bay leaves, two teaspoons of sugar, pepper and salt.
5. Let it simmer for about one and one-half hours, or until the meat is tender and the sauce has become the consistency of thick cream.
6. Have ready some boiled macaroni, put in with the meat and stir well.
7. Serve hot.

title: Lamb and Macaroni
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of concentrated tomato sauce
- one quart of water
- two medium-sized onions
- olive oil or drippings
- one and one-half pounds of lean neck of lamb

## Directions

1. Dilute one can of concentrated tomato sauce with one quart of water
2. Mince two medium-sized onions very fine and fry slowly in olive oil or drippings until they are a golden brown, and add to tomatoes
3. Fry one and one-half pounds of lean neck of lamb in a little drippings until the meat is nicely browned all over and add to the tomatoes

title: Lamb and Peas
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- best end of a neck of lamb
- chopped onion
- pepper
- salt
- water
- peas
- lettuce
- mint
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take the best end of a neck of lamb, either keep it whole or divide it into chops as may be preferred
2. Put it into a saucepan with a little chopped onion, pepper, salt, and a small quantity of water
3. When half done add half a peck of peas, half a lettuce cut fine, a little mint, and a few lumps of sugar
4. Let it stew thoroughly
5. When done, there must not be too much liquor

title: Lamb and Sprew
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck or breast of lamb
- water
- sprew
- celery
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a fine neck or breast of lamb
2. Put it in stewpan with as much water as will cover it
3. Add to it a bundle of sprew cut in pieces of two inches in length, a small head of celery cut small, and one onion, pepper, salt, and a sprig of parsley
4. Let it simmer gently till the meat and sprew are tender
5. Add a couple of lumps of sugar to improve the flavor
6. Ensure there is not too much liquor and remove all fat
7. When served, the sprew should surround the meat and also be thickly laid over it

title: Lamb Cutlets with Cucumbers
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- vinegar
- stock
- eggs
- flour
- essence of lemon
- lamb cutlets

## Directions

1. Take two fine cucumbers, peel and cut them lengthways
2. Lay them in vinegar for an hour
3. Stew them in good stock till tender
4. Stir in the yolks of two or three eggs, a little flour and essence of lemon, which must all be first mixed up together with a little of the stock
5. Have ready some cutlets trimmed and fried a light brown
6. Arrange them round the dish and pour the cucumbers in the centre

title: Lamb Cutlets With Cucumbers
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 fine cucumbers
- vinegar
- a little good stock
- salt
- pepper
- 2 eggs
- a teaspoonful of flour
- the juice of a lemon
- lamb cutlets
- a little saffron

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the cucumbers in lengths of 3 inches
2. Lay them in vinegar for an hour
3. Stew them in a little good stock with salt and pepper till tender
4. See that there is not too much liquor
5. Beat 2 eggs with a teaspoonful of flour and the juice of a lemon
6. Stir it into the liquor and see that it does not boil
7. Have ready some lamb cutlets trimmed nicely and fried a light brown
8. Arrange the cucumber in the middle of the dish and put the cutlets round
9. Colour the sauce with a little saffron
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Lamb Stew—Tocane
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of leek or young onions
- one tablespoon of drippings
- neck or breast of lamb
- white pepper
- salt
- parsley
- dill

## Directions

1. Brown slices of leek or young onions in one tablespoon of drippings.
2. Add neck or breast of lamb, cut in small pieces.
3. Season with white pepper, salt and parsley.
4. Cook until tender.
5. Just before serving season with dill.

title: Lamplice
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of currants
- half a pound of raisins
- half a pound of sugar
- a little citron
- ground cloves
- ground cinnamon
- eight apples finely chopped
- a rich puff paste
- one ounce of fresh butter
- a tea-cup full of clarified sugar
- essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of currants, the same quantity of raisins and sugar, a little citron, ground cloves and cinnamon, with eight apples finely chopped
2. Mix all together
3. Cut a rich puff paste into small triangles
4. Fill them with the fruit like puffs and lay them in a deep dish
5. Place the pieces closely together
6. When the dish is full, pour over one ounce of fresh butter melted in a tea-cup full of clarified sugar, flavoured with essence of lemon
7. Bake in an oven not too brisk

title: Lamplich
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight medium-sized apples
- one-half pound raisins
- one-half pound currants
- one-half pound sugar
- citron peel
- two or three cloves
- one teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
- puff paste
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- one teacup of thick syrup
- essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Chop finely apples, raisins, currants, sugar, citron peel, cloves and cinnamon to make a mince-meat
2. Cut puff paste into little triangles
3. Fill with the mince, turning the corners of the paste over it to make little puffs
4. Place these closely together on a buttered baking-dish until it is full
5. Mix melted butter with thick syrup flavored with essence of lemon
6. Pour it over the puffs
7. Bake until done in a rather slow oven

title: Lavender Water
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three drachms of English oil of lavender
- one pint spirits of wine
- one ounce of orange flower-water
- one ounce of rose-water
- four ounces of distilled water
- three drachms of essence of ambergris or musk

## Directions

1. Take three drachms of English oil of lavender and one pint spirits of wine
2. shake in a quart bottle
3. then add one ounce of orange flower-water, one ounce of rose-water, and four ounces of distilled water
4. those who approve of the musky odour which lavender water sometimes has, may add three drachms of essence of ambergris or musk

title: Leaf Puffs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sifted flour
- sweet cream
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of butter until soft, add two cups of sifted flour, mix well, and add just enough sweet cream to make a nice dough, not too soft.
2. Roll thin, cut in long strips or squares, bake in long pans in a moderately hot oven.
3. When light brown, draw to the door of the oven, sprinkle with powdered sugar and let stand a few minutes longer in the oven.

title: Leberknadel (Calf Liver Dumplings)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound calf's liver
- four ounces marrow
- thyme
- one clove garlic
- pepper
- salt
- lemon peel
- yolk of two eggs
- one whole egg
- grated bread crumbs or rolled crackers
- boiling salt water
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Chop and pass through a colander one-half pound of calf's liver
2. Rub to a cream four ounces of marrow, add the liver and stir hard
3. Add a little thyme, one clove of garlic grated, pepper, salt and a little grated lemon peel, the yolks of two eggs and one whole egg
4. Add enough grated bread crumbs or rolled crackers to permit its being formed into little marbles
5. Drop in boiling salt water and let cook fifteen minutes
6. Drain, roll in fine crumbs and fry in hot fat

title: Lebkuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one pound of brown sugar
- one-eighth pound of citron shredded
- one-eighth pound of shelled walnuts (broken)
- one and one-half cups of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- two teaspoons of cinnamon
- one-fourth teaspoon of allspice

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and brown sugar well.
2. Add citron and walnuts.
3. Add flour, baking-powder, cinnamon, and allspice.
4. Spread the dough in long pans with well-floured hands, have about one and one-half inches thick.
5. Bake in very moderate oven.
6. When baked, cut in squares and spread with icing.
7. Set in a cool stove or the sun to dry.

title: Leek Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a small piece of butter
- six or eight leeks
- water
- salt
- pepper
- piece of stale bread
- croutons
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Put a small piece of butter in saucepan and then six or eight leeks cut in small pieces
2. Keep turning for about five minutes so they will get brown
3. add water for amount desired
4. season with salt and pepper and put in piece of stale bread
5. Strain through the strainer
6. Put in croutons and serve with grated cheese

title: Lekach
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of sifted flour
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- three eggs
- one teaspoon of allspice
- one teaspoon of soda
- the grated rind and juice of one-half lemon
- three tablespoons of honey
- almond or walnut

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well.
2. Roll on board to one-fourth inch in thickness and cut with form.
3. Brush with white of egg or honey diluted with water.
4. On each cake put an almond or walnut.
5. Bake in moderate oven from fifteen to twenty minutes.

title: Lemon Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup butter
- 1.5 cups pulverized sugar
- 3 eggs
- 0.5 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking-powder
- 0.5 lemon

## Directions

1. Rub to a cream one-half cup of butter with one and one-half cups of pulverized sugar
2. Add gradually the yolks of three eggs, one at a time, and one-half cup of sweet milk
3. Sift two cups of flour with one teaspoon of baking-powder, add alternately with the milk and the stiffly-beaten whites of three eggs
4. Add the grated peel of one-half lemon and the juice of one lemon
5. Bake in moderate oven thirty minutes

title: Lemon Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- old carrots
- salted water
- one teaspoon of minced parsley
- loaf sugar
- one-half teaspoon of paprika
- one tablespoon of butter
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Pare and cut carrots into dice, and simmer in salted water until tender, but not pulpy
2. Drain, return to the fire, and for one pint of carrots add one teaspoon of minced parsley, a grating of loaf sugar, one-half teaspoon of paprika, one tablespoon of butter and the juice of half a lemon
3. Heat through, shaking the dish now and then, so that each piece of the vegetable will be well coated with the mixture or dressing

title: Lemon Cream Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of five eggs
- one-half cup of granulated sugar
- juice of three lemons
- grated rind of one lemon
- water
- beaten whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Put on to boil the yolks of five eggs, one-half cup of granulated sugar, the juice of three lemons and grated rind of one, and about a brandy glass of water
2. Stir constantly so as to prevent curdling
3. When it has thickened and comes to a boil take it from the range and add the beaten whites of eggs

title: Lemon Creams
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 3/8 pint of water
- 8 yolks
- juice of three large lemons
- rind of one lemon
- whites of eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. 1 lb. of loaf sugar melted in 3/8 pint of water.
2. Beat 8 yolks well, squeeze the juice of three large lemons, scrape the rind of one on several lumps of sugar, throw them into the water.
3. Put the juice of the lemon into the sugar and water, mix it well, then stir in the yolks of the eggs, stirring well all the time.
4. Strain through a fine sieve, put it into a jug with a spout, stand the jug in a saucepan of boiling water over the fire, stirring all the time till it thickens like a custard.
5. Have the whites beat to a stiff snow, stir it when you take it off, till it looks quite light.
6. Fill your custard glasses, and put a little cinnamon on top.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Lemon Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons vinegar
- one sliced onion
- six whole peppers
- salt
- one piece stick cinnamon
- water
- sliced fish
- two egg yolks
- two lemons
- sugar
- twelve grated almonds
- parsley
- sliced lemons

## Directions

1. Boil three tablespoons of vinegar, one sliced onion, six whole peppers, salt, one piece of stick cinnamon, and a little water
2. Add sliced fish
3. When fish has boiled twenty minutes remove and arrange on platter
4. Strain the gravy and add the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, juice of two lemons, sugar to taste and twelve grated almonds
5. Let all come to a boil
6. Pour over the fish
7. Sprinkle finely chopped parsley on top and garnish with sliced lemons

title: Lemon Ginger Sherbet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two lemons
- eight lemons
- one-fourth pound of ginger
- four cups of sugar
- two quarts of boiling water
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Shave very thin bits of the yellow peel from two lemons, being careful not to get any of the white
2. Cut eight lemons (using the first two) into halves
3. Extract seeds and press out the juice
4. Cut one-fourth pound of ginger in strips
5. Boil until clear, four cups of sugar, two quarts of boiling water, ginger and shaved lemon peel
6. Add lemon juice and strain through a cheese-cloth
7. Freeze until thick and add the stiff-beaten whites of two eggs
8. Mix well
9. Finish freezing, and pack

title: Lemon Ice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large, juicy lemons
- peel of three lemons
- two oranges
- juice of both oranges
- peel of one orange
- one pint of sugar

## Directions

1. Take six large, juicy lemons and grate peel of three lemons
2. Take two oranges, juice of both, and peel of one
3. Squeeze out every drop of juice
4. Steep the grated peel of lemon and orange in juice for an hour
5. Strain and mix in one pint of sugar
6. Stir until dissolved and freeze

title: Lemon Jelly for Layer Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- yolks of eight eggs with two whole ones
- juice of five large lemons
- grated peel of two lemons
- one-quarter pound of butter

## Directions

1. Take one pound of sugar, yolks of eight eggs with two whole ones, the juice of five large lemons, the grated peel of two, and one-quarter pound of butter
2. Put the sugar, lemon and butter into saucepan and melt over a gentle fire
3. When all is dissolved, stir in the eggs which have been beaten, stir rapidly until it is thick as honey
4. Spread some of this between the layers of cake
5. Pack the remainder in jelly glasses

title: Lemon Pie, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich puff paste
- a large juicy lemon
- one cup of boiling water
- one cup of sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- one teaspoon of cornstarch
- one teaspoon of butter
- whites of eggs
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cover the reverse side of a deep pie-plate with a rich puff paste, and bake a light brown
2. Remove from the oven until the filling is prepared
3. Take a large juicy lemon, grate and peel and squeeze out every drop of juice
4. put it into a cup of boiling water to extract every particle of juice
5. Put the cup of water on to boil with the lemon juice and grated peel, and a cup of sugar
6. beat up the yolks of four eggs very light and add to this gradually the boiling lemon juice
7. Return to the kettle and boil
8. wet a teaspoon of cornstarch with a very little cold water, and add also a teaspoon of butter
9. when the boiling mixture has thickened remove from the fire and let it cool
10. Beat up the whites of the eggs to a very stiff froth, add half of the froth to the lemon mixture and reserve the other half for the top of the pie
11. Bake the lemon cream in the baked pie-crust
12. Add a few tablespoons of powdered sugar and half a teaspoon of cream of tartar

title: Lemon Pie, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Nice pie crust
- Two slices of bread about two inches thick
- One cup of boiling water
- One dessertspoon of butter
- Juice and rind of one lemon
- One cup of sugar
- Yolks of two eggs
- Salt
- White of two eggs
- Four tablespoons of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Line a deep pie-plate with nice crust
2. Remove the crust from two slices of bread about two inches thick, pour over it one cup of boiling water
3. Add one dessertspoon of butter, and beat until the bread is well soaked and smooth
4. Add the juice and rind of one lemon, one cup of sugar, the yolks of two eggs well beaten, and a little salt
5. Mix well
6. Fill pie with mixture and bake in hot oven until firm
7. Beat white of two eggs to a stiff froth, add four tablespoons of powdered sugar and spread on top and brown

title: Lemon Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- seven lemons
- water
- two pounds of sugar
- two cups of water
- one-half pound of large raisins

## Directions

1. Take seven lemons, slice thin and remove seeds
2. Draw string through slices, fasten ends
