3. Lay them in a pan with water
4. Boil a short time
5. Remove the lemon, pour off water
6. Cook two pounds of sugar with two cups of water
7. When the sugar is syrupy add one-half pound of large raisins
8. Put in the lemon and let cook until the syrup is thick

title: Lemon Puffs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- beaten whites of four eggs
- two cups of milk
- salt
- three tablespoons of fresh butter melted
- five level tablespoons of flour
- butter for cups

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of four eggs until very light, add the stiffly-beaten whites and then stir in two cups of milk, add a pinch of salt, three tablespoons of fresh butter melted, and five level tablespoons of flour that have been wet with a little of the milk.
2. Stir well together and divide equally between cups.
3. Butter the cups before pouring in the mixture.
4. Bake in hot oven until brown (generally twenty minutes).
5. Turn out carefully in the dish in which they are to be served, and pour over them the lemon sauce.

title: Lemon Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- soup stock
- a few slices of lemon
- a little sugar
- grated nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- a teaspoon of flour or yolk of egg

## Directions

1. Boil some soup stock with a few slices of lemon, a little sugar and grated nutmeg
2. Add chopped parsley
3. Thicken with a teaspoon of flour or yolk of egg

title: Lemon Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of water
- juice of two lemons
- sugar
- cinnamon bark
- three teaspoons of cornstarch
- cold water
- yolks of two eggs
- beaten whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Put on to boil one and one-half cups of water with juice of two lemons, sweeten to taste, add a few small pieces of cinnamon bark.
2. When boiling stir in three teaspoons of cornstarch that have been dissolved in a little cold water.
3. Boil a few minutes, then pour over the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, stirring all the time.
4. Stir in stiffly-beaten whites of eggs, and pour over and around puffs when cold.
5. Serve cold.

title: Lemon Sauce, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one-half cup of water
- rind of one lemon
- juice of two lemons
- one-half teaspoon of butter
- one scant teaspoon of cornstarch
- cold water

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of sugar with one-half cup of water, rind of one lemon, juice of two, and one-half teaspoon of butter
2. When boiling stir in a scant teaspoon of cornstarch dissolved in a little cold water
3. Serve hot

title: Lemon Sauce, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strained juice of two lemons
- grated peel of one lemon
- one cup of sugar
- one glass of white wine or water
- yolks of three eggs
- whites of three eggs
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the strained juice of two lemons and the grated peel of one with a cup of sugar and one glass of white wine or water
2. When boiled to a syrup add the yolks of three eggs well beaten
3. also half of the whites beaten to a froth
4. Use the other half of the stiffly-beaten whites, sweetened with powdered sugar, to decorate the sauce
5. Serve immediately

title: Lemon Souffle
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of the crumb of bread
- pint of almond milk
- 2 oz. of melted fat
- rind of 2 fresh lemons
- 1/2 lb. powdered sugar
- juice of a lemon
- yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Crumble 1 lb. of the crumb of bread
2. Pour over a pint of almond milk and 2 oz. of the melted fat
3. Beat this all up together till it is like a cream
4. Grate the rind of 2 fresh lemons
5. Add 1/2 lb. powdered sugar and the juice of a lemon
6. When this is all well mixed stir in the yolks of 6 eggs, well beaten
7. Then stir in the whites beat to a snow
8. Put it in a greased mould
9. Bake the same as the others
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Lemon Tart
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- the peel of six lemons
- the juice of one lemon
- a quarter of a pound of pounded almonds
- a quarter of a pound of preserved lemon and orange peel
- half a pound of powdered white sugar
- six eggs well beaten

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of six lemons and add the juice of one
2. Mix with a quarter of a pound of pounded almonds, a quarter of a pound of preserved lemon and orange peel, half a pound of powdered white sugar, and six eggs well beaten
3. Mix all together and bake in a dish lined with a fine pastry

title: Lemon Tart (Fleischig)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- three whole eggs
- yolks of three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- juice of three lemons
- grated rind of one lemon
- juice of one orange
- three egg whites
- three tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Make a rich crust and bake in small spring form
2. Beat three whole eggs and yolks of three very light with one cup of sugar
3. Add juice of three lemons and grated rind of one and juice of one orange
4. Put whole on stove and stir until it comes to a boil
5. Put on baked crust
6. Spread a meringue made of the remaining three whites and three tablespoons of sugar on top
7. Put in oven to brown

title: Lemon Tart Another Way
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- six lemons sliced
- sugar
- two savoy biscuits
- three ounces of orange and lemon peel
- three ounces of ground almonds
- one ounce of whole almonds blanched

## Directions

1. Slice six lemons and lay them in sugar all night
2. Mix with them two savoy biscuits, three ounces of orange and lemon peel, three ounces of ground almonds, one ounce of whole almonds blanched
3. Bake in a dish lined with pastry

title: Lemonade in Large Quantities
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen lemons
- one pound of sugar
- one gallon of water

## Directions

1. Take one dozen lemons, one pound of sugar and one gallon of water to make lemonade for twenty people.

title: Lentil Sausages
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- bread crumbs
- onion
- powdered mixed herbs
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- drippings
- egg
- flour
- fat

## Directions

1. For each person soak one tablespoon of lentils overnight
2. Then drain and leave them spread on a dish for a day
3. When ready to use, chop them finely and cook gently in a covered jar in an outer vessel of water for about one hour, adding from time to time just as much water as they will absorb
4. When fully cooked, stir in about twice their bulk in bread crumbs (preferably whole wheat), a slight flavoring of very finely chopped onion, powdered mixed herbs and nutmeg, salt and pepper to taste, and drippings to make the whole fairly moist
5. When cool, shape into sausages (or cutlets or round cakes for luncheon)
6. coat them with egg and bread crumbs or seasoned flour
7. brown them in a little fat in a frying-pan or in a fairly hot oven
8. Gravy or diluted meat extract should be served with them
9. They are no less good when fried overnight and reheated in the gravy

title: Lentil Soup (Linzen), No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups lentils
- cold water
- one sliced onion
- two tablespoons drippings
- three stalks celery, cut in small pieces
- celery seed
- pepper
- salt
- two quarts warm water

## Directions

1. Soak two cups of lentils over night in cold water
2. Drain and add to a sliced onion which has been browned in two tablespoons of drippings
3. When these have been fried for five minutes, add three stalks of celery cut in small pieces or some celery seed, pepper and salt to taste, and two quarts of warm water
4. Boil all these slowly, stirring occasionally until the lentils are quite soft
5. Pass all through a sieve, return to saucepan, heat again and serve

title: Lentil Soup, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- water
- smoked meat
- one cup strained tomatoes
- small slices sausage

## Directions

1. Make same as Dried Pea Soup
2. One cup of strained tomatoes may be added or small slices of sausage

title: Lettuce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- oil
- white wine vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. After dressing the salad, mix it in one tablespoon of oil, then take only two tablespoons of white wine vinegar, mixed with a very little pepper and salt, and just turn the lettuce over and over in this mixture.

title: Lettuce Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh lettuce leaves
- thin layers of bread
- Mayonnaise or Boiled Dressing

## Directions

1. Wash and dry fresh lettuce leaves.
2. Put lettuce leaves between thin layers of bread.
3. Spread with Mayonnaise or Boiled Dressing.

title: Lima Bean Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of cold, cooked Lima beans
- two stalks of celery
- one dozen olives
- onion juice
- salt
- red pepper
- lettuce leaves
- French dressing
- green and red peppers

## Directions

1. Take two cups of cold, cooked Lima beans, two stalks of chopped celery, one dozen chopped olives, one teaspoon of onion juice, one teaspoon of salt, and a dash of red pepper
2. Mix thoroughly and serve on lettuce leaves with French dressing and garnish with green and red peppers cut in squares

title: Ling Fish Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fish
- onion
- parsley
- marjoram
- nutmeg
- pepper
- dried currants
- eggs
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. chop the cold fish nicely with onion, parsley, a little marjoram, grated nutmeg, pepper, and a few dried currants
2. mix it up with 2 or 3 eggs
3. fry in hot oil or butter
4. Dish them up on a fish napkin with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Linseed Bandoline
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cupful of linseed
- boiling water
- three table spoonfuls of rose-water

## Directions

1. Take a cupful of linseed
2. pour over it sufficient boiling water to cover
3. let it stand some hours
4. pour over three table spoonfuls of rose-water
5. stir the seeds well about
6. strain it off into a bottle

title: Linser Tart
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound flour
- one-half pound sugar
- one-half pound almonds grated with peel on
- two eggs
- allspice
- citron
- salt
- brandy
- strawberry jam

## Directions

1. Make a dough of flour, sugar, almonds, eggs, allspice, citron, salt flavored with brandy
2. Take a little more than half and roll it out
3. Line a pie-pan
4. Put strawberry jam on
5. Cut rest of dough in strips and cover the same as prune pie
6. Brush strips with yolk of egg
7. Bake in moderate oven

title: Linzer Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- four whole eggs
- three-fourths pound of pounded almonds or walnuts
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-fourth teaspoon of cloves
- one pound of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- a few drops of bitter almond essence
- apricot, strawberry, or raspberry jam
- pineapple marmalade

## Directions

1. Cream one pound of butter with one pound of sugar until foamy
2. Add one by one four whole eggs
3. Mix well
4. Stir in three-fourths pound of pounded almonds or walnuts, one teaspoon of cinnamon, one-fourth teaspoon of cloves, one pound of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, and a few drops of bitter almond essence
5. Put in four layer pans and bake in slow oven
6. Put together with apricot, strawberry, or raspberry jam and pineapple marmalade, each layer having a different preserve
7. Ice top and sides

title: Lip Salve
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of white wax
- one ounce of beef marrow
- a small piece of alkanet root

## Directions

1. Mix together one ounce of white wax, the same of beef marrow, with a small piece of alkanet root tied up in muslin
2. Perfume it according to fancy
3. Strain and pot while hot

title: Little French Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-fourth cup of butter
- one-fourth cup of sugar
- one cup of flour
- one egg
- half a pint of milk
- jam

## Directions

1. Beat one-fourth cup of butter to a cream with one-fourth cup of sugar.
2. Add one cup of flour.
3. Stir well.
4. Add one egg which has been beaten into half a pint of milk, a little at a time.
5. Fill buttered saucers with the mixture.
6. Bake.
7. When done, place the cakes one on top of another with jam spread between.

title: Little Short Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of flour
- four ounces of butter
- four ounces of white powdered sugar
- two eggs
- orange flower-water
- sweet wine

## Directions

1. Rub into a pound of flour four ounces of butter, four ounces of white powdered sugar, and two eggs
2. Make it into a paste and roll it thin
3. Cut into small cakes with tin cutters
4. A little orange flower-water or sweet wine improve the flavour of these cakes

title: Liver Kloese (Dumplings)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a small onion minced
- one tablespoon of chicken fat
- a small liver chopped fine
- chopped parsley
- two tablespoons of flour
- nutmeg
- red and white pepper
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Brown a small onion minced in one tablespoon of chicken fat
2. Add a small liver chopped fine, chopped parsley, two tablespoons of flour
3. Season with nutmeg, red and white pepper, and add two eggs
4. Drop with teaspoon in the boiling soup
5. Let cook ten minutes and serve

title: Loaf Cocoanut Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of milk
- whites of four eggs
- three cups of flour
- three teaspoons of baking-powder
- a grated cocoanut

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter and two cups of sugar to a cream
2. Add one cup of milk and whites of four eggs
3. Add three cups of flour (measure after sifting) and three teaspoons of baking-powder added in last sifting
4. Add a grated cocoanut and last the stiffly-beaten whites
5. Line tin with buttered paper
6. Bake in a loaf

title: Lotion for Removing Freckles
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dram of spirit of salts
- half a pint of rain water
- half a tea-spoonful of spirit of lavender

## Directions

1. Mix one dram of spirit of salts, half a pint of rain water, and half a tea-spoonful of spirit of lavender
2. Bottle for use

title: Macaroni Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of macaroni
- salted water
- one-half pint of milk
- one-half pint of thick white sauce
- two ounces of grated cheese
- two well-beaten eggs
- browned bread crumbs
- stewed tomatoes and mushrooms

## Directions

1. Break three ounces of macaroni into small pieces
2. Boil in rapidly boiling salted water
3. When tender drain off the water and add half a pint of milk
4. Cook slowly till the macaroni has absorbed most of the milk
5. To half a pint of thick white sauce add two ounces of grated cheese and mix with the macaroni
6. Add two well-beaten eggs
7. Butter a pudding mold, sprinkle it with browned bread crumbs and pour in the macaroni mixture
8. Steam gently for about half an hour
9. Turn out and fill the centre with stewed tomatoes and mushrooms

title: Macaroni with Cheese
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound macaroni
- boiling water
- salt
- 1/2-lb. Dutch cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Have a saucepan ready with boiling water with a little salt.
2. Put in the macaroni, let them boil till they look white and soft.
3. Take them out, lay them on a sieve to drain.
4. Have a deep dish ready, grate 1/2-lb. Dutch cheese, not too fresh.
5. Put a layer of macaroni in the dish with some pieces of butter and a layer of grated cheese.
6. Continue the layers till you have used the macaroni, cheese, and butter, 1/2-lb. will be required for this dish.
7. Put it into the oven for 20 minutes.
8. It must be sent to table in the same dish it is baked in.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Macaroon Island
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroons
- lady fingers
- nuts
- crystallized cherries
- one cup of wine
- one cup of sugar
- one-half cup of water
- sweetened and flavored cream

## Directions

1. Fill a glass bowl with alternate layers of macaroons and lady fingers, sprinkle a layer of finely-chopped nuts over the cake, then a layer of crystallized cherries.
2. Boil one cup of wine, one cup of sugar and one-half cup of water together until syrupy and thick, pour it over the contents of the bowl.
3. Let this cool, then place a thick layer of thickly-whipped sweetened and flavored cream over all.
4. Serve very cold.

title: Macaroon Tarts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich pie dough
- preserve
- whites of three eggs
- one-half pound of powdered sugar
- one-half pound of grated almonds
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Line a gem or muffin-pan with rich pie dough
2. Half fill each tart with any desired preserve
3. Bake in a quick oven
4. Beat the whites of three eggs to a stiff froth
5. Add one-half pound of powdered sugar and stir about ten minutes or until very light
6. Gradually add one-half pound of grated almonds
7. Divide this macaroon paste into equal portions
8. Roll and shape into strips, dusting hands with powdered sugar
9. Place these strips on the baked tarts in parallel rows to cross each other diagonally
10. Return to oven and bake in a slow oven about fifteen minutes
11. Let remain in pans until almost cold

title: Maccaroni and Cheese
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- maccaroni
- milk or water
- butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil some maccaroni in milk or water until tender, then drain them and place on a dish with bits of butter and grated Parmesan cheese
2. when the dish is filled grate more cheese over it and brown before the fire

title: Macr Otes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of French roll dough
- six ounces of fresh butter
- two eggs
- flour
- best Florence oil
- clarified sugar
- essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Take one pound of French roll dough, six ounces of fresh butter, two eggs, and as much flour as will be requisite to knead it together
2. Roll it into the form of a long French roll and cut it in thin round slices
3. Set them at a short distance from the fire to rise
4. Fry in the best Florence oil
5. When nearly cold, dip them in clarified sugar flavored with essence of lemon

title: Macrotes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of good light dough
- two eggs
- six ounces of butter
- flour
- oil
- thick syrup
- essence of lemon

## Directions

1. Blend one pound of good light dough with two eggs and six ounces of butter
2. Add as much flour as may be needed to make the whole sufficiently dry
3. Make it into the shape of a French roll
4. Cut off rather thin slices
5. Place before the fire to rise
6. Fry in oil
7. Let them drain carefully
8. When nearly cold dip each in very thick syrup flavored with essence of lemon

title: Macrotes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of good light dough
- two eggs
- six ounces of butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Blend one pound of good light dough with two eggs, six ounces of butter, and add as much flour as may be needed to make the whole sufficiently dry.
2. Make it into the shape of a French roll, and cut off rather thin slices.
3. Place slices before the fire to rise.
4. Fry in oil.
5. Let them drain carefully.
6. When nearly cold dip each in very thick syrup flavored with essence of lemon.

title: Maintenon Cutlets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- greased paper
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- lemon peel
- thyme
- beaten eggs
- piquante sauce

## Directions

1. Make a paste of bread crumbs, chopped parsley, nutmeg, pepper, salt, grated lemon peel, and thyme, with a couple of beaten eggs
2. spread this seasoning on the cutlets
3. broil or fry cutlets in greased paper
4. serve a piquante sauce in a tureen

title: Maitre d'Hotel Butter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup butter
- lemon juice
- one teaspoon minced parsley

## Directions

1. Work into one-half cup of butter all the lemon juice it will take, and add a teaspoon of minced parsley.

title: Maitre d'Hotel Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a heaping tablespoon of drippings or goose-fat
- two teaspoons of flour
- a small cup of hot soup or water
- chopped parsley
- the juice of a lemon
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a heaping tablespoon of drippings or goose-fat, heat it in a spider
2. Stir two teaspoons of flour into this
3. Add gradually and carefully a small cup of hot soup or water
4. Add some chopped parsley, also the juice of a lemon
5. Add salt and pepper
6. Stir up well

title: Mamouras (Turkish)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one matzoth for each person to be served
- boiling salted water
- olive oil
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Dip in boiling salted water for one minute, one matzoth for each person to be served
2. Put the soaked matzoth in a dish
3. Pour over it a little olive oil and grated cheese and repeat this until you have made as many layers as you have persons to serve
4. Cut in slices and serve
5. Use Hashkeval — Greek Cheese

title: Mandel (Almond) Strudel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as for Apple Strudel No. 2
- one-half pound of almonds
- yolks of four eggs
- one-quarter pound of granulated sugar
- grated peel of one lemon
- oil, butter or fat

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for Apple Strudel No. 2
2. Blanch one-half pound of almonds and grind
3. When dried beat the yolks of four eggs light with one-quarter pound of granulated sugar
4. Add the grated peel of one lemon and mix in the almonds
5. Spread over the dough with plenty of oil, butter or fat and roll
6. Bake; baste very often

title: Maple Bisque
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of maple syrup
- yolks of three eggs
- whites of eggs
- one pint of cream
- one-half cup of chopped nuts

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of maple syrup until quite thick
2. Beat yolks of three eggs
3. Add to syrup while hot, stirring constantly until well mixed
4. Let cool
5. Beat whites of eggs to a froth
6. Whip one pint of cream
7. Mix all together
8. Add one-half cup of chopped nuts
9. Have a pudding-mold buttered and see that the edges fit close
10. Pack in rock salt and ice four hours

title: Maple Mousse
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of cream
- two eggs
- one-half cup of maple syrup

## Directions

1. Whip one pint of cream until quite thick
2. Break two eggs into another bowl and beat until light
3. Add gradually one-half cup of maple syrup
4. When the two are well mixed, whip them gradually into the cream
5. Pour the whole into a freezer can, without the dasher
6. Cover
7. Pack in ice and salt and let stand for three hours

title: Maple Sugar Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of maple sugar
- one-half cup of boiling water
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Boil two cups of maple sugar with one-half cup of boiling water until it threads from the spoon
2. Pour it upon the beaten whites of two eggs and beat until cold
3. Spread between layers and on top of cake

title: Maraschino Lemonade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of four lemons
- twelve tablespoons of sugar
- eight cups of water
- one cup of maraschino liquor
- a few cherries

## Directions

1. Take the juice of four lemons, twelve tablespoons of sugar, eight cups of water, one cup of maraschino liquor and a few cherries.

title: Marble Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking-powder
- bitter chocolate
- lemon
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Take two cups of sugar, one cup of butter, four eggs (yolks), one cup of milk, three cups of flour, and three teaspoons of baking-powder (scant)
2. Cream the butter and sugar, and add the yolks of eggs
3. Then add the milk, flour, baking-powder, and the beaten whites of the eggs; flavor with lemon
4. To make the brown part; take a square of bitter chocolate and melt above steam, and mix with some of the white
5. Flavor the brown with vanilla
6. Put first a tablespoon of brown batter in the pan, and then the white
7. Bake in quick oven thirty-five minutes

title: Marinated (Pickled) Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- new Holland herring
- milk or water
- onions
- lemon
- cloves
- whole peppers
- bay leaves
- capers
- whole mustard seed
- brown sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the heads and scales from new Holland herring.
2. Wash well, open them and take out the milch and lay the herring and milch in milk or water over night.
3. Next day lay the herring in a stone jar with alternate layers of onions cut up, also lemon cut in slices, a few cloves, whole peppers and a few bay leaves, some capers and whole mustard seed.
4. Take the milch and rub it through a hair sieve.
5. Stir in a spoon of brown sugar and vinegar and pour it over the herring.

title: Marinated Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pickerel, pike or any fish that is not fat
- salt
- whole onion
- two cups of vinegar
- one cup of water
- four bay leaves
- twelve allspice
- pepper
- ginger

## Directions

1. Take pickerel, pike or any fish that is not fat, cut into two-inch slices, wash well, salt and set aside in a cool place for a few hours.
2. When ready to cook, wash slightly so as not to remove all salt from fish.
3. Take heads and set up to boil with a whole onion for twenty-five minutes.
4. Add the other pieces and two cups of vinegar, one cup of water, four bay leaves and twelve allspice, a little pepper and ginger.
5. Cook for thirty-five minutes longer.
6. Taste fish, add a little water or a little more vinegar to taste.
7. Remove fish carefully so as not to break the pieces and let cool.
8. Strain the sauce, return fish to same, adding a few bay leaves and allspice.
9. Set in a cool place until sauce forms a jelly around the fish.

title: Marmalades
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar

## Directions

1. Measure the fruit and sugar, allowing one pint of sugar to each quart of fruit
2. If the marmalade is made from berries the fruit should be rubbed through a sieve to remove the seeds
3. If large fruit is used have it washed, pared, cored, and quartered
4. Rinse the preserving kettle with cold water that there may be a slight coat of moisture on the sides and bottom
5. Put alternate layers of fruit and sugar in the kettle, having the first layer fruit
6. Heat slowly, stirring frequently
7. While stirring, break up the fruit as much as possible
8. Cook about two hours, then put in small sterilized jars

title: Marmelitta
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of coarse cornmeal
- four cups of cold water
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- butter
- sharp cheese or pot cheese or cream cheese

## Directions

1. Take two cups of coarse cornmeal and four cups of cold water, put on to boil
2. Add one-half teaspoon of salt
3. Stir the cornmeal continually and when done place on platter
4. Spread with butter, sharp cheese or any cheese such as pot or cream cheese
5. To be eaten warm

title: Marrons Glace, or Chestnuts in Preserve
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 dozen of picked large Spanish chestnuts
- 2 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 1 pint of water
- fine castor sugar
- vanille

## Directions

1. Parboil the chestnuts
2. Peel them very carefully with a small knife and pick out the pieces of inside skin with a darning needle
3. Put in a preserving pan 2 lbs. of loaf sugar with 1 pint of water and let it boil to a syrup
4. Put the chestnuts in the syrup and let them boil up
5. Take each out carefully and lay them on a large dish, one not to touch the other
6. Roll them well over with fine castor sugar
7. Let the syrup boil up again and dip the chestnuts in again
8. Take them out and put a few drops of vanille into the syrup
9. Let it boil till quite thick and dip each chestnut in again
10. Roll each chestnut in fine sugar and dip them again
11. Keep each chestnut separate and put them in a dry place till quite cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Marrow Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon marrow
- pinch of salt
- little nutmeg
- yolk of one egg
- finely chopped parsley
- matzoth meal

## Directions

1. One tablespoon marrow creamed.
2. Add a pinch of salt, little nutmeg and the yolk of one egg mixed in gradually; some finely chopped parsley and then enough matzoth meal to hold.
3. Wet the hands and roll the mixture into small balls.
4. Add to the boiling soup, and boil fifteen minutes.

title: Marrow Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Half-pound of marrow
- 1/2 lb. sifted sugar
- 1/2 lb. of ground almonds
- 2 oz. of candied lemon and orange peel
- orange-flower water
- 8 eggs
- good puff paste

## Directions

1. Beat marrow to a froth
2. Mix in 1/2 lb. sifted sugar, 1/2 lb. of ground almonds
3. Cut up 2 oz. of candied lemon and orange peel
4. Flavour with orange-flower water
5. Beat in 8 eggs, each one beat in separately
6. Line a dish with good puff paste
7. Lay in the mixture, crossbar
8. Bake it in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Marrow-Bones
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Marrow-bones
- thick dough
- red pepper
- toast
- cloth

## Directions

1. Have the bones cut into pieces two or three inches long
2. Scrape and wash them very clean
3. Spread a little thick dough on each end to keep the marrow in
4. Tie each bone in a piece of cloth and boil them for one hour
5. Remove the cloth and paste, and place each bone on a square of toast
6. Sprinkle with red pepper and serve very hot
7. Or the marrow-bone can be boiled without being cut, the marrow then removed with a spoon and placed on squares of hot toast
8. Serve for luncheon

title: Marrow-Bones
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bones
- thick dough
- cloth
- toast
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Have the bones cut into pieces two or three inches long.
2. Scrape and wash them very clean.
3. Spread a little thick dough on each end to keep the marrow in.
4. Tie each bone in a piece of cloth and boil them for one hour.
5. Remove the cloth and paste, and place each bone on a square of toast.
6. Sprinkle with red pepper and serve very hot.

title: Marshmallow Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound marshmallows
- one cup of sugar
- one-quarter cup of cold water
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. Melt one-half pound marshmallows over hot water
2. Cook together one cup of sugar and one-quarter cup of cold water until it threads thoroughly
3. Beat up the white of an egg and syrup and mix, then add to the melted marshmallows and beat until creamy and cool
4. Can be used for cake filling or spread between two cookies

title: Marshmallow Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound of marshmallows
- contents of one-half can of pineapple
- one-quarter pound of shelled pecans
- one yolk of egg
- one-half cup of oil
- lemon juice
- two tablespoons of mayonnaise
- four tablespoons of whipped cream
- fresh green lettuce-leaves

## Directions

1. Cut up one-quarter pound of marshmallows into small squares, also contents of one-half can of pineapple
2. Let the marshmallows be mixed with the pineapples quite a while before salad is put together
3. Add to this one-quarter pound of shelled pecans
4. Make a drip mayonnaise of one yolk of egg into which one-half cup of oil is stirred drop by drop
5. Cut this with lemon juice, but do not use any sugar
6. To two tablespoons of mayonnaise, add four tablespoons of whipped cream
7. Serve on fresh, green lettuce-leaves

title: Mashed Potatoes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Boil till perfectly tender; let them be quite dry, and press them through a cullender, or mash and beat them well with a fork
2. Add a piece of butter, and milk, or cream, and continue beating till they are perfectly smooth
3. Return them to the saucepan to warm, or they may be browned before the fire
4. The chief art is to beat them sufficiently long, which renders them light

title: Mashed Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt water
- large tablespoon of butter
- milk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Pare as many potatoes as required
2. Boil in salt water
3. Drain thoroughly when done
4. Mash in the pot with a potato masher
5. Work in a large tablespoon of butter and enough milk to make them resemble dough
6. Do not allow any lumps to form in your dish
7. Garnish with parsley

title: Matrimonies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of flour
- two scant cups of sour milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- yolks of three eggs
- whites of three eggs
- one level teaspoon of soda
- one teaspoon of flour
- butter or butter-substitute
- preserves

## Directions

1. Sift three cups of flour in a bowl
2. Pour in two scant cups of sour milk
3. Beat very thoroughly
4. Add one teaspoon of salt and the well-beaten yolks of three eggs
5. Mix well
6. Add the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs and one level teaspoon of soda sifted with one teaspoon of flour
7. Mix well and fry at once in very hot butter or butter-substitute
8. Baste the grease over them with a spoon until they are nicely browned
9. Serve with preserves

title: Matso Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit powder
- milk
- water
- butter
- egg yolk
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Make a stiff paste with biscuit powder and milk and water
2. Add a little butter, the yolk of an egg, and a little white sugar
3. Cut into pieces and mould with the hand
4. Bake in a brisk oven
5. These cakes should not be too thin

title: Matso Diet Bread
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of white sugar
- a quarter of a pint of water
- eight well-beaten eggs
- one pound of matso flour
- a little grated lemon-peel

## Directions

1. Simmer one pound of white sugar in a quarter of a pint of water
2. Pour hot upon eight well-beaten eggs
3. Beat till cold
4. Add one pound of matso flour and a little grated lemon-peel
5. Bake in a papered tin, or in small tins
6. Remove the cake while hot

title: Matso Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a shin of beef
- four pounds of gravy beef
- a calf's foot
- three or four quarts of water
- celery
- carrots
- turnips
- pepper
- salt
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- half a pound of matso flour
- two ounces of chopped suet
- ginger
- nutmeg
- four beaten eggs
- a small onion
- a desert spoonful of oil

## Directions

1. Boil down half a shin of beef, four pounds of gravy beef, and a calf's foot may be added, if approved, in three or four quarts of water
2. Season with celery, carrots, turnips, pepper and salt, and a bunch of sweet herbs
3. Let the whole stew gently for eight hours, then strain and let it stand to get cold
4. When the fat must be removed, then return it to the saucepan to warm up
5. Ten minutes before serving, throw in the balls
6. To make the balls: Take half a pound of matso flour, two ounces of chopped suet, season with a little pepper, salt, ginger, and nutmeg
7. Mix with this, four beaten eggs, and make it into a paste
8. A small onion shred and browned in a desert spoonful of oil is sometimes added
9. The paste should be made into rather large balls, and care should be taken to make them very light

title: Matzoth Charlotte, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one matzoth
- two eggs
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- one-fourth cup of chopped almonds
- one-fourth cup of raisins
- one-fourth cup of currants

## Directions

1. Soak one matzoth
2. Beat and add to the beaten yolks of two eggs
3. Add one-fourth teaspoon of salt, one-fourth cup of chopped almonds, one-fourth cup of raisins, one-fourth cup of currants
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Fold in the stiffly-beaten whites of two eggs
6. Bake in a greased baking-dish

title: Matzoth Charlotte, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of sugar
- pinch of salt
- three matzoth
- one grated apple
- one lemon
- one-fourth cup of almonds
- one-fourth cup of raisins

## Directions

1. Prepare four egg yolks with one cup of sugar and pinch of salt
2. Soak three matzoth in water and squeeze out
3. Add one grated apple, one lemon rind and juice
4. Add one-fourth cup of almonds, and one-fourth cup of raisins
5. Put the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs in last, before putting into oven
6. Bake in an even oven about one-half to three-quarters of an hour
7. To be eaten warm

title: Matzoth Dipped In Eggs, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- matzoth
- milk
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- grated peel of a lemon

## Directions

1. Beat up as many eggs as are required
2. Into these dip matzoth that have been soaked in milk
3. Fry quickly to a light brown on both sides
4. Lay on a large platter, sprinkle with a mixture of sugar, cinnamon and grated peel of a lemon
5. Fry in butter

title: Matzoth Dipped In Eggs, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one-half tablespoon of salt
- two tablespoons of goose fat or olive oil
- four matzoth
- sugar
- cinnamon
- grated lemon rind

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs very light, add one-half tablespoon of salt
2. Heat two tablespoons of goose fat or olive oil in a spider
3. Break four matzoth into large, equal pieces
4. Dip each piece in the egg mixture and fry a light brown on both sides
5. Serve hot, sprinkled with sugar, cinnamon and a little grated lemon rind

title: Matzoth Eirkuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of water
- one-quarter cup of matzoth meal
- one teaspoon of salt
- yolks of four eggs
- whites of four eggs
- sugar
- jelly or preserves

## Directions

1. Pour one-half cup of water on one-quarter cup of matzoth meal, add one teaspoon of salt
2. Beat the yolks of four eggs very light, add to the meal mixture
3. Let stand five minutes
4. Beat whites of eggs very stiffly, fold lightly into the yolk mixture
5. Drop mixture by spoonfuls in small cakes on hot greased spider
6. Turn when brown and brown on other side
7. Serve with sugar, jelly or preserves

title: Matzoth Kleis, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four matzoth
- cold water
- pepper
- salt
- sugar
- parsley
- half onion
- four eggs
- matzoth meal
- three tablespoons of fat
- minced onions

## Directions

1. Soak four matzoth in cold water and press them after being thoroughly saturated.
2. Add a little pepper, salt, sugar, parsley, and a half onion chopped fine, first browning the onion.
3. Beat four eggs and add all together.
4. Then put in enough matzoth meal so that it may be rolled into balls.
5. The less meal used the lighter will be the balls.
6. They should boil for twenty minutes before serving.
7. Serve matzoth kleis in place of potatoes and garnish with minced onions browned in three tablespoons of fat.

title: Matzoth Kleis, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six matzoth
- three eggs
- two cooking-spoons of chicken fat
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- boiling water
- matzoth meal

## Directions

1. Take six matzoth, three eggs, two cooking-spoons of chicken fat, parsley, onion, salt, pepper and ginger.
2. Soak the matzoth in boiling water a minute, then drain every drop of water out of them.
3. Press through sieve.
4. Fry about three onions in the two tablespoons of chicken fat, and when a light brown, put the matzoth in the spider with the fat and onions to dry them.
5. Add one teaspoon of salt, dash of pepper and ginger and one tablespoon of chopped parsley.
6. Add the three yolks of eggs and beat all this together a few minutes; last, add the well-beaten whites.
7. Form into balls by rolling into a little matzoth meal.
8. Drop in boiling salt water and boil fifteen minutes; drain and pour over them hot fat with an onion, cut fine and browned.

title: Matzoth Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three matzoth
- two tablespoons of fat
- one-quarter pound of matzoth meal
- five eggs
- two ounces of pounded almonds
- grated peel of one-half lemon
- one large sour apple
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Soak three matzoth
2. Heat two tablespoons of fat in a spider
3. Press all the water out of the matzoth with your hands and dry them in the spider of heated fat
4. Add about one-quarter pound of matzoth meal
5. Stir the matzoth and matzoth meal well with a large spoon
6. Add by degrees the yolks of five eggs and two ounces of pounded almonds
7. Add the grated peel of one-half lemon
8. Add one large sour apple, grated, a pinch of salt
9. Last add the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
10. Line a kugeltopf

title: Matzoth Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three matzoth
- two tablespoons of fat
- one-quarter pound of matzoth meal
- yolks of five eggs
- two ounces of pounded almonds
- grated peel of one-half lemon
- one large sour apple
- a pinch of salt
- whites of eggs
- about a quarter pound of hot fat

## Directions

1. Soak three matzoth
2. Heat two tablespoons of fat in a spider
3. Press all the water out of the matzoth with your hands and dry them in the spider of heated fat
4. Add about one-quarter pound of matzoth meal
5. Stir the matzoth and matzoth meal well with a large spoon
6. Add by degrees the yolks of five eggs and two ounces of pounded almonds, and the grated peel of one-half lemon
7. Add one large sour apple, grated, a pinch of salt, and last the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
8. Line a kugeltopf well with fat, and pour about a quarter pound of hot fat over the kugel
9. Bake immediately
10. Serve with wine sauce

title: Matzoth Meal Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- one and a half cups of pulverized sugar
- grated rind of a lemon
- two teaspoons of ground cinnamon
- two squares of chocolate
- one teaspoon of allspice
- juice of an orange
- one-half wine-glass of wine
- three-quarters of a cup of matzoth meal
- one-quarter pound almonds

## Directions

1. To the yolks of eight eggs add one and a half cups of pulverized sugar
2. Stir until the consistency of batter
3. Add the grated rind of a lemon, two teaspoons of ground cinnamon and two squares of chocolate grated, one teaspoon of allspice
4. Add the juice of an orange, and one-half wine-glass of wine
5. Add three-quarters of a cup of matzoth meal finely sifted, and one-quarter pound almonds finely pounded
6. Fold in the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
7. Bake in a moderate oven for three-quarters of an hour
8. Try with a straw

title: Matzoth Meal Kleis, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- matzoth meal
- egg
- ground ginger
- powdered cinnamon
- ground almond
- pepper and salt
- chicken fat or clarified dripping

## Directions

1. To each tablespoon of matzoth meal take one egg.
2. Beat the egg separately, adding a very little ground ginger, powdered cinnamon, ground almond, pepper and salt.
3. Now stir in the matzoth meal and make into a paste with chicken fat or clarified dripping.
4. Form this paste into small balls and boil them for twenty minutes in the Yom-tov soup.

title: Matzoth Meal Kleis, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of chicken schmalz
- one cup of boiling water
- one egg
- matzoth meal
- chopped parsley
- ginger
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Beat one tablespoon of chicken schmalz till quite white; pour one cup of boiling water over one egg.
2. Add it to the dripping; stir these together, then add the flour, seasoning, a little chopped parsley, ginger, pepper and salt, and enough matzoth meal to form into small balls the size of a marble.
3. Drop these into the boiling soup and cook about fifteen minutes.
4. Test one in boiling water and if it boils apart add more meal.

title: Matzoth Meal Macaroons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolk
- one teaspoon of matzoth meal
- pinch of salt
- egg white
- butter or fat
- prunes

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolk separately
2. Add one teaspoon of matzoth meal and pinch of salt
3. Whip white to a snow, fold in the whites
4. Fry by tablespoonfuls in butter or fat
5. Serve with prunes

title: Matzoth Meal Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one-eighth teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons of matzoth meal
- fat

## Directions

1. Add one-eighth teaspoon of salt to two eggs, beat slightly, stir in two tablespoons of matzoth meal.
2. Heat a little fat in spider, pour in egg mixture; when cooked on one side turn on the other.
3. Roll the pancake and cut into noodles one-eighth inch wide.
4. Drop into boiling soup before serving.

title: Matzoth Plum Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of chopped suet
- one-half pound of moist sugar
- one-half pound of raisins (stoned and chopped)
- one-half pound of currants
- one-half pound of mixed peel
- two matzoth soaked in cold water and well drained and beaten
- one-quarter pound of sifted meal
- rind of half a lemon
- one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- eight eggs
- a wineglass of rum

## Directions

1. Beat all ingredients thoroughly together
2. Boil for eight hours in a pudding mold or basin
3. Serve with rum sauce

title: Matzoth Shalet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four soaked matzoth
- nine eggs
- one cup of sugar
- two grated apples
- one and one-half cups of seeded raisins
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- grated rind of an orange or a lemon
- a few pounded almonds
- half a pound of rendered fat

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar, eggs, and cinnamon until light
2. Add all the ingredients, except the matzoth, mixing well
3. Drain the matzoth, gradually adding them to the mixture, beating until very light
4. Melt half a pound of rendered fat into the dish for baking, and then pour in the mixture
5. Bake in a moderately hot oven for one and one-fourth hours
6. Serve hot with wine, fruit, or prune sauce

title: Matzoth Spice Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- one-half tablespoon of matzoth meal per egg
- one tablespoon of sugar per egg
- one-half tablespoon of ground ginger
- one-half tablespoon of cinnamon
- one-fourth tablespoon of cloves

## Directions

1. To every egg add one-half tablespoon of matzoth meal and one tablespoon of sugar
2. Sift meal five times, mix with sugar, one-half tablespoon of ground ginger, one-half tablespoon of cinnamon, one-fourth tablespoon of cloves
3. Mix with the well-beaten yolks
4. Cut and fold in gently the stiffly-beaten whites

title: Matzoth With Scrambled Eggs (Ueberschlagene Matzoth)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six matzoth
- three whole eggs
- four tablespoons of goose fat or oil
- four tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Break six matzoth in small pieces in a colander
2. Pour boiling water through them, drain quickly
3. They should be moist but not soggy
4. Beat three whole eggs well, fold the matzoth in lightly
5. Heat four tablespoons of goose fat or oil in a spider, add the egg mixture
6. Scrape and scramble carefully with spoon from the bottom of the pan, and while scrambling add four tablespoons of sugar
7. Cook gently until eggs are set
8. Serve at once

title: Mayonnaise Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg yolk
- salt
- red pepper
- English mustard
- olive oil
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat the yolk of one egg in a cold dish with a silver or wooden fork
2. If the weather is very warm, place the bowl in a larger vessel filled with chopped ice
3. When the egg is beaten add one-half teaspoon of salt, dash of red pepper, one-half teaspoon of English mustard and olive oil, drop by drop, being careful to beat well without reversing the motion for fear of curdling
4. When the dressing thickens, begin adding the vinegar or lemon juice, drop by drop
5. Then add more olive oil, then more acid, continuing until one cup of olive oil and two teaspoons of vinegar or lemon juice are all used
6. Be sure to have all the ingredients and dishes as cold as possible
7. If the mixture should curdle, begin immediately with a fresh egg in a fresh dish and when it is well beaten add carefully the curdled mixture, drop by drop

title: Mayonnaise Especially for Salmon
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- eight tablespoons of cream
- white pepper
- pinch of salt
- cayenne pepper
- two tablespoons of white vinegar

## Directions

1. Rub the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs to a powder, then add eight tablespoons of cream very gradually to them, also white pepper, a pinch of salt and a mere suspicion of cayenne pepper.
2. Lastly add two tablespoons of white vinegar.
3. It is very important that this last ingredient be put in drop by drop, otherwise the mixture will curdle.

title: Mayonnaise of Flounder
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fillets of flounder
- salt
- lemon juice
- chopped lettuce
- mayonnaise sauce
- slices of tomatoes
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Put some fillets of flounder into boiling water with a little salt and lemon juice, and cook until tender, then drain thoroughly.
2. When cold, put them in the center of some chopped lettuce, cover with mayonnaise sauce and garnish with slices of tomatoes and hard-boiled eggs.

title: Mayonnaise of Tomatoes (Whole)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- lettuce leaf
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Select tomatoes that are of uniform size, round, smooth and spotless
2. Scald and take off outer skin
3. Set away on ice until ready to serve
4. Serve on individual dishes, putting each on a lettuce leaf and pour a tablespoon of mayonnaise dressing over each tomato

title: Mayonnaise Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 2 eggs, very fresh
- a tablespoonful of oil per yolk
- salt
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of white vinegar
- a few capers or chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put the yolks of 2 eggs in your sauceboat
2. To each yolk put a tablespoonful of oil
3. Using a wooden spoon, rub the yolks and oil till it comes quite thick
4. Put in a little salt and pepper
5. Add a tablespoonful of white vinegar, the best that can be got
6. Rub all together
7. You can add a few capers or chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mayonnaise with Whipped Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mayonnaise or French dressing
- one pint whipped cream

## Directions

1. Add one pint of whipped cream to your prepared dressing, stirring thoroughly, just before ready to serve.

title: Meat Balls for Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. chuck steak
- a small piece of onion
- a little parsley
- a little marjoram
- a few crumbs of stale bread
- a little salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- a small piece of lemon peel chopped
- an egg

## Directions

1. Chop very fine 1/2 lb. chuck steak, a small piece of onion, a little parsley, a little marjoram, a few crumbs of stale bread, a little salt, pepper, nutmeg, a small piece of lemon peel chopped
2. Beat up an egg, use only half to mix these balls
3. Mix all the ingredients well, form them into very small balls, sometimes round and sometimes in the shape of small olives
4. Use them as you require
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Meat Balls Rolled in Onions
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large Spanish onions
- 1 lb. of chopped meat
- salt
- pepper
- a little ground mace
- the juice of a lemon
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Boil 2 large Spanish onions and lay them on a plate
2. Open them leaf by leaf
3. Make up 1 lb. of chopped meat according to recipe for forced meat
4. Form it into balls the size of a small apple
5. Roll each ball in one of the leaves
6. Chop an onion in the stewpan with salt, pepper, and a little ground mace
7. Let it boil and put in the balls, keeping the liquor very short
8. When done, stir in the juice of a lemon with a little browning
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Meat Blintzes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potato flour
- eggs
- water
- fat
- meat from soup
- two small onions
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Use the same pancake batter as for Prune Blintzes
2. Fry two small onions with a little fat and chop with the meat
3. Add two eggs, salt and pepper to taste
4. Fill pancakes with meat mixture
5. Fold and fry

title: Meat Dressing for Poultry
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage meat
- fat
- veal
- beef
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- thyme
- celery root
- garlic
- onion
- tomato
- stale bread
- egg

## Directions

1. Have sausage meat chopped, adding a little fat
2. Mix in some veal with the beef while chopping
3. Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg or thyme
4. Grate in a piece of celery root and a piece of garlic about the size of a bean
5. Add a small onion, a minced tomato, a quarter of a loaf of stale bread, also grated
6. Mix up the whole with one egg
7. If you prefer, you may soak the bread, press out every drop of water and dry in a heated skillet with fat

title: Meat Olives
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flank steak
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- onion
- red pepper
- salt
- melted fat
- water
- flour

## Directions

1. Have a flank steak cut in three inch squares
2. Spread each piece with the following dressing: one cup of bread crumbs, two tablespoons of minced parsley, one chopped onion, a dash of red pepper and one teaspoon of salt
3. Moisten with one-fourth cup of melted fat
4. Roll up and tie in shape
5. Cover with water and simmer until meat is tender
6. Take the olives from the sauce and brown in the oven
7. Thicken the sauce with one-fourth cup of flour moistened with water to form a thin paste

title: Meat Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- left-over beef, lamb or veal
- one green pepper
- two cups of potatoes
- one-half cup of carrots
- one onion
- one-fourth cup of flour
- cold water
- one cup of flour
- one heaping teaspoon of drippings
- pinch of salt
- one-fourth teaspoon of baking powder
- one teaspoon of sugar
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut any left-over beef, lamb or veal in small pieces, removing all excess of fat.
2. Parboil one green pepper (seeds removed) cut in strips, two cups of potatoes and one-half cup of carrots cut in dice, and one onion chopped fine.
3. Add to the meat.
4. Thicken with one-fourth cup of flour moistened in cold water.
5. Put in a baking dish.
6. For the crust: Mix one cup of flour, one heaping teaspoon of drippings, pinch of salt, one-fourth teaspoon of baking powder, one teaspoon of sugar and cold water to mix, about one-third cup.
7. Roll out to fit baking dish and cut holes for steam to escape, after covering the contents of the dish.
8. Bake in a quick hot oven one-half hour.

title: Meat Pie with Crust
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- meat strips
- two cups of potatoes
- one-half cup of carrots
- one onion
- one-fourth cup of flour
- cold water
- one cup of flour
- one heaping teaspoon of drippings
- pinch of salt
- one-fourth teaspoon of baking powder
- one teaspoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut meat in strips, potatoes and carrots in dice, and onion chopped fine and add to the meat
2. Thicken with one-fourth cup of flour moistened in cold water
3. Put in a baking dish
4. Make crust: mix one cup of flour, one heaping teaspoon of drippings, pinch of salt, one-fourth teaspoon of baking powder, one teaspoon of sugar and cold water to mix, about one-third cup
5. Roll out to fit baking dish, cut holes for steam to escape, after covering the contents of the dish
6. Bake in a quick hot oven one-half hour

title: Melange
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- sugar (1 pound per pound of fruit)
- red, black, and white cherries
- bananas
- pineapples
- oranges
- peaches
- 1 pint pure alcohol per 2 quarts berries and 2 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Pick two quarts of the largest and soundest berries in the market and lay them in a new and perfectly clean two-gallon stone jar and cover with two pounds of the finest granulated sugar
2. Stone as many pounds of red, black, and white cherries as you wish to use, and add the same quantity of sugar
3. You may also use bananas, pineapples or oranges
4. Seed the latter carefully
5. Be sure to weigh all the fruit, and allow one pound of sugar to every additional pound of fruit
6. Pour over the fruit a pint of pure alcohol
7. Tie up the jar with thick paper
8. In season add peaches

title: Melted Butter
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of butter
- a desert spoonful of flour
- three table spoonfuls of water

## Directions

1. Mix with four ounces of butter, a desert spoonful of flour
2. When well mixed, add three table spoonfuls of water
3. Put it into a clean saucepan kept for the purpose, and stir it carefully one way till it boils

title: Milk and Cheese Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of milk
- one-half tablespoon of flour
- one cup of grated cheese
- salt
- paprika

## Directions

1. Thicken three cups of milk with one-half tablespoon of flour and cook thoroughly in a double boiler, stirring very often
2. When ready to serve add one cup of grated cheese and season with salt and paprika

title: Milk Lemonade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of boiling water
- two cups of granulated sugar
- three-fourths of a cup of lemon juice
- one and a half pints of milk
- pounded ice

## Directions

1. Dissolve in one quart of boiling water two cups of granulated sugar, add three-fourths of a cup of lemon juice, and lastly, one and a half pints of milk.
2. Drink hot or cold with pounded ice.

title: Milk of Roses
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of rose-water
- one ounce of oil of almonds
- fifty drops of oil of tartar

## Directions

1. Put into a bottle one pint of rose-water and one ounce of oil of almonds
2. shake well together
3. then add fifty drops of oil of tartar

title: Milk or Cream Toast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale light bread
- milk or cream
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Toast as many slices of stale light bread as desired a light brown
2. Heat milk or cream, allowing one-half cup for each slice
3. add small lump of butter
4. When just at the boiling point, pour over bread which has been placed in dish
5. sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
6. cover, and serve immediately

title: Milk Or Potato Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight potatoes
- two eggs
- salt
- flour
- butter
- milk
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil seven or eight potatoes, peel and let them stand several hours to dry
2. Grate them and add two eggs, salt, and enough flour to make a dough thick enough to roll
3. Roll into long, round noodles as thick as two pencils and cut to length of baking-pan
4. Butter pan and lay noodles next to each other
5. Cover with milk and lumps of butter and bake fifteen minutes, till yellow
6. Serve immediately with bread crumbs browned in butter

title: Milk Punch
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of new milk
- thinly pared rind of a lemon
- four ounces of lump sugar
- yolks of two eggs
- a little cold milk
- a tea-cup full of rum
- a tea-cup full of brandy

## Directions

1. Put into a quart of new milk the thinly pared rind of a lemon, and four ounces of lump sugar
2. Let it boil slowly
3. Remove the peel
4. Stir in the yolks of two eggs, previously mixed with a little cold milk
5. Add by degrees a tea-cup full of rum, the same of brandy
6. Mill the punch to a fine froth
7. Serve immediately in quite warm glasses
8. The punch must not be allowed to boil after the eggs have been added

title: Milk Punch
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of new milk
- the thinly pared rind of a lemon
- four ounces of lump sugar

## Directions

1. Put into a quart of new milk the thinly pared rind of a lemon and four ounces of lump sugar
2. Let it boil slowly
3. Remove the peel and stir in

title: Milk, or Cream Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk or cream
- one tablespoon of sweet butter
- one spoon of flour or cornstarch
- cold milk
- pieces of toasted bread
- crackers

## Directions

1. Heat a quart of milk or cream, add a tablespoon of sweet butter and thicken with a spoon of flour or cornstarch, wet with cold milk
2. Pour, boiling, over pieces of toasted bread cut into dices; crackers may also be used

title: Mina (Turkish)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- matzoth
- cold water
- olive oil
- chopped meat
- egg
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- mashed potatoes
- well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Place some matzoth in cold water to soak
2. Take the matzoth out and dry them on a towel
3. Grease a pan with olive oil and put in matzoth enough to cover bottom of pan
4. Take chopped meat, bind with an egg, season with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
5. Cover this with the matzoth, add some olive oil
6. Cover with mashed potatoes and one or two well-beaten eggs
7. Bake until brown

title: Mince Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds lean, fresh beef
- One-half pound chopped suet
- Two pounds of seeded raisins
- Two pounds of currants
- One-half pound of citron
- Two tablespoons of cinnamon
- Two tablespoons of mace
- One grated nutmeg
- One tablespoon of cloves
- Allspice
- Salt
- Two cups of white wine
- Two and one-half pounds of brown sugar
- Four apples
- Rich crust

## Directions

1. Boil two pounds lean, fresh beef
2. When cold, chop fine
3. Add one-half pound chopped suet, shredded very fine, and all gristle removed
4. Mix in a bowl two pounds of seeded raisins, two pounds of currants, one-half pound of citron, chopped very fine
5. Mix two tablespoons of cinnamon, two tablespoons of mace, one grated nutmeg, one tablespoon of cloves, allspice, and salt with meat and suet
6. Take two cups of white wine, two and one-half pounds of brown sugar and let stand
7. Chop fine four apples, and add meat to fruits
8. Mix wine with whole, stir well, and put up in small stone jars
9. Let stand at least two days before using
10. Line pie-plates with a rich crust, fill with mince meat mixture
11. Put a rich paste crust on top, or strips if preferred, prick slightly and bake
12. Serve warm, not hot

title: Mince Pies
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine puff paste
- mincemeat

## Directions

1. Grease and line tin patty-pans with a fine puff paste rolled out thin
2. Fill them with mincemeat, cover them with another piece of paste
3. Moisten the edges, close them carefully, cut them evenly round, and bake them about half an hour in a well-heated oven

title: Minced Fowl
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- remnants of cold fowl
- parsley
- onion
- rind of lemon
- a little marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- ground mace
- bones and remnants
- the juice of half a lemon
- sippets of toast

## Directions

1. The remnants of cold fowl chopped very fine with parsley, onion, rind of lemon, add a little marjoram, salt, pepper, ground mace.
2. When it is well mixed put on all the bones and remnants, stew them down to half a pint, strain it, put it in a small stewpan.
3. Put in your mince, stir in the juice of half a lemon, let it simmer till it thickens.
4. Have some sippets of toast made, place the mince in the dish and the sippets round.
5. Dish up very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Minced Goose (Hungarian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- entire breast of a goose
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- grated stale bread
- eggs
- goose fat

## Directions

1. Take the entire breast of a goose, chop up fine in a chopping bowl
2. Grate in part of an onion
3. Season with salt, pepper and a tiny piece of garlic
4. Add some grated stale bread and work in a few eggs
5. Press this chopped meat back on to the breast bone
6. Roast, basting very often with goose fat

title: Minced Goose Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled or roast goose
- pickles or catsup
- bread or fresh rolls
- goose oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Take either boiled or roast goose (which has been highly seasoned) and mince in a chopping bowl
2. Add one or two pickles, according to quantity, or a teaspoon of catsup
3. Spread thin slices of bread or nice fresh rolls with a thin coating of goose oil, slightly salted
4. Spread the minced goose and cover with a layer of bread which has been previously spread

title: Minced Meat
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of tender roasted meat
- two pounds of shred suet
- three pounds of currants
- six chopped apples
- a quarter of a loaf grated
- nutmegs
- cloves
- pepper
- salt
- one pound of sugar
- grated lemon and orange peel
- lemon juice
- two wine glasses of brandy
- two wine glasses of white wine
- two ounces of citron
- two ounces of candied lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take one pound of tender roasted meat, two pounds of shred suet, three pounds of currants, six chopped apples, a quarter of a loaf grated, nutmegs, cloves, pepper, salt, one pound of sugar, grated lemon and orange peel, lemon juice, and two wine glasses of brandy, the same of white wine, and two ounces of citron, and the same of candied lemon peel
2. mix all well together
3. the ingredients ought to be added separately
4. Minced meat should be kept a day or two before using

title: Minced Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold dressed veal
- a little water or gravy
- salt
- pepper
- grated or minced lemon peel
- sippets of toast

## Directions

1. Cut in small square pieces about the size of dice, cold dressed veal
2. Put it into a saucepan with a little water or gravy
3. Season simply with salt, pepper, and grated or minced lemon peel
4. Garnish the mince with sippets of toast

title: Minced Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal
- a little onion
- parsley
- a little of the rind of lemon
- a cup of stock
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- a slice of bread

## Directions

1. Mince cold veal very fine with a little onion, parsley, and a little of the rind of lemon
2. Put it into a stewpan with a cup of stock, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
3. Let it simmer for 1/2 an hour
4. Toast a slice of bread and cut it in three-cornered pieces
5. Put it round the dish after you have turned in your mince
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mint Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- brown sugar
- chopped mint

## Directions

1. Mix vinegar with brown sugar
2. Let it stand about an hour
3. Then add chopped mint, and stir together

title: Mint Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mint
- half a cup of vinegar
- one tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Chop some mint fine
2. Boil half a cup of vinegar with one tablespoon of sugar
3. Throw in the mint and boil up once
4. Pour in a sauceboat and cool off a little before serving

title: Mint Sauce for Roast Lamb
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mint
- 3 spoonfuls of mint
- 2 spoonfuls of brown sugar
- a teacup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the mint well and strip the leaves from the stalk
2. Chop it fine and put it in the sauce tureen
3. Add to it 3 spoonfuls of mint, 2 of brown sugar, and a teacup of vinegar
4. Stir it all well together
5. Make it half an hour before dinner
6. If not all used it can be put away for the next day to eat it with the cold lamb
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mirlitious
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six macaroons
- one tablespoon of grated chocolate
- one pint of hot milk
- yolks of three eggs
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Pound and sift six macaroons
2. Add grated chocolate and hot milk
3. Let stand ten minutes
4. Add yolks of three eggs well beaten, sugar and vanilla
5. Line patty-tins with puff paste
6. Fill with the mixture
7. Bake twenty minutes

title: Miroton of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal or poultry
- a little grated tongue, or smoked beef
- a few crumbs of bread
- sweet herbs
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- essence of lemon
- two or three eggs
- a very small quantity of good gravy

## Directions

1. Mince finely some cold veal or poultry
2. Add a little grated tongue, or smoked beef, a few crumbs of bread, sweet herbs, pepper, salt, parsley, and if approved, essence of lemon
3. Mix all well with two or three eggs, and a very small quantity of good gravy
4. Grease a mould, put in the above ingredients and bake for three-quarters of an hour
5. Turn out with care, and serve with mushroom sauce

title: Mixed Pickle Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six tablespoons of mustard
- one tablespoon of turmeric
- one cup of flour
- two cups of sugar
- two quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix six tablespoons of mustard, one tablespoon of turmeric, one cup of flour, two cups of sugar and two quarts of vinegar
2. These ingredients must be thoroughly mixed and then cooked until thick
3. Stir in the pickles
4. Heat thoroughly
5. Empty into glass jars and stand away until needed

title: Mixed Pickles
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a little of each kind of pickling vegetables
- salt
- peppercorns
- cloves
- allspice
- mace, a few blades
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare a little of each kind of pickling vegetables
2. Put them in a jar with salt, peppercorns, cloves, allspice, and a few blades of mace
3. When you have filled the jar, put in as much vinegar as it will take cold
4. Tie it down tight
5. Put it in a dry place
6. It will be fit for use in 3 months
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mixed Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of large cucumbers
- one quart of small cucumbers
- one quart small silver-skinned onions
- one quart small green tomatoes
- two red peppers
- one large cauliflower
- one quart of water
- one cup of salt
- six tablespoons of mustard
- one tablespoon of turmeric
- one cup of flour
- two cups of sugar
- two quarts of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash one quart of large cucumbers and cut in cubes
2. Wash one quart of small cucumbers and leave whole
3. Prepare one quart small silver-skinned onions
4. Chop one quart small green tomatoes coarse
5. Chop two red peppers fine
6. Break one large cauliflower in small pieces
7. Pour over them a weak brine solution made of one quart of water and a cup of salt
8. Let stand twenty-four hours
9. Bring to a boil in same solution and drain
10. Mix six tablespoons of mustard, one tablespoon of turmeric, one cup of flour, two cups of sugar and two quarts of vinegar for the dressing
11. Thoroughly mix these ingredients and then cook until thick
12. Stir in the pickles
13. Heat thoroughly
14. Empty into glass jars and stand away until needed

title: Mocha Frosting
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of pulverized sugar
- two dessertspoons of dry cocoa
- two tablespoons of strong hot coffee
- piece of butter the size of a walnut
- vanilla

## Directions

1. One cup of pulverized sugar into which sift two dessertspoons of dry cocoa
2. Mix two tablespoons of strong hot coffee in which is melted a piece of butter the size of a walnut
3. Beat well and add a little vanilla

title: Mocha Mousse
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one-half cup of granulated sugar
- one-half pint of cream
- one-half cup of grated macaroons
- two tablespoons of mocha essence
- one teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream yolks of three eggs with one-half cup of granulated sugar
2. Add one-half pint of cream, whipped
3. Add one-half cup of grated macaroons
4. Add two tablespoons of mocha essence and one teaspoon of vanilla
5. Add lastly beaten whites
6. Put in a mold and pack in salt and ice for three hours

title: Mocha Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of powdered sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- one cup of sifted flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- three tablespoons of cold water
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla
- one tablespoon essence of mocha
- stiffly-beaten whites
- one-half pint of cream
- one tablespoon of mocha essence
- one and one-half tablespoon of powdered sugar
- pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Beat one cup of powdered sugar with the yolks of four eggs
2. When very light, add one cup of sifted flour in which has been mixed one teaspoon of baking-powder
3. Add three tablespoons of cold water
4. Add one-half teaspoon of vanilla
5. Add one tablespoon essence of mocha
6. Add the stiffly-beaten whites
7. Bake fifteen to twenty minutes in two layer pans in a moderate oven
8. Spread when cold with one-half pint of cream to which has been added one tablespoon of mocha essence, one and one-half tablespoon of powdered sugar and then well whipped
9. Garnish with pounded almonds

title: Mock Cherry Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich crust
- Two cups of cranberries
- One cup of raisins
- Two cups of sugar
- Butter the size of walnut
- Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of pie-plate with rich crust and reserve enough for upper crust
2. Cut cranberries in halves and raisins in pieces
3. Mix two cups of cranberries, one cup of raisins, two cups of sugar, butter the size of walnut
4. Dredge with flour, sprinkle with water
5. Bake thirty minutes in a moderate oven

title: Mock Chile Con Carne
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of kidney beans
- one quart of water
- fresh water
- boiling water
- one tablespoon of butter
- four green peppers
- one small red pepper
- one onion
- one pint of tomatoes
- one teaspoon of salt
- three tablespoons of uncooked rice

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash two cups of kidney beans, soak in one quart of water
2. Next morning bring to a boil in fresh water
3. drain, cover beans with boiling water and cook until tender
4. Half an hour before beans are to be served, put one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan
5. chop and add four green peppers, one small red pepper, one onion, one pint of tomatoes, one teaspoon of salt
6. cook fifteen minutes
7. add to beans with three tablespoons of uncooked rice
8. simmer until thick

title: Mock Duck
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloin
- stuffing for poultry
- onion
- turnip
- carrot
- stock or water
- parsley
- one bay leaf
- three cloves
- one tablespoon of flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Take the tenderloin, lay it flat on a board after removing the fat.
2. Make a stuffing as for poultry.
3. Spread this mixture on the meat evenly; then roll and tie it with white twine; turn in the ends to make it even and shapely.
4. Cut into dice an onion, turnip, and carrot, and place them in a baking-pan.
5. Lay the rolled meat on the bed of vegetables.
6. Pour in enough stock or water to cover the pan one inch deep.
7. Add a bouquet made of parsley, one bay leaf and three cloves.
8. Cover with another pan, and let cook slowly for four hours, basting frequently.
9. When cooked, strain off the vegetables.
10. Thicken the gravy with one tablespoon of flour browned in fat and serve it with the meat.

title: Mock Fish Chowder
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large tomatoes
- six large potatoes
- two large onions
- salt
- pepper
- three pints of water
- one-half pint of cream
- one-fourth cup of butter
- paprika
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Omit fish and use same ingredients
2. sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve

title: Mock Mince Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight tart apples
- One cup of seedless raisins
- One-half cup of currants
- One ounce of chopped citron
- One-half teaspoon each of cinnamon, cloves, spice and mace
- Salt
- Grated nutmeg
- One tablespoon of brandy
- Juice and rind of one lemon
- Pie crust

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and chop fine eight tart apples
2. Add one cup of seedless raisins, one-half cup of currants, one ounce of chopped citron, one-half teaspoon each of cinnamon, cloves, spice and mace, a tiny bit of salt and grated nutmeg
3. Pour over whole one tablespoon of brandy, and juice and rind of one lemon
4. Line bottom and sides of plate with crust, fill in with mixture, and put strips of dough across

title: Mock Olives
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sea salt or rock salt

## Directions

1. Take plums when just beginning to ripen, but still green
2. Make a brine out of sea salt or rock salt strong enough to hold up an egg
3. Pour the brine over the fruit, hot, cover and let stand twenty-four hours
4. Pour off and make a new brine, heat, add the fruit, heat one minute and seal in the hot brine

title: Mock Turtle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a well-cleaned calf's head
- some good stock made of beef and veal
- flour
- fried shalot
- pepper
- parsley
- tarragon
- a little mushroom ketchup
- a pint of white wine
- balls of force-meat
- egg-balls
- the juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Half boil a well-cleaned calf's head
2. Cut off all the meat in small square pieces and break the bones
3. Return it to the stew-pan with some good stock made of beef and veal
4. Dredge in flour and add fried shalot, pepper, parsley, tarragon, a little mushroom ketchup, and a pint of white wine
5. Simmer gently until the meat is perfectly soft and tender
6. Put in balls of force-meat and egg-balls a short time before serving
7. Stir in the juice of a lemon as an improvement

title: Mock Turtle Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's head
- four and one-half quarts of water
