- two red peppers
- onions
- celery
- carrots
- cloves
- salt
- cabbage
- meat stock
- fat
- two large tablespoons of flour
- calf's tongue
- hard-boiled eggs
- one glass of sherry
- one lemon

## Directions

1. Take one calf's head, wash well
2. Put on to boil with four and one-half quarts of water
3. Add two red peppers, onions, celery, carrots, cloves, salt to taste, and a little cabbage
4. Boil six hours
5. Have ready some meat stock
6. The next day put fat in a skillet with two large tablespoons of flour; let it brown
7. Take the calf's head and cut all the meat from it in pieces
8. Add the calf's tongue, cut in dice
9. Slice hard-boiled eggs, one glass of sherry, and one lemon sliced
10. Put all in the stock
11. Allow it to come just to a boil

title: Mock Turtle Soup from Stock
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of stock
- flesh of the foot
- small meat balls
- egg balls
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- juice of half a lemon
- a spoonful of browning
- a glass of sherry or madeira
- a little cayenne

## Directions

1. Put one quart of the stock into a moderate sized saucepan and let it warm gently.
2. Cut the flesh of the foot into nice pieces the size of a small biscuit and put that into the saucepan.
3. Have ready some small meat balls made according to the recipe, also a few egg balls.
4. Boil the meat balls first and throw them into the saucepan with the liquor they were boiled in.
5. Let the whole boil together.
6. Throw in the egg balls, which only take ten minutes to boil.
7. Have in a basin 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, the juice of half a lemon, and a spoonful of browning.
8. Rub it all together and turn it back into the saucepan.
9. Stir it well.
10. Turn it into the tureen with a glass of sherry or madeira.
11. Never let the wine boil in the soup.
12. Add a little cayenne according to taste.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mock Turtle Soup Made Easy
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a bullock's foot
- two quarts of water
- salt
- pepper
- 2 cloves
- 2 allspice
- a few sticks of marjoram
- thyme
- parsley
- a large carrot
- a tooth of garlic
- 2 good sized onions
- any dry crumbs of bread
- a few meal balls
- a few egg balls
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a little browning
- the juice of a lemon
- a little cayenne

## Directions

1. Put the bullock's foot in a good size saucepan with two quarts of water, salt, pepper, 2 cloves, 2 allspice, a few sticks of marjoram, thyme, parsley, a large carrot, a tooth of garlic, 2 good sized onions (only wash them, do not peel them), and any dry crumbs of bread you may have
2. Put it on the fire for a quick boil, then let it simmer for 4 hours
3. Take out the foot, strip off all the flesh, put it in a plate, return the bones to the saucepan
4. Let the whole of it simmer till the last thing at night
5. Strain it in a nice clean pan, put it by till the morning
6. On Friday skim off all the fat, put the stock in the saucepan
7. Cut in pieces the flesh of the foot, make a few meal balls, let them boil in the soup
8. Have ready a few egg balls, boiling ten minutes
9. Put in a basin a tablespoonful of flour, a little browning, the juice of a lemon, a little cayenne
10. Let it all boil up, turn it into your pan, and on Saturday you will have a fine soup
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mock Turtle Soup Made Easy
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a bullock's foot
- two quarts of water
- salt, pepper
- 2 cloves
- 2 allspice
- a few sticks of marjoram, thyme, parsley
- a large carrot
- a tooth of garlic

## Directions

1. Put the bullock's foot in a good size saucepan with two quarts of water
2. Add salt, pepper, 2 cloves, 2 allspice
3. Add a few sticks of marjoram, thyme, parsley
4. Add a large carrot, a tooth of garlic
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mock Whipped Cream Filling
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large sour apple
- three-quarters cup of white sugar
- white of one egg
- vanilla or grated rind of one-half lemon

## Directions

1. Peel and grate one large sour apple
2. Mix three-quarters cup of white sugar with the apple
3. Add white of one egg
4. Beat all together a long time
5. Flavor with vanilla or grated rind of one-half lemon

title: Mohn (Poppy Seed) Roley Poley
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- one cup of black poppy seed
- one cup of milk
- two tablespoons of butter
- one-half cup of seeded raisins
- one-half cup of walnuts or almonds
- two tablespoons of molasses or syrup
- citron
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Roll out a piece of dough large enough to cover your whole baking-board, roll thin
2. Let it rise until you have prepared the filling
3. grind one cup of black poppy seed in a coffee-mill as tight as possible and clean it well
4. throw away the first bit you grind so as not to have the coffee taste
5. put it on to boil with one cup of milk
6. add two tablespoons of butter, one-half cup of seeded raisins, one-half cup of walnuts or almonds chopped up fine, two tablespoons of molasses or syrup, and a little citron cut up fine
7. When thick, set it away to cool
8. if not sweet enough add more sugar and flavor with vanilla
9. When this mixture has cooled, spread on the dough which has risen by this time
10. Take up one corner and roll it up into a long roll, like a jelly-roll
11. put in a greased pan and let it rise an hour
12. then spread butter on top and bake very slowly
13. Let it get quite brown, so as to bake through thoroughly
14. When cold cut up in slices, as many as you are going to use at one time only

title: Mohn Wachtel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cake dough
- poppy seed mixture
- one-half pound of poppy seed
- milk
- one-quarter of a pound of sugar
- yolks of three eggs
- beaten whites of eggs
- two ounces of sifted flour
- one-quarter of a pound of melted butter

## Directions

1. Take coffee cake dough
2. Let the dough rise again for an hour
3. spread with a poppy seed mixture, after cutting into squares
4. fold into triangles and pinch the edges together
5. Lay in well-buttered pans, about two inches apart
6. let them rise again
7. spread with poppy seed filling
8. Take one-half pound of poppy seed (mohn) which have previously been soaked in milk and then ground
9. add one-quarter of a pound of sugar and the yolks of three eggs
10. Stir this all together in one direction until quite thick
11. then stir in the beaten whites to which you must add two ounces of sifted flour and one-quarter of a pound of melted butter
12. Fill the tartlets and bake

title: Mohntorte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich puff paste
- half a pound of white mohn (poppy seed)
- milk
- a quarter of a pound of sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- beaten whites
- two ounces of sifted flour
- a quarter of a pound of melted butter
- vanilla or rose frosting

## Directions

1. Line a form with a rich puff paste.
2. Soak half a pound of white mohn (poppy seed) in milk and then grind.
3. Add a quarter of a pound of sugar and the yolks of six eggs.
4. Stir all together in one direction until quite thick.
5. Stir the beaten whites, to which add two ounces of sifted flour and a quarter of a pound of melted butter.
6. Fill and bake.
7. When done, frost either with vanilla or rose frosting.

title: Molina Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal or chicken
- smoked beef or tongue
- lemon-juice
- nutmeg
- good beef or veal gravy
- yolks of four eggs
- rich pastry

## Directions

1. Mince finely cold veal or chicken, with smoked beef or tongue
2. Season well, add lemon-juice and a little nutmeg
3. Let it simmer in a small quantity of good beef or veal gravy
4. While on the fire, stir in the yolks of four eggs
5. Put it in a dish to cool, and then cover with a rich pastry, and bake it

title: Monterey Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fine lemons
- one box of boneless sardines
- one teaspoon of French mustard
- two hard-boiled eggs
- tabasco sauce
- mayonnaise
- chopped parsley
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Select fine lemons, wipe carefully, scoop out the pulp, remove the tough inner skin and seeds, and to the rest add one box of boneless sardines, finely chopped, one teaspoon of French mustard, two hard-boiled eggs chopped, some tabasco sauce, and mayonnaise.
2. Fill each cup with the mixture.
3. Cut a small slice from the bottom of the lemon, so that it will stand firmly.
4. Garnish with chopped egg and chopped parsley, and serve on lettuce leaves.

title: Mother's Delicious Cookies (Merber Kuchen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ten boiled eggs
- two raw eggs
- one pound of best butter
- half a pound of almonds
- one lemon
- some cinnamon
- one wineglass of brandy
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- about one pound and a half of flour

## Directions

1. Set a dish of boiling water on the stove, when it boils hard, break the eggs carefully, one at a time, dropping the whites in a deep porcelain dish, and set away in a cool place.
2. Take each yolk as you break the egg and put it in a half shell, and lay it in the boiling water until you have ten boiling.
3. When boiled hard take them up and lay them on a plate to cool.
4. Cream the butter with a pound of pulverized sugar, add the grated peel of a lemon, a teaspoon of cinnamon and half of the almonds, which have been blanched and pounded or grated.
5. Add the hard-boiled yolks, which must be grated, and the two raw eggs, sift in the flour, and add the brandy.
6. Beat up the whites of the twelve eggs very stiff, add half to the dough, reserving the other half, but do not make the dough stiff, as it should be so rich that you can hardly handle it.
7. Flour the baking-board well, roll out about an eighth of an inch thick.
8. Spread with the reserved whites of eggs, reserving half again, as you will have to roll out at least twice on a large baking-board.
9. Sprinkle well with the pounded almonds after you have spread the beaten whites of the eggs on top, also sugar and cinnamon.
10. Cut with a cookie-cutter.
11. Have at least five large pans greased ready to receive them.
12. Bake five to ten minutes.
13. Pack them away when cold in a stone jar or tin cake-box.

title: Mother's Dill Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- dill
- whole peppers
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Examine the cucumbers carefully, discard all that are soft at the ends, and allow them to lay in water overnight
2. In the morning drain, and dry them with a clean towel
3. Put them in a wooden pail or jar, along with the dill, putting first a layer of dill at the bottom then a layer of cucumbers, a few whole peppers, then a layer of dill again, and so on until all are used
4. Lay a clean, white cloth on top, then a plate and a stone to give it weight, so that the pickles will be kept under the brine
5. To a peck of cucumbers use about a cup of salt
6. Dissolve the salt in enough cold water to cover them
7. You may add one or two tablespoons of vinegar to the brine
8. If the cucumbers are small, and if they are kept in a warm place, they will be ready for the table in five or six days
9. Always examine the pickles weekly
10. Take off the cloth, wash it, and remove all the scum that adheres to the pail, and lay a clean cloth over the pickles again

title: Motsa Ball
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 biscuits
- 2 oz. of fine chopped suet
- marjoram
- parsley
- grated nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- fat or oil
- couple of spoonfuls of sieved meal
- 2 eggs beaten with 2 spoonfuls of water

## Directions

1. Soak 2 biscuits
2. Squeeze the water well out of them
3. Put them in a basin with 2 oz. of fine chopped suet
4. Add a little marjoram, parsley, a little grated nutmeg, salt, pepper
5. Fry a little onion in a little fat or oil
6. Turn it into your mixture
7. Add a couple of spoonfuls of the sieved meal
8. Mix with 2 eggs beaten with 2 spoonfuls of water
9. Roll it up and form the balls
10. Boil 20 minutes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one well-beaten egg
- two tablespoons of butter
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one scant cup of milk
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- sufficient flour

## Directions

1. Light the burners of the gas oven before beginning to mix the muffins and work rapidly
2. Place in a mixing-bowl one well-beaten egg, two tablespoons of butter, one tablespoon of sugar, one-half teaspoon of salt, one scant cup of milk and two teaspoons of baking-powder that have been sifted with sufficient flour to form a batter that will ribbon from the spoon
3. Beat the batter steadily for five minutes
4. Stir in one tablespoon of melted butter
5. Bake in muffin-pans in a quick oven
6. Bake ten minutes if pans are only half filled

title: Muligatawny Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- flour
- four onions
- a piece of clarified fat
- pepper
- salt
- two table spoonfuls of curry powder
- three quarts of strong beef gravy
- the juice of a lemon
- rice
- a pinch of saffron

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens up small, as if for fricassee
2. Flour them well
3. Put them in a saucepan with four onions shred, a piece of clarified fat, pepper, salt, and two table spoonfuls of curry powder
4. Let it simmer for an hour
5. Add three quarts of strong beef gravy
6. Let it continue simmering for another hour
7. Before sent to table stir in the juice of a lemon
8. Some persons approve of a little rice being boiled with the stock and a pinch of saffron is also sometimes added

title: Mulled Wine
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cinnamon
- ginger
- cloves
- water
- wine
- sugar
- nutmeg
- a strip or two of orange rind

## Directions

1. Boil a little spice, cinnamon, ginger, and cloves in water till the flavor is gained
2. Add wine as much as may be approved
3. Add sugar and nutmeg
4. A strip or two of orange rind cut thin is a great improvement

title: Mulled Wine
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cinnamon to taste
- allspice to taste
- one cup of hot water
- three eggs
- sugar
- one pint of wine

## Directions

1. Put cinnamon and allspice (to taste) in a cup of hot water to steep.
2. Add three eggs well beaten with sugar.
3. Heat to a boil a pint of wine, then add spice and eggs.
4. Stir for three minutes and serve.

title: Mulligatawny Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts of liquor in which fowls have been boiled
- three onions
- two carrots
- one head of celery
- one tablespoon of curry powder
- one tablespoon of flour
- chicken or veal

## Directions

1. Add to three quarts of liquor, in which fowls have been boiled, the following vegetables: three onions, two carrots, and one head of celery cut in small dice
2. Keep the kettle over a high heat until soup reaches the boiling point
3. Then place where it will simmer for twenty-five minutes
4. Add one tablespoon of curry powder, one tablespoon of flour mixed together; add to the hot soup and cook five minutes
5. Pass through a sieve
6. Serve with small pieces of chicken or veal cut in it

title: Mushroom and Barley Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of hot bouillon
- one-quarter pound barley
- water
- one ounce of dried mushrooms
- an onion
- carrot
- bayleaf
- parsley
- dill

## Directions

1. Take one quart of hot bouillon, add a quarter pound barley which has been boiled in water
2. Add one ounce of dried mushrooms which have been thoroughly washed and cut in pieces, an onion, carrot, bayleaf, parsley and dill
3. Boil all these and when the vegetables are nearly tender, remove from soup
4. Add the meat from the bouillon, cut up in small pieces
5. Let soup come to a boil and serve

title: Mushroom and Barley Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart hot bouillon
- one quarter pound barley, boiled in water
- one ounce dried mushrooms, thoroughly washed and cut in pieces
- one onion
- one carrot
- one bayleaf
- parsley
- dill
- meat from the bouillon

## Directions

1. Add the quarter pound barley which has been boiled in water to the hot bouillon
2. Add the dried mushrooms which have been thoroughly washed and cut in pieces, onion, carrot, bayleaf, parsley and dill
3. Boil all these
4. When the vegetables are nearly tender, remove from soup
5. Add the meat from the bouillon, cut up in small pieces
6. Let soup come to a boil and serve

title: Mushroom Catsup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Green ginger root
- Peppercorn
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Mace

## Directions

1. Wash and look over one pint of mushrooms carefully.
2. Put them in an earthen jar with alternate layers of salt.
3. Let stand for twenty-four hours in a comparatively warm place.
4. Put through a fruit press and add one-fourth ounce of green ginger root cut in small pieces.
5. Measure the mushroom liquor; to one pint of liquor add one-half ounce of peppercorn.
6. Simmer for forty minutes.
7. Add one-fourth ounce of allspice and of cloves and one blade of mace and boil for fifteen minutes.
8. Take from fire and cool.
9. Strain through a cloth, bottle and seal.

title: Mushroom Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- about a pint of fine young button mushrooms
- a white veal gravy
- salt
- pepper
- a blade of mace
- the grated peel of half a lemon
- flour
- the yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Let about a pint of fine young button mushrooms stew gently in a white veal gravy seasoned with salt, pepper, a blade of mace, and if approved, the grated peel of half a lemon
2. Thicken with flour and the yolk of an egg stirred in it, just before serving

title: Mushrooms au Naturel
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- a large piece of butter
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Clean some fine fresh mushrooms, put them in a saucepan with a large piece of butter, pepper and salt
2. let them simmer until tender, and serve them with no other sauce than that in which they have been dressed

title: Musk Melons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- melon
- crushed ice
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cut melon in half, seed and put on ice one hour before serving
2. When ready to serve, fill with crushed ice and sprinkle with powdered sugar
3. Allow one-half melon for each person
4. For dinner serve before soup

title: Mustard Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of one hard-boiled egg
- two teaspoons French mustard
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- oil
- vinegar
- egg white

## Directions

1. Take yolk of one hard-boiled egg and rub smooth in a bowl. Add two teaspoons of French mustard, salt, pepper, and little sugar. Add a little oil, and then a little vinegar. Garnish top with the white, cut in pieces.

title: Mustard Pickle
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mixed pickles from No. 411
- best mustard, 1/2 lb.
- coarse brown sugar, 1/2 lb.
- cold vinegar
- salt
- ground marjoram
- lemon thyme
- fresh lemon rind, chopped
- dried saffron, a pennyworth

## Directions

1. Prepare a jar of mixed pickles and let it stand for 6 weeks
2. Take 1/2 lb. of the best mustard
3. Mix it in a basin with 1/2 lb. of coarse brown sugar and a little cold vinegar and salt
4. Pour off the whole of the jar of pickles that you had prepared
5. Let them drain through a sieve or colander
6. Put that vinegar in a clean saucepan with a pint more, some ground marjoram and lemon thyme, and a little chopped fresh rind of a lemon
7. Let it boil
8. Stir in the mixed mustard and sugar with a pennyworth of dried saffron
9. Let it boil, it will come quite thick
10. Let it cool
11. Return the pickles to the jar and pour this mixture over
12. Let the jar be uncovered till the next day
13. Tie it up and place it away for 6 weeks
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mustard Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small cucumbers or gherkins
- salt
- green or red peppers
- cloves
- mustard seed
- bay leaf
- ground mustard
- white wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Choose small cucumbers or gherkins for this purpose
2. Reject all that are specked or misshapen
3. Wash them thoroughly; drain off all the water, and allow them to lay in a tub overnight, thickly salted
4. In the morning wipe the pickles carefully
5. Lay them in a stone jar or a wooden bucket, in this way: Put in a layer of pickles
6. Cut up a few green or red peppers; put a few pieces in each layer, also a few cloves and a tablespoon of mustard seed, and one bay leaf
7. Then proceed in this way until the pickles are used
8. Take half a pound of the very best ground mustard, tie it in a cloth loosely, and lay this mustard-bag on top of the pickles
9. Boil enough white wine vinegar

title: Mustard Sardine Paste for Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one box of mustard sardines
- one tablespoon of tomato catsup
- one teaspoon of Worcestershire sauce
- juice of one lemon
- cayenne pepper
- white pepper
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- one tablespoon of olive oil
- thinly cut bread

## Directions

1. Take one box of mustard sardines and bone and mash.
2. Add one tablespoon of tomato catsup, one teaspoon of Worcestershire sauce, juice of one lemon, a pinch of cayenne pepper, as much white pepper as will cover the end of a knife, two tablespoons of vinegar, and one tablespoon of olive oil.
3. Mix thoroughly until it becomes a paste.
4. Spread on thinly cut bread for sandwiches.

title: Mustard Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons vinegar
- one tablespoon mustard
- one teaspoon oil or butter
- pepper
- salt
- two hard-boiled eggs
- one small onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Mix two tablespoons of vinegar and one of mustard, one teaspoon of oil or butter melted, pepper and salt to taste.
2. Add this to two hard-boiled eggs chopped fine, with a small onion and about the same quantity of parsley as eggs.
3. Mix all well together.

title: Mutton a l'Hispaniola
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- small piece of mutton, shoulder or fillet of the leg
- beef gravy
- herbs
- carrot
- turnip
- Spanish onions

## Directions

1. Take a small piece of mutton, either part of a shoulder or a fillet of the leg
2. partly roast it
3. put it in a stewpan with beef gravy enough to cover it
4. previously season with herbs, a carrot and turnip
5. cut in quarters three large Spanish onions, and place in the stewpan round the meat
6. a stuffing will improve it
7. take care to free the gravy from every particle of fat

title: Mutton Broth
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of neck of lamb or lean shoulder
- three quarts of water
- two tablespoons well-washed rice
- flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut three pounds of neck of lamb or lean shoulder into small pieces
2. Cover closely and boil with three quarts of water, slowly, for two hours
3. Add two tablespoons well-washed rice to the boiling soup
4. Cook an hour longer, slowly
5. Watch carefully and stir from time to time
6. Strain and thicken it with a little flour
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste

title: Mutton Broth
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of the best end neck of mutton
- a quart of water
- 3 or 4 turnips
- a little celery
- a little salt, pepper
- 1/2 lb. of rice
- A large onion (Spanish)
- parsley
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of the best end neck of mutton in your soup saucepan with a quart of water
2. Add 3 or 4 turnips cut in good size pieces
3. Add a little celery cut thin, a little salt, pepper
4. Add 1/2-lb. of rice, well washed
5. Let it boil well
6. Add a large onion (Spanish) boiled whole
7. Let it boil, skim it well, then let it simmer for two hours
8. Serve the mutton with the boiled onions in the dish with caper or tomato sauce
9. Have parsley and grated nutmeg in the tureen
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mutton Chops with Green Peas
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs mutton chops
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- onion
- 3 or 4 lumps of sugar
- flour
- chopped mint
- parsley
- cold water

## Directions

1. Lay the chops in a stewpan with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, an onion chopped, and 3 or 4 lumps of sugar
2. Let them cook till tender
3. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour, some chopped mint and parsley, and a little cold water
4. Skim off the fat
5. Put in the sauce when the mutton is done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mutton Chops with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- 3 or 4 large tomatoes
- water
- lemon
- parsley
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Take some nice mutton chops with the fat trimmed off and put them in a stewpan with salt, pepper, an onion chopped
2. Slice 3 or 4 large tomatoes
3. Let it all cook together with about a cupful of water
4. When the chops are tender take them out and let them drain well from the gravy
5. Turn the tomatoes and liquor into a sieve, pulp it well
6. Put the juice of a lemon with it
7. Turn it all over the chops
8. Let it boil before you dish it up
9. Sprinkle over a little chopped parsley, and if liked a little cayenne
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Mutton Collops
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- knuckle of mutton
- forcemeat for cutlets
- potatoes or Jerusalem artichokes
- bread
- gravy
- fried parsley
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take from a fine knuckle a couple of slices
2. Cut and trim them in collops the size of a tea cup
3. Flatten them and spread over each side a forcemeat for cutlets
4. Fry them
5. Prepare potatoes or Jerusalem artichokes cut in slices of the same size and thickness, or pieces of bread cut with a fluted cutter, prepared as the collops and fried
6. Place alternately in the dish with them
7. Serve with a pure simple gravy, or very hot and dry on a napkin, garnished with fried parsley and slices of lemon

title: Mutton Collops
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices from a fine knuckle
- forcemeat
- potato or Jerusalem artichokes
- bread

## Directions

1. Take from a fine knuckle a couple of slices
2. cut and trim them in collops the size of a tea cup
3. flatten them and spread over each side a forcemeat for cutlets
4. fry them
5. serve with potato or Jerusalem artichokes cut in slices of the same size and thickness, or pieces of bread cut with a cutter

title: Mutton Cutlets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of mutton
- cutlet preparation
- vegetable puree or sauce

## Directions

1. Have a neck of mutton, cut the bones short, and remove the chine bone completely
2. Cut chops off so thin that every other one shall be without bone
3. Trim them carefully, that all the chops shall bear the same appearance
4. Flatten them well
5. Cover them with a cutlet preparation
6. Fry of a delicate brown
7. Serve with a fine puree of any vegetable that may be approved, or any sauce that may be selected
8. Arrange in various ways in the dish, either round the dish or in a circle in the centre

title: Mutton Ham
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- brown sugar
- common salt
- saltpetre

## Directions

1. Choose a fine leg of mutton
2. Rub it in daily with a mixture of three ounces of brown sugar, two ounces of common salt, and half an ounce of saltpetre
3. Continue this process for a fortnight
4. Hang it to dry in wood smoke for ten days longer

title: Mutton or Lamb Chops
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton or lamb chops
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- ginger

## Directions

1. Trim off some of the fat and heat in the spider.
2. Season the chops with salt and pepper, or salt and ginger.
3. Have the spider very hot with very little fat in it.
4. To be nice and tender they must be sauted quickly to a nice brown.
5. Or the chops may be broiled over the hot coals or in gas broiler, eight or ten minutes is all the time required.
6. Serve at once.

title: Mutton Stewed with Celery
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- best end of a neck of mutton or fillet from the leg or shoulder
- water
- carrot
- turnip
- forcemeat balls
- celery
- yolks of two eggs
- flour
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take the best end of a neck of mutton, or a fillet taken from the leg or shoulder
2. place it in a stewpan with just enough water to cover it
3. throw in a carrot and turnip, and season, but not too highly
4. when nearly done remove the meat and strain off the gravy
5. return both to the stewpan with forcemeat balls and some fine celery cut in small pieces
6. let all stew gently till perfectly done
7. stir in the yolks of two eggs, a little flour, and the juice of half a lemon
8. mix the yolks and lemon juice with a little of the gravy before pouring in the stewpan
9. take care to prevent curdling

title: Nahit (Russian Peas)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound Russian peas
- one tablespoon of salt
- hot water
- one-quarter teaspoon of soda
- one pound of brisket of beef or back or neck of fat chicken
- two tablespoons of fat
- two tablespoons of flour
- two tablespoons of brown sugar
- one cup of the liquid from the peas

## Directions

1. Place one pound Russian peas in granite kettle
2. Add one tablespoon of salt and hot water to more than cover and let soak twelve hours or more
3. Drain, return to the kettle, cover with boiling water
4. Let cook fifteen minutes
5. Add one-quarter teaspoon of soda and one pound of brisket of beef or back or neck of fat chicken
6. Let cook slowly until peas are tender
7. Melt two tablespoons of fat
8. Add two tablespoons of flour and two tablespoons of brown sugar
9. Let brown
10. Add one cup of the liquid from the peas
11. Cook until thick and smooth
12. Pour over the peas
13. Cook thoroughly
14. Then place in casserole and bake in a moderate oven one-half hour

title: Napkin Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half loaf of stale white bread
- water
- two tablespoons of clear fat or butter
- five eggs
- one cup of sugar
- rind of one lemon
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Soak one-half loaf of stale white bread in water until moist, squeeze perfectly dry
2. Put in skillet two tablespoons of clear fat or butter, and when hot add bread, and stir until smooth and dry
3. Beat five eggs light with one cup of sugar, stir bread in, mix well, and flavor with rind (grated) and juice of one lemon
4. Grease a bag or very large napkin, place pudding in this, tie, leaving plenty room to rise
5. Place in boiling water and boil two hours
6. Make a jelly sauce, not as thin as usual, and pour over just before serving

title: Neapolitan Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fully-ripe strawberries
- raspberries
- currants
- red cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of fully-ripe strawberries, raspberries, currants and red cherries.
2. The cherries must be stoned, taking care to preserve the juice and add to rest of juice.
3. Mix and press through a jelly-press or bag.
4. Measure the juice, boil a pint at a time, and to every pint allow a pound of sugar and proceed as with other fruit jellies.

title: Neapolitan Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white meat of a turkey
- pickles
- beets
- carrots
- potatoes
- asparagus
- celery
- celery root
- salt
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves or white cabbage leaves
- hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Take some white meat of a turkey, cut up fine.
2. Cut up a few pickles the same way, a few beets, one or two carrots, a few potatoes (the carrots and potatoes must be parboiled).
3. Also a few stalks of asparagus.
4. Chop up a bunch of crisp, white celery and a whole celery root (parboiled).
5. Sprinkle all with fine salt and pour a mayonnaise dressing over it.
6. Line the salad bowl with lettuce leaves or white cabbage leaves.
7. Add a few hard-boiled eggs and capers.
8. Garnish with sprigs of fresh parsley.

title: Nesselrode Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of shelled and skinned chestnuts
- yolks of five eggs
- one-half pound of sugar
- one pint of sweet cream
- salt
- one-fourth pound of crystallized cherries
- one-fourth pound of crystallized pineapple
- one ounce of citron
- one-fourth cup of stoned raisins
- one-half cup of maraschino cordial
- one pint cream
- two tablespoons of powdered sugar
- rock salt and ice

## Directions

1. Put one-half pound of shelled and skinned chestnuts in cold water
2. Let them boil until very tender
3. Press them through a puree sieve
4. Beat the yolks of five eggs with one-half pound of sugar until light
5. Add the mashed chestnuts
6. Stir in one pint of sweet cream
7. Put on to boil in a double boiler
8. Add a few grains of salt
9. Stir until the mixture begins to boil
10. Remove at once from fire and set aside to cool
11. In a bowl put one-fourth pound of crystallized cherries, cut in half; one-fourth pound of crystallized pineapple cut up, one ounce of citron cut fine, one-fourth cup of stoned raisins and one-half cup of maraschino cordial
12. Put the chestnut cream in a freezer
13. Freeze ten minutes
14. Add one pint cream that has been whipped stiff with two tablespoons of powdered sugar
15. Turn until it begins to get stiff
16. Add the fruits and turn awhile longer
17. Pack in a pudding-mold in rock salt and ice two hours

title: New Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six potatoes
- salted boiling water
- melted butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Brush and scrape off all the skin of six potatoes
2. Boil for half an hour in salted boiling water
3. Drain and salt
4. Dry for a few minutes
5. Pour melted butter over them
6. Sprinkle with chopped parsley

title: Niagara Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one thick slice of cold, cooked salmon
- mayonnaise
- one tablespoon of French mustard
- one green onion
- one tablespoon of small Mexican peppers
- one tablespoon of pimentos

## Directions

1. Pick or grind one thick slice of cold, cooked salmon.
2. Make a dressing of mayonnaise, to which add one tablespoon of French mustard, one green onion chopped fine, one tablespoon of small Mexican peppers, one tablespoon of pimentos.
3. Mix this dressing into the picked salmon.

title: Noodle Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of broad noodles
- salted boiling water
- three-fourths cup of chicken or goose fat
- four eggs, well beaten
- raspberry jelly or stewed fruit of any kind

## Directions

1. Cook three cups of broad noodles in salted boiling water ten minutes
2. Drain and add three-fourths cup of chicken or goose fat and four eggs, well beaten
3. Place in a well-greased iron pot and bake until the top of the kugel is well browned
4. Serve hot with raspberry jelly or stewed fruit of any kind

title: Noodle Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- sufficient flour to make a paste
- a pinch of salt
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. of raisins
- grated peel of a lemon
- 2 oz. of orange and lemon peel
- 1/2 lb. of melted suet
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Break 8 eggs, mix it with sufficient flour to make a paste and a pinch of salt
2. Roll the paste out as for frimsells, cut it in strips of an inch wide
3. Boil them for a few minutes
4. Put them in a sieve to drain
5. Put the noodle strips in a basin with the currants, raisins, grated lemon peel, orange and lemon peel, and melted suet
6. Mix all well together with 6 eggs well beaten
7. Grease and sugar a mould
8. Boil for 2 hours
9. Serve with wine or brandy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Noodle Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- four eggs
- one cup of powdered sugar
- a quarter of a cup of pounded almonds
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Make noodles with two eggs
2. Boil in boiling salt water for ten minutes and drain
3. Beat the yolks of four eggs with one cup of powdered sugar until light
4. Add a quarter of a cup of pounded almonds and a pinch of salt
5. Add the drained noodles and the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
6. Mix well
7. Pour into a greased pudding mold and boil one and one-half hours

title: Noodle Puffs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- noodle dough
- chicken fat
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough with as many eggs as desired
2. Roll out somewhat thin
3. Cut in strips four inches long by one inch wide
4. Have a skillet half full of boiling hot chicken fat
5. Drop in the strips, a few at a time
6. Baste with the hot grease until brown on both sides
7. Remove to a platter
8. Sprinkle generously with powdered sugar and cinnamon
9. Serve

title: Noodle Schalet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- three eggs
- one-fourth cup of sugar
- pounded almonds
- grated peel of one lemon
- raisins
- goose grease or drippings
- melted fat

## Directions

1. Make the quantity of noodles desired, then boil.
2. When done, drain through colander, pouring cold water over the noodles.
3. When all the water has drained off, beat up three eggs in a large bowl.
4. Mix the noodles with the beaten eggs.
5. Grease an iron pudding dish with plenty of goose grease or drippings.
6. Put in a layer of noodles, then sprinkle one-fourth cup of sugar, some pounded almonds, the grated peel of one lemon and a few raisins.
7. Sprinkle some melted fat over this, then add another layer of noodles, some more sugar and proceed as with the other layer until all the noodles are used.
8. Bake two hours.

title: Noodle Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of meat off the neck, about two and one-half pounds
- three quarts of water
- an onion
- one celery root
- two carrots
- a large potato
- some parsley
- three tomatoes
- the giblets of poultry
- salt
- noodles

## Directions

1. For six persons, select a piece of meat off the neck, about two and one-half pounds; add three quarts of water, an onion, one celery root, two carrots, a large potato, some parsley, three tomatoes and the giblets of poultry
2. Cook in a closely covered kettle, letting the soup simmer for four or five hours
3. Remove every bit of scum that rises
4. Strain; add salt and remove every particle of fat
5. Put in noodles; boil about five minutes and serve at once
6. If allowed to stand it will become thick

title: Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- flour
- salt
- two tablespoons of butter
- a tablespoon of butter
- a cup of milk

## Directions

1. Beat three whole eggs very light and sift in sufficient flour to make a stiff paste.
2. Work until smooth, break off a piece and roll out on board very thin.
3. Break off another piece and roll and continue until all is used.
4. Let rolled-out dough dry, then cut all except one piece in long strips one inch wide.
5. Fold the one piece in layers and cut very fine noodles.
6. Boil large noodles in pot of salted boiling water, drain in colander when tender and stir in two tablespoons of butter.
7. Heat a tablespoon of butter in the frying-pan and brown fine noodles in this butter.
8. Sprinkle these over the broad noodles, pour a cup of milk over the whole and brown in stove.
9. Serve in same dish in which it was baked.

title: Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large egg
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat one large egg slightly with one-fourth teaspoon of salt, add enough flour to make a stiff dough; work it well for fifteen or twenty minutes, adding flour when necessary.
2. When the dough is smooth place on slightly floured board and roll out very thin and set aside on a clean towel for an hour or more to dry.
3. Fold in a tight roll and cut crosswise in fine threads.
4. Toss them up lightly with fingers to separate well, and spread them on the board to dry.
5. When thoroughly dry, put in a jar covered with cheese cloth for future use.
6. Drop by handfuls in boiling soup, ten minutes before serving.

title: Noodles and Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six apples
- broad noodles made out of three eggs
- two tablespoons of fresh butter
- cinnamon
- sugar
- fresh butter
- milchig pie crust (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel and cut six apples.
2. Take broad noodles made out of three eggs, boil them fifteen minutes, drain, then mix with two tablespoons of fresh butter.
3. Add some cinnamon and sugar to noodles.
4. Put a layer of noodles, then apples and so on until pan is filled, being careful to have noodles on top.
5. Put bits of fresh butter on top.
6. Bake until apples are tender.
7. If so desired, a milchig pie crust may be made and used as an under crust and when apples are tender and crust done, turn out on a large platter with crust side on top.

title: Noodles and Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- broad noodles
- melted butter
- a tablespoon of butter
- one-half tablespoon of flour
- liquor of mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Make broad noodles, boil and serve with melted butter spread over the noodles and this sauce.
2. Brown a tablespoon of butter in the skillet, add one-half tablespoon of flour, then liquor of mushrooms, pinch of salt and pepper.
3. When smooth, add mushrooms.
4. Let boil and serve in a separate dish.
5. When serving, a spoon of mushrooms is to be put over each portion of noodles.

title: Noodles with Butter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of noodles
- two quarts of boiling water
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- two teaspoons of white pepper
- one ounce good butter

## Directions

1. Plunge one pound of noodles into two quarts of boiling water and cook for fifteen minutes.
2. Drain well, replace in the same pan, season with one-half teaspoon of salt, two teaspoons of white pepper, adding one ounce good butter.
3. Gently mix without breaking the noodles until the butter is thoroughly dissolved, and serve.

title: Noodles with Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs (for homemade noodles) or one-quarter of a pound macaroni
- salt water
- cold water
- a cup of cheese
- a piece of fresh butter (about the size of an egg)
- a heaping tablespoon of flour
- a pint of rich milk
- butter
- cracker crumbs
- butter flakes

## Directions

1. If you make the noodles at home, use two eggs for the dough; if you buy macaroni use one-quarter of a pound, cut up and boil in salt water.
2. Boil about fifteen minutes; drain off the water and let cold water run through them.
3. Grate a cup of cheese.
4. Melt a piece of fresh butter, about the size of an egg, in a saucepan, stir in a heaping tablespoon of flour, add gradually to this a pint of rich milk, stirring constantly.
5. Take from the fire as it thickens.
6. Butter a pudding dish, lay in a layer of noodles, then cheese, then sauce, then begin with noodles again until all is used up.
7. Sprinkle cheese on top, a few cracker crumbs and flakes of butter here and there.
8. Bake until brown.

title: Nut and Cheese Relish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one package cream cheese
- one cup of chopped nut meats
- one teaspoon of chopped parsley
- two tablespoons of whipped cream
- salt
- red pepper
- parsley and chopped nuts for garnish

## Directions

1. Mix one package cream cheese with one cup of chopped nut meats, one teaspoon of chopped parsley, two tablespoons of whipped cream, salt and red pepper
2. Roll into balls and serve cold, garnished with parsley and chopped nuts

title: Nut and Raisin Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nuts
- raisins
- cream or grape juice
- thin slices of bread

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of nuts and raisins.
2. Moisten with cream or grape juice and spread on thin slices of bread.

title: Nut Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of butter
- three eggs
- one and one-half cups of sugar
- two and one-half cups of flour
- two and one-half level teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half cup of milk
- one cup of any kind of nuts

## Directions

1. Rub the butter and sugar to a light white cream
2. add the eggs beaten a little
3. add the flour sifted with the powder
4. Mix with the milk and nuts into a rather firm batter
5. Bake in a paper lined tin in a steady oven thirty-five minutes

title: Nut Honey Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of brown sugar
- two cups of honey
- six egg yolks
- three cups of flour
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- three teaspoons of ground cinnamon
- one-half teaspoon of ground cloves
- one-half teaspoon of ground nutmeg
- one-half teaspoon of allspice
- one and one-half teaspoons of soda
- one cup of chopped raisins
- one-half ounce of citron cut in small pieces
- one-half ounce of candied orange peel cut in small pieces
- one-half pound of almonds coarsely chopped
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of brown sugar, two cups of honey, six egg yolks and beat them thoroughly
2. Sift together three cups of flour, one-quarter teaspoon of salt, three teaspoons of ground cinnamon, one-half teaspoon each of ground cloves, ground nutmeg and allspice, and one and one-half teaspoons of soda
3. Add one cup of chopped raisins, one-half ounce of citron cut in small pieces, one-half ounce of candied orange peel cut in small pieces, one-half pound of almonds coarsely chopped
4. Beat the whites of three eggs very stiff and add them last
5. Pour the dough to the depth of about half an inch into well-buttered tins and bake in a slow oven for one-half hour

title: Nut Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of soft bread crumbs
- one cup of chopped walnut meats
- six tablespoons of butter or butter substitute
- one-half cup of hot water
- one and one-half teaspoons of salt
- one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
- one tablespoon of chopped onion
- a sprig of parsley chopped
- one egg

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of soft bread crumbs and one cup of chopped walnut meats with six tablespoons of butter or any butter substitute
2. Add one-half cup of hot water, one and one-half teaspoons of salt, one-quarter teaspoon of pepper, one tablespoon of chopped onion, a sprig of parsley chopped
3. Bind with one egg
4. Shape into a loaf
5. Place in a greased baking-dish and bake in a moderate oven one hour
6. As the liquor boils out of the loaf it may be used for basting
7. A brown sauce may be made in the dish in which the loaf is cooked

title: Nut Roast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of lentils
- one-half cup shelled roasted peanuts
- one-half cup of toasted bread crumbs
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half saltspoon of pepper
- milk
- parsley or watercress

## Directions

1. Soak one-half cup of lentils overnight
2. In the morning drain, cover with fresh water and bring to a boil
3. Drain again, put in fresh water and cook until tender
4. Drain once more, throw away the water, and press the lentils through a colander
5. To them add one-half cup shelled roasted peanuts, either ground or chopped
6. Add one-half cup of toasted bread crumbs, one-half teaspoon of salt and one-half saltspoon of pepper
7. Add milk sufficient to make the mixture the consistency of mush
8. Put into a greased baking-dish
9. Bake in a moderate oven for an hour
10. Turn out on a heated platter
11. Garnish with parsley or watercress and serve

title: Nut Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- grapefruit
- finely chopped almonds or pecans
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Make a plain grapefruit salad.
2. When you have it ready to serve, cover the top thickly with finely chopped almonds or pecans mixed.
3. Pour over French dressing.

title: Nutmeg Cakes (Pfeffernuesse)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- four eggs
- a piece of citron grated or chopped very fine
- the peel of a lemon
- one whole nutmeg grated
- one tablespoon of ground cinnamon
- one-half teaspoon of ground cloves
- half a teaspoon of allspice
- a heaping teaspoon of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Sift one pound of flour and one pound of pulverized sugar into a large bowl.
2. Add four eggs, a piece of citron grated or chopped very fine, also the peel of a lemon, one whole nutmeg grated, one tablespoon of ground cinnamon, one-half teaspoon of ground cloves, and half a teaspoon of allspice.
3. Mix all thoroughly in a deep bowl.
4. Sift a heaping teaspoon of baking-powder in with the flour.
5. Work into little balls as large as hickory nuts with buttered or floured hands.
6. Bake on waxed or buttered tins, an inch apart.

title: Oatmeal Porridge
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of oatmeal
- two cups of salted boiling water
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Put a cup of meal into two cups of salted boiling water (a teaspoon of salt)
2. Let it cook in a double boiler the required time
3. Keep covered until done; then remove the cover and let the moisture escape

title: Oatmeal with Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of oatmeal
- one tablespoon of butter
- one cup grated cheese

## Directions

1. Cook one cup of oatmeal overnight
2. Just before serving add one tablespoon of butter and one cup grated cheese
3. Stir until the cheese is melted and serve at once

title: Oil Twist
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quartern of dough
- one gill of the best Florence oil
- half a pound of currants
- half a pound of moist sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix all well together
2. Make it up in the form of a twist
3. Bake it

title: Okra Gumbo Soup (Southern)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart ripe tomatoes
- one quart okra, cut into small rings
- about two quarts water
- one piece soup meat (no bone)
- one onion
- one carrot
- one sprig parsley
- one chicken
- rice
- salt
- pepper
- corn
- Lima beans

## Directions

1. Take one quart of ripe tomatoes, stew with one quart of okra, cut into small rings
2. Put this on to boil with about two quarts of water and a piece of soup meat (no bone)
3. Chop up an onion, a carrot and a sprig of parsley, add this to the soup
4. Fricassee one chicken with some rice
5. Let the soup simmer four or five hours
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. A little corn and Lima beans may be added; they should be cooked with the soup for several hours
8. Cut the soup meat into small cubes and leave in the soup to serve
9. Dish up with the soup, putting a piece of chicken and one tablespoon of rice into each soup plate before adding the soup

title: Old-Fashioned Hamburger Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- yolks of six eggs, hard-boiled
- flour enough to make a dough that is not too stiff
- three cents worth of ammonia (hartshorn)
- one cup of scalding milk
- one-half cup of cold milk

## Directions

1. Dissolve three cents worth of ammonia (hartshorn) in scalded milk. Place the ammonia in a large bowl and pour one cup of scalding milk over it.
2. After this has cooled add it to the dough with one-half cup of cold milk. Flavor to taste.
3. Flour the pans and the cookie dough. Roll and proceed as with sugar cookies.

title: Old-Fashioned Lebkuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- two cups of brown sugar
- three eggs
- one level teaspoon of baking-soda dissolved in a little milk
- flour
- one cup of milk
- one tablespoon of mixed spices
- half cup of brandy
- one small cup of chopped nuts
- one small cup of citron

## Directions

1. Heat one cup of molasses.
2. Mix it with two cups of brown sugar and three eggs, reserving one white for the icing.
3. Add one level teaspoon of baking-soda that has been dissolved in a little milk.
4. Put in alternately a little flour and a cup of milk.
5. Add one tablespoon of mixed spices, half cup of brandy, one small cup each of chopped nuts and citron.
6. Add lastly, flour enough to make a stiff batter.
7. Place in shallow pans and bake slowly.
8. When done, cover with icing and cut in squares of strips.

title: Old-Fashioned Molasses Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one generous cup of butter
- two cups of New Orleans molasses
- two teaspoons of powdered ginger
- one teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
- half a large nutmeg
- two teaspoons of baking-soda
- half a cup of hot water
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Put in a mixing bowl one generous cup of butter which has stood in a warm place until quite soft.
2. Add two cups of New Orleans molasses.
3. Whip these ingredients to a foam.
4. Add two teaspoons of powdered ginger, one teaspoon of powdered cinnamon and grate in half a large nutmeg.
5. Stir these spices well through the mixture.
6. Dissolve two teaspoons of baking-soda in half a cup of hot water.
7. Stir it through the mixture.
8. Stir in enough sifted flour to make a light dough just stiff enough to roll out.
9. Dust the pastry board well with flour and rub the rolling-pin well with flour, then flour the hands well.
10. Take out some of the dough, put it on the pastry board, quickly roll it out to the thickness of a quarter of an inch.
11. Cut the dough out with a round cutter, with or without scallops.
12. Put them in well-floured baking-pans and bake in a slow oven till a golden brown.

title: Olio
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds of beef
- water to cover
- two fine white cabbages without any of the stalk or hard parts
- pepper
- salt
- a tea-spoonful of white sugar
- half a pound of chorisa

## Directions

1. Put eight pounds of beef in sufficient water to cover it
2. when the water boils take out the meat, skim off the fat
3. return the meat to the stew-pan, adding at the same time two fine white cabbages without any of the stalk or hard parts
4. season with pepper, salt, and a tea-spoonful of white sugar
5. let it simmer on a slow fire for about five hours
6. about an hour before serving, add half a pound of chorisa, which greatly improves the flavor

title: Olive Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe or green olives
- Mayonnaise dressing
- whole-wheat or graham bread

## Directions

1. Take either ripe or green olives and remove the seeds.
2. Mince and mix thoroughly with Mayonnaise dressing.
3. Spread between slices of whole-wheat or graham bread.

title: Omelet Souffle
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of six eggs
- six tablespoons of powdered sugar
- juice of one lemon
- grated rind of lemon
- whites of six eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of six eggs and six tablespoons of powdered sugar added gradually together until thick and smooth
2. Add juice of one lemon and a little grated rind
3. Beat whites as stiff as possible
4. Stir together
5. Put into a warm well-buttered dish
6. Bake in quick oven ten minutes

title: Omelet Soufflé
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of castor sugar
- 1/2 a spoonful of the best flour
- 10 drops of orange-flower water
- almond oil
- sugar

## Directions

1. Break 6 eggs, the yolks in one dish, the whites in another
2. Add to the yolks 3 tablespoonfuls of castor sugar, 1/2 a spoonful of the best flour, 10 drops of orange-flower water
3. Beat the yolks and sugar well together
4. Whip the whites to a stiff snow
5. Add the yolks to the whites, beating the snow all the time
6. Have ready a silver or plated dish, or for want of either a round cake tin
7. Grease it with the almond oil and sift some sugar over it
8. Place the mixture in, put it in a hot oven
9. Look that it rises, if so, run a knife round it
10. Sift some more sugar lightly over it, put it in the oven again
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Omelette Soufflee
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- five yolks and two whites of eggs
- preserve
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs as directed for sweet omelet, using about five yolks and two whites, all of which require being finely beaten and strained
2. Soften a little preserve by holding it over the fire, or mixing a little warm water with it
3. Spread it slightly over the omelette
4. Whisk the remainder of the whites to a froth with white sugar
5. Lay the whisked whites on the preserve
6. Slide the omelette onto a hot dish
7. Double it and serve directly

title: One Egg Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-fourth cup of butter
- one-half cup of sugar
- one egg
- one and one-half cups of flour
- two and one-half teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half cup of milk
- vanilla or lemon

## Directions

1. Cream one-fourth cup of butter with one-half cup of sugar, add sugar gradually, and one egg, well-beaten.
2. Mix and sift one and one-half cups of flour and two and one-half teaspoons of baking-powder.
3. Add the sifted flour alternately with one-half cup of milk to the first mixture.
4. Flavor with vanilla or lemon.
5. Bake thirty minutes in a shallow pan.
6. Spread with chocolate frosting.

title: One-Egg Waffles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of flour
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one and three-fourths cups of milk
- one well-beaten egg
- two tablespoons of melted butter

## Directions

1. Mix one and one-half cups of flour, one teaspoon of baking powder, one-quarter teaspoon of salt
2. Add one and three-fourths cups of milk, add the milk slowly
3. Add one well-beaten egg and two tablespoons of melted butter
4. Drop by spoonfuls on a hot buttered waffle iron, putting one tablespoon in each section of the iron
5. Bake and turn, browning both sides carefully
6. Remove from the iron
7. Pile one on top of the other and serve at once

title: Onion Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three onions
- a little oil
- a little beef gravy
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- the juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Slice finely, and brown in a little oil, two or three onions
2. Put them in a little beef gravy
3. Add cayenne pepper, salt, and the juice of a lemon

title: Onion Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- finely-chopped onions
- fat
- half a clove of garlic
- a very little flour
- salt
- pepper
- soup stock

## Directions

1. Stew some finely-chopped onions in fat
2. You may add half a clove of garlic, cut extremely fine
3. Brown a very little flour in this
4. Season with salt and pepper and add enough soup stock to thin it

title: Onion Sauce for Roast Shoulder of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a large Spanish onion or two smaller ones
- cold water
- salt
- pepper
- a teaspoonful of flour
- a tablespoonful of the fat from clear broth
- a 1/2 pint of the liquor the onions were boiled in

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and lay them in cold water for 1/2 of an hour
2. Put them on to boil with a little salt
3. When quite tender rub them through a sieve into a basin
4. Take a small basin and put in a teaspoonful of flour and a tablespoonful of the fat from clear broth
5. Rub it with the flour and season with a little salt and pepper
6. Take about a 1/2 pint of the liquor the onions were boiled in and put it in a nice clean saucepan with the onions you rubbed through the sieve
7. When it boils stir in the flour and fat you mixed together
8. Let it boil up and serve it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Onion Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three large onions
- one tablespoon of butter
- three tablespoons of flour
- one pint of boiling water
- three good-sized potatoes
- one quart of scalded milk
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- croutons

## Directions

1. Slice two or three large onions
2. fry them in a tablespoon of butter until they are soft and red
3. then add three tablespoons of flour and stir until it is a little cooked
4. To this add slowly a pint of boiling water, stirring all the time, so it will be smooth
5. Boil and mash three good-sized potatoes
6. Add to them slowly a quart of scalded milk, stirring well so it will be smooth
7. Add the potato and milk mixture to the onion mixture
8. Season with salt and pepper
9. Let it get very hot, and pass it through a strainer into the tureen
10. Sprinkle over the top a little parsley chopped very fine, and a few croutons

title: Orange Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 2 cups pulverized sugar
- 1 large orange
- 0.5 cup cold water
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- 2 eggs
- sugar for icing

## Directions

1. Beat light the yolks of five eggs with two cups of pulverized sugar
2. Add juice of a large orange and part of the peel grated, one-half a cup of cold water and two cups of flour, sifted three times
3. Add two teaspoons of baking-powder in last sifting
4. Add last the stiff-beaten whites of three eggs
5. Bake in layers, and spread the following icing between and on top
6. For icing: beat the whites of two eggs stiff, add the juice and peel of one orange and sugar enough to stiffen

title: Orange Chips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Salt water
- Two cups of sugar
- One cup of water

## Directions

1. Halve oranges, scoop out, then scrape inside
2. Lay the peel in salt water overnight
3. Make syrup of two cups of sugar and one cup of water
4. When boiled thick, cut orange-peel in small strips and drop them into boiling liquid
5. Let them remain about ten minutes
6. Remove strips carefully, spreading them on waxed paper to dry

title: Orange Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- two spoons of sugar
- juice of quarter of a lemon
- flour
- beaten whites of eggs
- oil or butter-substitute
- pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of two eggs with two spoons of sugar
2. Stir into this the juice of quarter of a lemon and just enough flour to thicken like a batter
3. Add the beaten whites
4. Dip in one slice of orange at a time
5. Take up with a large kitchen spoon and lay in the hot oil or butter-substitute
6. Fry a nice brown
7. Sprinkle pulverized sugar on top

title: Orange Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- two spoons sugar
- juice of quarter of a lemon
- flour
- beaten egg whites
- orange slices
- oil or butter-substitute
- pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of two eggs with two spoons of sugar
2. Stir into this the juice of quarter of a lemon and just enough flour to thicken like a batter
3. Add the beaten whites
4. Dip in one slice of orange at a time
5. Take up with a large kitchen spoon and lay in the hot oil or butter-substitute
6. Fry a nice brown
7. Sprinkle pulverized sugar on top

title: Orange Ice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of sugar
- four cups of water
- two cups of orange juice
- one-half cup of lemon juice
- grated rind of one orange and one lemon

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of two cups of sugar and four cups of water
2. Boil fifteen minutes
3. Add two cups of orange juice, one-half cup of lemon juice and the grated rind of one orange and one lemon
4. Freeze and serve in glasses

title: Orange Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peel of one-half orange
- two tablespoons of orange juice
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- beaten yolk of one egg
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of one-half orange, mix with two tablespoons of orange juice and one tablespoon of lemon juice and let stand fifteen minutes
2. Strain and add to the beaten yolk of one egg
3. Stir in enough powdered sugar to make it the right consistency to spread upon the cake

title: Orange Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of water
- one ounce of isinglass
- peel of one lemon and one orange

## Directions

1. Simmer one ounce of isinglass in one quart of water
2. Add the peel of one lemon and one orange

title: Orange Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of water
- one ounce of isinglass
- peel of one lemon and one orange
- juice of a lemon and six fine oranges
- half a pound of white sugar
- a Seville orange

## Directions

1. Simmer the isinglass in the water
2. Add the peel of one lemon and one orange
3. When the isinglass is dissolved, add the juice of a lemon and six fine oranges
4. Sweeten with half a pound of white sugar
5. Add a Seville orange if there should not be much flavor in the others
6. Pour it through a flannel bag or thick cloth dipped first into hot water
7. Pass the jelly through it until clear
8. Pour it into moulds and put them in a cool place to set

title: Orange Marmalade Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- orange marmalade
- pecan nuts
- cream cheese
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Take orange marmalade, pecan nuts and cream cheese in equal quantities
2. Mix thoroughly
3. Spread on thin slices of buttered bread

title: Orange-Flower Soufflé
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of castor sugar
- a spoonful of the best flour
- 10 drops of orange-flower water
- almond oil
- sugar

## Directions

1. Break 6 eggs, the yolks in one dish, the whites in another
2. Add to the yolks 3 tablespoonfuls of castor sugar, a spoonful of the best flour, 10 drops of orange-flower water
3. Beat the yolks and sugar well together
4. Whip the whites to a stiff snow
5. Add the yolks to the whites, beating the snow all the time
6. Have ready a silver or plated dish, or for want of either a round cake tin
7. Grease it with the almond oil and sift some sugar over it
8. Place the mixture in, put it in a hot oven
9. Look that it rises, if so, run a knife round it, sift some more sugar lightly over it
10. Put it in the oven again
11. When well risen serve it quickly with a table napkin round the shape
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Orangeade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four large, juicy oranges
- six tablespoons of sugar
- a very little water
- shaved ice
- water
- a little lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take four large, juicy oranges and six tablespoons of sugar.
2. Squeeze the oranges upon the sugar, add a very little water and let them stand for fifteen minutes.
3. Strain and add shaved ice and water, and a little lemon juice.

title: Orgeat
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of almonds
- a table-spoonful of orange-flour water
- one bitter almond
- one pint of new milk
- one pint of water
- sifted white sugar
- half an ounce of gum-arabic

## Directions

1. Beat three ounces of almonds with a table-spoonful of orange-flour water and one bitter almond
2. Pour one pint of new milk and one pint of water to the paste
3. Sweeten with sifted white sugar
4. Add half an ounce of gum-arabic for those who have a tender chest

title: Orgeat Water
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. sweet almonds
- about 1 dozen bitter almonds
- 2 pints of water
- orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 lb. sweet almonds and about 1 dozen bitter almonds
2. Pound them well in a marble mortar
3. Add to them 2 pints of water or more to taste
4. Flavour with orange-flower water
5. Put it into bottles for use
6. It will only keep for 2 or 3 days
7. It is very nice iced
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Orgeat Water
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. sweet almonds
- about 1 dozen bitter almonds
- 2 pints of water or more to taste
- orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 lb. sweet almonds, about 1 dozen bitter
2. pound them well in a marble mortar
3. add to them 2 pints of water or more to taste
4. flavour with orange-flower water
5. put it into bottles for use
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Ox Tail Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two well cleaned tails
- a neat's foot
- a large piece of clarified suet or fat
- between three and four quarts of cold water
- four onions
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- a carrot
- a turnip
- a head of celery
- whole pepper
- allspice
- two or three cloves
- salt
- browned flour
- a glass or more of port wine
- a little mushroom ketchup

## Directions

1. Have two well cleaned tails and a neat's foot, cut them in small joints and soak them in water
2. Put them in a stew-pan with a large piece of clarified suet or fat, and let them simmer for ten minutes
3. Put to them between three and four quarts of cold water, four onions, a bunch of sweet herbs, a carrot, a turnip, a head of celery
4. Season with whole pepper, allspice, two or three cloves, and salt
5. Let it stew till the meat is tender enough to leave the bones, then remove it from them
6. Thicken if necessary with browned flour
7. Just before serving, add a glass or more of port wine, and a little mushroom ketchup

title: Ox Tail Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a large ox tail
- 1 large carrot
- a tooth of garlic
- a little salt and pepper
- pint and half of water
- a few small meat balls
- a quart of stock
- the juice of a lemon
- a dessert spoonful of browning

## Directions

1. Nicely chop a large ox tail, making every joint into 2 pieces.
2. Put it into a saucepan with 1 large carrot, a tooth of garlic, a little salt and pepper, and pint and half of water.
3. Let it boil till quite tender.
4. Boil a few small meat balls with it.
5. When done take out the carrot and cut it up in pieces the size of dice.
6. Put it back into the saucepan with a quart of stock.
7. Let it simmer for an hour.
8. Have in your tureen the juice of a lemon and a dessert spoonful of browning.
9. When you turn it into the tureen stir it well.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Oxtail Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two large oxtails
- one onion
- one tablespoon drippings
- four quarts cold water
- one tablespoon salt
- one tablespoon mixed herbs
- four cloves
- four peppercorns
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Wash two large oxtails and cut into pieces
2. Cut one onion fine and fry in one tablespoon of drippings
3. When brown, add oxtails to brown
4. Put into soup kettle with four quarts cold water
5. Add one tablespoon of salt, one tablespoon of mixed herbs, four cloves, four peppercorns
6. Simmer for three or four hours
7. Skim off fat, strain
8. Cut vegetables into fancy shapes and boil twenty minutes, then add to soup

title: Oyster Plant—Salsify
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- white sauce
- flour
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape salsify and put at once in cold water with a little vinegar to keep from discoloring
2. Cut one-half inch slices and cook in boiling, salted water until soft
3. Drain and serve in white sauce
4. Or boil in salted, boiling water until tender and cut in four pieces lengthwise
5. Dredge with flour and sprinkle with a little salt
6. Fry in hot butter or fat until nicely browned

title: Palestine Salad
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen fine Jerusalem artichokes
- salad mixture

## Directions

1. Take a dozen fine Jerusalem artichokes, boil till tender, let the water strain off
2. When cold cut them in quarters, and pour over a fine salad mixture
3. The artichokes should lay in the sauce half an hour before serving

title: Palestine Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- a calf's foot
- one pound of chorissa
- a large fowl
- four quarts of water
- a piece of fresh lemon peel
- six Jerusalem artichokes
- a bunch of sweet herbs
- a little salt
- white pepper
- a little nutmeg
- a blade of mace
- flower or ground rice

## Directions

1. Stew a knuckle of veal and a calf's foot and one pound of chorissa and a large fowl in four quarts of water
2. Add a piece of fresh lemon peel, six Jerusalem artichokes, a bunch of sweet herbs, a little salt, white pepper, a little nutmeg, and a blade of mace
3. When the fowl is thoroughly done, remove the white parts to prepare for thickening
4. Let the rest continue stewing till the stock is sufficiently strong
5. Pound the white parts of the fowl and sprinkle with flower or ground rice
6. Stir them in the soup after it has been strained until it thickens

title: Palestine Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of Jerusalem artichokes
- two quarts of stock
- one onion
- one turnip
- one head of celery
- pepper and salt to taste
- yolks of three eggs
- potato flour

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the vegetables into slices and boil them in stock until tender, then rub through a hair sieve.
2. Beat the yolks of three eggs, add to the soup, and stir over the fire till just to the boiling point.
3. The soup should be about the thickness of rich cream.
4. If not thick enough, a little potato flour may be added.

title: Pan Roast Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cross-rib or shoulder of beef, about two and one-half to three pounds
- very little fat
- onions

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cross-rib or shoulder, about two and one-half to three pounds, put in a small frying-pan with very little fat.
2. Have the pan very hot, let the meat brown on all sides, turning it continually until all sides are done, which will require thirty minutes altogether.
3. Lift the meat out of pan to a hot platter.
4. Brown some onions, serve these with the meat.

title: Pancakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- flour
- milk or water
- boiling butter or clarified suet
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix a light batter of eggs with flour and milk or water
2. Fry in boiling butter or clarified suet
3. They may be fried without butter or fat, by putting more eggs and a little cream, the pan must be very dry and clean
4. Those fried without butter should be fried of the very lightest colour
5. They are good also made of rice, which must be boiled in milk till quite tender; then beat up with eggs, and flavoured according to taste, and fried like other pancakes

title: Pancakes for Children
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of finely grated bread crumbs
- milk and water
- cinnamon or lemon peel grated
- a couple of beaten eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Simmer the bread crumbs in a little milk and water, flavour with cinnamon or lemon peel grated
2. Add a couple of beaten eggs, and sweeten to taste
3. Drop a small quantity into the pan and fry like pancakes

title: Paprika Carp
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- onions
- water
- paprika

## Directions

1. Slice and salt three pounds of carp
2. Steam four sliced onions with one cup of water, to which has been added one teaspoon of paprika
3. Add the sliced carp and cook very slowly until the fish is done

title: Parsnips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- weak salt water
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape parsnips
2. boil in weak salt water until tender
3. drain and put in white sauce

title: Parve Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- four eggs
- one-quarter pound of poppy seeds
- three tablespoons of oil
- two pounds of sugar
- a little salt

## Directions

1. To one pound of flour take one teaspoon of baking-powder, four eggs, one-quarter pound of poppy seeds, three tablespoons of oil, two pounds of sugar and a little salt
2. Knead not too stiff
3. Put on tins and bake in hot oven till a nice brown (Do not let burn)

title: Parve, Cookie and Pie Dough
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
- a generous half cup of oil
- an exact half cup of very cold (or ice) water
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Sift into a mixing-bowl one and one-half cups of flour and one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
2. Make a depression in the centre; into this pour a generous half cup of oil and an exact half cup of very cold (or ice) water
3. Add pinch of salt, mix quickly with a fork
4. Divide in two portions; do not knead
5. Roll on a well-floured board, spread on pans, fill and bake at once in a quick oven
6. Instead of pinching or crimping the edge, trim the edges in the usual way, then place the palms of the hand on opposite sides of the pie and raise the dough until the edges stand straight up

title: Passover Currant Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit powder
- beaten eggs
- well-washed and dried currants
- sweet sauce
- wine or shrub
- moist sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a thick batter, add some well-washed and dried currants, and fry of a rich brown
2. Serve with a sweet sauce, flavored with wine or shrub, and sweetened with moist sugar
3. These are often made in the shape of small balls, and fried and served in the same sauce

title: Passover Currant Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick batter
- well-washed and dried currants
- sweet sauce
- wine or shrub
- moist sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a thick batter
2. add some well-washed and dried currants
3. fry of a rich brown
4. serve with a sweet sauce, flavored with wine or shrub, and sweetened with moist sugar
5. these are often made in the shape of small balls, and fried and served in the same sauce

title: Passover Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a tea-cup of biscuit powder
- beaten eggs
- white sifted sugar
- grated lemon peel
- a spoonful of orange flower-water

## Directions

1. Mix into a smooth batter a tea-cup of biscuit powder with beaten eggs, and sweeten with white sifted sugar
2. Add grated lemon peel, and a spoonful of orange flower-water, and fry of a light brown
3. The flavor may be varied by substituting a few beaten almonds, with one or two bitter, instead of the orange flower-water

title: Passover Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit powder
- shred suet
- currants and raisins
- a little spice and sugar
- an ounce of candied peels
- fine well beaten eggs
- sweet sauce
