## Directions

1. Mix equal quantities of biscuit powder and shred suet, half the quantity of currants and raisins, a little spice and sugar, with an ounce of candied peels, and fine well beaten eggs
2. Make these into a stiff batter, and boil well, and serve with a sweet sauce
3. This pudding is excellent baked in a pudding tin, it must be turned out when served

title: Passover Pudding Another Sort
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit powder
- shred suet
- currants
- apples minced finely
- sugar
- a little spice

## Directions

1. Mix the various ingredients, substituting for the raisins, apples minced finely, add a larger proportion of sugar, and either boil or bake

title: Passover Pudding Another Sort
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup full of biscuit powder
- a little milk
- a couple of eggs
- three ounces of sugar
- two ounces of warmed butter
- a little shred of lemon peel
- a table-spoonful of rum

## Directions

1. Mix into a batter a cup full of biscuit powder, with a little milk and a couple of eggs, to which add three ounces of sugar, two ounces of warmed butter, a little shred of lemon peel, and a table-spoonful of rum
2. Pour the mixture into a mould, and boil or bake

title: Passover Rock Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of butter
- 1/4 lb. sugar
- 1/4 lb. of currants
- rind of a lemon
- 4 eggs
- 1/4 lb. of the sieved meal
- 2 oz. of ground almonds
- almonds for decoration

## Directions

1. Mix the butter, sugar, currants and lemon with 2 oz. of ground almonds and the meal
2. Beat the eggs and add them
3. The mixture will form a thick paste, and if not thick enough, add more meal
4. Break them in pieces, and put them on a tin to bake
5. Chop a few almonds, and stick a few pieces in each rock
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Paste for Rendering the Skin Supple and Smooth
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of mutton or goose fat
- two eggs
- a glass of rose-water
- a table-spoonful of honey
- oatmeal

## Directions

1. Mix half a pound of mutton or goose fat well boiled down and beaten up well with two eggs, previously whisked with a glass of rose-water
2. Add a table-spoonful of honey, and as much oatmeal as will make it into a paste

title: Pea Puree
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of dried peas
- three pints of fresh, cold water
- one and one-half teaspoons of salt
- one-half teaspoon of sugar
- white pepper
- one-eighth of a pound of hard brisket fat
- one large onion

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash two cups of dried peas
2. Soak them overnight or for several hours in cold water
3. Put them on to boil in three pints of fresh, cold water and let them simmer until dissolved
4. Keep well scraped from the sides of the kettle
5. When soft, rub through a strainer
6. Add a little boiling water or soup stock
7. Add one and one-half teaspoons of salt, one-half teaspoon of sugar and a speck of white pepper
8. Beat the mixture well
9. Put hard brisket fat chopped in small pieces, about one-eighth of a pound, into a spider and cook until a light yellow
10. Add a large onion cut in dice and continue cooking with the fat until brown
11. Serve the puree like mashed potatoes
12. Pour the onion and fat over it before serving
13. Serve hot

title: Peach Butter
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar
- peach kernels
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Weigh the peaches after they are pared and pitted.
2. Allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit.
3. Cook the peaches alone until soft, then add one-half of the sugar and stir frequently.
4. In half an hour put in the remaining sugar.
5. Watch carefully, stirring almost constantly for two hours.
6. Boil slowly, and add one-quarter of the peach kernels.
7. Spice with cinnamon and cloves, using whole spices.

title: Peach Cocktail
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced peaches
- orange or lemon juice
- shaved ice

## Directions

1. Fill the glasses with sliced peaches
2. cover with orange or lemon juice
3. sweeten to taste
4. add a little shaved ice and serve

title: Peach Compote
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sweetened water
- cloves
- cinnamon bark
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pare the fruit, leave it whole
2. Put on to boil with sweetened water
3. Add a few cloves (remove the heads)
4. Add a stick of cinnamon bark
5. Boil the peaches until tender
6. Take up with a perforated skimmer
7. Lay them in your fruit dish
8. Boil the syrup until thick
9. Pour over the peaches
10. Eat cold with sweet cream

title: Peach Cream Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich crust
- Peaches
- Peach kernels
- One cup of rich cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with a rich crust and bake
2. Fill with a layer of sweetened grated peaches which have had a few pounded peach kernels added to them
3. Whip one cup of rich cream, sweeten and flavor and spread over the peaches
4. Set in ice-chest until wanted

title: Peach Cream Tarts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of butter
- Salt
- Flour
- One cup of ice-water
- One whole egg
- Yolks of two eggs
- One tablespoon of brown sugar
- Two teaspoons of baking-powder
- Pulverized sugar
- Compote of peaches
- Whipped cream or ice-cream

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of butter and a little salt with just enough flour to thoroughly mix
2. Add one cup of ice-water, one whole egg, and the yolks of two eggs mixed with a tablespoon of brown sugar to the flour in which you have previously sifted two teaspoons of baking-powder
3. Handle the dough as little as possible in mixing
4. Bake in round rings in a hot oven until a light brown
5. When baked, sift pulverized sugar over the top and fill the hollow centre with a compote of peaches
6. Heap whipped cream or ice-cream on top of each one

title: Peach Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of flour
- a pint of milk or water
- a tablespoon of shortening
- salt
- one egg
- a spoon of sugar
- a piece of compressed yeast
- water
- peaches
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves
- white of egg

## Directions

1. Make a dough of a quart of flour and a pint of milk, or water, a tablespoon of shortening, a pinch of salt, one egg and a spoon of sugar
2. Add a piece of compressed yeast, which has previously been dissolved in water
3. Let the dough raise for three hours
4. In the meantime make a compote of peaches by stewing them with sugar and spices, such as cinnamon and cloves
5. Stew enough to answer for both sauce and filling
6. When raised, flour the baking-board and roll out the dough half an inch thick
7. Cut cakes out of it with a tumbler, brush the edges with white of egg
8. Put a teaspoon of peach compote in the centre of a cake and cover it with another layer of cake and press the edges firmly together
9. Steam over boiling water

title: Peach Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- tablespoon of shortening
- pinch of salt
- one egg
- spoon of sugar
- piece of compressed yeast
- flour
- white of egg
- teaspoon of peach compote

## Directions

1. Add compressed yeast previously dissolved in water to water, shortening, salt, egg, and sugar
2. Let the dough raise for three hours
3. In the meantime make a compote of peaches by stewing them with sugar and spices such as cinnamon and cloves, stewing enough to answer for both sauce and filling
4. When raised, flour the baking-board and roll out the dough half an inch thick
5. Cut cakes out of it with a tumbler
6. Brush the edges with white of egg
7. Put a teaspoon of peach compote in the centre of a cake and cover it with another layer of cake
8. Press the edges firmly together
9. Steam over boiling water and serve with peach sauce

title: Peach Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- clarified butter
- rich coffee cake dough
- peaches
- sugar
- meringue

## Directions

1. Grease your cake-pans thoroughly with good clarified butter
2. Line them with a rich coffee cake dough which has been rolled very thin and set in a warm place to rise
3. Pare and quarter enough peaches to cover the dough
4. Lay the peaches in rows and sweeten and set in oven to bake
5. Make a meringue quickly as possible and pour over the cakes and bake a light brown

title: Peach Pie, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich pie-crust
- Peaches
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Line a pie-plate with a rich pie-crust
2. Cover thickly with peaches that have been pared and sliced fine (canned peaches may be used when others are not to be had)
3. Add sugar and cover with strips of dough
4. Bake quickly

title: Peach Pie, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich paste
- Peaches
- Flour
- Sugar
- Peach kernels
- Whites of three eggs
- Pulverized sugar
- Cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Pare, stone, and slice the peaches
2. Line a deep pie-plate with a rich paste
3. Sprinkle a little flour over the bottom crust and lay in your fruit
4. Sprinkle sugar liberally over them in proportion to their sweetness
5. Add a few peach kernels, pounded fine, to each pie and bake with crossbars of paste across the top
6. Beat the whites of three eggs to a stiff froth and sweeten with about four tablespoons of pulverized sugar, adding one-fourth of a teaspoon of cream of tartar
7. Spread over the pie and return to the oven until the meringue is set
8. Eat cold

title: Peach Preserves
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar
- peach kernels
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Weigh the peaches after they are pared and pitted
2. Allow a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit
3. Cook the peaches alone until soft
4. Add one-half of the sugar and stir frequently
5. In half an hour put in the remaining sugar
6. Watch carefully, stirring almost constantly for two hours
7. Boil slowly and add one-quarter of the peach kernels
8. Spice with cinnamon and cloves, using whole spices

title: Peach Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- three tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of butter
- a saltspoon of salt
- half a grated nutmeg
- a cup of milk
- a cup and a half of flour
- three-quarters of a cup of Indian meal
- three teaspoons of baking-powder
- fine ripe peaches

## Directions

1. Whip to a cream two eggs, three tablespoons of sugar, and two tablespoons of butter
2. Add a saltspoon of salt and half a grated nutmeg
3. Stir these ingredients well into the mixture
4. Stir in a cup of milk
5. Add a little at a time, stirring well in to make a smooth batter, a cup and a half of flour and three-quarters of a cup of Indian meal which have been sifted together with three teaspoons of baking-powder
6. Butter well the inside of a two-quart pudding mold
7. Put a layer of the pudding batter an inch deep in the mold
8. Cover this with a layer of fine ripe peaches that have been peeled and cut in quarters or eighths
9. Sprinkle the layer of peaches with a light layer of sugar
10. Pour in a layer of batter, then a layer of peaches
11. Repeat this process till all the material is in, leaving a layer of batter on top
12. Steam for two hours

title: Peach Shortcake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of pulverized sugar
- a pinch of salt
- one cup of flour
- grated peel of a lemon
- peaches
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Make a sponge cake batter of four eggs, one cup of pulverized sugar, a pinch of salt and one cup of flour
2. Beat the eggs with the sugar until very light
3. Beat until the consistency of dough and add the grated peel of a lemon, and last the sifted flour
4. No baking-powder necessary
5. Bake in jelly tins
6. Cut the peaches quite fine and sugar bountifully
7. Put between layers
8. Eat with cream

title: Peach Syrup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peach peelings
- peach kernels
- water
- juice of two or three lemons
- sugar

## Directions

1. Use the peelings of your peaches when you are through canning and preserving.
2. Add one-third of the peach kernels and put all on to boil in a stone jar on the back of the stove with a little water.
3. When soft, strain through a jelly-bag by letting it drip all night.
4. In the morning add the juice of two or three lemons and boil as you would jelly.
5. Set a pint of juice on to boil and boil for five minutes.
6. Add a pound of sugar and boil five minutes more, but it must boil very hard.
7. Bottle in wide-mouthed bottles or jars.
8. Seal.

title: Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, ripe freestone peaches
- pulverized sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Peel fine, ripe freestone peaches
2. Cover plentifully with pulverized sugar
3. Serve with whipped cream (ice cold)
4. Do not slice until just before dining, or they will be very apt to change color

title: Peanut and Rice Croquettes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of freshly cooked rice
- one cup of peanut butter
- four tablespoons of minced celery
- one teaspoon of grated onion
- one tablespoon of canned tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- white of one egg
- one tablespoon of water
- stale bread crumb dust

## Directions

1. To one cup of freshly cooked rice allow one cup of peanut butter, four tablespoons of minced celery, one teaspoon of grated onion, one tablespoon of canned tomatoes, and salt and pepper to taste
2. Mix well
3. Add the white of one egg, reserving the yolk for coating the croquettes
4. Shape into croquettes
5. Let stand in a cold place for an hour
6. Coat with the egg yolk mixed with one tablespoon of water
7. Roll in stale bread crumb dust until well covered
8. Fry in any hot oil or butter substitute

title: Pear Dumpling (Birne Kloesse)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a loaf of white bread or stale white bread
- one cup of suet chopped very fine
- one cup of flour
- one egg
- salt
- spices
- one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
- a tiny plate
- prunes and pears, about a pound of each
- one cup of brown sugar
- two pieces of stick cinnamon
- dash of claret
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take half a loaf of white bread or as much stale white bread, soak the white part and grate the crust
2. Add one cup of suet chopped very fine, one cup of flour, one egg, salt and spices to taste, and one-half teaspoon of baking-powder
3. Make this into a dumpling, put it on a tiny plate in a large kettle
4. Lay prunes and pears around, about a pound of each, one cup of brown sugar, two pieces of stick cinnamon, dash of claret and cold water to almost cover
5. Cover kettle tightly and boil four hours
6. Serve hot

title: Pear Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rendered fat
- one cup of sugar
- one-half loaf of bread
- water
- little salt
- one-fourth cup of flour
- pears
- water
- sugar
- claret

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of rendered fat with one cup of sugar
2. Add one-half loaf of bread previously soaked and pressed dry, a little salt, one-fourth cup of flour
3. Grease pudding-dish and put in alternate layers of the mixture and pears that have been boiled with water, sugar and claret
4. Bake slowly three hours

title: Pear-Syrup or Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- coarse brown sugar

## Directions

1. Heat the pears in a saucepan over the fire until the pulp, skins, &c., have separated from the juice.
2. Strain the juice.
3. Boil the juice with coarse brown sugar to the thickness of treacle.

title: Peas and Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 red bread pan with cover
- 1 bullock's foot
- 3 lbs. boned brisket
- 1 lb. worsht
- 1 pint Spanish peas
- 1 lb. small white beans
- 2 large onions, sliced
- 2 teeth of garlic
- 2 white sticks of celery, cut fine
- pepper
- salt
- cayenne
- 3 large potatoes
- 6 eggs
- dried saffron
- cold water

## Directions

1. Season the red bread pan by standing it on the hob with boiling water for 2 or 3 hours before using
2. Lay the peas and beans in soak from the night previous
3. Put the foot at the bottom of the pan
4. Layer in the peas and beans, onions, and all the seasoning
5. Put in the beef, the potatoes, and the eggs
6. Add some dried saffron and the worsht
7. Fill 3 parts with cold water
8. Put it in the oven as soon as ready so that it boils well
9. Leave in the oven all night
10. When dishing up, put the foot and meat in one dish and the eggs and potatoes in another
11. Remove the fat before putting the soup in the tureen
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Peas Stewed with Oil
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a peck of peas
- half a lettuce
- mint
- small onion
- two table-spoonsful of oil
- dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Put half a peck of peas into a stew-pan, half a lettuce chopped small, a little mint, a small onion cut up, two table-spoonsful of oil, and a dessert-spoonful of powdered sugar, with water sufficient to cover the peas
2. Watch, from time to time, that they do not become too dry
3. Let them stew gently, taking care that they do not burn, till perfectly soft

title: Pecan, Walnut, or Hickory Nut Macaroons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of pulverized sugar
- one cup of finely-pounded nut meats
- unbeaten whites of two eggs
- two heaping teaspoons of flour
- one scant teaspoon of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Take one cup of pulverized sugar, and one cup of finely-pounded nut meats, the unbeaten whites of two eggs, two heaping teaspoons of flour, and one scant teaspoon of baking-powder
2. Mix these ingredients together and drop from a teaspoon which you have previously dipped in cold water, upon buttered paper
3. Do not put them too near each other, for they always spread a great deal
4. Bake about fifteen minutes

title: Pepper and Cheese Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- green peppers
- cream cheese
- olives
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Fill green peppers with a mixture of cream cheese and chopped olives
2. Set on the ice and then slice the peppers and serve a slice (shaped like a four-leaf clover) on a leaf of lettuce

title: Pepper Mangoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large green peppers
- one head of cabbage
- one cup of mustard seed
- two tablespoons of grated horseradish
- one nutmeg
- one clove of garlic
- ground ginger
- one dozen whole peppercorns
- half a tablespoon of prepared mustard
- one teaspoon of sugar
- half a teaspoon of best salad oil
- salt brine
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take large green peppers
2. Extract the seeds and core with a penknife, being careful not to break the peppers
3. Chop up one head of cabbage after boiling it in salt water
4. When cold add one cup of mustard seed, two tablespoons of grated horseradish, one nutmeg grated, one clove of garlic grated, a pinch of ground ginger, one dozen whole peppercorns, half a tablespoon of prepared mustard, one teaspoon of sugar and half a teaspoon of best salad oil
5. Lay the peppers in strong salt brine for three days
6. Then drain off the brine and lay them in fresh water for twenty-four hours
7. Fill the peppers with the above mixture
8. Sew or tie them up with strong thread
9. Pack them in a large stone jar and pour scalding vinegar over them
10. Repeat this process three times more, at intervals of three days
11. Then tie up the jar and set it away in a cool, dry place for three months

title: Pepper Pot
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- small pieces of any vegetables
- pieces of smoked or salt beef
- any cold poultry, roast beef or mutton
- two or three quarts of water
- whole peppers
- allspice
- mace
- Jamaica pickles
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut small pieces of any vegetables
2. Add pieces of smoked or salt beef and also of any cold poultry, roast beef or mutton
3. Stew all these together in two or three quarts of water, according to the quantity of meat
4. Season highly with whole peppers, allspice, mace, Jamaica pickles, and salt
5. Thoroughly stew it
6. Serve without straining in a tureen

title: Pepper Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peppers
- flaked, cold cooked fish
- celery
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut the peppers lengthwise in half
2. Fill with a mixture of flaked, cold cooked fish and minced celery, mixed with mayonnaise

title: Peppers Stuffed with Meat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peppers
- one onion
- fat
- one-fourth cup of cold cooked chicken or veal
- one-fourth cup of mushrooms
- one-half cup of water
- two tablespoons of bread crumbs
- salt and red pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a slice from the blossom end of each pepper, remove seeds and parboil ten minutes
2. Chop one onion fine and cook in fat until straw color
3. Add one-fourth cup of cold cooked chicken or veal, and one-fourth cup of mushrooms
4. Cook two minutes, add one-half cup of water and two tablespoons of bread crumbs
5. Cool, sprinkle peppers with salt and a pinch of red pepper
6. Fill with stuffing, cover with crumbs and bake ten minutes

title: Peppers Stuffed with Nuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- green peppers
- chopped nuts
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Parboil peppers
2. stuff them with a forcemeat made of chopped nuts and bread crumbs moistened with salt and pepper
3. Bake, basting occasionally with melted butter for twenty minutes

title: Perch Boiled
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 parsley roots
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- blade of mace
- perch
- water
- melted butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan 2 or 3 parsley roots, an onion in slices, some salt, pepper, blade of mace
2. Wash the perch well free from scales
3. Boil in the liquor for 3/4 of an hour, only 1 pint of water
4. Take them out and put them in a dish
5. Pour over them a little melted butter and chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pesach Borsht
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Beet-root
- Water
- Relishes
- Spices
- Whole eggs

## Directions

1. About three weeks before Pesach take twenty pounds of beet-root, which may be thoroughly washed and scraped.
2. Place the whole in a six-quart crock, cover with water.
3. Place the cover on the crock and over this cover put a clean cloth.
4. When ready for use the liquor is boiled with any relishes and spices that are liked.
5. Boil as much as required for the meal, for twenty minutes or longer if desired.
6. Thicken with beaten whole eggs that have been mixed with a little of the unboiled borsht.
7. Add the hot soup and serve.
8. Do not boil after adding the eggs.
9. To two quarts of borsht take three eggs.

title: Pesach Cake with Walnuts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of nine eggs
- one-half pound of powdered sugar
- one pound of walnuts
- two tablespoons of well sifted matzoth flour
- a pinch of salt
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- nine beaten whites

## Directions

1. Cream together the yolks of nine eggs and one-half pound of powdered sugar
2. Weigh one pound of walnuts before shelling; when shelled, grind
3. To the creamed yolks and sugar add two tablespoons of well sifted matzoth flour, a pinch of salt, and one teaspoon of vanilla
4. Mix in the ground walnuts
5. Fold in gently the nine beaten whites
6. Bake three-quarters of an hour

title: Peter Pan Dessert
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- banana
- ice-cream
- chopped walnuts
- pistachio nuts
- marshmallows

## Directions

1. Cut a banana in four strips
2. Cross two over two in basket-shape
3. Fill centre square with a tablespoon of ice-cream
4. Sprinkle over all some chopped walnuts, pistachio nuts and marshmallows, cut in strips

title: Petits Fondeaus
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice flour or arrow root
- eggs
- milk or cream
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Make a batter as for a fondu, but use rice flour or arrow root instead of potatoe flour
2. Add the egg in the same manner as for a fondu
3. Pour the mixture into small paper trays fringed round the top
4. The mixture should only half fill the trays or cases

title: Petits Vol-au-Vents
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- patty preparations or preserved fruits

## Directions

1. These are made in the same way, but cut in small rounds
2. The crumb of the larger is scooped out, and the hollow filled with any of the varieties of patty preparations or preserved fruits

title: Pfarvel or Grated Egg for Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of one egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Into the yolk of one egg stir enough flour until it is too stiff to work.
2. Grate on coarse grater, and spread on board to dry.
3. After soup is strained, put in and boil ten minutes before serving.

title: Pfarvel—Fleischiq
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- egg barley
- two tablespoons of fat
- one-quarter cup of onions
- three cups of hot soup stock or water

## Directions

1. Make as much egg barley as required.
2. Heat two tablespoons of fat, add one-quarter cup of onions, fry until golden brown, add the dried egg barley and brown nicely.
3. Place in a pudding-dish, add three cups of hot soup stock or water to more than cover.
4. Bake in a moderate oven about one hour or until the water has nearly all evaporated and the egg barley stands out like beads and is soft.
5. The onion may be omitted.
6. Serve hot in place of a vegetable.

title: Piccalili
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- gherkins
- French beans
- cauliflower
- carrots
- onions
- capsicums
- white cabbage
- celery
- brine
- vinegar
- ginger
- whole black pepper
- whole allspice
- bruised chillies
- shalots
- garlic
- flour of mustard
- turmeric

## Directions

1. Pickle gherkins, French beans, and cauliflower, separately, as already directed
2. Cut the other vegetables (carrots, onions, capsicums, white cabbage, celery) in small pieces, and soak in brine; boil the carrots in it to make them tender
3. Into half a gallon of vinegar put two ounces of ginger, one of whole black pepper, one of whole allspice, and one of bruised chillies, three ounces of shalots, and one ounce of garlic
4. Boil together nearly twenty minutes
5. Mix a little of it in a basin, with two ounces of flour of mustard and one ounce of turmeric
6. Stir it in gradually with the rest
7. Pour the liquor over the vegetables

title: Piccalilli
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half peck of green tomatoes
- three red peppers
- one cup of salt
- five chopped onions
- one pound of brown sugar
- one-quarter ounce of allspice
- whole cloves
- one bunch of celery
- one-half ounce of mustard seed
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take one-half peck of green tomatoes, three red peppers, chopped
2. Put in one cup of salt
3. Let stand overnight, then strain off the water
4. Add five chopped onions, one pound of brown sugar, one-quarter ounce of allspice, and whole cloves put in a bag; one bunch of celery, one-half ounce of mustard seed
5. Cover with vinegar and boil three hours

title: Pickle For Salmon
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- white wine
- water
- mace
- clove
- ginger root
- whole peppers
- grated horseradish
- salmon
- strong salt and water

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of vinegar, white wine and water.
2. Boil these with a little mace, a clove or two, a bit of ginger root, one or two whole peppers and some grated horseradish.
3. Take out the last named ingredient when sufficiently boiled.
4. Pour the pickle over the salmon, previously boiled in strong salt and water.

title: Pickle Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- one teaspoon salt
- one tablespoon chopped pickle
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Cream two tablespoons of butter, add one teaspoon of salt and one tablespoon of chopped pickle.
2. A speck of red pepper may be added.

title: Pickled Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of wax beans
- salt
- one-half cup of sugar
- one cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the strings and cut one pint of wax beans into one inch pieces
2. Wash and cook in boiling salt water (one teaspoon of salt to one quart of water), until tender, but not soft
3. Drain beans and save the water in which they were cooked
4. Reserve enough of this bean liquor to fill cans, add one-half cup of sugar and one cup of vinegar
5. Let just cook up
6. Add the drained beans
7. Cook all together and pour boiling hot into the cans
8. Seal at once

title: Pickled Beef Tongue
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, fresh beef tongue
- saltpetre, size of walnut
- one teacup of salt
- one teaspoon of pepper
- three small cloves of garlic
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Soak in cold water one-half hour.
2. Crush a piece of saltpetre, size of walnut, one teacup of salt, one teaspoon of pepper, three small cloves of garlic cut fine; mix seasoning.
3. Drain water off tongue.
4. With a pointed knife prick tongue; rub in seasoning.
5. Put tongue in crock; add the balance of salt, etc.; cover with plate and weight.
6. Allow to stand from four to five days.
7. Without washing off the seasoning, boil in fresh water until tender.

title: Pickled Beetroot
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- beetroots, 2 or 3 fine ones
- salt
- ground pepper
- allspice
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Parboil 2 or 3 fine beetroots
2. Skin them and let them drain well
3. Slice them
4. Put them in a jar with salt, ground pepper, and a few allspice
5. Boil as much vinegar as you require to cover them
6. Pour it over boiling hot
7. Tie down when cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Beets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of cold, boiled beets
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-eighth teaspoon of pepper
- one teaspoon of brown sugar
- one teaspoon of caraway seed
- one pint of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of cold, boiled beets, slice
2. Place in crock in layers
3. Sprinkle with one teaspoon of salt, one-eighth teaspoon of pepper, one teaspoon of brown sugar, one teaspoon of caraway seed, if you like, and cover with one pint of vinegar
4. Cold, hard-boiled eggs may be placed in the vinegar, and sliced over the beets for decorations

title: Pickled Button Onions
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bunches of button onions
- salt
- spice
- fresh lemon rind
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Get a few bunches of button onions as they come in
2. Skin them
3. Lay them in a colander
4. Well rub them with salt
5. Let them lay in salt for one day
6. Put them in glass bottles
7. Put very little spice to them
8. Add a large spoonful of salt to each bottle
9. Add a little of the rind of a fresh lemon
10. Fill up with cold vinegar
11. Cork them down tight
12. They will be fit for use in 6 weeks
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Sliced cabbage
- Red onions
- Salt
- Cold water
- Cider vinegar
- Tarragon vinegar
- Granulated sugar
- Bay leaves
- Allspice
- Peppercorns
- Stick cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place sliced cabbage in layers about two inches thick in a jar or stone-crock.
2. Over each layer place two or three thin slices of red onions, and sprinkle about four generous tablespoons of salt.
3. Repeat this process till all the sliced cabbage is in the jar or bowl.
4. Let the last layer be one of salt.
5. Pour a pint of cold water over this.
6. Cover it with a plate that fits closely and lay a weight of some sort on the plate and stand the bowl in a cool place overnight.
7. In the morning pour the cabbage, brine and all, in a large colander to drain; let the cold water from the tap run over it for about five minutes.
8. Return the cabbage to the receptacle in which it was salted.
9. In a kettle or saucepan over the fire add a pint of good cider vinegar, a gill of tarragon vinegar, a half pint of cold water, a half pound of granulated sugar, four bay leaves, a level tablespoon of allspice, a teaspoon of peppercorns and three ounces of stick cinnamon broken in half-inch pieces.
10. Let this all boil one minute and while boiling hot pour it over the cabbage in the jar.
11. Place a porcelain plate over it; then put the cover of the jar on and let this stand for twenty-four hours.
12. Pour off the vinegar, heat it again till it just boils, pour it over the cabbage, cover it and put it in a cool place.

title: Pickled Cantaloupe or Muskmelons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, ripe melons
- vinegar
- 3 pounds and a half white sugar per dozen small cantaloupes
- 3 ounces stick cinnamon
- 1 ounce cloves
- 2 ounces allspice (whole spices)
- 1 pint fresh vinegar

## Directions

1. Take fine, ripe melons, pare, take out the seeds and wash
2. Cut into slices about three inches long and two inches wide
3. Lay them in a stone jar and cover with vinegar for twenty-four hours or longer
4. Lay the fruit on a clean board to drip
5. Throw away one quart of the vinegar to each quart remaining
6. Boil the spices, vinegar and sugar, adding a pint of fresh vinegar to the old
7. When well skimmed put in the melons, boil fifteen minutes, twenty is still better
8. Take out the fruit, put it in jars and boil the syrup awhile longer
9. Skim it again and pour boiling hot upon the fruit
10. Seal when cold

title: Pickled Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four heads of cauliflower
- one cup of salt
- one-quarter of a cup of mixed pickle spices
- thin bag
- two quarts of vinegar
- two cups of sugar

## Directions

1. Separate flowerettes of four heads of cauliflower
2. Add one cup of salt and let stand overnight
3. Place in colander, rinse with cold water and let drain
4. Tie one-quarter of a cup of mixed pickle spices in a thin bag
5. Boil with two quarts of vinegar and two cups of sugar
6. Throw in the cauliflower

title: Pickled Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four heads of cauliflower
- one cup of salt
- one-quarter of a cup of mixed pickle spices
- two quarts of vinegar
- two cups of sugar

## Directions

1. Separate flowerettes of four heads of cauliflower
2. Add one cup of salt, and let stand overnight
3. Place in colander, rinse with cold water and let drain
4. Tie one-quarter of a cup of mixed pickle spices in a thin bag and boil with two quarts of vinegar and two cups of sugar
5. Throw in the cauliflower
6. Boil a few minutes and pour to overflowing in wide-mouthed bottles or cans
7. Cork or cover and seal airtight

title: Pickled Cauliflowers
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fine white cauliflower
- salt
- cloves
- allspice
- mace, 1 or 2 blades
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Select a fine white cauliflower
2. Lay it in water for an hour with a handful of salt
3. Take it out and let it drain in the colander
4. Strip off all the green
5. Divide each little white bunch by itself
6. When dry, put them into a jar or bottles seasoned with salt, a few cloves and allspice, and a blade or two of mace
7. Fill up with cold vinegar
8. Cork jar or bottles very tight
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Crab-Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarts tart, firm, red or yellow crab-apples
- three cups of cider vinegar
- three cups of sugar
- one cup of water
- two tablespoons of cloves
- two sticks of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Select tart, firm, red or yellow crab-apples, three quarts and remove all decayed spots but leave the stems
2. Put three cups of cider vinegar, three cups of sugar, and one cup of water in preserving kettle and let boil two minutes
3. Add two tablespoons of cloves and two sticks of cinnamon broken; these spices must be tied in a bag and let cook ten minutes
4. Lift out carefully with perforated skimmer and put in glass jars
5. When all the apples have been cooked, pour over enough syrup to cover and set spice bag away in a cup
6. Cover jars and let stand twenty-four hours
7. Pour off syrup and boil again
8. Wait two days then boil apples, sugar, with spice bag until apples are tender but firm
9. Place apples in jars and cover to keep hot
10. Boil down syrup a little and fill the jars to overflowing with the hot syrup and seal

title: Pickled Cucumbers
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- green cucumbers
- salt
- cloves
- allspice
- black peppercorns
- mace, 2 or 3 blades
- cold vinegar
- garlic, 3 or 4 teeth

## Directions

1. Select a few green cucumbers when they get cheap
2. Lay them in cold water for 2 or 3 hours to make them crisp
3. Put them on a flat dish with salt for a day
4. Put them in a jar with a few cloves, allspice, black peppercorns, 2 or 3 blades of mace, and plenty of salt
5. Fill the jar up with cold vinegar
6. Cover it over air tight
7. Put it in a dry place
8. Optionally put 3 or 4 teeth of garlic in to make them a nice flavour
9. Choose the cucumbers as near one size as possible
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- 7 pounds fruit
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 ounces whole cinnamon
- 2 ounces whole peppers
- 1 ounce cloves
- 1 orange
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Boil the figs in water one and one-half hours, then drain and weigh
2. Boil syrup one-half hour
3. Add fruit and boil slowly two hours

title: Pickled French Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh young French beans, 1 lb.
- salt
- peppercorns
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Get 1 lb. when very fresh and young
2. Strip them both sides
3. Put them in bottles whole with salt and peppercorns only
4. Fill up the bottles with cold vinegar
5. Cork them tight
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Gherkins
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- gherkins, 50 or 100 nice sized ones
- salt
- cloves
- allspice
- peppercorns
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick 50 or 100 gherkins, nice sized ones
2. Put them in glass bottles with salt, cloves, allspice and peppercorns
3. Fill the bottles up with cold vinegar
4. Tie down air tight
5. Keep them in a dry place
6. Do not put too much of each spice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Green Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- green tomatoes
- allspice
- cloves
- pepper
- mustard pods

## Directions

1. Dry the green tomatoes
2. Put them in a jar with allspice, cloves, pepper, and some mustard pods
3. This makes an excellent pickle done in this way
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Husk Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds husk tomatoes
- 4 pounds sugar
- mace
- cinnamon
- cloves
- vinegar (not more than 1 pint per 7 pounds tomatoes)

## Directions

1. Prick each one with a needle, weigh
2. Put into the kettle with alternate layers of sugar
3. Heat slowly to a boil; skim and add vinegar
4. Add spices and boil for about ten minutes, not longer
5. Take them out with a perforated skimmer and spread upon dishes to cool
6. Boil the syrup thick, and pack as you would other fruit

title: Pickled Meat—Home-Made Corned Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of water
- salt
- four or five pounds brisket of beef
- whole and ground peppers
- one large clove of garlic
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one bay leaf
- one teaspoon of salt-petre

## Directions

1. Take four quarts of water, adding enough salt to float an egg, boil this salted water
2. When cool take four or five pounds brisket of beef, seasoned with whole and ground peppers, one large clove of garlic, pierced in different parts of the beef, one tablespoon of sugar, one bay leaf and one teaspoon of salt-petre
3. Put meat into deep stone pot, pour the boiled water over it and store in a cool place for ten days or two weeks

title: Pickled Nasturtiums
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- nasturtiums
- salt
- pepper
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick them into little pieces
2. Put them in small bottles with salt and pepper only
3. Fill up the bottles with cold vinegar
4. Cork them tightly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small white onions
- salt
- vinegar
- white peppercorns
- cloves
- horseradish
- mustard seed
- dill
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour hot salt water over the onions, which should be small and perfectly white
2. Peel them with a silver spoon (a knife would injure their color)
3. Let them lay in a salt brine for two days
4. Drain the onions and boil enough vinegar to cover them
5. Throw the onions in the boiling vinegar and let them boil only a few minutes
6. Take from the fire and lay them in glass jars, with alternate layers of whole white peppercorns and a few cloves (removing the soft heads, which would turn the onions black), a stick of horseradish sliced, and mustard seed and dill (used sparingly)
7. When the jars are filled heat the vinegar and add a cup of sugar to a gallon of vinegar
8. Cover the jars to overflowing with the vinegar, and seal while hot

title: Pickled Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- cloves
- sugar
- stick cinnamon
- allspice
- cider vinegar
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Brush but do not peel the peaches and select medium-sized ones
2. When all are well brushed, stick each peach quite full of cloves
3. Make a thick syrup of half a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit
4. Cook the peaches in the syrup until they may be easily pierced with a broom splint
5. Carefully skim them from the syrup and after they have cooled on the platters put them in glass jars or stone crocks
6. To the syrup in the kettle add a few pieces of stick cinnamon and a few whole allspice
7. Add half a pint of good cider vinegar and a tablespoon of tarragon vinegar to each quart of syrup
8. When the syrup just comes to a boil after adding the vinegar pour it over the peaches

title: Pickled Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- 4 pounds and a half white sugar per 10 pounds of fruit
- half a cup of water
- 1 quart strong vinegar
- stick cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Pears should always be peeled for pickling
2. If large cut them in half and leave the stems on
3. Throw each pear into cold water as you peel it
4. When all are peeled weigh them
5. Put into the kettle with alternate layers of sugar and half a cup of water and one quart of strong vinegar
6. Add stick cinnamon and a few cloves (remove the soft heads)
7. Heat slowly and boil until tender
8. Remove them with a perforated skimmer, and spread upon dishes to cool
9. Skim the boiling syrup and boil fifteen minutes longer
10. Put the pears in glass jars or a large earthen jar and pour the syrup and spices boiling hot over the fruit
11. When cold seal

title: Pickled Plums
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds plums
- 4 pounds white sugar
- 2 ounces stick cinnamon
- 1 ounce cloves
- 1 pint best pickling vinegar

## Directions

1. Prick the plums with a large needle then weigh them
2. Boil the vinegar, sugar and spices
3. Pour boiling hot over the fruit, which must be packed in a large jar
4. Repeat this three times
5. While the vinegar boils the third time, pack the plums in glass jars and pour the syrup over the plums
6. When cold seal

title: Pickled Plums
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- four pounds of white sugar
- two ounces of stick cinnamon
- one ounce of cloves
- one pint of best pickling vinegar

## Directions

1. Prick the plums with a large needle then weigh them
2. To every seven pounds of fruit use four pounds of white sugar, two ounces of stick cinnamon, one ounce of cloves and a pint of best pickling vinegar
3. Boil the vinegar, sugar and spices and pour boiling hot over the fruit, which must be packed in a large jar
4. Repeat this three times
5. While the vinegar boils the third time, pack the plums in glass jars and pour

title: Pickled Red Cabbage
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- hard cabbage
- salt
- water
- allspice
- cloves
- peppercorns
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Select a very hard cabbage
2. Strip off the outside leaves
3. Cut it in halves
4. Lay it in salt and water for an hour
5. Drain it in a colander
6. Shred it very fine
7. Lay it on a dish with plenty of salt for 3 or 4 hours
8. Put in a jar with allspice, cloves, peppercorns and salt
9. Cover with cold vinegar
10. Cover over air tight
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pickled Red Cabbage (Hungarian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium-size, very hard head of red cabbage
- red onions
- salt
- cold water

## Directions

1. Select a medium-size, very hard head of red cabbage
2. Remove the outer leaves and cut the stalk off close to the head
3. Then cut the cabbage in quarters and take out the heart close to the leaves
4. With a very sharp, thin-bladed knife cut the cabbage in shreds as fine as possible
5. After the cabbage is all finely cut let cold water run over it through a colander
6. Put the cabbage in a big kitchen bowl or a stone-crock in layers about two inches thick
7. Over each layer place two or three thin slices of red onions, and sprinkle about four generous tablespoons of salt
8. Repeat this process till all the sliced cabbage is in the jar or bowl
9. Let the last layer be one of salt
10. Pour a pint of cold water over this
11. Cover it with a plate that fits closely and lay a weight of some sort on the plate and stand the bowl in a cool place overnight
12. In the morning pour the cabbage, brine and all, in a large colander to drain
13. Let the cold water from the tap run over it for about five minutes
14. Then return the cabbage to the receptacle in which it was salted
15. A stone-crock is really the best, as the cabbage will keep

title: Pickled Walnuts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fine large walnuts
- salt
- allspice, 1/2 oz.
- cloves
- peppercorns
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Select some fine large walnuts
2. Prick them all over with a large darning needle
3. Lay them on a flat dish
4. Sprinkle them well with salt
5. Lay them out in the sun
6. Turn them every day and add more salt; in 5 or 6 days they will become quite black
7. Put them in a jar with 1/2 oz. of allspice, cloves, some peppercorns, and some more salt
8. Fill the jar with cold vinegar
9. Tie it down tight
10. Put it in a dry closet
11. They will be ready for use in 4 or 5 months
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pie Crust
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half matzoth
- one tablespoon of fat
- one-half cup of matzoth meal
- two eggs
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one-eighth teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Soak one and a half matzoth and press dry
2. Heat one tablespoon of fat and add the soaked matzoth
3. When dry add one-half cup of matzoth meal, two eggs, two tablespoons of sugar and one-eighth teaspoon of salt
4. Mix well and press into pie-plate with hands
5. Have dough one-quarter inch thick

title: Pie Crust (Merberteig)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- four cups of sifted flour
- a pinch of salt
- a tablespoon of brown sugar
- the yolk of an egg
- a wine-glass of brandy
- one-half cup of ice-water

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter to a cream
2. Add four cups of sifted flour, a pinch of salt and a tablespoon of brown sugar; work these together until the flour looks like sand
3. Take the yolk of an egg, a wine-glass of brandy, one-half cup of ice-water and work it into the flour lightly
4. Do not use the hands; knead with a knife or wooden spoon, knead as little as possible
5. Roll out the dough using as little flour as possible
6. Work quickly, handle dough as little as possible and bake in a hot oven

title: Pie Made From Cold Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal
- fat of smoked beef
- onion
- parsley
- rind of lemon
- grated nutmeg
- forced-meat balls
- cupful of gravy
- smoked beef
- hard-boiled egg
- pastry
- yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Cut cold veal in nice pieces and put it in a stewpan with some of the fat of smoked beef, an onion, and parsley chopped very fine, also a little of the rind of lemon, grated nutmeg
2. Make a few forced-meat balls
3. Let it all simmer for ½ an hour with a cupful of gravy
4. Turn it nicely into a pie dish, put the smoked beef at the bottom, the veal on top, the balls all round
5. Cut a hard-boiled egg in quarters, place it in the four sides of the dish
6. Make a nice crust according to the receipt you will find for pastry
7. Cover your dish with it, trim it all round nicely, shape it to rise in the centre
8. Cut a piece of pastry in 4 leaves, shape them on the dish
9. Wash it all over with the yolk of an egg well beat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pie Made from Cold Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal
- fat of smoked beef
- an onion
- parsley chopped very fine
- a little of the rind of lemon
- grated nutmeg
- forced-meat balls
- a cupful of gravy

## Directions

1. Cut cold veal in nice pieces
2. Put it in a stewpan with some of the fat of smoked beef, an onion, and parsley chopped very fine
3. Add a little of the rind of lemon and grated nutmeg
4. Make a few forced-meat balls
5. Let it all simmer for 1/2 an hour with a cupful of gravy
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pigeon Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- chopped onion
- garlic
- marjoram
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- pickled walnut
- water
- 1/4 lb. of first cut of chuck steak
- small meat balls
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- puff paste
- yolk of egg
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Stew the pigeons the same as stewed pigeons recipe
2. Add 1/4 lb. of first cut of chuck steak, put it at the bottom of the dish
3. Put the pigeons on top, the small balls round
4. Add 2 hard boiled eggs, cut in quarters intermixed with the balls
5. Make a fine puff paste, ornament it with 4 leaves
6. Put on the top crust, wash over with yolk of egg
7. Bake in a brisk oven
8. Put a few pieces of nice green parsley on the crust to garnish when you send up
9. Cut off the little feet, and when you have put on the crust, before you put it in the oven, stick the bone of the feet in at each end leaving out their little claws
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pigeon Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- 4 or 5 large floury potatoes
- salt
- white pepper
- dried marjoram
- chopped parsley
- good dripping or melted fat
- 2 eggs
- egg yolk for washing

## Directions

1. Stew the pigeons the same as in No. 143
2. Boil 4 or 5 large floury potatoes, cut in pieces in a very little water
3. When done, strain them dry
4. Mash them with a fork, some salt and white pepper
5. Add a little dried marjoram and chopped parsley
6. Add a little good dripping or melted fat
7. Mix in 2 eggs
8. Put a layer round the bottom and sides of a pie dish
9. Lay in the stewed pigeons
10. Cover over with the potatoes
11. Shape the edge all round nicely
12. Form it well on the top just as another pie
13. Wash it over with the yolk of an egg
14. Bake in a good oven
15. Have a nice gravy with pigeons
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pigeon Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- pepper
- fat
- onion
- hard-boiled eggs
- minced parsley
- chicken fat
- flour
- rich paste
- crust

## Directions

1. Prepare as many pigeons as you wish to bake in your pie
2. Salt and pepper
3. Melt some fat in a stew-pan, and cut up an onion in it
4. When hot, place in the pigeons and stew until tender
5. Line a deep pie plate with a rich paste
6. Cut up the pigeons, lay them in, with hard-boiled eggs chopped up and minced parsley
7. Season with salt and pepper
8. Put flakes of chicken fat rolled in flour here and there
9. Pour over the gravy the pigeons were stewed in
10. Cover with a crust
11. Bake slowly until done

title: Pigeon Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beef soup
- one pigeon
- vegetables
- beaten yolk of one egg
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a beef soup
2. An hour before wanted add a pigeon
3. Boil slowly, with all kinds of vegetables, provided your patient is allowed to have them
4. Strain
5. Add the beaten yolk of an egg, salt to taste

title: Pigeon Stew with Olives
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- chopped onion
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- half a pint of liquor from beef
- half a pint of French olives
- dried saffron
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Dry the pigeons well, plump them up nicely
2. Put in a stewpan a chopped onion, a tooth of garlic, pepper, salt, a little nutmeg
3. Place the pigeons in whole, with half a pint of liquor from beef
4. Let them stew gently till nearly done
5. Take half a pint of French olives, cut them round and round off the stone, so that they will form the olive again
6. Put in the olives a little dried saffron, and the juice of half a lemon
7. When done put the pigeons in the centre of the dish, the olives round, and the gravy poured over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pigeons
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- lard
- forcemeat
- egg balls
- strong gravy

## Directions

1. Lard and stuff the pigeons
2. Glaze them if desired
3. Stew them in a strong gravy
4. When done enough, remove the pigeons and thicken the gravy
5. Add a few forcemeat and egg balls
6. Serve in the dish with the pigeons
7. Alternatively, split them down the back, broil, and then finish in the stew-pan

title: Pike Polish Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- butter or oil
- carrots
- parsley roots
- onions
- mace
- cloves
- thyme
- bay leaves
- water
- flour
- dried saffron
- lemon juice
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the pike clean and cut it in slices
2. Put in a stewpan ½ lb. of butter or 2 spoonfuls oil
3. Cut 2 carrots in square pieces, a few roots of parsley, and 2 or 3 onions seasoned with mace, cloves, thyme, and 2 bay leaves
4. When this has fried a little in the butter, moisten it with ½ pint of water and boil it for an hour gently
5. Strain it through a sieve over the slices of pike
6. Let them stew till done, be careful they do not break
7. Rub in a basin a tablespoonful of flour with a little dried saffron and the juice of a lemon
8. Stir it in and dish it up garnished with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pike With Egg Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter about the size of an egg
- one onion
- celery root
- parsley root
- lemon slices
- water
- salt
- a dozen whole peppers
- ground white pepper
- two eggs
- one dozen almonds
- one tablespoon of cold water
- curly parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the fish thoroughly, and wash it in hot water, wipe dry and salt inside and out
2. Heat the salt so it will penetrate through the meat of the fish in less time
3. Take a kettle, lay in it a piece of butter about the size of an egg
4. Cut up an onion, some celery root, parsley root and a few slices of lemon, lay the fish in, either whole or cut up in slices
5. Boil in enough water to just cover the fish, and add more salt if required
6. Add a dozen whole peppers, black or white
7. Season with ground white pepper
8. Let the fish boil quickly
9. In the meantime beat up the yolks of two eggs, and pound a dozen almonds to a paste
10. Add to the beaten yolks together with a tablespoon of cold water
11. When done remove the fish to a large platter
12. To ascertain whether the fish has cooked long enough, take hold of the fins; if they come out readily your fish has cooked enough
13. Strain the sauce through a sieve, taking out the slices of lemon and with them garnish the top of the fish
14. Add the strained sauce to the beaten eggs, stirring constantly
15. Return the sauce to the kettle, and stir until it boils
16. Remove quickly and pour it over the fish
17. When it is cold garnish with curly parsley

title: Pilaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of water
- juice of two tomatoes
- a pinch of salt
- one cup of rice
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Put two cups of water on to boil
2. Add juice of two tomatoes and a pinch of salt
3. When boiling, add one cup of rice
4. Let cook until the water has evaporated
5. Add melted butter, mix well
6. Keep in warm place, covered, until ready to serve

title: Pilaf (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- two cups of soup stock
- one-quarter of a white onion
- one-half can of tomatoes
- olive oil or chicken-fat
- salt
- pepper
- a jointed chicken slightly parboiled, or slices of cold cooked chicken or turkey
- two tablespoons of olive oil or chicken-fat

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of rice in cold water for one hour
2. Pour off the water
3. Put the rice with two cups of soup stock and one-quarter of a white onion on to boil
4. Stew until the rice absorbs all the stock
5. Stew one-half can of tomatoes thoroughly and season with olive oil or chicken-fat, salt and pepper
6. Mix it with the rice
7. Sauté in chicken-fat to a light color, a jointed chicken slightly parboiled, or slices of cold cooked chicken or turkey
8. Make a depression in the rice and tomato and put in the chicken
9. Add two tablespoons of olive oil or chicken-fat, and stew all together for twenty minutes
10. Serve on a platter in a smooth mound, the red rice surrounding the fowl

title: Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- sugar
- orange
- leaves or small fruit

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple
2. Dig out all the eyes
3. Cut from the core downward, or chop in a chopping-bowl
4. Set on ice until ready to serve
5. Sugar the fruit well
6. Form into a mound in a dish
7. Garnish the base well with leaves or small fruit of any kind
8. Squeeze the juice of one orange over all

title: Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- half a pound of sugar to a pound of fruit
- water to cover

## Directions

1. Take off rind and trim.
2. Cut into slices and divide into thirds.
3. Fill into glass jars and dissolve sugar in water enough to cover the jars to overflowing.
4. Pour this sweetened water over the pineapples; proceed as in canning fruit in a water bath.
5. Let them boil steadily for at least twenty minutes.
6. Draw the boiler aside or lift it off the coal range and allow the cans to cool in the water in which they were boiled.
7. Remove each can carefully, screwing each can as tightly as possible.
8. Wipe dry and put away in a cool place.

title: Pineapple and Banana Cocktail
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- banana
- fresh or canned pineapple
- lemon or pineapple juice
- autumn leaves or sprays of green fern

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of banana and fresh or canned pineapple
2. cut into small cubes and cover with lemon or pineapple juice
3. Serve in glasses or orange shells placed on autumn leaves or sprays of green fern

title: Pineapple Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple slices
- sherry or white wine
- sugar
- batter
- hot oil
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Soak slices of pineapple in sherry or white wine with a little sugar and let stand one hour
2. Drain and dip slices in batter
3. Fry in hot oil
4. Drain on brown paper
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar

title: Pineapple Ice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four cups of water
- two cups of sugar
- one can grated pineapple
- juice of six lemons
- four cups of ice-water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of four cups of water and two cups of sugar
2. Boil fifteen minutes
3. Add one can grated pineapple and juice of six lemons
4. Cool and add four cups of ice-water
5. Freeze until mushy, using half ice and half salt

title: Pineapple Lemonade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a ripe pineapple
- juice of four lemons
- two cups of sugar
- same quantity of water
- a quart of water
- a cherry in each glass
- a few strawberries or raspberries

## Directions

1. Pare and grate a ripe pineapple.
2. Add the juice of four lemons and a syrup made by boiling together for a few minutes two cups of sugar and the same quantity of water.
3. Mix and add a quart of water.
4. When quite cold strain and ice.
5. A cherry in each glass is an agreeable addition, as are a few strawberries or raspberries.

title: Pineapple Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- sugar
- rich pie dough
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pare and core the pineapple and cut into small slices and sprinkle abundantly with sugar and set it away in a covered dish to draw enough juice to stew the pineapple in.
2. Bake two shells on perforated pie-plates of a rich pie dough.
3. When the pineapple is stewed soft enough to mash, mash it and set it away to cool.
4. When the crust is baked and cool whip half a pint of sweet cream and mix with the pineapple and fill in the baked shell.

title: Pineapple Pie, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich paste
- Pineapples
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Line your pie-plate with a rich paste
2. Slice pineapples as thin as possible
3. Sprinkle sugar over them abundantly and put flakes of sugar here and there
4. Cover and bake

title: Pineapple Pie, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapple
- Sugar
- Rich pie dough
- One-half pint of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pare and core the pineapple and cut into small slices
2. Sprinkle abundantly with sugar and set it away in a covered dish to draw enough juice to stew the pineapple in
3. Bake two shells on perforated pie-plates of a rich pie dough
4. When the pineapple is stewed soft enough to mash, mash it and set it away to cool
5. When the crust is baked and cool whip half a pint of sweet cream and mix with the pineapple and fill in the baked shell

title: Pineapple Soufflé
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nice ripe pineapple
- eggs
- cream

## Directions

1. Take a nice ripe pineapple
2. Grate it and sweeten to taste
3. Beat the whites of two eggs stiff
4. Mix with the pineapple
5. Before serving, whip half a pint of cream
6. Put on the pineapple

title: Pineapple, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Large juicy pineapple
- Fine granulated sugar, allowing one pound to each very large pineapple

## Directions

1. Have your scales at hand, also a sharp-pointed knife and an apple-corer, a slaw-cutter and a large, deep porcelain dish to receive the sliced pineapple
2. Pare the pineapple carefully and dig out all the eyes as you go along
3. Lay the pared pineapple on a porcelain platter
4. Stick an apple-corer right through the centre of the apple, first at one end and then at the other; if it acts stubbornly put a towel around the handle of the corer and twist it, the whole core will come out at once
5. Screw the slaw-cutter to the desired thickness for slicing the pineapple
6. Slice into receiving dish
7. Weigh one pound of fine granulated sugar and sprinkle it all over the apple, and so on until all are pared and sliced
8. Cover the dish until next day
9. Strain all the juice off the apples and boil in a porcelain or bell metal kettle, skimming it well
10. Throw in the sliced pineapples and boil about five minutes
11. Can immediately, filling the cans to overflowing and sealing immediately
12. As the cans grow cold screw tighter and examine daily for three or four days, and screw tighter if possible

title: Pineapple, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapples
- Sugar, allowing half a pound to two pounds of fruit
- Water, allowing a tumblerful to a pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the pineapples as in the first method
2. Steam the sliced pines in a porcelain steamer until tender
3. In the meantime make a syrup of the sugar, allowing a tumblerful of water to a pound of sugar
4. Skim the syrup carefully
5. Put in your steamed pineapples and can as above

title: Pinoche
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of packed medium brown sugar
- One-quarter cup of cream
- One-third cup of nut meats
- One-quarter pound pecans, weighed in shell
- One-third pound hickory in shell

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and cream to soft ball test
2. Cool until you can bear your hand on bottom of pan
3. Stir until it begins to thicken, add chopped nuts
4. When it is too thick to pour easily, spread quickly on a buttered pan
5. Cut in squares and cool

title: Pistachio Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of pistachio nuts
- one tablespoon of brandy
- one pint of rich cream
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Take out the kernels of half a pound of pistachio nuts and pound them in a mortar with one tablespoon of brandy.
2. Put them in a double boiler with a pint of rich cream and add gradually the yolks of three eggs, well beaten.
3. Stir over the fire until it thickens and then pour carefully into a bowl, stirring as you do so and being careful not to crack the bowl.
4. Put a silver spoon into the bowl before pouring in the cream, as this will prevent it cracking.
5. When cold, stick pieces of the nuts over the cream and serve.

title: Plain Boiled Calfs Head, French Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 calf's head
- 1 quart water
- salt
- pepper
- bunch of marjoram, thyme, and parsley
- large onion
- flour
- chopped onion
- fine chopped parsley
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil half a calf's head very carefully in a quart of water seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Add a bunch of marjoram, thyme, and parsley, and a large onion whole
3. Be sure to take out the bunch of herbs
4. Flour the head lightly before putting it on to boil
5. Be careful of the brains and tongue
6. When done serve it on a very hot dish
7. Send up in another small dish an onion chopped very small and some very fine chopped parsley
8. The only sauce used with this is plain oil and vinegar
9. When the head is carved each person is served with a little of the onion and parsley, and then they assist themselves to the oil and vinegar to taste
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Plain Bunt or Napf Kuchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake compressed yeast
- a pinch of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- about two tablespoons of lukewarm water
- two and one-half cups of flour
- a scant cup of lukewarm milk
- one-half cup of butter
- three-fourths cup of powdered sugar
- three eggs
- peel of a lemon or an orange
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Take one cake compressed yeast, add a pinch of salt, one tablespoon of sugar, and about two tablespoons of lukewarm water. Stir the yeast until it is a smooth paste and set it in a warm place to rise
2. Sift two and one-half cups of flour, make a depression in the centre, stir in the yeast and a scant cup of lukewarm milk, make batter, and let it rise until you have prepared the following
3. Rub one-half cup of butter and three-fourths cup of powdered sugar to a cream, just as for cup cake, then add gradually one egg at a time, using three altogether, and stirring all the time in one direction
4. Work in the risen batter two or three spoons at a time between each egg
5. Grate in the peel of a lemon or an orange
6. Butter the bunt-form well; blanched almonds may be set in the grooves of the cake-form after buttering it
7. Put in the dough, set it in a warm place and let it rise for an hour and a half or two hours
8. Bake in a moderate oven one full hour, covered at first

title: Plain Frosting
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of confectioner's sugar
- milk or water
- vanilla
- orange juice and rind
- lemon juice
- chocolate melted over hot water

## Directions

1. To one cup of confectioner's sugar add some liquid, either milk or water, to make it the right consistency to spread
2. Flavor with vanilla
3. Instead of the water or milk, orange juice can be used with a little of the rind
4. Lemon juice can be substituted in place of vanilla
5. Chocolate melted over hot water and added to the sugar and water makes a nice chocolate icing; flavor with vanilla

title: Plain Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- three tablespoons of sweet milk
- a saltspoon of salt
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Whip the yolks of the eggs, the milk and salt to a light foam with an egg whip.
2. Slowly add the yolk mixture to the whites of the eggs, which should be beaten to a stiff froth in a big bowl.
3. After the yolks and milk are well whipped through the whites, beat the whole together for a few minutes with the egg-beater.
4. In an omelet pan or a large frying-pan put a tablespoon of good butter.
5. When the butter is bubbling hot, pour in the omelet mixture.
6. Stir it lightly for the first minute with a broad-bladed knife, then stop stirring it; and, as the mixture begins to stiffen around the edge, fold the omelet toward the centre with the knife.
7. As soon as it is properly folded, turn it over on a hot platter.
8. Decorate with sprigs of parsley and serve.

title: Plain Puff Paste
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of flour
- water
- two ounces of butter
- half a pound of butter total

## Directions

1. Mix a pound of flour into a stiff paste with a little water, first having rubbed into it about two ounces of butter
2. Roll it out
3. Add by degrees the remainder of the butter (there should be altogether half a pound of butter)
4. Fold the paste and roll about two or three times

title: Plain Short Crust
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of flour
- yolks of two eggs
- powdered sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of fresh butter to a pound of flour
2. Add the yolks of two eggs and a little powdered sugar
3. Mix into a paste with water
4. Roll out once

title: Plain Wafers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of flour and one teaspoon of salt together
2. Chop in one tablespoon of butter, and add milk to make a very stiff dough
3. Chop thoroughly and knead until smooth
4. Make into small balls and roll each one into a thin wafer
5. Place in shallow greased and floured pans and bake in a hot oven until they puff and are brown

title: Plain Whey
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- lemon juice or vinegar
- hot water

## Directions

1. Put into boiling milk as much lemon juice or vinegar as will turn it and make the milk clear
2. Strain and add hot water
3. Sweeten

title: Plum Conserve, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of blue plums or German Prunes
- four oranges
- rind of two oranges
- one pound of seeded raisins
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash five pounds of blue plums or German Prunes, cut them in halves and remove the stones.
2. Peel four oranges, slice them fine and cut each slice in half.
3. Cut the rind of two of the oranges into small squares.
4. Add one pound of seeded raisins.
5. Take a measure of sugar and a measure of the mixture, place in preserving kettle on the stove and let come slowly to the boiling point.
6. Cook steadily for several hours until the fruit is clear and thick.
7. Put in jelly glasses or jars.

title: Plum Conserve, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of German prunes
- one pound of seeded raisins
- two oranges
- juice of two lemons
- one pound English walnuts
- three pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Wash three pounds of German prunes, remove the stones and cut them into small pieces.
2. Mix one pound of seeded raisins, two oranges cut in small pieces, the juice of two lemons, one pound English walnuts broken in chunks, and three pounds of sugar.
3. Place all the ingredients in the preserving kettle on the stove and let come slowly to the boiling point.
4. Cook steadily until the fruit is clear and thick.
5. Put in jelly glasses or jars.

title: Plum Jam
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- large long black plum
- good moist sugar

## Directions

1. Choose the large long black plum; to each gallon of which add three pounds of good moist sugar.
2. Bake them till they begin to crack.
3. Put them in pots, of a size for once using, as the air is apt to spoil the jam.

title: Plum Jam
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar in equal proportion to plums
- kernels from some of the plum stones

## Directions

1. Take out the stones
2. Break some of the stones, and put the kernels in the jam
3. Always pound to pound
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Plum Knoedel (Hungarian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- several potatoes
- one egg yolk
- salt
- flour
- German plums
- sugar
- cinnamon
- boiling salted water
- bread crumbs
- butter or schmalz

## Directions

1. Boil several potatoes, mash
2. Mix with one egg yolk, a little salt and enough flour to make a dough soft enough to hold the impress of the finger
3. Roll out and cut into four-cornered pieces
4. In each square place a German plum which has had the pits removed and a mixture of sugar and cinnamon; put in in place of the pit
5. Roll each square into a round dumpling
6. Put these into a pan with boiling salted water and let them cook covered for six or eight minutes
7. When done, serve with some bread crumbs browned in butter or schmalz and spread over the knoedel

title: Plum Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen large purple plums
- rich paste
- one cup of sugar
- powdered sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Select large purple plums, about fifteen plums for a good-sized pie; cut them in halves and remove the kernels and dip each half in flour.
2. Line your pie-tin with a rich paste and lay in the plums, close together, and sprinkle thickly with a whole cup of sugar.
3. Lay strips of paste across the top, into bars, also a strip around the rim, and press all around the edge with a pointed knife or fork, which will make a fancy border.
4. Sift powdered sugar on top.
5. Eat cold.

title: Plum Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of currants
- one pound of raisins
- one pound of shred suet
- one pound flour or half a pound bread crumbs and half a pound of flour
- a quarter of a pound of candied orange and lemon peel
- a little citron cut thin
- half a pound of moist sugar
- six beaten eggs
- a glass of brandy
- ginger
- nutmeg
- cloves
- sifted sugar or whites of eggs beaten to a froth

## Directions

1. Add to one pound of currants one pound of raisins, one pound of shred suet, one pound flour (or half a pound bread crumbs and half a pound of flour), a quarter of a pound of candied orange and lemon peel, a little citron cut thin, half a pound of moist sugar
2. Mix all well together as each article is added, then stir in six beaten eggs and a glass of brandy
3. Beat the pudding well for half an hour, let it stand some time, then put it into a basin and boil six or seven hours in plenty of water
4. It should be seasoned according to taste with ginger, nutmeg, cloves, &c.
5. Serve with sifted sugar or whites of eggs beaten to a froth

title: Plum Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Half lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. soaked bread
- 1 lb. well washed and dried currants
- 1 lb. well stoned raisins
- 1/2 lb. citron, orange, and lemon peel
- 1/2 oz. of mixed spice
- 1 lb. of finely chopped suet
- 2 oz. of almonds blanched and cut in long strips
- the rind of a fresh lemon
- 1/2 lb. of moist sugar
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 glass of good ale
- a good wine or brandy sauce
- blanched almonds
- castor sugar

## Directions

1. Beat all well together for an hour
2. Grease a mould or basin well, and sift some sugar over it
3. Tie it in a cloth (if a mould it will have a cover)
4. Let it boil for 6 or 7 hours
5. Serve with a good wine or brandy sauce, have some almonds blanched and cut in equal sizes to stick it like a porcupine, or whip some whites of eggs to a snow with a little castor sugar, and flavoured with lemon, or put some rum or brandy in the dish, and light it at the door of the dining-room before it goes in
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Plum Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. suet
- 1 lb. plums well stoned
- 1 lb. currants
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1/2 oz. of mixed spice
- a little salt
- 2 soaked biscuits
- 1 lb. of the fine sieved meal
- 8 eggs
- 2 oz. of chopped almonds
- rind of a fresh lemon chopped finely
- burnt rum
- Wine or rum sauce

## Directions

1. Whisk 8 eggs and mix well
2. Add 2 oz. of chopped almonds and the rind of a fresh lemon chopped finely
3. Mix in the suet, plums, currants, sugar, mixed spice, salt, soaked biscuits, and fine sieved meal
4. Beat it up for 1/2 an hour
5. Boil in a shape or cloth
6. Serve with burnt rum and Wine or rum sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Plum Pudding for Thanksgiving Day
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a small loaf of bread
- one cup of suet
- yolks of six eggs
- one cup of currants
- one cup of raisins
- one-half cup of citron
- three-quarters cup of syrup
- one wineglass of brandy
- one cup of sifted flour
- whites of six eggs

## Directions

1. Soak a small loaf of bread and press out every drop of water
2. Work into this one cup of suet shaved very fine, the yolks of six eggs, one cup of currants, one cup of raisins seeded, one-half cup of citron shredded fine, three-quarters cup of syrup, and one wineglass of brandy
3. Add one cup of sifted flour and the stiffly-beaten whites of eggs last
4. Boil four hours in greased melon mold

title: Plum Pudding No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a half box of raisins and currants
- one-quarter pound of citron
- one-quarter pound of suet
- two eggs
- one and one-half cups of sugar
