- a wineglass of brandy
- two cups of cider
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of ground cloves
- flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder

## Directions

1. Chop a half box of raisins and currants, one-quarter pound of citron, and one-quarter pound of suet very fine
2. Mix with two eggs, one and one-half cups of sugar, a wineglass of brandy, two cups of cider, one teaspoon of cinnamon and ground cloves
3. When all these are well mixed add enough flour with a teaspoon of baking-powder in it to thicken well
4. Cook in a greased mold and allow to steam for three hours

title: Plum Rock Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. butter
- 1 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1/2 lb. sultana raisins
- 2 oz. of chopped lemon and orange peel
- 2 oz. of ground almonds
- a little grated rind of lemon
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all well together with two eggs
2. Break the paste in pieces like little rocks
3. Bake on a tin in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Plums, Sweet Potatoes and Meat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of prunes or plums
- one pound of brisket of beef or any fat meat
- five medium-sized sweet potatoes
- one-half cup of sugar
- sour salt (citric acid)
- water

## Directions

1. Wash one pound of prunes or plums and put on to boil with one pound of brisket of beef or any fat meat
2. When the meat is tender add five medium-sized sweet potatoes which have been pared and cut in small pieces
3. Place the meat on top
4. Add one-half cup of sugar and a piece of sour salt (citric acid)
5. Cover and bake until nicely browned
6. If gravy should cook away add some warm water

title: Poached Egg Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread from a round loaf
- pimentoes
- poached eggs
- Mayonnaise
- watercress

## Directions

1. Slice as many pieces of bread from a round loaf as you have persons to serve.
2. Toast these slices and let cool.
3. Across each slice place three strips of pimentoes (use the canned pimentoes).
4. On top of that place a cold poached egg.
5. Put a teaspoon of Mayonnaise on the top of the egg and surround with sprigs of watercress.

title: Poached Eggs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling water
- salt
- bread

## Directions

1. Put on the pan with some boiling water
2. Add enough salt to season the water
3. Crack each egg separately into a teacup
4. Slip them into the pan
5. See that the water covers them
6. Have a round of bread toasted and laid in the dish at the side of the fire
7. Take out each egg separately and lay it on the toast
8. Each egg should look like a snowball
9. Lay them on the toast
10. Send up with hot plates
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Poached Eggs With Fried Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- slices of bread
- eggs
- salt water
- two tablespoons of cream
- one teaspoon of any pungent sauce
- one teaspoon of mushroom catsup
- juice of half a lemon
- one teaspoon of flour

## Directions

1. Fry tomatoes (cut one-half inch thick) in butter, pepper and salt
2. Prepare slices of bread cut round and fried in butter
3. Put on a hot platter with a slice of tomato on each
4. Poach as many eggs as required in boiling salt water
5. Lift out very carefully, placing one egg on each tomato
6. Add to the gravy in which tomatoes were fried two tablespoons of cream, one teaspoon of any pungent sauce, one teaspoon of mushroom catsup, juice of half a lemon, and one teaspoon of flour to thicken
7. Cook up once and pour over eggs
8. Serve very hot

title: Poached Or Dropped Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- boiling salted water
- salt

## Directions

1. Fill a pan with boiling, salted water
2. Break each egg into a wet saucer and slip it into the water
3. Set the pan back where water will not boil
4. Dip the water over the eggs with a spoon
5. When the white is firm and a film has formed over the yolk, they are cooked
6. Take them up with a skimmer, drain and serve hot, on toast
7. Season with salt

title: Pocket Books
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cake dough
- flour
- yolk of one egg
- two tablespoons of milk
- raisin
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Take as much of the coffee cake dough as you desire, lay it on a well-floured biscuit board and mix just enough more flour with it to enable you to roll it out without sticking to the board
2. Roll out about one-fourth inch thick and cut the dough in squares about as long as your finger
3. Beat the yolk of one egg and two tablespoons of milk together; wet each square well with the mixture, lay one raisin in the centre (after the seed has been removed from it), sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon mixed together, then put a small dab of butter on top
4. Catch the four corners of each square together, so that the inside is protected
5. Lay the pocket books, not too closely together, in a greased pan and set aside to rise
6. When well risen bake in a moderately hot oven until well baked and browned nicely

title: Polenta
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of yellow cornmeal
- three cups of cold water
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of pepper
- one and one-half cups of grated cheese
- hot vegetable oil
- white or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Place one cup of yellow cornmeal and three cups of cold water in a double boiler
2. Add one teaspoon of salt, one-half teaspoon of pepper and cook for forty minutes
3. While still hot add one and one-half cups of grated cheese to the mixture and heat until it melts
4. Turn the mixture into a greased bowl and allow it to set
5. The meal may be sliced an inch thick or cut with a biscuit cutter and then fried in hot vegetable oil
6. Serve with white or tomato sauce as desired

title: Polish Salad, Or Salad Piquant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen or more large salt pickles
- water
- two sour apples
- one large onion or two small ones
- white pepper
- sugar
- salt
- white wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Lay half a dozen or more large salt pickles in water for about six hours, then drain off all the water.
2. Chop up two sour apples, one large onion or two small ones, chop the pickles and mix all thoroughly in a bowl.
3. Sprinkle over them a scant half teaspoon of pepper (white) and a tablespoon of sugar (either white or brown).
4. Add a pinch of salt if necessary.
5. Pour enough white wine vinegar over all to just cover.
6. Do not make more at a time than you can use up in a week, as it will not keep longer.

title: Pommes Frites
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- five fine apples
- egg
- white powdered sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cut five fine apples in half
2. Dip them in egg and white powdered sugar
3. Fry in butter
4. When done, strew a little white sugar over them

title: Popovers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- two cups of well-sifted flour
- yolks of three fresh eggs
- one teaspoon of salt
- butter
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Mix to a smooth batter two cups each of milk and well-sifted flour, the yolks of three fresh eggs and a teaspoon of salt
2. Butter well the inside of six or eight deep earthen popover cups and stand them in a pan in a hot oven
3. While the cups are heating, beat to a froth the whites of the three eggs
4. Stir the whites quickly into the batter
5. Open the oven door, pull the pan forward
6. Pour the batter in the hot buttered cups up to the brim
7. Push the pan back, close the oven door
8. Bake the popovers till they rise well and are brown at the sides where they part from the cups
9. Serve them hot, folded lightly in a napkin

title: Poppy Seed Cookies (Mohn Plaetzchen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- sugar
- butter
- one cup of poppy seed
- two eggs
- Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Take an equal quantity of flour, sugar and butter, and mix it well by rubbing with the hollow of the hands until small grains are formed
2. Add one cup of poppy seed, two eggs, and enough Rhine wine to hold the dough together
3. Roll out the dough on a well-floured board, about half a finger in thickness
4. Cut into any shape desired

title: Pot Roast Braised Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fat or goose fat
- onion
- salt
- paprika
- hot water
- one-half a sweet green pepper
- one small carrot
- two tablespoons of tomatoes
- two onions
- two and a half pounds of brisket shoulder

## Directions

1. Heat some fat or goose fat in a deep iron pot.
2. Cut half an onion very fine and when it is slightly browned put in the meat.
3. Cover up closely and let the meat brown on all sides.
4. Salt to taste, add a scant half teaspoon of paprika and half a cup of hot water and simmer an hour longer, keeping covered closely all the time.
5. Add one-half a sweet green pepper (seeds removed), one small carrot cut in slices, two tablespoons of tomatoes and two onions sliced.
6. Two and a half pounds of brisket shoulder or any other meat suitable for pot roasting will require three hours slow cooking.
7. When the meat is tender, remove to a warm platter, strain the gravy, rubbing the thick part through the sieve and after removing any fat serve in a sauce boat.

title: Potato Balls with Parsley
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- medium potatoes
- boiling water
- salt
- two tablespoons of butter
- one-half tablespoon of lemon juice
- one tablespoon of finely minced parsley
- salt
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Pare very thin, medium potatoes as near a size as possible
2. Have ready a pot of boiling water, salted
3. Drop in the potatoes and keep them at a quick boil until tender
4. Beat to a cream two tablespoons of butter, one-half tablespoon of lemon juice and one tablespoon of finely minced parsley
5. Add salt and a dash of cayenne pepper
6. Spread over the hot potatoes and it will melt into a delicious dressing

title: Potato Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.67 cup butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 0.5 cup milk
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup hot mashed potatoes
- 1 cup chocolate
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- jam

## Directions

1. Cream two-thirds cup of butter with two cups of granulated sugar
2. Add one-half cup of milk, yolks of four eggs, one cup of hot mashed potatoes, one cup of chocolate, one teaspoon each of cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg, one teaspoon of vanilla, one cup of chopped walnuts, two cups of flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder, then beaten whites of four eggs
3. Bake slowly in two pans, and cut in half when cold
4. Put jam between layers

title: Potato Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- eggs
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- butter or drippings

## Directions

1. Make just as pancakes, only bake in the oven in a long cake pan with plenty of butter or drippings under and above

title: Potato Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold mashed potatoes
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Take cold mashed potatoes or cold baked or boiled potatoes that have been mashed and seasoned
2. Roll into balls, dusting the hands well with flour first
3. Flatten into cakes
4. Saute in butter or place on a buttered tin with a small piece of butter on the top of each
5. Bake in a hot oven until golden brown

title: Potato Croquettes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of mashed potatoes
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- one egg
- salt
- nutmeg
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- hot oil or butter substitute

## Directions

1. Work into two cups of mashed potatoes, a tablespoon of melted butter, until smooth and soft
2. Add one egg well-beaten
3. Beat all together with a wooden spoon
4. Season with salt and nutmeg
5. Roll each in beaten egg then in bread crumbs
6. Fry in hot oil or butter substitute

title: Potato Flour Noodles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one-half cup of potato flour
- one-half cup of water

## Directions

1. Take three eggs, beat until a light yellow and add one-half cup of potato flour and one-half cup of water, beat well.
2. Heat a frying-pan, grease well and pour in the batter; fry in thin leaves or wafers.
3. Cool, cut thin as noodles.
4. Just before serving soup, strain, then let it come to a boil and add noodles and let soup again come to a boil and serve.

title: Potato Flour Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound of goose-oil
- yolks of ten eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of sifted sugar
- grated peel of a lemon
- juice of a lemon
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half pound of potato flour
- whites of ten eggs

## Directions

1. Stir goose-oil to a cream
2. Stir in gradually the yolks of ten eggs and three-quarters of a pound of sifted sugar
3. Add the grated peel of a lemon, also its juice and one-half teaspoon of salt
4. Add last one-half pound of potato flour and the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
5. Have the pudding form well greased before putting in the mixture
6. Bake in a moderate oven
7. Serve with raspberry sauce

title: Potato Flour Sponge Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nine eggs
- one and three-quarter cups of sugar
- juice and rind of one lemon
- one scant cup of potato flour

## Directions

1. Separate the whites and yolks of nine eggs
2. Beat the whites of seven eggs very stiff
3. To the well-beaten yolks of nine eggs and the whites of two, add one and three-quarter cups of sugar and juice and rind of one lemon
4. Beat thoroughly
5. Add one scant cup of potato flour, and beat again
6. Fold in the beaten whites very carefully
7. Bake slowly in a moderate oven forty to fifty minutes

title: Potato Marbles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of plain mashed potatoes
- generous teaspoon of finely chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- one egg
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Mix one-half pound of plain mashed potatoes smoothly with a generous teaspoon of finely chopped parsley, pepper and salt to taste
2. Beat one egg, add it to the potato, mix well
3. Make it into little balls the size of a cherry
4. Lay a tiny sprig of parsley on each
5. Arrange the balls on a greased tin
6. Bake till a light brown

title: Potato Pancakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large potatoes
- two eggs per pint of grated potato
- one tablespoon flour
- one-half teaspoon salt
- pepper
- onion juice

## Directions

1. Peel six large potatoes and soak several hours in cold water
2. Grate and drain
3. For every pint allow two eggs, about one tablespoon of flour, one-half teaspoon of salt, a little pepper, a little onion juice if desired
4. Beat eggs well and mix with the rest of the ingredients
5. Drop by spoonfuls on a hot greased spider in small cakes
6. Turn and brown on both sides
7. Serve with apple sauce

title: Potato Pie with Cold Meat
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- tomatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- floury potatoes
- water
- grated nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- egg
- lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare some cold meat exactly as you did for the marrows, with the difference that you chop the tomatoes and onion with the meat
2. Season it well, and add to the mixture two extra eggs
3. Boil some floury potatoes according to the size you wish to make it
4. Cut them in pieces, put a little salt and very little water
5. When they are done drain them well
6. Mash them in the saucepan, taste and see if they are salted enough
7. If so, put some pepper, a little grated nutmeg, a little chopped parsley, 1 egg
8. Mash it all well together
9. Lay some at the bottom of your dish, grease it well first
10. Then put in the forced meat
11. Sprinkle the juice of half a lemon
12. Cover it over with the remainder of the mashed potatoes
13. Smooth it well, and crossbar it with a knife
14. Put in the oven to bake
15. Serve it in the same dish with another dish under
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Potato Plum Knoedel (Hungarian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight large potatoes
- salt
- four whole eggs
- one and one-half tablespoons of melted chicken fat
- a little more than a cup of matzoth meal
- one tablespoon of prune jam
- boiling salt water
- cup of hot melted chicken fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and cook seven or eight large potatoes, place in a bowl
2. Add salt, four whole eggs, one and one-half tablespoons of melted chicken fat and a little more than a cup of matzoth meal
3. Knead in bowl to smooth consistency
4. Take a handful at a time, pat smooth and flat
5. In the centre put a tablespoon of prune jam
6. Form into a dumpling
7. Place dumplings in boiling salt water, kettle half covered
8. Allow to cook twelve to fifteen minutes
9. Take out with strainer and serve hot
10. Have ready a cup of hot melted chicken fat and sugar and cinnamon
11. Serve over knoedel to taste

title: Potato Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of eight eggs
- a cup of sugar
- four tablespoons of blanched and pounded almonds
- grated peel of a lemon
- its juice
- half a pound of grated potatoes which have been cooked the day previous
- whites of eggs
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Stir the yolks of eight eggs with a cup of sugar
2. Add four tablespoons of blanched and pounded almonds, and grate in the peel of a lemon
3. Add also its juice
4. Have ready half a pound of grated potatoes which have been cooked the day previous
5. Last add the stiffly-beaten whites
6. Add one teaspoon of salt
7. Grease your pudding form well, pour in the mixture and bake
8. Set in a pan of boiling water in the oven
9. The water in the pan must not reach higher than half way up the pudding form
10. Bake for half an hour
11. When done turn out on a platter
12. Serve with a wine or chocolate sauce

title: Potato Puff
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold mashed potatoes
- melted butter
- eggs
- cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Take two cups of cold mashed potatoes and stir into them one tablespoon of melted butter, beating to a white cream before adding anything else.
2. Put with this two eggs beaten extremely light, one cup of cream, and salt to taste.
3. Beat all well and pour into a deep dish, and bake in a quick oven until it is nice and brown.

title: Potato Ribbon
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- fat

## Directions

1. Pare and lay in cold water, ice-water is best, for half an hour
2. Select the largest potatoes
3. Cut round and round in one continuous curl-like strip
4. Handle with care and fry a few at a time for fear of entanglement, in deep fat

title: Potato Salad, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ten small, round potatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- mustard seed
- celery seed
- sugar
- egg
- goose or chicken fat
- vinegar
- hot water
- hard-boiled eggs
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Boil ten potatoes (small, round ones preferred) in their skins
2. When done, peel them while still hot and slice in thin, round slices
3. Spread over the potatoes one onion, sliced fine
4. Sprinkle generously with salt and pepper
5. Add one tablespoon of mustard seed, one-half tablespoon of celery seed, and one-half tablespoon of sugar
6. Beat one egg until light
7. Pour two tablespoons of goose or chicken fat, melted, over the eggs
8. Stir well
9. Add one-half cup of vinegar
10. Pour over the seasoned potatoes
11. Add one-quarter cup of hot water and if necessary, add a little more vinegar, salt or pepper
12. One or two chopped hard-boiled eggs may be added to improve the salad
13. Line a salad bowl with lettuce leaves
14. Pour in the salad and decorate the top with grated hard-boiled eggs

title: Potato Salad, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of small Bermuda potatoes
- cold water
- salt
- white onions
- oil
- vinegar
- tarragon vinegar
- pepper
- mayonnaise
- cream
- hard-boiled eggs
- boiled beets
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and scrub the potatoes thoroughly in cold water
2. Put them in a kettle with enough cold water, slightly salted, just to cover them
3. Stand them over a brisk fire with the kettle covered until the water begins to boil
4. Turn down the heat, lift the cover of the kettle slightly, and let the potatoes cook slowly till done
5. Drain off the water and stand the potatoes where they will get cold, but do not put them in a refrigerator
6. When quite cold, peel the potatoes and slice them very thin in a salad bowl
7. To every two layers of potato slices sprinkle over a very light layer of white onions sliced very thin
8. Pour over the salad a French dressing made of equal parts of oil and vinegar with tarragon vinegar, salt and pepper
9. When ready to serve, cover the surface of the salad with a stiff mayonnaise in which a suggestion of cream has been mixed
10. Ornament with quarters of hard-boiled eggs, boiled beets cut in fancy slices and a fringe of parsley around the edge of the bowl

title: Potato Salad, No. 3
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of olive oil
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- six hot potatoes
- two small onions
- garlic
- parsley
- six stuffed olives
- three hearts of celery
- six radishes
- three slices of red beets
- two hard-boiled eggs
- mayonnaise dressing
- whole olives
- lettuce
- celery leaves

## Directions

1. Put into a bowl two tablespoons of olive oil, one tablespoon of sugar, one teaspoon of salt, some pepper and one tablespoon of vinegar and mix all together
2. Cut into this in slices six hot potatoes
3. Cut into small pieces two small onions, a little garlic, some parsley, six stuffed olives, three hearts of celery, six radishes, three slices of red beets and two hard-boiled eggs
4. Add this to the gravy in the bowl, mix well, and season to taste
5. Put all into a glass dish and pour over this a prepared mayonnaise dressing
6. Decorate with parsley, olives (whole), some lettuce and put in the centre some celery leaves

title: Potato Schalet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six large potatoes
- one onion
- bread
- two or three crackers
- salt
- two tablespoons of boiling fat
- one beaten egg
- hot fat
- one cup of cold water

## Directions

1. Peel and grate five or six large potatoes and one onion.
2. Soak some bread and two or three crackers.
3. Press out the water and add to the potatoes and onion, salt to taste.
4. Add two tablespoons of boiling fat and one beaten egg.
5. Have plenty of hot fat in pan, put in the pudding.
6. Pour over it one cup of cold water.
7. Bake in hot oven one hour.

title: Potato Soufflet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large potatoes
- small cup of milk
- 2 oz. of butter
- salt
- 1/2 lb. of pounded sugar
- lemon
- orange-flower water
- yolks of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Wash potatoes well, select the best shaped, put them in the oven
2. When baked sufficiently make a hole at the top of each
3. Scrape out the insides with a spoon, and put it in a stewpan with a small cup of milk, 2 oz. of butter, a pinch of salt, 1/2 lb. of pounded sugar
4. Rasp a lemon on a piece of sugar, or flavour with a little orange-flower water
5. Add the yolks of 2 eggs
6. Beat the white to a snow, mix it with the rest
7. Fill the potatoes with this mixture
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Potato Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four potatoes
- one tablespoon of butter
- one-half tablespoon of flour
- one teaspoon of chopped onion
- one pint of milk
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- croutons

## Directions

1. Boil and mash three or four potatoes, one tablespoon of butter, one-half tablespoon of flour, and one teaspoon of chopped onion, letting the onion cook in the butter a few minutes before adding the flour
2. When this is cooked add to it a pint of milk, making a thin, white sauce
3. Add this to the mashed potato and pass the whole through a strainer
4. Return it to the fire for a few minutes to heat and blend it
5. Season it with salt and pepper
6. Sprinkle on the soup chopped parsley and a few croutons

title: Potato Stuffing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- hot mashed Irish potatoes
- sweet potatoes
- bread stuffing

## Directions

1. Add two cups of hot, mashed Irish or sweet potatoes to bread stuffing
2. Mix well and stuff in goose, stuffed veal or lamb breast, or in beef casings, cleaned and dressed

title: Potato Surprise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large potatoes
- cooked mutton
- pepper
- salt
- potato pulp
- gravy
- fat

## Directions

1. Take large potatoes, parboil without peeling.
2. Cut a small piece of one end of the potato and scoop out the inside.
3. Mince two ounces cooked mutton, season with pepper and salt, mix with the potato pulp and a little gravy.
4. Return end of potato to its place and bake for about twenty minutes with a little fat on top of each potato.

title: Potato Wall, or Edging
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely-mashed potatoes
- egg

## Directions

1. Raise a wall of finely-mashed potatoes, of two or three inches high, round the dish
2. Form it with a spoon to the shape required, brush it over with egg, and put it in the oven to become hot and brown
3. If it does not brown nicely, use the salamander

title: Potato-Rye Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of potatoes (diced)
- boiling water
- one-quarter cup of salt
- one and one-quarter cups of cornmeal
- two tablespoons of any kind of fat
- two cups of flour
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one cake of yeast
- one cup of lukewarm water
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Cook one quart of potatoes diced, in boiling water until tender
2. Strain, reserving potato water
3. Measure and add enough more water to make three cups
4. Let come to a boil, add one-quarter cup of salt, and very gradually one and one-quarter cups of cornmeal
5. Cook two minutes, stirring constantly until thick
6. Remove from fire, add two tablespoons of any kind of fat, the potatoes riced or mashed and when cooled two cups of flour
7. Add one tablespoon of sugar and one cake of yeast dissolved in one cup of lukewarm water
8. Mix and knead to a stiff dough adding wheat flour to keep it from sticking
9. Cover, set aside in a warm place overnight, or until double its bulk
10. Shape into four loaves, let rise again
11. Glaze with egg diluted with water before putting in the oven
12. Bake in a moderate oven one hour or more, until well done

title: Potatoe Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fine potatoes
- two quarts of water
- the trimmings of any meat, about a pound in quantity
- a cup of rice
- a few sweet herbs
- a head of celery
- a little thickening
- white pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Grate a pound of fine potatoes in two quarts of water
2. Add to it the trimmings of any meat, amounting to about a pound in quantity
3. Add a cup of rice, a few sweet herbs, and a head of celery
4. Stew well till the liquor is considerably reduced
5. Strain it through a sieve
6. If when strained it is too thin and watery, add a little thickening
7. Flavour only with white pepper and salt

title: Potatoes (Hungarian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- onion juice
- flour
- hot milk
- salt
- paprika
- egg yolk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash, pare and cut potatoes in one-third inch pieces, there should be three cups.
2. Parboil three minutes, and drain.
3. Add one-third cup of butter, and cook on back of range until potatoes are soft and slightly browned.
4. Melt two tablespoons of butter, add a few drops of onion juice, two tablespoons of flour, and pour on gradually one cup of hot milk.
5. Season with salt and paprika, then add one well-beaten egg yolk.
6. Pour sauce over potatoes and sprinkle with finely chopped parsley.

title: Potatoes and Corn
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- potatoes
- corn
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- cracker crumbs
- milk

## Directions

1. Butter well a deep baking dish, holding a quart or more
2. In the bottom place a layer of potatoes, sliced thin
3. Add a layer of corn, using one-half the contents of a can
4. Sprinkle a little grated onion and season with salt, pepper and bits of butter
5. Add another layer of potatoes, then the rest of the corn, seasoning as before
6. Cover the whole with a layer of cracker crumbs
7. Dot well with butter
8. Pour on milk until it comes to the top
9. Bake three-quarters of an hour
10. Use cooked potatoes, having them cold before slicing

title: Potatoes and Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons fat
- onion
- two tablespoons flour
- one and one-half cups water
- salt
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- four medium-sized cooking pears
- six medium raw potatoes

## Directions

1. Heat two tablespoons of fat
2. Add chopped onion and two tablespoons of flour
3. When flour is brown, add one and one-half cups of water
4. Stir and cook until smooth
5. Add salt, brown sugar and a little cinnamon to taste
6. Quarter four medium-sized cooking pears, but do not peel
7. Cook them in the brown sauce
8. Add six medium raw potatoes, pared
9. Cook until tender

title: Potatoes au Gratin
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups cold boiled potatoes
- two cups hot cream sauce
- three tablespoons grated cheese
- salt
- pepper
- buttered bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Slice cold boiled potatoes
2. Add them to hot cream sauce
3. Bring all to a boil
4. Remove and add grated cheese, salt and pepper to taste
5. Pour all into a baking dish
6. Sprinkle buttered bread crumbs over the top
7. Set in the oven to brown

title: Potatoes Boiled in Their Jackets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- boiling water
- cloth

## Directions

1. Brush potatoes well
2. Put on to boil in a saucepan of boiling water
3. Continue boiling at the same degree of heat until done, when a fork will easily pierce them
4. This will take from twenty-five to thirty minutes
5. Drain the potatoes
6. Draw the saucepan to a low flame
7. Place a clean cloth folded over the top of the saucepan and press the lid down over it
8. This dries the potatoes and makes them a good color
9. Hold the potatoes in a cloth and peel them
10. Reheat for one minute and serve

title: Potatoes with Caraway Seeds
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized potatoes
- two tablespoons butter or fat
- salt
- one teaspoon caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Boil medium-sized potatoes in their jackets until tender
2. Peel while hot
3. Put two tablespoons of butter or fat in spider
4. When hot add potatoes and brown well all over
5. Drain
6. Sprinkle with salt and one teaspoon of caraway seeds
7. Serve hot

title: Pound Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 glass brandy
- 9 eggs
- 1.25 pounds sifted flour
- 0.5 teaspoon baking-powder

## Directions

1. Rub one pound of butter and one pound of powdered sugar to a cream
2. Add the grated peel of a lemon, a glass of brandy and the yolks of nine eggs, added one at a time
3. Add last one pound and a quarter of sifted flour with one-half teaspoon of baking-powder and the beaten whites of the eggs
4. Bake slowly

title: Prenesas
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- flour
- ten eggs
- clarified butter
- clarified sugar
- lemon essence

## Directions

1. Take one pint of milk and stir in as much flour as will bring it to the consistency of hasty pudding
2. Boil it till it becomes thick and let it cool
3. Beat it up with ten eggs
4. When smooth, take a spoonful at a time and drop it into a frying-pan in which there is a good quantity of boiling clarified butter
5. Fry of a light brown and serve with clarified sugar flavored with lemon essence

title: Preparation for Cutlets of Fowl or Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- a little salad oil
- two eggs
- a little white pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- slices of lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make a smooth batter of flour, and a little salad oil, and two eggs, a little white pepper, salt, and nutmeg
2. Turn the cutlets well in this mixture, and fry a light brown
3. Garnish with slices of lemon, and crisped parsley
4. Crisp the parsley by putting it in after the cutlets have been fried
5. Drain it to prevent its being greasy

title: Prepared Mustard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon of sugar
- saltspoon of fine salt
- one tablespoon of best salad oil
- two tablespoons of ground mustard
- vinegar
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Rub together one teaspoon of sugar, saltspoon of fine salt and one tablespoon of best salad oil
2. Do this thoroughly
3. Mix two tablespoons of ground mustard with vinegar enough to thin it
4. Then add to the mixture of sugar, and if too thick, add a little boiling water

title: Preserved Apricots
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe apricots
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Halve and pare ripe apricots, or if not quite ripe, boil them till the skin can easily be removed
2. Lay them in a dish hollow downwards
3. Sift over them their own weight of white sugar
4. Let them lay for some hours
5. Put the fruit, with the sugar and juice into a preserving pan
6. Simmer till the fruit is clear
7. Take it out and put it carefully into pots

title: Preserved Blackberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Weigh the fruit and allow a pound of sugar to every pound of fruit
2. Tie spices in a bag, such as cloves and cinnamon
3. Make a thick syrup of the sugar before you put in the berries
4. Boil half an hour and seal when cold

title: Preserved Cherries
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar in equal proportion

## Directions

1. Lay them with the sugar the night before
2. Drain off the syrup to boil the sugar with before you put in the cherries
3. Keep them as whole as you can
4. Put them in pots
5. Cover with the syrup
6. Cover down the same as the others
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Preserved Cherries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sour red cherries
- sugar
- cherry stones

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries, preserving every drop of juice, then weigh the cherries
2. For every pound take three-quarters of a pound of sugar
3. Set the sugar and juice of the cherries on to boil, also a handful of the cherry stones pounded and tied in a thin muslin bag
4. Let this boil about fifteen minutes
5. Skim off the scum that rises
6. Now put in the cherries, and boil until the syrup begins to thicken like jelly
7. Remove from the fire, fill in pint jars
8. When cold, cover with brandied paper and screw on the cover tight

title: Preserved Damson Plums
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Pick the plums over carefully, removing every one that has a decayed spot or blemish. Leave the stems on
2. After picking the fruit over, wash it carefully in cold water; then weigh it and allow one pound of sugar to each pound of fruit
3. Put a gill of water in the preserving kettle for each pound of sugar
4. Stand the kettle over a moderate fire and add the sugar
5. Stir it almost constantly with a wooden spoon until the sugar melts; then turn on a little more heat and let the melted sugar boil gently until it is a thick syrup
6. Stir, and skim it frequently
7. When the required thickness (which should be like syrup used for griddle cakes) put the plums in the boiling syrup and let them cook gently for half an hour
8. Then skim out the plums and put them in glass jars, filling each jar half full
9. Let the syrup boil till almost as thick as jelly, then pour it in the jars, filling them quite full
10. Fasten the tops on and stand the jars upside down until the preserves are cold; then put them where they are to be kept for the winter

title: Preserved Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh figs
- sugar
- water
- lemons
- ginger

## Directions

1. Lay fresh figs in water overnight
2. Then simmer in water enough to cover them until tender, and spread upon dishes to cool
3. Make a syrup of a pound of sugar to every pound of fruit. Allow a small teacup of water to a pound of sugar
4. Boil until a very clear syrup; remove every particle of scum
5. Put in the figs and boil slowly for ten minutes
6. Take them out and spread upon dishes, and set them in the hot sun
7. Add the juice of as many lemons as you have pounds of sugar, and a few small pieces of ginger
8. Boil this syrup until thick
9. Boil the figs in this syrup for fifteen minutes longer
10. Then fill in glass jars three-quarters full, fill up with boiling syrup and cover
11. When cold, screw air-tight or seal

title: Preserved Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh figs
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Lay fresh figs in water overnight.
2. Then simmer in water enough to cover them until tender, and spread upon dishes to cool.
3. Make a syrup of a pound of sugar to every pound of fruit.
4. Allow a small teacup of water to a pound of sugar.

title: Preserved Ginger Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces of melted veal fat
- 6 oz. of flour
- pint and a half of almond milk
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of preserved ginger
- almond oil
- sifted sugar
- brandy or rum sauce

## Directions

1. Mix 6 oz. of flour with 6 ounces of melted veal fat
2. Stir it over a slow fire
3. Add a pint and a half of almond milk gradually with the above over the fire
4. Beat up the yolks of 6 eggs
5. Cut 1/2 lb. of preserved ginger fine
6. Beat the whites of the eggs to a snow
7. Mix in well
8. Grease a mould well with almond oil and sifted sugar
9. Tie it down and let it steam for 2 hours
10. Serve with a brandy or rum sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Preserved Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- peaches
- water

## Directions

1. Weigh one pound of sugar for each pound of fruit
2. Brush each peach with a stiff whiskbroom
3. Peel the peaches very thin with a sharp silver knife
4. As fast as the peaches are peeled lay them on porcelain platters
5. Put the peelings in the preserving kettle with enough water to keep from sticking
6. Stand the kettle over rather a quick fire and let the peelings boil with the kettle covered until very soft
7. Then drain them through a colander and pour the juice strained back into the kettle
8. Add sugar to this and let it simmer gently until it is a thick syrup
9. During the time the syrup is cooking it must be frequently stirred and skimmed
10. As soon as the syrup is thick enough, drop in the peaches, twelve at a time if for quart jars, and six at a time if for pint jars
11. Let the peaches cook gently until each one may easily be pierced with a broom splint
12. Then quickly skim them out and lay them on a platter to cool
13. Repeat this process until all the peaches are done
14. Let the syrup cook until thick as molasses
15. Skim it thoroughly
16. When cool put the peaches, one at a time, in the jars with a spoon
17. When the syrup is sufficiently thick, pour it through a strainer over the peaches in the jars until they are full, then seal down quickly and stand them upside down for several hours before putting them in the store-room

title: Preserved Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. To one pineapple take three-quarters of its weight in sugar and one cup of water
2. Peel the pineapple and put it through the food-chopper
3. Weigh and add three-quarters of the weight in sugar
4. Bring slowly to a boil and simmer for about twenty minutes, or until the consistency of marmalade

title: Preserved Quinces
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of sugar for each pound of fruit
- quinces
- water

## Directions

1. Weigh a pound of sugar for each pound of fruit
2. Wipe each quince carefully with a coarse linen towel
3. Peel, quarter and core the quinces
4. Put peels and cores in the preserving kettle with just water enough to cover them, and let them simmer with the kettle covered for two hours
5. Strain the liquor through a fine sieve and return it to the kettle
6. Cut the quartered quinces in small pieces and put as many of them in the kettle as the liquor will cover
7. Let them boil gently, with the kettle uncovered, until so tender they may be easily pierced with a broom splint
8. Take them out with a skimmer and lay on flat dishes to cool
9. Repeat this process until all the fruit is properly cooked
10. Put the sugar in the liquor and let it boil gently to a thick syrup
11. Put in as many of the cooked quinces as the syrup will cover and let them cook in the syrup for twenty minutes
12. Skim them out and lay on flat dishes to cool
13. Repeat this process until all the quinces are cooked in the syrup
14. When they are cool put the quinces in glass jars, filling each one half full
15. Let the syrup boil until very thick, stirring it frequently and skimming it clear
16. Pour it through a fine strainer, while very hot, over the fruit
17. As soon as a jar is full, fasten on the cover

title: Preserved Ripe Tomatoes for Winter Use
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- hard red tomatoes, not too large
- good salad oil

## Directions

1. Select some hard red tomatoes, not too large
2. Wipe them carefully
3. Put them in a glass jar if you have one
4. Do not press them down
5. Fill the jar with good salad oil
6. The oil can be used for cooking purposes when you have used the tomatoes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Preserved Strawberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. To one pint of strawberries take one pint of sugar and one-half cup of water
2. Bring the sugar and water to a boil
3. Add the strawberries and cook ten minutes
4. Remove the berries carefully with a skimmer and cook the syrup until it is of the consistency of jelly
5. Return the berries to the syrup; bring all to a boil
6. When cool put in glass tumblers

title: Pressed Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one or more chickens
- water
- salt
- parsley
- lemon
- pickles
- olives

## Directions

1. Boil one or more chickens just as you would for fricassee, using as little water as possible
2. When tender remove all the meat from the bone and take off all the skin
3. Chop as fine as possible in a chopping bowl
4. Add all the liquor the chicken was boiled in, which ought to be very little and well seasoned
5. Press it into the shape of a brick between two platters, and put a heavy weight over it so as to press hard
6. Set away to cool in ice-chest and garnish nicely with parsley and slices of lemon before sending to the table
7. It should be placed whole upon the table, and sliced as served
8. Serve pickles and olives with it

title: Prince Albert Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sweet butter
- half a pound of sifted powdered sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- grated peel of one lemon
- half a pound of raisins
- a little citron
- half a pound of the finest flour
- whites of six eggs
- blanched and pounded almonds

## Directions

1. Rub to a cream half a pound of sweet butter and half a pound of sifted powdered sugar
2. Add the yolks of six eggs, one at a time, and the grated peel of one lemon
3. Stone half a pound of raisins and add also a little citron, cut very fine
4. Add gradually half a pound of the finest flour, sifted three or four times
5. Add the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
6. Pour this mixture into a well-buttered mold, into which you have strewn some blanched and pounded almonds
7. Boil fully three hours
8. Serve with sweet brandy or fruit sauce

title: Prune and Raisin Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of prunes
- one-half cup of raisins
- one-half cup of sugar
- ground clove or lemon juice
- pie crust

## Directions

1. Cook prunes until soft enough to remove the stones.
2. Mash prunes with a fork and add the juice in which they have been cooked.
3. Cook raisins in a little water for a few minutes until soft.
4. Add raisins to the prune mixture with one-half cup of sugar and a little ground clove or lemon juice to improve the flavor.
5. Bake with two crusts.

title: Prune and Raisin Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half pound of prunes
- One-half cup of raisins
- Water

## Directions

1. Use one-half pound of prunes, cooked until soft enough to remove the stones
2. Mash with a fork and add the juice in which they have been cooked
3. Cook one-half cup of raisins in a little water for a few minutes until soft
4. Add to the prune

title: Prune Blintzes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of potato flour
- three eggs
- water
- fat
- prunes
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix three cups of potato flour with three eggs
2. Add a little water and mix well
3. Heat a small frying-pan, grease with a little fat
4. Pour into it enough batter to make thin pancakes
5. Chop prunes, add a little sugar
6. Fill each cake with this mixture
7. Fold into three-cornered pieces and fry
8. When done put in a pan, sprinkle with sugar and bake in oven
9. Do not let burn

title: Prune Cake (Kuchen)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- kuchen dough
- prunes
- sugar
- cinnamon
- grated peel of a lemon or lemon juice
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Line one or two plates with a thin roll of kuchen dough and let it rise again in the pans which have been heavily greased
2. Have some prunes boiled very soft, take out the kernels, mash them until like mush
3. Sweeten to taste, add cinnamon and grated peel of a lemon or lemon juice
4. Put in the lined pie-plates and bake immediately
5. Serve with whipped cream, sweetened and flavored

title: Prune Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a little more than one pint of sweet milk
- a heaping tablespoon of butter
- a little nutmeg
- one cup of stewed prunes
- cream

## Directions

1. Heat a little more than a pint of sweet milk to the boiling point.
2. Stir in gradually a little cold milk in which you have rubbed smooth a heaping tablespoon of butter and a little nutmeg.
3. Let this just come to a boil, then pour into a buttered pudding-dish, first adding one cup of stewed prunes with the stones taken out.
4. Bake for fifteen to twenty minutes, according to the state of oven.
5. A little cream improves it when it is served in the saucers.

title: Prune Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich pie paste
- prunes
- lemon
- sugar
- prune juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a rich pie paste.
2. Roll out the paste thin and line the pie-plate with it.
3. Put in a layer of prunes that have been stewed the day before with the addition of several slices of lemon and no sugar.
4. Split the prunes in halves and remove the pits before laying them on the pie crust.
5. After the first layer is in sprinkle it well with sugar, then pour over the sugar three or four tablespoons of the prune juice and dust the surface lightly with flour.
6. Repeat this process till there are three layers.
7. Cut enough of the paste in strips to cover the top of the fruit with a lattice crust and bake the pie in a rather quick oven.

title: Prune Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- one cup of sugar
- two well-beaten eggs
- one cup of cream of wheat or farina
- stewed prunes
- plain cream

## Directions

1. Take one quart of milk, one teaspoon of salt, one cup of sugar and two well-beaten eggs
2. Heat this and then pour in slowly one cup of cream of wheat or farina, stirring constantly
3. Boil fifteen minutes
4. Butter a deep pudding dish and put in a layer of stewed prunes that have been cut up in small pieces with a scissors on the bottom
5. Pour a layer of the above over this, alternating in this order until all has been used
6. Bake ten minutes in a hot oven
7. Serve with plain cream, not whipped or sweetened

title: Prune Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- a cup of granulated sugar
- thirty prunes
- two tablespoons of sweet chocolate
- two tablespoons of grated almonds
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Take the yolks of four eggs and a cup of granulated sugar and stir to a cream
2. Chop fine thirty prunes (prunes being boiled without sugar)
3. Add two tablespoons of sweet chocolate and two tablespoons of grated almonds
4. Add the whites, which have been beaten to a snow
5. Boil two and one-half hours in a pudding form
6. Serve with whipped cream

title: Prune Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- about one pound of Turkish prunes
- ground cinnamon
- juice of an orange

## Directions

1. Wash the prunes in hot water
2. put on to boil in cold water
3. Boil until they are very soft
4. Remove the pits or kernels
5. strain over them the water they were boiled in
6. sweeten to taste
7. Flavor with ground cinnamon
8. mash them until a soft mush
9. If too thick, add the juice of an orange

title: Prune Soufflé
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a large cup of stewed prunes
- whites of three eggs
- a half cup of sugar
- butter
- whipped cream
- cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from a large cup of stewed prunes and chop fine.
2. Add the whites of three eggs and a half cup of sugar beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Mix well, turn into a buttered dish and bake thirty minutes in a moderate oven.
4. Serve with whipped cream.
5. If it is desired to cook this in individual cups, butter the cups, fill only two-thirds full, to allow for puffing up of the eggs, and set the cups in a pan of water to bake.
6. Some like a dash of cinnamon in this.

title: Prune Soufflé
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stewed prunes
- eggs
- sugar
- butter
- whipped cream
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from a large cup of stewed prunes and chop fine
2. Add the whites of three eggs and a half cup of sugar beaten to a stiff froth
3. Mix well
4. Turn into a buttered dish
5. Bake thirty minutes in a moderate oven
6. Serve with whipped cream
7. If it is desired to cook this in individual cups, butter the cups, fill only two-thirds full, to allow for puffing up of the eggs, and set the cups in a pan of water to bake
8. Some like a dash of cinnamon in this

title: Prune Whip
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of prunes
- cold water
- one-half cup of sugar
- three eggs
- one-half teaspoon of lemon juice
- thin custard or cream

## Directions

1. Soak one-half pound of prunes in cold water overnight.
2. In the morning let them simmer in this water until they are very soft.
3. Remove stones and rub through strainer.
4. Add one-half cup of sugar and cook five minutes or until the consistency of marmalade.
5. When the fruit mixture is cold, add the well-beaten whites of three eggs and one-half teaspoon of lemon juice; add this gradually.
6. Heap lightly in buttered dish and bake twenty minutes in a slow oven.
7. Serve cold with thin custard or cream.

title: Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- lemon
- stick cinnamon
- sugar
- water
- potato flour
- juice of an orange

## Directions

1. Wash the prunes well, first in warm water, then in cold
2. Cut up half a lemon, some stick cinnamon and sugar to taste
3. Cook them in the oven, covered tight, allowing a liberal quantity of water
4. Stew slowly for two hours
5. Thicken with a teaspoon of potato flour, and wet the potato flour with the juice of an orange before adding

title: Prunes Without Sugar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- water

## Directions

1. Wash prunes thoroughly
2. Pour boiling water over same and let them stand for ten minutes
3. Drain and pour boiling water over them again
4. Put in sealed jar
5. See that prunes are all covered with water
6. Ready for use after forty-eight hours

title: Pudding a la Grande Belle
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- crumbed bread
- dried English currants
- three eggs
- one pint of milk
- four tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Brush small molds with butter, fill with crumbed bread and dried English currants
2. Beat three eggs without separating, add one pint of milk and four tablespoons of sugar
3. Pour carefully over the bread and let stand five minutes
4. Place molds in baking-pan of boiling water and bake in the oven thirty minutes, or steam half an hour
5. Serve with liquid pudding sauce

title: Pudding à la Royale
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of savoy biscuits
- 2 oz. of almond cakes
- 1 oz. of ratafias
- 1/4 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 a tumbler of sherry
- 1 wine-glass of rum
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- 1 oz. of isinglass
- 1 oz. of orange and lemon peel
- 1 oz. of angelica
- oil of almonds

## Directions

1. Boil the isinglass in a little water to dissolve, then strain it into the milk
2. Beat up the eggs, stir in the wine and rum
3. Stir it into the almond milk in which you have boiled the sugar
4. Let it all boil to a custard, turn it out to cool
5. Crumble up the whole of the cakes
6. Grease the mould with oil of almonds, dust it well with sifted sugar
7. Cut up the orange and lemon peel and angelica, all cut in nice strips
8. Ornament the mould well with them
9. Put a layer of the crumbled cake and one of custard till the mould is filled
10. Put it in the ice to set, turn out just before it is wanted
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Puff Paste or Blaetter Teig
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of butter
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half pound of flour
- one-fourth to one-half cup of ice-water

## Directions

1. Cut off one-third of the butter and put the remaining two-thirds in a bowl of ice-water
2. Divide this into four equal parts; pat each into a thin sheet and set them away on ice
3. Mix and sift flour and salt; rub the reserved butter into it and make as stiff as possible with ice-water
4. Dust the slab with flour; turn the paste upon it; knead for one minute, then stand it on ice for five minutes
5. Roll the cold paste into a square sheet about one-third of an inch thick; place the cold butter in the centre and fold the paste over it, first from the sides and then the ends, keeping the shape square and folding so that the butter is completely covered and cannot escape through any cracks as it is rolled
6. Roll out to one-fourth inch thickness, keeping the square shape and folding as before

title: Puff-Paste
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. melted fat (veal or beef)
- 2 tablespoonfuls of oil
- 1 lb. flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Let the melted fat get warm and soft like butter, put with 2 tablespoonfuls of oil, mix it well and beat it till it is white as snow and just the consistency of butter
2. Place in a basin 1 lb. of flour to make puff-paste, it must be the best
3. Take up the lb. of fat you have beaten in the lump, lay it on the flour, turn it over to cover it with flour, lay it on the paste board
4. Make a paste with the remainder of the flour with a little cold water, not harder or softer than the fat
5. Roll it out on the board, then take the lump of fat, lay it on top and roll it over the paste
6. Sprinkle a little flour over, then fold it up on the board and cover it with the basin you made it in, and let it be for one hour
7. If in summer it must be put in a very cold place for the fat not to melt; if in winter, you can leave it in the kitchen, as the fat must not get hard
8. Be very particular not to handle it too much, and as light as you can; before beginning it in the summer let the cold water run well over your hands
9. Sometimes in the summer you have to put it on ice to get it the proper firmness
10. After it has laid for an hour, put it on your paste board, roll it out lightly 3 times
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Pumpkin Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of stewed pumpkin
- Four eggs
- Scant cup of sugar
- One pint of milk
- One-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- Less than one-half teaspoon of mace
- Grated nutmeg
- Very rich crust

## Directions

1. Press through a sieve one pint of stewed pumpkin
2. Beat yolks and sugar together until very thick
3. Add one pint of milk to the beaten eggs
4. Add the pressed pumpkin, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon, less than one-half teaspoon of mace and grated nutmeg
5. Stir the stiffly-beaten whites in last
6. Bake in a very rich crust without cover

title: Punch Ices
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of two lemons
- three-quarters of a pound of loaf sugar
- two or three tablespoons of rum
- one pint of water
- rind of the lemons

## Directions

1. Rub the rind of the lemons onto the sugar
2. Boil the sugar and water together for fifteen minutes
3. Add the lemon juice and rum
4. Mix well
5. Strain and set aside to cool
6. Put the mixture into the freezing can and freeze till set

title: Punch Sauce for Puddings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 oz. of sifted sugar
- 1 teacupful of water
- juice of a lemon
- 1/2 a teaspoonful of arrowroot
- 1 wine-glass of good rum

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of 3 eggs with an ounce of sifted sugar
2. Boil a teacupful of water with the juice of a lemon
3. Add 1/2 a teaspoonful of arrowroot to the eggs
4. When you have beaten them, pour the beaten eggs and sugar into the boiling water and lemon
5. When it is thick, stir in a wine-glass of good rum
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Puree of Celeriac
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- celeriac
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of cream

## Directions

1. Boil celeriac: pare the celeriac, cut in thin, narrow slices, and put into cold water
2. Drain from this water and drop into boiling water and boil thirty minutes
3. Drain and rinse with cold water
4. Press through a sieve
5. To one quart take two tablespoons of butter blended with two tablespoons flour and cooked until smooth and frothy, add the strained celeriac and cook five minutes, stirring frequently
6. Add one teaspoon of salt and a half cup of cream, cook five minutes longer and serve hot on toast or fried bread

title: Purim Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one tablespoon of sugar
- four eggs
- two tablespoons of oil
- one pound of honey
- one-quarter pound of poppy seeds
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take two cups of flour, one tablespoon of sugar, add four eggs and two tablespoons of oil
2. Knead all these together
3. Roll out not very thin
4. Cut in squares
5. Close two sides
6. Prick with a fork so they will not blister
7. Put on tins and bake well
8. Take one pound of honey, boil, and put the squares in this and let boil a bit
9. Drop in one-quarter pound of poppy seeds and put back on fire
10. When nice and brown sprinkle with a little cold water
11. Take off and put on another dish so they do not stick to each other

title: Quark Strudel (Dutch Cheese)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel or roley-poley dough
- half a pound of cheese
- salt
- yolks of two eggs
- one whole egg
- grated peel of one lemon
- two ounces of sweet almonds
- four bitter almonds
- four ounces of sultana raisins
- citron chopped fine
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Make a strudel or roley-poley dough and let it rest until you have prepared the cheese
2. Take half a pound of cheese, rub it through a coarse sieve or colander
3. Add salt, the yolks of two eggs and one whole egg, sweeten to taste
4. Add the grated peel of one lemon, two ounces of sweet almonds, and about four bitter ones, blanched and pounded, four ounces of sultana raisins and a little citron chopped fine
5. Roll out as thin as possible
6. Spread in the cheese
7. Roll and bake, basting with sweet cream

title: Queen Bread Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of grated bread crumbs
- one pint of sweet milk
- three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- a small piece of butter
- vanilla
- fresh fruit (apricots or peaches or strawberry preserves)
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of grated bread crumbs in one pint of sweet milk
2. Break three eggs and separate the whites
3. Add to the yolks one cup of sugar and a small piece of butter
4. Beat it well
5. Squeeze the bread crumbs out of the milk, and add this to the yolks and flavor with vanilla
6. Grease the pans with butter, put the mixture in the pan, and pour the milk over it
7. Set in the oven to bake until nearly dry
8. Add a layer of fresh fruit (apricots or peaches are the best or strawberry preserves)
9. Add the whites of eggs that were beaten stiff
10. Serve cold with cream or milk. This can also be served hot

title: Queen Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of water
- one-fourth cup of fresh butter
- one cup of sifted flour
- three eggs
- salt
- butter or Crisco
- preserves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a deep skillet on the fire one cup of water and one-fourth cup of fresh butter
2. When it comes to a boil, stir in one cup of sifted flour
3. Continue stirring until the dough leaves the side of the skillet clean
4. Remove from the fire and when cool break in three eggs, one at a time, stirring continually
5. Add a little salt
6. Mix all well
7. Drop pieces about the size of a walnut into plenty of boiling butter or Crisco
8. Fry a light brown
9. Drain
10. Make an opening in each and fill with preserves
11. Sprinkle with sugar and serve at once

title: Queen of Trifles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one quart of milk
- one teaspoon of cornstarch
- one-half pound of macaroons
- sherry wine
- one-quarter pound of almonds
- one-quarter pound of dried figs
- one-quarter pound of crystallized cherries
- one-half pound of lady fingers
- whipped cream
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a rich custard of four eggs, one cup of granulated sugar and one quart of milk to which has been added one teaspoon of cornstarch.
2. Let this cook in double boiler, stirring constantly, until the custard is very thick.
3. Cool.
4. Soak one-half pound of macaroons in sherry wine, blanch and chop one-quarter pound of almonds, cut fine one-quarter pound of dried figs.
5. Line a deep glass bowl with the lady fingers cut in half, add macaroons, fruit and almonds in layers until all are used.
6. Pour the boiled custard over all.
7. Set on ice and when cold, fill the bowl with whipped cream that has been sweetened and flavored with vanilla.
8. Decorate with a few cherries.

title: Quick Bearnaise Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- four tablespoons oil
- four tablespoons water
- one cup boiling water
- minced onion
- capers
- olives
- pickles
- parsley
- tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of four eggs with four tablespoons of oil and four of water.
2. Add a cup of boiling water and cook slowly until thick and smooth.
3. Take from the fire, and add minced onion, capers, olives, pickles, and parsley and a little tarragon vinegar.

title: Quick Coffee Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1.5 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking-powder
- 0.5 cup milk
- chopped almonds
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cream one-half cup of butter with one cup of sugar
2. Add three eggs, one and one-half cups of flour, two teaspoons of baking-powder, mixed with the flour, and one-half cup of milk
3. Mix well together
4. Bake in a long bread or cake pan, and have on top chopped almonds, sugar and cinnamon

title: Quick Lemonade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two lemons
- four tablespoons of sugar
- one quart of water
- cracked ice
- a little fresh fruit or slices of lemon if convenient

## Directions

1. Wash two lemons and squeeze the juice.
2. Mix thoroughly with four tablespoons of sugar, and when the sugar is dissolved add one quart of water, cracked ice, and a little fresh fruit or slices of lemon if convenient.
3. If the cracked ice is very finely chopped and put in the glasses just before serving it will make a better-looking lemonade.

title: Quince Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar for every pound of fruit
- water
- juice of an orange for every two pounds of fruit

## Directions

1. Wipe off each quince before paring, core and slice them
2. Weigh your fruit and sugar, allowing three-quarters of a pound of sugar for every pound of fruit and set the sugar aside until wanted
3. Boil the skins, cores and seeds in a clean vessel by themselves, with just enough water to cover them
4. Boil until the parings are soft, so as to extract all the flavor, then strain through a jelly-bag
5. When this water is almost cold, put the quinces in the preserving kettle with the quince water and boil until soft
6. Mash with a wooden spoon or beetle
7. Add the juice of an orange to every two pounds of fruit, being careful not to get any of the seeds into the preserves
8. Add the sugar and boil slowly for fifteen minutes, stirring constantly
9. If not thick enough boil longer, being very careful not to let it burn
10. Take off the fire and pack in small jars with brandied paper over them

title: Quince Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the fruit and cook peels and cores as directed for preserving.
2. Cut the quinces in small pieces and let them boil in the strained water for one hour with kettle uncovered.
3. When cooked the desired length of time, pour the whole into a jelly-bag of white flannel or double cheese-cloth; hang over a big bowl or jar and let the liquor all drain through.
4. This will take several hours.
5. When all the liquor is drained, measure it and return to the kettle.
6. To each pint of liquor weigh a pound of sugar.
7. While the liquor is heating put the sugar in the oven, then add to the boiling hot liquor and stir it until sugar is melted.
8. When the whole is thick, and drops from the spoon like jelly, pour it through a strainer into the jelly glasses; and when the jelly is cool, put on the covers — first pouring a film of melted paraffin over the surface.

title: Quince Marmalade
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sharp apples
- two pounds of quinces
- one pound of white sugar powdered
- half a pint of water
- a little bruised cochineal

## Directions

1. Peel, cut into quarters, and core two pounds of sharp apples, and the same quantity of quinces
2. Put them into a jar
3. Sprinkle one pound of white sugar powdered over them
4. Cover them with half a pint of water
5. Put in also a little bruised cochineal tied in a muslin
6. Set them in a slack oven till tender
7. Take out the cochineal
8. Pulp the fruit to a marmalade

title: Quince Marmalade
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fine quinces
- sugar
- brandy
- white of eggs

## Directions

1. Boil and pulp some fine quinces
2. Pulp them through a sieve
3. Take equal weights of sugar and quince and put them in a preserving pan
4. Let it simmer gently, keeping stirring all the time to prevent it burning
5. When thoroughly done it looks quite glossy
6. Put it into saucers
7. When cold put over the top of the saucers fine tissue papers, dip them in brandy
8. Fasten the marmalade down with paper washed over with the white of eggs
9. When thoroughly cold it will cut out in pieces
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Quinces
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of pared, cored and quartered quinces
- one and one-half quarts of sugar
- two quarts of water

## Directions

1. Rub the fruit hard with a coarse, crash towel, blanch for six minutes.
2. Pare, quarter, and core; drop the pieces into cold water.
3. Put the fruit in the preserving kettle with cold water to cover it generously.
4. Heat slowly and simmer gently until tender, taking each piece up as soon as it is tender enough that a silver fork will pierce it readily.
5. Drain on a platter.
6. Strain the water in which the fruit was cooked through cheese-cloth.
7. Put two quarts of the strained liquid and the sugar into the preserving kettle; stir over the fire until the sugar is dissolved.
8. When it boils skim well and put in the cooked fruit.
9. Boil gently for about forty minutes.

title: Radish Preserves (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- black radishes
- one pound of honey
- one and one-half pounds of sugar
- water
- one-quarter pound of white ginger
- walnuts

## Directions

1. Take black radishes, clean and cut them in strips
2. Weigh, and to three pounds of radishes take one pound of honey and one and one-half pounds of sugar
3. Set the radishes on to boil with water
4. Pour off this water
5. Add fresh water and let cook awhile
6. Pour off the second water
7. Add the honey to radishes and let cook well
8. Then add the sugar and let cook again
9. When the radishes begin to get brown add one-quarter pound of white ginger, and some walnuts broken into quarters
10. Stir
11. When brown, remove from stove
12. Must come out of the pan dry; no syrup must remain

title: Ragout Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- fat of smoked beef
- 1/2 pint beef gravy
- allspice
- cloves
- pepper
- salt
- garlic
- glass of port-wine
- lemon
- cayenne
- mushroom ketchup
- forced-meat balls

## Directions

1. Take a nice piece of beef, well cut, lard it with the fat of smoked beef
2. Place it in a stewpan with 1/2 pint of beef gravy highly seasoned with allspice, cloves, pepper, salt, and garlic
3. When the beef is cooked remove it from the gravy
4. Skim the gravy carefully and remove from it every particle of fat
5. Add to it a glass of port-wine, the juice of a lemon, a pinch of cayenne, and a little mushroom ketchup
6. Glaze the beef if you wish to cook extra nice
7. A few small forced-meat balls may be put round the dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rahm Strudel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as for Apple Strudel No. 2
- one quart of thick sour milk
- one cup of grated bread crumbs
- two cups of granulated sugar
- one cup of chopped almonds
- one cup of raisins
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- butter
- vanilla sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for Apple Strudel as directed in the foregoing recipe
2. Drip one quart of thick sour milk on it lightly, with a large spoon
3. Put one cup of grated bread crumbs over the milk
4. Add two cups of granulated sugar, one cup of chopped almonds, one cup of raisins, and one teaspoon of cinnamon
5. Roll and place in well-buttered pan
6. Put small pieces of butter over the top
7. Baste frequently
8. Serve warm with vanilla sauce
9. One-half this quantity may be used for a small strudel

title: Raisin Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as directed for Butter Barches
- one-quarter cup of raisins
- egg diluted with water

## Directions

1. Make dough as directed for Butter Barches, using one-quarter cup of raisins and omitting the lemon and egg
2. Form in loaves, fill well-greased pans half full
3. Cover and let rise until light; about one hour
4. Glaze with egg diluted with water
5. Bake forty-five minutes

title: Raisin Compote
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six oranges (California)
- three pints of cold water
- three pounds of seeded raisins
- three quarts of currants (picked and washed)
- three pounds of granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Peel six oranges, cut the skin in very small narrow strips, or run through a food chopper
2. Slice the oranges very thin and quarter the slices
3. Let it stand overnight in three pints of cold water
4. Place this in a preserving kettle with three pounds of seeded raisins, three quarts of currants and three pounds of granulated sugar
5. Boil all together for two hours and put in glass jars, closing them while hot

title: Raisin or Currant Buns
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two large potatoes
- two-thirds cake of yeast
- cup of sugar
- large lump of butter
- teaspoon of salt
- pint of flour
- currants or raisins

## Directions

1. Boil two large potatoes and strain the water into a pitcher
2. dissolve two-thirds cake of yeast in a cup
3. Put potatoes in a pan with a cup of sugar, large lump of butter, and teaspoon of salt
4. The heat of potatoes will melt the sugar and butter
5. Mash with large masher to a cream
6. pour in rest of potato water
7. add pint of flour and mix together
8. cover and set in a warm place all night
9. In the morning add more flour, mix quickly and put currants or raisins in as you turn the dough
10. Put in hot pans and bake in a hot oven

title: Raisin Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- a tablespoon of flour
- hot soup
- a handful of raisins
- some pounded almonds
- a few slices of lemon
- a tablespoon of vinegar
- brown sugar
- a few cloves
- cinnamon
- horseradish
- rye loaf crust

## Directions

1. Brown some fat in a spider
2. Stir in a tablespoon of flour
3. Stir until it becomes a smooth paste
4. Add hot soup, stirring constantly
5. Add a handful of raisins, some pounded almonds, a few slices of lemon, also a tablespoon of vinegar
6. Add brown sugar to taste
7. Flavor with a few cloves and cinnamon, and if you choose to do so, grate in part of a stick of horseradish and the crust of a rye loaf

title: Raisin Stuffing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of stale bread crumbs
- one-half a cup of melted chicken fat
- one cup of seeded raisins cut in small pieces
- one teaspoon of salt
- one-fourth teaspoon of white pepper

## Directions

1. Take three cups of stale bread crumbs; add one-half a cup of melted chicken fat, one cup of seeded raisins cut in small pieces, one teaspoon of salt and one-fourth teaspoon of white pepper.
2. Mix thoroughly.

title: Raisin Stuffing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread crumbs
- chicken fat
- seeded raisins
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Take three cups of stale bread crumbs
2. Add one-half a cup of melted chicken fat
3. Add one cup of seeded raisins cut in small pieces, one teaspoon of salt and one-fourth teaspoon of white pepper
4. Mix thoroughly

title: Raisin Wine, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of raisins
- three quarts of cold water
- a few tablespoons of sugar
- a handful of stick cinnamon

## Directions

1. To two pounds of raisins (cut in half if desired), add three quarts of cold water.
2. Either place the mixture on a corner of the range and let it simmer for two or three days or boil it until one-third of the water has evaporated.
3. A few tablespoons of sugar and a handful of stick cinnamon can be added if additional sweetness and flavoring are wished.
4. When cold strain through a fine cloth.

title: Raisin Wine, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of raisins, seeded and chopped
- one pound of white loaf sugar
- one lemon
- six quarts of boiling water

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of raisins, seeded and chopped, one pound of white loaf sugar, and one lemon.
2. Put all into a stone jar, pour six quarts of boiling water over all and stir every day for a week.
3. Then strain and bottle.
4. Ready for use in ten or twelve days.

title: Ramakins
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one ounce of warmed butter
- two ounces of fine cheese grated

## Directions

1. Mix together three eggs, one ounce of warmed butter, and two of fine cheese grated, and bake in small patty pans

title: Ramekins of Egg and Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three new-laid eggs
- two ounces of grated cheese
- one ounce of partly melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat three new-laid eggs and blend thoroughly with two ounces of grated cheese and one ounce of partly melted butter
2. Place the mixture in little pans or saucers and bake in the oven

title: Raspberries and Currants
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- currants
- pulverized sugar
- white or black currants or blackberries
- cream or wine

## Directions

1. Mix berries together
2. Set on ice until ready to serve
3. Pile in a mound, strewing plenty of pulverized sugar among them
4. Garnish the base with white or black currants (blackberries look pretty also) in bunches
5. Eat with cream or wine

title: Raspberries and Currants
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ten quarts of raspberries
- three quarts of currants
- two and one-half quarts of sugar

## Directions

1. Heat, crush and press the juice from the currants and proceed as directed for raspberries.

title: Raspberry and Currant Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half raspberries
- half currants
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for Currant Jelly, using half raspberries and half currants.

title: Raspberry Cocktail
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of ripe, red currants
- one pint of red raspberries
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mash a pint of ripe, red currants
2. strain them through cheesecloth
3. pour the juice over a pint of red raspberries and set on the ice to chill
4. At serving time sweeten to taste and pour into the glasses, putting one teaspoon of powdered sugar on the top of each

title: Raspberry Ice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- strong lemonade
- raspberry juice
- grated pineapple

## Directions

1. Make a strong lemonade
2. Add raspberry juice to taste, and some grated pineapple
