3. Put into freezer and turn like ice cream and pack
4. Let stand five hours

title: Raspberry Jam
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of red raspberries (not too ripe)
- five pounds of loaf sugar
- one quart of currant juice

## Directions

1. Mash the whole well in a preserving kettle using a potato masher
2. Add one quart of currant juice, and boil slowly until it jellies
3. Try a little on a plate; set it on ice
4. If it jellies remove from the fire, fill in small jars, cover with brandied paper and tie a thick white paper over them
5. Keep in a dark, dry, cool place
6. If you object to seeds, press the fruit through a sieve before boiling

title: Raspberry Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for Currant Jelly.

title: Raspberry Roll, Swiss Pastry
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup of castor sugar
- 1 cup of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- almond oil
- raspberry or strawberry jam or orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs thoroughly
2. Add a cup of castor sugar and 1 cup of flour
3. Stir them well together
4. Add 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar and 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda dissolved in a very little warm water
5. Grease the tin with almond oil
6. Pour the mixture in
7. Bake quickly
8. Place a sheet of writing paper over the top to prevent its scorching
9. It must be quite yellow when done
10. Slip it out of the tin on a clean cloth, the bottom side up
11. Spread over it raspberry or strawberry jam or orange marmalade
12. Gently roll it up and cut it as wanted
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Raspberry Vinegar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of raspberries
- two quarts of vinegar
- two quarts of fresh raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put two quarts of raspberries in a bowl and cover them with two quarts of vinegar
2. cover and stand in a cool place for two days
3. Mash the berries
4. strain the vinegar through cheese-cloth
5. pour it over two quarts of fresh raspberries
6. let stand for another two days
7. strain and put in a preserving kettle with sugar, allowing a pound of sugar to a pint of juice
8. Heat slowly, skimming when the vinegar begins to boil
9. Boil twenty minutes and put in sterilized bottles
10. Serve as a drink, using two tablespoons to a glass of water

title: Ratafia Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- the crumb of a French roll
- half a pound of ratafia cakes
- milk or cream
- three ounces of warmed fresh butter
- yolks of five and the whites of two eggs
- sugar
- one ounce of pounded almonds
- a few bitter almonds
- dried cherries
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Soak the crumb of a French roll and half a pound of ratafia cakes in milk or cream, then mix with them three ounces of warmed fresh butter, the yolks of five and the whites of two eggs, sweeten to taste
2. Add one ounce of pounded almonds, and a few bitter almonds
3. Boil in a shape lined with dried cherries, or bake in a cake-tin first well buttered, and sprinkled with bread crumbs

title: Ratafia Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of ratafias
- 6 sponge cakes
- 3 oz. of citron
- grated rind of a lemon
- 1/4 lb. of castor sugar
- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- 6 eggs, leave out 3 whites
- orange marmalade
- cut almonds

## Directions

1. Soak the cakes in 1/2 pint of almond milk
2. Beat in 6 eggs, leave out 3 whites
3. Grease and sugar your mould
4. Lay over it some orange marmalade
5. Turn in the mixture
6. Boil for an hour the same as the other puddings
7. Serve a sauce round
8. You can sometimes ornament these puddings with cut almonds
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Ratafia Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of ratafias
- 6 sponge cakes
- 3 oz. of citron
- grated rind of a lemon
- 1 lb. of castor sugar
- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- 6 eggs
- orange marmalade
- cut almonds

## Directions

1. Soak the cakes in 1/2 pint of almond milk
2. Beat in 6 eggs, leaving out 3 whites
3. Grease and sugar your mould
4. Lay over it some orange marmalade
5. Turn in the mixture
6. Boil for an hour the same as the other puddings
7. Serve a sauce round
8. You can sometimes ornament these puddings with cut almonds
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Reception Cocoa
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup boiling water
- 2 tablespoons cocoa
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- salt
- 3 cups milk
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- whipped cream or marshmallows

## Directions

1. Stir one cup of boiling water gradually onto two tablespoons of cocoa, two tablespoons of sugar and one teaspoon of cornstarch, a few grains of salt (that have been well mixed) in a saucepan
2. Let boil five minutes, stirring constantly
3. Heat three cups of milk in a double boiler
4. Add the cocoa mixture and one-half teaspoon of vanilla
5. Beat with egg-beater until foamy
6. Serve hot in chocolate cups, with a tablespoon of whipped cream on top of each cup, or place two marshmallows in each cup and fill cups two-thirds full of hot cocoa

title: Red Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- salt
- sour apple
- one tablespoon goose or soup drippings
- onion
- cloves
- vinegar
- brown sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut fine on slaw-cutter, put cabbage in a colander, pour boiling water over it and let it stand over another pan for ten minutes.
2. Salt, mix well, and cut up a sour apple in the cabbage.
3. Heat one tablespoon goose or soup drippings, brown in this an onion cut fine.
4. Add the cabbage and stew slowly, keep covered.
5. Add a little hot water after it has boiled about five minutes.
6. When tender add a few cloves, vinegar, brown sugar and cinnamon to taste, and serve.

title: Red Cabbage With Chestnuts and Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- one tablespoon of butter
- one cup of water
- one-fourth cup of vinegar
- one tablespoon of sugar
- salt
- pepper
- one-fourth cup of raisins
- blanched chestnuts
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Clean cabbage and cut off outside leaves, cut on cabbage-cutter — blanch.
2. Take one tablespoon of butter, put in kettle and let brown.
3. Add cabbage, let simmer about ten minutes, stir and let simmer ten minutes more.
4. Add about one cup of water, one-fourth cup of vinegar, and one tablespoon of sugar, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Add one-fourth cup of raisins and blanched chestnuts and cook until tender, adding to cabbage just before serving.
6. Take one tablespoon of flour smooth with cold water, add to cabbage, let cook a few minutes and serve.

title: Red Currant Ice Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- red currants
- juice of a few raspberries
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Make the same as strawberry ice cream, only adding the juice of a few raspberries
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Red Currant Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- quite ripe currants
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Strip carefully from the stems some quite ripe currants.
2. Put them into a preserving pan.
3. Stir them gently over a clear fire until the juice flows freely from them.
4. Squeeze the currants and strain the juice through a folded muslin or jelly bag.
5. Pour it into a preserving pan, adding, as it boils, white sugar, in the proportion of one pound of sugar to one pint of juice.
6. Jellies should be continually skimmed till the scum ceases to rise, so that they may be clear and fine.
7. Jams and jellies should be poured into pots when in a boiling state.

title: Red Mullet In Cases
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four mullets
- one dozen button mushrooms
- one tablespoon of finely chopped parsley
- two shalots
- juice of a lemon
- salt
- pepper
- oil

## Directions

1. Oil some pieces of foolscap paper
2. Lay the fish on them and sprinkle over them the mushroom, parsley, shalot, lemon juice, pepper and salt
3. Fold them in the cases and cook on a well-greased baking-sheet in a moderate oven for about twenty or thirty minutes
4. Send to the table in cases very hot

title: Red Pepper Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two chopped hard-boiled eggs
- one tablespoon of chopped red peppers canned
- a saltspoon of salt
- a tiny pinch of mustard
- two tablespoons of grated American cheese
- melted butter
- rounds of fried bread
- watercress

## Directions

1. Mix together two chopped hard-boiled eggs, one tablespoon of chopped red peppers, a saltspoon of salt, a tiny pinch of mustard and two tablespoons of grated American cheese with sufficient melted butter to form a paste
2. spread over the rounds of fried bread and place in a very hot oven for about three minutes
3. Serve on a folded napkin, garnished with watercress

title: Red Raspberry or Currant Float
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pint glass of red raspberry or currant juice
- one quarter cup of sugar
- four eggs
- one quarter cup of powdered sugar
- cold whipped cream

## Directions

1. Take a half-pint glass of red raspberry or currant juice and mix it with a quarter cup of sugar.
2. Beat the whites of four eggs to a stiff froth and add gradually a quarter cup of powdered sugar.
3. Press the raspberries through a strainer to avoid seeds and by degrees beat the juice with the sugar and eggs until so stiff that it stands in peaks.
4. Chill it thoroughly and serve in a glass dish half filled with cold whipped cream.
5. Heap on the mixture by the spoonful, like floating island.

title: Red Wine Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of good red wine
- one-half cup of water
- sugar
- three whole cloves
- three small pieces of cinnamon bark
- yolk of one egg

## Directions

1. Put on to boil one cup of good red wine and one-half cup of water
2. sweeten to taste
3. add three whole cloves and three small pieces of cinnamon bark
4. let boil ten minutes
5. and pour while boiling over the well-beaten yolk of one egg
6. Eat hot or cold

title: Redsnapper with Tomato Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- redsnapper
- salt
- pepper
- celery
- parsley root
- onions
- carrot
- water
- fresh butter
- tomatoes
- eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scale thoroughly, salt and pepper inside and out, and lay upon ice, wrapped in a clean cloth overnight
2. When ready to cook cut up the celery or parsley root, or both, two large onions, a carrot or two, and let this come to a boil in about one quart of water
3. Lay in the fish, whole or in pieces, let the water almost cover the fish
4. Add a lump of fresh butter and three or four tomatoes (out of season you may use canned tomatoes, say three or four large spoonfuls)
5. Let the fish boil half an hour, turning it occasionally
6. Try it by taking hold of the fins, if they come out readily, the fish is done
7. Take it up carefully, lay on a large platter and strain the sauce
8. Let it boil, thicken it with the well-beaten yolks of two eggs, adding the sauce gradually to the eggs and stirring constantly
9. Garnish the fish with chopped parsley, letting a quantity mix with the sauce

title: Restorative Milk
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of an ounce of isinglass
- a pint of new milk
- sugar candy

## Directions

1. Boil a quarter of an ounce of isinglass in a pint of new milk till reduced to half
2. Sweeten with sugar candy

title: Rhubarb And Orange Marmalade
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of pie plant
- three oranges
- two cups of sugar
- grated rind of one orange
- three pounds of granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Cut three pounds of pie plant into small pieces (unpeeled).
2. Peel three oranges and cut into small pieces.
3. Put with this two cups of sugar and the grated rind of one orange.
4. Let stand overnight.
5. Cook until clear, stirring often.
6. Then add three pounds of granulated sugar heated in oven.
7. Cook until clear; ten to twenty minutes.
8. Pour into jelly glasses and cover with paraffin.

title: Rhubarb Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- sugar
- flour
- rhubarb
- strawberries or raspberries
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a very rich crust, and over the bottom layer sprinkle a large tablespoon of sugar and a good teaspoon of flour.
2. Fill half-full of rhubarb that has been cut up.
3. Scatter in one-fourth cup of strawberries or raspberries.
4. Sprinkle with more sugar and flour.
5. Over the top dot bits of butter and another dusting of flour.
6. Use a good cup of sugar to a pie.
7. Pinch the crusts together well after wetting them, to prevent the juice from cooking out.

title: Rhubarb Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale rye bread
- bunch of rhubarb
- baking-soda
- boiling water
- butter
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- nuts

## Directions

1. Grate some stale rye bread and take a bunch of rhubarb.
2. Cut rhubarb fine without peeling, put the cut rhubarb in a pan with a big pinch of baking-soda, and pour boiling water over to cover.
3. While that is steeping, grate the rye bread and butter pudding-form well, and put crumbs all over the pan about one-quarter inch deep.
4. Add one-half the rhubarb that has been well drained of the water.
5. Season with brown sugar, cinnamon, nuts and any other seasoning you like.
6. Then some more crumbs, and other one-half of rhubarb, and season as before.
7. Top with crumbs, put flakes of butter all over top.
8. Bake until done.

title: Rhubarb Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Strip the skin off the stalks with care
2. Cut them into small pieces
3. Put into a saucepan with very little water
4. Stew slowly until soft
5. Sweeten while hot, but do not boil the sugar with the fruit
6. Eat cold

title: Ribbon Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two slices of white bread
- two slices of brown bread
- butter
- hard-boiled eggs
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Cut two slices of white bread and two of brown.
2. Butter three and spread with a thick paste made of hard-boiled egg very finely chopped and mixed with mayonnaise dressing.
3. Build the slices up one above the other, alternating brown and white, and placing the unbuttered slice on top.
4. Before serving, slice down as you would a layer cake.

title: Rice and Cheese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter
- one finely minced onion
- one-quarter pound of well-boiled rice
- grated cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Melt two ounces of butter in a stew-pan
2. Fry in the butter a finely minced onion
3. When this is of a nice golden color stir into it a quarter of a pound of well-boiled rice
4. Work it well with a fork and then pour all into a buttered pie dish
5. Dredge over with a good coating of grated cheese
6. Sprinkle the surface with melted butter and bake until nicely browned

title: Rice And Nut Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rice (brown preferred)
- an equal quantity of bread crumbs
- one level teaspoon of salt
- one-half saltspoon of black pepper
- one cup of chopped nuts (pecans or peanuts)
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- one egg

## Directions

1. Boil one-half cup of rice (brown preferred)
2. Drain and dry it
3. Mix with an equal quantity of bread crumbs
4. Add level teaspoon of salt and one-half saltspoon of black pepper
5. Stir in one cup of chopped nuts — pecans or peanuts
6. Add one tablespoon of chopped parsley and one egg
7. Mix thoroughly and pack in bread-pan to mold it
8. Turn it from pan into baking-pan and bake slowly three-quarters of an hour
9. Serve with cream sauce or puree of peas

title: Rice Broth
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or mutton broth
- one-half cup of rice
- potatoes
- the beaten yolk of an egg
- chicken broth

## Directions

1. Boil one-half cup of rice separately in a farina kettle
2. Strain the beef or mutton broth
3. Add the rice and boil one-half hour longer, with potatoes, cut into dice shape; use about two potatoes
4. Add the beaten yolk of an egg
5. Strained stock of chicken broth added to this soup makes it very palatable and nutritious for the sick

title: Rice Cake with Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. butter
- 1 lb. castor sugar
- 12 eggs
- 1/2 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. ground rice

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in your pan, only warm, not to oil.
2. Add the sugar, stir it well till it is like a cream.
3. Break the eggs three at the time, and stir the paste all the time.
4. When all the eggs are worked in, add the ground rice and flour.
5. Bake it in a round tin buttered.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rice Cake Without Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 12 oz. of castor sugar
- rind of 2 lemons
- 6 oz. of flour
- 6 oz. of ground rice
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Separate the yolks from the whites
2. Beat the whites to a strong snow
3. Mix the yolks and powdered sugar and the lemon rasping in a basin
4. Stand in a flat saucepan with boiling water over a slow fire till it comes thick and light; it will take 1/2 an hour
5. Take it off and stir it till cold
6. Stir in the flour and ground rice
7. Grease some paper with the almond oil to line the tin
8. Bake in rather a slow oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rice Caudle
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of milk
- a spoonful of ground rice
- a little milk
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil half a pint of milk
2. Add a spoonful of ground rice mixed with a little milk till quite smooth
3. Stir it into the boiling milk
4. Let it simmer till it thickens
5. Carefully strain it and sweeten with white sugar

title: Rice Croquettes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- two cups of boiled or steamed rice
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of cold water
- fine bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Separate the white and yolk of one egg and reserve about half the yolk for coating the croquette
2. Beat the rest with the white
3. Mix with two cups of boiled or steamed rice and one-half teaspoon of salt
4. Form into oblong croquettes or small balls
5. Mix the reserved part of the egg yolk with a tablespoon of cold water
6. Dip croquettes in this and then roll in fine bread crumbs
7. Repeat until well-coated
8. Fry brown in deep oil

title: Rice Croquettes, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- cold water
- one tablespoon of butter
- yolks of three eggs
- pinch of salt
- one tablespoon of flour
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Put on with cold water one cup of rice, and let boil until tender
2. Drain
3. Mix with the rice, one tablespoon of butter, yolks of three eggs, and pinch of salt
4. About one tablespoon of flour may be added to hold the croquettes together
5. Beat the whites of the three eggs to a stiff froth, reserving some of the beaten white for egging croquettes
6. Mix this in last
7. Shape into croquettes
8. Fry in hot oil or butter substitute
9. Place on platter and serve with a lump of jelly on each croquette

title: Rice Custard
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of whole rice
- three pints of milk
- half a pound of pounded sweet almonds
- sugar to taste
- a stick of cinnamon
- a piece of lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take two ounces of whole rice and boil it in three pints of milk until it thickens
2. Add half a pound of pounded sweet almonds and sweeten to taste
3. A stick of cinnamon and a piece of lemon peel should be boiled in it, and then taken out

title: Rice Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of cooked rice
- two cups of sweet milk
- one lemon
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- lemon sauce

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs light with one cup of sugar.
2. Add one cup of cooked rice, two cups of sweet milk, juice and rind of one lemon, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon.
3. Pour in pudding-pan and place in a pan filled with hot water.
4. Bake until firm in moderate oven.
5. Serve with lemon sauce.

title: Rice Fritters
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- a small quantity of water
- six eggs
- three spoonfuls of flour
- a little grated lemon peel
- butter or oil
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of rice in a small quantity of water to a jelly
2. Let it cool and beat it up with six eggs, three spoonfuls of flour, a little grated lemon peel
3. Fry like fritters, either in butter or oil
4. Serve with white sugar sifted over them

title: Rice Fruit Tarts
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole rice
- milk
- white sugar
- currants, raspberries, cherries, or any other fruit
- the whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Boil in milk some whole rice till perfectly soft and sweeten with white sugar
2. When nearly cold, line a dish with it
3. Have ready some currants, raspberries, cherries, or any other fruit, which must have been previously stewed and sweetened, and fill the dish with it
4. Beat up the whites of three eggs to a froth, mixed with a little white sugar
5. Lay over the top and place it in the oven for half an hour

title: Rice in Milk
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rice
- one and one-half cups of hot milk
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- seeded or sultana raisins

## Directions

1. Clean the rice as for boiling in water
2. Cook one-half cup of rice with one and one-half cups of hot milk and one-half teaspoon of salt, adding a few seeded or sultana raisins

title: Rice In Milk
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rice
- one and one-half cups of hot milk
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- a few seeded or sultana raisins (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the rice as for boiling in water
2. Cook one-half cup of rice with one and one-half cups of hot milk and one-half teaspoon of salt, adding a few seeded or sultana raisins if desired
3. Serve hot like boiled rice or press into small cups, cool and serve with cream and sugar

title: Rice Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- water
- one-fourth cup of chicken-fat
- scant cup of powdered sugar
- little cinnamon
- grated peel of one lemon
- yolks of three eggs
- one-half cup of raisins seeded
- one-half pound of stewed prunes pitted
- one-half cup of pounded almonds
- bitter almonds
- pudding sauce, either wine or brandy

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of rice in water until done, then let it cool
2. In the meanwhile rub one-fourth cup of chicken-fat to a cream
3. Add a scant cup of powdered sugar, a little cinnamon, the grated peel of one lemon, the yolks of three eggs adding one at a time
4. Add one-half cup of raisins seeded, one-half pound of stewed prunes pitted, then add the cold rice
5. One-half cup of pounded almonds mixed with a few bitter ones improves this pudding
6. Serve with a pudding sauce, either wine or brandy
7. This pudding may be eaten hot or cold and may be either baked or boiled
8. If baked, one hour is required; if boiled, two hours, the water must be kept boiling steadily
9. Left-over rice may be used, butter instead of the fat, and the rice may be boiled in milk

title: Rice Milk
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 2 oz. loaf sugar
- 1 teacupful of the best rice
- lemon rind or orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well
2. Let it soak for a quarter of an hour
3. Rinse out the saucepan
4. Put in 3/4 a teacupful of cold water
5. Put the milk and the rice well drained from the water
6. When you wish to flavour it with lemon, cut some of the rind very thin and boil it with the rice and milk
7. With orange-flower water, put that in when it is done
8. Turn it out in a china or glass dish
9. Put over some powdered cinnamon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rice Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cold rice
- two eggs
- one cup of sweet milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- flour
- one tablespoon of melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat one cup of cold rice, two eggs, one cup of sweet milk, one teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of sugar, two teaspoons of baking-powder
2. Add enough flour to make a stiff batter
3. Add lastly one tablespoon of melted butter
4. Bake in muffin-tins

title: Rice Pancakes Or Griddle Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint milk
- three tablespoons rice
- two tablespoons granulated sugar
- one tablespoon vanilla
- yolk of one egg
- one tablespoon flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil in a double boiler one pint of milk, three tablespoons of rice and two tablespoons of granulated sugar
2. Cook for fifty to sixty minutes until rice is thoroughly cooked and mixture thickens
3. Remove from fire and when a little cool, add one tablespoon of vanilla and the yolk of egg into which one tablespoon of flour has been smoothly stirred
4. Mix all thoroughly together
5. Pour by spoonfuls on hot buttered griddle
6. Let the cakes brown on one side, turn over and brown on the other

title: Rice Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of milk
- half a cup of rice
- yolks of three eggs
- three tablespoons of white sugar
- salt
- vanilla
- whites of the eggs
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. To three cups of milk, add half a cup of rice, which you have previously scalded with hot water
2. Boil in a double boiler until quite soft
3. Add salt to the rice while boiling
4. Beat the yolks of three eggs with three tablespoons of white sugar, add this just before taking it off the fire
5. Stir it thoroughly with a wooden spoon, but do not let it boil any more
6. Flavor with vanilla
7. Beat the whites of the eggs with powdered sugar to a stiff froth
8. Put the custard into the pudding dish in which you wish to serve it
9. Spread with the beaten whites and let it brown slightly in the oven

title: Rice Souffles
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine picked rice
- pure fresh milk
- bay leaf
- lemon peel
- stick of cinnamon
- apples
- whites of eggs
- lemon or noyau essence

## Directions

1. Boil well some fine picked rice in pure fresh milk
2. Sweeten and flavour with a bay leaf, lemon peel, and a stick of cinnamon
3. Remove the bay leaf, lemon peel, and cinnamon stick when the rice is done
4. Line a round dish or souffle dish with the rice
5. Have ready apples previously boiled, sweetened, and beat up smoothly
6. Place the apple lightly in the centre rather higher in the middle than at the sides
7. Beat up the whites of eggs to a froth
8. Sweeten and flavour with lemon or noyau essence
9. Place it in small heaps tastefully on the apple and rice
10. Brown delicately with a salamander

title: Rice Strudel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of rice
- milk
- two ounces of butter
- the yolks of four eggs
- four ounces of sugar
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- salt
- the beaten whites of two eggs
- pounded almonds (optional)
- raisins (optional)
- sweet cream or butter for basting

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough same as for Apple Strudel
2. Leave it in a warm place covered, until you have prepared the rice
3. Wash a quarter of a pound of rice in hot water — about three times
4. Boil it in milk until very soft and thick
5. Let it cool, and then add two ounces of butter, the yolks of four eggs, four ounces of sugar and one teaspoon of vanilla, some salt and the beaten whites of two eggs, mix thoroughly
6. When your dough has been rolled out and pulled as thin as possible, spread the rice over it and roll
7. Add pounded almonds and raisins if desired
8. Put in a greased pan and bake until brown, basting with sweet cream or butter

title: Rice With Grated Chocolate
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rice
- grated sweet chocolate

## Directions

1. Cook one-half cup of rice
2. Place in hot serving dish
3. Sprinkle generously with grated sweet chocolate
4. Set in oven one minute and serve

title: Rich Currant Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. fresh butter
- 1 lb. castor sugar
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of currants
- 1 lb. mixed citron, lemon and orange peel
- 1/2 lb. ground almonds
- 10 eggs
- 1/2 oz. of allspice
- 1/2 oz. of cinnamon
- a glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Melt the butter to a cream
2. Put in the sugar and mix it well together
3. Add the ground allspice and cinnamon
4. Take the yolks of the eggs, 2 or 3 at the time, and work them in
5. Beat the white to a strong snow
6. Work in gently
7. Cut the sweetmeats in long pieces
8. Add the currants, first well-washed and dried
9. Add the flour and a glass of brandy
10. Bake it in a round deep tin with 3 or 4 rounds of paper at the bottom well buttered; it will take 3 hours to bake
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rich Plum Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- one pound of sifted loaf sugar
- one pound of fine flour
- ten eggs
- half a nutmeg
- one pound of well-washed currants
- half a pound of mixed candied peels
- a glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Beat to a cream one pound of butter
2. Add the same quantity of sifted loaf sugar and of fine flour
3. Add the whites of ten eggs beaten to a froth and the yolks of the same also beaten till quite smooth and thin
4. Add half a nutmeg grated
5. Work in one pound of well-washed currants, half a pound of mixed candied peels cut small, and a glass of brandy
6. Bake for two hours

title: Richmond Maids of Honour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- A teacup of cream
- 6 oz. of fresh butter
- the yolks of 4 eggs
- a glass of brandy
- 6 oz. of castor sugar
- a very floury potato
- 1 oz. of sweet almonds
- 1/2 an oz. of bitter almonds
- 1 oz. of Barcelona nut
- the rind of 2 lemons
- the juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 grated nutmeg
- a rich puff paste

## Directions

1. Boil a very floury potato, and when cold rub it up to a flour quite smooth
2. Pound 1 oz. of sweet almonds, 1/2 an oz. of bitter almonds pounded very fine, pound also with them 1 oz. of Barcelona nut
3. Grate the rind of 2 lemons, the juice of 1, 1/2 grated nutmeg
4. Break the yolks of 4 eggs into a basin
5. Beat them with a glass of brandy
6. Add to this 6 oz. of castor sugar, beat this well together
7. Mix all well together, add the cream and butter, stir well together
8. Have a rich puff paste, line the little patty pans, trim the edges neatly
9. Put in some of the mixture
10. Bake quickly in a hot oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Ripe Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nice, large, well-shaped tomatoes
- pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Select nice, large, well-shaped tomatoes
2. Pare and slice
3. Put on ice
4. When ready to serve sprinkle each layer thickly with pulverized sugar

title: Rissoles
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat, poultry, or fish
- a very rich puff paste

## Directions

1. Make a fine forcemeat of any cold meat, poultry, or fish
2. enclose it in a very rich puff paste, rolled out extremely thin
3. They may be made into balls or small triangular turnovers, or into long narrow ribbons
4. the edges must be pressed together, that they may not burst in frying

title: Rissoles
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat, veal or poultry
- cold smoked beef or sausage
- veal fat
- yolk of egg
- clarified dripping or melted veal fat
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make a good forcemeat of cold meat, veal or poultry, well seasoned if of veal or poultry
2. Chop with it a little cold smoked beef or sausage you may have in the house
3. Make a fine puff paste of the veal fat
4. Roll it out very thin
5. You may make them in balls or small triangular shapes, or long narrow ribbons
6. The edges must be well pressed together and brushed over with yolk of egg to prevent their bursting in the frying
7. Fry them in good clarified dripping or melted veal fat
8. Flavour the forced meat with a little lemon juice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Roast Beef (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cross-rib or shoulder
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- water
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Place a piece of cross-rib or shoulder weighing three pounds in roasting-pan
2. Slice some onions over it, season with salt and pepper, add some water and let it cook well
3. Peel a few potatoes and put them under the meat
4. When the meat becomes brown, turn it and cook until it browns on the other side

title: Roast Beef Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- dripping
- mustard
- salt
- roast beef

## Directions

1. Spread on the bread the dripping, mustard, and salt
2. Remember to cut the meat very thin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Roast Beef, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prime rib roast
- small onion
- celery root
- carrot
- parsley
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut up a small onion, a celery root and part of a carrot into rather small pieces and add to these two or three sprigs of parsley and one bay leaf
2. Sprinkle these over the bottom of the dripping-pan and place your roast on this bed
3. Heat the oven very hot when the roast is first put in
4. When the roast is browned sufficiently to retain its juices, moderate the heat and roast more slowly until the meat is done
5. Do not season until the roast is browned, then add salt and pepper
6. Baste frequently and turn occasionally, being very careful not to stick a fork into the roast

title: Roast Beef, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- salt
- paprika
- flour
- fat
- water

## Directions

1. Season meat with salt and paprika
2. Dredge with flour
3. Place on rack in dripping-pan with two or three tablespoons fat, in hot oven, to brown quickly
4. Reduce heat and baste every ten minutes with the fat that has fried out
5. When meat is about half done, turn it over, dredge with flour, finish browning
6. If necessary, add a small quantity of water
7. Allow fifteen to twenty minutes for each pound of meat

title: Roast Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- chicken-fat
- hot water

## Directions

1. Stuff and truss a chicken, season with pepper and salt and dredge with flour.
2. Put in a roasting-pan with two or three tablespoons of chicken-fat if the chicken is not especially fat.
3. When heated add hot water and baste frequently.
4. The oven should be hot and the time necessary for a large chicken will be about an hour and a half.
5. When done, remove the chicken, pour off the grease and make a brown sauce in the pan.

title: Roast Duck
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- giblet sauce
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Draw the duck
2. Stuff, truss and roast the same as chicken
3. Serve with giblet sauce and currant jelly
4. If small, the duck should be cooked in an hour

title: Roast Goose
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt
- ginger
- garlic
- bread dressing, or chestnut dressing, or dressing of apples
- onion
- celery

## Directions

1. All goose meat tastes better if it is well rubbed with salt, ginger and a little garlic a day previous to using
2. Stuff goose with bread dressing, or chestnut dressing, a dressing of apples is also very good
3. Sew up the goose
4. Line a sheet-iron roasting-pan with a few slices of onion and celery and place the goose upon these
5. Cover closely and roast three hours or more, according to weight
6. If the goose browns too quickly, cover with greased paper or lower the heat of the oven
7. Baste every fifteen minutes

title: Roast Goose Breasts
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breast
- skin from neck
- garlic or onion
- salt
- ginger

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the neck and sew it over the breast
2. Fasten it with a few stitches under the breast
3. Make an incision with a pointed knife in the breast and joints of the goose
4. Insert a little garlic or onion in each incision
5. Add a little salt and ginger
6. Keep closely covered all the time, so as not to get too brown
7. Cut up nicely cold for sandwiches

title: Roast Mutton with Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one shoulder of mutton
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- flour
- whole mace
- onion
- one cup of water
- potatoes
- currant or cranberry jelly

## Directions

1. Take a shoulder of mutton—must be young and tender—wash the meat well and dry with a clean towel.
2. Rub well with salt, ginger and a speck of pepper, and dredge well with flour.
3. Lay it in a covered roasting-pan.
4. Put a few pieces of whole mace and a few slices of onion on top.
5. Pour a cup of water into the pan.
6. Cover it up tight and set in a hot oven to roast, basting frequently.
7. Allow twenty minutes to the pound for roasting mutton; it should be well done.
8. Add more water if necessary (always add hot water so as not to stop the process of boiling).
9. Skim the gravy well and serve with currant or cranberry jelly.
10. Pare potatoes of uniform size and wash and salt them about three-quarters of an hour before dinner.
11. Lay the potatoes in pan around the roast and sprinkle them with salt and return to the oven to roast.
12. Let them brown nicely.

title: Roast Neck of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of the best end neck of veal
- stuffing
- a slice of fat smoked beef or a piece of large sausage
- flour
- lemon juice
- gravy
- cayenne pepper
- cresses

## Directions

1. Take three pounds of the best end neck of veal
2. Make a stuffing as for stewed veal, adding a slice of fat smoked beef or a piece of large sausage
3. Season it well and flour it over
4. Put it down to roast with a brisk fire for 1 hour
5. When roasted nice and brown squeeze the juice of a lemon over it and a little gravy and a little cayenne pepper
6. Serve it up very hot and put a few cresses round
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Roast Pigeons
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- melted veal fat
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- greased paper
- a very rich gravy

## Directions

1. Wash them, dry them well after they have been soaked.
2. Take a piece of the melted veal fat, rub it in salt, pepper, and chopped parsley, put it inside to keep it moist.
3. Secure both ends of pigeons so that it does not fall through, roll them in greased paper, put them back to back.
4. You can roast two at once by turning them.
5. Serve them up with a very rich gravy, and very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Roast Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet or Irish potatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Take either sweet or Irish potatoes, or both
2. Pare, wash, and salt them
3. Lay them around the meat
4. Let them roast for about three-quarters of an hour
5. Turn them about once so they will be nicely browned

title: Roast Sweet Potatoes with Meat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- salt
- roast meats or poultry

## Directions

1. Pare, cut lengthwise, salt and put them around roast meats or poultry of any kind.
2. Roast about three-quarters of an hour, or until brown.

title: Roast Turkey
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- bread stuffing
- chestnut stuffing
- salt
- fat
- flour
- boiling water
- stock
- giblets

## Directions

1. Singe and clean the turkey the same as chicken
2. Fill with plain bread stuffing or chestnut stuffing
3. Tie down the legs and rub entire surface with salt and let stand overnight
4. Next morning place in large drippings or roasting-pan on rack
5. Spread breast, legs and wings with one-third cup of fat creamed and mixed with one-fourth cup of flour
6. Dredge bottom of pan with flour
7. Place in a hot oven and when the flour on the turkey begins to brown, reduce the heat
8. Add two cups of boiling water or the stock in which the giblets are cooking
9. Baste with one-fourth cup of fat and three-fourths cup of boiling water
10. When this is all used, baste with the fat in the pan
11. Baste every fifteen minutes until tender; do not prick with a fork, press with the fingers
12. If the breast meat and leg are soft to the touch the turkey is done
13. If the oven is too hot, cover the pan; turn the turkey often, that it may brown nicely
14. Remove strings and skewers and serve on hot platter
15. Serve with giblet sauce and cranberry sauce

title: Roast Veal
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder or breast
- salt
- ground ginger
- flour
- slices of onion
- goose-fat or drippings

## Directions

1. Choose the shoulder and breast of veal, always buy veal that is fat and white
2. Wash and then dry
3. Rub it well with salt, a very little ground ginger, and dredge it well with flour
4. Lay in roasting-pan and put slices of onion on top with a few tablespoons of goose-fat or drippings
5. Cover tightly and roast, allowing twenty minutes to the pound and baste frequently
6. Veal must be well done

title: Rolled Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5-lb. of thin flank, free from bone
- 1 pound of chopped meat
- marjoram
- chopped onion
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- sausage
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Lay the beef on a chopping board and beat it quite flat
2. Have a pound of chopped meat well seasoned with marjoram, chopped onion, parsley, pepper, salt, and grated nutmeg
3. Spread this flat on the beef
4. Put a few slices of sausage cut thin on top of the forced meat
5. Add a couple of hard-boiled eggs cut in slices
6. Commence rolling it carefully
7. Sew it with a coarse needle and thread
8. Roll it in a coarse cloth and tie the ends tight with string
9. Put it in a long stock pot and let it boil for 4 hours gently
10. Take it out of the saucepan and unroll it from the cloth carefully
11. If to eat hot, make a nice liquor seasoned well and coloured with a little browning, and have some meat balls to garnish it
12. If cold, press it flat between two chopping boards by putting two heavy weights upon it
13. Cut the rough ends off and garnish it with parsley and slices of beetroot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rolled Beef— Pot-Roasted
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and one-half of tenderloin
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- soup stock or water
- carrot
- bay leaf
- tomato or brown sauce

## Directions

1. Take one pound and one-half of tenderloin, sprinkle it with parsley and onion.
2. Season with pepper and salt; roll and tie it.
3. Place it in a pan with soup stock (or water if you have no stock), carrot and bay leaf and pot roast for one and one-half hours.
4. Serve with tomato or brown sauce.

title: Rolled Breast of Mutton Roasted
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- forced meat
- lemon
- fat
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Lay the mutton on the board, take out the bones, beat it flat
2. Make a forced meat as for the balls and lay it on
3. Squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
4. Roll it up nicely, sew the seam along tight, sew the ends also
5. Put it down to roast, or if you have a good oven, bake it
6. Put some fat over it
7. Boil some potatoes carefully so they do not break
8. Put them in the tin with the mutton to brown
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rolled Breast of Mutton Roasted
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of mutton
- forced meat
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Lay the mutton on the board
2. Take out the bones and beat it flat
3. Make a forced meat as for the balls
4. Lay it on and squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
5. Roll it up nicely and sew the seam
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Rolled Oats Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of rolled oats
- boiling water
- one cake of compressed yeast
- one-fourth cup of brown sugar
- one-half cup of lukewarm water
- two tablespoons of shortening
- flour
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over two cups of rolled oats, cover and let stand until lukewarm
2. Dissolve one cake of compressed yeast and one-fourth cup of brown sugar in one-half cup of lukewarm water
3. Add two tablespoons of shortening, the oatmeal and the water in which it has been swelling
4. Beat well, add enough flour to make a dough, also add one teaspoon of salt
5. Let rise until it doubles in bulk
6. Mold into two loaves in pan and bake forty-five minutes

title: Rosel, Beet Vinegar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- cold water

## Directions

1. Place beets in a stone crock, removing greens.
2. Cover with cold water and put in a warm place and let stand for three or four weeks or until the mixture becomes sour.
3. This is used as a vinegar during Pesach and to make beet soup, Russian style.

title: Rothe Gritze
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of currant juice
- a pinch of salt
- one-quarter cup of cornstarch
- cold water
- cream

## Directions

1. Take one cup of currant juice, sufficiently sweetened, and a pinch of salt.
2. Let this boil and add to it enough cornstarch to render it moderately thick and then boil again for ten minutes.
3. Dissolve about one-quarter cup of cornstarch in cold water.
4. It should be eaten cold with cream.

title: Rum Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- salt
- brandy
- huckleberries or other fruit compote
- rum

## Directions

1. Take six eggs, beat whites and yolks well, add a pinch of salt and a teaspoon of brandy.
2. Fry in a spider quickly and spread with a compote of huckleberries or any other fruit.
3. Roll up the omelet, pour a very small wineglass of rum over it, light it and serve at once.

title: Rum Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- stiffly-beaten whites of two eggs
- one tablespoon of rum
- one pint of cream
- lady fingers
- one-half pound of marron glace
- rock salt and ice

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of two eggs with one-half cup of sugar until light
2. Add stiffly-beaten whites
3. Flavor with one tablespoon of rum
4. Whip one pint of cream very stiff
5. Stir into beaten eggs
6. Line a melon mold with lady fingers, split in half
7. Put a layer of whipped cream over
8. Chop one-half pound of marron glace fine and sprinkle some over cream
9. Put another layer of lady fingers, cream and marrons, and so on until mold is filled
10. Close tightly and pack in rock salt and ice, from three to four hours

title: Rum Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of rum
- one pint of cream
- lady fingers
- one-half pound of marron glace

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of two eggs with one-half cup of sugar until light
2. Add stiffly-beaten whites
3. Flavor with one tablespoon of rum
4. Whip one pint of cream very stiff
5. Stir into beaten eggs
6. Line a melon mold with lady fingers, split in half
7. Put a layer of whipped cream over
8. Chop one-half pound of marron glace fine and sprinkle some over cream
9. Put another layer of lady fingers, cream and marrons, and so on until mold is filled
10. Close tightly and pack in rock salt and ice, from three to four hours

title: Rum Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of two eggs
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one small cup of cold water
- one wineglass of rum
- juice of a lemon
- whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of two eggs with a tablespoon of sugar, and a small cup of cold water, a wineglass of rum and the juice of a lemon
2. Bring to boiling point, stirring all the time
3. The two whites of eggs may be whipped very firm and spread over the pudding just before serving

title: Russian Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pint mayonnaise dressing
- two hard-boiled eggs
- two to four tablespoons tomato catsup
- one tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- one teaspoon finely chopped or grated white onion or shallot
- one cup mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Make one-half pint of mayonnaise dressing and add the following: Two hard-boiled eggs chopped fine, two to four tablespoons of tomato catsup, one tablespoon of finely chopped parsley, one teaspoon of finely chopped or grated white onion or shallot, after these ingredients are mixed, fold them into one cup of mayonnaise and serve.

title: Russian Fish Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of fish (weakfish or carp, pickerel or haddock or whitefish)
- one good-sized onion
- three-quarters cup of water
- one-half cup of soft bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- one-fourth cup of sugar
- two egg whites
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- one tablespoon of butter
- one onion sliced
- two egg yolks
- one teaspoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Take three pounds of fish and remove skin and bones from the fish and chop flesh very fine
2. Add a good-sized onion, minced or grated
3. Make a depression in the centre of the chopped fish and add three-quarters cup of water, one-half cup of soft bread crumbs, salt and pepper to taste, one-fourth cup of sugar, two egg whites and two tablespoons of melted butter
4. Chop until very smooth and form into cakes containing a generous tablespoonful each
5. Put the bones and skins into a saucepan with an onion sliced and a tablespoon of butter and add the fish cakes
6. Cover with water and simmer for one and a quarter hours
7. Remove the cakes and strain off the gravy into the two egg yolks which have been slightly beaten together with one teaspoon of sugar
8. Stir over the heat until thickened, but do not boil it
9. Pour over fish cakes and serve either hot or cold

title: Russian Fruit Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sliced pineapple
- apricots
- strawberries
- raspberries
- juice of two lemons
- two oranges
- one cup of water
- one pound sugar
- one-half teaspoon of powdered cinnamon
- grated rind of lemon
- one cup red wine
- one-half glass of Madeira, arrak or rum

## Directions

1. Peel and pit some peaches, cut in slices and add as much sliced pineapple, some apricots, strawberries and raspberries, put these in a dish.
2. Prepare a syrup of juice of two lemons, two oranges, one cup of water and one pound sugar, a half teaspoon of powdered cinnamon, grated rind of lemon, add one cup red wine and a half glass of Madeira, arrak or rum.
3. Boil this syrup for five minutes, then pour over the fruit, tossing the fruit from time to time until cool.
4. Place on ice and serve cold.

title: Russian Goulash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound beef
- one chopped green pepper
- one large onion
- two blades of garlic
- pepper and salt
- water
- medium sized can of tomatoes
- two cups of cooked spaghetti or macaroni

## Directions

1. To one pound beef, free from fat and cut up as pan stew, add one chopped green pepper, one large onion, two blades of garlic (cut fine), pepper and salt, with just enough water to cover.
2. Let this simmer until meat is very tender, adding a little water as needed.
3. An hour or so before using, put in medium sized can of tomatoes.
4. Have ready two cups of cooked spaghetti or macaroni and put this into the meat until thoroughly heated.
5. Let water cook away just before adding the tomatoes. This must not be too wet.

title: Russian Iced Tea
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tea
- loaf sugar
- rum or brandy
- lemon slice
- preserved strawberries, raspberries, cherries or pineapple
- lemon or orange rind

## Directions

1. Make tea for as many cups as desired
2. Strain and cool
3. Place in ice-box and chill thoroughly
4. Serve in tall glass with ice and flavor with loaf sugar, one teaspoon of rum or brandy, one slice of lemon or one teaspoon preserved strawberries, raspberries, cherries or pineapple
5. Or loaf sugar may be flavored with lemon or orange and packed and stored in jars to be used later to flavor and sweeten the tea
6. Wash the rind of lemon or orange and wipe dry, then rub over all sides of the sugar

title: Russian Punch Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three layers of almond tart
- a wine glass of arrack
- one-half pound of sugar
- one-fourth cup of water
- a wine glass of rum
- cake crumbs
- crab-apple jelly or apple marmalade
- brandy

## Directions

1. Bake three layers of almond tart and flavor it with a wine glass of arrack
2. When baked, scrape part of the cake out of the thickest layer, not disturbing the rim, and reserve these crumbs
3. Boil one-half pound of sugar in one-fourth cup of water until it spins a thread
4. Add to this syrup a wine glass of rum and the crumbs
5. Spread over the layers, piling one on top of the other
6. Another way to fill this cake is to take some crab-apple jelly or apple marmalade and thin it with a little brandy

title: Russian Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled cucumbers
- mustard pickles
- pickled beans
- capers
- six herrings
- one-half pound of smoked salmon
- six large apples
- one onion
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves
- hard-boiled eggs
- nuts

## Directions

1. Cut up all kinds of pickled cucumbers, small and large, sweet and sour, also mustard pickles, into very small lengths, also pickled beans and capers.
2. Add six herrings, which you have soaked in water for twenty-four hours; skin and take out every bone, cut up as you did the pickles.
3. Add half a pound of smoked salmon, also cut into lengths, six large apples chopped very fine, and one onion grated.
4. Mix all thoroughly and pour a rich mayonnaise dressing over all.
5. Next day line a salad bowl with lettuce leaves, fill in the salad and garnish with hard-boiled eggs, nuts, and capers.

title: Russian Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- thin slices of Neufchatel cheese
- finely chopped olives
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Spread bread with thin slices of Neufchatel cheese.
2. Cover with finely chopped olives moistened with mayonnaise dressing.

title: Russian Tea Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one cup of eggs (about five)
- one cup of sour cream
- flour
- three-fourths pound of brick butter
- one cup of chopped almonds
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of sugar, one cup of eggs and one cup of sour cream with enough flour to roll
2. Toss on board and roll out one-fourth inch thick
3. Spread with a thin layer of butter, fold the dough over, roll and spread again; repeat three or four times, using altogether three-fourths pound of brick butter
4. Place dough in a bowl, cover, and let stand on ice to harden
5. Roll as thin as possible, strew with one cup of chopped almonds, sugar and cinnamon, and cut into seven-inch strips
6. Roll each strip separately into a roll, cut into squares and strew top with chopped almonds, sugar and cinnamon
7. Bake in a hot oven

title: Rye Bread (American) No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake compressed yeast
- two cups of lukewarm water
- one cup of milk (scalded and cooled)
- two and one-half cups of rye flour
- one and one-half cups of sifted white flour
- one tablespoon of melted butter or oil
- two and one-half cups of rye flour
- one tablespoon of salt
- white of egg and water
- caraway seeds (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve one cake compressed yeast in two cups of lukewarm water and one cup of milk which has been scalded and cooled
2. Add two and one-half cups of rye flour or enough to make a sponge
3. Beat well
4. Cover and set aside in a warm place, free from draught, to rise about two hours
5. When light add one and one-half cups of sifted white flour, one tablespoon of melted butter or oil, two and one-half cups of rye flour to make a soft dough and last one tablespoon of salt
6. Turn on a board and knead or pound it five minutes
7. Place in greased bowl
8. Cover and let rise until double in bulk—about two hours
9. Turn on board and shape into loaves
10. Place in floured shallow pans
11. Cover and let rise again until light—about one hour
12. Brush with white of egg and water, to glaze
13. With sharp knife cut lightly three strokes diagonally across top, and place in oven
14. Bake in slower oven than for white bread

title: Rye Bread Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of rye bread crumbs
- yolks of four eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- a pinch of cloves
- allspice
- one-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- grated rind of one-half lemon
- one-quarter pound of chopped almonds
- three tablespoons of whiskey or brandy
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Dry one-half cup of rye bread crumbs in oven
2. Beat the yolks of four eggs very light with one-half cup of sugar
3. Add a pinch of cloves and allspice, one-half teaspoon of cinnamon, grated rind of one-half lemon and one-quarter pound of chopped almonds
4. Moisten crumbs with three tablespoons of whiskey or brandy, add to eggs, then add stiffly-beaten whites of four eggs
5. Put in mold and boil three hours
6. Serve with a brandy or whiskey sauce

title: Rye Bread Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of sifted dry rye bread crumbs
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- a pinch of salt
- one-half cup of ground almonds
- two tablespoons of sherry
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of four eggs very light with one cup of sugar
2. Add one cup of sifted dry rye bread crumbs to which one teaspoon of baking-powder and a pinch of salt have been added
3. Moisten one-half cup of ground almonds with two tablespoons of sherry
4. Add and lastly fold in the beaten whites of eggs
5. Bake in ungreased form in moderate oven

title: Rye Bread, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of rye flour
- three cups of wheat flour
- two teaspoons of salt
- one-half cake of compressed yeast
- two cups of lukewarm water

## Directions

1. Sift three cups of rye flour, three cups of wheat flour and two teaspoons of salt in a bowl
2. Dissolve one-half cake of compressed yeast in two cups of lukewarm water
3. When the yeast is dissolved pour it into the flour and make into a dough
4. Lay it on a kneading board, and knead until smooth and elastic
5. Put it back into the bowl, cover with a towel, and set aside overnight to rise
6. Next morning, lay the dough on a biscuit or kneading board again and knead well
7. Make into a loaf, put into a pan, and when well risen, moisten the top with a little cold water and bake in a moderate oven

title: Rye Flour Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of rye flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of baking soda
- one-half cup of molasses
- one well-beaten egg
- one-quarter cup of chopped raisins
- four tablespoons of melted shortening

## Directions

1. Sift one and one-half cups of rye flour with one-half teaspoon of salt and one teaspoon of baking soda
2. Add one-half cup of molasses and one well-beaten egg or one-half cup of water if the egg is omitted
3. Add one-quarter cup of chopped raisins and four tablespoons of melted shortening
4. Bake in muffin-pans in rather hot oven twenty-five minutes
5. Fill pans three-fourths full

title: Sago Pudding with Strawberry Juice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup berry juice
- a scant half teacup of sago
- water
- whites of two eggs
- cream or berry juice

## Directions

1. Prepare one cup berry juice and sweeten to taste.
2. Have ready a scant half teacup of sago soaked one hour in water enough to cover.
3. Boil the sago in the fruit juice until thick like jelly.
4. Beat up the whites of two eggs and add to the sago while hot and remove immediately from the stove.
5. Mold and serve with cream or berry juice.

title: Salad of Eggplant (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small eggplants
- lemon juice
- parsley
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Use small eggplants
2. Place on end of toasting fork under broiler gas flame until the peel is black
3. Remove the skin
4. The eggplant will then be tender
5. Chop with wooden spoon
6. Add lemon juice, parsley chopped fine, and olive oil

title: Salad Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mustard and cress
- eggs
- made mustard
- Tarragon vinegar
- white pepper
- salt
- oil
- bread
- boiled beetroot
- dry double parsley

## Directions

1. Use only mustard and cress as lettuces contain too much water
2. Wash and dry the salad in a cloth
3. Boil 3 or 4 eggs very hard
4. Take the yolks and pound them well in a mortar
5. Put in a dessertspoon of made mustard
6. Add 3 spoonfuls of Tarragon vinegar, a little white pepper and salt
7. When well mixed in the mortar, rub it through a sieve in a basin
8. Rub in 3 tablespoonfuls of the best oil
9. If not thick enough add more yolk of egg until it is thick
10. Lay it on the bread
11. Put in some of the salad with a piece of boiled beetroot to each sandwich
12. See that the salad does not hang out of the bread
13. Make the salad sandwich last of all
14. Garnish with dry double parsley, not too many on each dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salmon and Brown Bread Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup salmon
- mustard
- salt
- cayenne
- bread
- thin slices of cucumber
- parsley

## Directions

1. Flake one cup salmon and rub it to a paste.
2. Add mustard, salt, and cayenne.
3. Spread on the bread and cover with a layer of thin slices of cucumber.
4. Place another piece of bread on top, press lightly and arrange with sprigs of parsley on the platter.

title: Salmon Cutlets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled salmon
- mashed potatoes
- bread crumbs
- chopped parsley
- flour
- mace
- egg
- pepper
- salt
- oil
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take the remains of some boiled salmon or a small can of salmon, three tablespoons of mashed potatoes, one of bread crumbs, one of chopped parsley, a little flour, mace, an egg, pepper and salt
2. Mix the ingredients well together, bind with the egg, let stand an hour
3. Form into little flat cutlets, roll in bread crumbs and fry in hot oil
4. Drain on paper and send to table garnished with parsley

title: Salmon Fried
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon slices
- salt
- oil
- bread or garlic
- flour
- eggs
- cayenne
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut your salmon in slices and wash it well
2. Lay it in salt for half an hour
3. Wash it off and lay it in a cloth to dry
4. Have your fish pan on the fire with oil enough to cover the slices
5. Let it boil up
6. Put a very small piece of bread or a tooth of garlic to fry before you put the fish in, to take off the flavour of the oil
7. Have a plate with flour and one with some eggs beat in with a little salt and a little cayenne
8. Dip the fish first in the flour, cover it all over lightly
9. Dip it in the eggs
10. Put it carefully into the pan of oil
11. Let it fry nicely on one side then turn it on the other
12. Put it on a fish drainer
13. When the oil is drained off, if to eat cold, put it on a fish napkin and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salmon Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of salmon
- one cup of grated bread crumbs
- two beaten eggs
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of lemon juice
- one-half teaspoon of paprika
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- one tablespoon of onion juice

## Directions

1. Blend together one can of salmon, one cup of grated bread crumbs, two beaten eggs, one cup of milk, one teaspoon of lemon juice, one-half teaspoon of paprika, one-half teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of chopped parsley and one tablespoon of onion juice.
2. Place in a greased baking dish.
3. Sprinkle top with thin layer of bread crumbs.
4. Bake in hot oven for thirty minutes or until the crumbs that cover the dish are browned.
5. Serve with a white sauce.

title: Salmon Pie
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fine fresh salmon
- five or six anchovies
- a small pottle of mushrooms
- a handful of fresh parsley
- a couple of shalots
- a little green thyme
- three ounces of butter
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- tarragon
- juice of a lemon
- half a pint of good brown gravy
- six eggs
- good short paste

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of fine fresh salmon in slices about three quarters of an inch thick
2. Clean and scrape five or six anchovies and halve them
3. Chop a small pottle of mushrooms, a handful of fresh parsley, a couple of shalots, and a little green thyme
4. Put these together into a saucepan, with three ounces of butter, a little pepper, salt, nutmeg, and tarragon
5. Add the juice of a lemon, and half a pint of good brown gravy
6. Let the whole simmer, gently stirring it all the time
7. Slice six eggs boiled hard
8. Line a pie-dish with good short paste
9. Fill it with alternate layers of the slices of salmon, hard eggs, and fillets of anchovies, spreading between each layer the herb sauce
10. Cover the dish with the paste
11. Bake in a moderately heated oven

title: Salmon Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. fine fresh salmon
- 5 or 6 anchovies
- dozen button mushrooms
- parsley
- lemon thyme
- 2 shalots
- 3 oz. butter
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1 lemon
- 1 teacup water
- 4 eggs
- butter
- puff paste
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut 2 lbs. of fine fresh salmon in slices, about 3/4 of an inch thick.
2. Wash and clean 5 or 6 anchovies and halve them.
3. Chop a dozen button mushrooms, a little parsley, a little lemon thyme, a couple of shalots very fine.
4. Put them into a very clean stewpan with 3 oz. of butter, some pepper, salt, nutmeg, the juice of a lemon, a teacup of water.
5. Put in the salmon.
6. Let it simmer gently, not the anchovies.
7. Boil hard 4 eggs, when done put them in cold water, cut them in slices.
8. Grease a pie dish with butter, put layers of the slices of salmon with some of the anchovies, and the slices of hard egg.
9. When you have used it all throw over the gravy it was cooked in.
10. Have ready a good puff paste, make your pie as ornamental as you can, wash it over with yellow of eggs, with the paste brush.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salmon Salad
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- anchovies
- capers
- pickled cucumber
- 2 boiled eggs
- white lettuce
- 3 spoonfuls of oil
- 1 spoonful of vinegar
- parsley
- mint
- fennel
- beetroot

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon in pieces the size of dice
2. Halve some anchovies, wash them well, and roll each half up like a fillet
3. Add some fine capers
4. Cut up a pickled cucumber in pieces
5. Chop 2 boiled eggs
6. Have a nice white lettuce
7. Put all this in a salad bowl
8. Make a kind of mayonnaise by putting in 3 spoonfuls of oil, 1 of vinegar, some chopped parsley and mint, a little fennel
9. Beat all this together till it comes like a cream
10. Decorate your salad with some strips of beetroot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salmon Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled salmon or canned salmon
- shredded lettuce
- hard-boiled eggs
- cucumber
- olives
- mayonnaise or boiled dressing
- heart lettuce leaves
- capers
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Either cold boiled salmon or the canned variety may be used.
2. In the latter event wash the fish in cold water, drain and expose to the outside air for at least one hour, as this removes any suggestion of the can.
3. Flake the fish into small particles and to each cupful of the fish add the same quantity of shredded lettuce, one coarsely chopped hard-boiled egg, three slices of minced cucumber and six chopped olives.
4. Mix the ingredients well, moisten with either a mayonnaise or boiled dressing and serve in individual portions in nest of heart lettuce leaves.
5. Mask each portion with a tablespoon of dressing and garnish with capers and grated egg yolk.

title: Salmon Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mayonnaise
- relishing sauce No 34
- salmon
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the bread very even
2. Spread on it some of the mayonnaise or relishing sauce No 34
3. Cut up the salmon small
4. Spread it on lightly
5. Press it down
6. Cut into four
7. Remember to put a little salt and pepper on all fish sandwiches
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salt Cod Stewed in the French Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 teeth of garlic
- 1/2 pint of oil
- pepper
- ground mace
- a little dried saffron
- juice of 3 lemons
- 2 lbs. of the thick part of the salt cod
- a few large potatoes

## Directions

1. Pound 2 or 3 teeth of garlic
2. Put it into the stewpan with 1/2 pint of oil, pepper, and ground mace, and a little dried saffron
3. Let it fry on the fire for five minutes, keep stirring it
4. Squeeze and strain the juice of 3 lemons, add them
5. Have 2 lbs. of the thick part of the salt cod, dry it well, put it in the saucepan and let it simmer gently for 1/2 an hour
6. The salt fish must be boiled first
7. When done put the cod in a dish
8. Have a few large potatoes cut into small ones, and ready boiled by the time the stew is done
9. Lay them round the fish and throw over the gravy
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Salt Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salt herring
- cold water
- boiled potatoes
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Soak salt herring over night in cold water, that the salt may be drawn out.
2. Drain and serve with boiled potatoes, or bone and place in kettle of cold water, let come to a boil and let simmer a few minutes until tender.
3. Drain and pour melted butter over them and serve hot with boiled or fried potatoes.

title: Salt Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nice, large cucumbers
- coarse salt
- dill
- whole peppers
- grape leaves
- bay leaves
- water
- rye bread

## Directions

1. Wash and wipe cucumbers
2. Lay them in a jar or wooden pail
3. Sprinkle coarse salt over each layer
4. Add dill, whole peppers and grape leaves, if you have them, also a very few bay leaves
5. Cover with water up to the brim
6. Lay a piece of rye bread in the jar to help quicken the process of souring
7. Cover with a plate and put a clean, heavy stone on top of the plate to keep them well covered with the brine
8. Set them in a warm place, say back of the kitchen stove, for the first three days
9. They will be ready to use in a week

title: Salted Almonds
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- butter or olive oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water on the almonds
2. cool and remove the skins
3. dry thoroughly and brown in a hot oven, using a half tablespoon of butter or olive oil to each cup of nuts, which must be shaken frequently
4. When brown, sprinkle well with salt and spread on paper to dry and cool

title: Salzgurken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half-grown cucumbers
- fennel
- dill
- bay leaves
- whole peppers
- grape leaves
- cherry leaves
- salt
- water
- egg

## Directions

1. Lay cucumbers in water overnight
2. Wipe each one dry and reject all that are soft at the ends
3. Lay a layer of cucumbers in a new barrel or wine keg
4. Then a layer of the following spices: Fennel, dill, bay leaves, a few whole peppers
5. Cover with grape and cherry leaves
6. Begin again with a layer of cucumbers and fill in alternate layers until all are used
7. Boil enough salt and water to just cover them
8. Test the strength of the water by laying an egg in it, if it rises the water has enough salt in it, if not, add more salt
9. Pour this over the cucumbers when cold
10. Get a cooper to tighten up the barrel
11. Roll it in the sun and allow it to stay there for two weeks, turning over the barrel once each day

title: Sand Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of butter
- one-half pound of sugar
- yolks of six eggs
- one small wine glass of rum
- one-fourth pound of corn-starch
- one-fourth pound of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- whites of six eggs

## Directions

1. Cream one-half pound of butter with one-half pound of sugar
2. Drop in, one at a time, the yolks of six eggs
3. Add one small wine glass of rum
4. Add one-fourth pound of corn-starch and one-fourth pound of flour that have been thoroughly mixed
5. Add one teaspoon of baking-powder
6. Fold in the beaten whites of six eggs
7. Bake one hour in a moderate oven

title: Saratoga Chips
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- fat

## Directions

1. Proceed as with German Fried Potatoes but do not cover
2. Do not take as many potatoes at one time

title: Sardellen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound salted sardellen
- one-half pound sweet butter
- toasted cracker or bread
- grated yellow and grated white of egg

## Directions

1. Take one-quarter pound salted sardellen and soak in water over night
2. Bone the next morning, put in cloth and press until dry
3. chop very fine, almost to a paste
4. take one-half pound sweet butter, stir to a cream and add the sardellen
5. Serve on toasted cracker or bread
6. Sprinkle with the grated yellow and grated white of egg

title: Sardellen, or Herring Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon flour
- fat
- hot fish stock
- sardellen
- one finely-chopped onion
- vinegar
- sugar
- capers
- one wirjeglass white wine
- yolk of one egg

## Directions

1. Brown a spoon of flour in heated fat, add a quantity of hot fish stock and a few sardellen chopped fine, which you have previously washed in cold water, also a finely-chopped onion.
2. Let this boil a few minutes, add a little vinegar and sugar.
3. Strain this sauce through a wire sieve and add a few capers and a wirjeglass of white wine and let it boil up once again and thicken with the yolk of one egg.

title: Sardine Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- diamond-shaped slices of stale bread
- six sardines
- two tablespoons of lemon juice
- a few drops of Worcestershire sauce
- a dash of pepper
- two teaspoons of chopped parsley
- four tablespoons of creamed butter
- whites of hard-boiled eggs
- shredded olives

## Directions

1. Toast lightly diamond-shaped slices of stale bread
2. Skin and bone six sardines, put them in a bowl and run to a paste with a silver spoon
3. Add two tablespoons of lemon juice, a few drops of Worcestershire sauce, a dash of pepper, two teaspoons of chopped parsley and four tablespoons of creamed butter
4. Spread the sardine mixture on the toasted bread
5. Garnish with a border of whites of hard-boiled eggs, finely chopped
6. Scatter shredded olives on top

title: Sardine Canapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- sardines

## Directions

1. Toast lightly diamond-shaped slices of stale bread and spread with sardines.

title: Sardine Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- cayenne
- lemon juice or vinegar
- melted butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and bones from the sardines.
2. Rub to a paste, adding an equal quantity of chopped hard-boiled eggs, seasoned with salt, cayenne, lemon juice or vinegar.
3. Moisten with melted butter and spread between slices of bread.

title: Sassafras
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sassafras wood
- stick liquorice
- aniseed
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Take some of the sassafras wood, with some of the stick liquorice and some aniseed tied in a piece of muslin
2. Put it all in a jug
3. Pour over boiling water
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sauce a la Tartare
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolk
- oil
- vinegar
- chopped parsley
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- pate de diable or French mustard

## Directions

1. Mix the yolk of an egg with oil, vinegar, chopped parsley, mustard, pepper, and salt
2. A spoonful of pate de diable or French mustard renders the sauce more piquante

title: Sauce Bordelaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium-sized onion
- fat
- a cup of strong beef gravy
- a cup of claret or white wine
- pepper
- salt
- finely-chopped parsley
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Take one medium-sized onion, chopped very fine and browned in fat
2. Add a cup of strong beef gravy and a cup of claret or white wine
3. Add pepper, salt and a trifle of finely-chopped parsley
4. Allow this to simmer and thicken with a little browned flour

title: Sauce for Ducks
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little good gravy
- a glass of port wine
- the juice of a lemon
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Mix a little good gravy with a glass of port wine
2. Add the juice of a lemon
3. Season highly with cayenne pepper

title: Sauce for Salt Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 spoonful of flour
- a small tea-cup of the liquor the fish was boiled in
- parsley
- juice of a lemon
- 2 eggs boiled hard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a small saucepan 1/2 lb. of butter and a spoonful of flour
2. Add a small tea-cup of the liquor the fish was boiled in
3. Chop a little parsley and add the juice of a lemon to it
4. Have 2 eggs boiled hard and chopped very small
5. Put them in the sauceboat with the chopped parsley, a little salt and pepper
6. Pour over the melted butter
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sauce Hollandaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
