- half a pint boiling water
- yolks of two eggs
- one slice onion
- one bay leaf
- one clove garlic
- four tablespoons vinegar
- half a teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix one tablespoon of butter and one of flour in a saucepan and add gradually half a pint of boiling water.
2. Stir until it just reaches the boiling point.
3. Take from the fire and add the yolks of two eggs.
4. Into another saucepan put a slice of onion, a bay leaf, and a clove of garlic; add four tablespoons of vinegar, and stand this over the fire until the vinegar is reduced one-half.
5. Turn this into the sauce, stir for a moment.
6. Strain through a fine sieve.
7. Add half a teaspoon of salt and serve.

title: Sauce Piquante
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint drawn butter
- one tablespoon vinegar
- one tablespoon lemon juice
- two tablespoons chopped capers
- two tablespoons chopped pickles
- two tablespoons chopped olives
- one-half teaspoon onion juice
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. To one pint of drawn butter add one tablespoon each of vinegar and lemon juice and two tablespoons each of chopped capers, pickles, and olives, one-half teaspoon onion juice, a few grains cayenne pepper.

title: Sauce Robert for Steaks
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some onions
- a bit of clarified fat
- pepper
- salt
- a little gravy
- mustard
- lemon juice
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop up some onions, throw them into a saucepan with a bit of clarified fat, let them fry till brown
2. Add pepper, salt, a little gravy, mustard, lemon juice, and vinegar
3. Boil it all, and pour over the steaks

title: Sauce Tartare
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pint well-made mayonnaise dressing
- two olives
- one gherkin
- one small onion
- parsley
- capers

## Directions

1. Add to a half pint of well-made mayonnaise dressing two olives, one gherkin and one small onion, chopped fine.
2. Chop sufficient parsley to make a tablespoonful, crush it in a bowl and add it first to the mayonnaise.
3. Stir in at least a tablespoon of drained capers and serve with fried or broiled fish.

title: Sauce Vinaigrette
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one clove garlic
- one-half teaspoon salt
- white pepper
- one teaspoon cold water or ice
- four tablespoons oil
- ten drops tabasco sauce
- two tablespoons tarragon vinegar
- one tablespoon grated onion
- one tablespoon chopped parsley
- one chopped gherkin

## Directions

1. Rub the mixing bowl with a clove of garlic, add one-half teaspoon of salt, dash of white pepper, and a teaspoon of cold water or a bit of ice, then four tablespoons of oil.
2. Mix until the salt is dissolved, remove the ice and add ten drops of tabasco sauce, two tablespoons tarragon vinegar, one tablespoon grated onion, one tablespoon chopped parsley and one chopped gherkin.

title: Sauce Without Butter for Boiled Puddings
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a table-spoonful of flour
- two table-spoonfuls of water
- a little wine
- lemon peel
- a small bit of clarified suet
- grated nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a table-spoonful of flour, with two of water
2. Add a little wine, lemon peel grated, a small bit of clarified suet, of the size of a walnut, grated nutmeg, and sugar
3. Put on in a saucepan, stirring one way, and adding water if too thick
4. Lemon juice, or essence of noyeau, or almonds may be substituted to vary the flavour

title: Sauer Kraut
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight pounds of beef either brisket or a fillet off the shoulder
- water to cover
- sauer kraut

## Directions

1. Boil about seven or eight pounds of beef either brisket or a fillet off the shoulder, in enough water to cover it
2. when it has boiled for one hour, add as much sauer kraut, which is a German preparation, as may be approved
3. it should then stew gently for four hours
4. be served in a deep dish

title: Sauer Kraut
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight pounds of beef, either brisket or a fillet off the shoulder
- water
- sauer kraut

## Directions

1. Boil about seven or eight pounds of beef either brisket or a fillet off the shoulder in enough water to cover it
2. When it has boiled for one hour, add as much sauer kraut as may be approved
3. Let it stew gently for four hours
4. Serve in a deep dish
5. Remove the fat for a more delicate dish

title: Sauerbraten
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cross-rib or middle cut of chuck about three pounds
- boiling vinegar
- water
- four bay leaves
- whole peppercorns
- cloves
- whole mace
- one tablespoon drippings
- one or two onions
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cross-rib or middle cut of chuck about three pounds, and put it in a deep earthen jar and pour enough boiling vinegar over it to cover; you may take one-third water.
2. Add to the vinegar when boiling four bay leaves, some whole peppercorns, cloves and whole mace.
3. Pour this over the meat and turn it daily.
4. In summer three days is the longest time allowed for the meat to remain in this pickle; but in winter eight days is not too long.
5. When ready to boil, heat one tablespoon drippings in a stew-pan.
6. Cut up one or two onions in it; stew until tender and then put in the beef, salting it on both sides before stewing.
7. Stew closely covered and if not acid enough add some of the brine in which it was pickled.
8. Stew about three hours and thicken the gravy with flour.

title: Sauerkraut
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage leaves
- White crisp cabbage
- Salt
- Tart apples
- Malaga grapes

## Directions

1. Line the bottom and sides of a clean barrel or keg with cabbage leaves.
2. Cut into fine shreds one or two dozen large heads of white, crisp cabbage.
3. Put in a layer of cabbage, say about four inches deep, and press down firmly and sprinkle with about four tablespoons of salt.
4. Put one or two tart apples, cut up fine, between each layer, or some Malaga grapes.
5. When four layers have been put in, pound with a wooden beetle until the cabbage is quite compact.
6. Add more cabbage, and so on until all has been salted, always pounding down each layer.
7. Cover with cabbage leaves, then a clean cloth, a well-fitting board, and a heavy stone, to act as weight on top of all.
8. Set away in a cool cellar to ferment.
9. In two weeks examine, remove the scum, if any; wash the cloth, board and stone, wash also the sides of the keg or jar, and place all back again.
10. This must be done weekly.

title: Sausage and Rice in Spanish Choriza, German Worsht
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls of oil
- ½ pint of boiling water
- salt
- pepper
- ½-lb. of rice
- saffron
- ¾-lb. of large sausage
- 2 or 3 tomatoes

## Directions

1. Slice an onion in a small stewpan with 2 tablespoonfuls of oil, let it fry
2. Put in a ½ pint of boiling water, a little salt and pepper
3. Wash well ½-lb. of rice, the best, well drain it from the water
4. Put it in the saucepan with a little saffron to colour it
5. Have a ¾-lb. of the large sausage, take the skin off, put it in also
6. Let it simmer very gently till the rice swells and soaks up all the water
7. Take the sausage out of the saucepan, lay it on a plate, cut it in round slices
8. Put the rice in the dish, lay the slices of sausage round it
9. If tomatoes are in season slice 2 or 3 and cook with the rice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sausage Roll
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. flour
- 1/4 lb. chopped suet
- salt
- cold water
- 1 lb. sausage
- yolk of egg

## Directions

1. Make a paste of 1/4 lb. flour, 1/4 lb. of chopped suet, and a pinch of salt
2. Mix it with a little cold water
3. Roll it out three times
4. Boil 1 lb. sausage and skin it
5. Roll the paste out the size of the sausage and lay it on the sausage
6. Roll it up and fasten the ends with a little yolk of an egg
7. Brush the roll all over with the egg
8. Bake it in a brisk oven
9. This is very nice for lunch, or for a side dish cut in rounds and garnished with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sauted Cornmeal Mush
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- left-over mush
- flour
- one-half teaspoon of drippings
- syrup

## Directions

1. Put left-over mush into a dish and smooth it over the top
2. When cold cut into slices one-half inch thick
3. Dip each slice into flour
4. Melt one-half teaspoon of drippings in a frying-pan and let it get smoking hot
5. Brown the floured slices on each side
6. Drain if necessary and serve on a hot plate with syrup

title: Sauted Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- flour or cornmeal
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Clean fish
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Dip in flour or cornmeal
4. Cook in spider with just enough hot butter to prevent it sticking to the pan
5. Shake the pan occasionally
6. Brown well on under side
7. Turn and brown on the other side

title: Sauted Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- toast

## Directions

1. Wash, peel caps and stems of one pound of mushrooms
2. drain dry between towels
3. Place in spider with two tablespoons of butter and one-quarter teaspoon of salt
4. Cover and cook twenty minutes, tossing them
5. Serve on hot slices of toast

title: Savory Herb Powder
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- savory
- thyme
- sweet marjoram
- shalot
- chervil
- sage

## Directions

1. Select a variety of herbs in equal quantities: parsley, savory, thyme, sweet marjoram, shalot, chervil, and sage
2. Dry these in the oven
3. Pound them finely and keep them in bottles well stopped

title: Savory Herb Powder
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- savory
- thyme
- sweet majoram
- shalot
- chervil
- sage

## Directions

1. Select a variety of herbs in equal quantities
2. Dry these in the oven
3. Pound them finely
4. Keep them in bottles well stopped

title: Savory Macaroni
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- half a pound macaroni
- salted water
- butter
- one level tablespoon finely chopped onion
- three or four sliced tomatoes
- half teaspoon powdered mixed herbs
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- one pint milk
- two tablespoons browned flour
- water
- two tablespoons grated or finely chopped cheese

## Directions

1. Bake some flour to a pale fawn color and pass it through a sieve or strainer to remove its gritty particles
2. Break half a pound of macaroni into short pieces, boil them in salted water until fairly tender, then drain
3. In a little butter in a saucepan brown a level tablespoon of very finely chopped onion
4. Add three or four sliced tomatoes, a half teaspoon of powdered mixed herbs, a little nutmeg, salt and pepper
5. When the tomatoes are reduced to a pulp add one pint of milk and allow it to come to the boiling point
6. Mix in two tablespoons of the browned flour moistened with water
7. Stir and boil till smooth, press the whole through a strainer and return to the saucepan
8. When boiling, add the macaroni and a few minutes later stir in two tablespoons of grated or finely chopped cheese
9. Serve at once or keep the pan for half an hour by the side of the fire in an outer vessel of water, or turn into a casserole and finish off in the oven

title: Savoury Cheese Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- good cheese
- pepper and salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Grate finely an equal quantity of stale bread and good cheese
2. season with a little pepper and salt, mix into a batter with eggs
3. form into thin cakes and fry

title: Savoury Eggs
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- grated hung beef
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Boil some eggs hard, put them into cold water, cut them into halves, take out the yolks
2. beat them up in a mortar with grated hung beef, fill the halves with this mixture
3. fry lightly, and serve with brown gravy

title: Savoury Jelly
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bare knuckle of veal
- a calf's foot or cow heel
- a thick slice of smoked beef
- a few herbs
- a blade of mace
- two or three onions
- a little lemon peel
- pepper
- salt
- three or four pints of water
- a little tarragon vinegar
- the white and shell of an egg
- the juice of two or three lemons
- the yolks of eggs boiled hard

## Directions

1. Have a bare knuckle of veal, and a calf's foot or cow heel
2. Put it into a stew-pan with a thick slice of smoked beef, a few herbs, a blade of mace, two or three onions, a little lemon peel, pepper and salt, and three or four pints of water
3. When it boils skim it
4. When cold, if not clear, boil it a few minutes with the white and shell of an egg, and pass it through a jelly bag
5. Add the juice of two or three lemons
6. Pour into a mould, in which are put the yolks of eggs boiled hard

title: Savoury Jelly
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- a calf's foot or a cow heel
- a thick slice of smoked beef
- a few dried herbs, marjoram, and lemon thyme
- a blade of mace
- salt
- pepper
- a little of the fresh rind of a lemon
- 2 quarts of water
- a tooth of garlic
- the white of an egg
- the shell
- the juice of 3 lemons
- a little saffron
- oil
- hard eggs
- slices of sausage
- sprigs of parsley
- a couple of salted sheep's tongues

## Directions

1. Put knuckle of veal, calf's foot or cow heel, smoked beef, dried herbs, mace, salt, pepper, lemon rind, water, and garlic into a saucepan
2. When it boils skim it well
3. Simmer for 6 or 7 hours
4. The next day skim off the fat
5. If not clear boil it for a few minutes with the white of an egg, beat to a snow and the shell
6. Pass it through a jelly bag with the juice of 3 lemons and a little saffron
7. Oil a mould and cut some hard eggs and slices of sausage, some sprigs of parsley
8. Pour in the mould some of the jelly, about a quarter full
9. Lay in a few pieces of the sausage, egg, parsley, and beetroot, tastefully arranged
10. Let that get cold
11. Put on the tongues gracefully with some more of the egg, sausage, parsley, and beetroot
12. Fill the mould up
13. Ensure the jelly is very stiff, if not in very cold weather set it on ice
14. When ready to serve pass a cloth dipped in boiling water round the mould
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Savoury Jelly for Garnishing Collared Veal, Rolled Beef, Fowls, in Galantine
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a calf's foot
- salt
- pepper
- a little saffron
- a little marjoram
- grated nutmeg
- pint and a half of water
- the white of an egg
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take a calf's foot and put in a saucepan with some salt and pepper, a little saffron, a little marjoram, grated nutmeg, and pint and a half of water
2. Let it simmer for 3 or 4 hours
3. Strain it through a clean sieve into a clean pan
4. Put it by till next day
5. Skim off all the fat
6. Put it into a clean saucepan
7. Beat up the white of an egg and stir it well in
8. Let it boil till it all rises on top
9. Let it stand and throw in 2 tablespoonfuls of cold water
10. Strain it through a jelly bag the same as sweet jelly
11. When cold, cut it in any shape you like to garnish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Savoy Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- savoy cabbage
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut off the faded outside leaves and hard part of the stalk
2. Wash the vegetable well
3. Cook in boiling salted water
4. Drain and chop very fine
5. Proceed as with spinach in the foregoing recipe

title: Savoy Cabbage With Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- one cup of boiled rice
- three dozen raisins
- salt
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- two tablespoons of drippings

## Directions

1. Boil cabbage whole for five minutes; drain, separate the leaves after it has cooled.
2. Mix one cup of boiled rice with three dozen raisins, pinch of salt, one teaspoon of cinnamon and two tablespoons of drippings.
3. Put two tablespoons of this mixture in three or four leaves, roll them and tie together with string.
4. Place in pan and let cook for an hour until done.
5. There must be sufficient fat and gravy to prevent the cabbage rolls from sticking to the bottom of the pan which must be kept closely covered.

title: Scalloped Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- toasted bread crumbs
- one cup of boiling milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil and drain off the water
2. Grease a baking-dish, line with a layer of cauliflower, add a layer of toasted bread crumbs, another of cauliflower and so on alternately, letting the top layer be of bread crumbs
3. Over all pour one cup of boiling milk, dot the top with butter and bake in a moderate oven for twenty minutes

title: Scalloped Eggs
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four eggs
- warm butter
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- crumbs of bread

## Directions

1. Poach lightly three or four eggs, place them in a dish, pour upon them a little warm butter
2. sprinkle with pepper, salt, and nutmeg, strew over with crumbs of bread
3. brown before the fire

title: Scalloped Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- three tablespoons of flour
- one and one-half cups of milk
- one teaspoon of salt
- pepper
- six hard-boiled eggs
- slices of toast
- one cup of bread crumbs
- one tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the eggs with cream dressing recipe
2. Mix one cup of bread crumbs with one tablespoon of butter
3. Sprinkle this over dish
4. Bake fifteen minutes in a hot oven

title: Scalloped Eggs (Fleischig)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped tongue
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- a little parsley
- one tablespoon of melted fat
- soup stock
- eggs
- cracker dust

## Directions

1. Make a force-meat of chopped tongue, bread crumbs, pepper, salt, a little parsley, one tablespoon of melted fat, and soup stock enough to make a soft paste.
2. Half fill patty-pans with the mixture.
3. Break an egg carefully on the top of each.
4. Sprinkle with a little salt, pepper and cracker dust.
5. Put in the oven and bake about ten minutes.
6. Serve hot.

title: Scalloped Fish
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- dressed fish
- bread crumbs
- butter
- cream
- white pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- mushrooms
- anchovy

## Directions

1. Take any dressed fish and break it in small pieces
2. Put it into tin scallops with a few crumbs of bread, a good piece of butter, a little cream if approved, white pepper, salt, and nutmeg
3. Bake in an oven for ten minutes, or brown before the fire
4. Two or three mushrooms mixed, or an anchovy will be found an improvement

title: Scalloped Fish Another Way
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- beaten egg yolk
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Break the fish into pieces
2. Pour over the beaten yolk of an egg
3. Sprinkle with pepper and salt
4. Strew with bread crumbs, chopped parsley, and grated lemon peel
5. Squeeze in the juice of lemon
6. Drop over a little warmed butter
7. Brown before the fire

title: Scalloped Fish Roe
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three large roes
- water
- vinegar
- three hard-boiled eggs
- one cup of melted butter
- one teaspoon of anchovy paste
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- juice of half a lemon
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil three large roes in water with a little vinegar for ten minutes.
2. Plunge into cold water; wipe the roe dry.
3. Mash the yolks of three hard-boiled eggs into a cup of melted butter, teaspoon of anchovy paste, tablespoon of chopped parsley, juice of half a lemon, salt and pepper to taste.
4. Add a cup of bread crumbs and then mix in lightly the roe that has been broken into pieces.
5. Put all in baking dish, cover with bread crumbs and flakes of butter, and brown in oven.

title: Scalloped Fish, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold flaked fish
- salt
- pepper
- cold cooked rice
- butter
- cracker or bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Line a buttered baking-dish with cold flaked fish.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Add a layer of cold cooked rice, dot with butter.
4. Repeat and cover with cracker or bread crumbs.
5. Bake fifteen to twenty minutes.

title: Scalloped Fish, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cooked fish
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- white sauce
- flour
- milk
- buttered bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Butter a dish, place in a layer of cold cooked fish.
2. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, parsley, salt, butter and pepper.
3. Repeat.
4. Cover with white sauce, using one tablespoon of flour to two tablespoons of butter and one cup of milk.
5. Sprinkle top with buttered bread crumbs and bake.

title: Scalloped Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- white sauce
- onion juice
- bread crumbs
- cream

## Directions

1. Saute mushrooms and prepare two cups of white sauce for one pound of mushrooms
2. add one teaspoon of onion juice
3. Into a well-greased baking dish place one-quarter of the mushrooms
4. then one-quarter of the sauce and one-quarter of the bread crumbs
5. continue in this way until all the sauce is used
6. pour one cup of cream over this and sprinkle the remaining crumbs over the top
7. Bake fifteen minutes in a moderate oven, or until the crumbs are browned

title: Scalloped Noodles and Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- broad noodles made with three eggs
- prunes
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter or other fat
- prune juice
- crumbs

## Directions

1. Make broad noodles with three eggs.
2. Boil until tender; drain, pouring cold water through colander.
3. Stew prunes, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
4. In a well-greased baking-dish place one-quarter of the noodles, bits of butter or other fat, add one-half of the prunes, then another layer of the noodles, butter or fat, the remaining prunes, the rest of the noodles.
5. Pour over the prune juice and spread crumbs over top and bake in a moderate oven until crumbs are brown.

title: Scalloped Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled onions
- cream sauce
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut boiled onions into quarters
2. put them in a baking dish and mix well with cream sauce
3. cover with bread crumbs and bits of butter and place in the oven until the crumbs are browned

title: Scalloped Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- cloves
- hot butter sauce or cream

## Directions

1. Pare a number of peaches and put them whole into a baking-tin, together with layers of bread crumbs and sugar and add a few cloves
2. Bake until the top is brown
3. Serve with hot butter sauce or cream

title: Scalloped Potatoes, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Grease a pan with butter
2. Choose the potatoes that are so big or misshapen you wouldn't want to use them for boiling or baking
3. Cut them in thin slices
4. Spread them in the pan in a layer an inch thick
5. Sprinkle with pepper and salt to taste
6. Dot with butter here and there, perhaps a half teaspoon for each layer
7. Four or six bits of butter should be sprinkled over each layer
8. Repeat the layers of the raw potatoes until the pan is full
9. Cover them with milk
10. Place in the oven

title: Scalloped Potatoes, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups cold potatoes
- two cups cream sauce
- one cup bread crumbs
- butter
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Cut cold potatoes into cubes
2. Mix well with cream sauce, adding more seasoning if necessary
3. Pour into a baking dish
4. Cover with bread crumbs
5. Dot with small pieces of butter
6. Bake for about half an hour

title: Scalloped Salsify
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- butter
- cheese
- salt
- pepper
- milk or cream
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil and slice the salsify as in preceding recipe
2. Butter a baking dish
3. Fill it by adding alternate layers of salsify and small bits of cheese
4. Season with salt, pepper and butter
5. Pour over it a sufficient quantity of milk or cream to moisten thoroughly
6. Bake one-half hour
7. Bread crumbs may be added if desired

title: Scalloped Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- rolled crackers
- butter

## Directions

1. Drain off part of the juice from one quart of tomatoes and season with pepper, salt, and onion juice.
2. Cover the bottom of a baking dish with rolled crackers, dot over with dabs of butter, pepper, and salt, then another layer of tomatoes, then of crumbs, and so on until a layer of crumbs covers the top.
3. If fresh tomatoes are used bake one hour, if canned, one-half hour.
4. If the crumbs begin to brown too quickly cover the dish with a tin plate.

title: Scent Bags
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of roses
- half a pound of Cyprus powder
- half a drachm of essence of roses

## Directions

1. Pound the roses
2. with the powder put into silk bags
3. the essence may be dropped on the outside

title: Schalet Dough (Merber Deck)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of drippings
- pinch of salt
- two tablespoons of granulated sugar
- one egg
- one cup of sifted flour
- cold water, about three tablespoons

## Directions

1. Cream four tablespoons of drippings, add a pinch of salt, two tablespoons of granulated sugar.
2. Beat in well one egg.
3. Add one cup of sifted flour and enough cold water to moisten dough so that it can be rolled out — about three tablespoons will be sufficient; it depends on the dryness of the flour how much is required.

title: Schalet or Tscholnt (Shabbas Soup)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of white haricot beans
- one pint of coarse barley
- fat meat
- marrow bone or backs of two fat geese
- ginger
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash one pint of white haricot beans and one pint of coarse barley
2. Put them into a covered pot or pan with some pieces of fat meat and some pieces of marrow bone, or the backs of two fat geese which have been skinned and well spiced with ginger and garlic
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Add sufficient water to cover
5. Cover the pot up tightly
6. Place in the oven on Friday afternoon and let remain there until Saturday noon
7. This takes the place of soup for the Sabbath dinner

title: Schwem Kloese
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons of flour
- one egg
- one-half cup of milk
- butter
- two more eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Take three tablespoons of flour and stir with one egg and one-half cup of milk
2. Pour this in a pan in which some butter was melted
3. Stir until it loosens from the pan
4. When it is cold, add two more eggs and some salt
5. Shortly before needed form in little dumplings and put in boiling hot soup for five minutes

title: Scrambled Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Break into a bowl as many eggs as required, add salt and pepper.
2. Have some very hot butter in the frying-pan on the stove; pour in the eggs, stir constantly until set, not stiff, and serve on a hot platter at once.

title: Scrambled Eggs With Brains
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- hot water
- fresh water
- a little fat
- one teaspoon of chopped parsley
- salt
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Scald brains with hot water, clean and skin, and boil a few minutes in fresh water.
2. Melt a little fat in skillet, put in brains, finely chopped, and stir well until dry and done.
3. Add one teaspoon of chopped parsley, pinch of salt, and three eggs well-beaten.
4. Stir with a fork until eggs are evenly cooked.
5. Put on hot platter, and serve immediately.

title: Scrambled Eggs With Sausage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cold, boiled sausage
- two tablespoons of hot fat
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Take one pound of cold, boiled sausage, skin and slice in half-inch pieces.
2. Place in a frying-pan with two tablespoons of hot fat.
3. Brown on both sides a few minutes.
4. Just before serving add three eggs, beaten slightly.
5. Mix and cook until the eggs are set and serve immediately.

title: Scrambled Matzoth
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six matzoth
- four beaten eggs
- one-half teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Soak six matzoth in water until soft
2. Squeeze out the water and mix with four beaten eggs
3. Add one-half teaspoon of salt and fry

title: Seasoning for Ducks and Geese
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- chopped sage
- grated stale bread
- a little shred suet
- pepper
- salt
- a beaten egg

## Directions

1. Mix chopped onion with an equal quantity of chopped sage
2. Add three times as much grated stale bread
3. Add a little shred suet, pepper, salt, and a beaten egg to bind it

title: Seasoning for Ducks and Geese
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- chopped sage
- grated stale bread
- shred suet
- pepper
- salt
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Mix chopped onion with an equal quantity of chopped sage
2. Add three times as much grated stale bread
3. Add a little shred suet, pepper, and salt
4. Add a beaten egg to bind it
5. The onions may be boiled first to render them less strong

title: Seven Layer Schalet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one egg
- three tablespoons of fat
- one cup of water
- sugar
- pinch of salt
- one and one-half teaspoons of cinnamon
- three tablespoons of bread crumbs
- melted fat
- two ounces of sweet and bitter almonds, grated
- one-half pound of cleaned, seedless raisins
- jelly
- one-half pound of citron
- grated lemon peel
- juice of lemon

## Directions

1. Take two cups of flour, one egg, three tablespoons of fat, one cup of water, a little sugar, pinch of salt, and knead lightly.
2. Put dough aside in a cold place.
3. Prepare a mixture of one cup of sugar, one and one-half teaspoons of cinnamon and three tablespoons of bread crumbs.
4. Cut dough in seven pieces and roll out each piece separately.
5. Place one layer on a greased baking-tin and spread the layer with melted fat and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
6. Place upon this the second layer, sprinkle on this two ounces of sweet and bitter almonds which have been grated and mixed with sugar.
7. Place the third layer and spread with oil, sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and one-half pound of cleaned, seedless raisins.
8. Place the fourth layer on and spread with jelly and one-half pound of citron cut up very small.
9. Cover over with another layer, spread fat and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and grated lemon peel and juice of lemon.
10. Place the sixth layer and spread and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
11. Put on the last layer and spread with fat and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
12. Cut in four-cornered pieces and bake thoroughly and until a nice brown.

title: Shabbas Kugel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five wheat rolls
- water
- one cup of drippings or one-half pound of suet chopped very fine
- pinch of salt
- two eggs well beaten
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one grated lemon rind
- one-half cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of water
- warm melted fat

## Directions

1. Soak five wheat rolls in water
2. Press the bread quite dry
3. Add one cup of drippings or one-half pound of suet chopped very fine, a pinch of salt, two eggs well beaten, one teaspoon of cinnamon, one grated lemon rind, one-half cup of sugar, one tablespoon of water
4. Stir all together thoroughly
5. Grease the kugel pot well with warm melted fat
6. Pour in the mixture and send it Friday afternoon to the bakery where it will remain till Saturday noon and it will then be baked brown
7. Alternatively, if one has a coal range that will retain the heat for the length of time required, it will be baked nicely
8. The kugel must be warm when served

title: Shad
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- lemon

## Directions

1. Let the fishmonger split it open
2. Wash and dry it well
3. Lay it on a flat dish in a marinade (oil, vinegar, pepper, salt)
4. Let it lay till next day
5. Broil it and put over it oil and lemon
6. Serve for breakfast or lunch
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Shad Roe
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- roe
- salted water
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- beaten egg
- cracker crumbs
- hot oil or butter substitute

## Directions

1. Parboil the roe in salted water ten minutes.
2. Drain; season with salt, pepper and melted butter; form into balls, roll in beaten egg and cracker crumbs and fry in hot oil or any butter substitute.

title: Shad Stewed with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- egg sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare it exactly like the other fish
2. Stew white with egg sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Shad Stewed with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Prepare the shad just as you prepared the soles with tomatoes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Shavings (Kraus-Gebackenes)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of flour
- five eggs
- salt
- one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one tablespoon of pulverized sugar
- butter or fat
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Sift about one pint of flour in a bowl
2. Make a depression in the centre
3. Break in five eggs, a pinch of salt, one teaspoon of ground cinnamon and one tablespoon of pulverized sugar
4. Mix this as you would a noodle dough, though not quite as stiff
5. Roll out very thin
6. Cut into long strips with a jagging iron
7. Fry a light yellow
8. Roll on a round stick as soon as taken up from the fat or butter
9. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon or grated peel of a lemon
10. Mix both thoroughly

title: Sheep's Hearts Fried with Bread Crumbs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's hearts
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 2 eggs
- fat
- potatoes
- walnut liquor or ketchup
- cabbage or Brussels sprouts

## Directions

1. Prepare the hearts and cut them in slices
2. Dry them on a coarse cloth
3. Have in a plate some bread crumbs mixed with parsley chopped, some marjoram, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
4. Mix this all well together
5. Have in another plate 2 eggs well beaten
6. Put in the frying pan some fat and heat until boiling
7. Dip the slices well in the eggs and then in the crumb mixture
8. Put them into the boiling fat
9. Have some potatoes boiled and mashed with a little salt and pepper
10. Make a bed of them in the dish
11. Lay the heart upon the mashed potatoes
12. Strain the fat from the frying pan into a basin
13. Pour a little walnut liquor or ketchup on to the pan
14. Set it over the fire and when it boils pour it over the heart and potatoes
15. Alternatively, use boiled cabbage or Brussels sprouts instead of potatoes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sherry Cobbler
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple sliced in slices and quarters
- a few oranges sliced thin
- a lemon sliced thin
- one cup of powdered sugar
- one tumbler of sherry wine
- a few berries such as black and red raspberries and blackberries
- pounded ice
- water

## Directions

1. Cut pineapple in slices and quarters, a few oranges and a lemon, sliced thin.
2. Cover the fruit with the sugar, laid in layers at the bottom of your bowl with pounded ice.
3. Add the wine and twice as much water as wine.
4. Stir all up well before serving.
5. It is best to mix this in a large bowl and fill in glasses just before serving, and put a little of each kind of fruit in each goblet with pounded ice.

title: Short Rib of Beef, Spanish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small ribs of beef
- water
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- tiny clove of garlic
- can of tomatoes
- red peppers or paprika

## Directions

1. Get the small ribs of beef and put on with water enough to cover, seasoning with salt, pepper, an onion and a tiny clove of garlic.
2. Let it cook about two hours, then add a can of tomatoes and season highly either with red peppers or paprika.
3. Cook at least three hours.

title: Short Rib of Beef, Spanish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small ribs of beef
- water
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- garlic
- tomatoes
- red peppers or paprika

## Directions

1. Get the small ribs of beef and put on with water enough to cover
2. Season with salt, pepper, an onion and a tiny clove of garlic
3. Let it cook about two hours
4. Add a can of tomatoes and season highly either with red peppers or paprika
5. Cook at least three hours

title: Shoulder of Mutton Stuffed
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- bread stuffing
- water

## Directions

1. Have the butcher carefully remove the blade from the shoulder and fill the space with a bread stuffing.
2. Sew up the opening.
3. Roast in the oven with a very little water in the pan, and baste frequently.
4. Serve with the gravy from the pan after the grease has been carefully removed.

title: Shoulder or Neck of Veal—Hungarian Style
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four onions
- tablespoon of fat
- one teaspoon mixed paprika
- veal
- one sweet green pepper
- water
- dumplings

## Directions

1. Brown four onions light brown in a tablespoon of fat
2. Add one teaspoon mixed paprika and the meat cut in pieces
3. Leave the pan uncovered for a few moments, cover
4. Add one sweet green pepper, cut up, and let cook
5. Add a little water whenever the gravy boils down
6. When the meat is tender serve with dumplings

title: Siesta Cake
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of butter
- milk
- a pound of flour
- six eggs
- a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds
- two tablespoonsful of yeast
- pounded almonds
- sugar
- citron
- cinnamon
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Warm butter over the fire with a little milk
2. Put it into a pan with a pound of flour, six eggs, a quarter of a pound of sweet almonds finely pounded, and two tablespoonsful of yeast
3. Beat these ingredients well together into a light paste
4. Set it before the fire to rise
5. Butter the inside of a pan and fill it with alternate layers of the paste and of pounded almonds, sugar, citron, and cinnamon
6. When baked and while hot, make holes through the siesta with a small silver skewer, taking care not to break it
7. Pour over clarified sugar till it is perfectly soaked through

title: Simmered Carrots
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart carrots
- one tablespoon of butter or drippings
- three tablespoons of sugar
- one teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Wash, scrape and slice one quart carrots roundwise
2. Put them in a saucepan with one tablespoon of butter or drippings, three tablespoons of sugar and one teaspoon salt
3. Cover closely and let simmer on a slow fire until tender

title: Sister Cake, the Queen of Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. roll dough
- 1 lb. fresh butter
- 6 eggs
- 8 oz. ground almonds
- 1 lb. citron
- 1 lb. orange and lemon peel
- 3 lb. loaf sugar
- orange-flower water
- salt
- 2 oz. Jordan almonds
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Put the dough in a very nice pan, put it near the fire to warm.
2. Melt the 1 lb. of butter in a teacup of milk, taking a piece of the butter first to rub your dish with.
3. Beat up the 6 eggs, put them in the pan with the dough.
4. Put in the ground almonds, then the butter and milk, a small pinch of salt.
5. Beat it all well for 1 hour.
6. Have ready a nice round yellow dish, butter it well.
7. Cut up the citron in slices in one plate, then the orange and lemon in another.
8. Put at the bottom of your dish a layer of the mixture of dough, butter, almonds, etc., then a layer of citron, then dough, then orange and lemon.
9. Continue making layers till you have used up all your ingredients, making your layer very lightly.
10. Your oven must be ready for it as soon as it is made, not a fierce oven.
11. It will take an hour to bake and will rise very much.
12. Put an iron skewer through to see if it is done; if the skewer comes out clean it is done.
13. Have 2 oz. of Jordan almonds bleached, put them on a plate in the oven to dry and get a light brown colour.
14. Put the 3 lbs. of sugar in a very clean saucepan with a pint of water, let it boil till it comes like a syrup.
15. Throw the orange-flower water in.
16. Have the sugar ready when the cake is done, as it must be poured hot when the cake comes out of the oven.
17. Just pass a knife round to let it soak.
18. Let the last layer on the cake be citron, put it on in pattern.
19. Ornament it with the almonds and thousands.
20. When it is cold you can turn it in a china or deep glass dish.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Slaitta (Roumanian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound medium-sized white beans
- cold water
- salt
- garlic
- one or two teaspoons of sugar
- one pound of onions
- oil

## Directions

1. Soak one pound medium-sized white beans overnight
2. Put on to boil in cold water
3. when soft, mash, adding a little warm water while mashing
4. Add salt and mashed garlic to beans and one or two teaspoons of sugar
5. To a pound of beans take a pound of onions
6. Brown the onions in oil and add water so they do not become too brown or greasy
7. When beans are tender serve on platter with browned onions poured over them
8. May be served either hot or cold

title: Small Dill Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pickles of two to three inches in length
- dill
- mustard seeds
- bay leaf
- alum
- vinegar
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Select pickles of from two to three inches in length and scrub well with a small brush
2. Pack in layers in Mason jars, a layer of pickles, a layer of dill and a few mustard seeds, placing a bay leaf and a piece of alum the size of a pea on the top of each jar
3. Let one cup of vinegar, two cups of water and one tablespoon of salt come to a boil
4. Pour boiling hot over the pickles and seal

title: Small Mohn Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cake dough
- melted butter
- one cup of sugar
- one-half pound of ground poppy seed
- one-half cup of water

## Directions

1. Roll coffee cake dough out quite thin
2. spread with melted butter (a brush is best for this purpose)
3. Let it rise a little while
4. then sprinkle well with one cup of sugar
5. add one-half pound of ground poppy seed moistened with one-half cup of water
6. cut into strips about an inch wide and four inches long
7. roll and put in a well-buttered pan to rise, leaving enough space between each and brush with butter
8. Bake in moderate oven at first, then increase the heat; bake slowly

title: Small Sponge Cakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one-half cup of granulated sugar
- one small lemon, juice and grated rind
- one scant cup of flour, sifted three times

## Directions

1. Separate the whites and yolks of four eggs
2. Beat the whites stiff, and beat into them one-half cup of granulated sugar
3. Beat the yolks to a very stiff froth and beat into them one-half cup of granulated sugar
4. Beat this last mixture for exactly five minutes
5. Add the juice and grated rind of one small lemon
6. Beat yolks and whites together well
7. Stir in very gently one scant cup of flour that has been sifted three times
8. Fold the flour in just enough to mix well
9. Bake twenty or twenty-five minutes in moderate oven in small patty pans

title: Smoked Beef
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked beef
- cold water

## Directions

1. Soak overnight in cold water
2. Next morning place it in cold water and simmer till quite tender, reckoning one-half hour to the pound

title: Smoked Beef Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mustard
- smoked beef

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread the same as roast beef sandwiches
2. Spread lightly with mustard
3. Cut the beef very thin indeed
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Smoked Brisket Of Beef And Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of smoked breast of beef
- eggs
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Take slices of smoked breast of beef, brown in frying-pan.
2. Place on hot platter.
3. Slip as many eggs as are needed in frying-pan and cook gently by dripping the hot fat over them until done.
4. Place carefully on the beef slices and serve at once.

title: Smoked Goose
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fat goose
- garlic
- salt
- sugar
- saltpetre
- water

## Directions

1. Remove skin
2. Place legs, neck and skin of neck of geschundene goose to one side
3. Scrape the meat carefully from the bones, neck, back, etc., of the goose
4. Remove all tendons and tissues and chop very fine
5. Fill this in the skin of the neck and sew up with coarse thread on both ends
6. Rub the filled neck, the legs and the breast with plenty of garlic
7. Sprinkle with three-eighths pound of salt and one tablespoon of sugar and one teaspoon of saltpetre and enough water to form a brine
8. Place the neck, legs and breast in a stone jar
9. Cover with a cloth and put weights on top
10. Put aside for seven days, turn once in a while
11. Take out of the brine, cover with gauze and send to the butcher to smoke
12. When done, serve cold, sliced thin

title: Smoked Goose Breast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of a fat goose
- salt
- pepper
- saltpetre

## Directions

1. Take the breast of a fat goose; leave the skin on
2. Rub well with salt, pepper and saltpetre
3. Pack in a stone jar and let it remain pickled thus four or five days
4. Dry well
5. Cover with gauze and send away to be smoked

title: Smoked Tongue
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked tongue
- cold water
- hot water

## Directions

1. Put on to boil in a large kettle, fill with cold water, enough to completely cover the tongue.
2. Keep adding hot water as it boils down so as to keep it covered with water until done.
3. Keep covered with a lid while boiling and put a heavy weight on the top of the lid so as not to let the steam escape.
4. It should boil very slowly and steadily for four hours.
5. When tongue is cooked set it outdoors to cool in the liquor in which it was boiled.
6. If the tongue is very dry, soak overnight before boiling.
7. In serving slice very thin and garnish with parsley.

title: Smoked Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine fat thick breast of veal
- pickle
- woodsmoke

## Directions

1. Take a fine fat thick breast of veal, bone it
2. lay it in pickle, according to the receipt to salt meat
3. hang it for three or four weeks in woodsmoke
4. it will prove a very fine savoury relish, either boiled and eaten cold, or fried as required

title: Smothered Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tender chickens
- salt
- pepper
- one stalk celery
- onion
- flour
- fat
- two cups of hot water
- level tablespoon of flour
- minced parsley
- large cup of strong stock
- one can mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut chickens in half, split down the back
2. Place the pieces in a colander to drain well, after having been well salted
3. Season with pepper
4. Grease well the bottom of a baking-pan
5. Add one stalk finely chopped celery and onion
6. Lay the chicken on breast, side up
7. Sprinkle lightly with flour and fat
8. Add two cups of hot water
9. Have the oven very hot when putting chickens in
10. As soon as browned evenly, cover with a pan, fitting closely
11. Reduce the heat of the oven and allow to cook slowly an hour or so longer, until tender
12. Place on a hot platter and set in oven until sauce is made
13. Put the pan on top of stove in which chickens were smothered
14. Add level tablespoon of flour, thinned in cold water
15. Add minced parsley
16. Let this all cook two or three minutes, then add large cup of strong stock to the chickens
17. Broil one can mushrooms, and pour these over chicken when ready to serve

title: Smothered Chicken
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tender chickens
- salt
- pepper
- celery
- onion
- flour
- fat
- hot water

## Directions

1. Two tender chickens cut in half, split down the back.
2. Place the pieces in a colander to drain well, after having been well salted.
3. Season with pepper.
4. Grease well the bottom of a baking-pan.
5. Add one stalk finely chopped celery, onion.
6. Lay the chicken on breast, side up.
7. Sprinkle lightly with flour, fat.
8. Add two cups of hot water.
9. Have the oven very hot when putting chickens in.
10. As soon as browned evenly, cover with a pan, fitting closely.
11. Reduce the heat of the oven; allow to cook slowly.

title: Smothered Tongue
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- drippings

## Directions

1. Scald tongue, and then skin.
2. Season well with salt and pepper and slice an onion over it.
3. Let it stand overnight.
4. Put some drippings in a covered iron pot, and then the tongue, with whatever juice the seasoning drew.
5. Cover closely and let it cook slowly until tender — about three hours.

title: Snip Noodles, Fried
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- three teaspoons of salt
- sweet milk
- one-half cup of rendered butter
- one-half cup of cold water

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour with three teaspoons of salt in it
2. Make into a dough by adding enough sweet milk to make soft as biscuit dough
3. Break off small pieces and roll between the hands in the shape of croquettes
4. Put one-half cup of rendered butter in a skillet that has a top to it
5. When the butter is hot, lay in the pieces of dough (do not put too many in at one time)
6. Throw in one-half cup of cold water
7. Put on the cover and let cook until the water is cooked out and noodles are brown on one side
8. Remove the cover and brown on the other side

title: Snowballs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- nice large baking apples
- preserves or jam
- sugar
- rich pie crust
- boiled icing

## Directions

1. Pare and core nice large baking apples
2. fill the holes with some preserves or jam
3. roll the apples in sugar and cover with a rich pie crust and bake
4. When done, cover with a boiled icing and set back in the oven, leaving both doors open to let the icing dry

title: Snowballs (Hesterliste)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon of butter
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one egg
- one tablespoon of cream
- one teaspoon of brandy
- flour
- butter or Crisco
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix one teaspoon of butter, one-fourth teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of sugar with one egg
2. Add one tablespoon of cream and one teaspoon of brandy
3. Add flour to make stiff dough
4. Work the whole together with a spoon until the flour is incorporated with the other ingredients
5. Break the dough in pieces about the size of a walnut
6. Roll each piece out separately just as thin as possible without tearing
7. Make three lengthwise slashes in the centre of each piece of dough after rolling out
8. Heat a large deep skillet about half full with boiling hot butter or Crisco
9. Drop in the snowballs, not more than three at one time
10. Brown quickly on one side, then on the other
11. Turn carefully with a perforated skimmer as they are easily broken
12. Remove to a platter
13. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and cinnamon and a few drops of lemon juice

title: Snowflakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large cocoanut
- pulverized sugar
- whipped or plain sweet cream

## Directions

1. Grate a large cocoanut into a fruit dish
2. Mix it thoroughly and lightly with pulverized sugar
3. Serve with whipped or plain sweet cream

title: Soda Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of granulated sugar
- one and one-half ounces of tartaric acid
- one quart of hot water
- well-beaten whites of three eggs
- bicarbonate of soda
- ice-water

## Directions

1. Take three pounds of granulated sugar and one and one-half ounces of tartaric acid, both dissolved in one quart of hot water.
2. When cold add the well-beaten whites of three eggs, stirring well.
3. Bottle for use.
4. Put two large spoonfuls of this syrup in a glass of ice-water, and stir in it one-fourth of a teaspoon of bicarbonate of soda.
5. Any flavor can be put in this syrup.

title: Sofa d'Oro: or Golden Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- a quarter of a pint of water
- a quarter of a pint of orange flower-water
- a thin slice of toasted bread
- an ounce of sweet almonds
- the beaten yolks of four eggs
- pounded cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clarify a pound of sugar in a quarter of a pint of water and the same quantity of orange flower-water
2. Cut into pieces the size of dice a thin slice of toasted bread, or cut it into shapes with a paste cutter
3. Throw it, while hot, into the sugar with an ounce of sweet almonds pounded very finely
4. Take the beaten yolks of four eggs and pour over the sugar and bread
5. Stir gently and let it simmer a few minutes
6. Serve in a deep glass dish, sprinkled over with pounded cinnamon

title: Sole a la Normande
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- mushrooms
- parsley
- fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- eggs
- lemon
- button mushrooms
- milk
- hard boiled eggs
- flour
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take a fine large thick sole, wash it well and dry it
2. Open it carefully, draw the bone as though you were going to cut off the fillets, slip the knife lightly not to cut the flesh, loosen the bone down the centre, get it out
3. Chop very fine 6 mushrooms and a little parsley
4. Put this into a small stewpan with 2 ounces of fresh butter, salt, pepper, nutmeg
5. Stir it on the fire for 5 minutes till the mushrooms are cooked
6. Beat 2 eggs and stir them in and put it over the fire till the eggs are set
7. Turn it all on a plate to cool
8. When cold put the mixture inside the sole in the place of the bone taken out
9. Lay the sole on a baking dish covered over with paper buttered, first sprinkle over the fish salt and pepper, then put the paper
10. Lay 2 oz. of butter in the dish, with the juice of a lemon
11. Boil a few button mushrooms in a little milk to keep them white, drain them dry when done
12. Cut 2 hard boiled eggs in slices, ready for garnishing, also a little chopped parsley
13. Lay the sole, take off the paper, in a very hot dish
14. Turn the liquor into a small saucepan, mix a spoonful of flour with a glass of white wine, stir it into the liquor, let it boil to get thick like another sauce
15. Pour it over the sole, and garnish one slice of egg with a little chopped parsley on and one button mushroom all round the dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sole Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good sized pair of soles
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- capers
- 2 oz. butter
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoonful flour
- 1 lemon
- dried saffron
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- butter puff-paste
- 1 egg yolk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Fillet a good sized pair of soles.
2. Put them in a stewpan with a little salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, a few fine capers, 2 oz. of butter, a cup of water.
3. Rub a teaspoonful of flour in a basin with the juice of a lemon, a little dried saffron.
4. Stir it in the saucepan to thicken the gravy.
5. When done, put them in a nice sized pie dish.
6. Cut 2 hard boiled eggs in quarters.
7. Make a good butter puff-paste to cover the dish.
8. Ornament it with a few leaves easily shaped out of pastry, and brush it over with the yolk of an egg.
9. Bake it in a hot oven.
10. Garnish with parsley.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sole Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mayonnaise
- soles

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread with a little of the mayonnaise
2. Have the soles filleted and fried straight
3. Then cut them in pieces for the sandwiches
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sole Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mayonnaise
- soles
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread with a little of the mayonnaise
2. Have the soles filleted and fried straight
3. Cut them in pieces for the sandwiches
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Sole With Wine (French Recipe)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sole or fillets of any delicate fish
- white pepper
- salt
- shalot
- claret or white wine
- yolks of three eggs
- juice of a lemon
- two ounces of butter
- chopped parsley
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Take a sole or fillets of any delicate fish
2. Lay on a fireproof dish, sprinkle with white pepper, salt and a little shalot
3. Cover with claret or white wine, and let it cook in the oven till done
4. Draw off the liquor in a saucepan and let it boil up
5. Have ready the yolks of three eggs, well stirred (not beaten), the juice of a lemon, and two ounces of butter
6. Put all together in a bowl
7. Little by little add the hot sauce, stirring all the time
8. Pour it over the fish, and sprinkle with chopped parsley
9. Serve very hot
10. A few mushrooms are a palatable addition to this dish

title: Solomon Gundy; a West Indian Recipe
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled herring
- onion
- parsley
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Soak a pickled herring and then scald it
2. pick out all the bones
3. chop it up very fine with a little onion and parsley
4. put over some oil, vinegar, and pepper, also a little cayenne
5. Make it an hour before it is wanted
6. put it in a small deep glass dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Souffle of Raspberries
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of raspberries
- rind of a lemon
- juice of 1/2 a lemon
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 3 stale sponge cakes
- snow of 3 eggs
- 2 oz. of veal fat
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rub through a sieve a pint of raspberries
2. Grate the rind of a lemon and the juice of 1/2 a lemon
3. Add 1 lb. of powdered sugar
4. Let it simmer till it thickens a little
5. Turn it out to get cold
6. Beat up the yolks of 8 eggs
7. Grate 3 stale sponge cakes
8. Beat the eggs and cakes together
9. Add the snow of 3 eggs slowly
10. Mix in 2 oz. of melted veal fat
11. Grease a mould and sift sugar over
12. Bake in a slow oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Souffle of Raspberries
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of raspberries
- lemon
- loaf sugar
- 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 3 grated sponge cakes
- 2 oz. of melted veal fat
- whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Rub a pint of raspberries through a sieve
2. Rub the rind of a lemon on some loaf sugar and add to the raspberries 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar
3. Let it simmer thick
4. When cold beat the yolks of 8 eggs with 3 grated sponge cakes and 2 oz. of melted veal fat
5. Mix the whites of 3 eggs to a stiff snow
6. Mix gently together
7. Grease a mould and bake in a slow oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Soup a la Julienne
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- carrots
- turnips
- leeks
- cauliflower
- lettuce
- onions
- a little fine salad oil
- weak broth
- a few slices of bread
- a lump of white sugar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a variety of vegetables such as celery, carrots, turnips, leeks, cauliflower, lettuce, and onions
2. Cut them in shreds of small size
3. Place them in a stew-pan with a little fine salad oil
4. Stew them gently over the fire, adding weak broth from time to time
5. Toast a few slices of bread and cut them into pieces the size and shape of shillings and crowns
6. Soak them in the remainder of the broth
7. When the vegetables are well done add all together and let it simmer for a few minutes
8. Season with a lump of white sugar, pepper and salt

title: Soup Cressy
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- six carrots
- some onions
- a lettuce
- a few sweet herbs
- good broth
- the crumb of a French roll

## Directions

1. Grate six carrots and chop some onions with a lettuce
2. Add a few sweet herbs
3. Put them all into a stewpan with enough of good broth to moisten the whole
4. Add broth occasionally
5. When nearly done, put in the crumb of a French roll
6. When soaked, strain the whole through a sieve
7. Serve hot in a tureen

title: Soup de Poisson, or Fish Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cod's-head
- water
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- celery
- parsley
- sweet herbs
- two or three onions
- cutlets of fish, sole or brill
- equal quantities of water and wine
- a large lump of butter
- the juice of a lemon
- cream
- flour
- force-meat balls of cod's liver

## Directions

1. Make a good stock, by simmering a cod's-head in water, enough to cover the fish
2. Season it with pepper and salt, mace, celery, parsley, and a few sweet herbs, with two or three onions
3. When sufficiently done, strain it
4. Prepare cutlets of fish: cut very small, well-trimmed cutlets from any fish, sole or brill are perhaps best suited
5. Stew them in equal quantities of water and wine, but not more than will cover them, with a large lump of butter, and the juice of a lemon
6. When they have stewed gently for about fifteen or twenty minutes, add them to the soup
7. Thicken with cream and flour
8. Serve the soup with the cutlets in a tureen; force-meat balls of cod's liver are sometimes added

title: Soup Maigre
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three lettuces
- a large handful of spinach
- a little chervil
- a head of celery
- two or three carrots
- four onions
- half a pound of butter
- salt
- sifted white sugar
- white pepper
- five pints of boiling water
- yolks of four eggs

## Directions

1. Chop three lettuces, a large handful of spinach, a little chervil, a head of celery, two or three carrots, and four onions
2. Put them on the fire with half a pound of butter
3. Let them fry till slightly browned
4. Season with a little salt, sifted white sugar, and white pepper
5. Stew all gently in five pints of boiling water for about two hours and a half
6. Just before serving the soup, thicken it with the beaten yolks of four eggs, mixed first with a little of the soup, and then stirred into the remainder

title: Soup Meat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- soup
- tablespoon of fat or drippings of roast beef
- slices of onion
- half a clove of garlic
- tablespoon of flour
- soup stock or rich gravy
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- one-half teaspoon of caraway seed

## Directions

1. Cook the meat until very tender then lift it out of the soup and lay upon a platter and season while hot.
2. Heat a tablespoon of fat or drippings of roast beef in a spider and cut up a few slices of onion in it, also half a clove of garlic.
3. Add a tablespoon of flour, stirring all the time.
4. Add soup stock or rich gravy and the soup meat, which has been seasoned with salt, pepper and ginger.
5. Sprinkle the spices on both sides of the meat and add one-half teaspoon of caraway seed to the sauce.
6. If too thick add more soup stock and a little boiling water.
7. Cover closely and let it simmer about fifteen minutes.

title: Soupe a la Torque
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- shin of beef
- various sorts of vegetables of whatever may be in season
- spices
- pepper
- salt
- two quarts of soup
- one quarter of a pound of rice
- two yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Make a good gravy from shin of beef
2. Cut up very small various sorts of vegetables of whatever may be in season
3. Add spices, pepper, and salt
4. Stew it all well down together
5. Set it to cool and take off the fat
6. Place it again on the fire to boil
7. Add to two quarts of soup one quarter of a pound of rice
8. Beat two yolks of eggs with a little of the stock
9. When the rice is quite tender, stir them into the soup
10. Take the precaution not to let the soup boil and to stir always the same way

title: Sour Buttered Beets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- salt
- one-half cup of butter
- two tablespoons of strained lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash as many beets as required and cook in boiling water until tender
2. Drain and turn into cold water for peeling
3. Remove the skins and slice and sprinkle with as much salt as desired
4. Melt one-half cup of butter in a large frying-pan and add two tablespoons of strained lemon juice
5. Stir the butter and lemon juice until blended, keeping the fire low
6. Turn the beets into this sauce, cover the pan and shake and toss until the sauce has been well distributed
7. Serve hot at once

title: Sour Cream Dressing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup sour cream
- three eggs
- two tablespoons sugar
- one tablespoon mustard
- one-half cup vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- paprika
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of sour cream and three eggs, well beaten. Dissolve two tablespoons of sugar and one tablespoon of mustard in one-half cup of vinegar; salt, pepper and paprika to taste, and then stir this slowly into the cream and eggs. Put in double boiler, cook until thick, then add butter the size of an egg and cook about five minutes longer. Take from fire and bottle; this dressing will keep for months.

title: Sour Cream Kolatchen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of butter
- five yolks
- two tablespoons of sugar
- grated rind of a lemon
- one cup of thick sour cream
- one ounce or two cakes of yeast
- sugar
- two tablespoons of lukewarm milk
- three cups of flour
- a raisin or cherry
- beaten white of egg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cream one-half cup of butter, add five yolks, two tablespoons of sugar, grated rind of a lemon, one cup of thick sour cream and one ounce or two cakes of yeast dissolved with a little sugar in two tablespoons of lukewarm milk
2. Stir all together and add three cups of flour
3. Mix and drop from end of teaspoon on well-greased pans
4. Let rise until light in a warm place
5. Place a raisin or cherry on the top of each cake, spread with beaten white of egg, sprinkle with sugar and bake ten minutes in a hot oven

title: Sour Milk Biscuits
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half teaspoon of soda
- one tablespoon of butter
- sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix and sift two cups of flour, one-half teaspoon of salt and one-half teaspoon of soda
2. Cut in one tablespoon of butter
3. Stir in with a knife enough sour milk to make a soft dough
4. Roll one-half inch thick
5. Cut in small rounds
6. Bake in a quick oven about twenty minutes

title: Sour Milk Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- two or three eggs
- two-thirds of a cup of sour milk
- a teaspoon of soda
- hot water
- flour
- a teaspoon of cardamom seed

## Directions

1. Dissolve a teaspoon of soda in a little hot water.
2. Add part of it at a time to the milk until it foams as you stir it. Be careful not to get in too much.
3. Mix up soft, only using flour sufficient to roll out thin.
4. A teaspoon of cardamom seed may be sprinkled into the dough.

title: Sour Milk Pancakes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one level teaspoon baking-soda
- three cups sour milk
- one egg
- salt
- one-half cup flour
- one tablespoon syrup

## Directions

1. Mash fine and dissolve one level teaspoon of baking-soda in three cups of sour milk
2. Beat one egg well
3. Put in a little salt and one-half cup of flour
4. Stir in the milk and make a smooth batter
5. Last stir in one tablespoon of syrup
6. Bake on a hot griddle

title: Sour Milk Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- two generous tablespoons of flour
- butter
- maple sugar

## Directions

1. Let the milk stand until it jellies, but does not separate
2. Put it into a saucepan and let simmer one minute
3. Then thicken with two generous tablespoons of flour
4. blend to a smooth paste with butter
5. Strain through a fine sieve and serve in cups or soup plates
6. sprinkle the top with maple sugar

title: Sour Soup (For Purim)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound soup meat
- two soup bones
- boiling water
- two leeks, cut in slices like noodles
- cooked tomatoes, cooled and strained
- cauliflower
- two tablespoons sugar
- pinch sour salt
- pepper
- salt
- two egg yolks, beaten up with salt and cold water

## Directions

1. Take one pound of soup meat and two soup bones, put on to boil in boiling water
2. Cut two leeks in slices like noodles
3. Add some cooked tomatoes which have been cooled and strained, some cauliflower, two tablespoons of sugar, a pinch sour salt, pepper and salt
4. Let cook steadily
5. When the soup is done thicken it with two egg yolks that have been beaten up with a little salt and some cold water
6. Do not cook after adding yolks of eggs

title: Sour Spatzen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons flour
- one tablespoon sweet drippings
- small onion
- vinegar
- soup stock
- two tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Brown three tablespoons of flour with one tablespoon of sweet drippings
2. Add a small onion finely chopped, then cover the spider and let the onion steam for a little while over a low heat so there will be no danger of the onion getting too brown
3. Add vinegar and soup stock and two tablespoons of sugar
4. Let this boil until the sauce is of the right consistency
5. Serve with spaetzlen made according to the foregoing recipe, using water in place of the milk to form the dough
6. Pour the sauce over the spaetzlen before serving
7. By adding more sugar the sauce may be made sweet sour

title: Soused Carp
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1/2 pint of vinegar
- 3 cloves
- allspice
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- garlic

## Directions

1. Put it in a saucepan with 1/2 pint of water, 1/2 pint of vinegar, 3 cloves and allspice, salt, pepper and a little cayenne, a tooth of garlic
2. Let it cook till done
3. Lay the carp in a dish and strain the liquor over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Soused Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three herrings
- one cup of vinegar
- whole peppers
- salt
- cloves
- two bay leaves

## Directions

1. Split and half three herrings, roll and tie them up.
2. Place them in a pie plate, pour over them a cup of vinegar, add whole peppers, salt, cloves to taste and two bay leaves.
3. Bake in a slow oven until soft (about twenty minutes).

title: Soused Mackerel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- vinegar
- cloves
- allspice
- pepper
- salt
- cayenne
- bay leaves
- fennel

## Directions

1. Wash them well
2. split them open
3. cut them in four
4. place them in a stewpan with some vinegar, 2 or 3 cloves, the same of allspice, pepper, salt, and a little cayenne, and 2 or 3 bay leaves
5. let them boil quickly for half an hour
6. put them in a deep dish
7. serve as required with a little fennel
8. turn them over each day
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Spaetzlen Or Spatzen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- two eggs
- salt
- water or milk
- two tablespoons butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour into a bowl, make a depression in the centre and break into it two eggs
2. Add a saltspoon of salt and enough water or milk to form a smooth, stiff dough
3. Set on some water to boil, salt the water
4. When the water boils, drop the spaetzle into it, one at a time, using the spoon with which you cut the dough, or roll it on a board into a round roll and cut them with a knife
5. When the spaetzle are done, they will rise to the surface, take them out with a perforated skimmer and lay them on a platter
6. Heat two tablespoons of butter and add bread crumbs, let them brown for a minute and pour all over the spaetzle
7. Alternatively, put the spaetzle right into the spider in which you have heated the butter, or scramble a few eggs over all, stirring eggs and spaetzle together
8. Serve hot

title: Spaghetti and Meat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- spaghetti
- left-over meat
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Break spaghetti in small pieces and boil until tender.
2. Put left-over meat through chopper and mix with the spaghetti, salt, pepper and a little onion juice.
3. Grease a baking dish and put in the meat and spaghetti.
4. Sprinkle on top with bread crumbs and bake in a moderate oven.

title: Spanish Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of beans
- one onion
- two cloves of garlic
- one tablespoon of olive oil
- one-half can of tomatoes
- one teaspoon chili powder
- salt to taste
- half a dozen olives
- smoked beef or tongue (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak two cups of beans overnight
2. Drain and boil until the skin cracks, and let one cup of water remain on the beans
3. Chop fine one onion and two cloves of garlic and fry a light brown in one tablespoon of olive oil
4. Add one-half can of tomatoes, one teaspoon chili powder dissolved in a little cold water, salt to taste and half a dozen olives chopped

title: Spanish Cauliflower
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium-size onion
- small bunch of parsley
- one tablespoon butter
- celery salt
- one tablespoon flour
- one cup boiling water
- cauliflower
- three tablespoons tomato catsup

## Directions

1. Finely chop one medium-size onion and a small bunch of parsley
2. Melt one tablespoon butter in a pan and fry the onion until it is brown
3. Season with celery salt
4. Blend in one tablespoon flour, add one cup boiling water and let simmer for half an hour
5. Carefully clean the cauliflower and boil for one-half hour
6. Drain the onion sauce, add three tablespoons tomato catsup, drain the cauliflower, turn into a baking-pan, pour over the sauce, place in a moderate oven for five minutes and serve hot

title: Spanish Liver
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound calf's liver
- salt water
- one onion
- one tablespoon parsley
- some mint
- two cloves
- a little cinnamon
- a little tabasco sauce
- one tablespoon olive oil
- one cup of soup stock
- one cup of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil one-half pound calf's liver in salt water
2. Drain and cut into small cubes
3. Chop one onion, one tablespoon parsley, some mint
4. Add two cloves, a little cinnamon, a little tabasco sauce, one tablespoon olive oil, and one cup of soup stock
5. Add one cup of bread crumbs which have been soaked in hot water and then drained
6. Mix all with the liver and bring to a boil
7. Serve with Spanish rice

title: Spanish Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two nice large ripe tomatoes
- one large or two medium-sized white Texas onions
- two sprigs of parsley
- one large green-bell pepper
- olive oil or butter
- salt
- red pepper
- six eggs
- two tablespoons of water

## Directions

1. In a chopping bowl place two nice large ripe tomatoes, first peeling them; one large or two medium-sized white Texas onions, two sprigs of parsley, and one large green-bell pepper, first removing most of its seeds.
2. Chop these ingredients well together quite fine, turn them into a saucepan and let them cook over rather a brisk heat until quite soft.
3. Put no water in this mixture.
4. Add a tablespoon of olive oil or of butter before it begins to cook and season well with salt and red pepper.
5. Make the omelet the same as the plain one, but use water instead of milk in mixing it, and only use two tablespoons of water for the six eggs required.
6. After the eggs are sufficiently beaten, mixed, and in the pan over the fire, and when the edges begin to stiffen, cover the surface of the omelet to within an inch of the edge with the cooked vegetables.
7. Fold the omelet quickly and turn it on a hot platter.
8. Pour around it all the vegetables left in the pan and serve.

title: Spanish Onion Rarebit
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- butter
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- tabasco sauce
- prepared mustard
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Boil two large onions until very soft
2. drain and chop
3. return to the saucepan with a small piece of butter
4. Add milk, salt, pepper, a dash of tabasco sauce, one teaspoon of prepared mustard, one-half cup of grated cheese
5. Stir until of the consistency of custard

title: Spanish Onions Filled with Forced Meat
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Spanish onions
- salt
- 1 lb. forced meat
- pepper
- marjoram
- grated nutmeg
- onion hearts
- 1/2 pint stock
- 1 spoonful flour
- juice of 1 lemon
- browning

## Directions

1. Boil 3 Spanish onions in water with some salt for three-quarters of an hour
2. Take them out and put them to drain in a colander
3. When cold, make a slit down with a knife to enable you to strip each leaf without breaking them
4. Place each onion on a separate plate
5. Prepare 1 lb. of forced meat as for balls and season it well
6. Divide it in three and form them into balls
7. Begin with the smallest leaf, not the heart, and go on replacing the leaves till you make up the three onions again
8. Have in a stewpan the hearts of the onions, some salt, pepper, a little marjoram, grated nutmeg, and 1/2 pint of stock
9. Put in the onions and let them stew for an hour
