10. Rub in a basin one spoonful of flour, the juice of a lemon, and a little browning to colour lightly
11. Serve with egg sauce or tomato sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Spanish Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of cold chicken, duck or any poultry
- thin crust
- sweet red peppers
- thinly sliced sausage
- a glass of claret
- two tablespoons of flour
- thin crust of pastry

## Directions

1. Take one pint of cold chicken, duck or any poultry
2. Cut it into flakes and place it in a pudding dish which has been lined with a thin crust
3. On the layer of meat place a layer of sweet red peppers (seeds removed), cut in slices
4. Next, a layer of thinly sliced sausage, and so on until the dish is full
5. Pour a glass of claret into which have been rubbed two tablespoons of flour over this
6. Cover with a thin crust of pastry, and bake

title: Spanish Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of washed rice
- four or five tablespoons of poultry fat
- three onions
- two cloves of garlic
- one red pepper or one canned pimento or one teaspoon of paprika
- three ripe tomatoes or two cups of strained tomatoes
- one teaspoon of salt
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Put one cup of washed rice in frying-pan with four or five tablespoons of poultry fat
2. Add three onions chopped and two cloves of garlic minced fine
3. Fry ten minutes
4. Add one red pepper or one canned pimento chopped, or one teaspoon of paprika, and three ripe tomatoes or two cups of strained tomatoes and one teaspoon of salt
5. Cook slowly about one hour
6. As the water evaporates, add more boiling water to keep from burning

title: Spanish Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one onion
- green pepper
- hot butter
- four tablespoons of flour
- two cups of strained tomatoes
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook one onion and green pepper chopped fine in hot butter
2. Add four tablespoons of flour, stir until smooth
3. Add two cups of strained tomatoes
4. Season with salt and pepper

title: Spatzen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- three-fourths cup of flour
- one-third cup of water

## Directions

1. Beat one egg well, add one-half teaspoon of salt, three-fourths cup of flour and one-third cup of water, stirring to a stiff, smooth batter.
2. Drop by teaspoons into boiling soup ten minutes before serving.

title: Spice Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of brown sugar
- one-half cup of butter or butter substitute
- one-half teaspoon of ground cloves
- one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one cup of sour milk
- one teaspoon of baking-soda
- two cups of flour
- one cup of raisins chopped

## Directions

1. Cream one cup of brown sugar and one-half cup of butter (or a little less of any butter substitute)
2. Add one-half teaspoon of ground cloves and ground cinnamon
3. Add one cup of sour milk
4. Add one teaspoon of baking-soda, two cups of flour and one cup of raisins chopped
5. Have ready a warm oven and bake three-quarters of an hour

title: Spice Roll
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cake dough
- melted butter
- leb kuchen or plain gingerbread
- one-half cup of almonds or nuts
- one cup of seeded raisins
- one cup of cleaned currants
- brown sugar
- syrup
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roll out coffee cake dough quite thin and let it rise half an hour
2. Brush with melted butter
3. Grate some leb kuchen or plain gingerbread; add one-half cup of almonds or nuts, one cup of seeded raisins and one cup of cleaned currants
4. Strew these all over the dough together with some brown sugar and a little syrup
5. Spice with cinnamon and roll
6. Spread with butter and let it rise for an hour
7. Bake brown

title: Spiced Beef
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine thick piece of brisket of beef not fat
- pickle
- ground all-spice
- black pepper
- cloves
- ginger
- nutmegs
- a little brown sugar
- pounded dried sweet herbs
- water

## Directions

1. Take a fine thick piece of brisket of beef not fat
2. Let it lay three days in a pickle
3. Take it out and rub in a mixture of spices consisting of equal quantities of ground all-spice, black pepper, cloves, ginger and nutmegs, and a little brown sugar
4. Repeat this daily for a week
5. Cover it with pounded dried sweet herbs
6. Roll or tie it tightly
7. Put it into a pan with very little water
8. Bake slowly for eight hours

title: Spiced Beef
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine thick piece of brisket of beef not fat
- salt
- sugar
- saltpetre
- water
- ground all-spice
- black pepper
- cloves
- ginger
- nutmegs
- brown sugar
- pounded dried sweet herbs

## Directions

1. Let the beef lay three days in a pickle made of eight ounces of salt, eight ounces of sugar, and half an ounce of saltpetre in two quarts of water
2. Take it out and rub in a mixture of spices consisting of equal quantities of ground all-spice, black pepper, cloves, ginger and nutmegs, and a little brown sugar
3. Repeat this daily for a week
4. Cover it with pounded dried sweet herbs, roll or tie it tightly
5. Put it into a pan with very little water, and bake slowly for eight hours
6. Take it out, untie it and put a heavy weight upon it
7. Serve eaten cold

title: Spiced Cucumbers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- firm cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- mixed spices
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take nice firm cucumbers, slice thin and salt overnight
2. In the morning take vinegar sufficient for covering the quantity prepared, mixed spices and sugar according to taste
3. Put on to cook and when boiling put in the cucumbers and cook for thirty minutes
4. Put in airtight jars

title: Spiced German Plums
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- almonds
- best wine vinegar
- water
- sugar
- whole cinnamon
- cloves
- allspice

## Directions

1. Wash the plums, remove the stones and in place of the stones put in almonds
2. Take the best wine vinegar, water and sugar to taste
3. Tie in a bag some whole cinnamon, cloves, and allspice
4. Boil together with vinegar
5. After boiling, let it get lukewarm, then pour over the prunes
6. Let stand, and each day for nine days let vinegar come to a boil and pour over prunes
7. The last day cook the vinegar down some, then put in the prunes and let come to a boil
8. Keep in a stone or glass jar

title: Spiced Grapes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- seven pounds of Concord grapes
- four and one-half pounds of granulated sugar
- one pint of cider vinegar
- two tablespoons of ground cinnamon
- two tablespoons of ground cloves

## Directions

1. Pulp seven pounds of Concord grapes
2. Cook the pulp and skins until soft
3. Put them through a fine sieve
4. Add four and one-half pounds of granulated sugar, one pint of cider vinegar, two tablespoons of ground cinnamon, and two tablespoons of ground cloves
5. Bring to a boil then cook slowly for one and one-half hours
6. Put in an earthen crock when cool

title: Spiced or Pickled Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- apples (Pound Sweets)
- four pounds of white sugar
- nine pounds of fruit
- one ounce of cinnamon bark
- half an ounce of cloves
- one pint of water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pare the apples (Pound Sweets are best; crab-apples may be pickled the same way, but do not pare)
2. Leave on the stems and put into a kettle with alternate layers of sugar, taking four pounds of white sugar to nine pounds of fruit
3. Spice with an ounce of cinnamon bark and half an ounce of cloves, removing the heads
4. Heat slowly to a boil with a pint of water
5. Add the vinegar and spices and boil until tender
6. Take out the fruit with a perforated skimmer and spread upon dishes to cool
7. Boil the syrup thick and pack the apples in jars and pour the syrup over them boiling hot
8. Examine them in a week's time, and should they show signs of fermenting pour off the syrup and boil up for a few minutes and pour over the fruit scalding, or set the jars (uncovered) in a kettle of cold water and heat until the contents are boiling and then seal

title: Spiced or Pickled Cherries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- largest and freshest red cherries
- 1 cup best pickling vinegar per quart of cherries
- 1 pound sugar per 3 quarts of fruit
- 3 sticks whole cinnamon bark per 3 quarts of fruit
- one-half ounce cloves per 3 quarts of fruit

## Directions

1. Take the largest and freshest red cherries you can get, and pack them in glass fruit jars, stems and all
2. Put little splints of wood across the tops of the fruit to prevent rising to the top
3. Boil the vinegar and spices and sugar for five minutes steady
4. Turn out into a covered stoneware vessel, cover, and let it get cold
5. Pour over the fruit and repeat this process three days in succession
6. Remove the heads of the cloves, for they will turn the fruit black
7. You may strain the vinegar after the first boiling, so as to take out the spices, if you choose
8. Seal as you would other fruit
9. Be sure that the syrup is cold before you pour it over the cherries

title: Spinach
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach with large leaves
- salt
- water
- butter
- pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cut off the roots and pick spinach over carefully, cutting off all the withered leaves and stems
2. Put the leaves in cold salt water to soak for half an hour
3. Shake the leaves about and turn them over several times
4. Drop them in a large pan of water and rinse well
5. Lift them out separately and drop back into a second pan of water
6. Continue washing in fresh water until there is not a grain of sand to be found in the bottom of the pan
7. Let a cup of water come to a boil
8. Lift the spinach out of the cold water pan with the water dripping from it and put it into the pot into the boiling water
9. Put the lid on the pot
10. Turn the fire a little low and let it cook slowly for fifteen minutes, stirring every now and then to keep it from sticking to the bottom of the pot
11. Just before taking up the spinach put some salt in it
12. Drain off the water and put a big tablespoon of butter and one-quarter teaspoon of pepper in it
13. Take it out of the pot and place it in a long, flat dish
14. Slice some hard-boiled eggs and place the slices all around the spinach for a kind of border

title: Spinach Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of spinach
- one-half cup of chopped onions
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- two cups of milk
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash, pick over and cook two quarts of spinach for twenty minutes
2. drain, chop and rub through a sieve and return to the water in which it was cooked
3. add one-half cup of chopped onions, cook until thoroughly done
4. thicken with a white sauce made by melting two tablespoons of butter to which is added two tablespoons of flour
5. stir until smooth, add two cups of milk
6. season with one-half teaspoon of salt and pepper and add the spinach mixture

title: Spinach With Cream Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- cream sauce
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook spinach as directed
2. Drain through colander
3. Grind through machine
4. Make a rich cream sauce
5. Stir spinach in this sauce
6. Add pepper, salt, nutmeg to taste
7. Garnish with slices of hard-boiled egg

title: Spinach—Fleischig
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- drippings
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- soup stock or beef gravy
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of spinach about fifteen minutes
2. Drain thoroughly through a colander and chop extremely fine
3. Heat one tablespoon of drippings in a saucepan
4. Rub one tablespoon of flour in it
5. Add salt, pepper and ginger to taste
6. Add one cup of soup stock to the whole or some beef gravy
7. Put the spinach in the sauce
8. Let boil for five minutes
9. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs or use only the hard-boiled whites for decoration
10. Rub the yolks to a powder and mix through the spinach

title: Split Pea Soup (Milchig)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of peas
- one gallon of water
- a few potatoes
- a large celery root
- a little parsley
- a little onion
- a small carrot
- a small clove of garlic
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Soak peas in lukewarm water over night
2. Use one quart of peas to one gallon of water
3. Boil about two hours with the following vegetables: a few potatoes, a large celery root, a little parsley and a little onion, a small carrot cut up in cubes and a small clove of garlic
4. When boiled down to half the quantity, press all through colander
5. If soup is too thin, take a tablespoon of flour blended with a little cold water in a saucepan and add to the peas already strained
6. Serve with croutons

title: Sponge Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sugar, same weight as eggs
- flour, one-half the weight of eggs
- grated rind of one lemon to five eggs
- juice of one lemon to five eggs
- powdered sugar
- rose-water (optional)

## Directions

1. Weigh any number of eggs; take the same weight of sugar and one-half the weight of flour
2. Add the grated rind and juice of one lemon to five eggs
3. For mixing this cake, see the directions given on page 246; the mixture should be very light and spongy, great care being used not to break down the whipped whites
4. The oven should be moderate at first, and the heat increased after a time
5. The cake must not be moved or jarred while baking
6. Bake forty to fifty minutes, according to size of cake
7. Use powdered sugar for sponge-cake
8. Rose-water makes a good flavoring when a change from lemon is wanted

title: Sponge Cake, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- one pound of granulated sugar
- grated rind of a lemon
- six ounces of fine matzoth-meal

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs, sugar and lemon rind together until very light, to about the thickness of a custard
2. Add the meal, stirring it in without much beating
3. Bake in a moderately quick oven one-half hour

title: Sponge Cake, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- one and one-half cups of granulated sugar
- one cup of mixed matzoth-meal and potato flour
- flavoring to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs and the sugar together until very light
2. Add the flavoring, matzoth-meal and potato flour
3. Add last of all the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth
4. Stir lightly and bake in a moderately quick oven

title: Sponge Cakes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- half a pound of lump sugar
- half a pound of flour
- a quarter of a pint of water
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Mix six eggs and half the whites
2. Flavour a quarter of a pint of water by having lemon peel in it for some hours
3. Boil the sugar and water together
4. Pour over the eggs after they have been well whisked
5. Continue whisking while the liquid is being poured over them, and until they become quite thick and white
6. Stir in the flour, which must be warm and dry
7. Pour the mixture into a couple of cake tins
8. Bake in a gentle oven

title: Sponge Dumplings
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup of soup stock
- one-fourth teaspoon of salt
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Separate three eggs and beat the yolks
2. Add one cup of soup stock and one-fourth teaspoon of salt
3. Add the beaten whites
4. Pour into a greased cup and place in pan of hot water
5. Steam until firm
6. Cool and remove from cup and cut into small dumplings with a teaspoon
7. Pour the boiling soup over and just before serving add chopped parsley

title: Squab en Casserole
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- water
- soup stock
- mushrooms
- carrots
- small potatoes
- peas
- sherry wine

## Directions

1. Take fowl and brown in a skillet the desired color
2. Add to this enough water or soup stock preferred
3. Put it in casserole and add vegetables; add first those that require longest cooking
4. Use mushrooms, carrots, small potatoes and peas
5. If you like flavor of sherry wine, add small wine glass; if not, it is just as good
6. Season well and cook in hot oven not too long, as you want fowl and vegetables to be whole
7. You may add soup stock if it is too dry after being in oven

title: Squabs, Or Nest Pigeons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs or nest pigeons
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- bread dressing

## Directions

1. Pick, singe, draw, clean and season them well inside and out, with salt mixed with a little ginger and pepper
2. Stuff them with well-seasoned bread dressing
3. Pack them

title: Squabs, or Nest Pigeons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- bread dressing
- goose fat
- minced parsley
- chopped onion
- water

## Directions

1. Pick, singe, draw, clean and season them well inside and out, with salt mixed with a little ginger and pepper
2. Stuff them with well-seasoned bread dressing
3. Pack them closely in a deep stew-pan and cover with flakes of goose fat, minced parsley and a little chopped onion
4. Cover with a lid that fits close and stew gently, adding water when necessary
5. Do not let them get too brown. They should be a light yellow

title: Squash Fritters
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of boiled squash
- half a cup of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one egg
- two tablespoons of milk
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Use cold, left over portion of squash that has been prepared as a vegetable with seasoning and a little butter
2. Mix baking-powder with the flour
3. Add flour mixture to the squash
4. Add milk and stir all together
5. Beat egg and stir in
6. Have hot fat in pan
7. Drop fritters from spoon into pan
8. When browned on both sides remove to hot platter

title: Squash Salad (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- long squash
- olive oil
- vinegar
- one-half teaspoon of prepared mustard
- two tablespoons of olive oil
- two shallots or leeks

## Directions

1. Grate off the skin of long squash
2. Cut the squash in slices, one-quarter of an inch thick, and fry in olive oil
3. Prepare a sauce with a little vinegar, one-half teaspoon of prepared mustard, two tablespoons of olive oil, and beat these ingredients very well
4. Add two shallots or leeks, cut in small pieces
5. Pour sauce over the squash and serve

title: Staffin
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit prepared as for Lamplice
- paste
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Line the dish with the paste
2. Lay the fruit in alternate layers with paste till the dish is filled
3. The paste must form the top layer
4. Pour clarified sugar over before it is put into the oven

title: Steak and Mushrooms
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- steak
- dripping
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Take some nice mushrooms and peel them
2. Wash them well and let them drain in a colander
3. Put on your meat frying-pan a little dripping and let it boil
4. Put in your steak and sprinkle over it salt and pepper
5. When nearly done put in the mushrooms to fry
6. Have a hot dish ready and put your steak in
7. Place the mushrooms round
8. Strain the fat from the frying-pan
9. Put in a little salt, pepper, and the juice of a lemon, and a little boiling water
10. Set it over the fire and when it boils turn it over the steak
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Steak Stewed with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 good sized tomatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- steak

## Directions

1. Cut 3 or 4 good sized tomatoes and put them in a stewpan with an onion
2. Add salt and pepper
3. Put in the steak
4. As soon as it is tender take out the steak
5. Rub the tomatoes and onion through a sieve
6. Put back the steak in the saucepan and throw over the sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Steak Stewed with Walnut Liquor
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of the first cut of chuck steak
- 1/2 pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- small onion
- pickled walnut

## Directions

1. Take a pound of the first cut of chuck steak
2. Put it in a stewpan with 1/2 pint of water, salt, and pepper
3. Chop a small onion very fine to stew with
4. Let it stew gently for 1 hour till the liquor is quite reduced
5. When quite done rub a pickled walnut quite smooth and stir it in the liquor
6. When you put the steak in the dish put the liquor over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Steaks with Chestnuts
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fine thick steak
- flour
- a little good beef gravy
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- a quarter of a hundred good chestnuts
- a little Espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Take a fine thick steak and half fry it
2. Flour and place it in a stewpan with a little good beef gravy
3. Season with cayenne pepper and salt
4. When it has simmered for about ten minutes, add a quarter of a hundred good chestnuts, peeled and the inner skin scraped off
5. Let them stew with the steak till well done
6. A little Espagnole sauce heightens the flavor

title: Steamed Berry Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter or other shortening
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- yolk of one egg
- one-half cup of milk
- one cup of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of berries or pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Take one tablespoon of butter (or other shortening), one-quarter cup of sugar, yolk of one egg, one-half cup of milk, one cup of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, one-quarter teaspoon of salt, one-half cup of berries or pitted cherries rolled in flour
2. Put in a well-greased melon mold and cook in boiling water steadily for two hours
3. Serve with hard sauce

title: Steamed Berry Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- yolk of one egg
- one-half cup of milk
- one cup of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one-quarter teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of berries or pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Roll berries or pitted cherries in flour
2. Put in a well-greased melon mold
3. Cook in boiling water steadily for two hours
4. Serve with hard sauce

title: Steamed Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- powdered sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Steam until the fruit is swollen to its original size and is tender.
2. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and squeeze lemon juice over them.

title: Steamed Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of rice
- eight cups of cold water
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Wash two cups of rice carefully
2. Put in double boiler
3. Add eight cups of cold water and a pinch of salt
4. Steam for two hours; do not stir
5. Serve with any kind of stewed fruit or preserve

title: Steamed Sweet Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet apples
- beef drippings
- brown sugar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Quarter and core five apples without paring
2. Rut into the pot and melt beef drippings
3. When hot, lay a layer of apples in, skin down
4. Sprinkle with brown sugar
5. When nearly done, turn and brown
6. Place on a platter
7. Sprinkle with sugar
8. Steam in a closely covered iron pot for three-quarters of an hour

title: Stew Calves' Feet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 feet
- a pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- marjoram
- a little dried saffron
- an onion
- a tooth of garlic
- meat balls
- 2 eggs
- the juice of a lemon
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up 2 feet in pieces and put them in a stewpan with water, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, marjoram, dried saffron, onion, and garlic
2. Let it simmer for 2 or 3 hours till quite tender
3. Add some meat balls 1 hour before it is done
4. Just before dishing up, add the egg sauce made from 2 beaten eggs, strained lemon juice, and chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stew Sheep's Heart with Celery
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sheep's hearts
- celery
- a chopped onion
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- meat balls or motsa balls
- lemon
- browning

## Directions

1. Wash the hearts well and see that they are free from all blood
2. Cut them in slices
3. Cut and wash celery
4. Put it in a stewpan with chopped onion, pepper, salt, and grated nutmeg
5. Add a few meat balls or small motsa balls
6. Flavour with lemon and colour with browning
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stew Sweetbreads with Button Mushrooms
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 throat sweetbreads
- boiling water
- button mushrooms
- very small meat balls
- a couple of sheep's tongues
- 2 eggs
- the juice of a lemon
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put sweetbreads in a saucepan with boiling water for 10 minutes
2. Take them out and shape them nicely
3. Stew them exactly like calf's feet
4. Add a few button mushrooms and a few very small meat balls
5. Add sheep's tongues cut in pieces after they have been boiled
6. Add the egg sauce made from beaten eggs, lemon juice, and chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stew White Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint large white beans
- 3/4-lb. butter
- 1 onion
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Lay in soak 1 pint of the large white beans the night before you require them.
2. Put in a stewpan 3/4-lb. of butter, and an onion chopped very fine, let it fry.
3. Add pint of water, put in the beans.
4. Let them simmer gently till done.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Beef with Chestnuts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3-lbs. of beef
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1-lb. of chestnuts
- teaspoonful of flour
- juice of 1/2 a lemon
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Take a nice piece of beef and put it into a stewpan with salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg and 1/2 pint of water
2. Let it simmer gently for 2 hours
3. Boil 1-lb. of chestnuts for 1/2 an hour
4. Take them out of the water and peel them whole
5. 1/2 an hour before the meat is done put in the chestnuts to finish cooking
6. Rub a teaspoonful of flour with the juice of 1/2 a lemon and a little dried saffron
7. Be careful the chestnuts do not break when you put in the thickening
8. Dish them up nicely by putting them round the meat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Breast of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3-lb. of breast of veal
- soaked bread
- marjoram
- lemon peel
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- ounce of chopped suet
- egg
- flour
- 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Take 2 or 3-lb. of breast of veal and lay it on the chopping board
2. Have ready a stuffing made of soaked bread, marjoram chopped, lemon peel, parsley, pepper, salt, a little grated nutmeg, and an ounce of chopped suet
3. Mix this with one egg
4. Place it between the flesh and bones
5. Sprinkle flour over the breast
6. Put it in a flat stew pan with 1/2 pint of water, salt
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Breast of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3-lb. of breast of veal
- soaked bread
- marjoram chopped
- lemon peel
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- an ounce of chopped suet
- one egg
- flour
- 1/2 pint of water
- dried marjoram
- a good-sized onion sliced
- a tablespoonful of flour
- juice of a lemon
- dried saffron
- button mushrooms or Jerusalem artichokes

## Directions

1. Lay breast of veal on the chopping board
2. Make a stuffing of soaked bread, marjoram chopped, lemon peel, parsley, pepper, salt, a little grated nutmeg, an ounce of chopped suet, and mix with one egg
3. Place stuffing between the flesh and bones
4. Sprinkle flour over the breast
5. Put it in a flat stew pan with 1/2 pint of water, salt, pepper, a little dried marjoram, and a good-sized onion sliced
6. Let it stew gently for 2 hours
7. Take out the veal and put it on a dish
8. Strain the liquor
9. Put the meat back into the stewpan and pour over the strained liquor
10. Let it boil up
11. In a basin mix a tablespoonful of flour rubbed smoothly with the juice of a lemon and a little dried saffron
12. Turn this over your stew and baste it backwards and forwards with a spoon
13. Dish it up, optionally with a few button mushrooms or Jerusalem artichokes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- one tablespoon of drippings
- one-fourth of an onion
- one tablespoon of flour
- one-half pint of soup stock
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and drain cabbage, cut in small pieces and boil until tender.
2. Drain and rinse in cold water; chop fine.
3. Heat one tablespoon of drippings in spider, one-fourth of an onion cut fine and one tablespoon of flour; brown all together.
4. Add one-half pint of soup stock, add cabbage and cook ten minutes longer.
5. Salt and pepper to taste.

title: Stewed Calfs Head—Half
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 calf's head
- 1 quart water
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- tooth of garlic
- 2 cloves
- 2 allspice
- marjoram
- thyme
- parsley bunch
- rind of lemon
- small meat balls
- egg balls
- mushrooms
- browning
- juice of 1/2 large lemon
- 2 eggs
- lemon slices
- green parsley
- beetroot strips

## Directions

1. Take half calf's head and put it in a stewpan with quart of water, salt, pepper, onion, tooth of garlic, 2 cloves, 2 allspice, marjoram, thyme and parsley a bunch, and a little rind of lemon
2. Let it simmer gently till tender, not to break at all
3. Do it the day before you want it
4. When done take out the head carefully and take out the brains
5. Put them by themselves with the half tongue
6. Strain all the liquor into a basin
7. Put half of the liquor in a flat stewpan and put the head carefully
8. Make a few small meat balls and boil them separately
9. Add a few egg balls and a few mushrooms
10. Let it all simmer together
11. Put in a little browning to make it quite a light brown
12. The liquor must be quite transparent and will be quite like a syrup
13. Remember that it must be very nicely seasoned
14. Do not forget the juice of half a large lemon well strained
15. Put that in when you stir in the browning
16. Be very particular in the dishing up
17. Place the half head in the centre of the dish with the cheek up
18. Place the balls and mushrooms alternately
19. Remember the mushrooms must be buttons
20. Boil two eggs very hard
21. When cold cut them in slices in a plate and cut some thin slices of lemon
22. Chop a little green parsley
23. Put a slice of lemon, on top of that a slice of hard egg, on the egg a little chopped parsley, and on the top cross it with narrow strips of beetroot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Calves' Feet with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 calves' feet
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- marjoram
- pint of water
- meat balls
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut 2 calves' feet in several pieces
2. Slice an onion in a stewpan with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and a little marjoram
3. Put in the feet with a pint of water
4. Give them a quick boil to skim the liquor
5. Let them cook slowly for 2 hours
6. Have some meat balls, not too small
7. When the feet are very tender, put in the sauce made as directed before with a little chopped parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Cranberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of sugar
- one cup of water
- three cups of cranberries

## Directions

1. Boil together one and one-half cups of sugar and one cup of water for seven minutes
2. Add three cups of cranberries, well washed and picked
3. Cook until the berries burst

title: Stewed Fillets of Soles with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fillets of soles
- onions
- 2 spoonfuls of oil
- a cup of boiling water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1 spoonful of flour
- 2 eggs
- juice of a large or 2 small lemons
- a little dried saffron
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the fillets as for frying
2. Slice in a stewpan some onions with 2 spoonfuls of oil, let it fry
3. Then add a cup of boiling water
4. Lay in the fillets with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
5. Let them cook for an hour
6. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour, 2 eggs, the juice of a large or 2 small lemons, a little dried saffron to colour
7. Rub it quite smooth
8. Turn it into the saucepan
9. Dish the fillets up and lay the sauce over them
10. Garnish with a little parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Fish Balls with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. cod
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- lemon rind
- parsley
- marjoram
- soaked bread
- 1 egg
- 1 large onion
- 3 tablespoonfuls salad oil
- 1 teacupful boiling water
- 3 eggs
- 2 large lemons
- dried saffron
- chopped parsley
- 3 or 4 tomatoes

## Directions

1. Take 2 lbs. of cod and free it from all the bones.
2. Chop it and season it with pepper, salt, grated nutmeg, and a little of the rind of lemon chopped fine, parsley, and marjoram, a little soaked bread, with the water drained from it.
3. Mix well together with an egg, make them into nice-sized balls the size of an apple.
4. Slice in the stewpan a large onion, add 3 tablespoonfuls of salad oil, let it fry.
5. Add a teacupful of boiling water, let it boil up, put in the balls.
6. When done beat in a basin 3 eggs, strain the juice of 2 large lemons, with a little dried saffron and a little chopped parsley.
7. Stir and mix it all well together.
8. Dish them up by first taking out the balls, then strain the sauce over them.
9. Garnish with parsley.
10. 3 or 4 tomatoes added to the balls is a great improvement and makes it a pretty dish.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Fowl—White
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 1 onion
- a tooth of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- a little dried saffron
- a few slices of smoked beef or worsht
- 1/2 pint water
- egg sauce
- forced meats balls
- chopped green parsley
- a few white button mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut a fowl in pieces, the same as if you were carving the pieces for table from a roast fowl.
2. Chop in a stewpan an onion small, with a tooth of garlic, salt and pepper, with grated nutmeg, a little dried saffron, and a few slices of smoked beef or worsht.
3. Lay in the fowl with 1/2 pint water, let it simmer till done.
4. Have ready an egg sauce according to recipe, pour it over the stewed fowl, baste it backwards and forwards with a spoon so that it covers the fowl.
5. Cook a few small forced meats balls with, and after you have put in the sauce, be sure that it does not boil, or you would curdle the sauce.
6. Dish it up nicely, sprinkle over some chopped green parsley.
7. A few white button mushrooms are very nice with this stew.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Giblets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or more sets of giblets
- veal or chicken
- good gravy
- browning
- mushroom powder
- lemon-juice
- one glass of white wine
- forcemeat balls
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Scald one or more sets of giblets
2. Set them on the fire with a little veal or chicken or both in a good gravy
3. Season to taste and thicken the gravy
4. Color it with browning
5. Flavor with mushroom powder and lemon-juice and one glass of white wine
6. Add forcemeat balls a few minutes before serving
7. Garnish with thin slices of hard boiled eggs

title: Stewed Goose, Piquante
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- lemon peel
- goose fat
- flour
- vinegar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut up, after being skinned
2. Stew, seasoning with salt, pepper, a few cloves and a very little lemon peel
3. When done heat a little goose fat in a frying-pan
4. Brown half a tablespoon of flour
5. Add a little vinegar and the juice of half a lemon

title: Stewed Guard Fish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- large onion
- oil or butter
- guard fish
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- dried saffron
- flour
- lemon juice
- parsley

## Directions

1. Chop very fine a large onion and put it in a stewpan with 2 spoonfuls of oil or ½ lb. butter
2. Cut the guard fish and lay it in with salt and pepper, a little grated nutmeg, a little dried saffron
3. Put it on to stew
4. When done rub in a basin a dessert spoonful of flour with the juice of a lemon
5. Turn it over the fish
6. When dished up, chop some parsley and sprinkle it over
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Haddock with Turnip or Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddock
- onion
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- turnips
- water
- flour
- grated nutmeg
- dried saffron
- cayenne
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and clean well a fine haddock
2. Cut it in 7 or 8 pieces, do not slit it open
3. Put in a stewpan some onion sliced with 2 spoonfuls of oil, salt, pepper
4. Let it fry
5. Peel and cut some turnips as for stewed cod
6. Put the pieces in with a teacup of water
7. Let them boil till nearly tender
8. Put in the pieces of haddock
9. When cooked, take out the haddock and lay it in the dish ready
10. Stir in a basin a spoonful of flour, a little grated nutmeg, a little dried saffron, a little cayenne, and a little chopped parsley
11. Rub it all smoothly
12. Stir it in the stewpan with the turnip till it is a nice thickness
13. Place the turnip round the fish and spoon the sauce over both
14. Garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Knuckle of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a knuckle of veal
- a chopped onion
- salt
- pepper
- a blade of mace
- a bunch of marjoram
- lemon thyme
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put a knuckle of veal in a stewpan with a chopped onion, salt, pepper, a blade of mace, a bunch of marjoram, lemon thyme, and parsley
2. Let this cook for 3 hours
3. Remove the bunch of herbs when the meat is cooked
4. Make a sauce and throw over it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Mackerel with Winter Savory, Spanish Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- garlic
- oil
- lemon juice or vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- winter savory
- saffron
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and split the mackerel in halves
2. cut the halves in two
3. dry them in a cloth
4. cut in a stewpan 2 or 3 teeth of garlic, with 3 spoonfuls of oil
5. let it fry
6. take out the garlic when fried
7. put on the juice of 3 lemons or 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar, salt, pepper, and a little cayenne
8. lay in the mackerel with a spoonful of the dried leaves of winter savory, a little dried saffron
9. let it boil quickly for an hour
10. dish the mackerel up
11. pour the gravy over
12. garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Milt
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- milt
- soup meat
- cold water
- one tablespoon of drippings
- a clove of garlic
- one tablespoon of flour
- pepper
- salt
- soup stock
- a few caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Clean the milt thoroughly and boil with your soup meat
2. Set to boil with cold water and let it boil about two hours
3. Take it out and cut into finger lengths
4. Heat one tablespoon of drippings in a spider
5. When hot cut up a clove of garlic very fine and brown slightly in the fat
6. Add a tablespoon of flour, stirring briskly, pepper and salt to taste and thin with soup stock
7. Add the pieces of milt and let it simmer slowly
8. If the sauce is too thick add more water or soup stock
9. Some add a few caraway seeds instead of the garlic, which is a matter of taste

title: Stewed Mutton with Turnips and Potatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lb. of best end neck of mutton
- pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- three large turnips
- large onion
- 3 or 4 potatoes
- flour
- cold water
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Put 3 lb. of best end neck of mutton, cut off the fat into a stewpan with a pint of water, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg
2. Peel three large turnips and cut in quarters
3. Slice a large onion
4. Let it all simmer for two hours very gently
5. Half an hour before the meat is done peel and halve 3 or 4 potatoes
6. Lay them on top so that the steam from the stew will cook them
7. Be sure that they do not break when ready for dinner
8. Put the mutton in the dish, turnips and potatoes round
9. Skim off the fat
10. Rub in a small basin a spoonful of flour with a little cold water
11. Turn it into the stewpan and let it have one boil
12. Put it over the mutton and vegetables
13. Chop a little green parsley and throw over the whole
14. Send up very hot with hot plates
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Ox Tail
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- an ox tail, cut small
- salt
- pepper
- chopped onion
- a tooth of garlic
- a little marjoram
- 1 pint of water
- meat balls, made the size of an egg
- 3 tomatoes
- a dessert spoonful of flour
- the juice of a lemon
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Put the ox tail in a stewpan with salt, pepper, chopped onion, and a tooth of garlic, a little marjoram.
2. Put it on to stew with 1 pint of water and have some meat balls, made the size of an egg, to cook with it.
3. Will take 1½ hours to get tender; half-an-hour before it is done cut 3 tomatoes in halves, put on the top to cook.
4. When the meat is done lay the halves of tomatoes on a plate, rub in a basin a dessert spoonful of flour, the juice of a lemon, a little browning to colour, mix it in with the stew.
5. Arrange it prettily in the dish, the tail in the middle of the dish, the balls and tomatoes round.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Pears
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen fine large baking pears
- half a pound of white sugar
- sufficient cold water
- a small quantity of the peelings
- a few cloves
- a little cochineal

## Directions

1. Peel, core, and quarter a dozen fine large baking pears
2. Put them into a stewpan with half a pound of white sugar and sufficient cold water to cover them
3. Add a small quantity of the peelings, a few cloves, and a little cochineal tied up in a muslin bag
4. Let them stew gently and closely covered until tender

title: Stewed Peppers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- peppers
- salt
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Cut the peppers in half and remove the seeds, stems and pith
2. cut them in neat, small pieces and throw into boiling salted water
3. Boil for half an hour
4. Drain them and then add salt to taste
5. add one tablespoon of butter and four tablespoons of cream to four peppers
6. Heat thoroughly and serve

title: Stewed Pigeons
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- chopped onion
- garlic
- marjoram
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- pickled walnut
- half a pint of water
- small meat balls
- 2 or 3 small mushrooms
- flour
- juice of half a lemon
- hard eggs

## Directions

1. Cut them in quarters, put them in a stewpan
2. Add chopped onion, garlic, marjoram, thyme, salt, pepper, a little cayenne, a pickled walnut cut in pieces, with half a pint of water
3. Let it stew till done, about half an hour if very young
4. Put in a very few small meat balls, and 2 or 3 small mushrooms chopped
5. When done rub in a basin a dessert spoonful of flour, the juice of half a lemon, and put it into the stew
6. Garnish with slices of hard eggs
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Pippins
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pippins
- a little water
- white sugar
- a little lemon peel
- preserve

## Directions

1. Peel the pippins
2. Stew them gently with a little water, white sugar, and a little lemon peel
3. Use preserve to ornament the top of each apple
4. They should when done look white and rather transparent

title: Stewed Pippins
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pippins
- water
- white sugar
- lemon peel
- preserve

## Directions

1. Peel the pippins and stew them gently with a little water, white sugar, and a little lemon peel
2. Preserve is usually used to ornament the top of each apple
3. They should, when done, look white and rather transparent

title: Stewed Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- milk
- butter
- parsley
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Pare and quarter, and put on to boil.
2. When almost done drain off the water, add one cup of milk, one tablespoon of butter, a little chopped parsley and cook a while longer.
3. Thicken with a little flour (wet with cold water or milk), stir, and take from the fire.

title: Stewed Potatoes with Onions
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small potatoes
- one onion
- goose-oil or drippings from roast meat
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Take small potatoes, pare and wash them very clean
2. Use one onion to about ten potatoes
3. Add goose-oil or any kind of drippings from roast meat
4. Put them in a pot or spider
5. When hot cut up an onion very fine and add to the boiling fat
6. Add the potatoes
7. Salt and pepper to taste
8. Pour some water over all
9. Cover up tight and let them simmer for about three-quarters of an hour

title: Stewed Potatoes, Sour
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tablespoon of drippings
- onion
- potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- soup stock
- vinegar
- parsley
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a tablespoon of drippings in a kettle, and when it is hot cut up an onion fine and fry in the hot fat, cover closely.
2. Put in potatoes, which have been previously pared, washed, quartered and well salted.
3. Cover them tight and stew slowly until soft, stirring them occasionally.
4. Heat in a spider a little drippings.
5. Brown in this a spoon of flour and add some soup stock, vinegar and chopped parsley.
6. Pour this over the potatoes, boil up once and serve.

title: Stewed Potatoes, Sour
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- drippings
- onion
- potatoes
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Put a tablespoon of drippings in a kettle
2. When it is hot cut up an onion fine and fry in the hot fat
3. Cover closely
4. Put in potatoes, which have been previously pared, washed, quartered and well salted
5. Cover them tight and stew slowly until soft, stirring them occasionally
6. Heat in a spider a little drippings
7. Brown in this a spoon of flour

title: Stewed Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- prunes
- water
- granulated sugar
- claret or sauterne
- lemon or orange

## Directions

1. Cleanse thoroughly
2. Soak in water ten or twelve hours, adding a little granulated sugar when putting to soak
3. After soaking, the fruit will assume its full size
4. Simmer on the back of the stove
5. Do not boil prunes
6. Simmer, simmer only
7. Keep lid on
8. Shake gently
9. Do not stir
10. Never let boil
11. When tender they are ready for table
12. Serve cold
13. A little cream will make them more delicious
14. A little claret or sauterne poured over the prunes just as cooking is finished adds a flavor relished by many
15. Added just before simmering, a little sliced lemon or orange gives a rich color and flavor to the syrup

title: Stewed Red Cabbage
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- a large piece of butter
- a tea cup full of water
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and remove the outer leaves, slice it as thinly as possible
2. put it in a saucepan with a large piece of butter, and a tea cup full of water, salt and pepper
3. let it stew slowly till very tender

title: Stewed Red Mullet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- garlic
- oil
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- saffron
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the mullets
2. let them dry in a cloth
3. put in a stewpan a tooth of garlic fried in 2 spoonfuls of oil
4. when it fries a light colour take out the garlic
5. put in the juice of a large lemon
6. add a little salt, pepper, and a little cayenne, dried saffron to colour
7. put in the mullets
8. cook for half an hour
9. dish up
10. garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Rhubarb
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- sugar
- rind of lemon
- almond milk
- eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stew the rhubarb, cut very thin and peeled, with plenty of sugar and rind of lemon
2. Turn it in a dish
3. Make a custard of almond milk and eggs, flavoured with cinnamon
4. Pour over it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Rice to be Eaten with Stewed Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- an onion
- a tablespoonful of oil
- a pint of water
- a little salt
- pepper
- a little dried saffron
- a teacupful of rice
- 3 or 4 tomatoes

## Directions

1. Slice an onion in a small saucepan with a tablespoonful of oil
2. Let it fry
3. Put in a pint of water, a little salt, pepper, and a little dried saffron
4. Wash a teacupful of rice
5. Slice in 3 or 4 tomatoes
6. Let it simmer at the side of the fire till it has absorbed all the water and the rice is quite firm and dry
7. Dish this up round the knuckle of veal
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Sheep's Hearts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 sheep's hearts
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- marjoram
- water
- flour
- vinegar
- parsley
- saffron
- Jerusalem artichokes

## Directions

1. Clean the hearts well after soaking
2. Clear off all the blood settled in them
3. Cut them in slices and lay them in a stewpan with salt, pepper, onion, marjoram, and 1/2 pint of water
4. Let it cook well
5. Stir in a basin a spoonful of flour, 2 of vinegar, a little chopped parsley, and some saffron to colour
6. A few Jerusalem artichokes stewed with are very nice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Sheep's Tongues
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 sheep's tongues
- ½ pint of clear stock
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- garlic
- 3 cloves
- 3 allspice
- meat balls
- carrot
- dessert-spoonful of flour
- brown sugar
- vinegar
- browning

## Directions

1. Boil 4 sheep's tongues till tender enough to skin
2. Have ready in a stewpan ½ pint of clear stock, pepper, salt, an onion chopped, a tooth of garlic, 3 cloves, 3 allspice
3. Put in the tongues when skinned, and as soon as it boils put in a few meat balls
4. Boil a carrot with it
5. When quite done put a sieve over a deep dish or basin, lay the tongues and balls in the under dish, strain and rub the liquor through the sieve over them
6. Put back the tongues and balls, and turn the gravy over them
7. Cut the carrot in strips 2 inches long and 1 wide
8. Rub in a basin a dessert-spoonful of flour, one of brown sugar, one of vinegar, and a little browning to colour
9. Stir it in the liquor quite smooth
10. Dish it up very nicely, arranging the balls and carrots
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Shin of Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 lb. fleshy part of shin of beef
- 2 or 3 large sliced onions
- salt
- pepper
- 1 blade of mace
- marjoram
- dried saffron
- 1 pint water
- potatoes
- 1 spoonful flour
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut off all the flesh and cut it in nice pieces
2. Put pieces in a saucepan with 2 or 3 sliced onions, salt, pepper, a blade of mace, a little marjoram, a little dried saffron, and a pint of water
3. Half an hour before it is done, cut some potatoes in halves and lay them on top of the meat so they will cook with the steam
4. When quite done, rub a spoonful of flour with 2 spoonfuls of vinegar in a basin and throw it over
5. Serve as a relishing family dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Soles with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- onion
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- oil or butter
- red tomatoes
- lemon
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Slice in a stewpan an onion very thin, a tooth of garlic, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
2. Add 2 spoonfuls of oil or 2 oz. of butter, let it fry for 3 minutes
3. Lay in the soles with white skin on top with 4 or 5 red tomatoes sliced on the soles
4. Let it cook for 20 minutes, or 1 an hour if the soles are large
5. Take out the soles very carefully, lay them on the dish you intend serving them up
6. Rub the liquor through a sieve
7. When strained add the juice of a lemon and a little cayenne
8. Put it back in the saucepan to get hot, then lay it over the soles
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Spinach
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- brown sugar
- vinegar
- poached eggs or small forcemeat fritters

## Directions

1. Scald and chop some spinach small
2. Cut up an onion
3. Add pepper and salt and brown sugar, with a little vinegar
4. Stew all together gently
5. Serve with poached eggs or small forcemeat fritters

title: Stewed Squash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- cold water
- milk or cream
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel squash and cut in quarters
2. put on to boil in cold water and cook until tender
3. Drain and mash fine and smooth
4. add one-half cup of milk or cream, one tablespoon of butter, pinch of salt and pepper
5. put back on stove to keep hot
6. Beat well with a spoon to make light and smooth

title: Stewed Steak with Carrots and Turnips
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- steak
- carrot
- 2 turnips
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- teaspoonful of flour
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan the steak you intend to stew
2. Cut up a carrot in strips of 2 inches long
3. Cut 2 turnips in pieces of an equal size
4. Add an onion with salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
5. Let it all stew till tender
6. Thicken with a teaspoonful of flour and a little dried saffron
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Sweetbreads
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pair of sweetbreads
- warm water
- boiling water
- two cups of veal stock
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- one-half lemon
- one teaspoon of potato flour

## Directions

1. Soak one pair of sweetbreads for two or three hours in sufficient warm water to cover them, then drain
2. Put them in a stew-pan, with boiling water to cover them, and then boil gently for seven or eight minutes
3. Lay the sweetbreads in a stew-pan, pour two cups of veal stock over them
4. Add salt and cayenne pepper to taste, and simmer gently for one hour
5. Lift them out on to a very hot dish
6. Add juice of one-half lemon and one teaspoon of potato flour to the gravy
7. Stir smoothly, and boil up
8. Pour over the sweetbreads and serve at once

title: Stewed Sweetbreads with Egg Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 throat sweetbreads
- boiling water
- fat of smoked beef
- onion
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- small meat balls
- button mushrooms

## Directions

1. Take 2 or 3 throat sweetbreads and put them in boiling water to plump them up
2. Take them out and put them on your paste board and shape them nicely
3. Cut a piece of the fat of smoked beef in length of two inches
4. Put each piece through the larding needle and run it through the sweetbread till it looks like a porcupine
5. Place the sweetbreads in your stewpan with an onion sliced, a little marjoram, salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
6. Put in a few small meat balls and a few button mushrooms
7. Boil the mushrooms first so that they do not make the stew dark
8. Make an egg sauce as before directed
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stewed Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- boiling water
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the tomatoes; remove the skins; cut into small pieces and place in a saucepan over the fire.
2. Boil gently for twenty or thirty minutes and season, allowing for each quart of tomatoes one generous teaspoon each of salt and sugar and one tablespoon of butter.
3. If in addition to this seasoning a slice of onion has been cooked with the tomatoes from the beginning, the flavor will be greatly improved.

title: Stewed Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one small onion
- half a green pepper
- one tablespoon of butter
- six tomatoes
- sugar
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- one cup of thick cream
- toast

## Directions

1. Chop onion and green pepper fine and cook until tender in a tablespoon of butter
2. Cut six tomatoes in half, sprinkle with a little sugar, season on both sides with salt, pepper and a little flour
3. Put them into the pan with skin-side down to cook partially, then turn them once over a slow fire
4. Sprinkle one tablespoon of chopped parsley over them
5. Pour in one cup of thick cream and when this has become thoroughly hot and has been combined with the other ingredients, the tomatoes are ready to serve
6. Place half a tomato on a slice of toast with sufficient gravy to moisten

title: Stewed Veal
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- salt
- ground ginger
- parsley
- caraway seed

## Directions

1. Prepare as above, but do not have the meat cut in small pieces
2. If desired one-half teaspoon of caraway seed may be used instead of the parsley
3. Mashed potatoes and green peas or stewed tomatoes are usually served with veal
4. Any of the flour or potato dumplings are excellent served with stewed or fricasseed veal

title: Stirred Eggs with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 tomatoes
- 1/2 oz. of butter
- 4 or 5 eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil 2 or 3 tomatoes, that is to say, cut them in slices
2. Put them in a small saucepan at the side of the fire with no water
3. Pulp them through your sieve kept for butter purposes
4. Put in another small saucepan 1/2 oz. of butter to fry
5. Then put in the tomato
6. Beat 4 or 5 eggs with salt and pepper
7. Stir it with the butter and tomatoes till done
8. Send up in a hot dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stock for Brown Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- the strained white stock
- a bullock's foot
- bunch of dried herbs such as marjoram, lemon thyme, parsley
- a little basil
- a large onion
- a tooth of garlic
- 3 or 4 cloves
- a few allspice
- pepper
- salt
- a carrot
- sticks of celery
- any cooked bones
- crust of bread
- 2 shalots
- 3 quarts of water

## Directions

1. Put the strained white stock back into the stock-pot with a bullock's foot, a bunch of dried herbs, a large onion, a tooth of garlic, cloves, allspice, pepper, salt, a carrot, sticks of celery, any cooked bones, crust of bread, and 2 shalots.
2. Add 3 quarts of water.
3. Let it simmer, without stopping, on the hob for 4 or 5 hours.
4. Take out the foot, remove the flesh from it, and put it in the larder.
5. Return the bones, and let it simmer for two hours longer.
6. Stir it all well up with the kitchen ladle.
7. Strain it into a pan, rubbing the ingredients well through the sieve.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stock for White Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Knuckle of veal
- a brisket bone
- 3-lb. of clod
- 4 quarts of water
- pepper
- salt
- a blade of mace
- 1 large onion
- a tooth of garlic
- 1 leek
- 1 carrot
- 1 turnip
- 2 or 3 sticks of celery

## Directions

1. Put knuckle of veal, brisket bone, and clod into a very clean stock-pot
2. Add 4 quarts of water, pepper, salt, a blade of mace, 1 large onion, a tooth of garlic, 1 leek, 1 carrot, 1 turnip, and 2 or 3 sticks of celery
3. Let it boil up quick and skim it well
4. Put it at the side to simmer for 3 hours
5. Remember that fast boiling spoils everything
6. After it is well boiled and you find the meat leaves the bones, strain it through a nice clean sieve
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Stock or Consomme
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shin of beef or ox-cheek
- good gravy-beef
- bones
- water
- gravy beef
- knuckle of veal
- veal or white poultry
- bones and trimmings of meat or poultry

## Directions

1. Break the bones and cut up the meat, as the juices are better extracted
2. Put on, at first, but very little water, and add more when the first quantity is nearly dried up
3. Boil six pounds of meat for about five hours; if bones, the same quantity will require double the time
4. Very tolerable stock can be procured by boiling down bones and the trimmings of meat or poultry
5. The liquor in which beef or mutton intended for the table has been boiled will also, with small additions and skilful flavoring, make an excellent soup at a trifling expense

title: Stollen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- one cake of compressed yeast
- one teaspoon of salt
- one pint of lukewarm milk
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-half pound of creamed butter
- one-quarter of a pound of seeded raisins
- one-quarter of a pound of sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- four ounces of powdered almonds
- grated peel of a lemon
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Sift two pounds of flour into a bowl and set a sponge in it with one cake of compressed yeast, one teaspoon of salt, one pint of lukewarm milk and one tablespoon of sugar
2. When this has risen, add one-half pound of creamed butter, a quarter of a pound of seeded raisins and one-quarter of a pound of sugar, yolks of four eggs, four ounces of powdered almonds, and the grated peel of a lemon
3. Work all well, beating with the hands, not kneading
4. Let this dough rise at least three hours
5. Roll, press down the centre and fold over double
6. Form into one or two long loaves, narrow at the end
7. Brush the top with melted butter, let rise again and bake three-quarters of an hour in a moderate oven

title: Strawberries
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe strawberries
- pulverized sugar
- oranges

## Directions

1. Pick nice ripe berries
2. Pile them in a fruit dish
3. Strew plenty of pulverized sugar over them
4. Garnish with round slices or quarters of oranges, also well sugared

title: Strawberries A La Bridge
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large strawberries
- sugar
- marshmallows
- whipped cream
- shelled pecans

## Directions

1. Into a champagne-glass put large strawberries, halved and sugared, and an equal amount of marshmallows halved.
2. Place on top a mass of whipped cream, already sweetened and flavored.
3. Top with a single strawberry, sprinkle with shelled pecans.

title: Strawberries and Pineapple
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- strawberries
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Follow the recipe for Preserved Strawberries, using two-thirds pineapple and one-third strawberries

title: Strawberries in the Sun
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- berries
- water

## Directions

1. To two pounds of berries take two pounds of sugar and three-quarters cup of water
2. Put the syrup in the preserving kettle; bring it to a boil and cook for about ten minutes, or until it begins to thicken
3. Add the berries; cook for ten minutes and pour them out in shallow dishes or meat platters
4. Cover with sheets of glass, allowing a little air for ventilation; place in the sun until the juice is thick and syrupy
5. This will take two days or more
6. Put into small jars or tumblers and cover according to directions

title: Strawberries Preserved Whole
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- white sugar powdered
- red currant juice

## Directions

1. Weigh an equal quantity of fruit and white sugar powdered.
2. Sift all the sugar over the fruit, so that half of it shall equally be covered.
3. Let it lay till the next day.
4. Boil the remainder with red currant juice.
5. Simmer the strawberries in the red currant juice until the jelly hangs about them.
6. Put the strawberries into pots, taking care not to break them.
7. Pour over the syrup.

title: Strawberry Cocktail
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six large strawberries per glass
- orange juice
- powdered sugar
- shaved ice

## Directions

1. Slice five or six large strawberries into each glass and squeeze over them the juice of an orange
2. At serving time add one heaping teaspoon of powdered sugar and one tablespoon of shaved ice

title: Strawberry Dessert
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroons
- wine
- a box or quart of strawberries
- sugar
- yolks of four eggs
- one small cup of sugar
- grated rind of lemon
- half its juice
- egg whites
- whipped or plain cream

## Directions

1. Line a dish with macaroons, wet them with wine, put over this a box or quart of strawberries, and sugar them well.
2. Beat the yolks of four eggs with one small cup of sugar, grated rind of lemon and half its juice.
3. Beat the whites to a stiff froth, and half the yolks; pour over all in your pudding dish.
4. When baked spread the other half of the whites on top, having previously sweetened the remaining whites with sugar.
5. Bake a light brown.
6. Eat cold with whipped or plain cream.

title: Strawberry Ice Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of strawberries
- juice of 1/2 a lemon
- 4 oz. of sugar
- 1 pint of cream

## Directions

1. Mash 1/2 pint of strawberries through a sieve
2. Mix with them the juice of 1/2 a lemon and 4 oz. of sugar
3. Add this to a pint of cream
4. Do not boil it, put it in the freezer and work it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Strawberry Jam
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- six pounds of fine fresh fruit
- very little water
- powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Bruise gently, with the back of a wooden spoon, six pounds of fine fresh fruit.
2. Boil them with very little water for twenty minutes, stirring until the fruit and juice are well mixed.
3. Put in powdered loaf sugar of equal weight to the fruit.
4. Simmer half an hour longer.
5. If the preserve is not required to be very rich, half the weight of sugar in proportion to the quantity of fruit may be used; but more boiling will be requisite.

title: Strawberry Jelly
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five quarts strawberries
- one quart currants
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. To five quarts of strawberries add one quart of currants and proceed as with Currant Jelly; but boil fifteen minutes.

title: Strawberry Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich fleishchig pie-crust
- one quart of berries
- four eggs
- one-half cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Make a rich fleishchig pie-crust and bake on the reverse side of pie-pan.
2. Pick a quart of berries, wash and drain, then sugar.
3. Take the yolks of four eggs beaten well with one-half cup of sugar and stir the beaten whites gently into this mixture.
4. Pour over strawberries.
5. Put in pie-crust and bake until brown.

title: Strawberry Sherbet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of ripe strawberries
- a quart of water
- juice of two lemons
- a pound of sugar
- one tablespoon of rose-water
- chopped ice

## Directions

1. Crush a quart of ripe strawberries, pour a quart of water over them, and add the juice of two lemons.
2. Let this stand about two hours, then strain over a pound of sugar, stir until the sugar is dissolved, and then set upon ice.
3. You may add one tablespoon of rose-water.
4. Serve with chopped ice.

title: Strawberry Shortcake (Biscuit Dough)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- four teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one-quarter cup of butter
- three-quarters of a cup of milk
- one to one and one-half boxes of strawberries
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix flour, baking-powder, salt, and sugar
2. Work butter in with tips of fingers
3. Add milk gradually
4. Toss on floured board and divide in two parts
5. Pat and roll out
6. Bake twelve minutes in hot oven in layer-cake tins
7. Split and spread with butter
8. Pick, hull, and drain berries
9. Sweeten strawberries to taste
10. Crush slightly and put between and on top of short cake
11. Serve with cream, plain or whipped

title: Strawberry Shortcake with Matzoth-Meal
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of four eggs
- three-quarters of a cup sugar
- rind of one-half lemon
- a pinch of salt
- one-half cup of sifted matzoth meal
- whites of the eggs
- strawberries
- cream

## Directions

1. Beat until very light the yolks of four eggs and three-quarters of a cup sugar
2. Add rind of one-half lemon, a pinch of salt, one-half cup of sifted matzoth meal, and last the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
3. Bake in two shallow square pans in a moderate oven
4. When cold lay a cake on a platter, spread thickly with strawberries that have been well sugared
5. Put the other cake on top
6. Spread over the top and sides with cream that has been sweetened, flavored and whipped very stiff

title: String Bean Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart beans
- salt
- one tablespoon flour
- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons strained lemon juice

## Directions

1. String and remove the ends from one quart of beans. Cut into short lengths. Cover with boiling water, add one level tablespoon of salt and cook until tender, but not soft. Drain and save one cup of the liquor. Cream one tablespoon of flour with two tablespoons of butter. Pour the liquid over the flour and butter, stirring constantly to avoid lumping. Cook this sauce for five minutes, remove from stove and stir in two tablespoons of strained lemon juice. Pour this over the beans and serve.

title: String Beans (Raw)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- salt

## Directions

1. String the beans very carefully, and cut into fine short lengths
2. Sprinkle salt over and through them, mixing thoroughly, say to twenty-five pounds of beans, two pounds of salt
3. Let them remain in the salt overnight
4. Pack the shredded beans as tightly as possible into jars or kegs, without any of their juice
5. In two weeks look them over, remove the cloth and wash it
6. When cooking the beans, take out as many as may be required for a meal and soak them in cold water overnight
7. In the morning set on to boil in cold water
8. Boil for one hour

title: String Beans with Lamb
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small breast of lamb
- two large onions
- one-quarter peck of beans
- six large tomatoes
- two cups of water
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Take a small breast of lamb, two large onions, one-quarter peck of beans (string and cut in long thin pieces)
2. Skin six large tomatoes
3. Add two cups of water
4. Cook until the beans are tender
5. Add one tablespoon of flour to thicken

title: String Beans with Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- salt water
- fresh water
- butter
- three or four large potatoes
- four large tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut off both ends of the beans, string them carefully and break into pieces about an inch in length
2. Boil in salt water
3. When tender drain off this brine and add fresh water boiling from the kettle
4. Add a piece of butter and three or four large potatoes cut into squares
5. Add four large tomatoes, cut up
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a spider, stir into it one tablespoon of flour
8. Thin with milk and add this to the beans

title: String or Green Snap Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- string or green snap beans
- cold water
- salt
- one tablespoon of drippings
- part of an onion
- parsley
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt
- pepper
- meat gravy or soup stock
- potatoes
- cream or milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the tops and bottoms and string carefully
2. Break the beans in pieces about an inch long
3. Lay them in cold water with a little salt for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Heat one tablespoon of drippings in a stew-pan
5. Cut up part of an onion and some parsley in the stew-pan, cover and stew about ten minutes
6. Drain the beans, put into the stew-pan and stew until tender
7. Add one tablespoon of flour and season with salt and pepper
8. Meat gravy or soup stock will improve them
9. You may pare about half a dozen potatoes, cut into dice shape, and add to the beans
10. You may add cream or milk instead of soup stock and use butter

title: String or Wax-Beans, Sweet and Sour
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- string or wax-beans
- boiling water
- five sour apples per quart of beans
- thin peel of one lemon
- juice of one and one-half lemons
- small teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons of cider vinegar
- quarter cup of granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Put the beans into sufficient boiling water to just cover them
2. Cook for one hour and a half to two hours, depending upon the tenderness of the beans
3. Meanwhile, prepare five sour apples for each quart of beans; peel, core and cut in pieces
4. When the beans are done, add the apples, the thin peel of one lemon, the juice of one and one-half lemons, a small teaspoon of salt, and two tablespoons of cider vinegar
5. Let the apples cook on top of the beans until they are thoroughly done
6. Mix well with a good quarter cup of granulated sugar
7. Serve the next day warmed up for best results

title: Strudel Aus Kalbslunge
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lung and heart
- salt
- one onion
- a few bay leaves
- strudel dough
- fat
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- mace or nutmeg
- grated peel of half a lemon
- wine
- yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the lung and heart thoroughly in salt water, and put on to boil in cold water, adding salt, one onion, a few bay leaves and cook until very tender
2. Make the dough precisely the same as any other strudel
3. Take the boiled lung and heart, chop them as fine as possible and stew in a saucepan with some fat, adding chopped parsley, a little salt, pepper and mace, or nutmeg, the grated peel of half a lemon and a little wine
4. Add the beaten yolks of two eggs to thicken, and remove from the fire to cool
5. Roll out the dough as thin as possible
6. Fill in the mixture and lay the strudel in a well-greased pan
7. Put flakes of fat on top and baste often
8. Eat hot

title: Stuffed Chicken (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- almonds
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Steam chicken and when it is almost tender stuff it with the following: Take one-fourth pound of almonds, chopped; season with parsley, pepper and salt to taste, add one tablespoon of bread crumbs and bind this with one well-beaten egg.
2. Put chicken in roasting-pan and roast until done.

title: Stuffed Cucumbers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four cucumbers
- vinegar
- one cup pecans or Brazil nuts
- six tablespoons mashed potatoes
- one egg
- one teaspoon salt
- two tablespoons chopped parsley
- one saltspoon white pepper
- dash of nutmeg
- two tablespoons melted butter
- one cup white sauce
- dash of powdered cloves
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut four cucumbers in half lengthwise; remove the seeds with a spoon.
2. Lay the cucumbers in vinegar overnight.
3. Wipe dry and fill with a mixture made from one cup pecans or Brazil nuts chopped, six tablespoons of mashed potatoes, one well-beaten egg, one teaspoon of salt, two tablespoons of chopped parsley, one saltspoon of white pepper, dash of nutmeg and two tablespoons of melted butter.
4. Bake in a buttered dish until tender.
5. Serve hot with one cup of white sauce, dash of powdered cloves, one well-beaten egg, salt and pepper to taste.

title: Stuffed Dates
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Dates
- English walnuts, blanched almonds, or pecans
- Granulated sugar or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make a cut the entire length of dates and remove stones
2. Fill cavities with English walnuts, blanched almonds, pecans or with a mixture of chopped nuts
3. Shape in original form
4. Roll in granulated sugar or powdered sugar and serve on small plate or bonbon dish

title: Stuffed Eggs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- one tablespoon melted butter per six yolks
- one-half teaspoon mustard per six yolks
- one teaspoon salt per six yolks
- dash of cayenne pepper per six yolks

## Directions

1. Hard boil eggs, drop into cold water, remove shells, cut each in half lengthwise
2. Turn out yolks into a bowl
3. Carefully place whites together in pairs, mash yolks with back of a spoon
4. For every six yolks put into bowl one tablespoon melted butter, one-half teaspoon mustard, one teaspoon salt, dash of cayenne pepper
5. Rub these together thoroughly with yolks
6. Make little balls of this paste the size of the yolks
7. Fit one ball into each pair whites

title: Stuffed Figs
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried figs
- One-fourth cup of the pulp
- One-fourth cup of finely-chopped crystallized ginger
- One teaspoon of grated orange or lemon rind
- One tablespoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a slit in the side of dried figs
2. Take out some of the pulp with the tip of a teaspoon
3. Mix with one-fourth cup of the pulp, one-fourth cup of finely-chopped crystallized ginger, a teaspoon of grated orange or lemon rind and a tablespoon of lemon juice
