4. Fill the figs with the mixture, stuffing them so that they look plump

title: Stuffed Goose Neck
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fat skin from neck of a fat goose
- raw goose meat from breast or legs
- soft or linda fat
- garlic
- onion
- egg
- soaked bread
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- cold water

## Directions

1. Remove the fat skin from the neck of a fat goose, being careful not to put any holes in it
2. Clean carefully and sew up the smaller end
3. Grind fine pieces of raw goose meat, soft or linda fat, garlic, and onion
4. When fine add one egg and a little soaked bread
5. Season with salt, pepper, and ginger
6. Stuff through larger end
7. Sew up larger end
8. Lay in a pudding-pan
9. Pour a little cold water over it
10. Set in stove and baste from time to time
11. Let brown until crisp
12. Eat hot

title: Stuffed Goose Neck (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- skin from neck of goose, duck or chicken
- mixture for Kischtke

## Directions

1. Remove skin from neck of goose, duck or chicken in one piece
2. Wash and clean well
3. Stuff with same mixture as for Kischtke
4. Sew at both ends
5. Roast in hot oven until well browned

title: Stuffed Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of bread crumbs
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- two tablespoons of butter
- juice of one-half lemon
- pepper and salt to taste
- hot water

## Directions

1. Make a dressing of two tablespoons of bread crumbs, one tablespoon of chopped parsley, two tablespoons of butter, juice of one-half lemon, and pepper and salt to taste.
2. Add enough hot water to make soft.
3. Fill the herrings, roll up, tie in shape.
4. Cover with greased paper and bake ten to fifteen minutes.

title: Stuffed Herring
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of bread crumbs
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- two tablespoons of butter
- juice of one-half lemon
- pepper
- salt
- hot water
- herrings
- greased paper

## Directions

1. Make a dressing of two tablespoons of bread crumbs, one tablespoon of chopped parsley, two tablespoons of butter, juice of one-half lemon, and pepper and salt to taste.
2. Add enough hot water to make soft.
3. Fill the herrings, roll up, tie in shape.
4. Cover with greased paper and bake ten to fifteen minutes.

title: Stuffed Peppers
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six tender, sweet peppers
- bread crumbs (sufficient to make one pint when water is pressed out)
- one-fourth teaspoon basil
- herbs
- two teaspoons of salt
- two tablespoons of butter
- white sauce
- two cups of water

## Directions

1. Soak in water bread crumbs sufficient to make one pint when the water is pressed out
2. Mix with one-fourth teaspoon basil, herbs and two teaspoons of salt, add two tablespoons of butter
3. Cut off the stem end of each pepper
4. Carefully remove the interior and fill the peppers with the prepared dressing
5. Place in a shallow baking-pan and pour around them white sauce thinned with two cups of water
6. Bake about one hour, basting frequently with the sauce

title: Stuffed Peppers (Arday-Influs)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet green peppers
- one pound of chopped meat
- one egg
- one onion, grated
- salt
- citric acid (size of bean dissolved in a little water)
- one tablespoon of fat
- sliced tomatoes
- one-half cup of water
- sugar
- one-half tablespoon of flour
- one-half tablespoon of fat

## Directions

1. Take sweet green peppers, cut off blossom end
2. To one pound of chopped meat take one egg, grate in one onion, a little salt, citric acid (size of bean dissolved in a little water), mix all together
3. Place this mixture in the peppers, but do not fill too full
4. Set the entire top of peppers in place
5. Melt one tablespoon of fat in a saucepan, add sliced tomatoes, then the stuffed peppers and one-half cup of water
6. Let steam one-half or three-quarters of an hour
7. Make sweet sour with a little citric acid and sugar to taste
8. Thicken gravy with one-half tablespoon of flour, browned with one-half tablespoon of fat

title: Stuffed Potatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- milk
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Take as many potatoes as are needed; when done, cut off one end and take out inside.
2. Mash this and mix with it one tablespoon of butter, a sprig of parsley, pepper, salt, and enough milk to make quite soft.
3. Put back in the potato skins and brown in oven and serve very hot.
4. If so desired the open end of each may be dipped in beaten egg before being put in oven.

title: Stuffed Prunes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of best prunes
- Sherry
- Browned almonds
- Marshmallow or another prune
- Granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Take one pound of best prunes, stone and soak in sherry for about an hour (do not cover with the wine)
2. Fill prunes with one large browned almond and one-half marshmallow or with another prune
3. Roll in granulated sugar
4. When all are finished, put in oven for two or three minutes

title: Stuffed Shoulder of Veal
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- bread crumbs
- thyme
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- one egg
- chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Have the blade removed, and fill the space with a stuffing made of bread crumbs, thyme, lemon juice, salt, pepper to taste and one egg, also chopped mushrooms if desired
2. Sew up the opening, press and tie it into good shape and roast

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- round, very firm and even sized tomatoes
- cold slaw
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise dressing

## Directions

1. Select round, very firm and even sized tomatoes
2. Cut off the top (reserve to use as a cover)
3. Scrape out the inside, being very careful to not break the tomato
4. Fill each tomato with some finely prepared cold slaw
5. Cover with the top of the tomato
6. Lay them on lettuce leaves and pour a mayonnaise dressing over each
7. You may lay them en masse on a decorated platter, heaping them in the shape of a mound, or serve individually

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- bread crumbs
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- paprika
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Select tomatoes of uniform size, cut a slice from the stem-end and scoop out a portion of the pulp.
2. Have in readiness a dressing made from grated bread crumbs, parsley, a slice of minced onion, a high seasoning of salt and paprika and sufficient melted butter to moisten.
3. Fill this into the tomatoes and heap it up in the centers.
4. Place a bit of butter on top of each and bake in a quick oven until the vegetables are tender and the tops are delicately browned.

title: Stuffed Tomatoes, Cheese Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six small tomatoes
- cream cheese
- pimento
- French mustard
- French dressing
- lettuce leaves
- thick boiled dressing

## Directions

1. Wash and skin six small tomatoes
2. Cut a piece from the stem end of each and when cold remove a portion of the pulp from the centre
3. Sprinkle with salt and invert on the ice to chill
4. Mash to a paste one small cream cheese
5. Add two tablespoons of chopped pimento, one tablespoon of French mustard
6. Blend well, moisten with a French dressing and fill into the tomato shells
7. Arrange on a bed of crisp lettuce leaves and pour over each tomato a tablespoon of thick boiled dressing

title: Stuffed Turkey Neck (Turkish Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- neck of turkey
- almonds
- walnuts
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Take neck of turkey
2. Chop almonds or walnuts fine and season with chopped parsley, pepper and salt
3. Put two hard-boiled eggs in the centre of this dressing
4. Stuff neck, sew up the ends
5. When roasted slice across so as to have a portion of the hard-boiled egg on each slice
6. Place on platter and surround with sprigs of parsley

title: Stuffed Yellow Tomatoes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- small yellow tomatoes
- caviar
- lettuce or watercress

## Directions

1. Take small yellow tomatoes
2. Scrape out the centre
3. Fill with caviar
4. Serve on lettuce or watercress

title: Succotash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts corn
- equal parts any kind of beans
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix equal parts of corn, cut from the ear, and any kind of beans.
2. Boil them separately.
3. Stir them lightly together, and season with butter, salt, and pepper and add a little cream if convenient.

title: Suet Balls for Soups
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of suet
- 2 oz. of flour
- a little salt
- pepper
- a little ground ginger
- a little chopped parsley
- a little lemon thyme or marjoram
- a little cold water

## Directions

1. Chop 2 oz. of suet very fine, 2 oz. of flour, a little salt, pepper, a little ground ginger, a little chopped parsley, a little lemon thyme or marjoram
2. Mix it with a little cold water to a stiff paste
3. Divide it into small pieces for the balls
4. Throw them into the boiling soup
5. They require boiling for half an hour, in consequence of the raw suet
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Suet Pudding with Pears
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of suet
- one loaf of stale bread
- two eggs
- one cup of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- allspice
- cloves
- cinnamon
- grated peel of a lemon
- flour
- two teaspoons of baking-powder
- about half a peck of cooking pears
- sugar
- sliced lemon
- a few cloves
- some cinnamon bark
- three tablespoons of syrup

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of suet and chop it to a powder
2. Soak a loaf of stale bread, squeeze out the water and add to the suet
3. Work bread and suet well with your hands and add two eggs, one cup of sugar, one teaspoon of salt, allspice, cloves, cinnamon and grated peel of a lemon
4. Add flour enough to work into a huge ball; sift two teaspoons of baking-powder in flour
5. Pare about half a peck of cooking pears and cut in halves, leaving the stems on
6. Lay half the pears in a large kettle, put the pudding in centre of the pears, and lay the rest of the pears all around
7. Add sugar, sliced lemon, a few cloves, some cinnamon bark and three tablespoons of syrup
8. Fill up with cold water and boil half an hour on top of stove
9. Then bake for at least three hours, adding water if needed

title: Sugar Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sweet butter
- two cups of granulated sugar
- three eggs
- grated lemon rind
- four tablespoons of brandy
- three cups of flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- three teaspoons of baking-powder

## Directions

1. In a mixing bowl put a cup of sweet butter and two cups of granulated sugar; beat these ingredients to a cream, then add three eggs, grated lemon rind, and four tablespoons of brandy.
2. Beat the added ingredients thoroughly with the others till the mixture is smooth and creamy.
3. Sift three cups of flour in a big bowl with a teaspoon of salt and three teaspoons of baking-powder; stir this a little at a time in the bowl with the other ingredients, until the mixture is a light dough, just stiff enough to roll out.
4. Dust the pastry board well with flour, put part of the dough on the board, toss it lightly with your hands from side to side till the dough is covered with flour.
5. Dust the rolling-pin well with flour and roll the dough very thin; cut it in shapes with a cookie cutter.
6. Lift each cookie up carefully with a pancake turner, slip them quickly in a big baking-pan, the inside of which has been well rubbed with flour, and bake them in a moderate oven till light brown.
7. Just a moment before taking the pan out of the oven sprinkle the surface of the cookies lightly with granulated sugar.
8. When a little cool take the cookies out of the pan with the pancake turner and lay them on a big platter.

title: Sugar Peas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar pea pods
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Gather the pods while the seeds are still very small
2. String like beans and cut into pieces
3. Cover with boiling water and boil gently for twenty-five or thirty minutes or until tender
4. Pour off most of the water, saving it for soup
5. Season the rest with salt and butter and serve

title: Sugar Syrup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of brown sugar
- one cup of boiling water
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir two cups of brown sugar, one cup of boiling water, and cinnamon to taste together in a saucepan until the sugar is dissolved
2. Let the mixture simmer slowly until it thickens

title: Sulze von Kalbsfuessen (Calf's Foot Jelly), No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's head
- four calf's feet
- four quarts of water
- two or three small onions
- a few cloves
- salt
- one teaspoon of whole peppers
- two or three bay leaves
- juice of a large lemon
- one cup of white wine
- white wine vinegar
- one teaspoon of sugar
- whipped whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Clean calf's head and calf's feet carefully
2. Let them lay in cold water for half an hour
3. Set on to boil with four quarts of water
4. Add two or three small onions, a few cloves, salt, one teaspoon of whole peppers, two or three bay leaves, juice of a large lemon, one cup of white wine and a little white wine vinegar
5. Let this boil slowly for five or six hours until reduced one-half
6. Strain through a fine hair sieve and let it stand ten or twelve hours
7. Remove the meat from the bones and when cold cut into fine pieces
8. Add the boiled brains
9. Skim off every particle of fat from the jelly and melt slowly
10. Add one teaspoon of sugar and the whipped whites of three eggs and boil very fast for about fifteen minutes, skimming well
11. Taste and if not tart enough, add a dash of vinegar
12. Strain through a flannel bag, do not squeeze or shake it until the jelly ceases to run freely
13. Remove the bowl and put another under for the remaining jelly
14. Wet your mould, put in the jelly and set in a cool place
15. For variety, wet another mould and put in the bits of meat and the brains and lastly the jelly; set this on ice

title: Summer Pea Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a peck of peas
- good stock
- chopped lettuce
- cucumber
- mint
- pepper
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a peck of peas, separate the old from the young
2. Boil the former till they are quite tender in good stock
3. Pass them through a sieve, and return them to the stock
4. Add the young peas, a little chopped lettuce, small pieces of cucumber fried to a light brown, a little bit of mint, pepper, and salt
5. Two or three lumps of sugar give a fine flavor

title: Sunshine Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of five eggs
- one teaspoon of vanilla or grated rind of one lemon
- whites of seven eggs
- a scant one-half teaspoon of cream of tartar
- one cup of granulated sugar, sifted three times
- one cup of flour, sifted three times

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of five eggs lightly
2. Add one teaspoon of vanilla, or grated rind of one lemon
3. In another bowl beat seven whites to a froth with a scant one-half teaspoon of cream of tartar, then beat until whites are very stiff
4. Gradually add one cup of granulated sugar, sifted three times, to the beaten whites
5. Fold whites and sugar, when beaten, into the beaten yolks
6. Sift one cup of flour three times, then put into sifter and shake lightly
7. Fold into the cake
8. Bake forty minutes in ungreased cake pan
9. As directed for sponge cake invert pan
10. Remove cake when it has cooled

title: Superior Cold Cream
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one drachm of spermacetti
- one drachm of white wax
- two fluid ounces of oil of almond
- rose water

## Directions

1. Melt together one drachm of spermacetti, the same quantity of white wax, and two fluid ounces of oil of almond
2. While these are still warm, beat up with them as much rose water as they will absorb

title: Superior Milk of Roses
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh rose leaves
- asses milk

## Directions

1. Boil fresh rose leaves in asses milk
2. Bottle it off for immediate use

title: Superior Receipt for Almond Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of ten eggs
- whites of seven eggs
- half a pound of sweet almonds pounded finely
- half a pound of white sugar
- half an ounce of bitter almonds
- a table-spoonful of orange flower water
- powdered sugar
- clarified sugar with orange flower water

## Directions

1. Beat up the yolks of ten eggs, and the whites of seven
2. Add half a pound of sweet almonds pounded finely, half a pound of white sugar, half an ounce of bitter almonds, and a table-spoonful of orange flower water
3. Thoroughly mix
4. Grease a dish
5. Put in the pudding and bake in a brisk oven
6. When done, strew powdered sugar over the top, or pour over clarified sugar with orange flower water

title: Surprise Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- orange marmalade
- pecan nuts
- cream cheese
- thin slices of buttered bread

## Directions

1. Take orange marmalade, pecan nuts and cream cheese in equal quantities.
2. After mixing thoroughly spread on thin slices of buttered bread.

title: Sweet Almond Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of sweet almonds blanched
- four eggs
- four tablespoons of cream
- one tablespoon of butter
- powdered sugar
- salted almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare one-half cup of sweet almonds, blanched, chopped fine and pounded smooth.
2. Beat four eggs slightly, add four tablespoons of cream and turn it into a hot omelet pan on which you have melted one tablespoon of butter.
3. Cook carefully, drawing the cooked portion into the centre and tilting the pan to allow the liquid part to run over the bare pan.
4. When nearly all set, sprinkle the almonds over the surface and turn the edges over until well rolled.
5. Then slip it out on a hot dish and dredge with powdered sugar, and scatter several salted almonds over the top.
6. Serve immediately.

title: Sweet and Sour Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt water
- water
- vinegar
- sliced onions
- raisins
- brown sugar
- rye bread
- molasses
- sliced lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place the fish in strong salt water for one hour before cooking
2. Take three parts of water and one of vinegar
3. Put in saucepan with some sliced onions and some raisins
4. Let boil until tender
5. Add brown sugar to taste
6. Add a piece of rye bread from which the crust has been removed
7. Add some molasses
8. Boil the sauce, then place the fish in and let all cook twenty minutes
9. When done, arrange on platter with sliced lemon and chopped parsley

title: Sweet Entree of Ripe Peaches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, solid peaches
- boiling water
- sugar
- water
- sweet cracker
- raspberry jelly
- salted almonds or walnuts

## Directions

1. Take large, solid peaches, pour boiling water over them so that the skin may be removed smoothly.
2. Have ready thick syrup made of sugar and water.
3. When boiling hot add peaches and boil about five minutes; remove and place in ice chest.
4. When ready to serve have a sweet cracker on dish, place peach on same and pour over this a raspberry jelly slightly thinned and cover all with salted almonds or walnuts.

title: Sweet Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- two tablespoons of flour
- one cup of cold milk
- butter
- powdered sugar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Wet the flour with a little of the milk, then add the rest of the milk and the yolks of the eggs.
2. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth and pour into the flour, milk and yolks.
3. Put a piece of butter into a spider and let it get hot, but not so hot that the butter will burn.
4. Then pour the mixture in and put in a moderate oven to bake in the spider.
5. It takes about ten minutes to bake.
6. Then slip a knife under it and loosen it and slip off on a large plate.
7. Sift powdered sugar on top and serve with a slice of lemon.

title: Sweet Omelet For One
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- two tablespoons of cold sweet milk
- a pinch of salt
- compote or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat white separately.
2. Brown on both sides or roll, spread with compote or sprinkle powdered sugar thickly over it.
3. Serve at once.

title: Sweet Pickles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- five hundred tiny cucumbers
- one-half cup of salt
- four quarts of water
- hot water
- two ounces of cloves
- four sticks cinnamon
- three pounds of brown sugar
- one pint of cider vinegar
- glass jars

## Directions

1. Soak five hundred tiny cucumbers in salt water for twenty-four hours, using one-half of a cup of salt to four quarts of water
2. Drain and pour hot water over them and drain very dry
3. Take two ounces of cloves, heads removed, and four sticks cinnamon
4. Tie these spices in a bag and heat with three pounds of brown sugar and one pint of cider vinegar slowly, nearly to the boiling point
5. Add the pickles and remove from the stove
6. Put in glass jars and cover with vinegar

title: Sweet Potato Croquettes
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- hot baked sweet potatoes
- one teaspoon of butter or drippings
- yolks of two eggs
- pepper
- salt
- fine bread crumbs
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Press through a ricer sufficient hot baked sweet potatoes to measure one pint
2. Place over the fire
3. Add one teaspoon of butter or drippings, the beaten yolks of two eggs, pepper and salt to taste
4. Beat well with a fork until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan
5. Cool slightly
6. Form into cones
7. Roll in fine bread crumbs
8. Dip in beaten eggs
9. Roll again in crumbs
10. Fry in hot oil or fat

title: Sweet Potato Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of mashed, boiled sweet potatoes
- one pint of sweet milk
- three whole eggs
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-quarter of a nutmeg
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- one-half teaspoon of lemon extract
- pie crust

## Directions

1. Measure one cup of mashed, boiled sweet potatoes.
2. Thin with one pint of sweet milk.
3. Beat three whole eggs very light with one-half cup of sugar.
4. Mix with sweet potatoes.
5. Season with one-quarter of a nutmeg grated, one teaspoon of cinnamon, and one-half teaspoon of lemon extract.
6. Line pie-plate with crust, fill with mixture, and bake in quick oven.

title: Sweet Potato Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of grated, raw sweet potatoes
- one tablespoon of meat fat
- one tablespoon of chicken fat
- one half pound of brown sugar
- one-half pint of molasses
- one and one-half pints of cold water
- one saltspoon of salt
- black pepper
- grated orange peel
- ginger
- nutmeg
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pour into greased baking-pan
2. Bake in moderate oven until it jellies
3. May be eaten as a dessert, warm or cold

title: Sweet Potatoes and Apples
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- long sweet potatoes
- water
- salt
- apples
- maple syrup
- butter
- rum
- candied cherry

## Directions

1. Wash and pare long sweet potatoes.
2. Cook in boiling salted water until almost soft; drain and cut slices crosswise, two inches high.
3. Core, pare and cut apples in one-half inch rounds.
4. Into a spider, place the potatoes upright, with a slice of apple on top of each.
5. Pour over one-half cup of maple syrup, one-fourth cup of water and two tablespoons of butter.
6. Baste frequently until apples are soft.
7. Pour one teaspoon of rum over each section, place a candied cherry in the center of each apple and bake ten minutes.
8. Remove to platter and if desired, pour more rum over and around.
9. Light the liquor and bring to the table burning.

title: Sweet Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- cold water
- a pinch of salt
- two cups of milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- the yolk of an egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and wash one cup of rice
2. Put on to boil with cold water, add a pinch of salt
3. When done drain off the water, if any
4. Add two cups of milk, stir in and let boil for five minutes
5. Dish up, then sprinkle sugar and cinnamon generously over the top
6. The yolk of an egg can be added just before serving if desired

title: Sweet Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- melted butter or thin egg sauce
- white wine, brandy, lemon peel, or any other flavor

## Directions

1. Add sugar to melted butter, or thin egg sauce
2. Flavor it with white wine, brandy, lemon peel, or any other flavor approved of

title: Sweet Sour Beans
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- canned string beans
- fat
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of brown sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- vinegar
- juice of pickled peaches or pears

## Directions

1. Heat some fat in a spider
2. Put in one tablespoon of flour and brown slightly
3. Add one tablespoon of brown sugar, a pinch of salt, some cinnamon and vinegar to taste
4. Add the beans and let them simmer on the back of stove
5. Do not let them burn
6. The juice of pickled peaches or pears is delicious in preparing this dish

title: Sweet Sour Beans and Linzen
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- salt
- pepper
- drippings
- flour
- soup stock
- vinegar
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Soak overnight and drain the beans
2. boil in salted water until tender
3. drain and prepare by adding salt and pepper to taste
4. thicken with one tablespoon of drippings in which has been browned one tablespoon of flour and some soup stock
5. If the beans are to be made sweet sour add two tablespoons of vinegar and two tablespoons of brown sugar
6. boil for a few minutes and serve

title: Sweet Sour Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- one and one-half cups water
- one cup vinegar
- one onion
- one dozen raisins
- one lemon
- two bay leaves
- six cloves
- three tablespoons granulated sugar
- two tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Cut up and salt the fish
2. Put on fish kettle with one and one-half cups of water and one cup of vinegar
3. Add one onion cut in round slices, one dozen raisins, one lemon cut in round slices, two bay leaves, six cloves
4. When this mixture begins to boil, lay in fish and cook thoroughly
5. When done remove fish to platter
6. Put liquor back on stove
7. Add three tablespoons of granulated sugar which has been melted and browned in a pie plate without water
8. Add two tablespoons of flour which has been rubbed smooth with a little water
9. Let boil well and pour over fish
10. If not sweet enough add more sugar
11. Serve cold

title: Sweet Sour Fish with Wine
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass water
- one-half glass vinegar
- two tablespoons brown sugar
- one-half dozen cloves
- one-half teaspoon ground cinnamon
- one onion
- one lemon
- one goblet red wine
- one dozen raisins
- one tablespoon pounded almonds
- fish

## Directions

1. Put on to boil in fish kettle one glass water, one-half glass vinegar, two tablespoons of brown sugar, one-half dozen cloves, one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon, one onion cut in round slices
2. Boil thoroughly, then strain
3. Add to it one lemon cut in round slices, one goblet of red wine, one dozen raisins, one tablespoon of pounded almonds
4. Put on stove again
5. When it comes to a boil, add fish

title: Sweet Sour Fish With Wine
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass water
- one-half glass vinegar
- two tablespoons of brown sugar
- one-half dozen cloves
- one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one onion
- one lemon
- one goblet of red wine
- one dozen raisins
- one tablespoon of pounded almonds
- a small piece Leb-kuchen or ginger cake
- four eggs
- fish (ghad or trout)

## Directions

1. Put on to boil in fish kettle one glass water, one-half glass vinegar, two tablespoons of brown sugar, one-half dozen cloves, one-half teaspoon of ground cinnamon, and one onion cut in round slices
2. Boil thoroughly, then strain and add to it one lemon cut in round slices, one goblet of red wine, one dozen raisins, one tablespoon of pounded almonds
3. Put on stove again, and when it comes to a boil, add fish that has been cut up and salted
4. Cook until done, remove fish to a platter
5. To the liquor add a small piece Leb-kuchen or ginger cake, and stir in the well-beaten yolks of four eggs
6. Stir carefully or it will curdle
7. If not sweet enough add more sugar
8. Pour over fish

title: Sweetbread Glace, Sauce Jardinie re with Spaghetti
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry drippings
- onion
- sweetbreads
- salt water
- salt
- pepper
- tomatoes
- soup stock
- string beans
- canned peas
- currants
- drippings
- ginger
- flour
- spaghetti

## Directions

1. Put on some poultry drippings to heat in a saucepan
2. cut up an onion, shredded very fine and then put in the sweetbreads, which have been picked over carefully and lain in salt water an hour before boiling
3. Salt and pepper the sweetbreads before putting in the kettle
4. slice two tomatoes on top and cover up tight and set on the back of stove to simmer slowly
5. Turn once in a while and add a little soup stock
6. Boil one-half cup of string beans, half a can of canned peas, one-half cup of currants, cut up extremely fine, with a tablespoon of drippings, a little salt and ground ginger
7. When the vegetables are tender, add to the simmering sweetbreads
8. Thicken the sauce with a teaspoon of flour
9. Have the sauce boiled down quite thick
10. Boil the spaghetti in salted water until tender
11. Serve with the sweetbreads

title: Sweetbread Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- a pair of sweetbreads or calf's brains
- celery
- one can of French peas
- mayonnaise
- lettuce leaf

## Directions

1. Take cucumbers and cut lengthwise to serve the salad in.
2. Scrape out the inside and salt well, then squeeze and use this to mix with the filling.
3. Take a pair of sweetbreads, or calf's brains, wash well, and boil.
4. When done, throw in cold water at once and skim them.
5. Chop fine, add bunch of celery (if you can get it), one can of French peas, scraped part of cucumber.
6. Mix all together and season.
7. Make a mayonnaise, mix with it, and fill the cucumber shells.
8. Keep all cold, and serve on lettuce leaf.

title: Sweetbread Saute with Mushrooms
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbread
- one tablespoon of fat
- one tablespoon of flour
- canned mushrooms
- soup stock
- catsup
- mushroom catsup
- kitchen bouquet
- clove of garlic
- small onion
- salt
- pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Clean sweetbread, boil until tender, and cut in small pieces
2. Take one tablespoon of fat, blend in one tablespoon of flour
3. add half the liquor of a can of mushrooms and enough soup stock to make the necessary amount of gravy
4. add a little catsup, mushroom catsup, and a few drops of kitchen bouquet, a clove of garlic, and a small onion
5. salt and pepper to taste
6. Cook this about an hour, and then remove garlic and onion
7. Add sweetbreads, mushrooms, and two hard-boiled eggs chopped very fine

title: Sweetbreads
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- water
- salt
- pepper
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Wash the sweetbreads very carefully and remove all bits of skin and fatty matter
2. Cover with cold water, salt and boil for fifteen minutes
3. Then remove from the boiling water and cover with cold water
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, roll in beaten egg and bread crumbs, and fry a nice brown in hot fat

title: Sweetbreads Roasted
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- warm water
- egg
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- clarified veal suet
- puree of vegetables
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. First soak them in warm water, and then blanch them
2. cover with egg and bread crumbs, seasoned with salt and pepper
3. finish in a Dutch oven or cradle spit
4. frequently baste with clarified veal suet
5. serve either dry with a puree of vegetables, or with a brown gravy

title: Sweetbreads Stewed Brown
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- beef gravy
- smoked meat
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- cloves
- allspice
- morels
- truffles
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. After soaking and blanching, fry them till brown
2. simmer gently in beef gravy seasoned highly with smoked meat, nutmeg, pepper, salt
3. add a small onion stuck with cloves, and a very little whole allspice
4. thicken the gravy slightly
5. add morels and truffles
6. small balls of delicate forcemeat are also an improvement

title: Sweetbreads Stewed White
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- veal gravy
- celery
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- mace
- lemon peel
- yolks of eggs
- mushroom essence
- Jerusalem artichokes

## Directions

1. After soaking and blanching, stew them in veal gravy
2. season with celery, pepper, salt, nutmeg, a little mace, and a piece of lemon peel
3. serve with a fine white sauce, the gravy in which they are stewed will form the basis for it
4. add yolks of eggs and mushroom essence
5. Jerusalem artichokes cut the size of button mushrooms, are a suitable accompaniment as a garnish

title: Swiss Creamed Fish
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cold water
- one teaspoon of flour
- one cup of boiling milk
- cold fish
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of butter
- one well-beaten egg
- cayenne or paprika

## Directions

1. Mix smoothly in one cup of cold water a teaspoon of flour.
2. Stir it into one cup of boiling milk and when thick and smooth add the meat of any cold fish, picked free from skin and bones.
3. Season with salt, pepper and a tablespoon of butter.
4. If the cream is desired to be extra rich one well-beaten egg may be added one minute before removing from the fire.
5. Serve hot.
6. A pinch of cayenne or a saltspoon of paprika is relished by many.

title: Tamarind Whey
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of tamarinds
- two pints of milk

## Directions

1. Boil three ounces of tamarinds in two pints of milk
2. Strain off the curds and let it cool

title: Tapioca Custard
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons of tapioca
- one quart of sweet milk
- three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Soak four tablespoons of tapioca overnight in one quart of sweet milk.
2. In the morning beat the yolks of three eggs with one cup of sugar.
3. Put the milk and tapioca on in a double boiler, adding a pinch of salt.
4. When this comes to boiling point stir in the eggs and sugar.
5. Beat the whites to a stiff froth and stir quickly and delicately into the hot mixture.
6. Flavor with vanilla.
7. Eat cold.

title: Tart Did Moy
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of savoy biscuits
- a quart of milk
- six ounces of fresh butter
- four eggs
- one ounce of candid orange peel
- one ounce of lemon peel
- one ounce of citron
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Soak three-quarters of a pound of savoy biscuits in a quart of milk
2. Add six ounces of fresh butter, four eggs, one ounce of candid orange peel, the same quantity of lemon peel, and one ounce of citron
3. Mix all well together and sweeten with white sugar
4. Bake in a quick oven
5. When nearly done, spread over the top the whites of the eggs well whisked
6. Return it to the oven

title: Tartlets
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- very rich light puff paste
- sweetmeat or jam

## Directions

1. Make a very rich light puff paste, and roll it out to half an inch of thickness
2. It should be cut with fluted paste-cutters, lightly baked, and the centre scooped out afterwards, and the sweetmeat or jam inserted
3. A pretty dish of pastry may be made by cutting the paste in ribbons of three inches in length, and one and a half in width
4. Bake them lightly, and pile them one upon another, with jam between each, in the form of a cone

title: Tartlets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- jam
- eggs

## Directions

1. Roll puff paste one-eighth of an inch thick
2. Cut it into squares
3. Turn the points together into the middle and press slightly to make them stay
4. Bake until thoroughly done
5. Place a spoonful of jam in the centre of each
6. Cover the jam with meringue and brown the meringue in a quick oven
7. Brush the top of the paste with beaten egg diluted with one teaspoon of water for a glazed appearance

title: Tartlets
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- jam
- meringue (made with egg whites and powdered sugar)
- beaten egg diluted with one teaspoon of water (optional for glaze)

## Directions

1. Roll puff paste one-eighth of an inch thick
2. Cut it into squares
3. Turn the points together into the middle and press slightly to make them stay
4. Bake until thoroughly done
5. Place a spoonful of jam in the centre of each
6. Cover the jam with meringue and brown the meringue in a quick oven
7. For a glazed appearance, brush the top of the paste with beaten egg diluted with one teaspoon of water

title: Tchorba—Turkish Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound meat
- water
- rice
- one beaten egg
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Take one pound of meat, cover with water and boil till meat is tender
2. Boil rice in another pan until it is creamy
3. When ready to serve, add one beaten egg and juice of half a lemon

title: Tea
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon tea per person, and 1 extra
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald the tea-pot
2. When the water boils, pour off the water with which the pot was scalded
3. Put in the tea and pour boiling water over it
4. Let it draw three minutes
5. Tea should never be allowed to remain on the leaves
6. If not drunk as soon as it is drawn, it should be poured off into another hot tea-pot, or into a hot jug, which should stand in hot water

title: Tea (Russian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons tea leaves
- boiling water
- warm water
- lemon slice or lemon juice
- preserved strawberries, cherries or raspberries

## Directions

1. Use a small earthenware tea-pot, thoroughly clean
2. Put in two teaspoons of tea leaves
3. Pour over it boiling water to one-fourth of the pot
4. Let it stand three minutes
5. Fill the pot entirely with boiling water and let it stand five minutes
6. In serving dilute with warm water to suit taste, or serve cold, but always without milk
7. Allow a thin slice of lemon or a few drops of lemon juice for each cup
8. Consider preserved strawberries, cherries or raspberries an improvement

title: Tea Ice Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce of the best green tea
- a cup of boiling water
- 1 pint of cream
- sugar to sweeten

## Directions

1. Steep an ounce of the best green tea in a cup of boiling water
2. Let it stand for 1/2 an hour
3. Strain it into a pint of cream
4. Sweeten it to flavour
5. Put it in the freezer and work it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Tea Rolls
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- one cake of compressed yeast
- one and one-half cups of flour
- one-quarter cup of sugar
- one and one-half teaspoons of salt
- two eggs
- one-third cup of butter

## Directions

1. Scald one cup of milk and when lukewarm dissolve one cake of compressed yeast
2. add one and one-half cups of flour
3. Beat thoroughly, cover and allow to stand until light
4. Add sugar, salt, eggs, butter and enough flour to knead
5. Allow to rise again until light
6. Shape into round or small oblong finger rolls, and place in buttered pans close together
7. when light bake in hot oven

title: Teiglech
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- three tablespoons of oil
- four eggs
- a pound of chopped nuts
- one-half pound of honey
- one-half pound of sugar
- cold water
- dry ginger

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, three tablespoons of oil, and four eggs
2. Knead very well
3. Roll out in strips three inches long
4. Place on tins and bake
5. Take a pound of chopped nuts, one-half pound of honey, and one-half pound of sugar
6. Mix thoroughly with wooden spoon and boil with the cakes until brown
7. Take off the stove, wet with cold water, spread out on board
8. When cold, pat with the hands to make thin and sprinkle with dry ginger

title: Tendons of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- tendons from the bones of a breast of veal
- slices of smoked beef
- shavings of veal
- a few herbs
- a little sliced lemon
- two or three onions
- a little broth
- white wine
- mushrooms
- egg-balls
- a few peas
- a piece of mint
- a little white sugar

## Directions

1. Cut from the bones of a breast of veal the tendons which are round the front
2. Trim and blanch them
3. Put them with slices of smoked beef into a stewpan with some shavings of veal, a few herbs, a little sliced lemon, two or three onions, and a little broth
4. Let them simmer for seven or eight hours
5. When done, thicken the gravy and add white wine and mushrooms and egg-balls
6. Add a few peas with the tendons
7. Add a piece of mint and a little white sugar

title: Tendons of Veal
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of veal
- tendons
- slices of smoked beef
- shavings of veal
- herbs
- sliced lemon
- two or three onions
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut from the bones of a breast of veal the tendons which are round the front
2. Trim and blanch them
3. Put them with slices of smoked beef into a stewpan with some shavings of veal, a few herbs, a little sliced lemon, two or three onions, and a little broth
4. They must simmer for seven or eight hours
5. When done, thicken the gravy

title: Teufelsgurken (Hot Pickles)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- large, green cucumbers
- salt
- whole white and black peppercorns
- small pickling onions
- horseradish
- bay leaves
- fennel
- caraway seeds
- cloves of garlic
- Spanish pepper
- mustard seed
- pickling vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pare large, green cucumbers, cut each one lengthwise
2. Take out the seeds with a silver spoon
3. Cut each piece again so as to have four pieces out of one cucumber
4. When all are pared salt well and let them remain in the salt for twenty-four hours or more
5. Dry each piece, put in layers in a stone jar with whole white and black peppercorns, small pickling onions, which have been previously pared and salted overnight, pieces of horseradish, a few bay leaves, a little fennel, caraway seeds, a few cloves of garlic and also some Spanish pepper
6. Have a layer of the spices at the bottom of the jar
7. A handful of mustard seed put on the top layer will be an improvement
8. Boil enough pickling vinegar to cover well
9. Add a cup of sugar to a gallon of vinegar, boil and pour over hot
10. Boil again in three days and pour over the pickles after it gets cold
11. In two days pour off the vinegar and boil again and pour over the pickles hot
12. Boil three times altogether

title: The Almond Icing for the Bride's Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- half-pint of water
- 1 lb. of ground almonds
- 2 oz. of bitter almonds
- wine-glass of orange-flower water

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan 1 lb. of loaf sugar with half-pint of water
2. Let it boil to a syrup
3. Stir in 1 lb. of ground almonds
4. Have in the lb. of almonds 2 oz. of bitter
5. Let it boil till it looks thick
6. Put in a wine-glass of orange-flower water
7. When it has all boiled, put a small piece on a plate to see if it is sticky
8. If so, lay it all over the top of your cake as thick as the quantity will allow
9. If not thick enough, you can make some more and lay it over
10. Have ready the sugar icing, as soon as the almond icing has set you can lay on the sugar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: The French Way of Dressing Spinach
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash and boil till tender, then squeeze and strain it
2. Press it in a towel till almost dry
3. Put it on a board, and chop it as finely as possible
4. Return it to the saucepan, with butter, pepper, and salt
5. Stir it all the time, and let it boil fast

title: The Grosvenor Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of white sugar
- five eggs
- half a pound of flour
- a quarter of a pound of currants
- two ounces of candied orange and lemon peel
- a few drops of lemon essence

## Directions

1. Beat half a pound of butter with the same quantity of white sugar until it is like cream
2. Beat up five eggs and add them with half a pound of flour
3. Add a quarter of a pound of currants
4. Add two ounces of candied orange and lemon peel cut in thin slices
5. Add a few drops of lemon essence
6. When these ingredients are well mixed and beaten, butter a pudding tin
7. Pour in the mixture and bake in a moderately quick oven

title: The Original Almond Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 eggs, leave out 8 whites
- 1 lb. of ground almonds (2 oz. of bitter are included)
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- two-pennyworth of orange-flower water
- almond oil
- 1 lb. of sugar and 1/2 pint water for clarifying (optional)
- blanched almonds and thousands for garnish

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well with the orange-flower water
2. Add the sugar and almonds gently
3. Beat it all for 1 hour till it bubbles
4. Grease the dishes with almond oil
5. Put in the mixture
6. Bake in rather a moderate oven
7. When half done and the mixture is set, put over them a paper greased with the almond oil for them not to get too dark
8. Loosen them and let them cool before you take them out of the dish
9. If you wish to have them very rich, clarify 1 lb. of sugar and 1/2 pint water, and when the pudding comes out of the oven pour it over
10. Garnish with blanched almonds and thousands
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Three-Egg Waffles
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- yolks of three eggs
- one and one-fourth cups of milk
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- whites of three eggs

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, one-half teaspoon of salt
2. Sift these ingredients
3. Add the yolks of three eggs beaten and stirred into one and one-fourth cups of milk
4. Add one tablespoon of melted butter
5. Fold in the whites of the eggs
6. Bake and serve as directed under One-Egg Waffles

title: Timbale de Maccaroni
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- maccaroni
- water
- butter or grease for mould
- fricassee of chicken
- sweetbreads
- rich white sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the maccaroni in water till it slightly swells and is soft enough to cut
2. Cut it into short pieces about two inches in length
3. Grease a mould and stick the maccaroni closely together all over the mould
4. Fill up the space with fricassee of chicken, sweetbreads, or whatever may be liked
5. Close the mould carefully and boil
6. Serve with rich white sauce on the side, not poured over the timbale

title: Tipsy Pudding
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- stale sponge cake
- jelly or preserves
- wine
- rich custard
- frosting

## Directions

1. Cut stale sponge cake into thin slices, spread with jelly or preserves, put two pieces together like sandwiches and lay each slice or sandwich on the plate on which it is to be served.
2. Wet each piece with wine, pour or spread a tablespoon of rich custard over each piece of pudding, and then frost each piece with a frosting and put in a moderate oven for a few minutes.
3. Eat cold.

title: To Bake Sprats
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sprats
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- allspice
- cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash and clean them well and drain all the water from them
2. Dry them well
3. Put them in layers in a baking dish with salt, pepper, bay leaves, allspice, cloves, and ½ pint of vinegar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil a Cauliflower with Butter Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fine white cauliflower
- salt
- water
- melted butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put a fine white cauliflower in salt and water to boil, be careful it does not break
2. Put it to drain in the colander over the boiling water
3. Make a good sauce of melted butter with chopped parsley
4. Put the cauliflower in a dish, and pour over the sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil a Smoked Haddock
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- haddock
- water
- butter
- made mustard
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put a haddock in a flat stewpan
2. barely cover it with boiling water
3. let it simmer for 10 minutes
4. dish it up
5. have ready in a teacup a small piece of butter, a teaspoonful of made mustard, and the juice of a lemon
6. let it get hot in the cup
7. pour it over
8. garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Asparagus
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- melted butter
- toast

## Directions

1. Scrape it, tie it in bundles
2. Boil the same as kale
3. Dish it on a toast, serve with melted butter
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Cabbage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one small head of cabbage
- salt
- cold water
- salt
- boiling water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut a small head of cabbage into four parts, cutting down through the stock.
2. Soak for half an hour in a pan of cold water to which has been added one tablespoon of salt; this is to draw out any insects that may be hidden in the leaves.
3. Take from the water and cut into slices.
4. Have a large stewpan half full of boiling water; put in the cabbage, pushing it under the water with a spoon.
5. Add one tablespoon of salt and cook from twenty-five to forty-five minutes, depending upon the age of the cabbage.
6. Turn into a colander and drain for about two minutes.
7. Put in a chopping bowl, and mince.
8. Season with butter, pepper, and more salt if it requires it.
9. Allow one tablespoon of butter to a generous pint of the cooked vegetable.

title: To Boil Fowls
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- salt
- pepper
- water
- white cauliflower
- egg sauce or mayonnaise
- boiled tongue or smoked beef

## Directions

1. Fowls are prepared for boiling the same as turkeys
2. Season the water well that they are boiled in
3. Dish up and garnish the same with small white heads or pieces of a large white cauliflower
4. Serve with fowls egg sauce or mayonnaise according to recipe for same
5. Boiled tongue or smoked beef should always accompany them
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Guard Fish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- guard fish
- salt
- water
- parsley
- caper or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Boil them in salt and water
2. Turn them round with the tail in the mouth
3. Put them on a fish napkin, garnished with parsley
4. Serve with caper or anchovy sauce
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Potatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. To boil them well they must be as near as possible one size.
2. Put them on with salt and not much water.
3. Strain them five minutes before they are done, let them finish with their own steam.
4. Dish them up as soon as done.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Salt Cod
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- parsley
- boiled parsnips
- floury boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Mind that it is well soaked and looks very white
2. Put it to boil in cold water
3. As soon as it has boiled take it off the fire and let it stand at the side, simmering for 1/2 an hour
4. When done serve it on a fish napkin garnished with parsley
5. Serve with it boiled parsnips cut in nice pieces, and floury boiled potatoes
6. Make the sauce and serve separate in the sauceboat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Sea Kale
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sea-kale
- salt
- water
- bread
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Lay the sea-kale in salt and water for 1/2 an hour
2. Put it on to boil in a saucepan, cover it with boiling water and salt
3. Have ready a slice of bread toasted to lay the kale on
4. Serve it up with melted butter
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Soles
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash them well and let them lay in salt for 1/2 an hour before boiling
2. Put them in the fish kettle, just cover them with water
3. Lay them in with the white skin on top; that is the proper side to send them to table
4. 20 minutes will boil a good sized pair
5. As soon as the water boils skim it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Soles
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- water
- parsley
- caper or anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Wash soles well and let them lay in salt for half an hour before boiling
2. Put them in the fish kettle, just cover them with water, lay them in with the white skin on top
3. 20 minutes will boil a good sized pair
4. As soon as the water boils skim it, and when you lift it see that all scum is washed off
5. Have a dish ready with a fish napkin, slip them off the drainer on to the napkin, garnish round with parsley
6. Serve with caper or anchovy sauce in the sauce tureen
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil the Globe Artichoke
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- globe artichokes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Let them lay in water and salt to cleanse them well
2. Put them on in salt and water to boil
3. When you can pull off a leaf easily they are done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Tongues
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- plump tongue
- cold water
- salt
- pepper
- white turnips
- carrots
- spinach
- rice
- lemon
- hard eggs
- parsley
- beetroot
- glaze

## Directions

1. Choose a nice plump tongue, if salted at home it is preferable
2. Put it on in cold water, if you have a tongue saucepan it will keep the shape better; if not, boil it in a long stock pot, it should lay flat
3. Let it boil gently for 2.5 hours, then it will be tender enough to skin
4. Be careful you do not tear the flesh, return it to the water to finish boiling
5. To garnish it, if you send it up for company, boil some very white turnips and some rich coloured carrots
6. Boil them separately and mash them separately, with a little salt and pepper to flavour
7. Arrange them in alternate colours, with strips of spinach boiled very green
8. Cut a frill for the root of the tongue to cover it, fasten it on with a plated skewer
9. Another garnish: boil some rice white and very dry, make a bed of it in the dish, lay on the tongue
10. Have slices of lemon with slices of hard eggs, lay on chopped parsley, and strips of beetroot
11. The tongue, when taken out of the water, must be put on one of the chopping boards, and plump it up by pushing from the point to the centre, that must rise higher than the other part
12. A little glaze on the top makes it look rich
13. A smoked tongue must be soaked for 2 hours in cold water before you boil it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Boil Vegetable Marrows
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetable marrows
- toast
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Scrape them gently outside
2. Cut them in quarters, cut out all the seeds
3. Skim it well, lay it on a toast
4. Serve with melted butter
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Broil Salt Ling Fish
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- salt ling fish
- butter
- made mustard
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Buy 2 lb. of the thick part
2. lay it in soak for 2 days
3. take it out
4. dry it nicely on a clean cloth
5. take the black skin off before you broil it
6. put it on the gridiron
7. let it broil each side a bright gold colour
8. have in a teacup 2 oz. of butter, a teaspoonful of made mustard, some pepper, and the juice of a lemon
9. pour it over directly it is done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Broil Smoked Haddocks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- thick haddock
- butter
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take a nice thick haddock
2. cut off the tail and the top pieces by the head
3. grease the gridiron
4. lay it on, and put it before a clear fire
5. lay it on a hot dish
6. rub a piece of butter over it
7. pepper it
8. squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
9. Send it up quickly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Broil Steak by Gas
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- steak
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- melted chicken fat

## Directions

1. Wipe steak with a damp cloth
2. Trim off the surplus fat
3. Heat the oven for from five to seven minutes
4. Lay steak on a greased rack, as near the flame as possible, the position of the rack depending on the thickness of the steak
5. Let the steak sear on each side, thereby retaining the juice
6. Lower the rack somewhat, and allow the steak to broil to the degree required
7. Just before taking from the oven, salt and pepper and spread with melted chicken fat

title: To Clarify Suet
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine fresh suet, either beef or veal
- a sprig of rosemary, or a little orange flower water

## Directions

1. Melt down with care fine fresh suet, either beef or veal
2. put it into a jar, and set it in a stew-pan of water to boil
3. putting in a sprig of rosemary, or a little orange flower water while melting
4. it should be well beaten till quite light with a wooden fork

title: To Clarify Sugar
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- half a pint of water
- the whites of a couple of eggs

## Directions

1. Take the proportion of one pound of sugar to half a pint of water, with the whites of a couple of eggs
2. Boil it up twice
3. Set it by for the impurities to rise to the top
4. Skim it carefully

title: To Clarify Sugar
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- rind of a fresh lemon
- 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar with some of the rind of a fresh lemon in a saucepan
2. Add 1/2 pint of water
3. Boil to a syrup
4. Serve in a sauce-boat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Cook Bloaters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bloaters
- butter
- pepper
- vinegar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads
2. in so doing draw out the entrails
3. split them open down the back
4. lay them in a tin with a piece of butter and a little pepper
5. dish them on a hot plate
6. A little mixture of vinegar and mustard makes them very nice
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Cook Remnants of Cold Poultry
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- remnants of cold turkey or fowl
- 1/2 pint of stock
- 1 onion
- garlic
- pepper
- salt
- mace
- 2 or 3 allspice
- marjoram
- thyme
- a few pieces of smoked beef
- a pickled walnut
- a little flour
- the juice of a lemon
- a little browning
- slices of hard eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the pieces nicely and neatly, put them in a stewpan with 1/2 pint of stock, an onion, garlic, pepper, salt, mace, 2 or 3 allspice, marjoram, thyme, a few pieces of smoked beef, let it cook well, add a pickled walnut.
2. When done take out the fowl, strain the liquor through a sieve, return the fowl and gravy to the saucepan.
3. Rub in a little flour with the juice of a lemon, a little browning, stir it all well together.
4. Dish it up, and garnish with slices of hard eggs.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Cook the High Dried Herring
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- boiling water
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take off the head and skin them
2. put them in a tin whole with boiling water for 10 minutes
3. slit them down the back with a knife when done
4. put in a piece of butter with pepper
5. Dish upon a hot plate
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Draw Good Gravy
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- some gravy beef
- water

## Directions

1. Cut some gravy beef into small pieces, put them in a jar, and set it in a saucepan of cold water to boil gently for seven or eight hours
2. Add, from time to time, more water as the original quantity boils away

title: To Fry Cold Tongue
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- cold tongue
- fat
- bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- potatoes
- parsley

## Directions

1. If you have any left cut it in thin slices, and a few slices from the fat part
2. Have some fat boiling in your pan
3. Dip the slices in bread crumbs seasoned, and then into egg
4. Fry it a light brown
5. Have some potatoes well mashed, spread them in the dish
6. Place the slices on arranged nicely
7. Garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Fry Potatoes French Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- flour
- butter or oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel them, cut them in quarters rather thin.
2. Dry them in a cloth, sprinkle flour over them.
3. Have the butter or oil frying ready for them, put them in.
4. Keep turning to have them all one colour.
5. Drain them, sprinkle them with salt and pepper mixed together.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Fry Smelts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- smelts
- parsley

## Directions

1. Fry them the same as soles or any other fish
2. Dish on a napkin and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Fry Sprats in the Spanish Fashion
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- large sprats
- flour
- eggs
- boiling oil
- parsley

## Directions

1. Choose some very large sprats and wash them well in 3 or 4 waters
2. Take off the heads and split them open by running your thumb up the centre
3. Take out the bone carefully
4. Wash them well when boned and lay them in salt for ½ an hour
5. When you wash off the salt, lay each one separately in a very clean cloth
6. When well dried, put two sprats together, dip them in flour, then in eggs
7. Fry them in boiling oil until a nice colour
8. Put them on a dish for the oil to drain if to be eaten cold; if hot, send them up on a fish napkin in rows one above the other, garnished with layers of parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Fry the Fish During Passover
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- egg
- finest meal

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the fish well
2. Lay it in salt
3. Wash it off and drain it well
4. Lay it in a nice dry cloth
5. Beat up the egg in a plate and some of the finest meal you can pound and sieve
6. Let the oil boil well
7. Put in a piece of the dry biscuit to get brown
8. When well fried, take it out and begin to fry your fish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Hash Beef
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- a little broth or gravy
- a little fried onion
- pepper
- salt
- a spoonful of ketchup or any other sauce
- flour
- sippets of toast
- a little port wine
- a pinch of saffron
- a piece chorisa

## Directions

1. Put the meat on the fire in a little broth or gravy with a little fried onion, pepper, salt, and a spoonful of ketchup or any other sauce at hand
2. Let it simmer for about ten minutes
3. Mix in a cup a little flour with a little of the gravy
4. Pour it into the stewpan to thicken the rest
5. Serve sippets of toast with the hash
6. A little port wine, a pinch of saffron, or a piece chorisa may be considered great improvements

title: To Imitate Chestnuts
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of the best lump sugar
- 1 pint of water
- yolks of 6 very fresh eggs
- a glass of maraschino
- fine powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 lb. of the best lump sugar
2. Boil it in 1 pint of water till it comes quite thick
3. Test by dipping a little from a spoon on a plate to see if it sticks like hardbake
4. Remove it from the fire and work it up with a spoon like you would a preserve
5. Beat the yolks of 6 very fresh eggs well
6. Add to them a glass of maraschino
7. Work them well with the sugar till you produce a stiff paste
8. Turn it on a piece of marble with some fine powdered sugar
9. Roll it up into small balls the size of a chestnut, imitating the shape and making the dents like you see in a peeled chestnut
10. Take a piece of iron wire and make it red hot
11. Draw it across several parts of the chestnut to imitate the roasted nut
12. Put these sweetmeats in glass dishes
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Beef Steak Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 lb. of good chuck steak
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- juice of a lemon
- forced meat balls
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut up 1 or 2 lb. of good chuck steak
2. Put it on to stew with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg
3. Put in the juice of a lemon
4. Stir it well till the liquor thickens
5. Cook a few forced meat balls made very small
6. Cook a few potatoes, cut in halves stewed with
7. Arrange the dish as for pigeon pie
8. Bake in a good oven a light brown
9. Remember as soon as the potatoes brown it is done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Dutch Apple Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 good apples
- currants
- raisins
- almonds
- candied lemon and orange peel
- chopped almonds
- cinnamon
- cloves
- 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Peel and core 3 or 4 good apples, cut them small
2. Mix with a few currants, raisins, and almonds, candied lemon and orange peel, a few chopped almonds, cinnamon, and cloves, 1/4 lb. of moist sugar
3. Let this stew well together
4. Turn it out to get cold
5. Make a puff-paste, line and cover the dish
6. Bake in a very brisk oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Few Cutlets Quickly for Lunch
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large chops
- mashed potato or stewed spinach
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take 2 large chops
2. Cut out the bones free from any of the flesh
3. Cut each chop with a sharp knife into 3 small ones
4. Fry them
5. Have a little mashed potato or stewed spinach in a dish
6. Lay them on
7. Season with salt and pepper
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Greengage Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of greengages
- 4 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 oz. ground almonds
- cinnamon
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Wash and drain a quart of greengages, stew them with 4 lb. of loaf sugar, 1 oz. ground almonds, a little cinnamon
2. Remember not to put any water to stewed fruit for pies or tarts
3. Let it get cold
4. Line a dish with puff-paste, cover
5. Bake in a quick oven
6. These pies are always served with a baked custard
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Jaumange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- blancmange
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 2 oz. of sifted sugar

## Directions

1. When you have the blancmange quite ready, beat the yolks of 6 eggs with 2 oz. of sifted sugar
2. Stir the boiling mixture in, put it in the mould, turn out the same as the blancmange
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Pound Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of dry sifted flour
- 1 lb. of castor sugar
- 2 oz. of candied peel
- 1 oz. of citron
- 1 lb. of currants well washed and dried
- a little grated nutmeg
- a small piece of salt
- the rind of a lemon grated
- 8 eggs

## Directions

1. beat up 8 eggs well
2. work the butter and sugar well together
3. add the currants, peel, and all the other ingredients
4. add the eggs
5. put the flour in lightly
6. beat it well for half an hour
7. grease your cake tin, butter some paper
8. lay a round at the bottom, and strips round
9. be sure the oven is well heated before you put it in, or the ingredients will sink to the bottom
10. It will take nearly 2 hours baking
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Quince Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 quinces
- lemon rind
- ground cloves
- 1 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 eggs
- puff-paste

## Directions

1. Peel and core 4 or 5 quinces, boil them with the rind of a lemon chopped fine, a little ground cloves, and 1 lb. of moist sugar
2. When done, beat it up to a pulp
3. Mix with the yolks of 2 eggs well beaten
4. Line a pie-dish with puff-paste
5. Lay in the fruit quite cold, cover over
6. Bake in a quick oven
7. Remember always to dust your pies with sifted sugar, and damp with white of egg and water
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Seed Cake with Oil
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quartern of dough
- 1 lb. of sifted sugar
- the grated rind of a lemon
- 1 oz. of seeds, carraway or aniseed
- 1 pint of the best oil
- a small piece of bread or a piece of the dough
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the oil first with a small piece of bread or a piece of the dough
2. Let it cool before you put in the eggs
3. Beat the eggs well before you add them
4. Oil the tin
5. Bake in a good oven till done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Soda Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- a pinch of salt
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- a cup of milk
- 1/2 lb. currants
- 1/2 lb. raisins
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- 1 lb. peel
- 1 oz. mixed spice
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of carbonate of soda

## Directions

1. Warm 1/2 lb. of butter in a cup of milk
2. Mix all well together
3. Beat up for 20 minutes
4. Butter the tin
5. Bake in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Sponge Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 6 eggs well beaten
- 2 cups of flour
- rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Rasp the rind of a lemon
2. Beat the eggs well with the sugar
3. Place your dish in a pan of boiling water while you are beating them
4. Add the flour gently with the rasped lemon
5. Grease a tin well
6. Grease a thin paper and put round
7. Bake in a moderate oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make a Trifle
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cakes
- almond cakes
- ratafias
- dried cherries
- raspberry or strawberry jam
- custard
- 1 pint of sherry
- 2 wineglasses of brandy
- 1 pint of cream
- 2 ozs. of sifted sugar
- whites of 2 fresh eggs
- thousands and pieces of green citron
- whites of 4 eggs
- juice of 1/2 a lemon

## Directions

1. Put in a large glass dish some sponge cakes broken in pieces, break some almond cakes, put them in with a few ratafias, a few dried cherries, and pieces of raspberry or strawberry jam.
2. Then put a layer of the custard, continue so till you have piled the dish.
3. You must have a pint of sherry, into which put 2 wineglasses of brandy; upon each layer of the cakes pour some to soak them.
4. When it is all finished, if for butter, whip a pint of cream, with 2 ozs. of sifted sugar, the whites of 2 fresh eggs, lay that on at the last, garnish it with thousands and pieces of green citron, dried cherries.
5. If for meat, heat the whites of 4 eggs, with 2 ozs. of sifted sugar, and the juice of 1/2 a lemon; put it on as you would the cream.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make an Apple Pie
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 good cooking apples
- 4 lb. of loaf sugar
- cloves
- 1 dozen almonds
- 1 oz. of citron
- 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
- puff-paste
- powdered sugar
- white of egg
- water

## Directions

1. Peel and core 4 or 5 good cooking apples, boil them with 4 lb. of loaf sugar and a few cloves
2. When done, beat it up to a cream with a fork
3. Blanch and quarter a dozen almonds
4. Cut up 1 oz. of citron in small pieces
5. When cold, add to it 2 tablespoonfuls of orange-flower water
6. Line your dish with puff-paste, cover it over
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar, and damp with white of egg and water
8. Bake in a quick oven
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Apple Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 apples
- water
- 2 oz. of raisins
- rind of lemon
- a glass of wine
- whites of 6 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and core 6 apples, boil them with a little water, 2 oz. of raisins, a little of the rind of lemon; let them get cold.
2. Add a glass of wine, beat up the whites of 6 eggs with 1/2 lb. of sifted sugar, lay it over the top and bake in a slow oven.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Baked Fruit Puddings
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stew your fruit and let it get cold
2. Make a paste like the former, roll it out the three times
3. Grease the basin and sugar it
4. Roll out the paste in one round, line the basin with it, cut it off all round
5. Those pieces are to form the cover
6. Lay in your fruit, roll out what you cut off, cover it over, press it down
7. Bake it, turn it out like the last
8. These puddings can be filled with gooseberries, plums, damsons, cherries, currants and raspberries, in fact, any fruit in season
9. Always stew your fruit with sufficient sugar to sweeten
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Blancmange with Almond Milk
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- stock from calf's feet or bullock's foot
- almond milk
- thin rind of a lemon
- cinnamon
- cloves
- orange-flower
- vanille
- a glass of rum

## Directions

1. First have the stock ready if you make it from the jelly of calf's feet or bullock's foot
2. Put the quantity after you have warmed it into a saucepan, and add the same proportion of almond milk as you would wine and rum
3. You can flavour it with orange-flower, vanille, and add also a glass of rum
4. Prepare the stock as you would for calf's feet jelly, as far as boiling it with the thin rind of a lemon, cinnamon, and cloves
5. You need not put the white of eggs, as it does not need to be made clear for the almond milk
6. If you use isinglass, you dissolve it in water according to the directions for jelly, and add the almond milk and flavouring
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Bride's Cake
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lb. of raisins well stoned
- 3 lb. of currants well washed and dried
- 1 lb. of good fresh citron cut in slices
- 1 lb. of orange and lemon peel cut in slices
- 1/2 lb. of Valencia almonds blanched and cut in strips
- 2 lb. of moist sugar
- 2 oz. of ground cloves, cinnamon, and nutmeg mixed together
- 3 lb. of fresh butter
- 1/2 pint of rum or brandy
- grated rind of 3 fresh lemons
- 3 lb. of flour
- 36 eggs
- salt
- white paper
- thin wood

## Directions

1. Have a very large pan lined with white
2. Put in the fruit and stir raisins and currants well together
3. Put in the peel and stir
4. Add the almond and spice, sugar and stir
5. Add grated lemon peel and stir this well with a large wooden spoon
6. Add a teaspoonful of salt
7. Keep stirring it till you see that it is well mixed
8. Warm the butter and beat it to a cream
9. Add the butter and stir again for a good half hour
10. Beat up the eggs well till they froth, cracking each one separately
11. Mix this all well together
12. Add the brandy before you put in the flour
13. Stir the flour by degrees till it is all mixed
14. The mixing of it will take about 3 hours and it must look like a rich plum pudding
15. Grease a proper tin well
16. Grease some large sheets of writing paper to line the tin with
17. Put about six thick at the bottom to prevent the cake burning
18. Bake it at home if you have a good oven, if not send it to the baker's
19. A cake of this weight will take 4 or 5 hours to bake
20. The oven must be well regulated, not too quick nor yet too slow
21. When it is well done it must be left to soak for nearly an hour in a very moderate oven
22. It must not be turned out of the tin till the next day
23. Must be kept in a very dry place
24. A bride's cake should be made 3 or 4 weeks before it is wanted
25. The almond and sugar icing should only be put on a week before the wedding
26. You should have a round of thin wood cut the size of the bottom of the cake
27. Cover the wood with white paper gummed on and let it dry well
28. Put the cake on that before you put on the icing
29. After the almond icing is dry, you can purchase the ornaments you would like for it from any of the good confectioners
30. After the sugar icing is nearly dry, you put on the ornaments
31. Cut some tissue paper very fine for the frilling round
32. You can buy the silver bands for round the cake where you buy the ornaments
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Brine for Salting Meat and Tongues
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound salt
- ½ lb. of coarse brown sugar
- ½ oz. saltpetre
- 1 pint of water

## Directions
