1. When the meat or tongue is ready lay it in the pan, rub in the salt and sugar, and the powdered saltpetre, put over the ½ pint of water, press it down and put a heavy weight on it.
2. It must be turned every day.
3. This quantity is only for 1 tongue, 5 or 6 lbs. of meat, and a small shoulder of mutton; if you wish to put beef, tongue, and mutton at the same time, you must double the quantity, it will then be ready in a week.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Butter Cressy Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large carrots
- onion
- 2 oz. of butter
- water
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- fish
- flour
- lemon juice
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil and rub through a sieve 4 large carrots to a pulp
2. Fry an onion in a saucepan with 2 oz. of butter and fry it a light brown
3. Put in pint and 1/2 of water
4. Add the pulp of the carrot with a tooth of garlic chopped very fine, salt and pepper
5. Boil some fish and strain the liquor into the soup
6. If not thick enough stir in a basin a spoonful of flour with the juice of 1/2 a lemon, a little grated nutmeg
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Calf's-feet Jelly
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 calfs feet
- 6 pints of water
- rind of a lemon
- a small piece of whole cinnamon
- 2 cloves
- salt
- a little dried saffron
- 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar
- whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Put on to boil 2 calfs feet in 6 pints of water.
2. Let it boil till every part of the foot comes to pieces; strain it through a fine sieve, let it stand till the next morning (calf's-foot jelly cannot be made the same day).
3. Then take off all the fat and wipe the top with a clean cloth.
4. The liquor will have reduced to a quart; put it in a clean saucepan with the rind of a lemon, cut very thin, not a particle of the white or it will taste bitter; a small piece of whole cinnamon, 2 cloves, a pinch of salt, a little dried saffron, 1/2 lb. of loaf sugar.
5. Beat the whites of 2 eggs with the shells, stir it in well.
6. Set it on to boil, keep stirring all the time; when the whites of the eggs set on the top thick
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Chili Vinegar
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of the best vinegar
- 2 dozen ripe chilis
- 1 dozen of ripe chilis to every wine bottle of vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil a quart of the best vinegar
2. Pour it over 2 dozen ripe chilis if not for immediate use
3. To every wine bottle of vinegar put in 1 dozen of ripe chilis
4. Cork it down
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Cocoa Nut Sweet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- 1 glass of orange-flower water
- 1 lb. of grated cocoa-nut
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 lb. of loaf sugar with 1/2 pint of water and 1 glass of orange-flower water
2. Have ready 1 lb. of grated cocoa-nut
3. When the sugar has become a thick syrup, stir in the cocoa-nut
4. Let it boil till it looks quite a preserve
5. Part it in small glasses with a little cinnamon on top
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Fish Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- 2 ounces of butter
- vegetables
- water
- salt
- pepper
- blade of mace
- gurnet
- turbot
- fish
- small fish
- bread balls
- flour
- browning
- butter sauce
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Slice an onion thin in a saucepan and fry it in 2 ounces of butter
2. Have ready all kinds of vegetables, cut as for a julienne soup
3. Put in the saucepan pint of water, with salt, pepper, a blade of mace
4. Let it all stew well
5. Then put on a gurnet, a piece of turbot, or any other fish you intend dining off to boil with a little salt in a pint and half of water
6. When it is done, take it out and put it at the side to keep hot
7. Strain the water on to the vegetables
8. Have ready some small fish or bread balls and boil them in with the vegetables
9. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour with a little browning to thicken the soup
10. Serve the gurnet with a good butter sauce with capers, and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Fritters of the Meal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- meal
- eggs
- lemon peel rasped
- 1 oz. of ground almonds
- boiling oil
- sugar
- cinnamon
- clarified sugar
- lemon juice
- rum

## Directions

1. Sieve some meal very fine
2. To every spoon of meal allow 1 egg
3. Mix it to a batter with some lemon peel rasped and 1 oz. of ground almonds
4. Have a pan with boiling oil
5. Take up a spoonful at the time and put them in as round as you can
6. They will form a round ball
7. Sprinkle over some sugar and cinnamon
8. Serve them with clarified sugar, flavoured with lemon juice and rum
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Gingerlee
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of seed
- 1 lb. of honey
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 lb. of almonds blanched and toasted

## Directions

1. Pick clean 1 lb. of seed, put it in a pan to roast, over a slow fire, stirring it all the while with a spoon
2. It will take an hour or more
3. When toasted, turn the seed out
4. put in a saucepan kept for sweets 1 lb. of honey, 1 lb. of loaf sugar, melt it
5. when dissolved, put in the toasted seed
6. let it simmer
7. have ready 1 lb. of almonds blanched and toasted a light brown
8. When the seed boils put in the almonds, stir it well all together to prevent it sticking
9. when it gets hard, which you can tell by putting a spoonful on a plate if it sets
10. oil a clean board, turn the sweet on it
11. damp the rolling-pin with cold water, roll the sweet out about 1 inch thick
12. cut it in strips, then cut those strips in squares, or any shape you like
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Gravies
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- little bones of the rib or any little trimmings
- pepper and salt
- a few drops of browning

## Directions

1. Boil the little bones of the rib or any little trimming you do not want with a little pepper and salt.
2. Strain and colour with a few drops of browning.
3. Make it boiling hot and pour over your roast.
4. Baste it backwards and forwards with a spoon over the joint.
5. If for poultry, boil the legs and the neck with a small onion.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Jelly with Isinglass Instead of Calf's-Feet
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. of isinglass
- 1 quart of water

## Directions

1. Dissolve 2 oz. of isinglass in 1 quart of water
2. Make it exactly the same as the directions for calf's feet jelly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Lemon Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 14 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of rose water
- raspings and juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. The eggs must be very fresh
2. Separate the whites from the yolks
3. Beat the whites up to a strong snow
4. Beat the yolks well
5. Add the powdered sugar, the raspings of the lemon, and the rose water
6. Beat this altogether for half an hour till it becomes quite light
7. Beat the whites to a strong snow
8. Mix it very lightly with your other ingredients with a spoon
9. When well mixed add the flour
10. Put on the baking tin 3 greased sheets of paper
11. Bake these biscuits in oval small tins well greased, in a moderate oven; the less they are handled the better
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Lemon Water Ice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of syrup
- juice of 4 lemons
- fresh rind of lemon
- lump of sugar

## Directions

1. Add to the pint of syrup the juice of 4 lemons well-strained
2. Grate some of the fresh rind on a lump of sugar
3. Put it in
4. Mix it well for 10 minutes
5. Put it in the freezer, and work same as last
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Lemonade
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- rinds of 4 very fresh lemons
- 2 quarts of water
- 4 lemons
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 pint of water

## Directions

1. Take the rinds of 4 very fresh lemons and cut up as fine as possible
2. Put it in 2 quarts of water
3. Cut the lemons in halves and squeeze them through a sieve into the water
4. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar with a pint of water to a syrup
5. When cold, turn the syrup into the water and stir it all well together
6. If not acid enough put more lemon, or more sugar if required
7. Let it stand for an hour before using
8. This drink is very nice iced in the summer
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Masapanes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of good Valencia almonds
- 1 lb. of best loaf sugar
- half a pint of water
- yolks of 6 eggs
- 2 whole eggs
- 6 egg whites
- sufficient flour
- orange-flower water
- juice of a lemon
- a tablespoonful of the best oil

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound very fine 1 lb. of good Valencia almonds
2. Clarify 1 lb. of best loaf sugar in half a pint of water till it comes quite thick
3. Add the almonds, stirring it well together till it is well mixed
4. Put it away till the next day
5. Take the yolks of 6 eggs quite free from any of the whites
6. Mix them well with the mixture you put aside
7. Flavour with orange-flower water
8. Beat up 2 whole eggs with the 6 whites
9. Mix with sufficient flour to make a paste and add to the eggs
10. When beat, add the juice of a lemon and a tablespoonful of the best oil
11. Roll it out on your pasteboard and cut out rounds with a good size tumbler
12. Pinch up those rounds into a little square shape with 4 corners
13. Lay them on a baking tin slightly floured
14. Put a spoonful of the almond mixture into each
15. Place in a very quick oven for not more than 10 minutes to set the paste
16. When they come out of the oven, let them get cold
17. Brush over the top with thick clarified sugar
18. Sprinkle over each a few thousands
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Meringues
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 whites of fresh eggs
- 1 lb. of fine sifted sugar
- lemon or vanille for flavouring

## Directions

1. Beat up the whites of eggs to a snow
2. When firm, add in the sifted sugar by degrees, flavoured with any flavouring you like
3. If using vanille, take a small piece of vanille and pound it with a little of the sugar
4. If using lemon, rasp the rind off with 2 or 3 pieces of loaf sugar, pound it and add it to the other
5. When it is beaten up quite firm, have a clean piece of board that will go in the oven, cover it with writing paper
6. dust the paper over lightly with sifted sugar
7. take up a spoonful of the mixture the exact shape of the bowl of the spoon
8. do not let one touch the other
9. Place the board in a very cool oven
10. when just a very light brown take them out, turn them over very lightly
11. press in the liquid part with the back of the spoon to form the hollow
12. place them back in the oven to dry that part
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Orange Water Ice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of syrup
- juice of 6 good sized oranges
- 2 or 3 lumps of sugar
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Add to the pint of syrup the juice of 6 good sized oranges well strained
2. Rub off the essence from the rinds with 2 or 3 lumps of sugar
3. Dissolve it in the juice
4. Add the juice of 1 lemon to throw up the flavour of the oranges
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Pancakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of dry flour
- 1 teacupful of milk
- salt
- chopped rind of lemon cut very fine
- butter
- apple chopped fine
- powdered sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make a batter of 3 eggs, 3 tablespoonfuls of dry flour, 1 teacupful of milk, a little salt, and a little chopped rind of lemon cut very fine
2. Take a very clean pan and put a very small piece of butter
3. Drop in some of the batter
4. A little apple chopped fine in the batter is a great improvement
5. They can be fried in oil
6. If for dinner, instead of the milk add another egg and a little water
7. Sprinkle over them powdered sugar and a little lemon juice
8. They must be sent up very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Potato Flour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fine large potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Have 2 very clean flat pans and a new grater
2. Peel and wash the potatoes, divide them in halves
3. Put some water in each pan
4. Grate the potatoes into one, then pour the water off into the other
5. Continue doing this until there is no more sediment left
6. Run the white sediment at the bottom of the pan onto clean white paper
7. Put the paper with the flour near the fire to dry
8. When dry, it will be a beautiful white powder
9. When thoroughly dry you can use it like ordinary flour for thickening sauces or gravies
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Potato Fritters
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 floury potatoes
- a little salt
- 4 eggs
- a cup of almond milk (optional)
- boiling oil
- cinnamon
- sugar
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 4 floury potatoes
2. Mash them with a little salt until smooth, like a cream
3. Beat up 4 eggs with or without a cup of almond milk
4. Mix it well with the mashed potatoes like a butter
5. Have a pan with boiling oil
6. Put them in by a spoonful for each fritter
7. Let them drain from the oil
8. Arrange them in a glass dish
9. Put over some cinnamon and sugar
10. Pour over some clarified sugar made like for German puffs
11. Turn them over so that both sides are soaked alike
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Punch
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- two fine fresh lemons
- sugar lumps
- four ounces of powdered sugar
- one pint and a half of very hot water
- a quarter of a pint of rum
- a quarter of a pint of brandy

## Directions

1. Rub off the outer peel of two fine fresh lemons upon a few lumps of sugar
2. Put the sugar into a bowl with four ounces of powdered sugar
3. Press the juice of the lemons upon it
4. Pour over one pint and a half of very hot water that has not boiled
5. Add a quarter of a pint of rum and the same quantity of brandy
6. Stir well together and strain it
7. Let it stand a few minutes before it is drank

title: To Make Punch Ice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of syrup
- juice of 3 large lemons
- 2 wine-glasses of rum

## Directions

1. Add to the pint of syrup the juice of 3 large lemons well strained, and 2 wine-glasses of rum
2. Stir it all well together for about 10 minutes
3. Put it in the freezer, and work the same as described before
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Strawberry Jam
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- 1 lb. sugar to 1 lb. of strawberries

## Directions

1. Get the strawberries dry
2. Lay the quantity of fruit you intend to preserve the day before in an equal quantity of sugar
3. The next morning put it in the preserving-pan or a new saucepan
4. Stand it on a clear fire
5. Let it simmer for a little while
6. If you find too much syrup, drain it off, and boil it up, strain it, bottle it
7. Give it a quick boil till finished
8. Drop a little on a plate, and let it get cold
9. If you find it hard, put it in the jam pots
10. Let it stand till next day
11. Put on a little tissue paper steeped in rum or brandy
12. Cover them over with paper brushed over with white of egg
13. Keep in a dry place
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Tea Cakes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of roll dough
- 1/2 lb. of fresh butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1 egg
- a cup of milk
- a small pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Beat egg into a cup of milk
2. Warm the butter in the milk
3. Mix all well together
4. Divide the mixture into 4 pieces
5. Shape them round
6. Bake them in a brisk oven for 20 or 30 minutes
7. If you make them hot for tea, cut them open and butter them, or toast them first
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make Tomato Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen large ripe tomatoes
- salt
- white pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls of oil
- 2 teeth of garlic
- a little chili vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a dozen large ripe tomatoes
2. Slice them
3. Put them in a preserving pan
4. Let them simmer over a slow fire till all the juice is dry
5. Pulp it through a sieve
6. Add salt and white pepper
7. Fry 2 tablespoonfuls of oil with 2 teeth of garlic
8. When the garlic is brown take it out
9. Put in the pulp with a little chili vinegar to flavour
10. Do not make it too hot
11. Boil it for 10 minutes over a slow fire
12. When quite cold put it in small bottles
13. Cork tightly and seal them with melted rosin
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Make White Hard Soap
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six pounds of fat
- hot water
- one can of Babbitt's lye
- one quart of cold water
- two tablespoons of ammonia

## Directions

1. Save every scrap of fat each day; try out all that has accumulated, however small the quantity.
2. This is done by placing the scraps in a frying-pan on the back of the range.
3. If the heat is low, and the grease is not allowed to get hot enough to smoke or burn, there will be no odor from it.
4. Turn the melted grease into tin pails and keep them covered.
5. When six pounds of fat have been obtained, turn it into a dish-pan; add a generous amount of hot water, and stand it on the range until the grease is entirely melted.
6. Stir it well together; then stand it aside to cool.
7. The clean grease will rise to the top, and when it has cooled can be taken off in a cake, and such impurities as have not settled in the water can be scraped off the bottom of the cake of fat.
8. Put the clean grease into the dish-pan and melt it.
9. Put a can of Babbitt's lye in a tin pail; add to it a quart of cold water, and stir it with a stick or wooden spoon until it is dissolved.
10. It will get hot when the water is added; let it stand until it cools.
11. Remove the melted grease from the fire, and pour in the lye slowly, stirring all the time.
12. Add two tablespoons of ammonia.
13. Stir the mixture constantly for twenty minutes or half an hour, or until the soap begins to set.
14. Let it stand until perfectly hard; then cut it into square cakes.

title: To Make Wine or Rum Sauce
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 eggs
- a spoonful of sugar
- a cup of water or almond milk
- a glass of rum or 2 of wine
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Beat up 2 or 3 eggs well with a spoonful of sugar
2. Add a cup of water or almond milk
3. Add a glass of rum or 2 of wine and the juice of a lemon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Mash Potatoes with Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- 2 oz. of butter
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Boil 4 or 5 potatoes cut in pieces, put on with a very little water, a little salt and pepper.
2. Let them boil, when done mash them well with a fork.
3. Put in 2 oz. of butter, 1 egg, mash it quite smooth.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Mix Jelly and Blancmange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of jelly
- 1 pint of blancmange
- pink sugar from the confectioners

## Directions

1. Just slightly grease your 2 pint moulds
2. To make the blancmange pink you must get the pink sugar from the confectioners; they sell it all ready, and when you make the blancmange you colour it with that
3. Colour half of the blancmange and leave the other part white
4. Put in to one mould a portion of the pink, into the other a portion of the white
5. Put them both in a cold place to set
6. When quite cold, pour in a layer of the jelly (it need only be just warm like a thick liquid) into both moulds
7. Return them to the cold place
8. When set, use the remainder of the blancmange between the 2 moulds, let that set
9. Then divide the jelly between the two moulds
10. When turned out, they look very pretty
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Pickle Beetroot
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 fine beetroots
- salt
- ground pepper
- a few allspice
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Parboil 2 or 3 fine beetroots
2. Skin them and let them drain well
3. Slice them
4. Put them in a jar with salt and ground pepper, a few allspice
5. Boil as much vinegar as you require to cover them
6. Pour it over boiling hot
7. Tie down when cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Pickle Cauliflowers
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflowers
- brine
- spiced vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the stalks and leaves, break the flower into pieces
2. Parboil them in brine
3. Drain them, and lay them in a jar
4. Pour over boiling spiced vinegar

title: To Pickle Gherkins and French Beans
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine fresh gherkins
- brine
- vinegar
- spices
- fresh vine leaves

## Directions

1. Choose some fine fresh gherkins, and set them to soak in brine for a week.
2. Drain them, and pour over boiling vinegar, prepared with the usual spices, first having covered them with fresh vine leaves.
3. If they do not appear to be of a fine green, pour off the vinegar, boil it up again, cover the gherkins with fresh green vine leaves, and pour over the vinegar again.
4. French beans are pickled exactly the same.

title: To Pickle Lemon
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- very fresh lemons
- salt
- 2 or 3 teeth of garlic to each bottle
- 3 or 4 cloves
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Take some very fresh lemons
2. Cut them in quarters
3. Put them in glass bottles with plenty of salt, 2 or 3 teeth of garlic to each bottle, 3 or 4 cloves
4. Fill the bottle up with cold vinegar
5. Cork tight
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Pickle Melon Mangoes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- melons
- brine
- spices
- mustard-seed
- garlic
- spiced vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the melons in half
2. Remove the pulpy part and the seeds
3. Soak the halves for a week in strong brine
4. Fill them with the usual spices, mustard-seed and garlic
5. Tie them together with packthread
6. Put them in jars
7. Pour over boiling spiced vinegar

title: To Pickle Melon Mangoes
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- melons
- strong brine
- spices
- mustard-seed
- garlic
- packthread
- spiced vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the melons in half, remove the pulpy part and the seeds.
2. Soak the halves for a week in strong brine.
3. Fill them with the usual spices, mustard-seed and garlic.
4. Tie them together with packthread.
5. Put them in jars, and pour over boiling spiced vinegar.

title: To Pickle Mushrooms
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- small button mushrooms
- salt
- distilled vinegar
- mace

## Directions

1. Choose small button mushrooms, clean and wipe them
2. Throw them into cold water
3. Put into a stewpan with a little salt
4. Cover them with distilled vinegar
5. Simmer a few minutes
6. Put them in bottles with a couple of blades or so of mace
7. When cold, cork them closely

title: To Pickle Onions
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- brine
- distilled vinegar
- white wine vinegar
- mace
- ginger
- whole pepper

## Directions

1. Choose all of a size and soak in boiling brine
2. When cold, drain them and put them in bottles
3. Fill up with hot distilled vinegar; if they are to be white, use white wine vinegar; if they are to be brown, use the best distilled vinegar
4. In both cases, add a little mace, ginger, and whole pepper

title: To Pickle Walnuts
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts
- brine
- spiced vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak in brine for a week
2. Prick them
3. Simmer in brine
4. Let them lay on a sieve to drain, and to turn black
5. Place them in jars
6. Pour over boiling spiced vinegar

title: To Pickle White and Red Cabbage
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- white and red cabbage
- salt
- vinegar
- spices
- cochineal powder
- beet-root
- turmeric powder

## Directions

1. Take off the outside leaves, cut out the stalk
2. Shred the cabbage into a cullender
3. Sprinkle with salt
4. Let it remain for twenty-four hours
5. Drain it
6. Put it into jars
7. Fill up with boiling vinegar, prepared with the usual spices
8. If the cabbage is red, add a little cochineal powdered, or a slice or two of beet-root to make the pickle a fine colour
9. If it is white cabbage, add instead, a little turmeric powder

title: To Preserve Orange and Lemon Peel
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges and lemons
- cold water
- loaf sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Clear the insides out when you have squeezed the juice from them
2. Lay them in cold water for 3 or 4 days, change the water each day
3. Boil them tender
4. Put some loaf sugar in a clean saucepan with sufficient water to make a thin syrup
5. Boil the peels in that for 2 or 3 hours, simmer gently
6. Turn them into a basin
7. Put them on again the next day, let them simmer again for an hour
8. If the syrup is not thick enough, add some more sugar; the syrup must be thick
9. Put them in a jar, they are ready for use directly they are done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Preserve Seville Oranges Whole
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen Seville oranges
- salt
- water
- 3 lbs of sugar
- 3 pints of water

## Directions

1. Take a dozen oranges of equal sizes
2. Lay them in salt and water for 6 or 7 days, changing the water each day
3. At the end of the time dry them well
4. Carve each one in different patterns with a small knife
5. Put in a preserving-pan 3 lbs of sugar and set it on the fire with 3 pints of water and let it boil
6. Put in the oranges and let them simmer gently for an hour
7. Take out the oranges and lay them in a sieve to drain
8. Repeat this process for 3 days
9. On the 4th add another pound of sugar to the syrup
10. Put in the oranges and let them boil fast for half an hour
11. The syrup will then be very thick
12. Put the oranges in a glass jar and pour over the syrup
13. Let them get thoroughly cold and fasten it down tight
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Preserve Strawberries Whole
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 2 pints of water
- 2 lbs. of fine, fresh, hard strawberries
- brandy
- white of eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a preserving-pan 3 lbs. of loaf sugar with 2 pints of water, let it boil to a syrup, skim it as it boils up.
2. Have 2 lbs. of fine, fresh, hard strawberries, pick off the stalks, let them simmer in the syrup for 10 minutes, do this for 2 days.
3. Boil up the syrup very stiff, put the strawberries in pots, and pour over the thick syrup; let them get quite cold, therefore they must not be fastened down till the next day.
4. Cut round papers the size of the top of the jar, dip in brandy, then cover them over with papers brushed over with white of eggs.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Preserve Strawberries Whole
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of loaf sugar
- 2 pints of water
- 2 lbs. of fine, fresh, hard strawberries

## Directions

1. Put in a preserving-pan 3 lbs. of loaf sugar with 2 pints of water and let it boil to a syrup
2. Skim it as it boils up
3. Have 2 lbs. of fine, fresh, hard strawberries, pick off the stalks
4. Let them simmer in the syrup for 10 minutes
5. Do this for 2 days
6. Then boil up the syrup very stiff and put the strawberries in
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Remove Tan
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cucumber
- orange flower-water
- a small piece of camphor
- a wine-glass of soft water

## Directions

1. Cut a cucumber into pieces after having peeled it
2. Let the juice drain from it for twelve hours
3. Pour it off, and add to it an equal quantity of orange flower-water, with a small piece of camphor dissolved in a wine-glass of soft water
4. Bottle the mixture
5. Wash the parts that have been exposed to the sun two or three times in the twenty-four hours

title: To Render Goose, Duck or Beef Fat
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- fat cut into small pieces
- cold water
- raw potato slices (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the fat into small pieces.
2. Put in a deep, iron kettle and cover with cold water.
3. Place on the stove uncovered; when the water has nearly all evaporated, set the kettle back and let the fat try out slowly.
4. When the fat is still and scraps are shriveled and crisp at the bottom of the kettle, strain the fat through a cloth into a stone crock, cover and set it away in a cool place.
5. Alternatively, the water may be omitted and the scraps slowly tried out on back of stove or in moderate oven.
6. When fat is tried out, pour in crock.
7. Several slices of raw potato put with the fat will aid in the clarifying.

title: To Roast a Fillet of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of veal
- round bone
- bread, thick piece
- suet, 2 oz.
- lemon rind
- parsley, chopped
- powdered marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take out the round bone
2. Make a good stuffing: soak a thick piece of bread, drain well from water, chop 2 oz. of suet with a little lemon rind, a little chopped parsley, a little powdered marjoram, salt, pepper, and a little grated lemon peel, mix well with an egg
3. Fill the cavity with the stuffing
4. Salt and pepper the joint well
5. Dredge it with flour
6. Roast for about 3 hours to a beautiful brown
7. Dish it up with a nice gravy mixed with the juice of a lemon well strained
8. Throw the gravy over the meat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Goose
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- large onion
- sage leaves
- bread crumbs
- chopped suet
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- greased paper
- gravy
- apple sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the goose first by drying it well with a clean cloth
2. Parboil a large onion, chop it with about a dozen leaves of sage
3. Mix 2 oz. of bread crumbs, 1 oz. of chopped suet, pepper, salt, season rather high
4. Mix it well together, no eggs
5. Put it into the bottom part of the goose
6. Turn the rump inside, sew the bottom part together to secure the stuffing
7. Put on the pepper and salt
8. Dredge it over with flour
9. Skewer a greased paper over the breast
10. Remove it half an hour before serving it
11. Must be constantly basted, and roasted before a brisk fire to keep it crisp
12. Pour over a good gravy boiling hot, do not put too much in the dish
13. Send up a sauce tureen with some hot gravy, and one with apple sauce
14. A large goose will take 1 hour and a half to cook
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Leg of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, 8 to 10 lbs.
- force meat or veal stuffing
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Porg the leg
2. Make a nice stuffing of force meat or veal stuffing and put it in where the porging was taken from
3. Restore it to its original shape
4. Season well with pepper and salt
5. Sprinkle a little flour over it
6. Keep it well basted
7. Roast for two hours
8. Have a nice gravy for it
9. Send up with some currant jelly in a small glass dish
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Loin of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- loin of mutton, 4 to 5 lbs.
- pepper
- salt
- gravy
- jelly

## Directions

1. Porg the loin before using
2. Season well with pepper and salt
3. Roast for 1.5 hours
4. Serve with a good gravy and jelly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Saddle of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- paper, greased
- flour
- gravy
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Porg the saddle
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Lay over the back a sheet of paper well greased
4. Keep papered till within a half hour of sending it up
5. Remove the paper and dredge a little flour over it
6. Let it brown nicely
7. Roast very gently for about 2.5 hours
8. Send up very hot with a good gravy
9. Serve with currant jelly
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Sirloin of Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin of beef, 14 to 16 lbs.
- salt
- pepper
- browning
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly the same as ribs
2. Roast for about 3 hours
3. Dish up in a well-dish with horseradish round
4. Serve with a good gravy, adding a little browning to make it look rich
5. Strain the gravy carefully
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast a Turkey
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- water
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- white paper, greased
- stuffing

## Directions

1. Put it in water and salt it
2. Let all the water drain out of the inside
3. Dry the outside well with a clean cloth
4. Prepare the stuffing and put it in at the breast
5. Plump it nicely and sew the skin with a needle and thread
6. Do not fill it too much or the skin might burst
7. Rub it well outside with mixed pepper and salt
8. Flour it lightly
9. Lay it in a dry dish and leave it for one hour before roasting
10. Put a sheet of white paper well greased over the breast to prevent it being scorched
11. Remove it an hour before sending it up
12. Dredge it lightly with flour
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast Ducks
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- stuffing
- greased paper
- gravy
- apple sauce

## Directions

1. Ducks are prepared precisely the same as geese for roasting
2. They are also stuffed at the lower end
3. Cover the breast with greased paper
4. Serve with boiling gravy and apple sauce
5. A good-sized duck will take 1 hour
6. Dish it up very hot
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast Fowls
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- stuffing
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- greased paper
- gravy
- bread sauce
- water-cresses

## Directions

1. Prepare fowls for roasting the same as turkeys, well dry them
2. Put in the stuffing at the breast
3. Sew the piece of skin on to the backbone, but not too tight
4. Season them well
5. Dredge with flour
6. Put greased paper over the breast
7. Remove the paper a quarter of an hour before you dish them up
8. Baste the part well where the paper was
9. Have a little gravy nicely coloured, pour some over the fowls boiling hot
10. Serve with bread sauce
11. Garnish them with water-cresses
12. One hour to roast a good-size fowl
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast Partridges and Pheasants
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- pheasants
- bread sauce

## Directions

1. Roast partridges rather more than half an hour
2. Roast pheasants three-quarters of an hour if small, otherwise an hour
3. Serve with bread sauce

title: To Roast Ribs of Beef
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- ribs of beef
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Roll them nicely, and skewer them light, if necessary tie them round with string
2. Put plenty of salt and pepper, and dredge them over with flour
3. Under the weight of 10 or 12 lbs. time for roasting will be 2½ hours, with a good fire
4. Keep basting constantly
5. When done dish them up in a well dish
6. Strain the fat from the dripping pan through a sieve
7. Take the brown part, add a little more salt and pepper
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast the Best End of Neck of Veal
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- best end neck of veal, 3 to 4 pounds
- smoked beef or choriza
- salt
- pepper
- gravy
- lemon juice
- cayenne
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Divide the flesh at the top from the bones gently with your hand
2. Have ready some slices of smoked beef or a piece of choriza, cut lengthways
3. Lay it in and skewer up the flesh to the bone
4. Season it with salt and pepper
5. Hang it down to roast to a brisk fire for 2 hours
6. Dish it up with a good gravy and the juice of a lemon
7. A little cayenne is a great improvement to veal
8. Tomato sauce is very relishing with roast veal
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast the Best End of the Neck of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- best end of neck of mutton, 3 to 4 pounds
- gravy
- mashed turnips

## Directions

1. Roast for 1.5 hours
2. Serve it up very hot
3. Serve with a good gravy
4. Send up with mashed turnips
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Roast Venison
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison
- salt
- writing paper
- clarified fat
- flour
- water
- packthread
- white kitchen paper

## Directions

1. Wipe the venison dry
2. Sprinkle with salt
3. Cover with writing paper rubbed with clarified fat
4. Cover this with a thick paste made of flour and water
5. Tie with packthread white kitchen paper, so as to prevent the paste coming off
6. Set the venison before a strong fire
7. Baste it directly and continue until it is nearly done
8. Remove the paper, paste, etc.
9. Draw the venison nearer the fire
10. Dredge it with flour
11. Continue basting
12. It should only take a light brown, and should be rather under than over-done
13. A large haunch requires from three to four hours roasting, a small one not above three
14. Serve with the knuckle, garnished with a fringe of white paper, and with gravy and red currant jelly, either cold or melted, in port wine, and served hot

title: To Salt Beef
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of common salt
- half an ounce of saltpetre
- one ounce of coarse brown sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a pound of common salt, half an ounce of saltpetre and one ounce of coarse brown sugar
2. Rub the meat well with it, daily for a fortnight or less, according to the weather, and the degree of salt that the meat is required to have
3. Alternatively, boil eight ounces of salt, eight ounces of sugar, and half an ounce of saltpetre in two quarts of water, and pour it over the meat
4. Let it stand in it for eight or ten days

title: To Stew Apples
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small pippins
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of cold water
- orange-flower water
- marmalade

## Directions

1. Take 12 small pippins and peel them very thin
2. Lay them carefully in a stewpan with 1 lb. of loaf sugar, 1/2 pint of cold water, and a little orange-flower water
3. When the apples are nearly done, take them out and place them in a dish
4. When the syrup is cold, pour it over the apples
5. Place a little marmalade on each one
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Beef Sweet and Sour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 pounds of beef
- an onion
- 2 teeth of garlic
- a few cloves
- allspice
- salt and pepper
- pint of vinegar
- a spoonful of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of brown sugar
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Put in a deep dish 4 or 5 pounds of beef with an onion sliced, 2 teeth of garlic, a few cloves, and allspice, salt, and pepper.
2. Pour over it pint of vinegar.
3. Leave it for four or five days, turning it each day.
4. When you are ready to cook it put it in a stewpan with the vinegar and all the other ingredients.
5. Let it simmer, it will only take an hour-and-a-half.
6. Take the meat out of the saucepan as it becomes tender.
7. Strain the liquor through a sieve.
8. Put the meat back in the saucepan and throw over what you strained.
9. Rub in a basin a spoonful of flour, 2 of brown sugar, a little browning to colour.
10. Mix it all well with the liquor in the saucepan.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Cabbage and Rice
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 white cabbage
- salt
- 1/2 lb. of rice
- 1 onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls of oil or 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 pint of boiling water
- pepper
- dried saffron

## Directions

1. Get a nice white cabbage very hard, wash it well, lay it in water with a little salt, then drain it well.
2. Divide it into halves, slice very fine on a board.
3. Have washed 1/2 lb. of rice.
4. Take an onion, slice it into a stewpan with 2 tablespoonfuls of oil or 1/2 lb. of butter, let it fry.
5. Add 1 pint of boiling water, salt, and pepper, a little dried saffron.
6. Put in the chopped cabbage.
7. Let it cook for 1/2 an hour, then put in the rice, let it simmer till done, it will be set like a pudding, quite dry from the liquor.
8. Dish it up.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Carp Sweet and Sour
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- large Spanish onion
- one tablespoonful of oil
- salt
- pepper
- 3 cloves
- 3 allspice
- ground mace
- 1/2 pint of ale or porter
- pennyworth of gingerbread
- brown sugar
- vinegar
- browning
- lemon
- chopped parsley
- port wine

## Directions

1. Wash and cleanse the carp from the blood
2. Cut a good sized carp in 6 pieces
3. Slice in a stewpan a large Spanish onion with one tablespoonful of oil, salt, pepper, 3 cloves, 3 allspice, a little ground mace
4. Put on 1/2 pint of ale or porter and set it on to boil
5. Add the carp with pennyworth of gingerbread, crumble it to dust
6. When the carp is done, lay it in the dish you intend sending to table
7. Rub the liquor through a sieve and put it back into the saucepan
8. Mix in a basin a spoonful of brown sugar with 3 of vinegar
9. If not brown enough from the gingerbread, add little browning
10. Stir it all smooth and put it over the fish with a spoon
11. Cover it all over alike
12. Cut slice of lemon and garnish the dish with them
13. On top of each slice put a little chopped parsley as green as possible
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Cold Poultry with Vegetables
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Remnants of cold fowl or duck
- peas
- 1 onion
- a little dripping
- a good size cup of gravy
- 1/2 peck of peas
- a couple of spoonfuls of sugar
- pepper
- salt
- saffron
- turnips, Jerusalem artichokes, or small new potatoes

## Directions

1. Fry an onion sliced in a little dripping, pour in a good size cup of gravy, made from odd pieces.
2. Wash and drain well 1/2 peck of peas, put them with a couple of spoonfuls of sugar, pepper, salt.
3. When nearly done lay in the pieces of fowl or duck.
4. When done dish up, poultry in the centre of the dish, and vegetables round.
5. They are very nice done with turnips, Jerusalem artichokes, or small new potatoes, for all these vegetables colour with saffron, and dish up very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Duck with Green Peas
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- one onion
- mint
- one pint of beef gravy
- one quart of green peas
- lump sugar

## Directions

1. Stuff and half roast a duck
2. Put it into a stew-pan with an onion sliced and a little mint
3. Add about one pint of beef gravy
4. Simmer for half an hour
5. Add a quart of green peas and a little lump sugar
6. Simmer another half hour

title: To Stew Fowl—Brown
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 1 onion
- a tooth of garlic
- some marjoram
- lemon thyme
- a little walnut liquor or mushroom ketchup
- a few balls
- a spoonful of flour
- the juice of a lemon
- a little browning

## Directions

1. Cut up the fowl as for the last stew, put in a stewpan an onion chopped, also a tooth of garlic, some marjoram and lemon thyme, a little walnut liquor or mushroom ketchup.
2. Then place in the pieces of fowl, let it boil up, then put it at the side to simmer, put in a few balls.
3. When done rub in a basin a spoonful of flour, the juice of a lemon, a little browning to colour, stir it into the stew.
4. Dish it up.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Globe Artichokes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fresh globe artichokes
- 1 pint of oil
- 2 teeth of garlic
- 1 tea-cup of boiling water
- salt
- pepper
- a little dried saffron
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Take 6 fresh ones, cut off the stalks, peel them, and cut them in pieces
2. Take off all the hard leaves, cut them in quarters, cut out the chokes, wash them well
3. Put in a stewpan 1 pint of oil, cut in 2 teeth of garlic, let it fry a light brown
4. Take out the garlic, put in a tea-cup of boiling water with salt and pepper
5. Lay in the artichokes with a little dried saffron, let them stew till quite tender
6. Add the juice of a lemon, mix it well with the liquor
7. Dish them up in a deep dish. To be eaten cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Halibut's Head
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 onions
- 3 tablespoonfuls of oil
- pint of water
- salt
- pepper
- ground mace
- pieces of halibut head
- flesh of the halibut
- piece of the liver
- 4 eggs
- dried saffron
- chopped parsley
- juice of 2 large lemons or more

## Directions

1. Slice in a stewpan 3 or 4 onions with 3 tablespoonfuls of oil
2. Let it fry
3. Put in pint of water, salt, pepper, ground mace
4. Lay in the pieces of the head
5. Make a few balls of some of the flesh of the halibut and a piece of the liver
6. Lay them on top
7. Have a good egg sauce ready to pour over it
8. Rub in a basin 4 eggs, a little dried saffron, chopped parsley and the juice of 2 large lemons or more, according to taste
9. Dish it up very carefully
10. Lay the balls round
11. Pour over some of the sauce
12. Put the remainder in a sauce-boat
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Jerusalem Artichokes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- 1 onion
- 1 ounce of butter
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1 teacup of milk
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Before peeling the artichokes wash them well, free of all dirt, in 2 or 3 waters.
2. Dry them on a coarse cloth, then peel them and put them in cold water for 10 minutes.
3. Chop an onion very fine like a mince, put it in a saucepan with 1 ounce of butter, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, 1 teacup of milk.
4. Cut the artichokes in halves, let them simmer till done, careful they do not break.
5. Stir in a basin a teaspoonful of flour with a little water.
6. Pour it over the artichokes, dish them up.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Normandy Pippins
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- Normandy pippins
- water
- brown sugar
- rind of a lemon
- a few cloves

## Directions

1. Lay pippins in water for 2 or 3 hours
2. Use 1/2 pint of water to 1 lb. of pippins
3. Put 1 lb of brown sugar with a little of the rind of a lemon and a few cloves
4. Let them boil well
5. Put them in a dish and pour the syrup over the pippins
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Pears
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fine-looking pears
- 1 lb. of brown sugar
- 1 pint of water
- rind of a lemon
- a few cloves
- 2 spoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take 6 fine-looking pears, halve them, and take out the core
2. Lay them in a stewpan with 1 lb. of brown sugar, 1 pint of water, the rind of a lemon, a few cloves, and 2 spoonfuls of vinegar
3. Stew them gently for 4 or 5 hours
4. The vinegar will make them red as coral
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Peas with Eggs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion
- 2 spoonfuls of oil or 1/2 lb. of butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1 peck of peas
- 1 teacup of boiling water
- 3 or 4 lumps of sugar
- fresh mint
- 5 or 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Fry an onion sliced in 2 spoonfuls of oil, or 1/2 lb. of butter; salt and pepper.
2. Pick carefully 1 peck of peas, wash and drain them.
3. Put them in the saucepan with the oil and onion.
4. Add a teacup of boiling water, 3 or 4 lumps of sugar, chop a little fresh mint.
5. Let it all cook till the peas are done.
6. Break 5 or 6 eggs, each one separately in a cup, put them on the peas.
7. Let them cook till they are done, dish up carefully.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Potatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 large potatoes
- 1 onion
- 2 oz. of butter
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- flour
- 1/2 pint of cold milk
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel 4 or 5 large potatoes, cut them in square pieces.
2. Chop an onion very fine, put it in a saucepan with 2 oz. of butter, let it fry.
3. Add the pieces of potatoes, with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg.
4. Sprinkle over some flour very lightly, and stir it all together with a spoon.
5. Pour over 1/2 pint of cold milk.
6. Set them on to cook, let them simmer till done, be careful the potatoes do not break.
7. When done the sauce will look like cream.
8. Turn them into a vegetable dish and sprinkle over chopped parsley; send up very hot.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Quinces
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar
- water
- rind of a lemon
- a few cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, quarter and core them
2. Throw them into cold water as you do them and drain them
3. Lay them in a stewpan
4. Allow for every quince 2 ozs. of sugar, and to every 12 quinces add 1 pint of water, the rind of a lemon cut thin, and a few cloves
5. If the syrup is not thick enough add some more sugar
6. When nearly done add 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
7. Finish them off by quick boiling
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Salmon with Green Peas
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of very young green peas
- chopped onion
- 2 spoonfuls of oil
- salt
- pepper
- 2 lumps of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water
- salmon slices
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- lemon juice
- chopped mint
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and drain well a pint of very young green peas
2. Fry in a fish stewpan a chopped onion with 2 spoonfuls of oil
3. When fried, put salt, pepper, 2 lumps of loaf sugar, with 1/2 pint of water
4. Let it cook well
5. When half done lay in the slices of salmon
6. Rub in a basin 1 tablespoonful of flour with the juice of a lemon, a little chopped mint and parsley
7. Turn it into the stewpan
8. Dish up salmon in the centre of the dish with the peas all round and some of the juice over the salmon
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Sheep's Heart with Celery
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sheep's hearts
- celery
- 1 chopped onion
- pepper
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- a few meat balls or a few small motsa balls
- lemon
- browning

## Directions

1. Wash the hearts well, see that they are free from all blood
2. Cut them in slices
3. Cut and wash some celery
4. Put it in a stewpan with a chopped onion, pepper, salt and grated nutmeg
5. Add a few meat balls or a few small motsa balls
6. Flavour with lemon and colour with browning
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Spanish Beans and Peas
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- cold water
- two table spoonsful of oil
- pepper
- salt
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the beans over night in cold water
2. Stew them in only sufficient water to cover them, with two table spoonsful of oil, a little pepper and salt, and white sugar
3. When done they should be perfectly soft and tender

title: To Stew Spinach with Eggs
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- salt
- 1 onion
- 2 spoonfuls of oil or 2 ounces of butter
- 1 teacup of boiling water
- 5 or 6 eggs
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and pick the spinach well, pass it through several waters, put it to drain.
2. Put it in a saucepan with some salt, no water, put it at the side to boil, spinach gives a great deal of water.
3. When done put it in a colander to drain, then chop it very fine on a board.
4. Chop very fine in a saucepan an onion, fry it with 2 spoonfuls of oil, or 2 ounces of butter.
5. Put a teacup of boiling water.
6. Lay in the spinach, let it simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Break 5 or 6 eggs, each one separately in a cup, then put the eggs on the spinach.
8. When all the eggs are in, sprinkle over some pepper and salt, let them boil with the spinach.
9. When they are set dish them up gently, not to disturb the eggs.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Tangerine Oranges
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 tangerine oranges
- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1/2 pint of water

## Directions

1. Take 12 tangerine oranges, peel them very carefully, and peel off all the white skin
2. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar in 1/2 pint of water to a syrup
3. Put in the oranges to simmer for half an hour
4. Dish them up
5. When the syrup is cold pour it over the oranges
6. Cut a little of the peel in very thin strips and boil in with the sugar
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Turnips
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- 1 onion
- 1 ounce of butter
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 1 teacup of milk
- flour
- water
- parsley

## Directions

1. Turnips are stewed exactly the same as the artichokes.
2. When you dish up the turnips sprinkle parsley over.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew White Beans
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of large white beans
- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 onion
- 1 pint of water
- 1 teaspoonful of flour
- browning
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- 1 teaspoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Lay in soak 1 pint of the large white beans the night before you require them.
2. Put in a stewpan 1/2 lb. of butter, and an onion chopped very fine, let it fry.
3. Add 1 pint of water, put in the beans.
4. Let them simmer gently till done.
5. Rub in a basin a teaspoonful of flour with a little browning and a little chopped parsley.
6. Stir it in, mix it well, season it with pepper and salt, and a teaspoonful of vinegar.
7. Serve as a vegetable for butter dinners.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stew Windsor Beans with Butter
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck of beans
- a chopped onion
- salt
- pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- 1 lb of butter
- a tea cup of water
- a dessert spoonful of flour
- a spoonful of water
- a little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Pick the beans, and remove the inside shells
2. Put them in a stewpan with a chopped onion, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, 1 lb of butter, a tea cup of water
3. Let them cook till tender
4. Rub in a basin quite smooth a dessert spoonful of flour, with a spoonful of water, a little chopped parsley
5. Turn it into the beans, stir it with the liquor
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Stuff the Leg of Mutton
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- forced meat or mashed potatoes
- dried herbs
- parsley
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make the stuffing only of the forced meat or of mashed potatoes
2. Add dried herbs, parsley and grated nutmeg
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: To Warm Cold Poultry
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold poultry pieces
- seasoning for cutlets
- good gravy
- sippets of toast
- sliced lemon
- rice or mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut up the pieces required to be dressed
2. Spread over them a seasoning as for cutlets and fry them
3. Pour over a little good gravy
4. Garnish with sippets of toast and sliced lemon, or place them in an edging of rice or mashed potatoes

title: To Warm Cold Roast Beef When Not Sufficiently Done
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast beef
- beetroot or cucumber
- a little gravy
- two tablespoonsful of vinegar
- one of oil
- pepper
- salt
- a little chopped lettuce
- a few peas

## Directions

1. Cut the beef in slices
2. Slice some beetroot or cucumber
3. Put them in a saucepan with a little gravy which need not be strong
4. Add two tablespoonsful of vinegar, one of oil, pepper, salt, a little chopped lettuce and a few peas
5. Simmer till the vegetables and meat are sufficiently dressed

title: Toast Water
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- biscuit
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Toast a piece of biscuit well
2. Put it in a jug
3. Pour over boiling water
4. Let it get cold
5. Strain it; it is then ready to drink
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Toasted Almonds
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- salt

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and dry them
2. Lay them on a plate with salt sprinkled over them
3. Let them bake a light brown colour
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Toasted Cheese Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of soft, mild cheese
- one tablespoon of butter
- milk
- salt
- mustard
- paprika
- slices of bread

## Directions

1. Finely cut one cup of soft, mild cheese and stir over the fire with one tablespoon of butter until the cheese is melted.
2. Add enough milk to moisten, perhaps not more than one-eighth of a cup, with salt, mustard, and paprika to taste.
3. Stir until creamy and smooth.
4. Very thinly butter slices of bread and spread the cheese mixture on generously.
5. Cover each slice with another slice and set away until the filling cools and hardens.
6. Toast the sandwiches on both sides and serve hot.

title: Tomata Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen unpealed tomatas
- a bit of clarified suet or a little sweet oil
- a small Spanish onion
- flour
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- gravy or water
- rich stock
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a dozen unpealed tomatas, with a bit of clarified suet, or a little sweet oil, and a small Spanish onion
2. Sprinkle with flour, and season with salt and cayenne pepper
3. Boil them in a little gravy or water
4. It must be stirred to prevent burning, then pass it through a sieve
5. Thin it with rich stock to the consistency of winter pea-soup
6. Flavour it with lemon juice, according to taste, after it has been warmed up and ready for serving

title: Tomato Catsup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Garlic
- Onion
- Black pepper
- Red pepper
- Whole cloves
- Salt
- Cider vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut eight quarts of tomatoes in pieces and stew them until soft.
2. Press through a sieve to remove the skins and seeds.
3. Add one head of garlic or one-half onion, one-half tablespoon of black pepper, one-quarter teaspoon of red pepper, one-half ounce whole cloves, three-quarters of a cup of salt and one of cider vinegar.
4. Mix thoroughly and boil about three hours or until reduced one-half.
5. Bottle without straining, then seal.

title: Tomato Custards
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- eggs
- onion
- bay leaf
- parsley
- water
- cream sauce
- green peas

## Directions

1. Simmer for fifteen minutes in a covered saucepan four cups chopped tomatoes, four eggs, one sliced onion, one bay leaf, and sprig of parsley.
2. Strain and if there be not two cups of liquid, add water.
3. Beat four eggs and add to liquid.
4. Pour into greased baking cups and stand them in a pan of water and bake until firm — about fifteen minutes.
5. Turn out and serve with cream sauce containing green peas.

title: Tomato Puree
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald the tomatoes, take off the skins carefully and stew with one teaspoon each of butter and sugar; salt and pepper to taste.
2. When the tomatoes are very soft strain through a coarse sieve and if necessary thicken with one teaspoon of flour.

title: Tomato Salad (French Dressing)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six firm red tomatoes
- lettuce leaves
- salt
- pepper
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Take six firm red tomatoes, wash and wipe them neatly
2. Slice them in thin slices with a very sharp knife
3. Line a salad bowl with lettuce leaves
4. Lay the sliced tomatoes in
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. Serve with French dressing

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen fine tomatos
- a little water or gravy
- a little vinegar
- lemon juice
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- the yolk of an egg

## Directions

1. Skin a dozen fine tomatos
2. Set them on the fire in a little water or gravy
3. Beat them up with a little vinegar, lemon juice, cayenne pepper, and salt
4. Some persons like the yolk of an egg, well beaten added
5. Strain or not, as may be preferred

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter
- one minced onion
- one tablespoon of flour
- two cups of tomatoes
- one teaspoon of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- pepper
- red pepper
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- one tablespoon of tomato catsup

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon butter with one minced onion
2. Add one tablespoon of flour
3. When brown stir in two cups of tomatoes which have previously been cooked and strained
4. Add also one teaspoon of sugar, a pinch of salt, pepper, and red pepper, also one tablespoon of vinegar and one tablespoon of tomato catsup

title: Tomato Sauce (Chili)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Green peppers
- Red peppers
- Onions
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Ginger
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Allspice

## Directions

1. Skin and cut into pieces forty-five large tomatoes.
2. Cut fine twenty green peppers, twenty red peppers, and six onions.
3. Mix all together with two tablespoons of salt, six small cups of vinegar, and two cups of sugar.
4. Boil two hours.
5. Add one tablespoon each of ginger, cloves, cinnamon and allspice, and boil up once.
6. Bottle and seal at once.

title: Tomato Sauce for Fowls, Beef Steak, and Mutton Chop
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- several large tomatoes
- a tooth of garlic
- a tablespoonful of good oil
- salt
- pepper
- a dash of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut several large tomatoes into a saucepan and let them simmer gently at the side of your fire
2. When quite soft rub them through a sieve into a basin
3. Chop a tooth of garlic and fry it in a tablespoonful of good oil
4. When fried take out the garlic
5. Stir in the pulped tomatoes with a little salt, pepper, and a dash of vinegar to taste
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Tomato Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 7 large red tomatoes
- a quart of stock No. 1
- the juice of a lemon
- a little cayenne
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut tomatoes in halves
2. Put them in a saucepan at the side of the fire with no water
3. Let them simmer till quite tender
4. Rub them through a sieve till only their skins remain
5. Have a quart of stock No. 1 ready at the side
6. Stir them well in till it is quite smooth
7. Have ready in your tureen the juice of a lemon and a little cayenne
8. Many persons like toasted bread with this soup
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Tomato Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one large soup bone or two pounds soup meat
- one or two onions
- a few potatoes
- a few carrots
- one turnip
- soup greens
- one can tomatoes or one quart fresh tomatoes
- two ears sweet corn
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon flour
- cold water
- noodles

## Directions

1. Take a large soup bone or two pounds of soup meat, the latter preferred, one or two onions, a few potatoes, a few carrots, a turnip, soup greens and a can of tomatoes or a quart of fresh ones
2. Cook two hours
3. In season add two ears of sweet corn grated
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Thicken with a tablespoon of flour, dissolved in cold water
6. A nice addition to this soup is a handful of noodles cut into round disks with a thimble

title: Tomato Soup with Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one minced onion
- one tablespoon of butter
- one can of tomatoes or one quart of medium sized tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of sugar
- a pinch of paprika
- one tablespoon of flour
- cold water
- one cup of boiled rice

## Directions

1. Brown slightly one minced onion in one tablespoon of butter
2. add one can of tomatoes or a quart of medium sized tomatoes cut in small pieces
3. season with salt, pepper, one tablespoon of sugar and a pinch of paprika
4. Simmer a half hour
5. strain and thicken with one tablespoon of flour moistened with cold water
6. add the strained tomatoes and one cup of boiled rice
7. let come to a boil and serve

title: Tomato With Egg
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Cut top from tomatoes, remove seeds, put a raw egg in each tomato, dust with salt, pepper, and finely chopped parsley.
2. Place in moderate oven until egg is set.
3. Serve with cream sauce.

title: Tomatoes With Rice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six large tomatoes
- boiling water
- two onions
- butter
- water
- rice
- salt
- pepper
- cheese
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take six large tomatoes, pour boiling water over them and skin them.
2. Scrape all the inside out with a spoon, put in saucepan together with two onions, a tablespoon of butter, one pint of water; let this boil for a little while; strain, place back on stove.
3. Pour into this one-half pound of rice, let it cook tender; add salt, pepper, a tablespoon of butter and a little grated cheese.
4. Fill the tomatoes with this mixture, dip them in egg and bread crumbs, then fry till nice and brown.

title: Tomatoes, Eggs and Cheese (Hungarian Style)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- one small onion chopped fine
- one pint of tomatoes strained
- one-half pound mild cheese cut fine
- three eggs
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Place two tablespoons of butter in a pan after having the water boil to heat the pan
2. Let butter melt, add one small onion chopped fine and cook until soft
3. Add a pint of tomatoes strained and let come to a boil
4. Add one-half pound mild cheese cut fine, and stir until smooth
5. Break in three eggs and stir hard until eggs are done
6. Serve on buttered toast

title: Tongue Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mustard
- tongue

## Directions

1. Cut the bread exactly the same as fowl sandwiches
2. Spread mustard instead of mayonnaise
3. Cut them very even
4. Cut the tongue very thin
5. Arrange them on a dish with cut paper or a white napkin under
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Topfa Dalkeln. Cheese Cakes (Hungarian)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half ounce of yeast
- scalded milk
- one-half cup of flour
- two cups of scalded milk
- one pound of flour (four cups sifted flour)
- one pound of sweet pot cheese
- a pinch of salt
- three egg yolks
- rind of one lemon
- one-half cup of light colored raisins
- sugar to taste
- melted butter
- beaten whites

## Directions

1. Mix yeast with a little scalded milk which has cooled to lukewarm, one-half cup of flour and put aside in a warm place to rise
2. Allow two cups of scalded milk to become lukewarm
3. Add one pound of flour to the risen sponge, then the two cups of milk, mix these very well, cover with a cloth and put aside in a warm place to rise
4. Take one pound of sweet pot cheese, a pinch of salt, three egg yolks, rind of one lemon, one-half cup of light colored raisins and sugar to taste; mix very well and add the beaten whites and mix thoroughly
5. When the dough is very well risen, place on a pastry board, roll out and spread with melted butter, fold these edges over to the middle, then the top and bottom over, roll again and spread with butter, fold all sides in once more, roll, spread with butter, repeat the folding
6. Roll out to one-half inch thickness, cut in three-inch squares, place a tablespoon of the cheese mixture in the centre of each square, fold over opposite corners, spread egg white over the top of each pocket
7. Let rise fifteen minutes or one-half hour and bake in a hot oven; when they are well risen, lower heat and bake to a golden brown

title: Tourte a la Creme
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- very rich puff paste
- a fine custard or any rich preserves
- well whipped cream

## Directions

1. Roll out some very rich puff paste to the thickness of one inch
2. Cut two pieces with the larger tin cutter
3. Press the smaller cutter through one of these pieces and remove the border which will be formed round it
4. Lay this border very evenly upon the other piece of paste and slightly press to make it adhere
5. Place the tourte in an oven to bake for about twenty minutes
6. Let it become cool, but not cold
7. Fill it with a fine custard or with any rich preserves
8. If the latter, a well whipped cream may be laid lightly over
9. The pastry may be glazed if approved

title: Transparent Soap
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Windsor soap in shavings
- spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Put into a bottle Windsor soap in shavings
2. half fill it with spirits of wine
3. set it near the fire till the soap is dissolved
4. pour it into moulds to cool

title: Tripe a la Creole
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- onion
- parsley
- celery
- seasoning
- one tablespoon of fat
- two tablespoons of flour
- one can of boiled and strained tomatoes
- one can of mushrooms
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil tripe with onion, parsley, celery, and seasoning.
2. Cut in small pieces.
3. Brown fat with two tablespoons of flour.
4. Add one can of boiled and strained tomatoes, one can of mushrooms, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Boil up the tripe in this sauce.
6. Serve in ramekins.

title: Tripe, Family Style
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds tripe
- big kitchen spoon of drippings
- four large onions
- three small cloves of garlic
- water
- potatoes
- three tablespoons of flour
- catsup
- paprika
- ginger
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Scald and scrape two pounds tripe and cut into inch squares.
2. Take big kitchen spoon of drippings and put in four large onions quartered and three small cloves of garlic cut up very fine.
3. Let steam, but not brown.
4. When onions begin to cook, put in tripe and steam half an hour.
5. Then cover tripe with water and let cook slowly three hours.
6. Boil a few potatoes and cut in dice shapes and add to it.
7. Half an hour before serving, add the following, after taking off as much fat from the tripe as possible: Three tablespoons of flour thinned with little water; add catsup, paprika, ginger, and one teaspoon of salt.
8. It should all be quite thick, like paste, when cooked.

title: Truffle Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- a little butter
- good white or brown consomme
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- a very little white wine

## Directions

1. Peel and slice as many truffles as required
2. Simmer them gently with a little butter
3. When they are tender, add to them good white or brown consomme, lemon juice, pepper, salt, nutmeg, and a very little white wine

title: Tsimess
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- parboiled spinach
- sorrel
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- drippings

## Directions

1. Take equal portions of parboiled spinach and sorrel
2. Season to taste with ground nutmeg, pepper and salt
3. Add sufficient drippings to make all moist enough
4. Place in a covered dish in a slow oven

title: Turkey Boned and Forced
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- forcemeat
- veal stuffings
- sausage meat
- tongue
- mushrooms
- white sauce
- cradle spit

## Directions

1. Have the turkey boned by the poulterer
2. Fill it with a fine forcemeat, which may be varied in several ways
3. The basis should be according to the receipt given for veal stuffings, forcemeats, sausage meat, tongue, and mushrooms added as approved
4. If boiled, serve with any fine white sauce, French cooks use the veloute or bechamel
5. If roasted, use a cradle spit if available, or carefully tie the turkey to the spit

title: Turkish Coffee
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- powdered coffee
- water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Measure into your pot as many after-dinner coffee cups of water as you wish cups of coffee
2. Bring the water to a boil and drop a heaping teaspoon of the powdered coffee to each cup on top of the water and allow it to settle
3. Add one, two or three coffeespoons of powdered sugar, as desired
4. Put the pot again over the flame
5. Bring the coffee to a boil three times
6. Pour into the cups
7. Let the coffee stand a moment in the cup before drinking

title: Tutti-Frutti
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- bananas
- pineapples
- pulverized sugar
- wine or cream

## Directions

1. Slice oranges, bananas, pineapples and arrange in a glass bowl
2. Sprinkle with pulverized sugar
3. Serve either with wine or cream (you may use both)

title: Tutti-Frutti Ice Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three pints of cream
- one pound of pulverized sugar
- yolks of nine eggs
- one-half pound of crystallized fruit
- peaches, apricots, cherries, citron
- one wineglass of pale sherry
- juice of an orange or lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare just like the other creams
2. When half frozen add one-half pound of crystallized fruit, peaches, apricots, cherries, citron, etc., chopped very fine
3. Put in also a wineglass of pale sherry and the juice of an orange or lemon
4. Finish freezing

title: Unboiled Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- white of one egg
- water
- confectioner's sugar

## Directions

1. Take the white of one egg and add to it the same quantity of water
2. Stir into this as much confectioner's sugar to make it of the right consistency to spread upon the cake
3. Flavor with any flavoring desired
4. Color as desired by adding fruit coloring

title: Unfermented Grape Juice
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ten pounds of Concord grapes
- two pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and stem ten pounds of Concord grapes
2. put them in a preserving kettle and crush slightly
3. Bring to the boiling point and cook gently for one-half hour
4. Strain through cheese-cloth or jelly bag, pressing out all the juice possible
5. return to fire and with two pounds of sugar cook for fifteen minutes
6. strain again
7. reheat and pour into sterilized bottles thoroughly heated
8. Put in sterilized corks and dip the necks of the bottles in hot sealing-wax

title: Vanilla Cookies
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs
- two level teaspoons of baking-powder
- flour enough to make a dough
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub one cup of butter and one cup of sugar to a cream.
2. Add two eggs and two level teaspoons of baking-powder, flour enough to make a dough.
3. Flavor with vanilla, roll very thin, spread with beaten white of egg and sugar.
4. Proceed as for sugar cookies.

title: Vanilla Ice Cream, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- two cups of sugar
- one large tablespoon of flour
- cold milk
- two eggs
- one teaspoon of vanilla extract
- one quart of sweet cream
- vanilla, lemon and almond extracts

## Directions

1. Heat the milk in a double boiler
2. when it is at boiling point add the flour rubbed smooth in cold milk
3. add the eggs beaten light and one cup of sugar
4. Cook about twenty minutes, stirring very often
5. Let the mixture get cold
6. add the remaining sugar and the vanilla and cream
7. freeze

title: Vanilla Ice Cream, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- two cups of sugar
- one large tablespoon of flour
- cold milk
- two eggs
- one teaspoon of vanilla extract
- one quart of sweet cream

## Directions

1. Heat the milk in a double boiler
2. When it is at boiling point add the flour, eggs and one cup of sugar
3. Cook about twenty minutes, stirring very often
4. Let the mixture get cold
5. Add the remaining sugar and the vanilla and cream
6. Freeze

title: Vanilla Ice Cream, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- one pint of cream
- one cup of sweet milk
- vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Beat three whole eggs very light with one cup of granulated sugar until all grain is dissolved and mass is a light yellowish color
2. Whip one pint of cream until stiff
3. Add to eggs and sugar
4. Add one cup of sweet milk
5. Flavor with vanilla to taste
6. Put in freezer and turn until hard

title: Vanilla or Cream Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon cornstarch
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one cup of scalding milk
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. Mix one teaspoon cornstarch and one tablespoon of sugar thoroughly
2. on them slowly pour one cup of scalding milk, stirring all the time
3. Cook and stir in a double boiler for ten minutes
4. set aside to cool
5. When ready to use stir in one teaspoon of vanilla and the white of one egg, stiffly beaten
6. Serve in place of whipped cream

title: Vanille Cream
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint of almond milk
- a piece of vanille
- a piece of cinnamon
- 1/4 of an oz. of isinglass
- 1/2 lb. sugar (loaf)
- yolks of 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil for 5 minutes 1/2 pint of almond milk, a piece of vanille, a piece of cinnamon, 1/4 of an oz. of isinglass, 1/2 lb. sugar (loaf), then pass it through a sieve.
