2. When quite cold beat up the yolks of 8 eggs in a bowl, pour the milk in, pass it again through a sieve.
3. Put it in an earthenware mould, set in a flat saucepan with boiling water, cover it with the flat lid after it has been on the fire for a few moments.
4. Put on the lid some lighted charcoal to set the top of the cream.
5. When it is firm, take it out of the water. Remember the moment you see it looks firm, all creams must be taken out of the water, or they will turn watery.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Vanille Ice Creams
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a small piece of vanille
- 1/2 a stick
- 2 pints of milk
- 1 pint of cream
- sugar to sweeten

## Directions

1. Pound a small piece of vanille, 1/2 a stick
2. Boil it in 2 pints of milk
3. When cold, strain it and add it to a pint of cream
4. Sweeten it to flavour
5. Put it in the freezer and work it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Vanille Pudding
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 3 oz. flour
- 1/2 pint of milk
- 8 eggs
- a piece of vanille
- 1/4 lb. of sifted sugar
- butter and sugar for greasing

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, stir in the flour very dry, work it up well
2. Stir in the milk, stir it till it is quite thick
3. Let the butter get quite cold again
4. Beat in the yolks of 8 eggs one after the other
5. Pound a piece of vanille with the sugar, mix that in also
6. Beat the 8 whites to a snow, stir them in
7. Grease a shape well with butter and sugar, put the mixture in
8. Stand it in a flat stewpan half full of water
9. Let it boil slowly for 2 1/2 hours
10. Turn these puddings out very carefully, and always lay the sauce round in the dish, not over the pudding
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Variety Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cake of compressed yeast
- two cups of lukewarm water or milk
- two teaspoons of salt
- three cups of bread or wheat flour
- one cup of cornmeal
- one cup of rye flour
- one-half cup of dark molasses

## Directions

1. Dissolve yeast in water or milk
2. add salt, flour, cornmeal, rye flour and molasses, and mix very thoroughly
3. Let rise
4. shape into loaves
5. let rise again
6. bake in a moderate oven for forty-five minutes

title: Veal Cutlets with Tomatoes
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 large red tomatoes
- a sliced Spanish onion
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- 3 or 4 veal cutlets
- 1/2 pint of water
- juice of 1/2 a lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Slice 4 or 5 large red tomatoes in a stewpan with a sliced Spanish onion, salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
2. Lay on top 3 or 4 veal cutlets with 1/2 pint of water
3. Let it simmer for an hour
4. Take out the cutlets
5. Rub the tomatoes and onion through a sieve
6. Place the cutlets back into the stewpan
7. Pour over what you have pulped
8. Add the juice of 1/2 a lemon to the pulp
9. Serve it upon a hot dish with the cutlets first, then throw over the tomato sauce
10. Optionally chop a little parsley over it
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Veal Loaf
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of chopped veal
- four tablespoons of bread crumbs
- two beaten eggs
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- nutmeg
- water
- tablespoon of chicken-fat

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of chopped veal, four tablespoons of bread crumbs, two beaten eggs
2. Season with salt, pepper, ginger, nutmeg and a little water
3. Add a tablespoon of chicken-fat
4. Grease the pan, mix ingredients thoroughly, form into a loaf
5. Spread or lay piece of chicken-fat on top
6. Bake in oblong tin until done, basting frequently

title: Veal Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- yolks of three hard-boiled eggs
- two tablespoons of melted cold chicken or turkey grease
- one teaspoon of prepared mustard
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one teaspoon of mustard seed
- one teaspoon of celery seed
- five tablespoons of white vinegar
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cut cold veal in half-inch slices, season with two tablespoons of vinegar, pinch of salt and pepper.
2. Make a dressing using the yolks of three hard-boiled eggs, mashed smooth.
3. Add gradually two tablespoons of melted cold chicken or turkey grease, stir until smooth and thick.
4. Add one teaspoon of prepared mustard, large pinch of salt and pepper, one teaspoon of sugar, one teaspoon each of mustard and celery seed, and five tablespoons of white vinegar.
5. Mix the dressing well with the veal, and serve with or without lettuce leaves.

title: Veal Sandwiches
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- mayonnaise
- veal

## Directions

1. Cut the bread the same as fowl sandwiches
2. Spread over some mayonnaise
3. Prepare them the same as for fowl
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Veal Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- mustard

## Directions

1. Slice the veal in thin slices and spread with mustard

title: Veal Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece veal from the neck
- one or two veal bones
- two quarts water
- one sprig parsley
- one onion, cut up into small pieces
- yolks of two eggs
- one tablespoon cold water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of veal, off the neck, and one or two veal bones in two quarts of water
2. Add a sprig of parsley, one onion, cut up into small pieces
3. Strain and thicken with the yolks of two eggs slightly beaten with a tablespoon of cold water
4. Season with salt and pepper to taste

title: Veal Sweetbreads (Fried)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- slightly salted cold water
- salt
- pepper
- goose-fat

## Directions

1. Wash and lay your sweetbreads in slightly salted cold water for an hour
2. Pull off carefully all the outer skin, wipe dry and sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Heat some goose-fat in a spider
4. lay in the sweetbreads and fry slowly on the back of the stove, turning frequently until they are a nice brown

title: Vegetable Hash
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one or many vegetables
- meat and fish (optional)
- potato
- salt and pepper
- parsley, onion, chives or green pepper (if desired)
- stock, milk or water (one-quarter cup to a pint of hash)
- one-half tablespoon of butter or savory drippings

## Directions

1. Chop the vegetables fine
2. Season well with salt and pepper, and parsley, onion, chives or green pepper if desired
3. Moisten with stock, milk or water, using a quarter of a cup to a pint of hash
4. Melt one-half tablespoon of butter or savory drippings in a pan
5. Put in the hash, spreading it evenly and dropping small pieces of butter or drippings over the top
6. Cover the pan
7. Let the hash cook over a moderate fire for half an hour
8. Fold over like an omelet and serve

title: Vegetable Marrows Filled with Forced Meat
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetable marrows
- salt
- onion
- two tablespoonfuls of oil
- pepper
- tomatoes
- cold meat
- two eggs
- lemon or two tablespoonfuls of good vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Scrape lightly the vegetable marrows, wash them well
2. Put them on in a saucepan with boiling water and a little salt
3. Feel them with a fork, and when tender take them out carefully
4. Lay them on your pasteboard, divide them in halves lengthways
5. Scoop out the inside, let it drain well from the water
6. Slice in a deep pie dish an onion, with two tablespoonfuls of oil, a little salt and pepper
7. Put it in the oven to fry
8. If you have some tomatoes slice 2 or 3, and put with the onion
9. Take some cold meat, mince it like for the forcemeat balls, with the same seasoning
10. Put in a dish, mix it up with two eggs
11. Take the dish from the oven, lay the vegetable marrows in, do not put them too close
12. Fill them with the mixture you have just made
13. Beat in a plate two more eggs with the juice of a lemon, or two tablespoonfuls of good vinegar
14. Put it over the vegetable marrow, with a spoon
15. Put them in a rather brisk oven to bake until a nice light brown colour
16. Dish them up carefully
17. Put the eggs and lemon or vinegar custard round them and garnish with parsley
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Vegetable Meat Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of Lima beans
- one-quarter cup of almonds
- one-quarter cup of peanuts
- four potatoes
- hard-boiled egg
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- four tablespoons of hot milk
- one-quarter cup of flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Soak one-half cup of Lima beans overnight
2. In the morning let them boil rapidly for one-half hour
3. Drain, slip the beans from their skins and split them in halves
4. Blanch one-quarter cup of almonds and chop them with one-quarter cup of peanuts
5. Boil four potatoes, and when done cut two of them into small cubes
6. Mash the remaining two and use them for a dough, adding four tablespoons of hot milk, a little salt and one-quarter cup of flour
7. Put a layer of beans in the bottom of the baking-dish, a sprinkling of nuts, a little hard-boiled egg, then the potato blocks and one-half tablespoon each of chopped parsley and chopped onion, one-half teaspoon of salt and one-half saltspoon of pepper and so on until the material is all used
8. Roll out the potato dough the size of the baking-dish
9. Put it over the dish, brush with milk and bake half an hour in a moderately quick oven

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one small soup bone
- cold water
- one-half cup celery
- one-half cup carrots
- one-half cup onion
- fat
- one tablespoon chopped parsley
- one-half cup potatoes
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a small soup bone, cover with cold water
2. Cut one-half a cup each of celery, carrots, and onion
3. Brown in fat, cooking five to ten minutes
4. Add one tablespoon of chopped parsley and one-half cup of potatoes
5. Add to soup bone and cook one hour
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. Remove bone and serve

title: Vegetable Soup (Milchig)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of chopped onion
- one tablespoon of butter
- one and a half quarts of boiling water
- two cups of shredded cabbage
- one-half cup of chopped carrot
- one leak
- one tablespoon of chopped peppers
- one tablespoon of chopped celery
- one medium-sized potato
- one tomato
- one and a half teaspoons of salt
- one-quarter teaspoon of pepper
- a pinch of paprika and thyme
- two tablespoons of flour
- one-fourth cup of cold milk

## Directions

1. Brown one-half cup of chopped onion in one tablespoon of butter
2. add one and a half quarts of boiling water, two cups of shredded cabbage, one-half cup of chopped carrot, one leak, one tablespoon of chopped peppers, one tablespoon of chopped celery
3. Boil rapidly for ten minutes, then gently for one hour
4. Add one medium-sized potato diced and a tomato, one and a half teaspoons of salt and one-quarter teaspoon of pepper, a pinch of paprika and thyme
5. Cook one hour longer
6. Have the cover partially off the kettle during the entire time
7. Ten minutes before serving thicken with two tablespoons of flour mixed with one-fourth cup of cold milk

title: Vermicelli And Maccaroni Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces vermicelli or maccaroni
- one pint of milk
- raisins
- sweet pudding sauce

## Directions

1. Boil till tender four ounces of either of the above articles, in a pint of milk
2. flavor as directed in the preceding receipt
3. boil in a mould, which may be lined with raisins
4. serve with any sweet pudding sauce

title: Vermicelli Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- shin of beef or any other part preferred
- a handful of vermicelli
- vegetables
- herbs

## Directions

1. Make a fine strong stock from the shin of beef or any other part preferred
2. A short time before serving, add a handful of vermicelli, which should be broken so that it may be in pieces of convenient length
3. The stock should be more or less flavoured with vegetables and herbs according to taste

title: Vermicelli Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of stock
- 1 lb. of vermicelli
- saffron
- a couple of tomatoes
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Warm a quart of stock
2. When it boils put in 1-lb. of vermicelli
3. Let it boil for 10 minutes
4. The stock is already coloured with the saffron
5. Cut a couple of tomatoes small and boil them
6. Put them through a sieve
7. Put it into the tureen before you dish up the soup
8. Add a little grated nutmeg
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Very Rich Puff Paste
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of butter and flour
- an egg

## Directions

1. Mix in the same manner equal quantities of butter and flour, taking care to have the flour dried for a short time before the fire
2. It may be folded and rolled five or six times
3. An egg well beaten and mixed with the paste is sometimes added

title: Vienna Pastry for Kipfel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound of pot cheese
- one-half pound of butter
- two cups of flour sifted four times
- salt
- jelly
- one-half pound of walnuts ground
- one-half cup of sugar
- hot milk
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Take pot cheese and butter and flour
2. Add a pinch of salt and work these ingredients into a dough
3. Make thirty small balls of it and put on a platter on the ice overnight
4. In the morning roll each ball separately into two-inch squares
5. Fill squares with a teaspoon of jelly put in the centre and fold over like an envelope
6. Or fill them with ground walnuts, sugar and moisten with a little hot milk
7. Roll and twist into shape
8. Brush with beaten egg
9. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Vienna Prater Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of six eggs
- one cup of granulated sugar
- three-fourths cup of sifted chocolate
- three-fourths cup of flour
- one and one-half teaspoon of vanilla
- whites of six eggs
- one cup of milk
- yolks of two eggs
- one cup of chopped walnuts
- rum

## Directions

1. Cream the yolks of six eggs with one cup of granulated sugar
2. Add three-fourths cup of sifted chocolate, three-fourths cup of flour sifted twice, and one and one-half teaspoon of vanilla
3. Add the beaten whites
4. Bake thirty minutes
5. When cold, cut in half
6. For filling: Boil one cup of milk with yolks of two eggs and one cup of chopped walnuts, stirring constantly to prevent curdling
7. Sweeten to taste
8. After removing from the fire add one tablespoon of rum
9. Spread while hot

title: Vienna Sausage
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sausages
- boiling water
- grated horseradish or French mustard

## Directions

1. Wash and put on in boiling water
2. Boil ten minutes
3. Fill a deep dish with hot water, put sausages in, cover, and serve in hot water
4. To be eaten with grated horseradish or French mustard

title: Vol-au-Vent
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- white fricassee of chicken, sweetbread, or other filling

## Directions

1. It should be rolled out about an inch and a half in thickness
2. Cut it with a fluted tin of the size of the dish in which it is to be served
3. Also cut a smaller piece, which must be rolled out considerably thinner than an inch, to serve as a lid for the other part
4. Bake both pieces, and when done, scoop out the crumb of the largest, and fill it with a white fricassee of chicken, sweetbread, or whatever may be selected
5. The sauce should be well thickened, or it would soften, and run through the crust

title: Waffles
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cup and a half of thick yeast
- a little warm milk
- two pounds of flour
- one pound of fresh butter
- ten eggs
- a grated nutmeg
- a quarter of a pint of orange flower-water
- a little powdered cinnamon
- three pints of warm milk

## Directions

1. Mix the thick yeast with a little warm milk and set it with the flour before the fire to rise
2. Mix with them one pound of fresh butter, ten eggs, a grated nutmeg, a quarter of a pint of orange flower-water, a little powdered cinnamon, and three pints of warm milk
3. When the batter is perfectly smooth, butter the irons
4. Fill them with it and close them down tightly
5. Put them between the bars of a bright clear fire
6. When sufficiently done, they will slip easily out of the irons

title: Waldorf Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced celery
- sliced apples
- one-quarter of a pound of pecans or English walnuts
- one tablespoon of lemon-juice
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Mix an equal quantity of sliced celery and apples
2. Add a quarter of a pound of pecans or English walnuts, chopped fine
3. Put over a tablespoon of lemon-juice and sufficient mayonnaise dressing to thoroughly cover
4. Serve without lettuce or dish on lettuce leaves

title: Walnut Biscuits
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. ground almonds
- 6 whites of eggs
- 1/2 lb. castor sugar
- nutmeg
- allspice
- sugar for rolling

## Directions

1. Pound the almonds in a marble mortar with the whites of eggs
2. Add the sugar
3. Take it out of the mortar and put it in a basin
4. Work in some nutmeg and allspice to colour like the real walnut
5. Roll up and break it in pieces the size of a walnut
6. Shape it
7. Roll up the walnuts in a little sugar to prevent their sticking
8. Bake them on plates in a slow oven; they are soon done
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Walnut Torte, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of walnuts
- eight ounces of blanched almonds
- yolks of twelve eggs
- three-fourths pound of sugar
- one-fourth pound of sifted flour
- whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Grate eight ounces of walnuts and eight ounces of blanched almonds
2. Beat light the yolks of twelve eggs and three-fourths pound of sugar
3. Add the grated nuts and one-fourth pound of sifted flour
4. Fold in the whites beaten to a stiff froth
5. Bake in layers and fill with sweetened whipped cream

title: Walnut Torte, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one-half pound of granulated sugar
- a pinch of salt
- one-fourth pound of grated walnuts, whole pieces reserved for decorating
- two tablespoons of grated lady fingers or stale sponge cake
- almond or plain icing

## Directions

1. Separate the yolks and whites of six eggs, being very careful not to get a particle of the yolks into the whites
2. Sift one-half pound of granulated sugar into the yolks and beat until thick as batter
3. Add a pinch of salt to the whites and beat very stiff
4. Have ready one-fourth pound of grated walnuts, reserve whole pieces for decorating the top of cake
5. Add the pounded nuts to the beaten yolks and two tablespoons of grated lady fingers or stale sponge cake
6. Last add the stiffly-beaten whites of the eggs
7. Bake in layers and fill with almond or plain icing

title: Wash for Pimples
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dram of salt of tartar
- three ounces of spirit of wine

## Directions

1. Dissolve half a dram of salt of tartar in three ounces of spirit of wine
2. Apply with soft linen

title: Water Ices
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of loaf sugar
- 1 pint of water
- shell of an egg

## Directions

1. Boil 1 lb. of loaf sugar in a pint of water
2. Put in the shell of an egg to clarify it
3. Let it get cold
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Water Souchy
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water (3 pints)
- parsley roots
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a portion of the fish intended to be dressed, and stew it down with three pints of water, parsley roots, and chopped parsley
2. Pulp them through a sieve
3. Add the rest of the fish, with pepper, salt, and seasoning
4. Serve in a deep dish

title: Water-Lily Salad
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg for each lily
- heart of bleached lettuce
- butter
- vinegar
- mustard
- salt
- paprika
- parsley
- stuffed olives
- cut-glass dishes

## Directions

1. Boil twenty minutes, one egg for each lily
2. Remove shell and while still warm cut with silver knife in strips from small end nearly to base
3. Very carefully lay back the petals on a heart of bleached lettuce
4. Remove yolks and rub them with spoon of butter, vinegar, a little mustard, salt and paprika
5. Form cone-shaped balls, and put on petals, sprinkling bits of parsley over balls
6. Two or three stuffed olives carry out the effect of buds
7. Serve on cut-glass dishes to give water effect

title: Watermelon Pickle
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon rind
- one cup of salt
- one gallon of hot water
- one level tablespoon of alum
- one teaspoon each of cloves, allspice, cinnamon and ginger
- one cup of sugar
- one cup of vinegar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the green rind of watermelon and the inside pink portion that is left on after eating it
2. Cut it into two-inch pieces and pour over it a weak brine made in proportion of one cup of salt to a gallon of hot water
3. Let this stand overnight then drain and add clear water and one level tablespoon of alum
4. Boil in this water until the rind has a clear appearance
5. Drain and pour ice water over the rind and allow it to stand a short time
6. In a bag put one teaspoon each of cloves, allspice, cinnamon and ginger and place this in the preserve kettle with the vinegar and sugar
7. Allow one cup of sugar and one cup of vinegar (dilute this with water if too strong) to every pound of rind
8. Thin slices of lemon will give it a pleasant flavor — allow one lemon to about four pounds of rind
9. Bring this syrup to the boiling point and skim
10. Add the melon and cook until tender
11. It is done when it becomes perfectly transparent and can be easily pierced with a broom straw
12. A peach kernel in the cooking syrup will improve the flavor
13. As soon as rinds are cooked they should be put into the containers and covered with the syrup

title: Watermelon Sherbet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- good, pale sherry
- loaf sugar
- chopped meat of a very fine, sweet melon
- claret

## Directions

1. Take good, pale sherry and boil down to quite a thick syrup, with loaf sugar
2. Allow to cool
3. When cold mix with the chopped meat of a very fine, sweet melon
4. Use only the heart of the soft red part, not any near the white rind
5. Freeze in a freezer as you would ice, but do not allow it to get too hard
6. Serve in glasses

title: Watermelons
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe watermelon
- pale sherry or claret
- sugar
- whole spices

## Directions

1. Use only melons that are perfectly ripe
2. Do not select those that are very large in circumference
3. Select a rough melon with a bumpy surface
4. Either cut in half or plug
5. Put pale sherry or claret on to boil and boil down to quite a thick syrup with sugar
6. Pour this into either a plugged melon or over the half-cut melon
7. Lay on ice for a couple of hours before serving
8. If you use claret you may spice it while boiling with whole spices

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- good Cheddar cheese
- butter
- 1/2 teacup of ale or porter
- made mustard
- pepper
- hot toast

## Directions

1. Melt in the cheese tin some good Cheddar cheese with a small piece of butter
2. Add 1/2 teacup of ale or porter
3. Stir in a little made mustard and pepper
4. Have ready hot dry or buttered toast to send up with
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- two cups finely cut American cheese
- one-half cup of milk or stale beer
- one-half teaspoon of English mustard
- two beaten eggs
- salt
- toast

## Directions

1. Melt one tablespoon of butter
2. Add two cups finely cut American cheese
3. When it melts add one-half cup of milk or stale beer
4. Keep stirring until it is smooth
5. Add one-half teaspoon of English mustard
6. Add two beaten eggs
7. Cook one minute longer and salt to taste
8. Serve on toast

title: Wheat Muffins
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- three teaspoons of baking-powder
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one tablespoon of butter
- one egg
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of molasses

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of flour, one-half teaspoon of salt, three teaspoons of baking-powder, two tablespoons of sugar
2. Sift these ingredients twice
3. Rub in one tablespoon of butter
4. Separate one egg
5. Beat the yolk and add it to one cup of milk and one teaspoon of molasses
6. Mix with the dry ingredients
7. Stir until smooth
8. Fold in the beaten white of egg
9. Pour into hot, well-greased muffin-tins
10. Bake fifteen to twenty minutes in hot oven

title: Whipped Cream
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of rich thick cream
- one-quarter of a pound of powdered sugar
- one-half teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. To one pint of rich thick cream add one-quarter of a pound of powdered sugar and one-half teaspoon of vanilla.
2. Put in a large platter in a cool place and whip with a wire egg-whip until perfectly smooth and velvety.
3. Set on ice until wanted.
4. In the summer set the cream on ice before whipping.
5. A good plan is to set the bowl in another one filled with ice while whipping.

title: Whipped Cream Pie
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- jelly or jam
- one cup of sweet cream
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a crust as rich as possible and line a deep tin
2. Bake quickly in a hot oven and spread it with a layer of jelly or jam
3. Whip one cup of sweet cream until it is thick
4. Set the cream in a bowl of ice while whipping
5. Sweeten slightly and flavor with vanilla, spread this over the pie and put in a cool place until wanted

title: White and Brown Bread Sandwiches
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- brown and white bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Butter alternate slices of brown and white bread and pile them one above the other in a loaf.
2. Cut the new loaf across the slices, butter them and pile them so that when this second loaf is cut, the slices will be in white and brown blocks.
3. Press the slices very closely together before cutting at all.

title: White Bait
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- white bait
- flour
- butter
- brown bread
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Throw white bait from the dish into a cloth with a handful of flour
2. Shake them lightly, but enough to cover them well with the flour
3. Turn them into a sieve expressly for bait to free them from too great a quantity of the flour
4. Throw the fish into a pan with plenty of boiling butter
5. They must remain but an instant, for they are considered spoilt if they become the least brown
6. Place them lightly on the dish piled up high in the centre
7. Serve with brown bread and butter
8. When devilled, sprinkle with cayenne pepper and a little salt, and serve with lemon juice

title: White Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cake of compressed yeast
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of sugar
- eight cups of sifted flour
- one cup of lukewarm milk or water

## Directions

1. Set the dough at night and bake early in the morning
2. Take one-half cake of compressed yeast, set in a cup of lukewarm milk or water adding a teaspoon of salt and a tablespoon of sugar
3. Let this rise, if it does not, the yeast is not fresh or good
4. Measure eight cups of sifted flour into a deep bread bowl, add one teaspoon of salt
5. Make a depression in the centre, pour in the risen yeast and one cup of lukewarm milk or water
6. In winter be sure that the bowl, flour, milk, in fact everything has been thoroughly warmed before mixing
7. Mix the dough slowly with a wooden spoon and then knead
8. Set to rise again in a warm place free from drafts, and when it has risen to double its former bulk, take a knife, cut through the dough in several places
9. Place this dough on a baking board which has been sprinkled with flour
10. Work with the palm of the hand, always kneading towards the centre of the ball
11. When this leaves the board and the hands perfectly clean the dough may be formed into loaves or rolls
12. Place in pan greased slightly with a good oil, let rise until the imprint of the finger does not remain, and bake
13. Bake forty-five minutes in a moderate oven

title: White Cake
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.75 cup butter
- 1.25 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup cold water
- 8 eggs
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2.5 cups pastry flour
- 2 heaping teaspoons baking-powder

## Directions

1. Cream three-quarters cup of butter and one and one-quarter cups of sugar very well
2. Stop stirring, pour one-half cup of cold water on top of butter mixture and whites of eight eggs slightly beaten on top of water
3. Do not stir, add one teaspoon of vanilla
4. Sift two and one-half cups of pastry flour, measure, then mix with two heaping teaspoons of baking-powder, and sift three times
5. Add to cake mixture and then beat hard until very smooth
6. Turn into ungreased angel cake pan, place in slow oven
7. Let cake rise to top of pan, then increase heat and bake until firm
8. Invert pan, when cool cut out

title: White Caramel Icing
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of brown sugar
- one cup of milk
- a small lump of butter

## Directions

1. Put on to boil two cups of brown sugar, one cup of milk and a small lump of butter
2. Boil until it gets as thick as cream
3. Beat with a fork or egg whip until thick and creamy
4. Spread quickly on cake

title: White Caviar
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- roe of any fish
- salt
- two tablespoons of oil
- pepper
- a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove skin from roe and salt
2. Set aside over night
3. Next day beat roe apart
4. Pour boiling water over it and stir
5. When roe is white, pour off the water and let drain
6. Put in pan with two tablespoons of oil and salt, pepper, a little vinegar
7. Mix well
8. Let stand a few days before using

title: White Cucumber Sauce
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh young cucumbers
- vinegar
- water
- veal broth
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take out the seeds of some fresh young cucumbers
2. Quarter them and cut them into pieces of two inch lengths
3. Let them lay for an hour in vinegar and water
4. Simmer them till thoroughly soft in a veal broth seasoned with pepper, salt, and a little lemon juice
5. When ready for serving, pour off the gravy and thicken it with the yolks of a couple of eggs stirred in
6. Add it to the saucepan and warm up, taking care that it does not curdle

title: White Fondant
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half cups of granulated sugar
- three-fourths cup of hot water
- one-half salt-spoon of tartar

## Directions

1. Put two and one-half cups of granulated sugar in a saucepan
2. Add three-fourths cup of hot water and one-half salt-spoon of tartar
3. Stir until sugar is dissolved, but no longer
4. Boil without stirring until, when tried in cold water, it will form a soft ball
5. Wash down the edges of the pan with the finger first dipped in cold water, as the sugar boils up
6. Pour

title: White Mayonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon juice
- whipped cream or egg white

## Directions

1. Follow the ordinary directions for mayonnaise, using lemon juice instead of vinegar, omitting the mustard and adding, when finished, a half cup of whipped cream or half an egg white beaten very stiff.

title: White Mayonnaise
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolk
- salt
- red pepper
- lemon juice
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Follow the ordinary directions for mayonnaise, using lemon juice instead of vinegar, omitting the mustard

title: White Nut Bread
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one-half cups of flour
- four teaspoons of baking-powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- one-half cup of sugar
- one-half cup of walnut meats
- one egg
- one cup of milk

## Directions

1. Mix two and one-half cups of flour, four teaspoons of baking-powder, one-half teaspoon of salt, one-half cup of sugar and one-half cup of walnut meats, broken
2. Add one egg beaten with one cup of milk
3. Let this mixture stand for about twenty minutes in well-greased breadpan before placing in a moderate oven
4. Bake about an hour
5. Better day after it is made

title: White Sauce (for Vegetables)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons flour
- one-fourth teaspoon salt
- pepper
- one cup milk
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place two tablespoons of butter in a saucepan; stir until melted.
2. Add two tablespoons of flour mixed with one-fourth of a teaspoon of salt and a few grains of pepper.
3. Stir until smooth.
4. Add one cup of milk gradually and continue to stir until well mixed and thick.
5. Chopped parsley may be added.

title: White Sauce (For Vegetables)
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one-fourth of a teaspoon of salt
- a few grains of pepper
- one cup of milk
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Place two tablespoons of butter in a saucepan
2. Stir until melted
3. Add two tablespoons of flour mixed with one-fourth of a teaspoon of salt and a few grains of pepper
4. Stir until smooth
5. Add one cup of milk gradually and continue to stir until well mixed and thick
6. Chopped parsley may be added

title: White Sauce Omelet
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- pinch of pepper
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one-half cup of milk
- one-half cup of cream
- yolks of five eggs
- whites of five eggs
- two tablespoons of butter

## Directions

1. Make a white sauce of butter blended with flour, salt, pepper and sugar
2. Add milk and cream
3. Beat the yolks of five eggs and stir them into the sauce
4. Add the stiffly beaten whites of the eggs, folding them in carefully
5. Melt two tablespoons of butter in the omelet pan
6. When hot put in the mixture and let it stand in a moderate heat for two minutes
7. Place in a hot oven and cook until set
8. Remove from the oven, turn on a hot platter and serve

title: White Soup
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 lb. of boned brisket
- 8 pints of water
- salt
- pepper
- a blade of mace
- a little dried saffron
- one turnip
- a carrot
- a stick of celery
- an onion
- a tooth of garlic
- 2 eggs
- a little chopped parsley
- the juice of a lemon
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil boned brisket with water, salt, pepper, blade of mace, dried saffron, turnip, carrot, celery, onion, and garlic
2. Let it boil fast, then skim it well
3. Let it simmer till the meat is tender enough for dinner
4. Beat up in a basin 2 eggs, chopped parsley, juice of lemon, and grated nutmeg
5. Stir it in while boiling
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: White Soups from Stock No. 1
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of stock
- 1-lb. of vermicelli
- a couple of tomatoes
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Warm a quart of stock.
2. When it boils put in 1-lb. of vermicelli and let it boil for 10 minutes.
3. Cut a couple of tomatoes small and boil them.
4. Put them through a sieve and put it into the tureen before you dish up the soup.
5. Add a little grated nutmeg.
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Wiener Braten— Vienna Roast
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder
- vinegar
- fat or goose oil
- salt
- pepper
- ginger
- onion
- cloves
- bay leaf
- tomatoes
- soup stock
- white wine
- potato pancakes

## Directions

1. Take a shoulder, have the bone taken out and then pound the meat well with a mallet
2. Lay it in vinegar for twenty-four hours
3. Heat some fat or goose oil in a deep pan or kettle which has a cover that fits air tight and lay the meat in the hot fat
4. Sprinkle the upper side with salt, pepper and ginger
5. Put an onion in with the meat; stick about half a dozen cloves in the onion and add one bay leaf
6. Turn the meat over and sprinkle the other side with salt, pepper and ginger
7. Cut up one or two tomatoes and pour some soup stock over all, and a dash of white wine
8. Cover closely and stew very slowly for three or four hours, turning the meat now and then
9. Do not add any water
10. Make enough potato pancakes to serve one or two to each person with Wiener Braten

title: Wiener Kartoffel Kloesse
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- eight potatoes
- about one cup of flour
- two eggs
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- fresh goose fat
- part of an onion
- one-half cup of grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil eight potatoes
2. When they are very soft drain off every drop of water, lay them on a clean baking-board and mash them while hot with a rolling-pin, adding about one cup of flour
3. When thoroughly mashed, break in two eggs, salt to taste, and flavor with grated nutmeg
4. Flour the board thickly and roll out this potato dough about as thick as your little finger
5. Heat some fresh goose fat in a spider, cut up part of an onion very fine, add it to the hot fat together with one-half cup of grated bread crumbs
6. When brown, spread over the dough and roll just as you would a jelly-roll
7. Cut into desired lengths (about three or four inches), put them in boiling water, slightly salted, and boil uncovered for about fifteen minutes
8. Pour some hot goose grease over the dumplings

title: Wiener Studenten Kipfel
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- Wiener Kipfel dough
- melted butter
- one cup of chopped fresh walnuts
- one cup of brown sugar
- juice of a lemon
- cream
- sugar to taste
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Make dough same as for Wiener Kipfel
2. Roll it out quite thin on a well-floured board and let it rise
3. Cut into triangles
4. Before you cut them, spread with melted butter
5. Mix one cup of chopped fresh walnuts with one cup of brown sugar, juice of a lemon, or grind the nuts
6. Add cream to make a paste, sugar to taste and flavor with vanilla
7. Fill the triangles with the mixture
8. Take up the three corners and pinch together tightly
9. Set in well-buttered pans and let them rise again
10. Spread or brush each one with melted butter
11. Bake a light brown

title: Wine Blancmange
author: Estella Atrutel
body:
## Ingredients

- isinglass
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 oz. of castor sugar
- thin rind of a lemon
- a piece of cinnamon
- 1 pint of sherry

## Directions

1. Dissolve the isinglass as above
2. Beat the yolks of 4 eggs with 1/2 oz. of castor sugar
3. Boil in the isinglass the thin rind of a lemon and a piece of cinnamon
4. Skim it
5. Add to it the eggs and 1 pint of sherry
6. Grease your mould well
7. Put it in ice or a cool place
source_title: An Easy and Economical Book of Jewish Cookery

title: Wine Sauce
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of white wine
- one-half cup of water
- grated peel of lemon
- one teaspoon of potato flour
- cold water
- yolks of two eggs
- beaten whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Combine one cup of white wine, one-half cup of water, and grated peel of lemon
2. Wet one teaspoon of potato flour with cold water
3. Add the yolks of two eggs, stirring constantly
4. When thick, add the beaten whites and serve

title: Wine Sauce, No. 1
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of white wine
- one and one-half cups of water
- yolks of two eggs
- two teaspoons of white sugar
- grated nutmeg or cinnamon bark or grated rind of half a lemon
- one teaspoon of flour

## Directions

1. Take one-half cup of white wine and one and one-half cups of water
2. put on to boil in double boiler
3. beat up the yolks of two eggs very light, with two teaspoons of white sugar
4. some grated nutmeg or three small pieces of cinnamon bark, or the grated rind of half a lemon
5. add a teaspoon of flour to this gradually
6. When perfectly smooth add the boiling wine, pouring very little at a time and stirring constantly
7. Return to boiler and stir until the spoon is coated

title: Wine Sauce, No. 2
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one-half cup of cold water
- one cup of white wine
- one ounce of chopped citron
- one teaspoon of pistache
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- one teaspoon of red Curaqoa or Maraschino liquor

## Directions

1. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan
2. stir in one tablespoon of flour
3. add one-half cup of cold water, stirring constantly until smooth
4. add one cup of white wine
5. add one ounce of chopped citron
6. Remove from fire
7. let cool
8. flavor with one teaspoon each of pistache and vanilla
9. If desired, one teaspoon of red Curaqoa or Maraschino liquor can be added for flavoring

title: Wine Whey
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pint of milk
- white wine
- a little boiling water

## Directions

1. Set on the fire in a saucepan a pint of milk
2. When it boils, pour in as much white wine as will turn it into curds
3. Boil it up and let the curds settle
4. Strain off and add a little boiling water
5. Sweeten to taste

title: Winter Pea Soup
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of white peas
- water
- broth
- a little piece of chorissa or smoked beef
- mint
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Soak a quart of white peas in water
2. Boil them till soft, in as much water as will cover them
3. Pass them through a sieve, and add them to any broth that may be ready
4. A little piece of chorissa or smoked beef will improve the flavour
5. This soup should be served with mint and fried bread

title: Yeast Krantz
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee cake dough
- one-fourth cup of washed currants
- beaten eggs
- sugar
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Take coffee cake dough, add one-fourth cup of washed currants
2. Let rise in warm place, then toss on floured board
3. Divide into three or four equal parts, roll each part into a long strand and work the strands together to form one large braid
4. Place braid in form of a circle in greased baking-pan or twist the braid to resemble the figure eight, pretzel shape
5. Let rise again in a warm place and bake in a moderate oven one-half hour or until thoroughly done
6. Brush with beaten eggs and sugar, sprinkle with a few chopped almonds
7. Return to oven to brown slightly

title: Yom-Tov Soup
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of ribs of beef
- one chicken
- plenty of water
- a split carrot
- onion
- a head of celery
- a little parsley root
- pepper and salt to taste
- a pinch of saffron

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of ribs of beef and one chicken.
2. Place in a large cooking-vessel with plenty of water and add a split carrot and onion, a head of celery, a little parsley root, pepper and salt to taste, and a pinch of saffron.
3. Let the whole simmer for two hours.
4. The meat is then removed and can be used as a separate dish.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
author: Judith Cohen Montefiore (attr.)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of flour
- four eggs or two eggs
- one pint of milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix into a smooth batter half a pound of flour, four eggs, if intended to be rich, otherwise two, a pint of milk, and a little salt
2. it should be about an inch thick
3. it can be made with or without milk by using a greater proportion of eggs, but it is not so good

title: Zwieback
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup of milk
- one cake of compressed yeast
- one-fourth cup of sugar
- one-fourth cup of melted butter
- one-half teaspoon of salt
- three eggs unbeaten
- one-half teaspoon of powdered anise
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald one-half cup of milk and when lukewarm add to one cake of compressed yeast
2. Add one-fourth cup of sugar, one-fourth cup of melted butter, one-half teaspoon of salt and three eggs unbeaten, one-half teaspoon of powdered anise and enough flour to handle
3. Let rise until light
4. Make into oblong rolls the length of middle finger and place together in a buttered pan in parallel rows, two inches apart
5. Let rise again and bake twenty minutes
6. When cold, cut in one-half inch slices and brown evenly in the oven

title: Zwieback
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of five eggs
- one-half pound of sugar
- one tablespoon of water
- vanilla
- one-half pound of flour
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one-half of five cents worth anise seeds
- beaten whites of five eggs

## Directions

1. Take the yolks of five eggs, one-half pound of sugar, one tablespoon of water, vanilla, one-half pound of flour, one teaspoon of baking-powder, one-half of five cents worth anise seeds, and the beaten whites of the eggs
2. Butter square-tins and bake
3. When cooled cut in strips one inch wide and toast on both sides

title: Zwieback Torte
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of six eggs
- one and one-eighth cups of sugar
- one-half box of zwieback
- one teaspoon of baking-powder
- one tablespoon of rum or sherry wine
- one-half teaspoon of bitter almond extract
- whites of six eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of six eggs with one and one-eighth cups of sugar
2. Add one-half box of zwieback, which has been rolled very fine
3. Add one teaspoon of baking-powder
4. Season with one tablespoon of rum or sherry wine and one-half teaspoon of bitter almond extract
5. Fold in the stiffly-beaten whites of the six eggs
6. Bake in ungreased form in moderate oven three-quarters of an hour

title: Zwiebel Matzoth
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- cake of unleavened bread
- raw onion
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Rub a cake of unleavened bread with a raw onion, sprinkle lightly with salt
2. Place in the oven for a few minutes to dry
3. Butter and eat hot

title: Zwiebel Platz
author: Florence Kreisler Greenbaum
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of rye bread dough
- butter
- onions
- salt

## Directions

1. After dough has risen sufficiently roll out quite thin
2. butter a long cake pan and put in the rolled dough
3. Brush with melted butter
4. chop some onions very fine, strew thickly on top of cake
5. sprinkle with salt
6. put flakes of butter here and there
